It works much differently from the Progesterone (Progestin) that the M.D's usually order. WOnder if you have that in U.K. You might want to read through the messages about what hormones people are using on this message board. I read about someone else who is using the Bi-est (compounded estrogen) and the natural progesterone cream and she stated she thinks it has helped her I.C. Bev

I posted this message before in the related conditions section, but feel it may be more relevent here. Has anyone been found to have hypothyroid conditions due to thyroid antibodies? I was on the thyroid forum and Larrain Gillespie who use to be the cystitis diva is a consultant on the estrogen/proesterone/ and hypothyroidism message board. There are some women with IC there who said that thyroid therapy helped their bladder condition, along with the right combination of hormones. She said that the bladder has thyroid hormone receptors that are needed and she found that many times when women fix their thyroid, everything else falls into place. I have normal thyroid function by standard tests, but when other tests were done I was found to have very high thyroid antibodies causing an auto immune problem with my thyroid. Just wondering if anyone else has had their thyroid tested?
I was diagnosed several years ago with hypo thyroidism and have been on eltroxin> when my thyroid is out it makes the sypmtoms of my IC worse so I think that it is related somehow. Sharon B.

Hi, I have hypothyroidism and I take synthroid everyday. It does not seem to help my bladder at all. I have a blood test done every six months and have had to up my dosage but this still makes no difference with the pain in my bladder. Hope this helps. KellyB

Originally posted by sharber: I was diagnosed several years ago with hypo thyroidism and have been on eltroxin> when my thyroid is out it makes the sypmtoms of my IC worse so I think that it is related somehow. Sharon B. If you don't mind my asking, were your thyroid tests abnormally low, or like me, did you have thyroid antibodies and normal results? I am just so afraid that if I take the thyroid meds they may make my bladder worse. Thyrolar has both t3&t4. What is the medicine that you are taking? What feels better about your bladder when your thyroid is right? thanks so much.

I have Hashimoto's Thyroiditis and that is my thyroid gland is under attack by the auto-antibodies because my immune system is in overdrive. This causing the Thyroid to fail. The doc first put me on Synthroid but I had a burn in my bladder for the first time. Now I am on the natural Thyroid called Armour and I am doing well. I have not been able to take any Estrogen therapy since my complete hysterectomy because I am so drug sensitive. I tried cutting the Vivelle patch in quarters knowing I need Estrogen and I had severe cramping in my legs. I also couldn't sleep because it interfered with my Thyroid meds. It made me very hyper. A no-no for ICers. Anyway, I don't relate my thyroid much to my bladder except if I take too much ( being the right amount for the average person),it bothers my bladder. I can't be in the normal range because I can't be the old hyper person I used to be with IC. I have settled being slightly hypo and my doc agrees. Hope this didn't add more confusion. Everyone is different, that's for sure!
Because Larrian Gillespie had her medical license suspended I verify all the information she provided with another source --- see http://www.sonic.net/jill/icnet/features/296.html Donna

contents: I swear mine did...started 6 years ago when I was 46...had a hystorectomy when I was 26 but kept my ovaries. So, no periods or lack thereof to let me know what's going on. Told my gyno about infrequent flashes recently, and been tested with FSH test a number of times the past few years. Finally the test showed positive for menopause just a few weeks ago.... Now what to do???? I hate to start hormones right now, since I just started 3 new meds also a few weeks ago...one is Neurontin. Hate to add another to the mix. But here's the thing.... I've been having incredible hot flashes, up to 15 a day and through the night causing sleep disturbance (as if we need another thing causing sleep problems!). It's making me wonder if I ought to revisit this thing with my gyno. Been reading up here and lots of you say you've had problems with certain hormone combinations...and read about compounding hormones, etc. If you could help with any of these it would be appreciated.. 1) What info did you give your doc about IC and hormones? Any research studies, etc? 2) Have any of you tried tofu?? I hesitate since it's listed as a no-no on the IC diet. Have started eating some soy nuts, though. 3) Does the vagifem cream help with flashes? Thanks to all of you for reading my long post. It really helps, especially for a topic like this, to find out what others have tried. Here's to some cool flashes! Susan
signature: Positive thoughts and prayers that new treatments for IC will reach all who struggle with this disease, SusanC

contents: I've heard that evening primrose oil works wonders for hot flashes. As for a good source of information on menopause, I highly suggest any books by Christiane Northrup. She has some really great ideas.

Dear Susan - I got my IC at 46 also. It was my belief that I got it because my estrogen was too low. So, I went to my gynecologist and asked for it, but without progesterone. It worked for me and my IC symptoms went away. I am now 52 and have since had a hysto for another problem called adenomyosis (endometrosis inside the wall of the uterus instead of outside of it). Let me say, this is not a treatment for everyone and is not without risks (uterine cancer is one, although very small and if followed closely with ultrasounds can be caught early) because you cannot take progesterone with it. The progesterone will undo the beneficial effects of the estrogen on your bladder. Unfortunately, you will not find information about this treatment for IC. I have done all the preliminary research on this and sent it to Vicki Ratner of the ICA in Maryland who is trying to find someone to do some research in this area. She believes for some that this is also an area that needs research and further study. My gyno is a wonderful man and his wife is my urologist. She didn't think this would work, but it did. He now uses it for other ICers who are in my age bracket who get IC during this time of life. If you want any further information or your doctor would like to talk to my gyno, e-mail me at [email protected]. Hope this has been helpful. NancyB

Hi Susan, I'm 26 so obviously my IC doesn't have anything to do with perimenopause Anyway, I'm replying because my Dr is doing 2 hormone studies right now. One is about pregnancy and the other one is about hormones. I couldn't participate because I'm on the pill. Hopefully, we'll be hearing about results soon. take care, Mel
signature: Melanie __

contents: I also have wondered if my bladder problems are hormone related. I was 47 when my symptoms started and am now 49. Dr.'s will not order estrogen for me because I am still having regular periods and my mother died from breast cancer at a young age. I have to admit that I am leary of taking estrogen but nothing else has helped so far. Bev

Thanks to everyone for your replies. But I now have one more question: What type, or brand name, and what strength estrogen did you take that helped? You guys are so great! One thing I've learned in the past 24 hours though. My almost continual flashes lasted for 4 days. After I "upped" the Neurontin on Thursday night, I had fewer flashes than before. Only 5 or 6 compared to 15. There must be a hormone connection with the Neurontin. What do you think? Wishing everyone an IC "comfortable" weekend, Susan
signature: Positive thoughts and prayers that new treatments for IC will reach all who struggle with this disease, SusanC

contents: No--- I am 16 and have IC. Amanda

Dear Susan - I use the Climera patch. You can titrate the dose to what you need by cutting it yourself. The manufacturer doesn't recommend this, but you can do it anyhow. You would be surprised just how much difference a little one way or the other can do. I would get the .l mg one and start with half of that and then add more if you need it - actually .05 is what most gynos start people on and go up from there. I have been on others like Ogen and Premarin, but didn't like them, bothered me in some way. Estrace pills were fine after my system cleared up. You will probably know in a few days if it is helping you. It took me a few weeks for my bladder to completely clear up and another 2-3 months for the rest of my system (bowels, etc.- basically anything that had a lining) to feel right again. Hope this is helpful. NancyB

Thanks for the help. Wanted to let you know that I checked on alternative therapies for hormone replacement. Nothing has been tested and recommended. They still don't know the amounts, etc. to use of soy, tofu, primrose oil, etc. So I will ask gyno about Climera. Any other ideas out there? How confusing! We hear one thing in the media...and another with the real medical authorities and scientists! Sending healing wishes, Susan
signature: Positive thoughts and prayers that new treatments for IC will reach all who struggle with this disease, SusanC

contents: Dear Nancy B: I was 38 when I had my hysto for fibroids and adenomyosis. Had no problem with recovery, not even one round of antibiotics. My ovaries were left in also, so I had no idea what was going on with my body because my Dr. kept asking me if i were having any hot flashes, and I was not, so no hormone tests were ever done. Then at age 48, I get this huge UTI, My first ever, and THEN they start to do all of these tests and as it turns out I am waaayyy past menopause and have osteoporosis and everything. I have been taking compounded bi estrogen along with progesterone cream and vagifem and feel like this has helped. Wish this all could have been prevented but Drs back then were not interested in perimenopause. I definitely feel that my problem is hormone related.

Rather than tack on to several of the other posts, I think I'll just start over with a new one. I developed IC during perimenopause at age 40. I'm 46 now and 18 mos. past natural menopause. There is breast CA in the family so no HRT for me. I tried every natural product, soy, creams, EPO, combipatch, vegitabs, genistein, flax seed, progesterone cream, etc. and they all just killed me. I knew estrace was out because I'd get urethritis in a matter of hours so I opted for trying Vagifem. The dose is daily for 14 days then drop to twice/week. It's on the same par as estrace and considered safe for breast CA history in family. I made it to day 10 but by then it was killing my bladder! So I dropped to the maintenance dose on day 10 and found the 3 day gap was still too close and caused a problem. So I went to every 5 days and found I could tolerate that well. I was told it would help dryness and sexual response but not hot flashes but not so, it wiped out my hotflashes too which after 18 mos. of up to 25 day/ this is great! The flashes start to come back on day 4-5 so I'm next going to try vagifem every 4 but if it flares me, I'm going to stick with every 5. I am so happy to have found the right answer for me. My bladder has been better but I dont know if it relates to vagifem or the SANS unit. That's all I'm using for IC though.

I've seen evening primrose oil recommended in magazine articles and here on the ICN board. Anyone tried it or heard of it's success with and IC-er? What brand name and how much is taken to get a good effect? Thanks so much for your help. My husband thanks you, too! Between "Stella" the cat pushing against him and my blankets the poor guy is having a rough time at night! Healing hugs and positive thoughts coming your way, Susan

contents: Susan, you can purchase Evening Primrose oil at a health food store. I take Black Currant seed oil, which is very similar to Evening Primrsoe oil. I only take one a day. Some people need more. The info. I have read says start low on the oils and listen to your body. If you want more info., there are many books on the oils and your health food store can direct you to those books. Bev

I am not certain if this is the right board on which to post this message, but I am needing info. I have been having a period for the last ten or eleven days...not heavy the whole time, but spotting red today. I am normally a seven-day person like clock-work. I am forty-eight, and also take Elmiron and Effexor XR, and anti-depressant for pain control. The Elmiron is a mild blood thinner, I know, so that could explain the heaviness I experience. The Effexor can cause "menstrual disturbances", so the PDR book says, whatever that may be defined as. But, I was also wondering if this could occur with the onset of menopause...heavier periods that are longer-lasting than usual. Could anyone give any feedback? I have an appt. that was already scheduled with the dr. who prescribed the Effexor, so I will run all this past him. Just wondering if I am the only "lucky" one here, or not. Hugs to All, Kathy S.

I have had hormone implants done once a year for 7 years now and it is great. It is taken in your system as the body needs it. They put it in your lower ab and it is a bandaid insert, and then in two days take off bandaid and it is good for the next 9 months to a year. I have to go ever 9/10 months. I never have to worry about any side effects as there are very few. I had a hysterectomy 12 years ago and before this implant was allergict to everything and talk about hot flashes and such. This has been a god sent. Any ???? email me at: [email protected] Hope this helps some out there.

Sandie, I have been wondering for a long time if my Estrogen patches are causing me to have flares. How is your IC with the implant? Do you ever have bad effects from it? What is the procedure to have it implanted? I am very interested in finding out more about the implant. thanks christina
signature: Christina "Faith precedes the Miracle"

contents: Dear Christina: It is a simple procedure. They make a small cut on the skin on the abdomen and put in a small pellet(it is figured out by your size, how much you weight, etc)and then closed with sturey strips. 2-4 days later you can take off the strips. Nothing more to it. It is great, but not all doctors do the procedure. You have to find one who does in your area. Where are living? I am in the northwest(WA state). A lot of furtility clinics do this procedure(because they have messed with hormones for many years). My IC is better for the first 6 months or so, is doesn't bother or make my IC worse. I was allergeric to everything else. Any more ??? just email me [email protected]

Well, after 4 days on the patch, I have the mother of all flares so I just removed it. How long til this is out of my system!!? Aaahh. I'm so discouraged. I still have a uterus so I don't see what I can use without a flare since everything has progesterone in it, doesn't it? I finally had great pain-free sex last night after 4 days on the patch so I'm really bummed having to give it up but I can't live with this kind of bladder pain. I checked the archives for the last 100 days. Isn't prometrium the same as progesterone? Is anyone able to tolerate prometrium that couldn't tolerate progesterone or the patch?
signature: Dianne My bellydance "sisters" , our dogs, and me.

contents: Hi Dianne, I have been taking Prometrium for two years. It is natural micronized progesterone, which is NOT the same thing as the progestins found in most HRT products. I believe it has been available longer here in Canada than it has in the States. I was diagnosed with IC just a couple of months ago, and at first I was very concerned that the Prometrium had been a factor in my developing IC, but I've done some research over the past few days and I am no longer worried about it. I actually posted a question on this board about progesterone, but no one out there had any experience I guess, because I had no replies. I stopped taking it for two weeks and it made no difference to my IC, and have been back on it for 3 days with no flare. In her book, Dr. Gillespie talks about the difference between Prometrium (progesterone) and the artificial progestins in most HRT's. I hope that you can find a combination that works for you and doesn't cause flares.

Diane, I tried every kind of HRT out there and the ONLY thing working is this: First, I tried every kind of HRT and everything sent me to flaring. However, here is what I now know: Propolyn glycol and many fillers both in creams and in patch form set many of us off. The only thing that did work was to have my HRT compounded minus all the dyes and fillers. I have to refrigerate the estridiol cream but at least I can tolerate. Just this month we are switching to a dye free capsule. The pharmasist is making those today. I highly suggest you locate a compounding pharmacy in your area and then TELL YOUR OBGY to have your HRT compounded minus all the dyes and fillers. Start out with pure estridiol and then add the testosterone later if you can tolerate. Another thing, pure estridiol is much weaker than normal HRT so it may be necessary to take everyday. Good Luck Sherry

I have been on Premarin and Provera since my hysterectomy in September. If I am going to flare because of something I do it right away, and so far no flare. I would suggest you do the patch while on the IC bare mininum diet. Don't eat, drink or do anything that might cause a flare, while wearing the patch. If you still have a flare then blame it on the HRT. Otherwise, you may be associating something else with the patch when it is not guilty at all. I know I make that mistake a lot then find out when I do the real trigger again.....and bam I realize I made a mistake. I wish you the best in your search for HRT. Lisa

contents: There is a new product called Vagifem. It is estrogen in a pill that you insert with a prefilled applicator. There are benefits in using this as there a none of the chemicals that are in the cream. The only thing that may be a problem is that you do have to insert it into the vagina with a very, very thin plunger. Anyway, it may be something you want to talk to your Drs about as an option.

Thanks, Carrie. I appreciate knowing about this. I'll check it out tomorrow. Do you know-does this kind of work same as estrace, more in topical mode or would you have to use prometrium or progesterone of some sort if you have a uterus. That's where I'm stuck because I still have a uterus and can't tolerate the progesterone type category but could sure stand to get some estrogen to the tissues.
signature: Dianne My bellydance "sisters" , our dogs, and me.

contents: Dear Dianne I think it works like the estrace cream, but without the mess. You could try topical progesterone cream, but I think you would have to talk to your doctor about that. I can say that for me, the vagifem worked much better than the cream because I always had burning probably from the other chemicals. I do not know how much gets absorbed into your bloodstream, so I really do not know if you would need progesterone or not. The natural progesterone cream comes in many different strenghts and you rub into your palms, thighs, any thin skin. Hope this helps.

Well, I'm 1 week on vagifem now and so far, so good. Even the hot flashes are gone which supposedly isn't supposed to happen. My bladder has been a little worse but after doing a SANS treatment last night, I'm back to prior level and had a good night. I'm hoping to tough this out til the two weeks and then cut to twice/week maintenance and hopefully the bladder will be liveable while still getting the results. Thanks so much for telling me about this option. I had already tried progesterone cream and it about killed me.
signature: Dianne My bellydance "sisters" , our dogs, and me.

contents: Dear Dianne: I am so happy that I could help someone. My bladder was a little worse at first also, but I think it was because my muscles were so tight when I tried to insert the tablet,thatI stirred up my PVD. I am much better now, and am SOOO happy to hear that this has worked for you. Hope you have continued success. PS you might get a vaginal discharge which is your tissues getting engorged with estrogen. If this should happen, do not panic-talk it over with your GYN. Also, I have found the Upjohn people very helpful. THey did not know about IC and the vagifem and were very interested in hearing any adverse side effects. Take care.
Hi all I understood that as I have IC are am not able take up running because of putting strain on my pelvic? Is this true
Yes it is true. Running jars the pelvis and the pelvic floor. It's better doing exercises that keep your hips level like walking.. or elliptical. Jill

contents: Hi everyone, I wanted to share my experience with using ping pong as a way to manage my IC (Interstitial Cystitis) symptoms. While it may seem like an unlikely solution, playing ping pong has helped me to stay active and relieve stress, which in turn has helped to reduce my flare-ups. Here are a few reasons why I think ping pong can be beneficial for those with IC: Low Impact: Ping pong is a low-impact sport, which makes it easier on the joints and can help to reduce inflammation. Aerobic Exercise: Playing ping pong can be a great source of aerobic exercise, which has been shown to reduce stress and improve overall health. Social Connection: Joining a ping pong club or playing with friends can be a great way to stay socially connected, which is important for mental and emotional health. Distraction: When playing ping pong, you are focused on the game and not on your IC symptoms. This can be a welcome distraction and help to reduce anxiety and stress. I've found many informative contenet here https://pingpongacademy.org/ping-pong-backhand/ that incorporating ping pong into my routine has been a great way to stay active, relieve stress, and manage my IC symptoms. Have any of you tried using ping pong or other sports to manage your IC? I'd love to hear your thoughts and experiences. And if you haven't tried ping pong before, I encourage you to give it a shot!​
Love it. Whatever works right.

This may not be the appropriate place to post this, but I thought maybe it might fall under fitness if it keeps us well. That brings me to my question. I have had IC since 1994 and am much better as far as pain and frequency in my. bladder, however, I do have a number of autoimmune problems - fibromyalgia, IBS, arthritis, chronic fatigue, and Hashimoto's Thyroiditis, asthma. With all that is happening in the world with disease and thinking about how sick I got last year with something I got from my husband, I am seriously considering getting a flu shot and a pneumonia shot this year. I am tired of ending up in emergency rooms when I catch a cold or worse, and it makes my asthma go crazy, and I honestly think I am going to die. So my question is this: Have any of you gotten flu shots and did you have adverse reactions because of autoimmune problems? Same question for the pneumonia shot. I really think I should have it because of how sick I got last year, but I am afraid it will cause me to have the flu or pneumonia. Please let me know I what your experience was if you were brave enough to get these vaccines. Faye Combs

Re: Flu Shots - any adverse reactions in the IC population? I have had flu vaccines done for 20 years and have had a pneumonia one too. I have to get them done because of my heart. I haven't noticed anything IC way or anything. Sometimes you feel like you have a mini flu for 24 hours or so and sometimes you have a sore arm for a bit but other than that i just see the good in vaccines since a flu almost killed me before i got flu shots annually. Hope that helps
signature: Have had ic symptoms for 18 years, officially diagnosed 15 years....treatments--herbs, antidepressants, anticonvulsants, overactive bladder meds, painkillers, instils........ Had endo but had to have a hysterectomy, now menopause yay Vulvadynia, ibs, acid reflux, raynauds, TMJ, and suspected auto-immune disorder of some kind Other things, heart defect and scoliosis

contents: Re: Flu Shots - any adverse reactions in the IC population? I don't have an autoimmune condition but I do get a minor immune response from the flu vaccines. I've been told you don't and can't get the actual flu from the vaccine but can have a mild response for a day or two. I got the 4 strain vaccine this year and next year I will be getting the stronger one for those 65 and older. The one year I didn't get the vaccine I ended up in urgent care unable to breathe because I was so sick. Never again will I skip it. Does your Dr. feel it will be beneficial for you considering your autoimmune conditions?

Re: Flu Shots - any adverse reactions in the IC population? After having contracted the flu back in '96 I swore I would never go a year w/o getting the vaccine. If youve ever had influenza you'll wish you wouldve been the first in line for the vaccine! If you have any serious illness such as HBO, heart disease, kidney disease, etc etc etc you dont want to skip the vaccine as catching influenza can be very serious and possibly deadly. Ive never had any adverse reaction to the shot nor does it bother my bladder. I have felt a little off for a day or two in the past, however this year the shot didnt affect me at all...no after effects nor any arm soreness at the injection site. I got mine at my local Rite-Aid and was in and out in 5 minutes. Diana

contents: Re: Flu Shots - any adverse reactions in the IC population? I had the flu shot last week--no problem. My husband has an auto-immune disease (psoriatic arthritis, similar to rheumatoid arthritis) and he also got the flu shot--no problems. I would think that for folks with IC and auto-immune diseases getting the flu shot would be even more important. Also if you are over 50 , get the SHINGLES vaccine. I had shingles (before I had the shingles shot) and it was by far the worse thing I ever had (had shingles 4 years ago and I still have pain). Jo Ann

Re: Flu Shots - any adverse reactions in the IC population? Just a tip- the nurse this year told me that if you move your arm around a lot the day of the shot, you are less likely to get a stiff arm. So this year when I got the shot I actually went to the gym that night and it worked, but she said that even just rotating and shaking your arm occasionally from a seated position will help. I haven't had IC in a big way for every long but the two shots I have gotten have not caused me any problems. The one I just got was during a really good control week actually because symptoms have been minimal lately.

contents: Re: Flu Shots - any adverse reactions in the IC population? I went to the local VA clinic and got one last week.(as in flu shot) By the way, my arm didn't even bother me. I will state a purple heart recipient came in. I was aware of that because he was wearing a hat that was advertising it. OK. I asked how he came to be one. First of all, he is 96 years old by now. Second of all, he walks better than I do. Third of all, he was not showing obvious signs of being a disabled vet. (if he is actually considered one) OK. He was wounded in the Battle of Iwo Jima. Anyhow, I decided to mention that now. Yep.

This is an old thread but important topic! Just wanted to chime in to say I got my flu shot & didn't have any reaction at all. Same goes for covid shots & booster. I'd rather deal with a flare than have flu or covid (especially)

Flu and COVID shots have been given to billions and billions of people by now. They are clearly overwhelmingly safe. While there are risks, the most serious risks are almost certainly already known and documented. People can confidently make their decision based on current evidence and recommendations. They are job requirements for me, though that's also a choice.

Originally posted by anabelle Flu and COVID shots have been given to billions and billions of people by now. They are clearly overwhelmingly safe. While there are risks, the most serious risks are almost certainly already known and documented. People can confidently make their decision based on current evidence and recommendations. They are job requirements for me, though that's also a choice. My IC was diagnosed in 1975 and I have had a flu shot every year and I've had all of the Covid vaccines, including boosters, with no problem other than a sore arm for a few days. I suspect that side effects among those with IC/PBS occur in about the same frequency as the total population. Donna

contents: Back in the day I did yoga frequently. Recently getting back into it. I tried a "strengthen your pelvic floor" yoga video. About an hour later I was in serious trouble! Running to bathroom, had some leaking. I cried & called my doc! (Diagnosed a few days later) Luckily that horrible flare was over by that evening, but WOW I'm not doing that video again! If you're thinking of trying it for first time, stick with the beginners stuff, or "gentle" or even the videos made for pregnancy 😉 It is great for stretching, using muscles you normally don't use, stress reduction etc. Love it, but just like everything else, moderation, and start slow!

contents: Can I ask what other supplements you take for energy that you have found that doesn't flare you?

Yesterday I drove over three hours to see a physical therapist. After my appointment, I went for a hike. I ascended about 2000 feet/600 meters in 1.5 miles/2.5 km in 2 hours and 15 minutes. I am now experiencing a serious flare. Another possible cause of this flare is the fact that I spent a total of 9 hours behind the wheel (the hiking destination was 4.5 hours from my home). I thought that exercise would increase blood circulation and promote healing. Could it be that walking uphill is to be avoided?

I have to avoid any activity that puts a strain on my abdominal muscles --- so yes, walking uphill could be a problem. And yes, that many hours driving is a lot. You had a very busy day. If you will be going to the therapist again, you might want to consider stopping overnight so you can split the driving into two days --- or at least saving the hike for another day. I hope you feel better quickly. Donna

contents: Hi all, not sure if anyone has any suggestions here, but figured I'd try! With everything going on in the world right now, I can't go shopping to try out a bike seat to know how it will feel based on my posture, weight, pelvic concerns, etc. I thought I might reach out here and see if any women have experience with any specific bike seats that minimize pelvic problems/pain and go from there My Uro/Gyn said it would be great but limit to 30-45 min max per ride, my PF Physio said it would be great to help strengthen my quads and subsequently my core but I want to be comfortable!

One thing you might do is look at seats on the internet and see if you can find something that doesn't put a lot of pressure on a narrow seat. Since there aren't many stores open these days, I've been using the internet for a lot of things --- and if you order something and need to return it, I have found it's usually a simple process. Good luck! Donna

I'm okay with MultiRight vitamins, which were developed specifically for IC patients. I've never tried patches --- if you try them, please post with your reaction. Donna

contents: i have not tried weight watchers. i do love http://www.myfitnesspal.com though. you can log your calories & exercise there and there is even an app for your phone. and it's free. though, i haven't used it in awhile. only within about the last few weeks to a month did i start the IC diet. it's hard enough finding things i like to eat; i don't want to feel bad if the calorie count is too high.

contents: HI there! I've done weight watchers for almost two years, yet took a couple months off near the end of November 2011. Although, the last year has been a little rocky since they rolled out the new Points Plus plan - I've been up and down and gained some of my weight back (UGH!) I just restarted last week and had my first weigh in where I lost 2 pounds. I've injured my back in the middle of last week, so I've not been doing as good as I was during the first part of my week. I've been in a lot of pain and had a couple days where I did not eat all my points for the day (which I know is not good). I'm hoping to continue my loss and keep on plan as much as I can. Today, I did well, yet not sure I got in enough fruits and vegetables for the day. Keep us posted on how things go for you. Best wishes, Jennifer Last edited by theclownster; 03-03-2012, 02:07 PM.

contents: weight Thanks ladies for the info its really hard but I am giving it my best so far I have lost 7 pounds I stared on the 9th of January wish me good luck?
signature: Blessings, Ruth

contents: I dont know much about weight watchers I do know that my diet is to restricted to have some one else prepare my food. Or try to tell me what I can eat when I know it wont agree with me. I have seen 3 nutritionist and they all tell me that my body has been in starvation mode for way too long. I am to take in 1,500 to 1,900 cal per day to lose weight. I take in about 800 to 900cal per day. I am told that this causes my body to store every calorie. With the health problems I have it has been very difficult for a nutritionist to create a diet for me. My triglycerides are through the roof (so no carbs unless they are whole wheat, or grain) My cholesterol is through the roof and I take medication for this. I am a candidate for pancreas failure. My IC diet does not work with my IBS diet. My sister in law is also a nutritionist and she is at a loss to what I can and cannot eat to bring my calorie intake up to 1,900. If you find something that works pls let me know

You are welcome curlycue. I've lost 7.6 pounds total since I rejoined Weight Watchers on February 4th. It can be hard at times, yet I'm still enjoying all the foods I love, just not as much of the bad ones. I have learned through Weight Watchers is that a lot of time I don't eat enough food in a day. Weight Watchers helps me to get all the points in I need and to also be aware of my portions. I feel like I'm doing better now and hope to continue the loss - I have a long way to go! ChrisNes - I'm so sorry to hear what you are going through. It definitely sounds like eating more food might help. When I don't eat enough food I tend to gain weight or it slows down my loss. I'm sorry I don't have any suggestions for you. I hope you are able to come up with a plan that works for you. Best wishes everyone, Jennifer

contents: Thanks, Nope no plan. I dont know what to do about it. I am unable to move fast enough to burn off calories. Not enough variety to eat. I am afraid of being constipated because I wouldnt be able to have another bowel pouch fixed and would have to have a bag Any suggestions would be great. I can not lift more than 20 lbs on a one time lift. The pool irritates my skin. Hips and knees have bursists.

I'm so sorry to hear this. I have IBS too, yet am not as diet sensitive IC wise. In fact I'm very fortunate in that regard now that my IC symptoms are under control with medication. I do of course have some triggers, yet over time have gradually been able to eat some foods in moderation Are you able to eat whole grain hot cereal (I think it's a mix of oat, wheat, barley and something else)? I buy the Quaker brand and usually make mine in the microwave and it takes a few minutes. It's a lot like eating a bowl of oatmeal to me but it's mixed grains. I sometimes sweeten it or add something to give it a little flavor. Something like that (high in fiber) might help prevent constipation. I also have a history of high genetic cholesterol and the first time I learned this my triglycerides were a little high - yet I don't think they have been high in quite some time. I take natural over the counter supplements to help keep my cholesterol in check. I still test borderline high at times, but unfortunately I couldn't tolerate the statin drugs. I also buy butter/margarine type spread that is made to help lower cholesterol (has plant sterols in it). Not all of them melt well. The one I'm using most these days is the "Smart Balance Heart Right Light" (or something like that). What about cheese, yogurt , or milk? Do you tolerate any of those? Maybe adding in servings of dairy (as snacks) will help you get the extra calories you need. Greek yogurt also usually has a few more calories and more protein than regular yogurt. You would definitely need to read labels as yogurts can contain ingredients that irritate the bladdrer. Are there any fruits you tolerate? I can do some in moderation - for me I'm okay with apples, bananas, and some berries (in moderation). How about nuts? They might help too. I know for some IC'ers, soy can be a trigger, but I have found I'm able to drink soy milk - I usually drink a light vanilla soy milk (as well as regular skim milk). I'm definitely not a nutritionist or anything, just a few thoughts on things that might be able to increase your calorie intake. I'll be keeping you in my thoughts and prayers. Best wishes, Jennifer

contents: Hi, sorry 'no' to most of the food you suggested. Thank you for trying. I think the elmiron is the cause of a lot of my acid reflux. I dont like milk but I make myself have at least a cup of milk per-day. oatmeal has potassium in it. No aged cheese products, i can have a few cashews, no yogurt, no soy (we have breast cancer in my family so the doctors tell me to stay away from soy) I cant have many nuts with pouches in my colon. For fruit I eat red pairs, some blue berries, some strawberries, some times I can have grapes, and a few cherries. No citrus of any kind. Veggies I can have a few bites of baby potatoes, I can eat some carrots, mushrooms, celery, some lettuce. A lot of veggies have potassium and I cant have that. I am unable to eat any legumes. I dont eat a lot of breads, If i have breads they are whole wheat. I stay away from most red meat. My cholesterol problem is genetic. I can eat some fish/salmon, chicken, ham. Like today I had a small bowl of multi grain Cheerios with milk, I had a half of a bagel, (i am afraid to get constipated, i dont want to end up with a colostomy bag) I had a couple bites of pasta, a red pair and a nibble of a cheese curd, and a bowl of cantaloupe/melon. I am now paying for what I ate, I feel like I am going to throw up. I like to eat, but the 3 anti-depressants I am on make it so I dont feel hungry and I forget to eat...you would think I would be skin and bones but as I get older my metabolism has really slowed down. I have slowed down and I dont get much quality sleep with all the pain and going pee all night. I also think the cyst on my right-ovaries is getting bigger and messes with my hormones. My CFS has been hitting me hard today. Everything I do is taxing on me. I believe the Epstine-bar-virus I contracted in college is why I have Chronic Fatigue Syndrome. I appreciate the food suggestions....Suggestions welcome here...
Re: weight I have not tried weight watchers. Is it helpful?

Re: weight Originally posted by JasonSharpe I have not tried weight watchers. Is it helpful? This is a very old thread, but I will comment. I don't have a weight problem any more, but the Weight Watchers one is probably the most flexible one out there. Plus if you attend the meetings, they will help answer any questions and provide good encouragement. Good luck! Donna

contents: Re: Running a Half Marathon I used to run before IC diagnoses, so pre diet, pre meds, etc. I loved to run on wooded trails with my dogs, till I started have quite a bit of blood in my urine, visible. Nobody seemed to know i had blood in my urine, i was advised to stop running and see if it stopped, it did. then came along many other bladder problems, eventually with a diagnoses of IC. I would think that if you symptoms are mild enough or your in remission that you to run, then go for it, all the power to you. if it is going to bother your bladder you will know soon enough. We are all so different if you can run do it, embrace what you can do, the rest of us will through your blog and behind you all the way. You go
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Running a Half Marathon I just finished one a couple weeks ago! I am glad you are doing this and sharing. Unless I am feeling really bad and/or have a UTI, I don't find that running makes me feel any worse. If anything I feel better. Certainly psychologically better! I do have to be a little more careful I don't over do it, I occasionally get a UTI a couple days after a long run, take more frequent walk breaks (Jeff Galloway style), know where the potty stops are during long runs, Always makes a liner specifically for bladder leakage, make your own sports drink (my recipe is on this site), have a blast, and don't let anyone tell you you can't!

Re: Running a Half Marathon How awesome! I ran my first half marathon last October. This was pre "official" IC diagnosis, however, I had had a difficult delivery of my son about 6 months prior to the half marathon (he displaced my bladder and uterus due to an unexpected breech delivery) and was worried I wouldn't be able to do it. But I did it and felt so proud. I was super slow compared to my other two friends who ran with me so my advice would be just go at your own pace, have fun, and get lots of rest. Good luck to you!
signature: Diagnosed via cysto/hydro 2/6/2015 Symptoms: frequency, urgency. uretha discomfort, mild bladder pain (a raw sore type feeling.) CURRENT TREATMENTS: IC Diet (2/15) , Elmiron 100-300mg (3/15-5/16) insurance issues switched to PPS-DR (5/16-present) 2 150mg capsules daily (pentosan polysulfate sodium same formulation in Elmiron) DH Aloe 3-6 capsules daily (3/15), Schiff Digestive Advantage probiotics 1 pill daily (3/15), Amitriptyline 25mg- (5/15-8/16)attempting to wean down due to suspected weight gain side effect. Hydroxydine 25-50mg as needed at bedtime (5/15) Quercetin 2 pills daily to help immune system and body inflammation. Cystoprotek 4 capsules daily (8/15). Prelief as needed. USE DURING FLARES: Heating pad, ice packs for PFD, Vaginal Valium 10-20mg, Uribel, Pelvic floor stretches. PAST TREATMENTS: PFD physical therapy (6/15-10/15) & Counseling for stress mgt (9/15-12/15) both very helpful. GNC brand progesterone cream (not sure if this made a difference). Too many different diets and herbs to list. TRIGGERS: STRESS STRESS STRESS. BCP. Hormonal fluctuance (ovulation/period). Coffee, artificial preservatives and dyes.

contents: Re: Is Walking ok if you have PFD Does it help to make relaxing stretching exercises after walking? From Amy Stein book maybe?...

Re: Is Walking ok if you have PFD Walking helps loosen my muscles and I also stretch. My pt sometimes had me warm up on a treadmill after a PFD session, and then move on to squats and leg extensions for my back and hips. You will know if you are overdoing it by the tightening of the hip flexor in the front and that can in turn tighten the pelvic floor.

Re: Is Walking ok if you have PFD My symptoms started in June and I think I am just starting to see some decent improvement. I started PT last week for pelvic floor work. Prior to this flare I was very physically active. I ran 3-4x/week and did strength training 3x/week. I eventually backed off on this when it seemed I was just causing more pain. The first thing my PT told me last week was to get moving again. Her view is that we know that movement is good for the body and mind (it definitely is for me). I think I was afraid that if I tried to workout at all before I felt 100% better, I would ruin any progress I have made. She said a lot of my pain right now is b/c my pelvic floor is too tight b/c it senses pain and danger. Her recommendation is to start back slowly and maintain the same activity level for two weeks. I am supposed to watch for pain levels afterward. It's okay if I don't feel better, but I shouldn't feel worse. She also said that if I get 10 days in and all of a sudden I have a bad day, don't assume it's b/c of exercise. If it's been okay for 10 days, it is most likely not caused by that. I do have lots of exercises to do for stretching and working on opening my hips up more. I have a history of a disc herniation and hip pain, and the way I dealt with that was to focus on staying fit. I am SO happy to be able to move more again. I know each of us are individual. I would say that if you suspect that walking is making it worse, then cut back or take a day off and see how the symptoms are. The biggest thing she told me was that even though I feel pain, the movement is not injuring me. She believes that a part of pelvic floor issues is that the body becomes hyper aware of areas that have been through pain and we need to reassure the body that we aren't in danger. By doing this, we reduce the tension, which reduces pain and we help to reduce the pain cycle. Good luck!
Re: Is Walking ok if you have PFD Great post jstacey your pt sounds excellent. Thank you for all of that reassuring information. Always keep moving!

Warm up action begin gradually, complete a couple of warm up activities and stretches first, fabricate movement gradually begin with a 20 minute walk at that point increment steadily, utilize the right procedure stroll at a relentless pace, swing your arms unreservedly and remain as straight as possible My life is my message

contents: Hello All, My name is Nolan, I am a 23 year old man with diagnosed Pelvic Floor Dysfunction and likely IC. I have been struggling with chronic pelvic pain symptoms for 3 years now, though with the help of pelvic floor physical therapy (1-2 times per week) I have seen a tremendous overall improvement in my symptoms. Unfortunately though, my symptoms are still very bothersome on a daily basis. Over the past 3 years, largely due to the crippling depression I have suffered from, most especially at the beginning of this painful journey, I have gained a tremendous amount of weight. Food has always been my go to comfort, and unfortunately, it was my only comfort for the last few years. This brings me to my present issue/question. I am looking for a low impact exercise (in order to not irritate my Pelvic Floor Dysfunction), and swimming has been suggested to me. However, I have heard that swimming can prove very bothersome to many IC patients? I've read this is largely due to the chemicals in the pool. Would me being a man effect this? My layman's thought on this is that perhaps because such pool irritants have to travel further, perhaps I would be less effected? Would love to hear your thoughts, especially if you are an IC patient who swims! Thanks very much

Re: Swimming and IC unfortunately swimming pools can be an irritant for a lot of us, but i don't know re men...some people find putting vaseline on helps. i walk a lot, gentle walking...i aim for 10,000 steps a day and walk everywhere as i can't go to the gym for the moment. i find it relaxing (especially with a good podcast!) and it helps me stay fitter...can you walk even if you have to build it up? great that you are looking at ways to feel better in all ways, that is so important. i've had really bad anxiety over the last year but luckily and coming out of it...i know how hard it can all be!

Re: Swimming and IC I was wondering the same things about swimmimg pools bc we have a pool and have three little kids that love the pool. I have been scared to try and will try the vaseline. How long after going in the pool would I know if it bothered my IC? Would it be same day or day after? Just trying to know to make sure it's the pool and not something else. Thank you in advance

Re: Swimming and IC Originally posted by Melgator44 I was wondering the same things about swimmimg pools bc we have a pool and have three little kids that love the pool. I have been scared to try and will try the vaseline. How long after going in the pool would I know if it bothered my IC? Would it be same day or day after? Just trying to know to make sure it's the pool and not something else. Thank you in advance Usually it's the chlorine added to pools that's the problem. We have an above ground pool and use a generic for Baquacil instead of chlorine to keep it germ free. I can't swim in a chlorine pool, but do okay with Baquacil. I still have to get out of my wet swim suit as soon as I'm out of the pool, but I can swim with no problem. There's also a salt system available, but I think it's more expensive. It's possible to switch solutions in a pool. Since it's your own pool, you might want to check it out. Donna

contents: Re: Swimming and IC Thanks Donna. That is great to know because we actually have a salt water pool at our house which I have not tried to go in yet but will try. We have a family vacation in a few weeks at a resort that has a pool which I am sure is chlorine and I am nervous about. I have a 2 year old and a ten month old that don't know how to swim yet and my 4 year old can wear a life vest but it will be hard to stay out of the pool the entire weekend. I will try to take off my suit right after and hope for the best! If the chlorine bothers me will I know right away or would it affect me a couple days later? I was thinking maybe I could make it the two days and then deal with the discomfort when we get back.
Re: Swimming and IC What about Bromine, do you think that would work for me?
Re: Swimming and IC How do you use Vaseline, all over or just in one area? Sorry I don't quite understand how that would help.

Re: Swimming and IC Hi Dottie I am new to all this. I had researched swimming with IC bc I live in FL and it's a big part of my life and found some people had success with using vaseline. I asked my OB and he told me that it is an old wives tale but it won't hurt anything to try it to see if it helps me. So I went in a chlorine pool the other day and just put vaseline over my urethra opening and vaginal opening and tried to change clothes right after I was done. The next day I had some utethra burning and frequency but I am not sure if it was from swimming or something else so I am going to have to try it again.
Re: Swimming and IC Well Melgator let me know how it works out, because I am afraid to try it. I have a hot tub so I will just let the chlorine level drop get in and try to see if that helps

Re: Swimming and IC Originally posted by Dottie Marie Well Melgator let me know how it works out, because I am afraid to try it. contents: I agree with Donna just by chance my dr was talking about HCG with my husband the other day, himself our dr brought the subject up. So we have been reading up on it and I have alot of questions for my dr. now. From what I have been reading the HCG, may help me with my retention issues. The HCG not the diet, I read about one of the HCG diets, there are many out there, and there is way I would try the diet there is way to many foods on it I can not eat, so I am not considering that. I actually would love to try the diet part of it because with IC and trying so many med which help you gain weight and ic limiting my ability to exercise I would love to lose these extra pound on me but not at the expense of not following my IC diet, for me it is just not worth the chance of a flare. We are at the point of researching the HCG our dr. is willing to help us with this or I should say my husband, we did not discuss me using it at all, and I was not intereted in it until I read all of the things it can potentually help with not just weight loss. I am calling a dr this afternoon to discuss it avalability and which form is best to use I have read it comes in an injectable form, under tounge drops and a nasel spray. The injectable needs to be kept in the fridge. I will also be discussing the costs of this medication where I live it is not covered by any medical plans. I will say again I am just looking into the possiblities of the drug, I am no where near tring it. I have concerns it is a hormon and I don't do well on hormons. I am sure I am starting to go into meno mad my dr. wants me to try to get through it with out taking hormons because of all my problems with IC and the things that go with it. I still feel you need to dicsuss this with you dr first i would hate to see you try this and end up with a horable flare or making your ic symptoms worse. I also just feel this type of thing should not be taking lightly, they really don't know the long term effects of the drug. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Just to update you. I researched this quite a bit i would like to try it too, but my Dr. said he was okay with rxing it for my husband but not me I have been sick all summer with a kidney infection and he said if I can stay well for a month then he would let me try it, he thinks it could have quite a few bennifits to me but it may also make me sicker, so he wants me healthy before I strt. I won't hold my breath I have not gone a month with out a uti on a year. But i do respect his decission he has helpped me more than any other doc in my life. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Thanks everyone for all the info. A call to the doctor will definitely be made. I never considered not doing that I was just gauging if anyone had tried it. I would never do anything like this without calling him first All those who are calling doctors, let me know what you find out! Thanks!!!

Are you talking about Human Chorionic Gonadotrophin? The dr. I work for uses hcG to make our patients ovulate. This is a hormone I would not use to lose weight. How about just cutting down calories & exercising? Please be careful. Last edited by KarenAnne; 09-01-2010, 06:23 AM.

Yes it is a hormon for fertility, it has been around and used for weight loss since 1953. It has a good track record. I am not suggesting it for anyone, but it was something my Dr. suggested to my husband hence the research into the product. In canada you can't get it without a rx. The way I see it is if you make an informand decission, with a dr. and have monitering from a Dr. that is your choice. If you decided to watch what you eat and exercise that's your choice to do that too. At this point in time this is my choice is to watch what I eat, I would exercise too but most of the time I am not able too because of IC symptoms. But like thing else everyone is different and what is right for one may not be what is right for another. As I said before I am in no way, suggesting this is good for IC, In fact the orginal diet I don't think someone with IC could follow it. Also this is probably not the forum for this topic I am sure there is many other forums which directly address this program. Just my thoughts, not tring to tell anyone else what to do or not do. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: HCG diet I did multiple rounds of the diet using Homeopathic hcg* (not using the injections/real hcg). I plan do another round, probably after the holidays. I lost weight and it seemed to help with IC--probably bc of the dietary restrictions. Speaking of, it IS very limiting during certain phases of the diet, but those don't last long and if you're already limited you know how to deal with that. I really didn't have hunger--just had to get used to not running to kitchen every time to deal with boredom, emotions, pain...or saw a food commercial. And I wonder why I was overweight...! *This is not a medical endorsement. I don't have a medical degree or any intentions of trying out for a part on tv. I am not related to House. I did really like the show Miami Medical, but it wasn't renewed this season.

I have been on this diet for 35 days. Dr. supervised and i have done great, I have lost a considerable amount of weight with no hunger or hardship, I just didn't feel well the first few days of the diet, but I think I may have eaten some bad crab.. My husband is 2 weeks ahead of me on the this diet and I have nver seen him with out a beer belly, if he can do this iset anyon can. I have felt better on this diet thtan I have in years, I think it is the restricted amount of types of foods you can have on the diet. this tells me I am eating something bothering my Ic and i don't know about it, so next week when i get to go back to eating more food, anthing but sugar and carbs i will be adding things back in one at a time to see if I can pin point what it is. This diet is not for everyone and even thought you can order it on the net I would not adives it and you should do lots of reading about it first. I am not a big fan of Kevin Trudeu so i would not read his book on it, he adds in a lot of cleanses etc that you don't need. A friend did the drops but figures the alcohol in the drops did her bladder in. I repeat do not do this without a Dr. supervission and educate yourself before getting into it as it can muck up your system done wrong. But as a weight loss it works great. It is not the same as just watching you calories and exercise it targets different fats in didferent places I cn't explian it all this is why you have to read all about it. It targets your adipose fat only, not lean musecle mass of regular fat, it is that stubborn fat that will not leave the thighs or butt or belly. This is not for everyone bu to argue it is not the right diet for someone just means you don't understand the process. MG
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Liquid HCG This is an old thread, but Mothergoose, did the HCG work for you?? I did the diet several years ago with my mother and we both have IC. Both of us had a reduction in IC symptoms as well as other autoimmune diseases that we have. I did not inform my urologist that I was taking HCG because they were drops off of the internet and I did not make the connection between the HCG and my IC going into remission until recently. Dr Reo of the Florida International University published his research in March of 2016 and in November of 2016 the National Institute of Heath US National Library of Medicine published an article recommending HCG over Elmiron. I believe the HCG stops your body from attacking itself, and in the case of IC.. the bladder. It is supposed to work wonders for other autoimmune diseases and my Mother noticed that her Rheumatoid Arthritis and Sarcoidosis went into remission when on HCG as well as her IC. I would certainly be happy to post links to the research and articles if anyone was interested.

Re: Liquid HCG Yes it worked well for me. I had no problems with my bladder at all. I am not too sure if it is the actual HCG that helps or if it is the diet and the limited amount of good and choices. I would not be afraid to use it as a weight loss program. I have not read the literature about HCG and IC. I don't think you could just take the HCG and not do the diet, from what I have read it can muck up your metabolism.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Liquid HCG Thank you for your reply. I have tried to do the diet without the drops and have not found relief for my IC. Most of the research is within the past few years, but here is the article from Dr. Reo in favor of using HCG to suppress your immune system and keep it from attacking your bladder. http://atlasofscience.org/potential-...-gonadotropin/ I am trying to get a doctor to approve and monitor the HCg this time so that I can be sure that it is working.

Re: Liquid HCG Originally posted by HappySnapper Thank you for your reply. I have tried to do the diet without the drops and have not found relief for my IC. Most of the research is within the past few years, but here is the article from Dr. Reo in favor of using HCG to suppress your immune system and keep it from attacking your bladder. http://atlasofscience.org/potential-...-gonadotropin/ I am trying to get a doctor to approve and monitor the HCg this time so that I can be sure that it is working. AN UPDATE Hcg WORKS FOR ME! My Mom and I talked our Urologist into recommending Hcg after we showed him the research by Dr. Rao. Both of us achieved complete remission of our IC symptoms and the urologist told us to continue the Hcg injections as needed. The biggest problem has been finding somewhere to get the Hcg. We went to a local medical weight loss clinic, but there are places that you can get it online as some others in an IC facebook group I am in have done. The actual Hcg isn't expensive, but the consultations and weigh-ins at the weight loss clinic are pricy! This needs to make it to clinical trials so that insurance will cover it and Urologists can prescribe it. It feels so good to be pain free! Mom and I went to the University of Utah with hopes of getting some interest in doing a clinical trial, but they may not have any interest in that. If you have tried Hcg OR have had remission when pregnant PLEASE contact Dr. Rao and tell him your story. He is planning on re-publishing his work with real human examples in order to stimulate interest and possibly funding for trials. [email protected]
Re: Liquid HCG Thanks for the update! Does this doctor think the diet is necessary, or is it the HCG alone that is therapeutic?
signature: Diagnosed October 2012. Have never identified specific foods that trigger symptoms, but stress and hormone fluctuations are big triggers. I avoid foods/substances that are common triggers for other people. Have tried without long-term success: Cystoprotek (for 2+ years) Marshmallow root (for 1+ years) Desert Harvest aloe vera (for 1+ years) Corn silk D-Mannose Imipramine (Tofranil) Office instills (no relief from these, even temporary) Acupuncture Chinese herb blends Gluten-free diet (no improvement in anything, not even energy level) Also have: GERD and migraines.

contents: Re: Liquid HCG Originally posted by Owlie447 Thanks for the update! Does this doctor think the diet is necessary, or is it the HCG alone that is therapeutic? The 500 calorie diet is NOT recommended by my doctor, even though the weight loss place stressed that I wouldn't lose weight if I didn't stick their strict Simeons protocol. I lost 15 lbs. Just eat sensibly if you are trying to lose weight, more proteins and whole foods, Less sugar and processed foods. I will be doing another round in a few weeks. At the end of the first round I was able to eat tomatoes, grapefruit and other things that usually bothered me before.

Re: Liquid HCG I would also like to add that I tried EVERY supplement out there that claimed to help IC with little to no success. For me the IC was only healed when I did Hcg. I have found a new doctor that does hormone replacement that will prescribe it for me for the IC and then he is working to get my hormones back to normal.

Re: Liquid HCG The HCG diet has been popular for many years. It's an extreme diet, claimed to cause fast weight loss of up to 1-2 pounds per day. However, it is not recommended. The HCG diet limits caloric intake to around 500 calories per day for weeks at a time, making it an extremely weight loss diet. Therefore after taking proper knowledge and precautions you can start this diet otherwise you can stick to natural weight loss methods. Last edited by ICNDonna; 08-25-2017, 04:38 PM.

Re: Liquid HCG [QUOTE=JasonSharpe;704285]The HCG diet has been popular for many years. It's an extreme diet, claimed to cause fast weight loss of up to 1-2 pounds per day. However, it is not recommended. The HCG diet limits caloric intake to around 500 calories per day for weeks at a time, making it an extremely weight loss diet. Therefore after taking proper knowledge and precautions you can start this diet otherwise you can stick to natural weight loss methods. As I said earlier in this thread, My doctor did not recommend the 500 calorie diet, though I did cut out sugar and ate sensibly as he recommended. The Hcg is what put my IC in remission, not the diet. I did, however lose weight at a slower pace, but I feel like a normal person again. A half a dozen people in a facebook group decided to try it and out of them 4 went into remission and 2 did not. The 2 that it did not help had hunnars. I am working with my doctor that is now willing to prescribe Hcg as needed for my IC. Hormone Used to Treat Infertility May Be a Potential Treatment Option for IC/BPS | Interstitial Cystitis Association https://www.ichelp.org/hormone-used-to-treat-infertility-may-be-a-potential-treatment-option-for-icbps/ Last edited by ICNDonna; 08-25-2017, 04:38 PM.

contents: Hello everyone, I have recently started going to the gym after a few years hiatus due to the IC, and I can't drink the amount of water I need to make it through a workout without causing a mini flare. I will drink a bottle of water, maybe one and a half, during and after my workout, and then spend the next couple of hours peeing every 15 minutes. I tried working out and drinking way less and didn't flare, but it made me nauseous because I was dehydrated, and I would rather not do that again. I also tried drinking higher pH water and it didn't change anything, I just can't seem to drink more than a few sips a time without my bladder revolting If anyone has suggestions or ideas that have worked for them for how to stay hydrated without the flareups that would be lovely. Thanks!

contents: Re: Exercise and hydration without flaring Yeah Donna is right... small sips throughout the workout is best.. and this way you don't get dehydrated nor over fill your bladder. I wouldn't do the alkaline water... I don't see that helping with workouts at all. Rather, though, you have to consider that your pelvic floor muscles might also be getting a bit traumatized by your workout. You want to avoid sitting on a bicycle, spinning or stair master and focus, instead, on exercises that keep your hips level. Have you read our exercise section? Exercise and Fitness For IC and Pelvic Pain https://www.ic-network.com/interstitial-cystitis-resources/exercise-fitness-ic-and-pelvic-pain/ Exercise and Fitness for IC and Chronic Pelvic Pain Jill Osborne MA - ICN Founder The question that many pelvic pain patients face when contemplating exercise is "will it help or hurt?" The evidence is overwhelming in favor of the former. Exercise can lift our spirits, reduce depression

contents: Re: Aqua Zumba/ Aerobics So, I’m back from vacation—wherein I spent almost two full days in a spring fed river at a state park, and then almost one full day in a chlorinated lazy river at a resort. I know that’s not the same as Aqua Zumba, but I wanted to report that my bladder did fine!! I only had one night where my urethra “twinged” a tiny bit. A caveat—I will say I did this with my bladder calm...I don’t know that I would have participated in the water if my bladder was already irritated to begin with. Also, that lazy river was chlorinated, but it didn’t smell to high heavens of chlorine the way that some pools do. Those are two things to take into consideration. All that said, and I think I’m going to give Aqua Zumba a go in the coming weeks. I’ll report back. I would LOVE to find something fitness wise other than walking or stretches, which is what I stick with. I’m also toying with the idea of trying piyo. It has the stretches that are “yoga like” abs the movement that’s like Pilates...but didn’t look high impact...

Ok, just when I thought I had all my exercise figured out and I knew what was safe and what wasn't. I know that I can't jump up and down, can't run, and can't do abdominal exercises. I know what I can do. I can do the elliptical, I can walk, I can do yoga, I can do arm weights. So I was just getting over a flare that I wasn't sure was caused by bananas or by doing an abdominal workout. I'm leaning towards the abdominal workout, but I'm still not 100% sure. So for the last two days I added in deadlifts with 5-pound weights, good mornings, and lunges with 5-pound weights. This morning, I noticed my bladder was starting to feel pressure and sure enough, I feel like I'm in a flare again. Does anyone here know if doing squats, good mornings, and lunges are bad for the pelvic floor? I'm beginning to think that that's what's causing the discomfort. I don't have pain, but I do have a lot of pressure and feel like I have to use the bathroom a lot. Thanks for any help you can provide! :-)
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Are squats, dead lifts and good mornings a no-no with ic? I think those would bother me too. Everything you listed that bothers you would make me flare as well. Squats without weights are ok for me. Lunges not good for knees so I stay away; but they also stress the hip flexor so I’d steer clear of those. The pudendal nerve runs through your obturator. The other day I did a huge pelvic tilt while laying on my back (don’t ask why) and I peed all night every hour and it was nothing I ate. Oh and my sacrum got aggravated by laying on the floor and that contributed to the flare. I don’t know what good morning’s are Dead lifts seem like too much pressure.

Re: Are squats, dead lifts and good mornings a no-no with ic? Originally posted by my2boys99 Ok, just when I thought I had all my exercise figured out and I knew what was safe and what wasn't. I know that I can't jump up and down, can't run, and can't do abdominal exercises. I know what I can do. I can do the elliptical, I can walk, I can do yoga, I can do arm weights. So I was just getting over a flare that I wasn't sure was caused by bananas or by doing an abdominal workout. I'm leaning towards the abdominal workout, but I'm still not 100% sure. So for the last two days I added in deadlifts with 5-pound weights, good mornings, and lunges with 5-pound weights. This morning, I noticed my bladder was starting to feel pressure and sure enough, I feel like I'm in a flare again. Does anyone here know if doing squats, good mornings, and lunges are bad for the pelvic floor? I'm beginning to think that that's what's causing the discomfort. I don't have pain, but I do have a lot of pressure and feel like I have to use the bathroom a lot. Thanks for any help you can provide! :-) Boy, you and I are really in the same boat!!! I am usually ok with an elliptical but my gym just got all new ones and they seem to be more “step” oriented than the smooth rhythms the old ones used to be like. I have one at home but it seems to not be working properly either, ugh! I did my usual 8lb arm free weights, and that didn’t seem to bother me. I don’t do lunges because of my knees, but that could be irritating with weights. My bladder was really bothering me today and I don’t know if it was the new elliptical or maybe the quercitin I started taking. This disease is utterly frustrating! If I can’t get a decent work out I will go crazy!!!

Re: Are squats, dead lifts and good mornings a no-no with ic? Squats are ok with me but deadlifts and good mornings are a definite no no. I feel your frustration, I love to exercise and this dumb disease really limits what you can do

Re: Are squats, dead lifts and good mornings a no-no with ic? Yeah, I did some research yesterday and apparently dead lifts and good mornings really affect the glutes, which in turn, affect the pelvic floor. So good bye to them. Squats and lunges can also affect the glutes if done too deeply, so it is recommended that to do mini ones. I guess that's better than nothing. Songbird, good mornings are when you stand, hips a bit further apart. You have some sort of weight behind your head and you gently and slowly hinge forward at the waist, then back up. I have always loved them for hammies and glutes until IC came long. Melinda, I feel your frustration. I feel so much better when I'm working out, as long as I don't irritate my bladder. It could be the eliptical at the gym is stepping too much. Do they have another kind there to try? Stancie, sounds like we can't do the same things. Just trying to figure out how to strengthen my lower body without affecting my bladder. It's proving difficult!
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Are squats, dead lifts and good mornings a no-no with ic? I can't tolerate any exercise that puts any strain on my abdominal muscles. Donna

contents: Re: Are squats, dead lifts and good mornings a no-no with ic? I am 100% on the same page as Donna. No core work for me anymore.

Re: Are squats, dead lifts and good mornings a no-no with ic? For lower strengthening I do squats without weights and shallow lunges. As far as machines I can do quad sets and hamstring curls up to like 80 lbs , I can't go higher than that because I will flare. I have the strength to do up to 100 lbs but if I do my bladder doesn't like it. I guess when you lift heavy weights you recruit accessory muscles and the pelvic floor is part of that.

I've doing super well with my IC. As long as I'm careful with diet (although not as strict as before) and take my meds, I lead a pretty normal life. I even sleep through the night or maybe get up once to pee max. Well since I've been doing great I decided to go back to the gym and do some zumba. I was careful though and did not do any jumping just step side to side during the jumping things. I was so happy because I had missed it so much and did pretty good for a week or so. I then started feeling some pain and attributed it to some new bread I was trying. I stopped the bread but I kept getting worse. Good thing it was bit by bit and I don't think I made it to a full flare. Well I reluctantly stopped working out and voila I'm perfectly ok again. I'm very sad because I hate doing machines, it's so boring to me and I've put on the pounds. I also love doing group exercise because I'm a pure extrovert and get a lot of energy from a group of people. I can do some yoga classes but it's not really my favorite thing to do. I'm pretty much restricted to doing the treadmill, recumbent bike, row machine and some weights for upper body. Boring............ Well I guess this post is just for venting. I was in remission for many years in which I was able to do all sorts of exercises (even bootcamp). I'm venting to you guys because my family doesn't understand why I'm so upset about this. I just love working out and feeling that high. Thanks for reading and I hate this disease

Re: Recent flare from exercise Same here. Love be exercise before but have a bad hip.any lateral movement of my leg or outward turn will tweak it and I’ll flare. I’m on a horrible one now and too, I thought it was something I ate but I’m really strict with my diet. Now I have nerve pain running down the adductor, flaring red burning pain etc. what is your pain like? I was up to a good 10,000 steps a day too. I feel your frustration and pain. So sorry Stanncie.

Re: Recent flare from exercise I have managed to return to cycling on a recumbent bicycle. However all my bikey girlfriend ride ordinary bikes. We all ride and chat,but this doesn't work well with my recumbent...it is heavy,slower and very low to the ground,so I pretty much just ride by myself,so much not as fun.I took a circuit training class the day after a rescue instill(I felt so good) (before the class). Such a flare after-. So I too am working out on my own.I am able to hike fairly quickly,but today I apparently crossed the line-as far as I can tell this flare is pelvic floor. Oh well. I too love being able to come here and find kinship with others who are in the same boat and "get it" the way nobody else can.

Re: Recent flare from exercise Thanks for your understanding girls. It sucks right? I think I even cope better with the diet restrictions than the exercise ones. Songbird my pain is burning in the pubic area with some back pain. It's funny because I don't get crazy frequency from an exercise flare. I do get an increase but not nearly as bad as with a food flare. I've learned to distinguish them pretty well and I should've known it was from the exercise but I just wanted so badly to return to my exercises. Ah well back to some boring machines for me

Re: Recent flare from exercise I get crazy frequency. It’s so bad that I always go to the local clinic and have a full pelvic workip with ultrasound, urinalysis and vaginal swabs, which all turn out to be negative. Only then do I deduce that it’s nerve and muscle related. It sucks pretty badly. Ps. I can’t have sex either which is worse than the diet restrictions or the exercise for me.
Does any one with IC do crossfit?

Re: Crossfit Originally posted by Hkkirtley Does any one with IC do crossfit? I haven't tried it, but I did a brief search and it looks too intense for me. I can't do anything that puts strain on my abdominal muscles or exercises that are "jarring," such as jogging or running. Donna
