contents: With the weather turning springlike, I've been wanting to ride my bike. I haven't done this since I got IC and in fact, I'll have to get new inner tubes, tires & a new seat due to it sitting out on my balcony & not being used for so long. It's got a tiny bit of rust here & there too, but I think overall, with a cleanup, tuneup & tires etc it should be fine. But, of course, I've been wondering if it's worth repairing, since I don't really know if my IC will allow it. I did find this link to more IC friendly bike seats... (You have to scroll down the page to get to the bike seats) https://www.ichelp.com/TreatmentAndSelfHelp/DailyLivingWithICOTCProducts.html Anyone tried these? Anyone ok with biking?
signature: Kadi ------------------------------------------------------------- I am not a medical authority nor do I offer medical advice. In all cases, I strongly encourage you to discuss your medical treatment with your personal medical care provider. Only they can, and should, give medical recommendations to you. ------------------------------------------------------ New favorite quote: "God gives us only what we can handle. Apparently God thinks I'm a bad-ass" ~Author Unknown Source - Pinterest " Current treatments: -IC diet -Elavil 50mg at night -Continuous use birth control pills (4-5 periods/year) -Heparin/Marcaine/Sodium Bicarb home instills at night 3-4x per week, more often if needed -Pyridium if needed, -Pain medicine at bedtime daily, as needed during the day several times per week -Antibiotic when doing an instillation to prevent UTI -Colace & SmartFiber to treat chronic constipation from meds, Fleet enema as needed -Dye Free Benadryl 50 mg at bedtime -"Your Pace Yoga: Relieving Pelvic Pain" dvd, walking, treadmill at gym -Managing stress= VERY important! -Fur therapy: Hugging the cat!

contents: well to be honest with you... when I didn't have a car, I needed to go a few places and took the bike.. it wasn't ic friendly and it sure was not pfd friendly.. but you very well may have a better experience.. I have IC, fibro, myofascial pain syndrom, pfd, arthrititis(who don't) sacralititis, and 2 protruding disks....... good luck Brat
signature: 'The will of God will never take you where the Grace of God will not protect you.'

contents: Kadi, I just checked out the seat links- wow, my dream seat, just the thought of biking makes me cringe...I actually stopped years ago as it aggravated a back injury. I think you should get a cool and comfy seat and plunk it on a new bike....anyone who can spend 5 days a week educating our kids, deserves a reliable bike for fun! If you aren't sure on a new one, try one of the shops that resells sporting goods, etc. We have one here and the stuff they get is practically new...you might find a newer one and add the seat.
signature: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~"No one can make you feel inferior without your consent." Eleanor Roosevelt, This Is My Story, 1937

contents: I have a soft gel seat on my bike, and have no problem with it. However, the ones in the gym drive me up the wall within a couple of minutes. So.. it's a trial and error thing for me, but yes, I can ride (and I do ride on a regular basis!)

I wear padded shorts, smear on lots of "Butt cream" (don't remember the exact name) and tend to sit back on my hips rather than putting pressure on more sensitive areas. When I'm on my bike is the only time of the day I feel normal.

I miss bike riding myself. I have a 300 dollar exercise bike cant use. And I have a real nice mountain bike which cost about the same. It reaks havoc with my pelvic area. When I did ride I dont reccommend hills.I also looked into the seats but never got one. I love my bike and it just collects dust.Its all trial and error for each of us.-jojo

I tried this last week and was in pain for 2 days! I have a really nice gel seat. so I thought it would be ok, but I was wrong. I'll have to look at the link to the other seats and possibly try it again for my son's sake. I also have PFD and Urethral Instability, so it may just be the combination of everything that isn't working. good luck, KK
signature: Hopekeepers Support Group Leader Diagnosed IC 1/07/04 Fibromyalgia, IBS, PFD, Urethral Instability, GERD, Chronic Unexplained Pelvic Pain for most of my life Remember, all is not lost as long as we keep our sense of humor!

contents: Two years ago my family bought me a really nice mountain bike.......shocks on each tire and the seat..........gel seat...........and I topped it with another gel seat............I can ride it as long as I feel good otherwise........I LOVED getting back on a bike again......... (I rode it this morning after my shower, since it was already 85 degrees and a hair dryer did NOT sound like a good idea! LOL...........felt like a kid!)

I would love to be able to go cycling, since my boyfriend gets on his road bike several days a week, but I've been concerned that my VV will act up if I do -- even with a gel seat. It's been a while since I've been on a bike -- not sure if I can get up those hills anyway!

contents: I occcasionally ride my bike, I really miss it. I bought a seat, that finally seems to work, at least when I'm not flaring or feeling miserable. Its a "Terry Butterfly" seat made for women, has an opening in the middle, to take the pressure off some nerve endings. I just love it. It's not cheap! I got it on sale for $69, but sometimes you just have to ride your bike, and a little exercise with the right seat does wonders. Bianchi

Re: Bicycle seats? Anyone tried these? I am looking at the Terry Butterfly or the Spongy Wonder. Each quite different. That spongy wonder looks tempting. I have a Cannondale road bike and we ride 23 mile rides all summer. Any more recommendations?

contents: Re: Bicycle seats? Anyone tried these? See the 4/7/17 thread "Bicycle Seats" - I posted on it with a link to an article by Jim Langely about bicycle seats which is really comprehensive. Jill Osborne says some women she has consulted with have had good results with the seat that is 2 half seats that move independently. So far, I have done very well with my Lightening recumbent (I'm slow as can be,but that's a work in progress). And I'm seeing my uro in August & getting a referral for pelvic floor PT and give my road bike a try again. FYI,my pelvic floor PT advises just riding 5 minutes at a time to begin with,then inching the time up by 5 minutes every week. (But that was just for my pelvic floor issues). Let us know what you end up and how it is going! Have fun! shorty

This past Monday I started doing some light exercises. I walk back & forth through my house 2 times a day, for 10 minutes. I exercise my arms & legs while sitting on the couch, for 5-10 minutes. Each separated by a hour. I have had no IC symptoms since Sunday, except for yesterday evening. I had slight pelvic pain & perineum pressure. I think that may have been caused by the sour cream I put on my baked potato at supper last night. I haven't eaten any sour cream since being diagnosed. Also, I was "bad" yesterday & this morning! I ate 9 hershey's kisses hugs, 2 devil creme cakes, 3 hershey's kisses, & 2 assorted chocolates (strawberry cream & orange cream covered in milk chocolate). My monthly is due some time between tomorrow & next Tuesday. I have always craved chocolate the week before my monthly, like something awful. To the reason I'm writing this post. I just did my leg exercises, I had already walked 10 minutes & did my arm exercises, now I'm having severe pelvic pain!! So, how do I know what caused the sudden onset of pain? Is it the exercise, the chocolate, or my monthly coming? I had been in constant pain since the end of January! I started Elmiron (100 mg 3 times a day) about 2 weeks ago. so when I, finally had a pain free day Sunday, then again Monday, I was thinking that the Elmiron was working. When I say constant pain I mean, I was in bed, curled up in a ball sobbing day & night! So, any advice would be great! I'm in severe pain currently! I have done almost all my flare treatments. I take Hydroxyzine every night, Naproxen 550 mg, Acetaminophen 1000 mg, Cyclobenzarine 10 mg, a heating pad on my lower stomach, pelvic area & the Elmiron. I haven't taken the Acetaminophen. If it doesn't ease up by the time my husband gets home from work, I may have to go to the Doctor's office for a rescue treatment. I don't have insurance, so I only go for rescue treatments when I can't take the pain any more!

Re: In need of advice ASAP!! It's possible that it's all three causing your flare. It's pretty quick for elmiron to be working --- it can take six months or longer to become effective. It does sound like you ingested quite a bit of chocolate, which is a problem for many of us. There's also the possibility that it's related to the menstrual cycle's hormone changes. You might want to try some baking soda in water (or Tums) to see if that helps calm it down. And you might want to slow down on the exercise routine for a day or two until your flare slows down. I hope you feel better quickly. Donna

contents: Re: Bike seats This link,from the super interesting website at "pelvic pain rehab.com", http://www.jimlangley.net/crank/bicycleseats.html I am an avid cyclist,and had to give up my upright bike.I bought a recumbent bike and it does not flare me at all-yay! contents: I have been walking and would like to start doing leg and ab work outs at home . Since I have been diagnosed with IC almost a year ago I have not done any exercises besides walking. If someone knows of any workout that won't cause discomfort or a flare please let me know. I know that a lot of the legs and ab workouts do but I am afraid to try them again. Thanks=)

Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? Hi Dara30. I would only recommend soft gentle movements when exercising (i.e. walking, swimming). Nothing that jars or shakes your pelvis or bladder. I can do some Pilates type movements for my legs sometimes but I'd start really slow with that and see how your body reacts. The only ab exercise I've been able to do since I got IC is one my physical therapist taught me. You lie on your back with your knees bent, and you straighten one leg slowly while pressing your foot flat on the floor (kind of like slide it across the floor), then you slowly slide the foot back up toward your rear as far as you can go. Then switch back and forth between legs. As you slide your foot back up and bend your leg it's almost like a reverse crutch in the low abdominals. It sounds simple but it really does work the abs and I find it doesn't cause me any problems afterward. Any other ab exercise is a big no, no for me! Wouldn't even think of doing it. Take care! Kathy
signature: Diagnosed: IBS-D: 1997 Premature Ovarian Failure: 2008 Completely Menopausal: 2014 (age 39) IC: 2015 Treatments: Colestipol HCL: amazing for IBS-D Minivelle: estrogen patch Prometrium: oral progesterone to start period Tramadol: 1/2-50 mg. as needed for pain Xanax: 2.5 mg. as needed for anxiety Pelvic Floor Physical Therapy & Massage: Yay! 5% Lidocaine: for urethral & vaginal pain Ice packs: helps soooo much! Stopped letting doctors mess around with my bladder! No more invasive procedures for me...only made things worse! This is all definitely hormone related...bladder discomfort and pain is very minimal now!

contents: Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? Hi Dara, I can do most exercises fine, but there are some no nos for me. I don't do ab work as a rule, I can't run ( which is good because it's my most hated cardio), and any abductor exercise I leave well alone. I can do weights, most cardio activities including jumping if it's not too much, and my favourite is kick boxing. We are all different though so for you it will just be a trial and error

contents: Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? Is the ab exercise your talking about kind of a like your riding a bike. It works your abs

Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? I have to stick with what I call "smooth" exercises --- nothing that's jarring or that puts strain on the abdominal muscles. We are all so very different --- what I suggest is easing into an exercise program slowly and if you try one that's too much, back up and try something a little different. Walking is what saves me. In summer I do some swimming and pool walking. Donna

contents: Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? No I don't do any ab exercises on a bike, but that's something you have reminded me of. I don't go on a bike!!!!!!! The seat pressure is aggravating. We have these seated bikes that I use though:-)
signature: UTI 3/2015 IC ,PFD prescribed meds: Endep 60mg, Elmiron 400mg, BC. Enablex 7.5mg. Supplements: Chitomur peptide, colostrum, Echinacea, liver detox swisse, calcium citrate, glucosamine, Psyllium Husk. Daily diet: Breakkie- oats with chia seeds and no fat milk. Post workout-turkey breast with mixed veggies. Lunch-raw green soup with rye roll and handful of peanuts/almonds. Dinner- changes but meat and veg. Dessert-banana and sugar free custard (homemade) or banana and apple bake with almonds cinnamon and honey with a dollop of coconut cream. Tried: tramadol, cystoprotek, multi right, diazepam, vesicare, Ditropan, mirabegron, Flomax, lyrica, progesterone.

contents: Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? Originally posted by Dara30 Is the ab exercise your talking about kind of a like your riding a bike. It works your abs Hi Dara, I believe you were asking about the exercise I mentioned? You do lie on your back like you are riding an air bike, but you don't raise your legs into the air. Your feet stay flat and never leave the floor and you just slide them out and in alternating each leg. Similar motion to an air bike but doesn't put a lot of strain on the abs. You could give it a try and see how you feel afterwards, but I would just do a few and then build up. Doesn't cause me any problems and as I said it was recommended by my physical therapist. Any other ab exercise for me is out. Hope this helps. Take care! Kathy
signature: Diagnosed: IBS-D: 1997 Premature Ovarian Failure: 2008 Completely Menopausal: 2014 (age 39) IC: 2015 Treatments: Colestipol HCL: amazing for IBS-D Minivelle: estrogen patch Prometrium: oral progesterone to start period Tramadol: 1/2-50 mg. as needed for pain Xanax: 2.5 mg. as needed for anxiety Pelvic Floor Physical Therapy & Massage: Yay! 5% Lidocaine: for urethral & vaginal pain Ice packs: helps soooo much! Stopped letting doctors mess around with my bladder! No more invasive procedures for me...only made things worse! This is all definitely hormone related...bladder discomfort and pain is very minimal now!

contents: Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? Thank you I think I know the one that you are talking about. Any leg exercises that you have found to work?

Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? I can do recumbent bike, eliptical, lift weights, and walk. Haven't found an ab exercise yet that doesn't bother me...although Kapbow, I might try that one you mentioned. Can't jump or jog or do anything that jars my bladder. I probably could when I'm in remission, but I'm too afraid it will take me out of remission!
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? I have always been biking in town, although in worse days I most likely should not have.. I was anyway always in flare, so there was not big difference. But I tried some semester to do gymnastic training and finally gave up, while I had always bladder cramps after. I do not like sport anyway. But walking has always been ok.

Re: What exercises won't cause a flare? I can do a number of exercises without causing pain: I walk, ride a stationary bike, and can do kickboxing. I also do P90X workouts and T25 workouts (on DVD) but I modify all of the exercises that require jumping. I stopped doing all ab exercises. Mostly you just have to listen to your body. When my bladder starts to feel irritated, I skip a day of exercise, so as to not irritate it further. Whatever you try, I'd suggest starting slowly and maybe just a few minutes at first to make sure that you can tolerate it. Then you can increase your time if you feel good.

Hi everyone. Does anyone have flares from CrossFit or other high intensity exercise? Running, jumping, lot's of ab work. I've been a trainer for eight years and have given it up. Seems I can eat or drink anything, but as soon as I go hard at it again at the gym, boom. I also have a neurogenic bladder. Been through PT for that.

Re: Anyone have problems with CrossFit or other high intensity exercise? You are not alone.Lots of us here have modified our exercise activities.Walking,hiking weight lifting are fine for me.But vigorous circuit training class with cardio between sets was horrible.
Re: Anyone have problems with CrossFit or other high intensity exercise? Thanks. Any chance it's not dietary related at all, or that exercise initiates it and then diet may exasperate it?

Re: Anyone have problems with CrossFit or other high intensity exercise? Well, not following the diet strictly will always flare me. But when I've flared from exercise,I could eat the best IC diet ever and still be miserable.On the bright side,having been on the IC diet for 8 months and almost being done with a series of pelvic floor PT I am much much better. Brfore I got truly strict with the IC diet,just walking a mile would send me into an awful flare. Now I am doing fast hill climbing 10 mile hikes. The pelvic floor PT has made my pain less in almost every moment of my day...if you have an opportunity to have an evaluation & treatment for pelvic floor dysfunction,I would recommend it.I suspect that many athletic individuals have a high pain tolerance - I had no idea that parts of my pelvic floor were clenched & painful until I had a few sessions of pelvic floor PT. There are other threads here regarding exercise and flares. Everyone has different things they can or cannot do.I imagine this must be devastating for you. You can find a way to deal with what your bladder needs,it takes time.hugs,shorty

Re: Anyone have problems with CrossFit or other high intensity exercise? I would encourage you to try following the diet very strictly for several weeks,then see if you can tolerate exercise better.At least you will know you have followed through with that possibility.

Re: Anyone have problems with CrossFit or other high intensity exercise? I have had IC for a while now and have tried many different forms of exercise. Prior to having IC, I was big into cardio/bootcamp exercising. That has come to a screeching halt with IC. I have found that I can do the recumbent bike (even during flares), all arm/shoulder/chest weights, eliptical (most of the time), and walking. Every single time I have tried any kind of jumping, I flare. I also have not had success finding an ab/core workout that doesn't flare me. This has been one of the hardest challenges for me with IC. Exercise makes me feel better...I have just had to modify. I am 45 years old now and I don't want to lose my strength any more than I already have.
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Anyone have problems with CrossFit or other high intensity exercise? I have to stick with smooth exercises. Anything that jars my body is a problem for me. Donna

contents: Hi, I'm new to IC. I was just diagnosed a month or so ago. Apparently I have it very mild and I manage my discomfort well. I'm frustrated though because I was on my way to being healthier (exercise and healthy foods) and since my diagnosis, everyone says do yoga. I'm sure that's great for some, but I see zero results doing it. I would like to get back into exercise like I used to (ex. Kettlebells) but I'm not sure it's a good idea. So frustrating!!!!! Any advice from anyone? I've put on weight I want to get rid of but I don't think yoga will help....

Re: Kettlebells? I've had to give up cycling and I decided that the rowing machine at the gym is not my bladders friend either. When I was poorly controlled,I still managed to walk.I eventually got better control of my IC and really got into hiking(too muddy for that now though) so I'm just doing long walks. I can do most weight training but try to not overdo it. I've found in the past that keeping strong muscles with weight training really does kick up my metabolism & makes losing those pesky excess pounds easier.Flinging a kettle bell would probably be bad for me,but I hold them for squats & split squats. I do medicine ball slams on the floor-don't break your nose! Push ups,machine assisted pull ups,side planks. Circuit training that includes activities like running,jumping & pushing a weighted "sled" sets my bladder off to a regrettable flare.Walking on the treadmill works fine.Some ICers can jog/run,but not all. I think swimming would be good as long as the pool wasn't super chlorinated,and for me,aqua aerobics would be fine too.My pelvic floor pt has me do child's pose & breathe deep into my pelvic floor,also cat/cow with deep breath into my pelvic floor and spreading my hips in the cow part. Last edited by icshorty; 01-08-2017, 03:55 PM.

Re: Kettlebells? Hi K I'm a gym goer and do bodybuilding so am well verse with just about everything that you can get regarding the gym. I do most things including kettlebells. I find jogging and running to be a problem and I also find sit ups and lower leg raises can cause me issues. All the core exercises you may want to take pretty slowly and minimalisticly. You van get a trainer to help you train your core without including your lower abdominals which are my bug bear. Also remember to STRETCH your pelvic floor , lower back and adductors out everyday as to not get too tight in those areas if you're lifting weights. For cardio I stick to walking, elliptical trainers, rowers, and kick boxing.

contents: Re: Kettlebells? I second what most people say here, unfortunately any high impact exercise will send me to a flare. I used to do zumba, pilates, spinning, boot camp classes etc when I was in remission but no longer. Pretty much the only thing I can do is walking and yoga. I also have put on pounds especially since I'm back on the Amitriptyline. It is one thing that does frustrate me a lot because I enjoyed doing all those exercises and like you said Yoga is great but not great at helping you lose weight. At this point as long as I don't get past a size 8 I'm ok with it. It's better than feeling lousy, getting up a zillion times a night and being a walking zombie all day. My pain level no longer goes above a 2, my frequency is normal now and some nights I don't even get up to pee but I have to be careful with diet and what type of exercise I do. It is what it is

Re: Kettlebells? I love exercising but have had to drastically change my routine due to IC. As it's part of my job (I'm military), that's posed a bit of a challenge, but I get by. High impact exercise and sit-ups/heavy core work trigger flares for me. So I do alternative core work; a lot of pilates-style stuff and plenty of stretching! I have to do sit-ups every once in a while, but I've learned ways to relax the muscles afterwards and warm them up to prepare without too much pain afterwards. I also do pelvic physical therapy and I'm on muscle relaxers which probably don't hurt. I make sure to do yoga or some sort of stretching work at least once a week to give my body a chance to bounce back from a week of workouts. I love swimming and do quite a bit of hiking when my body allows and enjoy plenty of weight training. I can't bike really anymore, but there are plenty of other fun exercise options out there! As far as kettlebells go, I'd stick with lighter weights and make sure to listen to your body so you know what you can and can't handle. And make sure to stretch and relax those muscles after any workout you do!
signature: Diagnosed with severe IC and Endometriosis in 2016 Medications: Elmiron, Elavil, Myrbetriq, Uribel, Valium, Baclofen, Percocet Surgeries: Laparoscopy, Hydrodistention (2) Procedures: Regular bladder instillations Therapies: Pelvic Floor Physical Therapy 2x/week, homeopathic home-care, yoga and massage Helpful Links: Patient Help: http://www.ic-network.com/patientlinks.html IC Diet: http://www.ic-network.com/patient-re...-introduction/

contents: Re: Kettlebells? I pretty much have to stick with what I call "smooth" exercises, such as walking. Anything that puts strain on my abdomen or is jarring causes discomfort. Donna

contents: Hi everyone, it's been awhile since I posted here but had a question- has anyone tried Plexus Slim? Did you experience any issues with the IC? I have been taking the supplements without issues, but I started the "pink drink" yesterday. Has anyone experienced increased urination with this and if so did it go away? I have read it can be part of the detox but I also am concerned about it flaring the IC. I have read many testimonials about the triplex (slim, biocleanse and probio) helping the IC .... Yesterday I was fine but today I did just a half of the packet and am peeing every 15-30 min!! Thoughts????

Re: Plexus Slim? It sounds like it might not be the best thing for you personally. You might want to stop it for a few days, then try it again to see if it causes a flare --- if it does, you will have your answer. Donna

contents: At my last uro appt my Dr suggested that I try pre-medicating (pyridium & topical lidocaine) & going ahead & riding my bike,knowing that it will make me flare.Because I (eat,breathe & sleep) LOVE cycling.So far,I have done little rides (1 hr) & I usually flare the next day. Nothing grotesque,but just disheartening.At first I thought this was a bad idea.Then I thought of my favorite non hilly ride-it's at least 3 hours.I need to get back into the gym for 3 weeks to build up core & overall strength,but I think I'm going to go for the burn.My Dr says there is no evidence that this will worsen my disease. Any thoughts on this?

Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. The only thing you can do is try. Sometimes it is worth it to put up with a very mild flare to be able to do what you love. Do everything you can to prevent a flare and go for it. I find being dehydrated (due to exercise) is really hard on my IC even if it's just for a short while. Make sure you stay hydrated throughout. Good luck.

Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. I don't think exercise can make IC worse, you can flare from it from varying degrees. Try it if the flare you get us not worth the enjoyment lesson learned. If you get a small flare or none after a 3 hr rude all the better. Good luck
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. Thanks you guys! I will let you know how it goes when I work up the courage to try it.Really more afraid of the disappointment than pain. So if I anticipate that it may well annoy my bladder I will be less disappointed!

Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. Originally posted by icshorty Thanks you guys! I will let you know how it goes when I work up the courage to try it.Really more afraid of the disappointment than pain. So if I anticipate that it may well annoy my bladder I will be less disappointed! I'm back here because after being somewhat symptom free for a few years, I'm in a full out "flare" shaking, chills, frequency. For me it's a disappointment that I can't even put into words. I'm just gutted...it took a long time to get this under control....anyway, sharing my thoughts on riding. It does lead to a flare for me if I go for longer than 45 min. I was a runner for years, my knees gave up....I ran because I had control over it, but no control really over my IC. I had no flares when running, so my surgeon prescribed bicycling...30 lbs later (yes I gained that much) I can do a half an hour with NO flare on the bike. Wish you luck icshorty.

Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. Sorry to read you are having such a bad flare. Do you know what brought it on? Exercise? I used to run but stopped when disgnose eith IC, I kept getting visible blood in my urine after running. I used to bike hard, but stopped that too, I think it used my abs too much. I now bike casually, I still enjoy biking but I am out there to enjoy being outside and moving, not to best myself. Not too sure if you are looking for an exercise that you can go hard and get a good Erik out maybe try swimming.
signature: My are with you all. May you all find a way to peace and joy in your lives.

contents: Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. I also love cycling!! My husband bought me a special seat that really helps. I still can't do exceptionally long rides any more though. But the seat has allowed me to actually be able to ride again. It's a teeny little seat with no horn at all. Maybe you can look into this? Good luck and God bless!!🙂

Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. so far I am getting along ok on my upright bike for itty bitty rides.Do you use this bike seat for an upright position bike or a "road bike"? If you can find the brand name on this seat somewhere,I would like to check it out!Thanks so much!

Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. It's called ergo the seat hornless saddle. My bicycle is a mountain bike. We ride trails but also on the road too. The seat is adjustable so you can get the angle that works best for you and the type of bike you have... Hope that helps our last ride was about 8 miles, that's about my limit now. Although I'm scheduled to have the Botox injections in my bladder in a few weeks, so I'm hoping I'll be able to get back in the saddle soon God bless!!
Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. Awesome! Thank you- fingers crossed for Botox success!

Re: Shorty's ride of probable misery. I completely understand! I can't say whether not it will not worsen the disease (I'm actually quite curious about this myself) but I for sure push through. I'm an avid runner and it's really important for my physical and mental health that I continue to run even if it means flare ups after. Please let me know if you find any info about if it can worsen the disease.

I have been using the New Dawn Pilates for a few years now and am very happy with the results. But I've reached a point where I need to step it up abit. I see that it says Vol #1 on the dvd but I have done some searching and have yet to find vol. 2 (the next step up). Is there a followup to this dvd? Or does anyone have a suggestion on another one that would be a nice transition to the next level? Thank you in advance for any help you may be able to give

Re: New Dawn Pilates I haven't tried pilates, but just wanted to offer encouragement --- since you have found an exercise program that seems to be working for you, I suggest you move slowly when you go to a more vigorous program. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: Would love to know what you do for exercise. I see people who have had cancer doing runs. I cannot predict when I'll have an ok day in terms of pain level so I never sign up for anything much or join any teams or a gym. Sometimes I do walks with my dog and that's it. I just wonder how limited other ic patients are. It's very frustrating to me! Thanks for any ideas.

Re: How active are you? I'm with you, aside from my exercises from PT, it's just walking the dog at this point. Sometimes I take her on 3-4 short 10 or 15 minute walks, as being outside helps my mental state and I feel like walking is a really good exercise that doesn't bother me. Do you feel irritated in your lady parts when you exercise? I've started putting a small bit of coconut oil on after my shower and it helps keep it kind of moisturized so it doesn't feel all chafed when I'm walking the dog. It helps me a lot.

Re: How active are you? About the only exercises I can do are walking and (in summer) swimming. I can't do anything that is "jarring" without pain. I used to wear a step counter and had a goal of 10,000 steps per day. I need to pick up a battery for mine. Donna

contents: Re: How active are you? My pain is the least when I exercise so I do it as often as I can. One and a half hours in the morning which involves half hour weights followed by full hour cardio one day and one hour weights followed by half hour cardio the next. I don't skip a day so even go weekends. I walk the dog randomly too. I feel I am addicted to the gym because I feel so good when I'm there. It also heaps my mood and seems to pick me up. I do have to modify some strength training like active squats etc because of my pelvic floor dysfunction. It tightens me up even more. So lots of relaxation after those days. I throw in a pilates every now and then to get a really good stretch. Because my main pain is nerve related apparently my brain can't exercise and feel constant pain at the same time. That's what the pain psych says anyway.

contents: Re: How active are you? I went to training until some months ago, but I finally said I need to give up because every time afterwards my bladder cramps are so much worse. I know my body needs exercises, but my case I needed to give up. Sad.

Re: How active are you? Excercise usually makes me feel better (or the same at the worst). I feel like I have to-even (especially) when I feel bad unless I have a nasty UTI (more frequent lately). My usual week includes 2 runs, 2 swims, and 1 or 2 weights. The running is the hardest on the bladder, but is usually fine and I always feel great after. I know where all the potty stops are on my routes. ABliske, I see you are from Santa Cruz. Lots of good places to walk. Do you like to swim? I did the Masters open water swim there a few years ago. My feet were so frozen afterwards that I actually walked back to our hotel barefooted-across the Boardwalk parking lot! They were so numb I couldn't feel the ground, but I couldn't get them to work well enough to get them in shoes. Sounds like fun? Anyway, swimming is the best.

Re: How active are you? I can lift weights, treadmill, eliptical, recumbant bike, strength training videos. Things I can't do: anything requiring ab/core or running/cardio with jumping. Exercise makes me feel better too, as long as I stick with what's safe.
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: How active are you? I used to run, but it aggravates my symptoms. I do walking and the elliptical.
signature: Officially diagnosed January 2014 by cystoscopy. +Hunners. Hypothyroid, IBS? Tolreodine 2mg day, sometimes 4mg (causing retention & that aggravates symptoms) Claritin & Benadryl (worked great...for awhile...dried me out severely!) Cystex, azo orange, and 1/2 tsp baking soda as needed for rescue..d-Mannose too! Slippery elm and cayenne immediately after meals. (Helping...I was scared to take the cayenne but it actually helps & doesn't irritate my bladder) Aloe tabs(stopped-constipation) Lemon, tea tree, and frankincense essential oil. CBD oil (WORKS!) Most important of all AVOIDING my triggers: soy, msg and oxalates are known triggers. Also sugar & fatty foods. Seeking good physician. I moved away from the one who diagnosed me shortly after she did & have been hard pressed to find one who will listen & take me seriously. Just takes time. The glass is half full.

contents: Re: How active are you? Just finished another half marathon this weekend. My bladder behaved, and I was thrilled! My husband and 2 of 3 kids did it also. Very proud. My story of the day was, of course, my potty stop. This course went through our local zoo. There is a new section with...New Bathrooms! I thought they wouldn't be open since the zoo was not officially open. They were, and I chose that over the many ports potties along the way. Shiny, clean, space, flushable, warm running water, SOAP! I guess it doesn't take much to make my day. Quote seen. "There will be a day when I will no longer be able to do this. Today is not the day."

contents: Re: How active are you? Originally posted by ICNDonna Good for you! Donna SDM, Wow, congrats on the race and to your kiddos too! I did sign up to do the Wharf to Wharf race. Are you familiar with that one? I'm planning to walk but still very nervous that I may not be able to do it. Some days I'm hunched over in pain. It's be adrenaline will be helping me through it I do make it to the starting line. My sister are coming from out of town to do it so I want to participate. I thought about having my 7 year old (the baby in my picture ) do it too but then I wasn't so sure. So, hopefully this works out and we do it is the coming years. How old are your kids? I love your bathroom story!!! I know what you mean. Every time I go to any concert or festival I'm worried about what the lines will be like! The swimming sounds like too much for me. I should get a wetsuit sometime because I'm a baby about the cold ocean. A friend of mine was trying to convince me to swim around the wharf with her and even said I could bring a boogie board since I'm not a strong swimmer. I should have taken her up on this to be more active but I'm happier a little closer to shore. It's obviously overall much better to be active and get the endorphin high. It's been a long time of me on this!

Re: How active are you? I am familiar with Wharf to Wharf, but I haven't done it. Sounds like you will have a blast doing this with family. Walking is totally fine. Sometimes I feel like if I am feeling bad anyway, I might as well do something rather than nothing. Adrenaline and endogenous morphine cure a lot of what ails. My kids that ran are both 22. I use a wetsuit too when the water is cold. Staying closer to shore is probably wise. That pier is pretty long. I felt like I was part of the food chain. Some of the seals were a little too interested also.

Re: How active are you? Oh ABliske you should so do the wharf swim. Is it in the sea??? If it is I wouldn't be getting out to find a loo. That little bit of wee every few minutes won't hurt. Maybe just keep to the back of the pack :-)
signature: UTI 3/2015 IC ,PFD prescribed meds: Endep 60mg, Elmiron 400mg, BC. Enablex 7.5mg. Supplements: Chitomur peptide, colostrum, Echinacea, liver detox swisse, calcium citrate, glucosamine, Psyllium Husk. Daily diet: Breakkie- oats with chia seeds and no fat milk. Post workout-turkey breast with mixed veggies. Lunch-raw green soup with rye roll and handful of peanuts/almonds. Dinner- changes but meat and veg. Dessert-banana and sugar free custard (homemade) or banana and apple bake with almonds cinnamon and honey with a dollop of coconut cream. Tried: tramadol, cystoprotek, multi right, diazepam, vesicare, Ditropan, mirabegron, Flomax, lyrica, progesterone.

contents: I just bought my friend's old eliptical and I really would like to use it today. I am still in my flare (about 6 weeks now), but I'm not in pain. Only pressure and frequency. Anyone successfully use an eliptial without it affecting their IC?
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Can anyone use an eliptical? Using an elliptical is about the only thing I can do! I actually lost 40 lbs several years ago (I've since put 30 lbs back on). The elliptical was easy and a good cardio workout. If you're flaring I don't know if it would be a good idea. I just know it works your arms and legs and leaves your core alone. Anything involving my core brings on a flare. Even walking too much on a treadmill hurts my IC. I miss my elliptical. We sold it with our last house. We're actually thinking of buying another one. It was a great workout without hurting my IC. Good luck!

Re: Can anyone use an eliptical? Thanks Shelia. I agree, anything that involves my core = FLARE! I did the eliptical yesterday for 6 min (starting slow) and it went ok. No worse, no better. Although today, I threw my back out and am having back spasms!!! If it's not one thing, it's another.

contents: Re: Ab Exercises? What type is best? I have to stick with what I call "smooth" exercises, such as walking. In summer I can add some swimming. Donna

Re: Ab Exercises? What type is best? I have tried just about every ab and core exercise out there and they ALL affect my IC unfortunately. I hate it because that's the main area that needs exercise! I can only do arm and leg weights, walk, and simple exercises that do not affect my core at all. But we're all different. Maybe try planks?

contents: I used to run, play sports, etc. I was very active. One of the hardest parts for me so far with IC is the limited exercise I've been able to do. The jolting and quick movements seem to irritate me more and made it feel like I needed to use the bathroom. But I have found that walking on the treadmill at a moderately steep INCLINE relieves all of my symptoms! Yes... I feel normal! Once I lower it back to no incline at the end of my walk I feel the mild pain and pressure return. This was proven true for me all 3 times this week. Looks like gravity might be part of the problem for me. Thought I would share this in case someone else might like to try some non-irritating exercise and see if it works for them. Looks like I'll be spending lots of time walking to find some relief

Re: Found an exercise that relieves symptoms Thanks so much for the idea! I was also very active before my IC diagnosis. I cannot do any kind of jumping or ab/core exercises or I flare every time. My flare feels like I have to use the bathroom much more, often with bladder pressure. I really miss working out. I have found that I can lift weights and do other exercises as long as I don't use my core. Sometimes walking helps me, other times I think it makes it worse. But maybe you've got an idea with the inclined walking! I'll try it tomorrow, thanks!!

contents: Re: Found an exercise that relieves symptoms I hope walking works well for both of you! I can do weights too as long as I don't squat or do core exercises (plank seems to be ok though). I definitely think the endorphins help! I feel pretty good for a couple hours after I exercise. Good luck!

Re: Found an exercise that relieves symptoms Walking always helped me when my bladder was not doing well. It may have felt irritated at first but after about 5 or 10 minutes I felt a whole lot better. If you don't have access to a treadmill you can always walk outside where it isn't flat, like up hills etc. Good way to strengthen legs going up some hills.

Re: Found an exercise that relieves symptoms Walking is about the only exercise I can do. I can't tolerate anything that is jarring. Even before IC, I couldn't jog, run, do jumping jacks, etc. Swimming is also a good one for me --- we have an above ground pool so no big time swimming, but I do lots of water walking in summer. Donna

contents: Hi All After being diagnosed about 1.5 years ago, I have been nervous to exercise (outside my 3.5 roundtrip walk to/from work), fearing I would get a flare. Finally, this morning I went to a Hatha yoga class! I loved it! Tonight, I'm feeling a little sensitive (slight bladder spasms and urgency). Has anyone had success with Hatha yoga before? Also, in your experiences, is a little discomfort to be expected as you start a new exercise routine, no matter how gentle? I'd ideally like to go to one class per week to build up my strength. Would love to hear any thoughts/successes with yoga classes. Thanks

Re: Hatha Yoga and IC I do some at home but haven't gone to a regular class for a long time. Some of the yoga poses were very good for my pelvic floor muscles. I know I've read several posts here over the years where people have had positive reports from doing yoga. I believe the ICN shop here has dvds for exercise so some of this must be helpful. I think the only way you would know if it helps or hurts would be to start out slow and maybe even eliminate some of the poses that you feel are putting too much pressure on the pelvic area. I would keep going unless it puts you in a full painful flare.

Hi everyone, Well...Just when I was starting to feel optimistic about keeping my I.C. under control (I've been on Cystoprotek since the beginning of the year, and it's helped me alot). I decided after spending the last 2 years sitting on my butt and not exercising, that I needed to get back in to the gym and try to shed the 50 lbs that I've gained since this whole ordeal started for me My old standby is the Eliptical machine, it's always helped me alot for melting off the fat, keeping heart rate elevated...etc. But after spending the last 2 mornings in the gym, doing only a mere 20 min on the Eliptical each time.... my bladder has now been flaring for the past 48 hours like CRAZY! It's almost as if someone's poured a pot of coffee down me or something....can't stay off the toilet. peeing like nuts. No pain, just frequency. So this is majorly disappointing...to say the least. But I don't want to give up on myself yet. I know running, or Stairmaster workouts are OUT, too much josstling the bladder around. But I thought I could at least handle the Eliptical since it's not so "jarring" to the pelvic area. Well obviously not for me. So I'm thinking of trying the rowing machine next...I haven't tried lifting weights yet, I really wanted to find a good cardio exercise to engage in and warm up with that before hitting the weights. But does anyone out there have any advice? I know we're all different....but I really would love to hear any thoughts or ideas of exercises that have worked for others and that are friendly to a temperamental bladder like mine I appreciate all your help! Amy
signature: Treatments I've tried: ALL the OAB meds (Detrol, Enablex, Vescicare, Sanctura, Toviax,, Myrbetriq, etc). They either didn't work at all, or gave me bad retention Tried PTNS but gave up after 5 sessions with no improvement and a $50 copay every visit...ouch! Elavil - Had very adverse reaction to Elavi 25mg Bovine Colostrum- saw no improvement in symptoms after 4 months Elmiron: Adverse reaction within 2 weeks, had to stop Current Tx: Cystoprotek : 6 to 8 Capsules daily, helps to a degree especially with nightime frequenc

contents: Re: What Exercises WON'T cause a Flare??!! Hi Amychristina! I feel your pain on this. I use to be a big runner before all this. Running 5k's, 10k's and half marathons all the time. Although I'm working back up to getting there someday right now stretching, yoga, and walking are the exercises I find I can tolerate the best. Normally I would just force myself to go do the run no matter what but I'm trying to be kinder and listen to my body more these days. Sometimes I focus on upper body with weights and I have bought a low impact workout video (I think Prevention makes it) and also am using some of my old pregnancy workout DVDs since they don't have a lot of jumping type exercises. It is kind of boring but better than nothing I guess!

contents: Re: What Exercises WON'T cause a Flare??!! If your gym has a recumbent stationary bike, you're set. That's the kind of bike where you sit in a chair and the pedals are in front of you, so it doesn't involve much movement of the pelvis at all. (If your pelvis is moving while you pedal, you've got the seat adjusted too far from the pedals - the extended leg should still have the knee bent a little for good pedaling efficiency). I do a lot of walking, which seems to help my bladder more than it hurts it. But it's hard to work on cardiovascular fitness while walking, and it takes a long time to burn off significant calories. I'm an avid cyclist (conventional diamond-frame bike, not a recumbent) and my bladder tolerates that very well, but I understand that that's not the case for most people. I tried running for a while, and that was OK with my bladder (I wasn't flaring at the time - I think trying to run when I'm in a flare would be pretty excruciating) but I ended up with tendinitis in my knees and my orthopedist suggested I stick to cycling. Getting and staying in shape is one of the best things I can do for my mental health! I hope you can find exercises that work for you.

Re: What Exercises WON'T cause a Flare??!! I am able to do swimming. I try to go every day. My tip to not having a flare up is to use non petrolleum jelly and put it around my bladder opening and vaginal. Works beautiful. Even manage to swim in the sea this summer and body board too.

Has anyone else tried this? I'm in the process of trying new foods to figure out what I can and can't eat. I'm very careful only eating small amounts of new foods. When I eat a trigger food, I can feel a small flare staring up. I go to the gym, and I get on the elliptical or treadmill at a medium pace. When my heart rate goes up and I start to sweat, the flare goes away. After 30 to 45 minutes of cardio, my bladder feels normal again. I also drink lots of water afterward. There has been a couple times where it hit me later that night but only for a few minutes – like little zings in my bladder and urethra. Then it settles. But for the most part, the exercise really helps.
signature: Diagnosed with IC in early May of 2015 via cystoscopy bladder distention surgery. Currently taking the following: Elmiron 100mg three (3) times daily, Elavil 10 mg (1 daily), Atarax 25 mg (1 daily), Biotin, Probiotic. Improving with mild symptoms. Just started Cysto Protek. I've not had much luck with natural products. Got my fingers crossed.

contents: Re: Exercising your way out of a flare? Walking always helped me but it had to be on an even surface with no climbing so that it didn't put pressure on the pelvic area. I always felt it had something to do with getting more blood flow to the lower part of the body and I'm sure that getting the pain reducing endorphins to kick in helped a lot too. Sometimes the first ten minutes were painful but after that the pain went away and I felt good.

Re: Exercising your way out of a flare? Originally posted by earthlady ...Sometimes the first ten minutes were painful but after that the pain went away and I felt good. Yes. That's the same for me. The first 5 to 10 mins can be a little uncomfortable, but once I get past that I'm good. I really think getting the heart rate up everyday for 30 mins is important.

contents: Hi folks. Since we cannot do many tummy toning exercises, which tax the pelvic floor, I wonder if hula hopoing would be safe since no crunching or jumping involved. Anyone out there able to use a hula hoop? My "menupooch" could use a little work!
signature: Cheers, Cheryl Current Treatments: IC Diet 25mg Atarax Vaginal Valium (as needed) Pelvic Floor Therapy (1x/week) Quarterly bladder instillations

contents: Re: Hula hoping. I haven't done any hula hooping for a long time so I don't know how or if it would effect the pelvic floor but it sounds like it's worth a try! It may even help with increasing some blood flow to the area in a positive way? If you try it let us know how it goes. A friend of mine lost two inches off her waist from using the hula hoop every day.
Re: Hula hoping. 2 inches! Sounds like I definitely need a hula hoop.
signature: Cheers, Cheryl Current Treatments: IC Diet 25mg Atarax Vaginal Valium (as needed) Pelvic Floor Therapy (1x/week) Quarterly bladder instillations

signature: Cheers, Cheryl Current Treatments: IC Diet 25mg Atarax Vaginal Valium (as needed) Pelvic Floor Therapy (1x/week) Quarterly bladder instillations

contents: Haven't gained any weight but for some reason I'm getting fatter from the Prosac. I know it is from the pill because when I have been off of it for a week my cloths start to fit again. My fat cloths are starting to get to tight and I have no money at the moment to buy new ones and I really don't want to buy bigger cloths. I am at the point where I do not want to go off of it. Life is just to hard and I end up crying all the time and wanting to stay in bed and not even have anything to do with my fur kids. Soooooooooo, anyone else have this problem? Even if I don't eat much of nothing it stays the same. I hate being fat again. Have been on the yo yo diet since 6th grade when a boy called me fat. Wasn't fat just hadn't grown yet which I did that summer and I was perfectly proportioned when I went back to school. I don't even want to be as thin as I was before the IC started. I would settle for 20 pounds smaller. Or even 10 pounds or not to have this feeling of being "fatter" when the scales say I'm not. Hate going anywhere. I don't feel fatter at home but then I have on compfy cloths but the same compfy cloths I have been wearing. It's just odd.

Re: Getting fat from Prosac!! Help me pleaseeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee Hi waterflow! I'm not on prosac but I'm on amitriptyline which I've heard can also cause weight gain. As of now I haven't gained but I'm just curious do you think your hormones have anything to do with the weight? Or do you feel like it is definitely linked with the prosac?

Hydroxydine 25-50mg as needed at bedtime (5/15) Quercetin 2 pills daily to help immune system and body inflammation. Cystoprotek 4 capsules daily (8/15). Prelief as needed. USE DURING FLARES: Heating pad, ice packs for PFD, Vaginal Valium 10-20mg, Uribel, Pelvic floor stretches. PAST TREATMENTS: PFD physical therapy (6/15-10/15) & Counseling for stress mgt (9/15-12/15) both very helpful. GNC brand progesterone cream (not sure if this made a difference). Too many different diets and herbs to list. TRIGGERS: STRESS STRESS STRESS. BCP. Hormonal fluctuance (ovulation/period). Coffee, artificial preservatives and dyes.

contents: Re: Getting fat from Prosac!! Help me pleaseeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee I'm also curious about the hormone factor. Especially if you aren't gaining wait, but your clothes are getting tight. Hormones can dictate weight gain in specific parts of the body and can also cause water retention. I'm not sure what the Prosac would have to do with it since I'm unfamiliar with the medication, but something to think about...

contents: Re: Getting fat from Prosac!! Help me pleaseeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee Well, it isn't the pill this time. Didn't take it Saturday or Sunday and I gained 3 pounds. I am also having the worst pain ever and it feels more like cramping. The pain going from my lower back to my butt is awful and the Percocet isn't helping any. It never did when it came to cramps. I haven't had my period in 2 years since I had the uterus lining burned. OH, how I hope I'm NOT going to have my period again. Please....please.......please no!!! Did go back on the Prozac this morning in fact. didn't sleep much Saturday or last night. Then I woke up at 12:30 this morning feeling really sick and thought I had caught some awful bug but then realized it was withdrawal. You know, I was thinking how I have spent most of my life worrying about how fat I am and how much or I should say how little bit of hair I have. Have Alopecia which was bad enough to deal with. Anyways, this is turning into a soap opera.....Stay tuned as I turn fatter....bwaaaaaaa

Re: Getting fat from Prosac!! Help me pleaseeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee Waterflow, I would say at this point you need to talk to your doctor. This sounds like it could be edema or something going on with your kidneys. I wouldn't mess around with that....please let us know what happens! hugs hugs hugs

contents: Re: Getting fat from Prosac!! Help me pleaseeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee rshell88, amitriptyline was the very first pill they gave me waaaaaaaaaay back when the IC first started. I gained 1 pound a day. Yes, 1 pound a day and after 5 days I quit taking it. Told the doctor why to. I did a little googling last night and found that 1 in 5 people gain weight from antidepressants. Some stay the same weight. The really lucky ones lose weight and me...well, I'm that "1". I'm just so lucky I am. I'm always the odd ball. My gyno didn't believe me I was gaining weight from it. The two gynos I have been too didn't believe me I was gaining weight from the birth control. Geesh, for a second there I thought maybe I had lost my mind and was gaining weight for no reason. Or I was over eating or I was sitting to much. Last night while waiting to fall asleep I was trying to decide what to do. Do I stop taking the pill and get the fat off and fit into all my wonderful skinny cloths? BUT it means crying all the time again (for even no reason or when an ambulance drives by with sirens, funeral car line going by...or just for no good reason AND arguing with my family again.) Or do I keep taking it and cry over being fat and having no cloths to wear (and no money to buy any. No 2nd hand stores near by and one is closing.) What to do what to do??? So, as for now I have decided to try and stay off of it. Not sure if mentally I can do it but I'm giving it a shot again. Julie B, I don't think it's edema or my kidneys. My dad has that problem. It could just be hormones. I don't like my family doctors or any of the women who work there. To call means having to deal with their attitudes. I only go there when I really really really have to and that isn't very often. The only doc I go to all the time is my Uro.

I am wanting to start working out a little. I know from past experiences that I can't just go workout like I did when I was younger because it makes me flare up. If I try to workout now I take it in slow, light and small workouts. I have been seeing that Yoga looks safe to try out but I know looks can be deceiving and didn't want to end up over doing and not realize it. lol Also would it be safer to find a Yoga class so i can speak to the instructor and discuss with them about my health history so I don't over do it ? Or does anyone know of a Yoga CD that works for them? Redhead
Re: To Yoga or not to Yoga?? You could talk with an instructor to see if he/she can work with you to avoid problems. Or you could try a CD and approach with caution to see if it's okay for you. Donna

contents: Re: To Yoga or not to Yoga?? i got a yoga for pelvic pain DVD and started with the super easy version. i haven't been great about doing it because of time (I already have a lot of other exercises i'm meant to do daily) however it was super gentle and i just paused it on each exercise to make the stretches last as long as i wanted, so wasn't dictated by their pace... i think really important is to start slow and listen to your body!

Re: To Yoga or not to Yoga?? I have practiced yoga for years and I really recommend it. Search around for different classes in your area - I would recommend any beginner's classes or classes that have the words "slow" or "relaxing" in their class description. I recommend classes over DVDs because in class you are sure you are getting better alignment so you won't injure yourself. It can be easy to overstretch or pull something, and with a teacher there you are less likely to do that and more likely to learn good modifications. A pose you will probably learn to come into if you need a break is child's pose. It's kind of like you are curled up in a little ball. Most teachers will tell you that you can go into this pose at any point in class if you need a break, even if the teacher is teaching something else. Sometimes I find this puts pressure on my bladder, so I just lay down on my back instead. Definitely talk to your instructor. Any reputable, good teacher will be willing to listen to you and offer you suggestions. Most good teachers are grateful to learn about a student's history so they can help you modify and stay safe. If any teacher seems put off by that, I would say to look for a different teacher (not all teachers and classes are the same!). Yoga is good for exercise, but it also helps with relaxation and breathing. I hope you try it!

Re: To Yoga or not to Yoga?? I have done yoga as well, and norml gymnastics, I do not have really pain though. But I always avoid exercises that I feel can hurt my bladder, and stop. I have told trainers before that I have that condition and I will not make some exercises, and they have said just fine, or showed me some easier ones if others do harder version.

We are going to Las Vegas in June and I would like to try ziplining. It's a sit down one so the supports go around your legs instead of up your crotch area. Has anyone tried ziplining? I've never done it before and I don't want to end up in a flare. I do ok with roller coasters as long as they're not too rough and I can hike with no problems. It's the one in Boulder City right outside of Vegas (don't know if I'm allowed to post the name) . There's a video if anyone wants to check it out and let me know what you think. I feel pretty good these days, but I don't want to do anything to mess that up.

Re: Ziplining Originally posted by sleepingbe We are going to Las Vegas in June and I would like to try ziplining. It's a sit down one so the supports go around your legs instead of up your crotch area. Has anyone tried ziplining? I've never done it before and I don't want to end up in a flare. There's a video if anyone wants to check it out and let me know what you think. I feel pretty good these days, but I don't want to do anything to mess that up. I jut did the search. I would be terrified, but I don't do well with heights . If you're not nervous about it, it looks like a smooth ride an it probably would be okay for you. For me, the stress of fear would probably bring on a flare. Hugs, Donna

contents: Re: Ziplining Thanks Donna. I'm not too scared. I'm an adrenaline junkie. Wish me luck!
Re: Ziplining my hat goes off to you sleepingbe! let us know how it is and have fun!!!

Re: Ziplining Sounds like fun! I probably could have done this a few years back, but as I get older, I find I am more sensitive to heights. I always wanted to skydive though. Not sure if I could get past my fear of heights to do it though Lol! Hope you have a great time!
Re: Ziplining I did zip lining in Canada and you are well supported without anything around the crotch area at all, so you should be absolutely fine. Looks amazing, enjoy.

Re: Ziplining Thanks so much for your support, fellow icers ☺ Chrissyc, I'm glad to know you had no problems with it. It makes me feel a lot more confident. I'll report back after the trip and let y'all know how it goes.

I am starting to get really frustrated. It seems like every time I exercise lately, I get a day of frequency. I used to be able to do strength training with weights, but even that seems to bother me now. I can walk on the tredmill, but I have to walk so slow, as not to bounce my bladder too much. This morning, I tried a new strength training workout, and now I have bladder pressure again. I am wondering, if exercise bothers me that much, is it more likely that I'm having PFD issues or bladder issues? I was diagnosed with IC via hydro last year, but I'm really beginning to think I also have PFD because klonopin helps me so much. I just miss exercising so much. I've lost so much muscle, and because I'm 43 years old, I feel like I need to stay strong.
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me I can relate. I'm still trying to get this disorder managed. So I'm in the trial and error phase. However yoga at this time is the only physical activity that doesn't cause me to flair. Restorative yoga actually gives me pain relief. I'm going to go visit a PT who specializes with pelvic pain and I'm hopeful it will help. Try some gentle restorative yoga poses and see if that helps. At least it will relieve some stress. Ivette

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me About the only exercises I can do are what I call "smooth" ones, such as walking, either outdoors or on a treadmill. I can't do anything that is jarring (jogging, running). I can also do a bicycle or exercycle if I set it so it doesn't require a lot of pressure to pedal. I sometimes wear a step counter so I know how many steps I take in a day. Donna

contents: Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me I am with donna. i have a step counter that i use every day and push myself to walk 10000 steps or more, so that i get exercise. but i can't do majorly speedy walking at the moment. i had to give up the gym as well. yesterday i did the yoga for pelvic pain DVD, and today have a lot of pain, not sure if it is that or something else (over zealous walking?). so i think a lot of gentle walking is the way to go. i do a bit of light hand weights but in a way that doesn't move my torso. really frustrating, as i want to be doing weights to keep my body strong, but hoping it will come (so no answers, just sympathy!)

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me How about seated exercises? I know you are not a senior citizen like I am but at my YMCA there are "Silver Sneaker" seated exercise classes that can give you a surprisingly good work out. Some of them use hand weights and some use bands. Google it and I am sure you can find some seated exercises. If weights bother you, can you use resistance bands instead? They may be less jarring. Jo Ann

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me Ivette, what exactly is restorative yoga? And do you have a DVD for it or do you attend a class? Donna, yes, it may be that slowly walking will end up being the only exercise I can do. That really makes me sad. Kiki, I ordered a yoga DVD last year that was for people with pelvic pain. It was ok. It didn't flare me. Even though I've had IC for several years, I'm still baffled as to what I'm flaring from half the time. I just know that every time I exercise for more than one day, I end up with bladder frequency and pressure. Joann, thanks for giving me the idea of seated exercises. I will look it up. I really would like to keep my strength in my legs and arms. I obviously can't strengthen my core or abdomen any longer, but I've accepted that. And resistance bands might work too. Thank you all for your suggestions! Jane
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me thanks for that reminder that sometimes, who knows...i try to pin it on something, but then grasp at straws. maybe it wasn't the yoga...i did walk slightly faster for 45 minutes yesterday with hand weights. probably a bad idea...i really felt it pull when i started doing arm circles with the weights whilst walking. ok, bad idea, i know...i just miss feeling like i got a workout & have been feeling a lot better so pushed it! silly i know... today i did 15,000 steps...but all pretty gentle. but at least it is something to keep me moving. we have a family history of osteoporosis so it does worry me not to be exercising more...i hope that i can eventually.

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me It doesn't have to be "slow" walking. Before my cancer surgery, I could walk a mile in about 18 minutes, which isn't the best, but not slow either. During the same period I spent a half hour a day on my treadmill. I would plug in a disc and watch a part of a movie while I did the treadmill. Donna

contents: Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me The only thing that I can do that doesn't bother my IC or knees, back or just about any part of my snap crackle pop body is the elliptical. Can't do it for very long now because I'm really out of shape but so far it doesn't bother the IC. In fact, it seems my bladder likes it.
Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me Waterflow, that's good to know. I wish I owned one!
signature: Jane Diagnosed 1/7/14 via cysto/hydro Current Treatment: 10 mg amitriptyline, DH Aloe Vera Used During Flares: Uribel, extra DH Aloe Vera, Klonopin and Tramadol (as needed) Practicing bladder training and stress control (hot baths, pelvic stretching) Recently tried PT, do not appear to have PFD Things that have not helped in the past: Oxybutynin, Vesicare, Pyridium, Myrbetriq, Vagifem Also have migraines and IBS

contents: Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me And another blow to the system. I used to swim regularly for exercise. Have been back a few times since the ic but each time it takes almost a week to calm down the irritation. So having to see the fitness club manager to try and get out of our annual membership on medical grounds. This illness has robbed me of so much over the past 6 months. We cannot eat out anymore because of my wheat and gluten intolerance coupled with the limits of the ic diet. I am sick to death of just being offered a baked potato and salad. In this country (uk) providing snacks etc that are wheat and gluten and preservative free is a joke. Like living in the dark ages when so many people have food intolerances. Slowly losing the will to live...........

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me Hi Chrissy, I am a bit worried because we are leaving for a vacation in three weeks to Key West, FL. We are staying at a nice resort that has a big pool and hot tub. I don't think I've been in a pool with chlorine since I've had IC. Oh wait, yes I was, one time 2 years ago on a cruise. It didn't bother me then, but my IC acted differently then. I really want to relax by the pool and take a dip when I get hot, but I am scared it could aggravate or flare my IC. Sigh...always something to worry about.

contents: Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me I personally would avoid hot tubs and public pools at all costs. You never know if they are cleaned well enough or not. Also if you happen to be chemical sensitive, I would avoid it. I know it sucks, but it is better than messing up your bladder further.

We are staying at a nice resort that has a big pool and hot tub. I don't think I've been in a pool with chlorine since I've had IC. Oh wait, yes I was, one time 2 years ago on a cruise. We had planned a cruise, but cannot use the pools, food will be a problem so holidays this year are off until i can get some symptom free time. Very frustrating isnt it?

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me Oh Chrissy, i totally get it! it's so frustrating to not do things we want. i asked my physic about swimming as the house we are renting has a pool, she said the urologists she works with tend to say avoid it when super sensitive as usually irritates. I'd love to swim int he pool with my kids! i know, changes all...travel really needs to be self catering with good shops near by. eating out--can i suggest you find a couple decent restaurants you can order to your needs? Gluten free is actually pretty easy i find (been that for 14 years), most chains now do gluten free. problem is all the other stuff we can't have... my solution has been finding a few nice restaurants that will do totally plain fish + veg for me. not exciting, but means we can eat out. granted is more expensive, but then we barely eat out now. pain is that we can't just pop in somewhere if we are out all day. i have a day without kids next weekend, really looking forward to playing tourist--but as always means i have to pack food for the day. a argh! however if you have a Pret a Manger near you they now do pots with just boiled eggs and spinach--would at least keep you going ;-) good luck with the manager, hope that they are nice about it. re exercise, i got some cheap weights (Decathlon for those in UK great) and i just try to use them a couple times a day. put them in the kitchen to do whilst cooking in those waiting moments. at least my arms are getting some exercise and not losing muscles. thinking some squats as well, plus walking. just don't want to lose more muscle....any other do at hoe suggestions welcome by us all i'm sure!

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me Thanks kiki. Can i ask a general question here. I have constant urethral irritation, aching across pelvic bones and sometimes in bottom. I do not get burning when urinating, but pressure feeling as bladder fills and aching. I would not say extremely painful, just a feeling i need to pee often. Try to hold as long as i can. Can only pee maxiumum of 250ml. Is this more tending more towards pelvic floor dysfunction than anything else. I also have ibs which i know is affected by my muscle tone. Interestingly if i take a diazepam my pain, other than the irritation goes away for a couple of hours, and i read up on pfd and often diazepam is given to relax the muscles. Last edited by Chrissyc; 03-15-2015, 12:20 AM.

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me My2boys - The elliptical sounds like a really good idea for you to try. I'm sure most gyms have them. There's also the seated exercise where you lift yourself up and down from a chair to a standing position and then back down to a seated position that uses your core muscles but is not jarring. It's not aerobic but does get you to use some of your core muscles when lifting yourself up.

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me I can totally relate to your frustration. I am desperate to workout and eat healthy but every time I do I will get increase pressure and that gotta go sensation. I'm in my 40's too so I know how you feel. Once in a while I can get away withit but nothing consistent enough to make a difference in my appearance. Even yoga can bother my bladder and forget about Pilates or anything to strengthen my core muscles.... that immediately throws me in a flare. I do have PFD along with IC....

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me Originally posted by KikiUK Just to check, is the irritation once you pee or are you ok once you pee, and then a bit later get the irritation? Its there almost all the time, but gets worse as i need to pee. I still have the feeling of always wanting to go. Hold as long as i can and then whoops urgency - out of my way lady dashing to the loo!! The mornings are definitely the busiest and then it seems to calm down a little.

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me earthlady, I agree. I would like to try the eliptical machine. I never go to the gym. I've always worked out at home. I think squats bothers me too sometimes. Just out of curiosity, you say you have both PFD and IC. Were you diagnosed with both?

Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me I really think I do have some degree of PFD because the pelvic stretches really help my bladder - almost immediately. It seems like the more I do them, the better I feel. And I really don't know what I'm doing, except for what I've read online (squatting, hip stretches etc). Actually, squats might not bother me as much as lunges do. I'm still trying to figure it all out. That's another reason I think seeing a PT would be great - they could tell me what I can do to exercise and not flare! Dry needeling...that sounds painful, lol. When I get back from our spring break vacation, I'm going to arrange to see a PT that specializes in pelvic pain. I'm sure my uro will refer me.

contents: Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me I think seeing the physio is really worth it. I apparently don't have PFD but have very tight muscles on one side that are likely pulling on my bladder, and tight bits on top of nerves so it all irritates. however not classic PFD, but i'm hugely helped by all the stretches and trigger point worked + the osteopath. Chrissy, that sounds like what people talk about with IC...my experience of that sensation is it usually means some food is irritating me. i had to do a "reclean" of my diet to find the remaining foods that were really bugging it, as i thought all my foods were safe for me (they weren't). wondering if something else could be bugging you that you haven't tried cutting out? total pain to cut it all out i know. I just know that originally i was so flaring that some foods & supplements seemed safe, but once symptoms died down some then i was able to realize that actually they weren't safe--just not as screamingly bad as some others but were adding to that irritated feeling you describe. also this healing just takes time, doesn't it...
Re: Can't seem to find an exercise that doesn't flare me Kikiuk, that's good to know that even if you don't have "classic" PFD, you can still benefit from some of the PT. Thanks!
