contents: I do them daily plus other ones my pt gave me. I haven't had any pelvic floor spasms since starting the exercises, so it seems they are helping. The actual pt for pelvic floor hypertonicity did really help me but couldn't afford to continue pt on an ongoing basis.

I tried the stretches from the book for about a month. However, they didn't work so well for me. In my case, at the time I was in quite a bit of pain and very sore "down there" and taking hots baths twice a day was helping alot. The stretches (the series of about 10) didn't seem to help much and actually seemed to worsen the pain somewhat. The perineal massage definitely made things worse, and trying the internal work once per the book instructions put me into ALOT of pain. I read somewhere on the boards that someone reported holding stretches too long hurt them; they needed to do shorter stretches. So that's one theory I have, that I was holding the stretches too long as instructed in the book. Perhaps for people in alot of pain being helped by heat the stretches can be a bit much? I'm really not sure. I have seem a physical therapist more recently, and she focused alot on pelvic relaxation. I will still stretch sometimes using the squatting position, but I find the relaxation stuff more helpful. This includes tensing up the muscles (a kegel) and then relaxing fully, not too may times, to focus on the relaxation. Biofeedback can also be helpful; you can "see" how tense your muscles are on the screen and then how much of an effect consciously focusing on relaxing them has. Good luck!
signature: Symptoms began: January 2010 Diagnosed with IC: May 2010 Began with burning during/after urination, frequency, urgency, urethral spasms. Recurrent UTIs since 2002. Pelvic pain. Current Rx: Glucosamine/MSM tablets daily (1/2 a 1500 mg tablet Kirkland brand) IC diet stretches/relaxation/sitzbaths/heating pad Used in the Past: Gepan Instills (Chondroitin sulfate) 1x/mo until Sept 2010 Loratidine (Antihistamine) Tilidin (opiate analgetic not commonly prescribed in U.S.) as needed Cystoscopy w/Hydrodistention: May 2010 (found this temporarily relieved my spasms and urgency) Reside in Germany

contents: Internal One tip my therapist uses with me is to use a small vibrator. The vibration helps with nerve pain. If anyone like more information please send me a message. I started PT two years ago. When I first started I would jump off the table when internal was tried. It took a while and a lot of work to get to where I am today. I wanted to throw in the towel many times, but I stuck in there and I am glad I did today. Stretching the thighs hurt me a lot in the beginning as well. The main key I can offer is to go slow and breath when doing any exercise. If it hurts stop—and work on breathing relaxation. I also try to walk at least 15 minutes before I do any PT exercises. I have a pillow case filled with plain rice that I stitched together. I microwave the pillow (homemade heating pad, which I custom shape) and use for 10 minutes before and after my exercises. Warm up is important.

contents: Hi, I've had IC for many years. Five years ago I had a hysterectomy, and it got worse afterwards. It was worse because I got vulvodynia from it, and now it seems like the VV and IC are almost one and the same thing. I took Cymbalta 20-30 mg for two years and it helped a LOT. I had to stop taking it due to daily headaches from it, however, and it also started causing too much anxiety and weirdness. I stopped three months ago, so it's out of my system now and my body has adjusted back to normal, including bladder and vulvodynia pain! My question is: why does physical activity now cause a flare of both IC and VV? It never used to! I noticed that when I tried going back to the gym and pool recently, my symptoms got much much worse about a half-day to a day later. I feel fine during. This never used to happen. I can't tell if it's swimming laps (ultraviolet filter, low chlorine) or the elliptical, or even going up and down the steep hill I live on for a 2.5 mile walk. Any insights are appreciated. Are nerves being compressed by muscles during activity, or ............. ???? My new gyno thinks it's due to adhesions and offers to take a look inside, but I'm leery of another invasive pelvic procedure.

I've often wondered about that myself. I have a feeling it's irritated or inflamed nerves somewhere, whether inside the bladder or outside going to the bladder. When I had a lot of bladder irritation jarring or vibrational movements always made it worse. When the bladder wasn't irritated the movements never bothered me. My one doctor always said she felt it was a nerve inflammation somewhere- but didn't know where.

If you fall and skin a knee, that knee is going to hurt if you bend it or jiggle it a lot. If you sprain a muscle, exercise is going to hurt. It's reasonable to think that since I have a bladder my doctor described as very inflamed, it's going to hurt if I exercise the muscles around it and jiggle it. Donna

contents: True; but activity didn't used to bother my bladder I'm always careful not to do anything with impact like jogging because that would obviously jostle the bladder; but SWIMMING? Doing the freestyle stroke, I'm just kicking my legs. I just don't get it. Sigh. And what's the deal with doing abdominal exercise anyway? Does it pull on the bladder or something? Does anyone know how to stay fit with this stupid disease?

It's true what you say. I will try the recumbent bike, then do some upper body weight machines. Perhaps the recumbent keeps the torso and pelvis controlled enough that it doesn't bother the bladder and VV? I hope my knees can handle the bike. I remember the days when I would do an hour step class and then hit the weight room, then swim laps to cool down! Needless to say, I was in remission then!

Originally posted by naturegal My question is: why does physical activity now cause a flare of both IC and VV? . I think it is because after exercise the urine is more concentrated and more acidic. During exercise, urine has approximately the same sodium concentration as sweat.
That's an interesting theory. That means drinking plenty of water would help?

Landish, I just googled this and you are right, it looks as if there might be a connection between exercise and increase in bladder pain. This is super helpful to know about. I will do some more research on it including experimenting with keeping urine dilute during and after exercise. For me, my symptoms are worse afterwards, like even the next day. Thank you!
I think its the jogging and bouncing of the bladder is why.

chlorine well, i was diagnosed finally about 2 months ago. I've been going to water aerobics 2x a week. well, I had a horrible flareup last week, and i went to the doctor. she told me that the chlorine was making my bladder flare up. Has anyone had any issues with chlorine? i really love going to the pool, but i can't go back if my bladder doesn't like it. thanks Heather

Naturegal ---sorry I didn't see your post earlier. Drinking plenty of water during exercise help me, I have a little bit more frequency and I have to interrupt the exercise but I have no pain. It's still important to drink enough water after exercise until your urine becomes clear and diluted. It is just my experience. I hope you feel better or more comfortable during and after exercise.

Pebbles1 ---I can not even get me into the pool without the burning sensation and it takes me days to recover. I agree with your doctor, chlorine is one of the worst things for IC. If you live near a lake or the ocean, take advantage of it.

Exercise with IC and PFD I've had IC/PFD since December of 2000. At first I thought that babying myself and stopping the exercise that I had done up till that time would be helpful. Well I ended up with a bunch of extra weight to carry around and high blood pressure. Three years ago I came off all but a few of my medications and started exercising again. I quickly lost about 35 lbs. I have found that the exercise I do actually helps relieve my symptoms. I am able to walk at a fast pace (about 4 mph) on the treadmill and even do some jogging. I'm about to start adding cycling with my husband. We're hoping to slowly work our way up to a century ride. Wish me luck. We have found a special gel saddle which will provide some extra padding and I'm going to find some well padded cycling shorts. The fact that I'm retiring and will now be able to find the time to do some of the things that were impossible due to the demands of my job gives me hope that I will be able to get everything a bit more under control. I have actually found that sitting at work, or in the car (I had a 180 mile commute (90 miles each way) every day, aggravated my symptoms more than anything else. I'm hoping to also add some weight training again for the upper and lower body. I know that not everything works the same for everyone but wanted to share what works for me and hopefully give someone else some hope that they will be able to resume some type of normalcy to their life. I know the pain will never totally go away, but I'm trying to make it more manageable the only way I know how. Good luck to everyone. This site has always provided me with so much useful information that I always tend to come back here when I have something to share, or I need to find out if someone has tried something that works. Thanks everyone for sharing. This is such a personal problem and it's nice to know that there are people out there just like myself. People at work have no idea why I call in sick when I look so darned healthy. If only they knew that some days are just not possible. As I am not going to try and fight the system to try for disability due to the extra stress that would cause, I chose to resign and take my chances. By reducing the stress, the sitting and getting back to more relaxing activities I hope to reduce the number of flare ups. Wish me luck

Is anyone able to bicycle or use a stationary bike without setting off pain? I've been afraid to go back to this exercise not only due to the IC but also due to previous pelvic floor spasms. Anyone ok with this type of exercise, or has anyone modified the bike seat with a gel seat and been ok?

Typically bicycles are bad for ICer's. It is usually ok on a recumbent bike( the one that is like sitting in a chair) or I know some do get a different seat for their bikes and are ok with that also. I wouldn't recommend using a bicycle with the skinny seat ever! LOL In the past I tried one of the gel seats but found it was still too narrow for me personally. I hope you find something that works for you. Sandra

contents: Thanks. I guess the reclining stationary bike or a treadmill would probably be the best to try. There is a seat called a saddle seat that can be put on a regular bicycle but I imagine the vibration that comes from riding the bicycle might not be so good.

Go into your local bike shop, explain to the sales clerk your issue and s/he will be able to suggest to you the proper seat to use. You'll also be able to test the seats out on the bikes in the store. I found a comfortable seat (finally!) a year ago after struggling with uncomfortable biking. I not only got a comfortable seat, the guy sold it to me very cheap as it was slightly used ($15). With a bike seat the only way to positively know whether it'll work for you or not is to try it out. Also, the type of bike can also make a difference in how comfortable the ride will be. I have a very smooth riding Mongoose mountain bike (front and rear shocks) and also a street bike. Trial and error.
signature: (\__/) (o.O ) (> < ) This is Bunny. He's on his way to world domination.

contents: Thanks for the tip. I didn't realize they were able to do that.
I use a recumbent bike. Which has a nie wide comfortable padded seat. The most I have done on my bike is 20 minutes. All though I usually do 10 minutes.

@Earthlady---the people that work in bike shops are usually pro-am cyclers and are trained to properly fit a bike to the customer. I bet you'll be amazed after talking with a sales clerk just how many women come in looking for the proper seat. Women deal with more issues in the pelvic region than men when it comes to riding. If the bikes not properly fitted along with having the proper seat, the ride isnt ever going to be as comfortable as it should be. I know its probably a bit odd to think about discussing such issues with a sales clerk--trust me, they've heard it all. I've dealt with 4 different bike shops in my area and have never been made to feel anything less than comfortable when discussing my issues, with both male and female clerks. Good luck!
signature: (\__/) (o.O ) (> < ) This is Bunny. He's on his way to world domination.

contents: Hey - thanks everyone. I guess I thought it would be strange or too personal telling a sales person about why I would need a different seat. You're right, they probably deal with this all the time. Now if they could fit the bicycle with shock absorbers that would buffer all the bumps I'd be set - lol I should design a bicycle just for IC people.

What type of exercise does everyone do? I find that when I exercise my bladder feels better. I don't like the treadmill since I feel pain all over my body (especially thighs and gluts) a day or so after. I like the Elliptical machine the best especially the ones where you can move your arms. I do like the rowing machine but after while I want to get up. I don't like the exercise bike for the same reason. I tried yoga but it was too slow for me.

contents: I am very newly diagnosed with IC. In fact, I was just diagnosed by my family doctor and I haven't seen a urologist yet. It will be two weeks before I can even get in. But I did want to respond to your message because I just returned home from the gym. At first, I was in pain and almost left the exercise class. I felt like I had to go even though I just went minutes before. It was a mixture of pilates and yoga moves. I stayed in the class, did what I could, and then at the last few minutes she had us tense our muscles and then relax them. I didn't feel cured, but I felt so much better. Does this happen to you? I have been going to these classes for ten years now. I stopped going in the last few months and that is when I started to have problems. cj

contents: I'm glad that exercise seems to make your bladder feel better Anna. It does me too, depending on 'what' exercise. -Some have not been bladder friendly! Unfortunately, I don't have access to any machines where I live, and I'm too frugal to buy plus don't have room to store them for myself. I lift weights, dumb-bells, three nights a week (Power 90 Sculpt) and was walking dogs (aerobics) on the other two days. Now that I'm working I still lift the weights three days but then do a "Sketchers" aerobics workout for the other three. Taking one day off a week. Not only does it help my body, but even more-so, I think it does a world of good for my attitude. Do you find that too? Vicki

signature: ~ Stacey

contents: Here's something I've been trying to figure out. It seems that, with the relatively common incidence of IC, some professional athletes would be bound to develop it at some point during their careers. It also seems that, given how hard professional athletes push and how much they demand of their body and muscles, that IC would cause some of these athletes to cut their careers short or at least take a break, considering how even a moderate exercise can aggravate things for a lot of us. However, I can't recall ever hearing of a professional athlete being affected by IC. I wonder if this because a) they simply use cover stories, or b) there is something about being an elite athlete that prevents IC, or c) something else?

When I do cardio exercise, after about 10 to 15 minutes my IC feels a lot better. So if you're exercising all the time, my guess is that maybe it could help reduce the risk of developing IC. Who knows. But my guess is that some athletes probably have developed IC and they just don't announce it to the world.
signature: Update! Feeling much better these days and no longer on any meds! Pelvic pain began July 2008. Urinary frequency began in November of 2008. IC Doctor says I'm one of the worst frequency patients he has had. Things that helped me the most: IC Diet, Elavil (30mg), Elmiron since June '09 (500mg/day). Previously tried and quit: one series of six instillations, Neurontin, Ditropan, Oxytrol, electric stimulation to the pelvic floor, Desert Harvest Aloe, Cystoprotek and Flomax, Hydroxyzine (50mg), Alesse Birth control (this helped my period flares when my symptoms used to be bad), PTNS (I think this helped). contents: Originally posted by musiclover When I do cardio exercise, after about 10 to 15 minutes my IC feels a lot better. So if you're exercising all the time, my guess is that maybe it could help reduce the risk of developing IC. Who knows. But my guess is that some athletes probably have developed IC and they just don't announce it to the world. Interesting... I originally asked because I can't do anything physical without making my bladder pain worse - elliptical, swimming, even extended walking - and while it sounds like that's an extreme case, it sounds like its fairly common for strenous activity to make things worse for folks. I would think that this would make it very difficult to maintain a career as an athlete. I also wonder the same thing about singers, as singing does the same thing to me. But maybe that's just my own unique piece of luck. Mostly, I'm just very curious as to why there's such an extreme variability between cases when it comes to the effect of exercise, or basically doing anything that involves using the core muscles in any way. Some it seems to help, some it seems to hurt, and then there's me, for which it's my primary driver of bladder pain. Very confusing, considering the inflammation in IC is inside the bladder, and should not theoretically be connected to any muscle injuries/disfunctions outside of the bladder.

Originally posted by KatieB My PT looked at my lack of muscle and told me "Ya know, we've got to build up your muscle tone all over your body. I never have IC/PFD patients here who have lots of muscles". Hmmm... Bullsh*t. Your PT is going on total anecdotal evidence. I know someone (a woman) who's done 2 Ironmans who has both IC and Chrons disease.

contents: Katie I am soo sorry for your pain. Have you ever tried yoga? You can take it as easy as you need when you go. We have a studio by us that does the hot yoga and I love it. You can let the instructor know about IC and they can alter the positions for you. I know that this is such a frustrating disease and there are many days for me that are what I call "too much" days. As a side note, I have actually heard of MANY pro athletes having overactive bladder no so much IC or developing it. May be due to the stress or anxiety of competitive sports. I don't know. Amanda

Maybe those pro athletes have overactive bladder, or prostate problems, and that is how a lot of men are under or misdiagnosed for IC. They probably never heard of it although a lot of them have some of best doctors around, who probably also don't know what it is. Would think if you have really good muscle tone before an IC DX it may be helpful in allowing you to stay more active and not develop PFD. For those who it has just hit hard who are not athletes, the inactivity can eat away at muscle tone quickly. Pro athletes have many hidden things going on physically that we will never know until they retire. And how many pros are going to admit they have an "overactive bladder" anyway? Kinda embarrasing for them (shouldn't be, but everyone thinks oh gee, he/she has to wear depends, giggle giggle) It's a stigma that needs to be abolished and maybe there are some pros who have IC but just won't come out of the closet.

Rather harsh Josie, Katie is just relaying what her pt noted, perhaps the patients that this particular physical therapist has seen have lack of muscle tone. I know in the beginning I was in great physical shape, now, not so much. I have severe IC and have been in a lot of pain for a few years. Even though I was in great shape I still developed PFD (it has nothing to do with what kind of shape you are in previously) Let's all remember that we are here to support each other. Everyone of us sees different Drs, PTs and other medical authorities. IC is not very well understood so everyone is going to have differing opinions, but using that language and tone really isn't necessary, is it? Originally posted by JosieWales Bullsh*t. Your PT is going on total anecdotal evidence. I know someone (a woman) who's done 2 Ironmans who has both IC and Chrons disease.

contents: Originally posted by landangel I was a professional figure skater for years and an active ballet dancer. I am in amazing shape and still developed IC. I developed IC from the only UTI that I ever had. I still skate and dance today. Wow, that's great for you. I'm jealous! So is your pain just completely unaffected by your physical movements?

Hi Josh, As of right now I am in somewhat of a remission I just had a little guy 5 months ago and only had relief after having him. I am BF him, so I am sure that all of my symptoms will return after I stop. Pain was never a major symptom I had, just major pressure and the constant "I have to pee feeling" and the slight feeling of pinching/irritation. I knew that something was really wrong with me and kept annoying my Urologist early on. He finally tested my for IC (which I suspected) and found that yes, my bladder was irritated. I would say that I have mild IC, although it sometimes feels worse. The pain completely goes with excercise I find and it is just the pressure that bothers me when skating and jumping around. I refuse to let this disease take away the things I am passionate about. Amanda

Originally posted by KatieB I'm so glad for you as I've been unable to do hardly any physical activity due to pain. That's what brought up the comment from my PT. She wasn't kidding about my muscle tone. I have none. I've lost approx 50 lbs in the last year and a half and at last check weighed 93. I think I would feel better if I could do some physical activity but my IC pain and weakness holds me back. Katie, if you don't mind me asking... do you have either a history of abdominal/pelvic surgeries preceding your IC or tendonitis? (i.e. tennis elbow, plantar fasciatis, etc.) It seems that some people with IC - like you and I - are much, much, much more affected by movement and activity than others. The fact that some people's pain is helped by exercise while I can be almost pain-free and then get sore after walking for a few minutes leads me to believe that there must be some neuro-musco-skelatal connection to bladder inflammation in some cases. The two theories I've come across are a) adhesions from a surgery and b) tendonitis, so I'm curious if you would have been predisposed to either of those...

While I certainly was never on a professional level, I was a competitive swimmer from the age of 4 until I came down with IC when I was 18. I had planned to swim in college but had to quit due to the pain I was constantly in and the fact that I never slept well and therefore could not wake up for early morning practices. I wonder in regards to professional athletes if there is not a certain amount of self-selection, meaning the athlete has to give up the sport before they reach the professional level or at least before they become well known.

Re: this comment: My PT looked at my lack of muscle and told me "Ya know, we've got to build up your muscle tone all over your body. I never have IC/PFD patients here who have lots of muscles". Hmmm... __________________ I was on my bike pretty much every day & I still got IC. I had lots of muscles!

contents: Originally posted by CarolinTexas Could anyone tell me if Yoga poses help your PFD and IC? I do a couple of stretching poses in my normal routine, but don't know if they are the right ones. I'd love to get started on them right away, so please let me know the names of the poses you do. Also, I see there are some books and DVD's (on this website) that specifically address Yoga for IC/PFD. My budget only allows for one and I want to choose correctly. Any recommendations on any of them that helped you? Thanks! I appreciate your help! Hi Caroline. I think I have more of Chronic Pelvic Pain Syndrome than IC but they're obviously similar. Like you I'm on a limited budget and can't even afford yoga lessons until I get my new job but that may not be for another year or so. Have you found any yoga that helps you? I baraly know anything about yoga but I know PT is helpful for CPPS so I was hoping to try some things on my own. Any suggestions? Thanks

These are the yoga poses that I do from a book I have and they are all IC safe. You can do a search I am sure and find directions on each pose. Corpse Pose (sounds terrible) Goddess Pose Knee Down Twist Wall V stretch Pigeon Hamstring Wall pose Mermaid Stretch Ankle to knee Pretzel Pose Z stretch Child's pose Prayer squat The prayer squat is so great, it helps my pelvic floor relax without my even trying! I do it as often as I can. My book also has some pilates in it but I tried it once and couldn't move for a few days, didn't work for me. These poses really do work well. Be sure you read about breathing techniques. I also have a relaxation cd that I bought from my physical therapist and it helps a lot too. One more thing on the yoga, from what I have read, the yoga that we need to do is Hatha yoga. Hatha is a lot of breathing and slow stretches. My ymca has some beginner yoga classes but I think it is too hard for me to do. They have another one that I might try. If you live near a YMCA they great places to look for classes and they will give you a discount on membership if you need that. I am lucky, we have a fantastic Y by my house. Sandra

contents: Originally posted by sailawaygrl These are the yoga poses that I do from a book I have and they are all IC safe. You can do a search I am sure and find directions on each pose. Corpse Pose (sounds terrible) Goddess Pose Knee Down Twist Wall V stretch Pigeon Hamstring Wall pose Mermaid Stretch Ankle to knee Pretzel Pose Z stretch Child's pose Prayer squat The prayer squat is so great, it helps my pelvic floor relax without my even trying! I do it as often as I can. My book also has some pilates in it but I tried it once and couldn't move for a few days, didn't work for me. These poses really do work well. Be sure you read about breathing techniques. I also have a relaxation cd that I bought from my physical therapist and it helps a lot too. One more thing on the yoga, from what I have read, the yoga that we need to do is Hatha yoga. Hatha is a lot of breathing and slow stretches. My ymca has some beginner yoga classes but I think it is too hard for me to do. They have another one that I might try. If you live near a YMCA they great places to look for classes and they will give you a discount on membership if you need that. I am lucky, we have a fantastic Y by my house. Sandra Hey thanks that sounds great. Like I said I've been rediagnosed with Chronic Pelvic Pain Syndrome but so many of the symptoms are the same as IC and I definitely have muscular issues so I'll give these poses a try. Anything I guess. What symptoms does the yoga help you with the most. I've had so many symptoms come and go that I don't even know anymore which ones I want to go away the most. But I guess at this point I'm hoping to relieve the frequency of urination and the muscular soreness I have below my waist. Thanks again. Brendan
I am 99% sure I have IC. I'm seeing ob on Tues. and hope to get more tests done to rule out other things. Is there anyone here who's remained a triathlete/runner after dx?
signature: --Josie

contents: Josie, I really feel for you. You have worked yourself into almost a panic from what I'm seeing here. First of all you need to be religiously following the IC diet. No deviation. You should be drinking a lot of water and when you go to your doctor on Tuesday unless he/she has experience with IC, you should ask for a referral to a uro or uro/gyno. It's possible to scare yourself silly by "Internet doctoring". I know this for a fact because I've done it. Most of the people here (with some exceptions) are in the process of finding a treatment plan that will work for them. Which is why you will find some really bad, scary stories. The people who have found that plan and are feeling better and getting on with their lives, no longer need the support of this board. There's no reason to believe you are "done" with anything. I know it's frustrating and gives a lot of anxiety and fear but the only person who can diagnose you is a doctor. Other people can only give you their experiences. So my advice is to try to take some deep breaths and realize that IC MAY be your problem, it may not. If it is, then you will go on your journey to find out the treatment plan that will work for you and allow to enjoy your life. I'll keep you in my thoughts.

contents: KAtie, Thanks, you're right. I'm a little freaked out. The thing is, I can't see what else it could be that would be any better than IC, as I said in another thread. But, it could be, I know. I need to stop reading about personal experiences and start working on modifying my own diet. I guess I need to go to the store...a lot of my staples are on the trigger list!
signature: --Josie

contents: Just wanted to say that I agree with Katie. My diagnosis changed from probable IC in 2004 to PFD in 2007, and throughout it all, I continued to exercise. I was put on elavil, which helped me so much, that now I'm off of it, and most days, feeling pretty good. (I'm not a triathlete, mind you, but I do some pretty advanced workouts.) So guess what I'm trying to say is that there is a very wide range of experiences on these boards, but like Katie said, many of those who have found treatment plans that work for them and are now feeling good no longer need the board's support... they've moved on. Good luck at your doctor's appt. : )

Thank you, delfin...I'm a little better today. I've read some other posts, including responses to my questions elsewhere here, and I think it could be sensitive bladder due to the UTIs, all the lemon in my water, and all the coffee (which I sort of became addicted to overnight just a few months ago).
signature: --Josie

contents: running I am a runner also and enjoy running when my IC is not too angry. I am currently doing the Insanity workout challenge and signed up for a 5K in March and hoping my bladder will behave. Do try elimating the bladder irritating foods like soft drinks, alcohol, coffee, acidic foods, etc and increase your water intake. That may be all that you will need to do. Even ppl with healthy bladders can experience frequency and urgency with these. It will likely take a few days or possibly longer for your bladder to recover. Keep us updated on how you are doing.
signature: I have had IC since age 19 after a series of UTI's. Had a few remissions. No other health issues. I'm now 30. Trying to get my symptoms under control again, slowly getting there! On IC diet and artesian water, trying to eat more natural and less processed foods. Lots of hot baths (sometimes with baking soda and tea tree oil). My mom was recently diagnosed with IC with symptoms begining the end of 2010 and now my 8 year old son is having burning & stinging discomfort with and following urination and urgency/frequency. MEDS Elavil 50mg evening Atarax 25mg 1-2x daily Elmiron 2 tabs 2x daily Baclofen 10mg 2-3x daily (muscle relaxer prescribed after a urodynamics test) Orth Tri Cyclen Cystoprotek 2 tabs 2x daily, restarted 01/11 Fish oil tabs 2x daily, started 01/11 MSM 2x daily, started 01/11 Prelief as needed marshmallow root & chamomile tea (tastes better together) Seems like ALOT of meds (Rx & OTC) but I'm desperate in getting relief and I am feeling better.

contents: Josie, you will see many on here who continue with their hiking, excersize, etc. Sometimes you might have to slow down a bit, but don't stop, pay attention to what your body says. You will get in a rythym that will be right for YOU. You may have to do other things that keep you agile, like yoga, stretching, but there is no need to give it up. If that is your passion, keep it going. May be shorter sessions, but never give up. Think you will agree it is much more difficult to get back into it after stopping completely. Go with the flow, you are obviously in good shape and want to stay there. Good luck GF, and hang in there. Jill, wife of Bob

I decided that after the New Year I needed to start eating better and get some of this excess flab. The last few days I've been drinking more water and I also believe I'm getting rid of my water weight. While doing all of this I've had a slight increase in that "gotta go" feeling. I don't know if it's the new diet or something else. Wondering if anybody else is going through the same thing.
HI. What diet are you on? A low Carb. diet will cause us to go more often and in many cases can cause many problems. I hope you are feeling better soon Hugs, Ziggy
The low carb diet did a number on me too. How much are you drinking? I do drink six to eight cups a day, but usually not more than that. Donna

contents: I'm basically trying to lower the amount of carbs I'm eating and trying to just plain eat better. I'm not in agony or anything, just going #1 more often. I'm pretty sure it's the water weight since I'm down a few pounds since starting this week. If I still feel "poopy", I'll cut back on the veggies a little.

Yesterday I ate a pretty blah diet and that seems to have helped calm things down a bit. The nice this is that last night I didn't have to get up to go to the bathroom; first time all week. I'd really like to lose some weight, but not if I have to suffer with every salad or veggie dish I eat.

Had hub on low carb diet for about 2 months. He lost some weight but has other health issues, so it is difficult. Think any rapid diet change/weight loss/gain is probably detrimental. A person with no health issues can take that, but it seems that ICes and IBSers have probs. Some carbs are necessary, do a little googling, you'd be surprised. You can't deprive your body of carbs for too long, the chemistry changes. Pee needs some kind of dilution, would think, and a pure NO carb diet may hurt more than it helps. Hub did seem to have more pain while on it, and we eased back into whole grain healthy carbs, and some pasta (shoot, no tomato sauce) now and then. It's tough, and everyone reacts differently. Marshmallow root tea with a little honey really soothes his bladder and is comforting for him. Omelettes over a little chopped iceberg and cukes are great. Do some searching on here there are actually organic pure vegans. Good luck to you. Jill, wife of Bob

I did not know where else to put this. I think I remember reading a few members here were nutrition specialist or related. Anyone have suggestions for things I can eat to get some weight back. With the break up of my marriage and being really sick with IC and more so with the stress I have lost 15lbs. I am feeling great now, working out a little and trying to eat good but I have some things working against me. I am living in a hotel right now so all I have to cook is a microwave. I can't stand to eat to much process foods or eat out much for same reason. I need to gain back 10lbs to be at an ideal weight and look good. I've added almonds and walnuts to eat in between meals (and when missing meals) I have also added Ensure muscle health drink for some extra protein but I'm not gaining anything. I'm worried that with my living situation and fear of process foods and no good way to cook that I could even lose more weight. Any suggestions on high calorie, high protein foods I can eat ? I don't want to have to go for the GNC bulk protein stuff that body builder use but if I lose any more weight I might not have a choice.

contents: It's possible that you're getting adequate protein, but just need more calories --- if I could I'd send you 15 of MY pounds. You might want to think about adding some calorie foods like cream cheese and crackers and ice cream. Donna

contents: I have the same problem Over the last 2 years I have lost approx 40 lbs. I have had MANY doc's assuming I have some sort of ED. It's SO frustrating. I would give anything to add on 20 lbs thinking it would make me feel better in general and possibly help my IC. I've seen a nutrionist but she knows nothing about IC. Knowing it's not good for my cholesterol I still put butter or margarine on pretty much everything. Til I found the IC diet I was eating chocolate and many of the "no-no" foods thinking this would help me with my weight gain issues. I'm SO tired of doc's trying to explore whether or not I have an eating disorder. It's just so frustrating and tiresome. Sometimes I feel as though I go on the defensive which I know doesn't help my cause. I've tried bene-protien and bene-calorie with little to no success. I feel I eat what I can but at times the pain is SO bad that I feel as though no matter how hungry I am, the only thing I can think of is going to the bathroom for a little relief. Which puts food on the back burner. I know that this weight loss has not helped my IC, but I also think the IC has not helped my weight gain efforts. I'm not sure I have any advice for you except to try the bene-protein and the bene-calorie. I had to order them off the Internet from Walgreens.com. They may help you. Good luck to you and I understand. Katie

contents: Almond butter! I get the Trader Joe's one that's just almonds. Also you can put mozzarella on your sandwiches - heat it in the microwave for 15-20 seconds to melt it a bit before adding the lettuce or whatever. Go out to eat breakfasts - pancakes, eggs, & hashbrowns should do it. Vanilla milkshakes with real ice cream at restaurant sizes. Wow, was I shocked when I saw the weight watchers point totals.. Yikes! Sadly, I gained 40 pounds with IC. I've lost 15, but still have 30 to go Good luck! I know it's not easy to have people judging you for your weight, either too much or too little...

contents: I have the same problem -- A handful of years ago I lost 30lbs; nothing going on, just lost it! I have gain maybe 5lbs back (everyone has told me they will give me 10--of course if you add 10 from every person I would weight over 300lbs!! Don't want that. My problem is that I am probably not getting enough calories, so the doctors put me on Ensure (or the store brand) I can tolerate very well the vanilla. I can't however drink the high protein or whatever else is out there as it puts me in a slight flare.

Mary, If you find you can't get enough calories you may want to try the BeneCalorie It's just like BeneFiber in that it's a tasteless, odorless powder that can be mixed into any food (such as your liquid drinks). I ordered it online from Walgreens as they don't carry it in the stores. Good luck to you. I know what you're going through.

contents: Mary124 - Yeah those Ensure drinks are good for a calorie boost and they actually taste okay. KatieB I might try the bene-protein and the bene-calorie if I don't gain a few lbs over next few weeks. I'm looking for Thanksgiving to help me out first

contents: Thanks to some suggestions here I have officially gained 2lbs and it appears to be of mostly muscle. I weighed myself this morning and voila teetered on almost 2lbs more. The milkshakes, Ensure muscle health, walnuts, almonds, yogurt, bananas and a couple of choice pieces of beef and fish along with some small workouts have done it. Now I just need to keep that up while carefully watching the weight to keep from adding too fast and to be able to stop the gain when just right. I feel really great and I would almost bet that next blood test my hyperactive thyroid might be normal or only slightly high and my cholesterol will be good and I will be 5-6 lbs more lean muscle I hope. I have this goal of becoming really healthy as I can be with IC, looking good and then there is this stunningly beautiful red head I want to impress.

contents: My aunt had trouble gaining weight after having severe stomach problems. She ate a lot of pasta, potatoes and ice cream and managed to gain some weight. Those were pretty easy on her tummy.

Well I am now have 5lbs of myself back. A steady diet of walnuts, almonds, pumpkin seeds, yogurt, bananas, chicken and turkey, raw broccoli & carrots has helped, of course I eat other stuff but try to get the latter items in every day. My wife made me a pan of delicious home made macaroni and cheese which helped load up some calories. I drink a couple of high protein drinks, Ensure or sometimes Naked Protein which is a natural fruit drink with whey and soy protein - I like it better than the Ensure because I can pronounce all the ingredients. I am thinking I might need to raise the goal from 10lbs to 15lbs because I am gaining mostly muscle and it weighs more than fat therefore I might still look a little thinner than I would like with just adding the 10lbs. So I'm looking a little better with the 5lbs added on, feeling great with the good foods and workouts and I can't help but think that all that is good for my IC as well.

contents: Good JOB!!! I see the next 5 lbs in your future, as tomorrow is Thanksgiving!! Eat long and often, my friend!! I won't be cooking or traveling so we'll be having Thanksgiving dinner and leftovers from a great a caterer I've known for years. I'm hoping that stacks a few lbs on me too!!! I hope whatever you do, you have a great and thankful holiday!!!
signature: Katie-46 yr old female dx'd with IC after 15 years of symptoms off and on long term antibiotic use, GERD,IBS and now IC diet, gallbladder removed, endometrial ablation w/tubes tied Lexapro-20 mg Aciphex Ambien-as needed Percocet-7.5 up to 3 per day as needed Valium-10 mg x2 per day Phenergan-1 at night Prelief w/everything Now recovering from acute pancreatitis Currents treatments that help somewhat: Heating pad Hot baths Ice Being VERY still while lying down with legs elevated

contents: Gaining few lbs/getting healthy and crazy in love Hey Donna, Katie, Yeah things are looking up and yes I see some pounds coming my way tomorrow as I am going to my wife's moms house for dinner and of course this lovely red head will be there. Wow you know it just really hit me this morning not only do I love my wife but I am head over heels in love with her like it is brand new, like I just met her a few times and I have butterflies in my stomach trying to impress her. I have been buying clothes and working out to look good, all nervous feeling, just ran out to get a new belt for tomorrow because I'm so crazy about her I have to do everything I can to look good and get her to notice me more - this is really kind of crazy but it feels so good to feel so alive - scary as heck but good. Now of course I'm starting to think I need another pair of new shoes for tomorrow, a rugged boot look I think will work. Even if it does not work out like I hope, I'll be more healthy, look better and feel so much more alive again. Mike
signature: - Symptoms: started July 2007 New pain medication regimen has things under pretty good control for many weeks now. Exercise and eating healthy along with pain control have given me new life. Current Symptoms: pain in testicles, penis and general groin area. Other discomfort that is beyond pain, a maddening crazed feeling that defies description. New pain medication has most symptoms under good control. Frequency and urgency was a problem the first year or so but frequency is normal now. Diagnosed: Prostatitis first then IC by Cysto/Hydro Current Meds: Hydroxyzine 25mg 3 x day, Pyridium 200mg 3 x day (off 3-4 days on 3-4 days), Oxycodone, Norvasc (BP). Past medications and treatments: Protatitis and IC diet strict for 6 months, no change. Still watch diet although nothing seems to help or make worse. Have tried reverse, ate lemons to see if worse but no change at all. Meds tried - Amitriptyline, Detrol, Flomax, Cipro, Doxycycline. many other antibiotics, Uroxatrol, Vicoprofen, Elmiron (6 months at $320 mo nothing), Aloe, Cysprotek, Glucosamine, Marshmallow, various oils, tons more stuff to strange and numerous to recount. Hydrodistension for diagnostic was horrible for two weeks after I was sure I must be going to die because nothing could feel that way and let you live.

contents: I just seen these postings, I think I will buy some of this bene-calorie. tomorrow I will be cooking all day (probably some tonight as there is too much to do and its just the 4 of us) but my grown sons informed me if I even forget one little item than there is going to be a war in the household and I will lose! I usually make a lot of food this way we can eat all weekend long without me cooking!

Hey Some Guy So glad to hear you are doing better in all departments. LOL. Sure turkey day will add a pound or two and perhaps mominlaw will let you take some home. Those leftover turkey sambos with all the fixings can add up, and really turkey is pretty healthful in most departments, just eliminate the skin. Whole grain bread, of course. Another goodie is a smoothie with whole milk and whatever fruit you tolerate, bananas and/or berries, at any time of the day. Throw in some honey for extra cals. My hub is trying to lose, but I'm trying to gain so imagine that. Carbs, carbs, if you don't have a diabetes inclination. Keep the salads going, and add some chunks of cheese. Nibble all day, keep the blood sugar constant, and you may find your appetite actually improves. Lots of small meals rather than one big one, which turns most off who are looking to gain. Good luck tomorrow in all aspects, we are all pulling for you.

Thanks! - yes many things are improving for me, I feel great. I look forward to a bunch of turkey and stuffing and seeing everyone. I did just buy some whole milk the other day so I could start eating cereal for breakfast with some nuts, banana and maca powder sprinkled on it giving me an extra meal to start the day since I usually skip a real breakfast. I agree about eating many small meals and think that can work as well adding weight as losing weight. I know a guy who lost 40+ lbs by doing a eat something small every 30 minutes diet, he said it worked so well because eating that way he never became super hungry and his other theory was his body did not try to store fat because it was getting food so often. I hope everyone has a great Thanksgiving. Mike

contents: As of today I am now 7lbs towards my 10lb goal. I think I will have to up the goal another 2-5 lbs because the weight I am getting back is muscle and if I can keep that going then a little more weight will look and feel good. At this point I am pleased because i don't look to thin anymore, not that I was really thin just thinner than I should have been - anyway getting close to just right.

contents: vanilla milkshakes When I'm in a bad flare I LOVE vanilla milkshakes from mcdonalds.....turns out they have a bunch of calories and fat. I had a very bad flare a couple of weeks ago that put me in the hospital and my dad brought me one everyday. I left the hospital with 5 more pounds than i went in with lol.
signature: I am happy to say that I am off all pain medication and currently take Neurontin and Uribel for my IC. I went to the Cleveland Clinic Chronic Pain Rehabilitation program and it saved my life!! I still get bad flare ups but it turns out I was on so many narcotics it was making my pain worse.

contents: Good Job!!! Well I guess you're "picking up what I'm putting down"!!! I've lost about 10 lbs in the last 2 months. This IC has kicked my butt and I just feel like I'm in a downward spiral. I was really hoping that taking the Elavil would help me put on a few lbs. But I just can't take it. And lately the nausea from pain is keeping me from eating as much as I want and should eat. I go back to my pain doc on Friday and hopefully we can figure something out.

contents: Just be careful with those fast food shakes tho. That is not real ice cream in there, but rehydrated stuff. So glad it is tolerable for you and indeed loaded with calories, but if you start having problems after a bunch of them, you may want to think twice. If you can handle them, FANTASTIC! Just a heads up, don't want to spoil your fun...

weight loss I actually have the same problem. I have found that eating just isn't much fun anymore. About the only thing I can eat in a resturant is a breakfast. I am not skinny, but I don't want to lose any more weight. I too eat nuts if skipping a meal adding some raisens. Lots of yogurt. I found a blueberry juice I can drink that is 200 calories. I eat ice cream. Also try and do some resistance training. that keeps me a bit hungry when I train. Biking indoors also. You have some great suggestions. Ice cream probably being my favorite. Eggs, and nuts for protien. I don't think I would try a mix, I would be afraid of all the soy protien in it. Soy being a IC trigger for me. Good Luck!
signature: Sweet Texas Hugs, jane2

contents: Have also found that smaller meals are better. Bob cannot even finish a meal, and I do not overload him. Seems the fullness makes him more uncomfortable. His appetite is hardly there, except for dinner. But he's trying to lose weight. Just do not have a bunch of junk in the house any longer, only things he can tolerate (I know he cheats, but not very often). And the tummy fiber does help him. And marshmallow root tea. Wow, you can tolerate yogurt? Good for you! Happy 2011 all. Jill, wife of Bob

losing weight' yogurt I usually stick with plain yogurt but take a probotic as my URO suggested. I usually add Splenda and blueberries, some organic cereal and nuts- I have lived on that while visiting people that didn't have my kind of food. I try and make those kinds of dining fun and full of conversation. I want people to know I stil enjoy their company, just have food restrictions. I usually just say I am allergic. We went to China Buffet & the owner kept watching me on leaving I asked him if there was any food there without soy. he said no. I thanked him and told him I was just visiting with my friends. Guess I did that backwards. I am still learning .. ..but still laughing. Texas hugs, jane2
signature: Sweet Texas Hugs, jane2

contents: Wouldn't it be nice if it was a great thing to be fat instead of skinny? I know some places it is like that BUT I AM SOOOOOOOOOOOO TIRED OF BEING FAT...THINKING ABOUT BEING FAT...CRYING ABOUT BEING FAT............Part of me is saying "Just give up. Stay at home and not have to worry about it anymore". I am running out of cloths and if I did stay home who would care? Slim fast is the only thing I can actually eat/drink that does not affect the bladder anymore and most days I don't eat much but still.........nothing........guess I don't have the "crave" to exercise. Did get my treadmill back (stupid people poked holes in the arm) but haven't been on it yet. Heard on the radio if you mop nad wash windows for just one hour you burn 350 calories! Maybe that is why when I had a clean house I was skinny? Maybe the IC did turn me into a lazy slob? I don't know. Guess I feel like screaming. AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA...ok....hope I didn't burst anyones ear drums. Was also thinking maybe I should change my name from waterflow to: complainsalot or whinerwinner.

weight Waterflow, I have to say, you are really funny! I know that doesn't make sense given your post, but some people, and you are one of them, can be having a fit and be funny at the same time! Maybe we should move to a place where plumpness is okay and fashion doesn't matter. Where is that btw? If you find it, I'll go with you! Stay cool, girl and the heck with the weight. So there! Laurie
The bladder has affected my brain in more ways then one. ha

It's all in what you make it. There are lots of fat people in the world. Many are perfectly content. We are not all supposed to be the same size. The secret is to love yourself no matter what your size. Hey you have struggles just like everyone else. There are plenty of people out there who don't mind fat people. Actually there are probably more fat people than skinny people in the world. Remember this attractiveness comes from confidence. Work on being a good person. My wife always says she would rather be remembered as a good person, a good mom, a good wife and a good friend than be a size 4. She's a size 12 and very pretty. There is more to life than weight. Keep in mind that, not all skinny people are attractive. I've seen plenty of bony unattractive skinny women. You are probably your worst critic. Try to get some exercise, limit sweets and eat healthy when you can. Moderation is key. Most of us have limited control over our weight. Work on being healthy and stay away from the scale.
signature: What helps Elmiron 200 mg bid Wellbutrin XL anxiety and depression Oxybutynin ER 10 mg Yoga tried and failed Cardura, Hytrin, Flomax, Prosed DS, Vesicare, Pyridium, Cystoprotek (caused GI problems), Lyrica, Pamelor "We can't wait until the storm is over. We need to learn to dance in the rain."

contents: Some of the nicest people I know are either very fat or very skinny! Just be yourself and you'll be fine. If your clothes don't fit, try doing some shopping at a St. Vincent dePaul or Goodwill store --- they have some very nice things and they are very cheap. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: I can see being over weight is a concern for you. Frankly many of my friends are heavy and I don't even notice it most of the time. It's their sweet soul and their friendship I enjoy. Have you ever talk to your Doctor about help with your weight? My Daughter was only slightly over weight but it bothered her back. The Doctor gave her a wonderful diet and some pills to help. Wow I could not belive how great she looks now and her pain is much less. The diet was filled with very nice foods and not expensive. He never put her on an exercise program. She is a teacher so too worn out most of the time to do much walking or working out. I don't believe weight is important if one is healthy for the most part. Since it does seem to upset you talking to your Doctor is a good idea in my opinion. Many Hugs, Ziggy

Have you heard about the fat acceptance movement? http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fat_acceptance_movement http://www.naafaonline.com/dev2/the_issues/index.html There's no need to be ashamed. We're conditioned to feel ashamed of our bodies and it's just not right. It's one thing to be concerned about being healthy/eating healthy and another to be concerned about what other people think of our appearance. IMHO OF COURSE!

We would be great back in the Roman days I guess. But, what I thought was ironic is that one of my friends just had by-pass surgery to lose weight. She probably weights around 400 lbs and she said she wants to be about my size and weight. Which I think I'm fat. My IC days put about 30 lbs on me. My hope is to start exercising one of these days. Anyway.....are we ever truely satisfied with our weight? many hugs and blessings
signature: MARY Serenity isn't freedom from the storm.....it's peace within the storm.....I had my sick bladder removed Jan 7th 2010.....Even though I had many complications.......I would do it again in a moment.....I have no regrets......Sunflowers in memory of my sister who passed March 14th 2010......they were her favorite....

contents: I am 5' 4" tall and when the IC started 12 years ago I weighed 125-130 which was fine. Had lots of pretty dresses to wear when I went out in public or some times when I went to the store I wore jeans but nice shirt. I never wore jeans to the doctors. Always dresses. I gained weight over the years because I took the stupid anti-depressant pills and now I weigh 200 pounds. I do not like it. I have nothing to wear and I have gone to the 2 second hand stores for cloths but all they have are ugly fat women dresses. The good ones there and in other stores are for skinny women. I think about being fat every day. Sometimes I fall asleep thinking about and sometimes I wake up thinking about it but the worse for me is when family members comment on it. I eat everything...that is why I'm fat. (No one knows about the pills I took over the years) I'm lazy, I eat all the cookies in the house ONLY becasue I actually ate one. Just seems like my whole life really I have been called fat and been on a diet. My first diet started in 6th grade when a boy called me fat. I am doing the slim fast diet but it seems unless I can exercise like normal (which I can't unless I push myself so much the fibro and IC pain are unbearable) the weight will not come off now. I'm in menopause or afterpause now. Not really sure what pause of life I am in at the moment but the pounds just will not come off. Even if I don't eat hardly at all. I even thought about having that surgery done but when I saw what you went through on tv decided against it. I never judged anyone by how fat/thin they were or how they were dressed but I get judged that way all the time by family. Family you would think would help me but I just get put down. Yes, I do try to ignore it most of the time but sometimes I just can't. Guess over the last 12 years I lost more and more of my old life and body to where I basically am just a shell. Sounds dumb but true. Will only be going to Uro's twice a month now for the instill. Decided going every week was not worth the hassel I had to go through to borrow a vechile. If need be I will stop all together and actually if I did I would not have to worry about being fat or having cloths to wear. Would only go to the store once a month and no place else. Jereme, you are a one of a kind guy. The only guy I have ever meant that didn't see me as fat has been my Uro and he saw me for many years in the beginning when I was thin.

The main thing about weight is how you feel about yourself. For me, when I try to lose weight, I do several things. I DO NOT buy temptations, ie. cookies, sweets, etc. I keep a diary and write down everything single thing I put in my mouth, even if it is just one bite. You would be surprised at how much you really do eat that way and it makes you be more aware of what you are doing.I have a dog so I walk her LOTS! I hate exercising but I do like to walk and I garden when the weather is good. Give your self credit for the positive things you do. It took you a long time to put the weight on , so remember it is not going to come off overnight. You can do it.

For some odd reason, I lost about 40lbs about 2 years ago, never could find the cause. My Primary care doc said as long as I feel good and am healthy (pretty much so) and maintaining what weight I have than I am ok, but some of the other docs said no that I should be concern. I know that this is not what you wanted, but as long as you are healthy and happy then don't worry about it. If it does bother you, which apparently it does than talk to your doctor about diet/exercise program. A lot of my co-workers are heavy but they have a very sweet personality.

I struggle with weight too. I gained about 10 pounds after I stopped working, but my pain is so irritating that I just lay in bed all day. I've gained 30 pounds since my pain first started, so I don't know what to do either... I am trying to embrace my curvy shape, but my doctors said that they were worried about my weight gain, and told me to lose some weight. I am not fat, I am just really curvy. Honestly I see nothing wrong with my size 12 jeans. I guess the world just isn't ready to accept that big is beautiful...
signature: Newly Diagnosed and learning to cope.

contents: why can't fat people be in Originally posted by SSaxton1989 I struggle with weight too. I gained about 10 pounds after I stopped working, but my pain is so irritating that I just lay in bed all day. I've gained 30 pounds since my pain first started, so I don't know what to do either... I am trying to embrace my curvy shape, but my doctors said that they were worried about my weight gain, and told me to lose some weight. I am not fat, I am just really curvy. Honestly I see nothing wrong with my size 12 jeans. I guess the world just isn't ready to accept that big is beautiful... Oh sweetie, I haven't been able to get into size 12 in years! Not to worry about it .If you're healthy,(heart, cholesterol, BP), then maybe right at this time you need to just buy those size 12 jeans and not feel crummy about it. You'll loose the weight when you're ready, if you want to. Laurie

Originally posted by bubbe1 Oh sweetie, I haven't been able to get into size 12 in years! Not to worry about it .If you're healthy,(heart, cholesterol, BP), then maybe right at this time you need to just buy those size 12 jeans and not feel crummy about it. You'll loose the weight when you're ready, if you want to. Laurie You know, I think that is exactly right. Go for healthy and if you are or get to where you feel you are healthy and feel good it will also make you look good whether you are a bit heavy or a bit light.

If Romero zombies I think we are all good, just walk fast away from them but the new fast ones well that is a whole different ball game

contents: Zombies Originally posted by SSaxton1989 I'm talking like Zombieland fast super hungry zombies... I just need to do some cardio I guess. Laurie Ah, they do like poodles BUT! if the poodle only gets bitten and escapes then it becomes a zombie - then you have a quandary because your cute poodle still looks cute and you want to hug it but it is a zombie poodle so you dare not.

contents: big hugs. I know how you feel.
signature: 'The will of God will never take you where the Grace of God will not protect you.'

contents: Exercise is my therapy I'm a fitness instructor and have been for 12 years. I'm not "skinny" by any stretch of the imagination. For my BMI, I'm obese...BUT BMI isn't the best measure for an athletic person. I'm 5'2 1/2, and weight about 138lbs (Size 8ish). When I was first diagnosed with IC the medicines I was on increased my weight from 118lbs to 179lbs in almost 6 months. I feel all of your pain. One thing I learned during that time was the less I moved the worse it felt when I did... so starting out slowly...I taught again..., then increased the times I did the "activity" for the sole purpose of getting exercise...all other activities (cleaning, walking the dog etc.) were bonus burns.... Now, 5 years later, I'm not back down to my ideal weight, but I feel healthy and the exercise time is actually when my IC symptoms are the least bothersome... I have to wear a device for stress incont. but aside from that, I do feel better. Love and accept yourself for everything you are...and take baby steps to becoming the you you're wanting...doesn't mean losing weight, means do things that make you feel good inside and out...I know for the newly diagnosed its hard to believe, but trust in the stories here, we're all with you to support you....

Has anyone tried any of these methods and found them beneficial? I have a neighbot with Lupus and she does Tai Chi and says it makes her feel good. If none of these worked, can you recommend something?

I can do some mild yoga, if it doesn't include abdominal or pelvic floor exercises. I've gone to classes (talking to the instructor beforehand about what not to do) and done yoga tapes at home. Today, I'm doing a Leslie Sansone "Walking off the pounds" dvd in my living room. Too cold outside!!! (Yes, I'm a weather wimp.)

contents: I'd like to start working out at a saline, not chlorine, pool. BUT I'm so worried about not being able to hold my pee. It's been years since I went near a pool bc last time my bladder gushed as I got out (gravity?) but didn't empty completely and I had to put a towel between my legs and walk really weird all the way to the bathroom. Very embarrassing. Any tips on bladder control for pool? Oh -- I searched and found that incontinence sites mention a self-adhesive foam pad the size of a postage stamp that fit in between your labia. When you need to urinate, you remove and dispose of the pad you're wearing. After you urinate, you insert a new pad. I can't find anyone who really knows what these are. Don't know if they're waterproof either...

So, I bought a new Wii with the Wii fit package, thinking it would be great to be able to exercise at home, next to my bathroom, in comfortable clothes, with no one else around. :woohoo: As it turns out, I am so uncomfortable with IC, cystocele yet to be repaired and possible rectocyle, newly aching knees, lower back and hips, that I haven't even opened the box, and can't imagine actually doing the exercises. So the Wii is going back, if i can still return it. So sad. Laurie
I'm sorry Laurie. When are you having the cystocele repaired? That just sounds awful. I hope you're feeling better soon.
signature: ~Ashley

contents: wii wee Originally posted by LithEruiel I'm sorry Laurie. When are you having the cystocele repaired? That just sounds awful. I hope you're feeling better soon. Hi Ashley, Thanks for the sympathy. It always helps! My gyns,(I've spoken to two of them now), don't want me to have the cystocele repaired, they want me to try a pessery,(my Uro disagrees). Both gyns say it's better to try something conservative first, that there is a 30% failure and redo rate with cysto repair done using the anterior method and that the mesh sling can cause all sorts of problems. One gyno. said that he has done a lot of cysto repairs on women with IC and it seems the surgery causes more IC problems, like bladder irritation and difficulty urinating due to tightening of the urethral neck. Uro says it needs to be fixed and he hasn't seen that many problems. Who knows?! As for the rectocyle, I am supposed to see a colorectal surgeon about that, but not sure I'm ready to take that on. Anywho, I guess I will get this all sorted out eventually. I just have to drive myself crazy for a while first! In the meantime, the Wii has been returned and I now have all that money to go and buy something else. : Thanks again for writing. Laurie P.S. Love your elfin name

Thanks Wow, who knows which is the right way to go...I'm glad my urologist and gynecologist are the same person, lol. Do you have PFD too? I would think the pessary might irritate that if you do, I don't know. Well I hope it all gets worked out soon!
signature: ~Ashley

contents: This really isn't IC related, but I could use some advice. Well I thought I was in pretty decent shape...I'm a nurse's aid and am on my feet for 12 hours when I work and I've really noticed my muscles in my legs toning up. Also my legs have been getting stronger from yoga. So what did I do...I thought I could try a spinning class last night at the gym since even when I wasn't in as good of shape I was always good at riding the stationary bikes. Well the sitting on the bike part was fine, but there was also a lot of standing which I couldn't do...though I tried! I fell three times and cut my legs up and bruised my knee really good. My muscles are so sore I'm having trouble sitting down and getting up, getting in and out of the shower and car, and going up and down stairs (I feel like my legs are going to give out when I go down stairs, like they did in the class). I feel like a total idiot for trying this! Now that my IC has calmed down I really want to lose some weight...my yoga class is getting too easy for me, but now I'm going to be afraid to try other classes when my muscles finally heal. What a disaster! I'm not good at working out on my own because I slack off, so I feel like I need to go to a class. I really don't have anyone to work out with either. We were doing yoga and pilates in my yoga class, but now it's just yoga again and I don't think there are any other pilates classes around here. What else could I do?
signature: ~Ashley

contents: Mostly the same things you would do without IC although the types of exercise one could do would vary depending on individual ability. Walking is great exercise, especially if you get into a routine of doing it often. I found Curves worked well for me with fibro and IC. It also helps to learn about what a balanced diet is and isn't (it can be done with the IC diet, there are foods I don't eat but plenty of healthy ones in varied food categories available). And to learn about portion control.
signature: Currently dealing with IC (diagnosed May 2001), Fibromyalgia (January 2000) and ADHD (1998 sometime) and vulvodynia (Jan. 08) I work in the schools helping students with disabilities. I am a speech language pathologist working as an Assistive Technology Specialist. A very do-able job with IC. Medications Meds that I take: Elmiron 2 x day ; Effexor XR 37.5 ; Ritalin; Nasonex; Atarax 10 mg (night); Lidocaine 5%; monthly B-12 shots; Pyridium as needed Meds I am trying Urell Supplements that help: Condroitin Sulfate; Calcium Citrate; Magnesium Glycinate; Olive Leaf Extract; daily probiotic Other treatments that help: Physical Therapy for PFD; Massage for Fibro; Chiropractic for Fibro; IF Unit (like a tens unit) for pain

contents: Dear Gina, if your IC tolerates Pilates, that's really great for losing weight. More importantly, you get a toned and tight body...it even helps some people with their pelvic pain... Switching to whole wheat bread, brown rice, bran flakes etc. fills you up. Fibers! Fibers! Fibers!

Gina, I love yoga! I go to a really low intensity class though so it hasn't helped with weight loss, but the stretches have really helped my PFD and it's really helped me reduce stress which is a huge IC trigger for me!

contents: Started the slim fast diet and I've gained 3 pounds! How does one gain weight on Slim Fast?? I've done this diet before many times and always lost weght. Not gain it. Just seems I gain weight all the time easily and to drop just one pound can take a month. I've had blood work done twice this year and all is fine. Doc told me it's menopause.

Oh geesh, here's some weight and have a side of menopause to go with, lol. Ugh! Maybe too it's water weight gain - they say you should get up in the morning, pee and then weigh. That's supposed to be the most accurate...I know that my weight can fluctuate 3lbs in the same day!!! So, don't worry too much, lol.

contents: How many of you use or have used New Dawn Pilates and do you find that it is helpful as an IC friendly exercise program? I've had a lot of trouble finding ways of exercising that don't flair my IC or bother my back. I went to the doctor today for the results of my MRI and he told me I have a bad disk (not surprising, most of my family have back problems). He told me that yoga and pilates are some of the best things I could be doing and that I should avoid running (haha, can't do that with IC anyways) and squats, unless done with an exercise ball against a wall. I'm open to suggestions if you all have exercise programs which work for your IC. What do you all do to keep in shape? Right now I'm very overweight but I know in order to get better, I need to lose the weight. The added stress on my body carrying excess body weight around isn't healthy.

Some of the exercises on the dvd were fine for me, but others flared me. I recommend starting it very very slowly - one exercise at a time to see what's ok for you. I'm a big fan of walking & very gentle yoga tapes. What about seeing a physical therapist to have them design you a program for both your back & pelvic floor (since many of us with IC have pelvic floor issues)? My physical therapist designed me an exercise program for home & I love it!

Help I love to swim I have a pool and my kids love to swim when I swim I am fine but when I get out of the water I have pain stabbing pain and burning I put vasaline on me before I go swimming but i still hurt what can i do anyone have any ideas to help thank you.

I AM SO SORRY TO HEAR YOU ARE HAVING PROBLEMS AFTER YOU SWIM! SO IS THE PAIN THERE JUST AFTER GETTING OUT? OR IS THE PAIN THERE THE WHOLE TIME? THIS IS ALSO ONE THING MY KIDS LOVE TO DO WITH ME AND I CAN SWIM AND NOT HURT AS BAD. I USE TO BE A RUNNER AND NOW IT MAKES ME HURT TOOO MUCH... WHEN I SWIM I MAKE SURE I SHOWER GOOD BEFORE I GO THAN I SHOWER RIGHT AFTER GETTING OUT. YOU CAN TRY TO NOT SWIM THAT LONG AT FIRST AND SHOWER WHEN GETTING OUT THAN SLOWLY STAY IN LONGER AND LONGER ONLY IF THE PAIN AND BURNING ARE NOT THAT BAD. THIS IS WHAT I DID AND NOW I CAN STAY IN LONGER. I DON'T HAVE THE BURNING SO THIS MITE NOT HELP YOU. I CAN ONLY PRAY THAT THIS WILL HELP SO THAT YOU CAN SWIM WITH YOUR KIDS. TAKE CARE MINDEE

Thanks I do shower right after I do not burn while I am swimming but as soon as I dry off i start hurting. I also have vulvodynia so i cant win I am tired of not being able to do thing I was feeling ok tonight and i made my kids some cool aid i took one silp to see if it had eought suger and I am on fire from one little silp it just not fair I do so much better in the winter right now i am walking around feeling swollen and burning other people just dont understand and i dont want to tell everyone what hurting me. Thanks

This past Tuesday, I went swimming with the little 23 month old that I babysit in our Air Force base's pool. It seemed clean and the smell didn't wreak of chlorine so I thought it would be okay, especially since my IC has been under such good control with the Lyrica. Well, Wednesday morning (yesterday), I woke up in extreme pain!!! I then urinated and realized I was peeing clots of blood! So, it scared the dickens out of me and I went to the ER. Come to find out I have a bad, bad UTI. They prescribed Cipro for me for the next couple days while they wait on a culture. If this infection is resistant to Cipro like my last one, I might have to be admitted for I.V. medications. Not sure about that though so we'll see when they get the sensitivity report back. Also, does anyone else have flares...I mean, BAD flares from swimming in chlorinated pools? Thanks, April [email protected]
signature: Diagnosed in 2007, but had IC symptoms since 2002. My current regimen includes: home instillations of Marcaine, Heparin and Solu-Medrol plus vaginal valium suppositories nightly. I also use Tramadol for pain as needed.

contents: I have an opportunity to swim in a county lake by my house. The lake is tested for bacteria on a regular basis - I know they really do because my daughter works there. My IC is really been better. Does anyone have an opinion about the safety of someone swimming in lakes/rivers? Thank you.

The last time I swam in a lake, when I had my bladder. I did get an infection. But I think it depends on the lake too. If it's checked often for bacteria, If I were you I'd go for it! Just have fun!! hugs and blessings

contents: The only problem I have ever had swimming was in a pool with lots of clorine. Lake or ocean swimming is fine for me. Donna

contents: Hi, I'm new to this forum and IC. Still looking for information and treatments etc. I LOVE horseback riding and have had horses for 20+ years. Just getting back into it again after having a heart stent put in (took them 10 months to figure that one out) and now this! Is there anyone out there that rides with IC that can give me HOPE??? Jan

The only thing I can suggest is to give it a try. If you can manage to sit on some kind of cushion, such as the one in the ICN Shop, it might make it easier. You might want to try very easy riding first. I hope you're able to manage it. Hugs, Donna

contents: Jan A. - I also love horseback riding and my boyfriend actually just bought me a 3 year old black mare because my whole life I have always wanted a pure black horse. Unfortunately, I am not able to ride at all and have not been able to for almost 2 years now. He bought the horse to keep my spirits up that I will be able to ride again and hopefully I will but for now it is just too painful. I hope things are different for you. I would love to know if you try it and how it works out for you. Good luck!

First, as you can see from my signature I have not been diagnosed officially with IC after a couple of work ups. But I have had many of the same symptoms--lots of soreness and frequency and insomnia in bouts that last for months--and my first uro thought I likely had at least mild IC based on symptoms. I have a horse and ride regulalry--unless I am having a really really uncomfortable day. I know it is a great stress reliever and I think in my case I have some degree of Pelvic Floor Dysfunction and I think it actually acts as a form of physical therapy for me by releasing muscle tension I have heard that gaited horses are comfortable--a woman at my boarding barn has had serious bone and back issues and she rides one with her doc's ok. So yes, I'd start with a short little ride at a walk on a gentle horse and see how you do and build from there. I did buy a "seat saver" for my saddle but dont like the fact I have less feel of the horse with it.
signature: -Not officially diagnosed with IC but have had several inconclusive work-ups for it after several months-long bouts of frequency, soreness, and the insomnia that goes with it. -long stretches of being symptom-free in between -currently on low dose bcps, celexa, and Estrace cream for possible hormonal role in bladder symptoms (I'm 45)

contents: Is there anyone out there that can swim in a pool with chlorine? I want to go swimming so bad with my little boy this summer, but I'm afraid it will cause a bladder infection. I used to go swimming all the time, but not since I was diagnosed with IC. I guess I'm scared an infection will cause a major flare up. Beth

Some people have had success with applying vaseline before swimming. It's important to not sit around in a wet swim suit. You might give it a try to see. It doesn't usually cause infection; it's a chemical and does sometimes cause irritation. Donna

contents: I work out 3-5 days a week and prior to IC always had a protein bar or shake afterward. I have been searching and not found any to be IC friendly. I was just wondering if anyone knew of any? Thanks

The only advice I have is to read labels; there may be some bars out there that are IC safe. If you don't find some, you might try other protein snacks, such as cheese, nuts, or even a sandwich. Donna
