contents: I'm not sure that I should make this recommendation ... please be careful and only start out with a very small portion. But, I like Balance Bars. My favorites are Carmel Nut Blast, Chocolate Mint and Cookie Dough. I eat one a day and typically workout each day. At 210 calories, they are not a diet snack. I've been eating them for years (this Oct I will have had IC for 20 years). They can be found at your local grocery store.
signature: Bill

contents: Judy, Try the ICN shop on this site. They have bars & stuff. Look at top of page.

There are some protein powders that are better for ICers. I was actually looking into this yesterday. I checked the ICN diet list and I saw that it is okay to try protein powders that are made from whey or egg whites. You'll have to be careful, though, and read the ingredient labels. Make sure you get something that is 100% egg whites or 100% whey. I was doing a google search yesterday and found some. Just do a search and you'll probably find some brands.
signature: -24. -Started having symptoms in May 2009. -Doing pretty well on diet alone.

contents: I love liquid meals or meal bars because I dont cook! So let me get this straight, soy is bad for us but whey is fine? How can this be? I thought the two were pretty much the same.....no? Suzie

I also work out a lot, working in the fitness industry I need a quick fix or power drink. I certainly had to do away with my old stuff that I use to drink because there was so much additives in it I was so afraid to try it in the fears I would flare. So I have been ok with HEMP POWDER and I use goats WHEY POWDER. BUT i have to say I started out with very small amounts and worked into tablespoons full. So go slowly. I make my own power bars. Heck make your own, you will know the ingredients plus they will have no additives and pack them full of amazing things, nuts, seeds, oatmeal...etc...!! So much better for you. Let me know if you want any tips and I can send you some links to some great granola bars (BUT KEEP IN MIND they are gluten free) because this is how I eat. Take care Peace Blue
I've been looking into a replacement protein powder since I can't use mine anymore. I understand 100% whey and 100% egg white ones are fine. What about the ones that are rice protein?

As usual, I came kind of late to this party. I used to live on Luna bars, so that's been hard for me. I have been able to eat a couple of varieties of Larabars - specifically the cashew cookie (only ingredients are cashews and dates) and the peanut butter cookie (peanuts, dates and salt). They are pretty tasty. I noticed they sell these on the ICN site, but I can buy them at my local grocery store. They are a little high in calories (about 230 vs. the 180 in a Luna bar) and the cashews have quite a bit of saturated fat, but I think overall it's a good mix of carbs, protein and fats. I stick the cashew bars in my purse in case I get a little hungry while I'm running errands or something.

contents: Blue, What is your bar recipe? I have been wanting to make my own food bars.
signature: Elinor I would have lost heart, unless I had believed That I would see the goodness of the Lord In the land of the living. Wait on the Lord; Be of good courage, And He shall strengthen your heart; Wait, I say, on the Lord! ***Psalm 27:13-14*** http://facinglif.blogspot.com/

contents: Hello, I was wondering if anyone had any tips for propertly hydrating for a run when you have IC. I just started running this past year and I think I either under hydrate for fear if having to stop or overhydrate and then end up stopping severla times (I just did my first half marathon and had to stop 4 times for potty breaks). It bums me out becasue it screws up my time. When I underhydrate I feel crappy during the run. ANy suggestion on how to handle this? Has anyone found a particular way to handle this? Thanks in advance for any advice!

I don't have any advice right now. I just wanted you to be aware I did read your thread. Besides, I was surprised you actually run a half marathon with IC. Maybe I don't even know the exact distance that that happens to be. I would not figure a whole lot of IC'ers have taken up jogging. Also, you can know I did not know, but I must figure porta-pottys are available along the way. OK. I am not sure you actually under or over hydrated, but I don't want you to fallout at all.

Do you think that the need to urinate is not from being over hydrated, but just irritation from your bladder? I was a runner before IC hit. There is noway I can run now. If I try to run even a 1/4 mile and I have to pee! Maybe try running early in the am when it is cooler. Suzanne

Hi all! After a long time mulling over whether to get a bike or not (because I know that biking is a common trigger for many people with IC), I finally bought one. I rode it today for about 15 minutes and it was great! I had actually read old ICN discussion threads about biking (cached on Google) and saw while there was some talk about the bike seat, there wasn't much mentioned on the bike "fit." After researching on my own and talking to knowledgeable bike people, I went with a touring/adventure bike with a suspension seat. This style of bike is designed for comfortable, general biking (i.e. roads, light trail, around-town environments). The seat is larger and softer than you would find on speed or mountain bikes. The added suspension helps mitigate vibration and jarring. The most important thing, however, is that the bike is properly fitted to your specific body size. I'm tall for a woman (5'10'') and I ended up going with a Large size men's model. It sounds huge, but it's not. The bike shop employee made sure that my seat was high enough so that my legs almost full extend when pedaling. Basically, when I'm "standing up" on the pedals, my butt is just hovering maybe an inch or so above the seat. When I was riding it home across a busy bridge and residential roads, it was so easy to lift myself off the seat when I saw a bump or rocky area coming up. I made it home without any bladder pain (or any other pain to speak of). And I haven't been on a bike in years! I just wanted to share what I learned about bikes with other ICers out there who are thinking about getting a bike but just aren't sure.
signature: Me: Canadian. 31. IC symptoms: Cramping, burning, frequency. Pain! Pain management: Ibuprofen. AZO. Marijuana. Prelief. The IC Diet. My IC-safe, Gluten-free, Lactose-free food blog: Good for Your Guts

contents: Hello, I'm new to the forum and officially diagnosed as of last week. I just wanted to share with you all that yoga has been helping with some of my issues. I truly believe that a lot of my symptoms, and maybe some of yours are brought on my stress. About a month ago I started going to "hot yoga"- they keep the room at 90 degrees at all times and it's fast paced and a lot of fun. The 90 degrees might sound daunting, but it warms up your muscles so much that can stretch farther and easier than ever before (I couldn't even touch my toes when I started). So whether you practice at a gym or yoga studio or at home, I really think it can help. My PFD physical therapist told me today that yoga is one of the best exercises we can do with IC and painful bladder. If nothing else, for that hour or hour and a half I am totally at peace (although my muscles are definitely working hard) and I forget all about the urgency that I usually feel constantly. And I've had to stop running for now because it bothers my symptoms, so this is a good way to get in shape too. It's just too bad that I can't quit work and become a professional yogi :-D Are there any other typs of exercise that others like to do that seem to help with your symptoms? If so, let me know- I will try anything to get relief!
signature: -Diagnosed with Painful Bladder Syndrome/IC and PFD on March 23, 2010 -Current meds.: Hydroxyzine 25mg Pelvic floor therapy (completed) Elavil 25 mg. Elmiron 3 x daily Exercise

contents: Hey Cara, Welcome to the ICN - and great post. I've always thought that yoga would be helpful, but the hot yoga never crossed my mind. Will definitely put this on my "list of stuff to try". And I agree with you 100% that stress is a huge trigger for these IC problems!

Just make sure that the high temperatures don't cause problems with your medicines. Both my bottle of Elavil and my bottle of Ditropan state to be cautious in high temperatures... That said, yoga helps me as well... I've been doing a "gentle yoga, for the extreme beginner" dvd & it seems to relieve symptoms as well as reduce my stress in general. The dvd isn't perfect, the music doesn't match the program well, but it's been the best I've found so far that gives me enough challenge to get slightly sore muscles without a bladder flare...

contents: Thank you both for the replies! Lonely Soul (I hope you are not really lonely though!)- definitely give it a try- I thought the hot temps. would be awful because I usually like to have a nice cool temp. when running and doing other strenuous activities. But something about the heat and the sweating during yoga class makes me feel great (although I don't look so hot when I have to go straight to work after!). And Kadi, thanks for the caution about the heat and medications- I never really thought about that, but I will be sure to ask my doctor about that next time I see him. Stress relief seems to be key with this condition- I am a stress ball as it is and I know that is why my muscles are always so tense! Happy Yoga-ing! Cara
signature: -Diagnosed with Painful Bladder Syndrome/IC and PFD on March 23, 2010 -Current meds.: Hydroxyzine 25mg Pelvic floor therapy (completed) Elavil 25 mg. Elmiron 3 x daily Exercise

contents: Yeah, yoga helps me alot, too. I currently practice vinyasa, anusura, & kundalini, which always includes a meditation at the end. I've been wanting to try a bikram class, but I sweat so much when the class temperature is warm, that I'm afraid I'd turn into a puddle in a truly hot bikram class!

Hi everyone, My symptoms are through the roof lately (frequency/urgency/pain) but I just started Lyrica, will be starting atarax soon, and I have my first uracyst instillation on April 9th. I hope to get great relief soon! My question for all you yogis is that do you do yoga even when you have a lot of symptoms-- pain/urgency/frequency--- and that the yoga helps you with these symptoms? Or, do you have to wait until your bladder is calm and not acting up and then exercize/do yoga??? Thank you so much for answering my questions!

Glad you are feeling better. Yoga definitely helps with IC. Yoga helps regulate your breathing, release stress and has an overall increase in endorphins which help internally relax the mind and body. (and so much more!!) I am one of the lucky ones...because Yoga is my profession. Yoga has been a saving grace for me. Peace Blue

There are some mornings when I wake up to go to yoga class before work and all I can think about is how much I have to pee! Well, I know I don't really have to, but that feeling is still there. The feeling is still with me when I get to class and set up my mat, but as soon as I start doing the exercises I start to forget about having to go and eventually I don't feel the urgency. There are a few days that I feel a little twinge of urgency throughout class, but nothing like when I'm sitting at my desk at work or walking the dog. So I don't know if it's a long term fix, but I truly feel better for that hour, and I really believe that it will help my IC and PFD in the long run too if I keep it up! So Daniel, yes I do go even if I have urgency and frequency (there is also a bathroom 10 ft. away from me that I can rely on), and even if I have bladder pressure or mild pain (I, luckly, don't have intense pain from this). And on days where my baldder feels good I go and try to stretch into the poses a little bit more! Bluetou- you are so very lucky to be a yogi by profession- I'm jealous! I wish I was flexible and coordinated enough to teach! I'm usually the one in the back of the room who is falling over during the airplane pose! :-D
signature: -Diagnosed with Painful Bladder Syndrome/IC and PFD on March 23, 2010 -Current meds.: Hydroxyzine 25mg Pelvic floor therapy (completed) Elavil 25 mg. Elmiron 3 x daily Exercise

contents: Delfin- what is anusura and kundalini? Are they different poses or a different pace?- I am intrigued. The class I take isn't technically a Bikram class, it's hot like one and fast paced, but they call it vinyasa power yoga- so I dont know how different that is from Bikram. I do agree with the puddle comment though, I never knew I was capable of sweating so much! But at least everyone else in the class is too so I don't feel too bad
signature: -Diagnosed with Painful Bladder Syndrome/IC and PFD on March 23, 2010 -Current meds.: Hydroxyzine 25mg Pelvic floor therapy (completed) Elavil 25 mg. Elmiron 3 x daily Exercise

contents: Anusara and kundalini are just different types of yoga. Anusara is more of a flow-type vinyasa style... there's great attention paid to form and you seem to hold the poses for longer. Kundalini is very different... you do specific sets of poses and exercises called kriyas for different results. There's also a lot of breathwork, including a rapid one called breath of fire. PM me if you want any dvd recommendations. : )

I have been thinking about trying Alli but I'm so scared to try anything like that with IC. The ingredients are, Active Ingredients: Orlistat (60 mg) (Weight Loss Aid) Inactive Ingredients: FD&C Blue 2, Edible Ink, Gelatin, Iron Oxide, Microcrystalline Cellulose, Povidone, Sodium Lauryl Sulfate, Sodium Starch Glycolate, Talc, Titanium Dioxide Should we be staying away from it? Has anyone tried it? loosing weight for me is soooo hard.. I can do cardio for an hour a day 5-6 times a week for three months and not see any weight loss. It's very frustrating and would really like something that would help. I feel stuck with IC and not being able to eat "normal" Most veggies bother me and I'm finding it difficult to eat healthy with the IC diet. Is there anything else that has helped others loose weight?
When I've done Weight Watchers & stayed within the points, I've lost weight. I personally wouldn't try a diet pill. I'd be too afraid of provoking the IC further.

contents: Hello, One of the things I miss the most after getting this disease is being able to exercise. I know exercise is so good for the body and mind but when I tried it I always felt worse. Now that it`s almost spring I`m having such a hard time not exercising--- I love to exercise outside. I went for around a 35 minute walk today--- nice slow pace--- and now I`m feeling quite a bit worse than before I started. It felt so nice to be outside in the fresh air and sunshine but it was very painful walking for me and with each stride my bladder hurt....It felt sooo good in other ways though that I kept walking until I couldn`t stand it anymore despite the pain. My question is, does walking help with the bladder pain eventually or does it always make it worse...I really feel out of shape and I can`t stand sitting on the couch all day--- and since my diagnostic sugery of March 5th I haven`t been able to get out much because of the severe pain and frequency and urgency...I feel like I`m constantly in a bad flare with no relief. Does anyone have any suggestions on what exercises to do that won`t hurt my bladder anymore and may actually make it feel better... Thanks and I appreciate all suggestions.
If you haven't exercised for a while, 35 minutes is a pretty long walk as a starter. You might want to start off by doing ten minutes, and gradually increase. Donna

contents: Thanks for your responses Donna and Bri....It's such a beautiful day out today...maybe I will just go sit outside for awile and take it easy to enjoy the weather and soak up a bit of sun. It does make me said how restricted I am as I love to exercize...I can just look forward to a day soon when I will be well enough to do so again...keeping the hope/faith is so important right now. What is a recumbant bike? Maybe I can give that a try...

i was reading that walking is the best and to avoid anything that "jars" the hips! like a stairmaster or riding a bike ( or doing the "bike'" exercise or abs) doing deep breathing and stretching before helps too! i miss my exercise routine! not being able to do the things you want! but im learning theres always another way to do things we just have to find the alternative!

I can walk. Any heavy leg weight lifting increases my pain as does intense abdomenal work out. I have been able to return to my horse riding and my horse is very jarring. I really try to stretch well before and after. I also work on stretching my piriformis muscle in my seat and my inner thighs and focus on pelvic floor relaxation. I have a muscle relaxer in case I need it.

When I was in 24/7 pain last spring I felt like I could barely walk through the grocery store and did little trips only. As I started with trmts/meds and the pain lessened I started slowly walking again and built up to it. I now do the eliptical (sp) and walk without increasing my bladder pain. After doing physical therapy and lots of stretching I think that helps with muscle pain/pfd that in turns makes the bladder pain worse. Maybe start with stretching/gentle yoga like exercise and then maybe try a little walking after the muscles are stretched out. I don't know if that would help at all, but just a thought. Hope you feel better soon and can start exercising again!
signature: Holly 49 year old SAHM with 4 great girls and hubby. Symptoms started 1/09. Officially diagnosed 4/09. Symptoms are totally pain, very little frequency. Diagnosed with Sjogrens 9/09 Diagnosed with mild gastritis, GERD. Chronic Heartburn Current Meds. Quit Elmiron after 4 yrs Previousy had instills 6/09-2/10 and a few after that Started PT 12/09 - a great help too! IC Diet - an unfortunate must! Plaquenil (for Sjogrens) Prilosec Iron pills as tolerated due to iron deficiency Doing much better than my first year of diagnosis, but still have to carefully watch my diet if I want to stay out of pain.

contents: For me the best way to get back into exercise was a pelvic stretching program that I learned from my physical therapist. This helped my IC symptoms, relaxed the muscles that were affecting my bladder and released pelvic tension. This allowed me to do other exercise without such an immediate effect on my bladder. Now about ten years later, I can do aerobics and strength training exercise at a level that keeps me in shape. But, I still do my pelvic stretching program at least once a day.
signature: Bill

contents: Thanks everybody for your responses! I really appreciate it. I think that it was great advice to start doing pelvic stretches to try and relax those muscels to help my bladder. Once I get some money again I will be seeing a physical therapist--- I am in the process of applying for Disability Support. It is hard for me to find a physical therapist who specializes in pelvic floor dysfunction in London Ontario where I live. But, I will look harder soon. I feel sooo deprived now. I really need to get my pain and frequency/urgency under control now though--- it is through the roof since my last hydrodistention under anaesthesia on March 5th. I just started Lyrica last night, will be starting Atarax soon, and I will have my first uracyst instillation on April 9th. I hope I will feel some relief soon!! Then, I will see a naturopathic doctor, and physical therapist! Then, I hope to be able to exercise! I miss rollerblading, walking, dancing (I love ballet), excersizing in general, and I love to ride horses!! I also want to learn how to play tennis!!! I want to be healthy....
I love to walk each day for 1-2 miles...Is this OK ? I have been feeling good and DO NOT want any flare ups!!!! Thanks SO much! Becky

Walking is one exercise that most of us can do. I need to work back up to long walks; I was doing a walk every day plus 45 minutes of walking on my treadmill, but had to stop for surgery and just do short walks for a while. I feel much better when I walk a lot. Donna

contents: I agree with Donna, I feel so much better when I walk. I try to stay close to home if I don't feel well. I can walk a one mile circuit in my neighborhood which helps because if I need to get home, I am within 10 minutes of a potty.

contents: I have just started walking again now that the weather is nicer and I am feeling so much better from it. You might be careful of inclines though
signature: The combo of things that brought me out of remission were artificial sweetners, liquid vitamins with citric acid, tight pants, lower body workouts and stopping all my allergy meds cold turkey during height of allergy season. I wish I knew back then what I know now about IC First IC symptoms Spring of 2000; In-office cystoscopy 2002 -negative; Remission until 2007 (yeah!!); Negative PST spring of 2007; IC diagnosed via Cystoscopy and Hydrodistention 7/24/07; Another In-office cystoscopy 4/9/10 with biopsy showed inflammation Treatments for my IC/PFD: Elmiron 400 mg per day; walking, Zyrtec, Claritin, Singulair, Neurontin 100 mg per day; Ice packs and laying down; About to try Cytotec Tried and Failed: Heat (always makes me worse!), Heparin instills -too painful for my urethra; Atarax (interfered with my quality of sleep first time, second time dried my bladder out; Elavil makes me sick, lidocaine patches (just plain don't work), Cystoprotek (only tried a couple months but didn't seem to do much), Aloe Vera caps, pelvic floor therapy, trigger point work flared me worse, Lyrica (bad stomach pain) Other Diagnoses: PFD 2009; Fibromyalgia 2006, Osteopenia 2012, GERD 2006, Gastroparesis (delayed gastric emptying) Anxiety: Other medications: Ambien, Dexilant, Ibuprofren, Necon 1/35, Voltaren gel for tailbone BABY GIRL 10/28/08 *** BABY BOY 7/8/11

contents: Walking with IC symptoms? Sometimes I think that walking flares things up a bit for me! Is it OK to walk even though you have some symptoms eg low level discomfort and urgency, or is it best to wait untill everything settles down...(could be waiting a while)??!! I used to be an avid walker before this set in 6 weeks ago, I don't want to give up my fitness

I do the same thing Julie does. I walk a mile loop in my neighborhood. I try and do 3 or 4 miles a day. I find it makes me feel so much better both physically and emotionally. If I am flaring, I cut it back to one mile. But I still do it.

walking... I'll try walking half the distance and see how I go...I seem to have bladder aching with some movements, mainly lifting and bending and sometimes from sitting too long!! Most of my aching occurs down the sides of my pubic bone near where it meets each leg, so striding out is going to be off the menu foe a while... I see my specialist April 14th when I come back off holiday to talk about Elmiron...I sure won't be climbing any mountains this time!! Cheers, Lesley (New Zealand)
Lesley, does it help if you use one of those sticky heating pads over your bladder area?

contents: Hi, I went into a small flare this morning. I usually do the elliptical for 35 minutes after work, but I'm not sure if that is a good idea when I am in flare. I don't want to make it worse. Any ideas?

I'm not familiar with the eliptical, so have no idea about what muscles it works, intensity, etc. I do cardio and weight training on alternate days. I've found that for me, when I'm in a flare it's just better to skip my exercise for that day. It seems like that rest period allows my flare to pass faster and so that's all the sooner I'm back to being able to give 100% in my routine.
signature: "The happiest people don't necessarily have the best of everything; they just make the best of everything they have."

contents: I would suggest not going on the eliptical. I am surprised you can do the elipticial....I actually had to stop using the stair climber because it would cause a slight flare in me. Any up and down movement/jarring to the pelvis is said to trigger flares in people.... Now ...get this...I can walk on my treadmill at a good huff....and I am back to running (since about 7 months) I use to be a runner prior to IC...but then I had to give it up. SO started back into it seven months ago, and NO FLARE!! nadda!! I feel amazing! Ok...so not everyone can run....but for me it works and I do not flare. SO I thought...I would go back to my stairclimber, caues it was flaring me in the past...but where I have been painfree for such a long period of time I figured I would give it a whirl....well three days into using the stairclimber...I started feeling hot....and getting a bit of some bladder pain....AT first I thought I ate something...and then my husband reminded me about using the stairclimber....so not doing that again. SO phew...with all that being said...I would say avoid the eliptical for now....maybe just walk.... sorry i am long winded... hope you feel better soon and your flare will pass quickly.... Peace Blue Originally posted by ll79 Hi, I went into a small flare this morning. I usually do the elliptical for 35 minutes after work, but I'm not sure if that is a good idea when I am in flare. I don't want to make it worse. Any ideas?

I just finished a 20-30 minute session of gentle yoga from my yoga book. Some poses were a little difficult, but I feel great! I just went slow and breathed slowly and deeply. If some positions were too difficult, I skipped them. I feel loose, relaxed, and energized. I have some "burning" in the upper pelvic floor region, but that could be from actually using my pevlic floor and stretching it out because I haven't done that in a long while. Overall, I feel great! And I am going to try to do this in conjunction with Amy Stein's Heal Pelvic Pain book. Wish me luck!

I am currently diagnosed with IC, PFD, endometriosis, asthma, and vulvodynia/contact dermititis, IBS, and fibromyalgia. contents: good luck! I am trying yoga as well
signature: my name is Katie, 22 yr old mother & nursing student. I want to be a writer and midwife. I have severe IC w/ hunner ulcers and urethritis with lesions ENDO, chronic pelvic pain, PFD adhesions, scoliosis, arthritis,migraines,asthma chronic pain. Mother to a beautiful little girl. She has my heart and she's everything to me. Thinking positive and consciously untensing tense muscles helps a ton!!!! physical therapy also helps a lot.

contents: So I've been doing gentle yoga once a week since early December. There have been a couple of weeks I've missed or they didn't have it over the holidays, but out of the relatively few times I've gone so far, I have twice had IC problems. I feel like I'm okay, then we do certain stretches/poses and I notice my bladder hurting, then have frequency. I can't help but make a connection, here. Am I crazy, or is it just a coincidence maybe? Last night was the worst. I felt so ashamed that I ended up going to the basement of the center to just read and hang out near the bathroom so I wasn't disturbing everyone else. I've felt really depressed ever since, because I was rather enjoying going to yoga with my friend and girlfriend and now I feel like I shouldn't even bother.
You can always avoid the ones you know will be a problem. I have done exercise programs where I couldn't do all the moves --- nobody even seemed to notice. Donna

contents: Its kind of like food with me, though- I can't always tell what will and what won't affect me like that. I have no idea what it is specifically. And of course, like food and drink, it seems like some days are just fine and others are devastating. So frustrating.

I have had days where I felt some burning a few hours after taking power yoga. This past week in class (I hadn't been in 2 weeks) I could actually feel certain positions starting off the burn - I think maybe that I'm engaging my pelvic floor and it's not happy about it.....plank and chaturangas were not my friend this week. The meditation part always is good and chakra sounds!

When I was taking yoga classes, I went to class early & told the instructor I needed to avoid postures that put a lot of stress on the abdomen & pelvic floor. They were usually very happy to tell me how to modify poses & didn't embarrass me. To find yoga dvds that are ok for me, I've been checking them out from netflix so if one bothers me, I just mail it back...

contents: I'm sorry yoga isn't helping you more. When I started yoga, there were certain positions that would bother me as well. There were also times where I would have an a temporary increase in frequency when the class was over. But as time went on and I continued doing yoga on a consistent basis, the initial problems it caused went away and I started noticing beneficial effects on my symptoms. There is a possibility if you stick with it, you may be surprised how much it can help you.

You might be right. It's just that it's such an intimate space and I feel like I would be disturbing everyone. Like it's mostly relaxation and it's dark and quiet and everyone is trying to relax. I can imagine I would be disturbed if people kept going to the bathroom and flushing every 5 minutes... I was just so ashamed ;_;

If you can, i'd keep up with the yoga. Yoga is great to strengthen and stretch the muscles in the pelvic floor. Chances are it will help and not hurt. Also, remember that IC can wax and wane on it's own. Sometimes it gets better and worse without a reason. Your increase in symptoms might not be from the yoga. At least for me, trying to find things that flare symptoms is difficult since sometimes I flare and sometimes I don't. I do think that most cardio bothers me due to the bouncing around, but I'm not totally sure yet. I'm still working to figure that out. It's never ending. I know yoga doesn't bother me since I've been doing that for over a year and a half. I have good and bad days while doing yoga. That's how I rule something out as a cause of symptoms. I do it for a longer period of time and see how things go. If I wax and wane while doing something then I figure that activity or food isn't contributing to the disease. It's just the disease fluctuating on its own. The unpredictability is what makes this a challenge to deal with. I do try to keep a journal and I will do a voiding log intermittently. Overall I'm way better than I used to be. However, there are still days that are tough. Life goes on.
signature: What helps Elmiron 200 mg bid Wellbutrin XL anxiety and depression Oxybutynin ER 10 mg Yoga tried and failed Cardura, Hytrin, Flomax, Prosed DS, Vesicare, Pyridium, Cystoprotek (caused GI problems), Lyrica, Pamelor "We can't wait until the storm is over. We need to learn to dance in the rain."

contents: Originally posted by treehugger You might be right. It's just that it's such an intimate space and I feel like I would be disturbing everyone. Like it's mostly relaxation and it's dark and quiet and everyone is trying to relax. I can imagine I would be disturbed if people kept going to the bathroom and flushing every 5 minutes... I was just so ashamed ;_; Absolutely do not feel bad if you need to get up and use the bathroom. Try talking to the teacher beforehand about your problem, I did this and she was very supportive and sympathetic about the problems I was going through. Try to find a space closest to the the door or bathroom so you can leave with minimal interuption.

yoga I love yoga and want to go more. I find it so relaxing...and the poses seem to unlock the tension that is trapped in my body. I just do what I can (like everyone else around...they seem to some poses better than others..I see alot of 'cheating") Yoga is good...just find your pace. we need to relax/center/breathe/and expand. I took a tower pilates class a few times...I like to push myself..but I think I went too far last week...I pulled a muscle and I think it might be part of the reason I am in a mega flare. :-(

does anyone else have problems with pools/chlorine? I was swimming in Ontario on holiday last summer with little to no problem (chlorinated pool at a couple of hotels)..came back here to Nova Scotia, had a bit of a heat wave, resorted to taking my daughter to a pool 2 days as it was so hot, and literally had to go every 5 min, the burning was SO bad I swore it was a UTI..so I went to an after hours clinic in our area, and he prescribed me antibiotics without doing a culture, and it made the pain worse so now I'm scared to use a pool but we want to put our daughter in a gym n' swim program at our local community center, which I would have to take her to 1 day/week... suggestions or input??

Hi, I have problems with chlorine. I will get some burning. I also have problems with certain antibiotics. Alot of the antibiotics cause my symptoms to get worse, there are only a few I can take. I would call your doctor and let them know your symptoms are worse and see what they recommend? Good luck. I hope you feel better.

Severe anxiety and moderate depression since middle school. Current Meds: 60mg Oxycontin 3x daily (every 8 hours), 30mg Oxycodone 4x daily as needed, 350mg Soma as needed (twice a day), 10mg Valium Vaginal Suppositories as needed (after sex usually, what I call "the nuke" is two of these, and two oxycodone by mouth and one soma by mouth and I can usually prevent a flare if this is done RIGHT AFTER sex along with a cold pack right over my bladder), and Prelief as needed. contents: I kind of figured I wasn't alone, and am disappointed! I love swimming! I haven't been to a pool in about 6 months b/c I was so irritated the last 2 times darn it!

I am newly diagnosed and can say I am a bit overwhelmed but relieved to have a diagnosis finally. One of my concerns is Ihave a horse I love very much and he is not a smooth ride. I have a cushion in my saddle that helps. Is there anyone else out there with IC who is actively riding and has any ideas about saddle pads,saddle seats and cushions? This is one thing I can't give up. It gives me great joy,is a family activity and is my social life as well. Any help would be greatly appreciated. I have about 8 million other questions too,but will read more before asking. Thanks

If you can figure out a way to use one, the cushions available in the ICN Shop help cushion the bumps in the car --- they might also help ride your horse --- they take the pressure off the perineal area. Donna

signature: - Symptoms: started July 2007 New pain medication regimen has things under pretty good control for many weeks now. Exercise and eating healthy along with pain control have given me new life. Current Symptoms: pain in testicles, penis and general groin area. contents: I ride my horse all of the time when its warm out side. I have a walking horse so its a little smoother than some others. You just have to know how you are feeling that day and dont push past your limits on pain. I honestly think riding has helped me. There are alot of different cushions you can try. Ive got a gel one and I love it. Something else that helped me is riding in a saddle with a slightly higher back. Also if you go trail riding make sure you get off anytime you feel like your pelvic area, hips, or legs are getting tense and do some stretches. I definatly wouldnt give it up if it makes you happy. Just keep it up and know your limits.

contents: I notice you are newly diagnosed so chances are you have yet to get the IC symptoms you're experiencing quieted? I have a horse I love very much and he is not a smooth ride. It sounds like I had his brother! I don't know I've ever ridden a horse with gaits worse than mine, but like you, I loved him! I no longer have my horses but when I did, if I was in a flare there was no possible way I could ride. But, if I wasn't in a flare I could ride without suffering any consequences at all. After my IC diagnosis, with treatment & diet I reached the point where I rarely flared anymore so it really wasn't an issue for me. Now that you're diagnosed and hopefully starting some sort of treatment, you'll be on your way to getting your symptoms calmed down. I bet you'll find you can still ride, at least on your good days. And in time, chances are that your good days will far outnumber any bad ones. Vicki
signature: "The happiest people don't necessarily have the best of everything; they just make the best of everything they have."

contents: Not riding horses would KILL me! IC will NOT take that from me, it's taken too much already That being said..... First let me ask you what type of saddle you have? Western/English (I'm assuming Western)? Brand? Next....What do you ride in? Riding pants/Jeans? Of course everyone is different, but after changing a few things I'm able to ride as often and for as long as I want. I'm looking forward to helpin not only a fellow IC'r but RIDER!!

contents: I wish I HAD a horse! I love riding so much and never get the opportunity. The few times that I have ridden since being diagnosed, I did not have any problems. In fact a couple of years ago I went on a nose to tail trail ride in Glacier National Park, thinking it was better than nothing, put planned to be bored. I wanted to go into the forest in some sort of controlled way, however, so I choose the horses. First of all, they gave me a very head-strong horse, so I actually had a lot more of a challenge than I though, anf I was actually having fun. Half way through the ride, however, the horses sensed there was a thunderstorm coming long before us mere humans did. They all were crazy, trying to rub the riders off against trees, trying to sprint ahead past the guides, etc. It began to rain, then thunder, and it was chaos for a little while. There were kids on that ride that I can guarantee will never ride a horse again for as long as they live! My horse was SO mad he couldn't just run back to the shelters. But I am proud to say I held my own with him! I was worried that I would suffer later, but the only suffering I had to endure was soaking wet clothes for the rest of the day. I hope you can ride again!!!! As the others said, be sure you are doing ok, and have a rescue plan available in case you flare afterward.

My horse will act stupid before a storm also.How did they ever survive in nature? ha ha

signature: KellyAnn......Kell for short Official diagnosis of IC- 1994 I went to a Urologist, was dx'd and underwent 3 DMSO treatments, then my Uro shot himself. I couldn't bring myself to go back to another one until 2004. Of course insurance and 4 boyz later played a part in that one Interstim Implant 2006 Early stages of cervical cancer - 1992 caught in time Thank God! My family is very supportive and help me anytime I need it. I thank God every day for what he has given me Visit my family website, it hasn't been updated since January, but it's fun to look at anyway www.shanekellyandtheboyz.com try not to skip the intro it's great!

contents: Thats a tough call. I know it wasnt the best idea but I rode my horse after I had mine. I fell off one day and let me tell ya it hurt like crazy because I landed on my back. I was lucky I didnt have it turned on at the time. I had problems with mine from the get go but I did turn it on after the fall and the stimulation had definatly moved. Im sure if you fall it would proably hurt pretty bad. There is a risk for the lead to move but with it being in 3 years it might be scarred in enough if you dont take a hard fall it might not move it. I just know I was kind of in the same position when I got mine. You get it to get your life back but you find yourself having to make decisions about the stuff that makes you happy. If you want to risk it go for it just if you feel like you are starting to fall try to land on your stomach lol.

contents: I recently got a Wii and the Wii Fit Game and balance board. I am enjoying it so much. It is clever and fun and I do it everyday. If you are looking for a new way to get fit in the comfort of you home check it out. It weighs you, checks your balance, tracks your time and calories burned, and include very fun fitness games (i.e. bicycling around an island, hula hoop, step aerobics), yoga, and calisthenics. It gives you feedback if you don't have proper form and comments if you miss a day. You can do it free form or set routines to target certain areas. It would be adaptable to those who could not do certain exercises because of IC. I highly recommend this product.

contents: Wii fit is sooo much fun! My favorite are the balance games (especially the ones with the little balls - its very addictive). Although depending on if I get too addicted (i.e. play too long to keep beating scores) it can aggrevate my pfd slightly but not too much. Anywho, its something really fun to get into with the whole family and there are a lot of different games that can get your heart rate up that won't aggrevate your ic - I really think it is a great thing for folks to try that want to just at least get their heart rate up every day. Even the regular wii activity games can do that for those of you that don't want to go purchase the board. I'm looking into the new one with the band that goes around your leg...is that what the plus is?

I am training for a half marathon this spring and am considering trying to raise money to donate to IC research. I was thinking about making a t-shirt to wear during the race that says "Running with IC FOR IC" to generate awareness and asking my family and friends for donations. Would anyone feel comfortable supporting me if I can find a secure way to facilitate it? Of course I need more information on the best place to donate it to but I think it's about time we have events to generate money for research. I want to make it happen. By the way, this is preliminary because as the mileage increases my body may fight against this idea more, but if I've made a promise to other IC'ers to get this done I'll have a higher chance of success.

I am not sure I even know the distance of a half marathon. Of course, I do think many people with IC would not be able to make it.(or even try to) If you can handle it, then I would hope you can get the t-shirt as well. I would figure you could donate to the Interstitial Cystitis Association. Besides, I have seen where they have listed names of people that have donated generous amounts before. Ok. I am gonna have to get offline and get in bed. At least, I did respond now. Yep!

I started doing yoga for the past few weeks and I noticed that my urine flow was improved, almost back to normal. Yesterday, I was having problems with my slow urine flow and wondering if I was emptying my bladder completely (hadn't done yoga in a week or so), after I did a yoga class that evening it improved. Anybody else have experience with this? In insight as to why yoga would be helping me? -Tess

If you have a pelvic floor dysfunction, along with IC, (like many of us have) the yoga might be helping by lenghtening the pelvic floor thereby allowing the urine to release more easily. I on the other hand have started this new type of yoga called yin yoga where you hold each pose for almost 2 minutes and am in one of the worst bladder flares of my life!!!!!! It's probably just a coincidence because the dr did start me on a new med (atarex). I am going to post myself to see if anyone else had trouble with this med. If it ain't broke don't fix it!! If the yoga is helping continue with it and count youself lucky

I take power yoga (I know that probably makes me insane) and you definitely use your core like crazy. So, there are times when I think it may cause me to flare. My teacher will say things like "engage your pelvic floor and perineum" and I think to myself maybe this is a bad idea but I like what it does for me from other perspectives so for now I deal with a little suffering on occassion when it makes me feel bad. If I'm consistent, though, it's not bad. I think the key for me is to keep working on the muscles and then there's not a huge difference from them getting weaker and having to be strengthened again. My pelvic floor is tight enough and doesn't need any strength! Glad you are having success with yoga, the meditative aspect is so helpful.
I'm sooo out of shape & tense, thinking of getting back into yoga. Anyone recommend any dvd's or types of classes?

contents: I haven't been on the site in so long. Anyway, I now do a ton of yoga. I love how it makes me feel. I used to have constant SI joint pain, and it is basically gone now. If you can find a live class, that's the best. I currently take vinyasa, anusara, and kundalini yoga classes. For dvds, I mostly do the ravi/ana kundalini yoga dvds (you can find them on amazon). There's meditations in them that are very relaxing. (I've been diagnosed with PFD). Keep in mind that kundalini is a very different style of yoga, with "sets" of poses and exercises, as opposed to a "flow" with lots of sun salutations, etc. For flow-type yoga, I know alot of people love Seane Corn, E. Finn, Shiva Rea, and, of course, Rodney Yee, Baron Baptiste... there are so many dvds out there. Sting's wife just put one out that looks very good, too. My suggestion to you is to go to a site like videofitness.com where they have user reviews of many yoga dvds. Good luck!
has anyone tried tai chi? i think its supposed to be ic friendly???
signature: Formerly lorenab

contents: I can't do all of the moves, but many should be okay for an ICer. My primary exercise is walking. Donna

contents: I was getting into running and loving it too much late last year, and early this year until about April when I got shin splints and pretty sure I STILL have tendonitis...but anyway, I was just wondering if anybody is on this board who is a runner and uses the IC diet, and if so, what do you eat? I'm just curious so when and IF my leg ever heals, I know a routine I should follow...or can look at.

Hi Tamakat, I love running. but as I had my first cysto-hydro about a week and a half ago I haven;t run yet since the op. When I am really well I run up to 30km (18 miles) per week. when i am not as well I ride my bike and run once a week for only half an hour. I try and pilates when I can't do impact stuff... With eating, I am still following a lower carb diet than before IC and I find wheat makes me worse too so my diet isn;t the only way to refuel, just what I ahev worked out. Just make sure you refuel with carbs and protein, hydrate and have enough potassium and salts as these are what you deplete when you run a lot. A half hour run every few days does't really need substantial changes in diet as the calorie deficit isn't huge. But regular running needs a good healthy diet. I tend to refuel with dandelion coffee ( potassium because I'm not eating any fruit), an omlette with veggies for fibre and protein. Or a rice cereal with flax seed and almonds for protein and complex carbs. if you are eating fruit then bananas are great, high in potassium and complex carbs with vitamins to help repair. A real benefit to running is that it's very good for your bowels, which anyone with IC knows, if they're bowels are happy, it can help the blader be less irritated. My bladder is good when I am running because of how good my bowel is! But sometimes the irritation of chafing can worsen the symptoms.. how often do you run Tamakat?

Thanks for the reply, Amiel. I haven't been running for months now since I have a mysterious leg injury that I keep irritating when I think working out won't affect it, so it's taking forever to heal. I was just curious in case I eventually am able to start to run again...I was only running about 10 miles a week, but I over did it too much too quick in the beginning when I was training for races (which is why I haven't healed yet!).

we have to be more careful than 'normal people'! Hi Tamakat, sorry to hear about your injury. That must be annoying. I went for my first run in about a month this morning. I ran for about 20 mins all up but mostly walked for a whole hour. I felt the irritation externally and around my bladder and so I walked when it got back. Still it felt good to run at least a bit again! have you been to a physiotherapist about your leg to help it heal? They could give you special exercises and stretches (and maybe give it sports massage). When I injured my ankle, it got me back on my feet again quickly... take care

I run too! I was afraid and thought I should stop and then my husband said "You hurt if you run?" Yes, sometimes. "You hurt if you don't?" Yes, sometimes. "So, why are you going to stop running." Thanks, husband. Because even though sometimes I feel like crap, I'm going to feel even crappier if I'm not at least trying to be somewhat active. I hope that your injury heals and you can ease back into it. My goal is someday to have a run for IC (like Race for the Cure) and actual IC'ers running in that race. Pain be damned!

"My goal is someday to have a run for IC (like Race for the Cure) and actual IC'ers running in that race. Pain be damned!" Yes indeed shortstuff. How much pain do we suffer from ic, and how much pain do we get to suffer from doing things we WANT to do! Yes there are consequences from running, but for me too there are consequences from not running. Yes, I feel worse if I don't get exercise, our mood needs a lift to cope with the constant maintenance of keeping pain and other irritating symptoms at bay. Exercise keeps our bodies cleansing out our lymphatic system, and helps us to sleep. One of the worst things for me is getting constipated, and running is proven to be the best exercise for keeping you 'regular' - a healthy bowel helps the bladder. A constipated bowel means bladder spasms and low back pain and abdominal pain. Running makes us strong and increases our ability to ward things off. Of course, there are days, weeks and sometimes longer when running is not our friend... I live in Australia but I would like to 'run for the cure' too!

I love to run also. I have ran in three 5 K races earlier this year but sadly quit because my bladder has made me feel like yuck. I recently did a walk for kidney disease to raise $$. I think a run or walk for Interstitial Cystitis would be a wonderful idea! I'd be there!
signature: I have had IC since age 19 after a series of UTI's. Had a few remissions. No other health issues. I'm now 30. Trying to get my symptoms under control again, slowly getting there! On IC diet and artesian water, trying to eat more natural and less processed foods. Lots of hot baths (sometimes with baking soda and tea tree oil). My mom was recently diagnosed with IC with symptoms begining the end of 2010 and now my 8 year old son is having burning & stinging discomfort with and following urination and urgency/frequency. MEDS Elavil 50mg evening Atarax 25mg 1-2x daily Elmiron 2 tabs 2x daily Baclofen 10mg 2-3x daily (muscle relaxer prescribed after a urodynamics test) Orth Tri Cyclen Cystoprotek 2 tabs 2x daily, restarted 01/11 Fish oil tabs 2x daily, started 01/11 MSM 2x daily, started 01/11 Prelief as needed marshmallow root & chamomile tea (tastes better together) Seems like ALOT of meds (Rx & OTC) but I'm desperate in getting relief and I am feeling better.

contents: I can not run, but would love to. I do walk about 40 mins a day to walk my dogs.

I am also a runner. When first told I had IC...I could not run...it was too painful, and would only make my pain for IC worse. BUT since my IC seems to be sleeping right now (shhhh!) I have picked back up on my running (since about six months now, maybe longer, I just know I am glad I am running....)....I was a bit skeptical at first but slowed into it and now am running like I use to again! YEAH! If you are slowly getting back into it...get into a routine of walking a few minues then running a minute...walk a few minutes, then run a minute...then slowly build this up. I also only run on MY treadmill, I paid extra for it to have extra padding. So it is a controlled run. As far as diet goes...inless you are a marathon runner you really do not have to worry too much as far as refueling. A regular run of 30-40 minutes is burning about 300-400 calories. (depending on where you are training without your heart rate zone...etc) If your diet is a well balanced diet you should not have an issue with finding a balanced diet. Eat a very balanced diet. Although the IC diet can be challenging when looking at it at first glance....you can pick and choose really great high energy grains and get creative and make some great snacks/meals....to keep your energy high and your body strong...etc... I eat a lot of quinoa, millet for a high protien grain, energy food!! LOVE THIS STUFF! I love eggs for a quick protien grab! I always have boiled eggs on hand. I also have a lot of quinoa or millet already precooked and kept in the fridge to eat with almond milk (as a cereal)....you can add rasins, apples cut up to it, dates...etc. I have never heard of dandelion coffee...I have heard of the tea but not the coffee?? can you give info on this??? is it ic friendly...do you think? I know we are all different....but curious what you think? this would be very high in antioxidants. Peace Blue Last edited by bluetou; 10-12-2009, 10:18 AM.

I have been off running for awhile, but after watching the Chicago Marathon yesterday, seeing some of the athletes that were running with health challenges, I am starting again tonight. Slowly!! I think a run for IC Awareness would be great. Something like IC HOPE. I would definitley do that! and those who can't run--walk. Great idea.
I would definitely walk for IC...since I am kinda waiting around for my leg to heal, I can't run. I wonder how something like this could get started??

Hi ladies, this idea of a run for IC has really been on my mind. I've been rollercoastering the past few months but still trying to run and today actually did 5 miles for the first time in awhile. I would really like to see runs in the big cities like New York, Washington, Chicago, LA, etc. where tons of money could be raised similar to the Race/Walk for the Cure or the Leukemia and Lymphoma Society races. I'm not sure if it can be done on this large of a scale to start.....but even if something could be done in our respective communities, something to spark the movement and maybe it could turn into that? I feel like we are the redheaded stepchild of disease and that we need more funding to push research and get young med students interested in working on this issue. When Jonas Salk was working on the polio vaccine there was a huge push from the March of Dimes to GET IT DONE. I would like IC docs and researchers to GET IT DONE and soon! Because I want us all to not have to suffer anymore! I would be working on it if I could but sadly lack the medical education. Bummer for me. Wishing you all well.

I think I noticed that the eliptical machine makes my frequency worse as does tae bo and the treadmill. Walking outside doesn't seem to do anything, but it is winter so that's difficult at times. Especially today with freezing rain. Anyone else have this problem. How can I get a good cario work out. I do yoga and I can lift weights without issues. Its just the cario?
signature: What helps Elmiron 200 mg bid Wellbutrin XL anxiety and depression Oxybutynin ER 10 mg Yoga tried and failed Cardura, Hytrin, Flomax, Prosed DS, Vesicare, Pyridium, Cystoprotek (caused GI problems), Lyrica, Pamelor "We can't wait until the storm is over. We need to learn to dance in the rain."

contents: It could be. I think the jarring can cause issues. It does seem a little strange that you didn't have this problem while walking outdoors but do with the treadmill. Are you wearing good (shock absorbing) shoes? I wonder if you may have been more relaxed, maybe taking it a bit easier while walking outside? The only thing I can think of to suggest is to back off of both the intensity and duration of your routine to get your bladder calmed. Then start again, doing half (or even less) of what you think you should do and slowly increase the time & degree of difficulty over a period of weeks/months to see what happens. This is what I had to do. Eventually I was able to build up to a good workout session without IC consequences. Vicki
signature: "The happiest people don't necessarily have the best of everything; they just make the best of everything they have."

contents: I am having the same trouble. I am up to 1,000 steps now, but at a snail's pace. I am lifting weights - only have the strength for the 3 pounders, but it is something! Skyn
signature: IBS diagnosed - 1987 Severe IC diagnosed - July 10, 2009 (Sister diagnosed 2004) IC/IBS WILL NOT DEFEAT ME OR DEFINE ME Allergic to: Penicillan, Imitrex, Levbid, Loestrin, Detrol, Cipro, Morphine, Dilaudid, Pryridium Plus, Fentyl,Toradol, Neurontin, Zoloft, Amitriptyline, , Elmiron, Atarax, Levaquin and most generics. Tried: Dye Free Benadryl (nausea) and Prelief Trying: Valium Suppositories, Vitamin E Suppositories, Estrace Cream, Marcaine Home Instills and Lutera. Emergency Flare Meds: Percocet (pain management), Valium (anxiety management) and Pyridium (before installations). What's working: Cystoprotek, Shaklee Pro/Prebiotics and DH Aloe Vera. * Stay with me God the night is dark, the night is cold. My little spark of courage dies. The night is long. Be with me God and make me strong.

contents: Walking is something I do almost everyday.I am sure to walk where there are bathrooms nearby if needed.Sometimes me and hubbie walk 4 1/2 miles.

My physical therapist told me to avoid certain machines & exercises that stess the pelvic floor muscles. Can you look into seeing a physical therapist to get some PT for your pelvic floor? I did the recumbent bike with no problem. Maybe avoid anything jarring for awhile & see if it helps?

I can't go fast on my treadmill. Actually for the past three months I haven't been able to use it due to a neck problem. Hopefully the neck pain will be resolved soon so I can get back to walking. My usual on the treadmill is 45 minutes per day. Donna

contents: Exercise Machines My PT told me that Elliptical aggravates sciatic problems and PFD because the feet are not close together, the foot pads are positioned more for a long legged male. She said any exercise machine that does not move the hips and feet straight ahead such as the EP, stairclimber, Kick boxing all aggravate IC and PT. Believe it or not a basic aerobics class without the step may work for you.
signature: Sammi Meds: Melatonin 3mg @ bedtime if needed. Estrogen 1.5 mg troche and 0.1 mg Estrace cream. Diagnosis: IC, PFD (both in remission)

contents: I find that by exercising especially on elliptical, my bladder feels better.
signature: In memory of my beloved best friend in the whole world! Timmy (West Highland White Terrier) 11/24/04-9/5/2011 God Bless, Anna

contents: Hey all! I hope that all of you are having great holidays. I recieved some yoga and pilates books for Christmas. I was wondering how many of you did either yoga or pilates or both and how has this worked out for you? Thanks for your responses!

Medications: IC: -Amitryptyline 10 mg Fibromyalgia: -Neurontin (in place of lyrica because I can't afford it- lol) 300 mg x3 a day Flexeril. 10 mg. As needed. - Endometriosis: -Microgestrin. First laparoscopy showed moderate to severe endo

contents: Happy Holidays! I've done both yoga and pilates with mixed results. Yoga seems to work better for me, because I could choose which stretches and how far to take them. If anything ever got uncomfortable for me or began to put too much pressure on my abs, I would stop. Pilates is so "core" based though, that it's hard to stray from those movements. For me, extended and repetitive ab movements cause some soreness in my bladder which causes pain. Good luck with both of them or whichever you choose to take part in
signature: 23, symptoms for 9 years, severe for 7.5. Diagnosed for 6.

contents: I do some yoga (I got CDs on Viniyoga which is therapeutic yoga) and none of it has bothered the IC. It actually helps me feel somewhat better (with the fibromyalgia).
signature: Currently dealing with IC (diagnosed May 2001), Fibromyalgia (January 2000) and ADHD (1998 sometime) and vulvodynia (Jan. 08) I work in the schools helping students with disabilities. I am a speech language pathologist working as an Assistive Technology Specialist. A very do-able job with IC. Medications Meds that I take: Elmiron 2 x day ; Effexor XR 37.5 ; Ritalin; Nasonex; Atarax 10 mg (night); Lidocaine 5%; monthly B-12 shots; Pyridium as needed Meds I am trying Urell Supplements that help: Condroitin Sulfate; Calcium Citrate; Magnesium Glycinate; Olive Leaf Extract; daily probiotic Other treatments that help: Physical Therapy for PFD; Massage for Fibro; Chiropractic for Fibro; IF Unit (like a tens unit) for pain

contents: My husband and I are trying yoga for stress release. So far we have only done easy floor positions, and I haven't had any problems. Yoga seems to work better for me right now then my elliptical machine, I tryed that last night and couldn't get off the 1st level of resistance!
signature: Diagnosed IC Jan 2008 What works for me: Singulair, Elmiron, Hydroxyzine, doing right for my body (light exercise, eating healthy, plently of sleep), OTC allergy meds What does not work for me: MSM/Glucosamine (no effect what-so-ever), cystoprotek (flair city!), tofranil (so constipated it made the whole situation worse) birth control pills (to many negative side effects)

contents: I want to just cry. As if giving up all the foods I love wasn't enough. I just FINALLY lost 40lbs. After two kids it was a long haul. How did I lose this weight? Mostly by becoming addicted to Spin classes. (Indoor cycling) I hate exercising, but this has become a passion. So much so, that I wanted to become an instructor. I'm now reading everywhere that bike riding is a no-no. I just found my one hour of sanity and now I feel like that's being taken away? I do notice sometimes that it aggravates my crotch region, but other times the exercise makes me feel better. I just don't know what to do about this. I'm so sad.

Some IC-ers are able to tolerate recumbent bikes better than regular ones, I don't know if that would be close enough to be satisfying? I'm ok with the elliptical trainer as long as I keep the resistance low. Maybe that would work for you? The rowing machine is also fine for me. Walking is one of the most IC friendly ways to exercise. Just listing some alternatives to consider... I totally get what you're saying, though! I gained a lot of weight with IC, mainly from not exercising like I used to and overdoing the comfort foods... Exercise is important and I think you'll find a way to do that. Congratulations on the 40 pounds! That's a huge accomplishment

contents: if it doesn't make you hurt, then don't stop!!!!!!!! even if it does make your bladder hurt, a hot pad right after over your bladder and the surrounding area's may help to head off the flare. you may find that once you start treating your bladder, it may not bother you at all. ic does change your life, but you will get back to a point where you will have a good life that you love!!!!!!
signature: Current: -pelvic floor therapy, -weekly uracyst instills

contents: Honestly, the thought of having to go back to walking etc makes me want to leap off a bridge. I've only been Spinning since June, but I go almost every day because after 30 years of hating exercise I have FINALLY found an exercise that I love and am passionate about....again, to the point of wanting to make a career of it. Unfortunately ignorance is bliss on this one when it comes to the benefits. Walking for an hour and I'll burn under 200 calories...Spin for an hour and I can burn 550. If I do an elliptical I have the resistance and hill cranked up. I have never been in this good a shape before...the irony that I just started to get healthy and then this happens 6 months later is not lost on me! I just can't stand to think that battling the depressed feelings I already have about losing my favorite drinks and foods (COFFEE!) etc, and not feeling myself for the rest of my life, will then have to endure the added sadness of not getting my stress relief of my Spin class and very possibly gaining weight. I was a 14 in June and I'm now a 6 for the first time in my life. I really don't want to give that up too. The symptoms that I have are really random and come and go during the day so it's hard to say if the exercise does anything. I usually feel BETTER while I'm exercising and afterward. I definitely felt bad Spinning the day after I got cathed...that was stupid! But I'll feel symptoms on days I don't exercise, and feel pretty good on days that I do! So I'm basically really confused! I just want this to all go away! 6 weeks ago I was just living my life....now everything has changed and it's overwhelming me to say the least. :-(

You might want to consider backing off a little until you feel better, rather than giving it up. It could be that if you go a little slower or a little less time, you could still enjoy it. I love "spinning" but the spinning I do involves a spinning wheel and making yarn. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: Hi grays! I know exactly how you feel and it really is so frustrating! I used to run 4 miles everyday, including sprinting. Now that just seems such a huge no-no. I have tried to substitute other less jarring cardio such as the arc trainer or eliptical. And yes I know it doesn't burn calories as fast..it's kind of a trade off for me. I just make myself do the arc trainer or elliptical until I reach the calories I would have burned running. So far I haven't flared from doing this. But I'm like you, off and on symptoms. I will say though I haven't touched a spin bike since this summer and I'm scared to now. I have done a lot of the reclining bike though... I'm a health/fitness nut and do yoga 4 times a week also, I bet this really helps relaxing those muscles after the cardio. I know everyone is different but if isn't making you flare I don't really see why you can't still do it. I would try maybe doing it only a couple times and week and supplement it with some stretching/muscle relaxing yoga classes (it will help with your mind too!) good luck to you! I'm right there with you!
signature: best, Kate I am a generally happy 23 year old, newly diagnosed(Nov 2009), newly-wed living in Tampa. I graduated from college in Boston in May. And since then have been trying to get my pain regime for this disease under control! Medications currently taking: Elmiron (2 in the morning 1 at night) Benadryl (1 in the morning and 1 at night) Vesicare (1 a day) Prosed as needed I am constantly looking for alternatives to these medications to heal my body! I am open to suggestions (thinking about aloe, cystoprotek, accupuncture, etc) until then I am keeping my fingers crossed that elmiron works for me!!! I started PT last week....this consists of electro stimulation through my ankle of my prudential nerve. oh yeah I also practice yoga every day I can and am wishing I could work out like I used to! I am lucky to have an extremely supportive husband and family Thank you all for your help and support! It means more than I could ever say in words "Standing on a hill in my mountain of dreams, telling myself it's not as hard as it seems." -Led Zeppelin

contents: Hi Kate. Thanks! It's like the thought of giving up one more thing is more than I can mentally handle right now. Like I mentioned before, my 4 year old is special needs and I have a 1 year old so I had a few things that would make my day a little easier and that I'd look forward to...coffee, something chocolate, a diet rootbeer, spinning. Honest to goodness those were my four treats that helped me through rough patches in my day up until mid November. It's like I'm in shock. Did Spinning or running make you flare? Is that why you haven't touched one? Like I said, it's hard to say. There have been times where I've felt worse after Spinning....but sometimes not. And there are times I feel worse and I haven't exercised that day. There is no A-Ha moment. About 3 weeks into my symptoms I thought maybe the Spinning was hurting me because this all started with a UTI and I continued Spinning, coffee etc while recovering from the UTI. I thought maybe I wasn't letting myself heal so I didn't Spin for a week. I didn't see any change. The diet and aloe, and Cystoprotek and Prelief are the things I've changed and I've gone from the mild burny feeling all the time, to not much of anything anymore. It comes and goes. It's never painful...it's just there. And mildly annoying. DH and I haven't had sex since the UTI because I'm so afraid it's going to hurt. I'd never heard of this horrendous condition before until now! It's so crazy! How and what are your symptoms? Is the Elmiron helping?

I agree with Katie. I know everyone is different but if isn't making you flare I don't really see why you can't still do it. It seems we all have varying triggers with IC and just like one person can eat yogurt while the next cannot, some seem to be able to tolerate exercise with little or no problems. The big question is really, -how it affects you? That "aha moment" can be difficult to pin down, but I guess I would need to see a correlation between what I was doing and an increase in symptoms before I would give up something that I enjoy. I also feel better while exercising and afterwards. I can go too far, and then I know it. The "aha moment" I get from over-exertion is as strong as the one that came about eight hours after eating that pickled herring last Christmas! Vicki
signature: "The happiest people don't necessarily have the best of everything; they just make the best of everything they have."

contents: I know, giving up things are hard but I have learned to make exchanges and try some new things. It doesn't make it so bad if you put it that way.... I like having a little treat for myself too. Luckily I never got into coffee so I drank tea anyways and peppermint tea after dinner at this time of year is fun and also soothing to your bladder! My favorite treat though is kozy shack rice pudding. I tried it since I knew it was on the safe list and let me tell you I can't get enough!! Loosing weight can be hard since we could eat all carbs if we wanted to! A little tip, if you are trying to loose weight but need to have grains pick quinoa. It is the highest protein you can get(even rivals meat) and wayyy more healthy for you. I get filled it up from a couple spoonfuls and a little goes a long way. Try it with some veggies, I bet you'll be surprised, and of course it doesn't bother your bladder! (found out about this when my sister had to eliminate sugar from her diet and the dr suggested this!) I also was also diagnosed in november. I don't know everything but I'm learning as much as I can! Deep down I knew I had this for it least a year, just in denial about having a disease. Now that I know for sure I've found accepting it is helping me heal and obviously I feel better because of it...I know it's hard to stay positive, I have my bad days too I tried running and I felt like I could feel my bladder bumping up in down. Sounds crazy and it could have been in my head but either way I had to give in and stop. You don't have to gain your weight back just because of this disease. Give yourself a break you've worked really hard and I'm sure you will find ways to manage this, we all have to right? My symptoms are mostly urgency. Most of the day I have the feeling like I have to go and if I give in it is a vicious cycle and I could be going back to the bathroom every 5 minutes. With some hard work though I've been retraining my bladder with great results! Still get that twinge though and it takes all my might to ignore it but when I do the pay off is great! When I am flaring though look out...terrible lower back pain and lower abdominal pain. I live with a heating pad on me during those times..and take lots of warm baths. I have only been on elmiron for 5 weeks now. Obviously it takes much longer than that amount of time to work, although my husband has said he has noticed a huge difference in me. I think that is due to me being more positive and telling myself I am getting better and giving myself to time to heal...So maybe I'll let you know in a few more months? That's great cystoprotek and aloe are helping you!! that will be my next course of action if it's determined elmiron isn't right for me...would love to go natural anyways let me know if you need anything else!! Don't give up, you have a lot of support here!

contents: it is sooooo overwhelming at first!!!! if spinning helps you get through it and you feel that passionately about it, don't change a thing!!!!!!!!!!!! maybe when you feel a little less of the shock and grief of diagnoses, you'll be able to put forth the effort to do a little investigating and see how or if it effects your symptoms. until then, i would put giving it up out of your mind for a bit!!!
signature: Current: -pelvic floor therapy, -weekly uracyst instills

contents: Thanks everyone. You're awesome. Sorry if I'm being a bit whiny, this just came out of no where. I have always been healthy...had a couple UTI's in my college years, but since then 7 years UTI free. No problems at all except some leaking when doing jumping jack type exercise. No fibrodmialga, IBS etc. And I just turned 30. I blame this all on a thong!! LOL! I bought a couple of thongs for a wedding weekend with DH, thinking I was cute. I slept in one and we did have sex once. That was the moment. I caught a UTI with e.coli, but the symptoms never went away. Just like that. And here I am. The burning urethra has changed to now a weird pressure down in the vaginal/butt area. Like a baby's head pushing down. It doesn't hurt. Just weird and annoying. Anyone know of that feeling? Period is due soon. Maybe it's that? Kate, I'd do aloe and Cystoprotek along with the Elmiron. I couldn't hurt! Hopefully it would help you heal faster! :-)

The secret to a successful life is moderation. Even in exercise. I get frustrated that some of the exercises I want to do cause increased frequency so I limit those. We have to accept where we are and what are bodies are capable of. NOBODY is perfect. I think asking yourself, what the motivation for exercise is? Is it to be healthier or is it to maintain a perfect weight? If it is to maintain a perfect weight then the motivation is caused by an irrational belief that your weight is a measure of who you are. Weight is definitely not a measure of who you are. There is so much more to being human than how much you weigh. If your motivation to exercise is to keep flexible and keep yourself in shape then yoga and walking will do that and both are IC friendly. Good luck.

contents: Hi! The reason I have been hesistant to try the aloe and cystoprotek along with the elmiron is I have heard some controversy about the aloe and cystoprotek coating competing with what the elmiron is trying to do. If this is the case, I'm paying for the elmiron so I might as well give it the best shot I can without interfering with it. I'm not positive this is the case but it's worth giving some though too, you know? hope you're having a good day!!

contents: I posted before in this category - I love to run and don't want to stop. I went through several months when I stopped because I feared pain. My husband asked "Does it hurt when you run?" Yes. "Does it hurt when you don't run?" Yes. "Then why stop running if it hurts either way?" Wow, he had a point. And so I run because I want to and I'm not going to stop until the pain completely paralyzes me and it's 100% not possible. I am doing it anyway, this BS is not going to stop me yet. I hope all of you who are exercising continue to do so and show this mess of IC/PFD whatever in the world it is that you have (because I'm still not exactly sure) who is boss!

I know a good number of people on the board can't exercise fully. I know I wasn't able to until recently after completing months of PT, and some peoples' bladders are just sensitive to the impact. But for those who can exercise, just last week I got Jillian Michael's 30 Day Shred. She is one of the trainers from The Biggest Loser. The DVD was only $10 and it is composed of 3 separate 20 minute workouts. I love it because it's a quick workout but she combines two muscle groups in each exercise you do, so if you are doing bicep curls, you're also doing lunges. After just one week I am definitely getting stronger and even took off about a pound and a half of this medicine weight I've put on lately. Anyway, if you can exercise I recommend trying it out, it's been great!
signature: Update! Feeling much better these days and no longer on any meds! Pelvic pain began July 2008. Urinary frequency began in November of 2008. IC Doctor says I'm one of the worst frequency patients he has had. Things that helped me the most: IC Diet, Elavil (30mg), Elmiron since June '09 (500mg/day). Previously tried and quit: one series of six instillations, Neurontin, Ditropan, Oxytrol, electric stimulation to the pelvic floor, Desert Harvest Aloe, Cystoprotek and Flomax, Hydroxyzine (50mg), Alesse Birth control (this helped my period flares when my symptoms used to be bad), PTNS (I think this helped). [/COLOR][/SIZE][/FONT]

contents: During my PT appointment today, the therapist told me why exercise helps us to feel better, or our bladders anyway. I noticed some old posts asking why exercise makes their symptoms better. I guess the increased blood flow from cardio exercise helps to flush out the toxins that build up in the bladder and elsewhere. And the flushing out of those toxins makes the bladder feel better. I noticed after I workout, I (usually) feel better for about 2 hours afterwards. I know exercise hurts a lot of people with IC, too. I guess because the bladder and nearby aggravated muscles get moved around and irritated. But if you can find any way to do cardio, even if it's only with your upper body, I would recommend it. It really does help with the pain and irritation. My therapist recommended 20-30 minutes of cardio a day to get that blood moving. Last edited by musiclover; 07-27-2009, 12:21 PM.

contents: Briza, I loved Curves! I walk between 30 and 90 minutes everyday. During cold or rain, I use my treadmill. This time of year I walk my neighborhood. Our street is a 1/2 mile long horseshoe shape, so round trip it is 1 mile. I will walk it 2 to 4 times and I am never more than 5 minutes from my house. Just that alone gives me peace of mind. The repetitiveness makes my husband crazy, but I'm over it. Sounds like you have a great PT!
