The best way to get back into exercise is start out with a low enough intensity that your muscles are not stiff and you are not getting any burn in your bladder. Start very small, add to it very, very slowly after a few months you will be able to workout without any big problems. I have been feeling good for a couple months and decided to try and walk again yesterday. Walked abou 15 minutes slowly. I got back and within 15 minutes I was flaring. It is even worse today. Last summer I could walk 2 miles a day no problem and then all of a sudden it started causing flares! WTH?? Any thoughts?

Lactic Acid? Hey All, Physical activity--just about all physical activity for me --causes A LOT of the ongoing bladder pain that I have on a daily basis. My pain usually holds steady at "miserable moderate" on a daily basis, but can easily soar to severe whenever I do much of anything more than the basics of just moving around the house. I have been assuming that this is due to the unavoidable circumstance of physical activity requiring my pelvic muscles to move, which in turn works my bladder muscles causing my mangled bladder to hurt. But--if lactic acid is also released from my muscles whenever I am moving around, then this is perhaps yet another reason for my daily pain? Is this a "double whammy" situation? Gez....is there anyway around this dilemma? Not only do I seriously need some exercise and muscle toning, but heck...I have got to get to the point where I can just do the basics of life without being in pain! Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated! ~Beth
signature: I am not a physician, so please do not mistake any of my comments as medical advice. I am just a fellow sufferer sharing my experience with IC. Scheduled for Radical Cystectomy & Indiana Pouch, July 16, 2009 Hit with severe pelvic pain on 6/14/08 (7:05pm): GYN, MRI, CT, laparoscopy, colonoscopy: All Clear DX'ed with Severe IC: Findings during cystoscopy and hydro-distention on 9/11/08 showed: scattered areas of hemorrhage and glomerulizations, w/bloody efflux. Update: Had second cysto/hydro on 5/21/09: My uro said "there were thousands of lesions" all over my bladder. Current Meds: Bladder Instills (self-adm 3 x per week or PRN); MS Contin; Lortab (PRN); Zanaflex; Maxide; Estradiol Patch Other helpful remedies: IC Diet (very important!) especially no chemical additives or preservatives; Drink 1.5+ gal. of pH neutral water per day (I test my water frequently! This is very, very important!!); Prelief; Heating pad; and lots of hugs from my wonderful hubby! Meds I have tried, but could not take: Amitriptyline & Imipramine (both made me mentally nutzoid, gave me nightmares, and neither one touched the pain); Lyrica (didn't touch the pain either, plus it made me very nauseous after eating and then it triggered my IBS); Cymbalta (didn't touch the pain either and it made me horribly depressed--all I did was sleep and cry!); Percocet (did very little for the pain and gave me headaches); Discontinued using Sodium Bicarb & Kenalog in my instills (it burned really bad!). Discontinued Meds: Elmiron (replaced by Heparin in instills); Bentyl (antispasmodic and anticholinergic; Zanaflex works much better for me!); Valium Suppositories History: IBS (spastic colon) since 1982; Partial hysterectomy 1985--completed in 2002 with extensive adhesion removal (I think this surgery was my IC trigger!); Non-painful urinary frequency since 2002; No history of frequent UTI's; HPV and abnormal paps 1/07; Sporadic PFD

contents: Same Here I, too, cannot exercise in any way anymore. In fact, the only relief I get is from 2 Percoset, 1 2mg. Valium and laying flat on my back. I tried Lyrica, and it made it much worse. I ended up in the emergency room from that med and needed to be cateritized and given a shot of Dilaudid. I can't even do the slightest bit of exercise without it flaring. In fact, this all started when I began going on the treadmill and taking exercise and yoga classes! I am going to see a urogynecologist on Thurs. Urologists and gynecologists have not helped me or diagnosed anything. Krisssy

What if movement always makes you worse? When I first started having symptoms (long before diagnosis) I had been dieting and exercising, including regular elliptical trainer time. I gave it up over time, though, because I would get physically ill trying to push through the pain (nauseous, shakey, sweating). I am at a point now where I only do ANY activity (including cooking, house cleaning) in twenty-minute increments with at least an hour or more rest in between. Most of my DAY is spent resting, which worries me, because I KNOW it isn't healthy for the rest of my body. Hubby is doubling size of the garden, and last summer I was the ONLY one doing anything in it, and I'm really starting to get worried and stressed over this. He seems to be expecting the same me that there was two years ago, and I don't know how to get physically well enough to spend several hours in the hot sun weeding and hoeing, etc.? Can I get to a point where I can have four hours of physical stamina by May when I'm not even in my first remission yet? :-S
signature: Dx with IC March 2, 2009. If something I say sounds helpful, please discuss it with your doctor. I am only a patient sharing anecdotes of my own and others' experiences.

contents: Originally posted by MommaWuzzi I am at a point now where I only do ANY activity (including cooking, house cleaning) in twenty-minute increments with at least an hour or more rest in between. Most of my DAY is spent resting, which worries me, because I KNOW it isn't healthy for the rest of my body. Dear Wuzzi, I know exactly what you mean. I too am very worried about my lack of exercise, but I cannot find anything physical that doesn't make my bladder go ballistic! Even Pilates makes me hurt... I have lost almost 50 pounds over the past nine months (severe pain really burns up the calories!) so I am beginning to look like a deflated balloon, plus my muscle tone is weakening more every day. I truly don't know what to do! As far as your hubby is concerned, it looks like he will be getting a "reality check" very soon... Lots of hugs! ~Beth

contents: How do you exercise when just walking around the house doing the dishes puts you in pain? I saw some pictures today that my husband took over the weekend and am just disgusted with myself. I know I've got to do something but just don't know what to do. I'm trying to stick to the IC diet, which I'd hope would cause some weight loss, but nothing yet. I know the best thing for me to do is to exercise, but how am I supposed to exercise when just standing at the sink to do the dishes causes me so much pain? I tried to have a short walk this weekend with my hubby and puppy and we didn't even go half a mile before I was miserable. What do I do?
signature: Tobie (aka Westiemom) IC Diagnosed 1-26-09 with cysto/hydro (Suffering since 2006 - Maybe longer) (Neurontin, Utira C, Vicodin, DMSO Instills) PFD Diagnosed 2006 IBS w/C Diagnosed 2006 High Blood Pressure (Not a problem at the moment ) Severe Migraines (Ambien, Elavil, Midrin, Fioricet, Phenegren, Cephadyn, IV DHE when really bad w/IV steroids & IV magnesium) Depression - who wouldn't with all these problems (Pristiq) Hysterectomy 5/2005 due to Adenomyosis & Endometriosis Bursitis (both hips) TMJ Past IC Treatments: Elmiron, Atarax, Elavil, Ditropan, Pyridium Past Migraine Preventives: Elavil, Pamelor, Wellbutrin, Topomax, Depakote, Baclofen, Zonegran, Verapamil Past Migraine Abortives: Imitrex, Frova, Maxalt, Amerge, Migranal, Relpax, Axert, Vicoden

contents: are you able to get to a pool or somewhere to swim. Maybe just some realy light arm and leg exersize without using your pelvis. Have you tried yoga. I think there are some videos avaible on this website that you can buy that are for people with pelvic pain to help exersize. I do walk and have less of problem than walking, running seems to be and issue with me and lifting weights always works as long as I avoid crunchies. Maybe your doc has some ideas. Good luck

We do have a pool that we put up for the kids during the summer and we were talking about putting it up next weekend to give the water time to warm up. I've done yoga in the past and loved it, but I don't know any where here where I can take a class and $ is really tight at the moment. I tried DVRing a yoga program that comes on in the morning but it was pretty advanced and it's been so long since I did yoga it was really hard. I'll have to try it again. I do worry about all the bending and twisting with yoga though since I do have pain when I bend over at times. I've looked at the products available here but I didn't remember seeing any videos. I'll have to look again and see what the prices are like. I've had weight issues all my life but I've always been able to exercise to at least stay in some resemblence of decent shape. This IC thing has done so much to my self esteem the last several months, not being able to do anything about the weight just makes it that much harder. Thanks!

contents: I just received the New Dawn Pilates for people with pelvic pain as a B.day gift (funny what you ask for when you have IC eh?). It seems much more gentle than some of the stuff I find on TV (like on On Demand) and you can customize it to just do the arms workout or just the "had a bad day" prgrm. I plan to lay off the stomach section until I feel up to it. FYI I find I can't walk distances outdoors as much because the slight hills set off Pelvic Floor stuff but on good days I can do the treadmill with zero incline. LOL-J

I stopped running when I had a bad IC flare (May 30) and have been just walking or doing yoga for the past 6 weeks while I took care of getting a diagnosis (which just came in "official" today for IC). But I'm feeling better (some pelvic pain, and some urgency, but nothing that isn't manageable - a lot better than it used to be)...and I miss running. Is there anyone out there with IC who is able to go running? How much? Do you still flare if you take it easy? Or have you just had to totally give it up? I have wanted to let my body rest and get better (and it has been getting better). But I also know that running is good for my body (and my soul!). This feels like a big loss to me to give up running, so I'd love to hear about other people's experiences. Thanks, Steph

Steph, I use to run and I always had flares afterwards. This was before I was dxed with IC. I finally had to give up running. I use to love it also! It was just too hard on my bladder. You may be able to keep running after you give your bladder a rest. I do believe I have read that others were able to run. Running is a great stress relief. I now do zumba and yoga and it has been fine with my bladder. I hope it works out for you. Nina
signature: Meds on now: dextro 10mg 5000mg Vitamin D- when tested, I was sooo low. Compound Hormones- Had hysterectomy when 24, ovaries out 37 At night: 4mg Zanaflex- Fibro & PFD plendil- Raynalds I have Fibro, IC, IBS, PFD, Raynauds. Tried: Past Meds Lexpro- changed to Cymbalta for better pain relief –off both Ultram – three times a day. Helped , I was tired of always being so tired. Elmiron-took for a year- Topamax-Did not need anymore since on dextro. Atarax- did not need anymore Pictures: Hubby & Me, Ozzy, My three Sons!! What a proud Mother I am!! .

contents: i guess i'm a bit backwards... I never used to run, but since i was diagnosed with IC, I've started! Well, I don't know if what I do qualifies as "running"... more like jogging, or windsprinting. But I felt that I wanted to try to really be good to my body, and figured exercising is a must. I do notice when I do the windsprints, I tend to have a bit of a flare that night. It seems the harder I push it, the more likely I am to be in pain. However, jogging hasn't really bothered me. I jog about 2-3 miles a day. Right now, I'm actually flaring, so the past 3 days I haven't had the motivation to get out and jog...
signature: ~Cailyn~ first diagnosed with IC May 2008 second opinion and diagnosis June 2008 Elavil: 15mg a day Elmiron: 100mg 3x a day Pyridium: 200mg a day or as needed ...and I follow the IC diet PREGNANT!!! Due April 22, 2009!!!

contents: I was running for about 6 months last year with no problems bladder-wise, but I've also been symptom free for a few years. Running didn't set it off again, though - I would run about 3 miles.
signature: Kim Diagnosed August 2001 Current IC meds: Elmiron (since 2001), Levaquin (one pill after intercourse to prevent UTIs), Effexor (for depression & anxiety) Past IC meds: Amitriptyline (Elavil), Hydroxyzine (Vistaril), Detrol LA, Lexapro (for depression & anxiety, but also helped my IC) (They all helped, but I was able to discontinue them.) I've been virtually symptom free and able to eat & drink whatever I'd like for about 8 years now. ***************************** “We who lived in concentration camps can remember the men who walked through the huts comforting others, giving away their last piece of bread. They may have been few in number, but they offer sufficient proof that everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of the human freedoms -- to choose one's attitude in any given set of circumstances, to choose one's own way.” ~ Viktor Frankl “You cannot control what happens to you, but you can control your attitude toward what happens to you, and in that, you will be mastering change rather than allowing it to master you.” ~ Brian Tracy

contents: I run and love it. However, if I am flaring, I go back to walking or roller blading. Running does not cause me to flare, however, as long as I stay hydrated. Good luck!

running I am a runner....well, my job is fitness, I am a personal trainer, fitness instructor and a yoga practitioner, I use to run....but actually running is was started all my problems. I mean I am sure that MY HIGH CITRUS diet did something too.....plus being prescribed two drugs with sulfate that I was allergic too did not help with lowering my immunity and making me more suseptible to IC also..... BUT I was on a role of running, three mornings in a row.....it felt good to get back into my runs (husband on vacation so he was able to watch the kids while I ran)..... third day into running, that evening, I felt as though I had a prolapsed bladder...pressure in pelvis....etc...flare..... SO running is totally OUT for me...I will never run again. FOR ME it is not worth it. The pain of that flare (and it was my first ever) lasted over 8 days. I do not take rest well...so resting was very hard for me.....SO for me RUNNING is a huge no no! BUT again as I read on these boards, everyone is different....?? so hard to say. I am very active (as stated it is my job)...but I will not teach another fitness class (with all that moving and bouncing) NOPE! done with that. I will stick to my personal training, and my yoga. MY LIFESTYLE is totally changed. I do not want to have that pain again..... kind regards, Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:40 AM.

I HATE running with a passion, yet how I feel about swimming is similar, so we have that understanding in mind. I did a 25K swim earlier this month and the extra training didn't affect the IC symptoms (except holding my urine longer during the race, but that wasn't bad). My pain is 24/7 anyway and I can't do anything about it, so I just keep going and leave it at that. I have a harder time sleeping because it's more on my mind then. Running again should be o.k. It doesn't make the problem worse, but if you have other problems that could affect it, it would be good to seek medical advice about that.

Thought I'd report in. I'm continuing to feel better (provided I stick to the IC diet). So I've been running a few times. I run two minutes, and then walk one, for about 30 minute total. I'm feeling very out of shape, but otherwise it is going well! I'll scale back if I start hurting again, and I'll keep walking and doing yoga too, but I'm so relieved that I can reclaim this part of my life for now.

I Miss Working Out I don't like running, but this thread reminds me of how badly I miss exercise, and how I need to get back into it somehow. I used to work out after I got home from work. I'd work out around 4 days a week, at home, doing cardio and toning. About 2 months before my IC symptoms started, a new person moved into the apartment below me and my exercising bothered her, so I stopped. (She was always home. All the years before, the apartment-dwellers were at work.) I did continue to do weights. When my IC started I got so bad that I even gave up my weekly yoga class. I couldn't get through it without wanting to use the bathroom, and that really depressed me at the time. At first I actually lost weight, probably because I was so stressed I lost much of my appetite. But now over a year later the couch-potato-ness is beginning to show. My symptoms aren't as bad now so I have started doing some of my toning videos again. I'd like to start walking but it's too darn hot here during the summer. I forget where I read it (I don't think it was here) but I read that there's something called "runner's bladder" which is what happens when the inside surfaces of an empty bladder rub together from vigorous activity, which then causes irritation, and often blood in the urine. Has anyone else heard of this? I always wonder if this contributed to my IC, because I always used to urinate before working out, even if my bladder felt empty. Pucca

HI Pucca, I feel your pain about not exercising.....I am an avid exerciser and if i miss a day I do not feel well. When i had my first flare last month, I was down and out for over a week, and I thought my BODY was going to explode becuase it could not exercise. I was in so much pain anyway from the ic....but the thought of not working out was hard, my body started to tighten up (as I exercise also because if i do not then my back injury acts up), but my mind feels cob webby. I am walking everyday now (I will not run...as my symptoms worsen with running, and I do yoga, and this is what I do daily. You mention it it too hot, have you tried getting up early like at six am and going for a walk, this is what I do, and it is very refreshing, the sun has yet to come fully out and be hot. (just a thought) As far as yoga goes, I am a yoga practitioner, and if you were in my class making constant washroom trip, this would not bother me in the least, I would be proud that you were standing up and taking care of yourself during class. I have always taught my students that yoga is about nuturing your body and taking care of you first. I would tell any student through practice at any time they wanted to stop and go into child pose or quiet mountain to do so, to listen to their body. I think you getting back into yoga would be quite benificial, it is so cleansing and healing, and I know it is allowing me to look past my pain, giving me inner strength. kind regards, Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:38 AM.

Tanya -- Thanks for the kind words. I wish I could get up early to walk but I already get up at 5:15 in the morning for work, and that has already gotten more difficult since IC and hot flashes now disrupt my sleep. So for now I'm gonna wait for the more bearable days and walk after work, or during my lunch break at work. My yoga teacher, bless her heart, has called me regularly since I left to see how I'm feeling. I told her I was going to aim to come back in September. I used to be so flexible that she'd often have me demonstrate the poses for the class - so I'd better get practicing because I've gotten really stiff and weak! Pucca

running uggghhhh, i am in so much pain right now because of running on the treadmill I started running again on Monday, for only 10 minutes, then again on Tuesday and today, wednesday and I think that I may be paying for it now, I am in an awefully painful flare, the flare with the inside of my thighs burning and tingling, If I could wish for anything in the world it would be for me to be healthy I am feeling so frustrated because I was feeling really good about myself after my workouts and running was good for my soul, IDK what to do or think anymore, but I don't want to quit working out

I don't know how it will go for you, but I wanted to let you know that I am back to my running now. I totally understand the frustration of not being able to do something that really feeds your soul. I was in pain most of the summer, but have been able to control things better now through diet for the past few months (I still flare if I overdo things, travel, or eat the wrong thing). I don't run as hard or long as I used to, but it is good be out there. Since you are hurting now, I would say that it would be good to let your body rest and heal (maybe walks would be better for now?) and try running again slowly when you are feeling better. Take care, Steph

Have you tried speed walking? I don't do it but have seen it demonstrated. It might not be as jarring on your bladder as running. I agree that you should rest from activity for a while before trying anything.
signature: Sharon Shopping??? Did someone mention shopping? I'll get my hat... ;-) Where I can be found most days. contents: Running Thanks for the support ladies! I am feeling a whole lot better today, a nice bath seemed to help relax my body, I am definitely thinking of just sticking to the walking and mixing in some speed walking, I will let y'all know how it goes, wish me luck!!

I am sorry you flared due to running. I too was a runner....and although I said I WOULD never run because I am fearful of flaring and being set back.... I have incorporated a bit of speed to help me with my "run fix" (it is not perfect, and it not anywhere in comparrison to what I was doing....but it is a start, and I know in my heart I will someday run again). Here are a few suggestions to what I do: My cardio is daily. I do 30 minutes each day of either treadmill/or stairclimber. Neither activity bothers my IC. I am the type of person who needs to feel her heart rate up, and I need to sweat. SO I will walk on a slight incline to warm up, and then I will bump my treadmill up to a high incline, then I will lower it to a slight incline, and then I will do a slow jog with long soft strides (landing softly, not pounding). I find treadmill gentle jogging does not flare me. IF I do this on the road, forget it! If I do it in the grass, (backyard)....playing with the dog running around...etc. (as I am imbilical cord training her, she is a puppy), I do not flare. SO maybe if you try on a treadmill, my treadmill actually has a supported cushion belt (paid more money for this) so my landing is much softer. SO I have started out very lightly, at a few minutes....then 5 minutes, and I am up to 10 minutes. SO do not fret, you may run yet. But please do not try this if you are flaring now...wait till you are feeling well. Peace Blue

I am glad you got some advice/suggestions. Thank you for your post. I have stopped running but was scheduled for a half marathon a few weeks after being diagnosed. I still did it though, the small flare up was worth it!!! I am going to start gradually running again soon - I hope. It seems that running isn't as bad when there is not already a "flare up" from what I can recall before I was officially diagnosed.
signature: Joann Diagnosed October 2008

contents: I was really scared to exercise after being diagnoses with IC. I didn't want to risk a flare so I avoided it. However, after being out of the loop for about six months, my IC is at a level where I understand my body better and so decided to give the gym another whirl. I'm starting out slowly, only 30min or so two to three times a week and so far I've been doing well. I had a tiny flare the other night afterwords but nothing that a nights rest didn't cure. I used to go 6 night a week for an hour..I forgot how good (and how de-stressing) working out makes me. So, we'll see how I react in the coming weeks, but I'm satisfied with what I'm doing now because six months ago I never thought I'd be able to exersize without pain again
signature: 37mg Eavil each night 300 Elmiron a day Hydrocodiene and Pyridium as needed

contents: I just got off the treadmill and a flare started to hit me something awful (the pain right now is really bad)... so... I decided to give the ic network another look... and I saw this thread... Reading it just gave me such a feeling of support even though no one was talking to me personally. I just knew I wasn't alone... though I wouldn't wish this on anyone. I always loved working out... I miss my 3 hour runs... that feeling that I was just floating on air... so refreshed. It's such an invigorating feeling. It's nice that others here understand what it's like to feel that and then to feel to have lost that as well... despite the unniceness about this condition. I wish more people would be understanding when it comes to this condition. There's a lot of people who don't exercise, so it's hard for them to understand how difficult it is to -not- exercise. I'm at a point where I'll walk on the treadmill one day... and then the next day I try to run... but I do so in small intervals (1-2 minutes of running and a 30 minute break, followed by 1-2 minutes of running and a 30 minute break). Sometimes, I'll try a 5 minute jog and take a break. In terms of stamina.... I could go for longer. It's the bladder that stops me. I just can't keep doing it. People often tell me to take it slow these days... that I can build myself back up... (even with my 1 minute or 2 minute at a time intervals they are still telling me I'm pushing myself too hard!). I know I will never be back to how I used to be. The pain makes it impossible... all it's done is put me more out of shape than I ever was in before (I don't look out of shape... but I -feel- it). I still try to run now and again, but I have to do it on a treadmill (for the most part). I have one at home... and it's not far away from the bathroom... and I have a heating pad ready if I need it. The problem is... if I -don't- push myself to run... the rest of my body starts hurting. I start aching throughout my body from the lack of movement, so I figure... if my bladder is going to hurt anyways... whether I run or not... then I might as well run... or try to do the best I can... so that the rest of me doesn't start hurting. At the very least I can control one type of pain. Often enough... my bladder wins and keeps me couch bound for days at a time... (or floor bound)... Sometimes, I feel as though I am in this constant battle with me vs me. It's so frustrating. I have tried taking it especially easy and to just stick with walking, but any amount of impact (even walking) seems to make it worse. I guess that's why I end up pushing myself... if walking or running is going to be painful, I may as well stick to the more vigorous activity... I guess I'm pretty stubborn... and should listen to my body more... but then I probably wouldn't be doing anything. lol I haven't tried yoga yet... so I have to give it a try... The problem is... if I don't exercise the rest of my body flares up. One day, the pain, from not exercising, got so bad that I woke up with the strangest strong pain in my leg. The longer I ignored it, the stronger the pain became (it hit me at around 2am after I had just fallen asleep). I got up and I had to run... I did so for 15 minutes... Then I got stuck in the bathroom for running... then my leg started hurting more for being in the bathroom and not moving... so I had to get up and run again... but I was so exhausted... ... I tried laying down to sleep again... I laid there for about an hour before the pain, in my leg, intensified again. I've never felt pain like that before... I still don't know what caused it. I can only compare it to being burned... ie if you take your burnt hand, for example, away from the ice long enough, the painful sensation of the burn continues to intensify. It didn't feel like a "burn" but it definitely intensified... It seems to happen if I give myself a couple of days of rest... I just feel so lost when things like this happen. Like many here... I just would love to be able to be pain free... have a chance at some sort of "normal" life. It's definitely something that gets taken for granted.

Hope this works for you FINALLY I have got on the IC network (my flatmate's computer prevented this for a long time because of endless crashing/internet dropping out *sigh*) Anyway, I saw your post a little while ago and wanted to post a reply because I run every morning and I have a 'system' which I use to make this possible which may work for you. However it may not because I am believed to have 'early IC' and I have not had the cystoscopy yet. This is because the first one I was booked in for didn't go ahead because I got ill due to being exposed to snotty tissues around a public computer (manners and basic hygiene are a thing of the past in Australia, apparently). Anyway, every night I take a drug called Phenergen - which is an anti-histamine - which has a sixteen hour half life. I take two tablets of the 10mg strengh but you can take the 25mg strength if you like. The side effects of this drug are ironically exactly what someone with IC needs. It makes you very drowsy (which puts you to sleep) and it has a side effect of preventing urination. So bascially you don't get up during the night to go to the toilet at all. When you wake up in the morning the drug is still in your system. It will make it a little harder to wake up then normal but not impossible. I then get dressed for my run, go to the toilet twice, and go for a fast 15 minute run. Although if you need you could take some fast absorbing pain killers with milk and biscuits 15 minutes before your run to make things more comfortable. I don't drink during my run or before, only after. Also I invested in a pair of very good runners which are designed for the way my feet are balanced and very good orthotics (because I have flat feet) so my feet are perfectly balanced when running. This means there is no jolt -effect when I run. Any jolt effect or pressure on the joints for an IC sufferer would cause bladder pain so your feet need to be well supported. Hopefully this 'system' works for you but it may not - as I said, because I appear to be only in the early stages, according to the specialist. I wish you all the best Frankie.
I like running and exercise, but when I get a flare up I swim laps and walk the dogs instead. Cycling can also be relaxing with some music on the IPOD Jules

Blah, this is really hard for me! I am sooo overweight and I know losing it and exercising will make me feel better. Here is the thing. I am one of those nutso "all or nothing" people. So I think I have to be at the gym 5 days a week for 1 hour sessions or may as well do nothing. I know, crazy huh?? With my ankle still healing, I DEFINITELY can't do too much! So, Last week I started just moving a bit more. Like at work, when running reports, I would get up and walk to the other room to the printer after each report, instead of waiting until I printed them all- up and down quite a few times! I think I am going to aim to go to the gym 2 x a week. I would like to more, but my schedule is really super hectic right now, and I don't want to set unrealistic goals. I am going to start with light treadmill walking and maybe a yoga/pilates class here and there. Once I actually get to the gym, I enjoy it- I get a "break" because they have childcare I started physical therapy for my PFD/back issues so I go twice a week and do stretches at home and at work. I signed up for Weight Watchers at home (don't really have time for meetings). I am waiting for the big box of stuff to arrive! Just wanted to get that out there, lol. Since separating from my husband, I have lost weight (stress I guess) and I want to continue on that path. I also want to make myself stronger physically, I think that will help me feel stronger emotionally.

Please start out slowly so you won't wear yourself out and backslide. I used to decide I needed more exercise so would exercise until I was totally wiped out --- then I would be too sore to do anything for a week so I'd be back where I started. I finally learned to start with a 15-minute walk, increasing five minutes every other day to get to 45 minutes. I have a treadmill so it's fairly easy to time myself. I've been sick since just before Christmas, first with a flu bug, then a sinus infection --- so I'm about ready to dust off the treadmill and start at slow speed for 15 minutes. Good luck! Donna

contents: Thanks Donna! I am bad about backsliding, so my new mantra is "something is better than nothing!"

That's a good mantra! I do think it's wise to start out slowly and work up. Otherwise you're almost certainly setting yourself up for failure. I use one of those 'as seen on tv' exercise programs, using a three months on/three months off system. My very first time, I did as much of the routines as I could and ended up hurting so bad I couldn't resume for a week! It would have been easy to stick the DVD on the shelf at that point and say, "I can't do this!" Four weeks ago I began the program again, with a starting goal of just doing half of the routines. Funny how that one little change of lowering my goal to something attainable made me feel successful! Each week I added more and am now able to do the entire DVD from beginning to end. So yep, "something is better than nothing", or maybe "Less IS more!". I hope you reach your goals! Vicki
signature: "The happiest people don't necessarily have the best of everything; they just make the best of everything they have."

contents: Part of my Christmas was a recumbent bike and I LOVE that thing. I have gained some weight due to my meds and of course being less active than I was before I was dx'd with all this stuff. The bike allows me to sit while I exercise and that, to me, is the greatest thing since sliced bread! I can really get a good workout on the bike and use hand weights at the same time to get some upper body done as well. Like Donna said, start sloooooooooooooow. I have done the same thing so many times where I go nuts and over do it and then I can't do a thing for over a week.....so go slow. Start on the treadmill or the recumbent bike and maybe try to use the hand weights if they'll allow it. I have an entire home gym upstairs and since today is my 45th birthday I'm seriously thinking about starting a real exercise program. That will start....tomorrow! LOL Let us know how you're doing. Also, let me know how the weight watchers is going. I'd love to do the at home thing as well. Sandy

My bowel ruptured, I almost died from peritonitis and spent several days in the ICU then more in a private room on the floor. If you have any questions about severe constipation from pain meds please don't hesitate to send me a message.

I was noticing with me the pain would be "better" if I wasn't so fat. Really need to get the weight off but I was wondering if anyone else noticed it too? Or it didn't make any difference. 22 Voters Yes 31.82% 7 votes No 54.55% 12 votes Maybe 13.64% 3 votes

Well, I would say that I am thin but right now I am 10lbs over my fighting weight and it is all in my belly region, and that extra weight in the belly region does cause me more discomfort when sleeping and with clothing.
Were you a boxer or is "fighting weight" mean something else?

lol, no I was not and never have been a boxer or anything like lol...by my fighting weight I mean what is my best weight where my clothes fit just right, I have no belly, and also means that I am being active and eating just right (not too much and not too little) Lately I have not been too active so belly is showing that I need to get off computer and take dog for a LONG walk everyday!!!!
I lost 15 pounds over the last year and haven't noticed a change for the better with the IC. I'm 5'-9" and weigh 155 pounds.
I have lost 45 pounds and it does seem to help with flares. I still have pfd pain at times. I am such a mess right now physically in many areas I don't know what to think.
signature: esophagus spasams dyastalic disfuntion with stiffness of right heart valve lymphedema of left leg and ankle venous insufficensy hypothyrodisim IC Pelvic Floor Disorder-having pelvic Physical Therapy breast cancer 5 yrs remission GERD colitis swollowing difficulties have throat stretched Asthma Anemia Lots of medications more than 20 rx's Lots of doctors

contents: I said no but I really do not know. I am 5'6" and weigh about 125. Before IC I weighed at the highest 140. I was on anxiety meds that were first generation that really caused weight gain slowly over time. I did not know it was the meds. (I can not take any now because they caused flares. It is a shame because I really could use them these days) No laughing here but I really thought it was age. I was a really skinny (in a funny way) kid and teenager but everyone always said it would catch up with me so I thought it had. My family is very normally built (meaning size 12 is when they are at their thinnest). So wearing an 8 or 10 at 29 was still thin. My mother always knew different for me but she would never say anything to me (I am sensitive, she is not). She knew because I am really built (long arms and legs) just like her mom who weighed between 125 and 135 the majority of her life. Sorry for going on and on and on. My opinion would be that a healthy weight for each individual could have a positive effect on their IC. I think this might be true because stress on the body and pelvic floor muscles. But a healthy weight to me is all relative to the individual. What I think might be an interesting question is how has weight loss or gain effected peoples IC? I could see where loosing weight too fast could also put stress on the body and make are bladders scream. What do you think?
signature: Daily Meds: IC-Meds Ditropan XL 5 BID, Elavil 50 QD, Elmiron 200 BID, Hydroxine 50 BID. As Needed: Bacofen 20, Prosed DS, Hydrocodone/Apap. Other Meds Adderal 10 QD, Remmeron 37 QD (I take 1 1/2 of 25 mg) With the help of my doctor moving my meds around a bit by increasing this and decreasing that I have found a good combination of meds (for now and I pray a long time from now).

contents: I remember when I first went to my gyno's office only a few days after first being hit with IC pain. That was June 18, 2008. Of course, they HAVE to weight you every time you go in (I guess they can't help themselves!! lol). Since that day I have lost over 35 pounds! And--my IC pain got progressively worse over those same past 8 months. When the weight started falling off so fast, my husband was very concerned because I was eating regularly, getting zero exercise, and had even developed a "sweet tooth" (much more so than ever before). In fact, many days I remember being constantly hungry! So, you would think that I would be 35 pounds heavier...but...that has not been the case. After considering this issue a few months ago, I became convinced that my continuous moderate-to-severe level of pain day after day, month after month, has burned my intake of calories faster than I could consume them. I have started leveling off recently, so maybe I have reached a plateau for now. Even though my pain level remains at the moderate level of 5-6 most of the time, the horrific spikes into the severe levels of 9-10+ are not happening every other day like they were for all those months. This, I think, is because I am finally getting on track with a viable pain management program. So, I can't speak for anyone but me, but losing weight did not make my pain go away--rather, I truly think that the pain made my extra weight go away!! ~Beth p.s. I am not on any meds that have weight gain as a possible side effect, so that may also be a factor for some people who might normally lose weight from their severe pain. Last edited by BT2008; 02-14-2009, 09:27 PM.
signature: I am not a physician, so please do not mistake any of my comments as medical advice. I am just a fellow sufferer sharing my experience with IC. Scheduled for Radical Cystectomy & Indiana Pouch, July 16, 2009 Hit with severe pelvic pain on 6/14/08 (7:05pm): GYN, MRI, CT, laparoscopy, colonoscopy: All Clear DX'ed with Severe IC: Findings during cystoscopy and hydro-distention on 9/11/08 showed: scattered areas of hemorrhage and glomerulizations, w/bloody efflux. Update: Had second cysto/hydro on 5/21/09: My uro said "there were thousands of lesions" all over my bladder. Current Meds: Bladder Instills (self-adm 3 x per week or PRN); MS Contin; Lortab (PRN); Zanaflex; Maxide; Estradiol Patch Other helpful remedies: IC Diet (very important!) especially no chemical additives or preservatives; Drink 1.5+ gal. of pH neutral water per day (I test my water frequently! This is very, very important!!); Prelief; Heating pad; and lots of hugs from my wonderful hubby! Meds I have tried, but could not take: Amitriptyline & Imipramine (both made me mentally nutzoid, gave me nightmares, and neither one touched the pain); Lyrica (didn't touch the pain either, plus it made me very nauseous after eating and then it triggered my IBS); Cymbalta (didn't touch the pain either and it made me horribly depressed--all I did was sleep and cry!); Percocet (did very little for the pain and gave me headaches); Discontinued using Sodium Bicarb & Kenalog in my instills (it burned really bad!). Discontinued Meds: Elmiron (replaced by Heparin in instills); Bentyl (antispasmodic and anticholinergic; Zanaflex works much better for me!); Valium Suppositories History: IBS (spastic colon) since 1982; Partial hysterectomy 1985--completed in 2002 with extensive adhesion removal (I think this surgery was my IC trigger!); Non-painful urinary frequency since 2002; No history of frequent UTI's; HPV and abnormal paps 1/07; Sporadic PFD

contents: I lost 145 pounds and was on top of the world. I was training for my first half marathon. I felt great and then my IC symptoms started. I couldn't exercise and put on 25 pounds in no time. Over the last 2 years, due to all the physical inactivity, I put on the rest of my weight. I am back to my biggest size and my bladder is better now than it was when I was at my physical best.

contents: *I think* that weight can play a factor. Maybe not 10 or 15 pounds, but I am carrying a lot of extra weight and I know that it would be better. I think about it like this- if I were to sit down and put a bowling ball on my pelvic region, would my bladder hurt worse? Probably so. So it's not too much of a stretch to think that extra body weight can aggrevate it. My dr. told me that losing 10 lbs is like 40 lbs off of your knees! ETA: I wanted to add that I don't think it will make the pain or IC "go away"- just that it would probably alleviate my symptoms to some degree. I spoke with a spine specialist about my back. All other doctors pretty much told me to lose weight to fix my back pain. I told the spine dr. "I know losing weight will help". He said he sees too many skinny people with back pain to think it would fix my back pain. He usaid that it probably would do me some good, but that it wouldn't make the pain go away.
I am on the thin side- 5'7" and 125lbs. I use to weigh 150lbs a few years ago, but lost 35lbs for no reason- (wasn't trying/no apparent health reason either)-slowly gaining some back.

Hi all, I had read that one of you talked about the wii fit and I decided to try.. I am not in condition to have an hour class at the gym at the moment.. Anyway, I love it! you can do an exercise series tailored for you with your own rithms. The most innovative thing is that you have direct feedback of your posture in the screen, where your balance is visualized; in this way you can control if you are doing the exercise right.. There are also a series of games that are not very tiring, and without you knowing you are doing at least some exercise!
We just got the Wii Fit and I haven't tried it yet. I have done the bowling, tennis, boxing, golf one and it's definitely good exercise. I will try the "Fit" today. Donna

contents: I just drink good ol' tap water. I never would if I was at the Bundy house on Married with Children though! Remember the brown water that came out of their kitchen sink in an episode?!

well water has a bad irony taste to it, and smell! I agree trying some other wters with natural sugars or none. your body needs sugar. tou have to take into the consideration of insulin. Does your body produce enough, or not enough. may have typed this not making sense, but my mom had to explain it to me as she has diabeties. All indications point that I am hypoglycemic. make sure you are not drained and feel dizzy by cutting all your sugars out at once. It kind of makes you feel like yo have vertigo going on. Although I am sure you know what you are doing, I just wanted to add this just in case you needed this info.

Mary I so relate with the weight battle! What should I eat, what should skip...will it help, will it hurt? I don't drink juice or flavored water anymore, it just makes me pee like crazy. I could only lose weight with either: a prepackage food program that was 800-1000 cal/day. Weighing out food and portions just freaked me out and I'd give up. and 1000-1200 could just dissappear like nothing when I tried to do it on my own. :::::sigh:::: Anyways the other way I've lost weight is taking phenterimine aka "phen" (which is what I'm taking now). It supresses my appetite big time! (altho we all eat when we're not hungry.) And supposedly it "may" speed up metabolism in some. Altho it's not without some side effects including anger, insomnia, any anxiety or depression you may have---it cranks up the intensity of it and being jittery. Hang in there Mary! Something will hopefully click...there will be weeks where the scale won't move....don't let that scare you. georgecoolname...ha ha ha too funny!!! I just found they're airing re-runs of MWC really early in the morning, so I watch them while I'm getting ready for the gym. Remember that people thought that show went too far? Pfffft, there are shows now that are waaayyyyyyy worse

contents: I have been diagnosed with Pelvic Floor Dysfunction and a nerve problem at the neck of the bladder. When I asked Dr. Moldwin if I had IC, he said it wasn't relevant??????? I am on 2mg. of Valium 3x a day, 10 mg. of Elavil and 60 mg. of Cymbalta. My symptoms are urgency, frequency and a throbbing in my urethra (which the doc says is coming from my bladder neck). I have had a negative pelvic MRI, negative cystoscopy and negative cytology. My smptoms keep coming and going, and I am trying to figure out what brings on my flare ups. I have thought it could be my yoga classes, stress and anxiety, and caffeine. I was wondering what you all have found that brings on your flares besides food. Anyone else feel their flares may be related to stress, exercise or caffeine? (Before the meds, I had symptoms all the time.) Thanks. Krisssy

For me, stress and being overly physically active are the worst things for bringing on a flare. I avoid caffeine almost entirely. I don't think it causes me to flare, it just seems to make my daily IC symptoms worse ---especially frequency and burning.
Mainly foods, but also exercise, stress, too long in the car, stress, not enough water, stress, too much water, stress, medications, stress. See a pattern here?

contents: Hi all! I have a question about some meds, and didn't really know where to post it. Since it concerns exercising, I thought this would be the best place. I try to walk at least 2 miles daily to prevent weight gain from the amitryptiline. I started experiencing hip pain, and about 3 weeks ago, my doctor put me on Celebrex. I started flaring about a week after I began taking it (just about the same time my joints started feeling better..wouldn't you just know it?!!). My question is....Has anyone connected a flare to Celebrex? I know both Celebrex and Vioxx are anti-inflammatory, but which seems to be more IC friendly? Thanks, Dee

contents: I take Celebrex with no problems. Have been on it about a month.

I have basically done not very much exercise in the past 3-1/2 years since I've had IC. Now I do yoga (which still flares me up a little sometimes) and walking. But my doctor (who doesnt' understand IC very much) is saying that I need to do some aerobic workout for my depression/sleep/cholesteral. I will be joining a gym to be able to use the elliptical once I can afford it but cannot right now. Can anyone suggest a DVD that would be easy on me and work with IC in ways?? Thank you! Merry Christmakkah!
signature: Erin Thames Going on 4-1/2 years with IC/PFD/IBS/HPV/Myofacial pain

contents: I guess I made a big mistake. I started a boot camp which is great, but there is a lot of ab work and I think it is flaring me because I feel pretty bad now. Has anyone had ab work flare their bladder? I guess it makes sense???
yes ab work does make me flare. i havent even tried crunches because i might flare.
signature: Formerly lorenab

contents: Definitely ab work does make you flare. Being a advid exerciser (plus work in the industry) it did not take me long to find the connection. Because I am a yoga practitioner it tends to keep my abdominal area strong.... there are certain postures you can do for the abdomen that do not cause abdomen pressure (bladder compression)....plank, half pank, dolphin plank, dead bug pose (laying on your back, legs up in the air long and lean and arms up in the air towards ceiling long and lean) push into your lower back to engage the core. You can also do happy baby crawl on your back, almost like a bicycle, but pretend you are crawling, using the arms reaching upwards (to the ceiling)...you can also do relcined leg lifts (one at a time) or two if your abs are strong. Hope these help. Peace Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:24 AM.
I can't do any exercises that put strain on my abdominal muscles. Donna

contents: yep yep Ab exercises put me into a flare, but since I am always in a flare even when I don't exercise I decided that I might as well be in good shape if I'm going to be in pain no matter what. So I went back to kickboxing and ab exercises. I'm in great shape but still always in pain unfortunately. I stopped for 2 weeks and the pain didn't stop... So hence the reason I am still doing it. Do what you feel is necessary, but yes ab exercises do cause a flare.
My doc told me to stay away from all ab exercises completely but I know why--terrible flares!

contents: About a month ago when I had a few great days in a row I had a bunch of energy and decided to do a bunch of ab work..BAD idea. The next two days I had pain and was pretty much on the couch with a heating pad. I have been feeling pretty good lately and am thinking of trying the elliptical or treadmill..but am still afraid it will lead to a flare..so we'll see lol
signature: 37mg Eavil each night 300 Elmiron a day Hydrocodiene and Pyridium as needed

contents: I do a lot of abs work ... but, I have spent years on my fitness program. You might want to start with a stretching or yoga like program. Then slowly add exercises that progressively build core (torso) strength. When I started, I worked with a physical therapist for three months ... that was ten years ago. I feel that stretching and building strength in my pelvic muscles has helped them relax and been a huge benefit to me.
signature: Bill

contents: I definitely can't do abs work! Before IC I was very active, but I've had to stop any kind of exercise that strains those muscles. I do a lot of walking and that helps, but I still have a paunch! Donna

contents: Hello lovely ladies: For those of u near Mercer County NJ (borders PA). There is a wonderful gym I want to share with you called the Robert Wood Johnson Health & Wellness Center. I just joined about 2 weeks ago & I'm so impressed with how they cater to the needs of those with medical issues such as ours. The place is a gorgeous with state of the art equipment and has nurses and physical therapists on staff at all times. I cut pasted some info from their web site below: rwjhamiltonwellness.com is the website. I would love too meet some of you lovely ladies there if you every want to blow off some steam I have a few free passes if u want to check it out. :woohoo: The RWJ Hamilton Center for Health & Wellness (CHW) is an 86,000-square-foot medically-based fitness, rehabilitation and education center located at 3100 Quakerbridge Road, Mercerville, NJ. The CHW offers state-of-the-art strength and cardiovascular equipment; an aquatic center with a Jr. Olympic, therapy and spa pools; physical, occupational and speech therapy; nutrition programs and counseling; exercise physiologists and certified personal trainers; the Healthy Cooking Kitchen which hosts interactive cooking demos; massage therapy and spa services; a healthy café; and child care. The Medical Advisory Panel (MAP), a group of RWJ Hamilton physicians representing multiple specialties, oversees the content of educational programs at the CHW. MAP physicians often teach or participate in classes. Nurses, registered dietitians and nutritional counselors are on staff and offer fitness and health risk assessments, screenings, classes and programs. There is also a 220-seat conference center that can be subdivided for breakout groups. This unique meeting space includes opportunities for attendees to work out before, during or after meetings. (Maybe we could have some meetings of our own there! Lots of love ladies!

I do go a little slower than the average woman, but I feel like I'm getting a good workout and am seeing results. Its the best workout I have found since being diagnosed three years ago.
WTG!! good for you ! I am glad you have found something that is working for you and is not causing bladder discomfort! peace

YAY!!! I am so glad to hear it's working well for you!! I too loved going to Curves - I too did it at my own pace, and had no troubles. I think that's what I liked the most about it, I could do my own thing, and take bathroom breaks as needed, lol!! I was really disappointed when my local Curves moved out of town,

I wish I could go to Curves. Want to lose some weight to see if I can get off my bp meds. Can't afford curves on SS disability. Too bad as one of my bp med is 55.00 and atarax is also 55.00. Medicare rx program is not very good coverage. More outof pocket for meds I am used to paying 5.00 a med. Vn
Has anyone tried the Pilates DVD for those with PFD? It's called New Dawn Pilates for People with Pelvic Pain. I got a coupon and free shipping at the mini conference I attended. I want to order it.

I do pilates off and on, but my pilates is the Windsor Pilates, and it calls for a lot of ab work, which can cause discomfort to the bladder if your not careful. NOW with that being said, your pilates is specifically for pelvic pain, which makes me wonder if the ab work is not so intense. If i were you I would do a GOOGLE SEARCH on the title and also add the word "review"...so there maybe some feedback from those who have tried it already. Peace

i do clinical pilates which is equipment based pilates. i have one and one sessions with my physio she knows about my ic and we lay off doing ab excersises. i think mat pilates has ab work in it, becareful.
signature: Formerly lorenab

contents: I can't seem to lose weight even off those stupid pills now so I have no clue what to do. Wish I had never taken them along with all the others. It's no fun staying fat. Only other options I can think of is diet pills or throwing up, which I did try throwing up but that didn't work out right either.

Awww Mary don't give up!!! Hang in there...don't vomit, it's a gross icky, habit and it causes other health problems down the line (plus it'll make your teeth and breath nasty) Losing weight takes time. The weight didn't come on all at once...it won't come off like that either. Your posts reflect only days or weeks of looking to lose weight...I know it's frustrating...It takes MONTHS of dedication and discipline. I urge you to please keep takng your meds and really rethink your diet. I bet you eat more than you think. I think its true of A LOT of us...including me Elavil messed up my system bad, I mean really, really bad. I finally managed to lose 12 lbs in 9 days with a HARDCORE look at my diet. It wasn't pretty and those 9 days I ate. I ate every 2-3 hours. Each meal was healthy. No starvingI also worked out and hour of full-on sweaty exercise each time. Hang in there it is possible! It just takes planning...Like Bill Phillips says...if you fail to plan, you plan to fail.

contents: Mary, (((((hugs you)))))) sweetie I'm so sorry you're having such a rough time lately. Yvette is right - and developing an eating disorder is NOT THE WAY Mary - you take your life into your hands doing that. You know, you need to continue on meds to help you battle with depression - regardless of what one it is. So maybe you're looking at this "diet" thing the wrong way - because you're looking at it AS a "diet". You need to look at it as a lifestyle change sweetie. To lose weight - you need to eat! If you STOP EATING - you shut your metabolism down, period. Too many people think if they cut back on eating, they'll lose weight - nope! They may lose it to begin with, but then they just start burning muscle instead of fat and thats not good. My hubby's weight is supposed to be around 170 - he was at 205. I've been on him forEVER to drop the weight and his metabolism is just shot - he's pre-diabetic and if he even looks at food, ugh - he gains weight I assure you. Its very very frustrating to say the least. He cant do Atkins (too damaging to kidneys) and very very restrictive (not having carbs is bad for you btw) - so we do more like a modified southbeach/6 week body makeover - in which we are eating every 3 hours, can have carbs and dairy. And we eat a TON (and I mean a TON!) of food! Why just yesterday morning we had sweet potatoe, white potatoe, steak, onion and green pepper hash with egg white omlette (pepper jack and cheddar cheeses) on a low carb tortilla (Missions make these, only 7 carbs per tortilla and no sugar!) breakfast burito for breakfast and WHOO - I was very very full after only eating half of it! 3 hours later I had some berries and yogurt (blue berries) smootie (homemade) and then for lunch - a grilled chicken salad. Afternoon snack was a boiled egg - and I also had a No Sugar added fudgsicle (yum!) - dinner was roast, small potato, and lots of veggies on the side! I have NEVER eaten so much in my entire life and neither has hubby! Hubby is a Body Type B and I am a Body Type E - so different types and different things we can have at certain times of the day but we eat the same foods, just at different times of the day. Like he cant have carbs in the morning but can have them come afternoon/evening. It works - it really truely does. Sugar and processed is the hardest, and of course, sweets. I am a sweets person -and candy - Oh my lord do I love candy! But - I eat sugar free jello with real whipped cream instead or No sugar added fudgsicles (if you can tolerate chocolate that is...I can so I can have them without problems). I make all types of goodies with oatmeal by grinding it up and use it just like regular flour. He is down to 190 now - and we began 2 weeks ago. We've done this before and then went back to our "old eating habits" and he put it back on again. It simply works, and we dont go hungry. We also dont eat bad foods anymore - now if I want a cake, I will bake it from scratch using unbleached flour or oatmeal flour with HALF of regular flour. Its not as "bad" for us and we'll take a few bites and be done with it. I got my fix and he got his. I dont deprive myself because then I'm just sabotaging myself for failure in the end. I still have pizza, but I make it instead of buying it - I know whats in it and can control everything. So you can do it sweetie - and stay on your medications that you need so much. I hate to see you so down on yourself, and it doesnt help that you listen to what others say to you (meaning your family) - they just beat you down because they know it gets to you. You are a strong woman and can do anything you set your mind to! Do this for yourself and show yourself that you can do it Mary - I have so much faith and confidence in you! Lots of hugs and support! Brittany Last edited by BrittanysDance; 11-17-2008, 12:41 AM. Reason: added something :)

I agree that you need to take your meds. And I also have a suggestion for losing weight: Put a little notepad and a pencil or pen in a pocket or somewhere else handy --- and anything that goes in your mouth gets recorded in the pad. If you taste something while it's cooking, write it down. Then at the end of the day figure out how many calories you have taken in. It's not good to stop eating --- your body will respond by going into a "save itself" mode, assuming that you are starving, and will actually burn very few calories. Hang in there --- and please take your meds. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: same issues I am having the same issues that you are losing the weight I have gained and it is even harder for me since I am still recovering from anorexia. I intentionally put weight back on and now I regret it since there is too much. To get back to where I wanna be I am still eating healthy but not giving into the cravings that I used to. Then on saturdays I work out hard in the morning and make the rest of the day kind of a cheat day. The weight will come off I promise but I get just as discouraged when I get on the scale and it hasn't moved or worse it goes up. Water weight is a big issue too, just keep in mind that slow and steady is the way to do it and just eat lots of veggies. I find that if I limit myself to a few foods but as much of them as I want than there is no temptation to eat other stuff that has more calories. Just make sure you are satisfied with the foods you choose! you can message me if you need any other tips... I have pretty much studied every form of weight loss and I'm going back to school for holistic nutrition, I can get you in shape!! HUGS!!

I went on elavil last year and quickly gained 10 pounds that have been very hard to loose. Finally, I joined Weight Watchers and have almost reached my goal. I agree with Donna about writing everything down that you eat. I was quite surprised how much I ate without realizing it. Another thing that helps me is not to put serving dishes of food on the table. I fill my plate at the stove and that is all I get. Portion control is very important. I now realize how much I am capable of eating. Elavil caused my weight gain.... I just ate and ate when I was on that drug. My cravings for sweets were unreal. Another thing that has helped me is a weekly weigh-in. Knowing that someone will be weighing me each keeps me from cheating (well, not too much cheating).

I started my day off with being called "FAT< FAT< FAT". I'm just lazy and eating everything in the house. NOTHING WRONG WITH ME!!! It makes me want to stop the zoloft and I don't even know if I'm gaining weight on that yet because I didn't weigh myself this morning. Before the IC I ALWAYS weighed myself after going to the bathroom. I was that much afraid of gaining weight. The only way I know of how to lose it now is to increase the vicodin (which I know my PT Won't like but to bad) and that way I can clean the whole house and exercise too. Be normal again. I really hate the way my life has turned to but then of course I made it this way. I was a nice "yes" person.

I am sorry you having these negative feelings about yourself, please try not to be so hard on yourself. Having negative energy certainly does not help symptoms of IC. I am a bit unsure as to why you are feeling "fat" (or someone is making you feel this way)...is it your meds? I think what you need is to get stronger within your mind with positive affirmations. If you have any negative thoughts or self doubht or stress within the mind, it certainly overflows into our body and our spirit, causing us to abuse ourselves with outside stimulants, some turn to food, medication, drugs, alchohol...etc....to me the cure to becoming a stronger person inside is changing the thoughts within the mind not allowing them to stray to these negative ways of thinking. Do some complete breathing, (do a google search) on the yoga way of complete breathing and this will certainly be a start for you to find calming energy within. Most people in our society have no clue how to breathe, most women tend to breathe a HYPER VENTALATING type breath, high in the chest, which causes anxiety and stress within the body, causing tension in the mind which leads us to be drawn to external factors to give us the pleasing energy we desire. (for example - opening the fridge and eating chocolate ice cream and feeling the exotic feel of sugar and highs there)...try to turn inward and find the peace. FIND the breath and breathe ....relax....and find the calmness and positive energy inside you.....repeat positive affirmations to build your self confidence and give you the chance to release your negative energy. Also do not be a prisoner to the scale, this can RUIN your day before you even get up. Looking a number should not be the first think you do in the morning to judge how you are going to feel for the day. Know that if you eat right and do mild exercise, find peace within, it will all come together with a healthy body, mind and spirit. The scale can show five pounds higher if you do not have a bowel movement, or if you eat certain foods like brocolli or caulliflower, they can leave you bloated and carrying a bit of extra weight .....but only to lose it through a poop. So try not to stress, relax and be human, learn to love your body, and find your breath. Namaste, Peace Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:19 AM.

just for giggles Mary.. I have been in a rut, I eventually will get out of and my DH likes to go on a nit picking rampage occasionally. one Day he was screaming about the house (remember I have a 1 and 3 year old and I am 42 with IC and all its attachments) being dirty and I hadn't made dinner. I said you know what. you can't watch the kids 2 hours with out going crazy, and I do it all day, if you want a better house keeper or cook you should have married Martha Stewart or Betty Crocker. funny he hasn't said anything in a while.. I must confess there was alot more said, but this was the nice version. my new theory on life is if you don't like it do it your self. I am not your maid!! I am sorry that you are going thru all this bs, sending you big hugs
Sounds like someone in your house has loads of fat between their ears! Please don't let it get to you. Donna

contents: Gaining weight on zoloft too Stopped the zoloft today too. I just don't know what to do anymore. All the anti pills cause weight gain no matter what I do. Even had to give up the low fat diet doctor put me on because I"m tired of argueing over that as well. I'm told I can eat what they eat. I'm sure my cholesterol is over 300 by now. It was 296 last time it was checked. I'm tired, I don't feel good and I'm all out of options. Can't even take the algonot either. Have to argue over getting that too. I go for the instill this week but I don't know about telling him any of this. I've bugged and bugged him to where what more can he do? Last edited by waterflow; 11-10-2008, 02:12 AM. Reason: added

Who says these horrible things to you? I admit, before I was hit with these symptoms, I was probably less understanding than I should have been in regard to chronic pain, but i can't even imagine talking to someone like that! I know that it's hard for people to understand, especially when symptoms change day by day, but wow! Hang in there. Don't give up on talking to your dr, either. I'll be praying for you!

It's family. See no one believes there is something wrong with me. Plus they don't know about the meds. I had lost 16 pounds by back in January but then I got on the cymbalta and gained them back and more. I really don't know what else to do other then diet pills. I tried making myself throw up but all I ended up doing was gagging so there must be a trick for that to work right. All I know is I'm tired, and tired of fighting, tired of trying to get someone to believe me or at least have everyone leave me alone.
I don't understand Mary, why they are so cruel. Can you not get out on your own at all? they are very mean to you and that is so wrong.
Don't Give Up! There are people pulling for ya! I'm sorry that you are going through so much. I'll be praying for ya!

Greetings, I used to run a lot, but since IC took hold of me late in 1998, I haven't been been able to race like I used to. Curious if any one out there with IC runs for exericse, and if so, what meds or treatments do you use? I'd also be curious to know what is the furthest distance you can go before you have to urinate. A few years ago, I joined a gym determined to get back into running. My plan was to simply go as far as I could go on a treadmill and then stop to urinate and get right back on. It proved too frustrating and I gave up after a few weeks. I've tried all the usual things such as bladder instills, elmiron, BCG, etc. to get control of my IC but nothing has worked to date. Thank-you, Derrick Jacksonville, Florida

I run and I know there are a couple of IC runners out there. I even did a marathon last year. My pace is laughably slow, but I do get out there a couple times a week. Like I said SLOWLY. My doc actually thought that running my help my mental state-and it has. Feels like I have some control over this darn disease. Good luck!

I was a runner...I would like to say am still a runner...but since June, I have not been running. I am sure there will be a time I will get back into it...but right now I can not imagine the pounding of running and the effects on my bladder. RIght now, I am an avid hiker and walker and I am getting my fitness fix this way. I do miss the THRILL and HIGH of running though. I would think treadmill running would be better to start out, not as much pounding as road running. I also know that when I exercise, I try to keep my water intake before hand a bit lower, and then power up on the fluids afterwards. THis seems to help with my frequency during workouts. I also teach yoga classes and fitness classes, and again I try to keep fluids down before I teach....and then drink lots afterwards. THis seems to be working well for me. I am able to teach a yoga class for 1.5 hours without having to go. Good luck. Peace Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:22 AM.

I can't run because of my knees, but I do walk on the treadmill an hour a day. It is good because I can go to the bathroom any time I need to. Sometimes I will take pyridium (I use the over the counter version) beforehand to reduce my sensation of urgency. Maybe that would help some of you?

contents: I'm curious to know if anyone else has had this frustrating experience. Everytime I tell anyone that I have IC (and I avoid this whenever possible), I always get the same suggestion. Try using adult incontinence pads. This bothers me more than I can say. It frustrates and angers me! Everyone seems to think that IC is all about leakage. They don't seem to understand that no matter what you are wearing, that doesn't stop you from feeling the urgency. You can't just turn that off or ignore it because you feel that any accident will be invisible to others (and there is no guarantee of this either). Sometimes, I think this is one medical problem that (no matter how much you are told about it or read about it), you can't even begin to understand it unless you have it yourself. I am back to work but I did have to go on disability for awhile due to my IC. Everyone kept making the same suggestion to me (like my medical problems were so simply controlled and I wasn't smart enough to see it). I do feel alone because there is no one that I can talk to who really understands. There is only one person that I talk to about this. My sister listens and she sounds like she understands (even though I know she can't possibly), but at least she tries. No one else even makes the effort. I'm Frustrated!!!
signature: Kat Meds I'm On: 30 mgs Amitriptyline 5 mgs Ditropan XL

contents: Unfortunately the lack of understanding--along with the 'suggestions'--are going to be a continuous thing for a long time coming. You're just going to have to let these comments go in one ear and out the other, otherwise its going to drive you insane. We've all been there and completely understand your frustration. Wishing you well... Diana
signature: (\__/) (o.O ) (> < ) This is Bunny. He's on his way to world domination.

Maybe we can all be here for each other. I think we certainly need the support that is provided here. Who else could possibly begin to understand?

contents: Hey Hun, I know how you feel. When I first figured out that I had IC these were the same things I was feeling. It was like I had a perminate bladder infection. My doctor put me on Elmiron (300mg per day) it helped allot. Not right away, it took about 3 to 4 weeks. It helps you feel like you dont have to go all the time. Just hang in there. No one understands this condition!!! But everyone here does Stay strong.

contents: Dont feel bad. My MIL suggested the same to me once. I nicely explained to her that I do not have any leakage issues at all. I told her my issues are that I have severe pain(like a UTI from hell) and frequency issues. She has had UTI's before and knows how awful they are. After that she did a little research on it and now somewhat understands better what IC is. I have given up on explaining to people what IC is. I just say I have a condition with my bladder that is like having a UTI constantly, but 100,000,000 times worse. If you are talking to a person that has ever had a UTI, then they get it, but if not, well then I just dont get into it with them. Jen

Hi! I understand how you feel. I don't tell people that I have IC because as soon as they hear cystitis they think UTIs or incontinence. Cystitis and UTI are completely different diagnoses but they are often used interchangeably so few people understand the difference. I say I have an autoimmune condition or bladder ulcers. In any event, it is difficult to expect compassion from people who haven't faced a disabling condition themselves. I used to think that disability meant blindness or a loss of limbs. Little did I know.

contents: OMG I know exactly how you feel! I love my mom so much but if I mention that my bladder hurts she goes into this long story of how she suffers with bladder issues too! Ok....my mom has never had foods, juices or even sex cause her bladder to flare but she thinks that IC is nothing more than a UTI! It doesn't matter how many times I explain it she says the same thing! I've decided not to mention anything IC related to her. My mother in law is the same way! It drives me crazy when she calls and tells me that I don't sound sick to her! I finally asked her how was I supposed to answer the phone...crying, screaming in agony, sobbing? This board has now become my place to go for comfort and understanding. I have no one to talk to about this but these ladies..... and it's a good thing! Best wishes, Aleet7

contents: Cherise2040 I have problems with constipation. My urologist put me on a program with lots of bran. It worked except for the blatter pain. I found out that anything with potassium is going to cause pain. I tried Miralax which made me very nausious. My chemistry must be a mess. The doctors that I go to make me feel like I'm a freak. Can anyone out there tell me if they have a problem with constipation and what they are doing for it.[ I feel alone tobecause of this horrible disease. My sisters think that it is all make believe. My few friends who do not have this can't believe that this is for real. I'm so glad to have this website to read. [I] wish we could all get together at one of our houses. I'm in Florida. Hope to hear from you soon. Cherise2040

Hi Cherise! I don't have a lot of problems with constipation but mostly just the opposite. Several of us suffer from Irritable Bowel Syndrome and I'm sure there's a lot of suggestions for you. It's hard for some to believe in a disease that they've never heard of. No one in my family have ever heard of IC so I think that maybe they wonder if this is just something that I've concocted to get attention. Well, I wish it was just something that I made up but the symptoms are too real to make up. I hope that you make lots of friends here just as I have. I hope someone gives you some good suggestions for your problem. Best wishes, Aleet7

contents: For me it is like when I had fertility problems. no one except someone who has gone or is going through it can understand or help you, that is why this board is so great. I don't really have anyone I can discuss this with, they don't get it, and truthfully I think most people would rather not know. After a year of being diagnosed my mother FINALLY looked IC up on the internet and became more sympathetic (she was previously sure I was stressed out, just as she was when I had fertility problems), especially when she read that most ICers suffer from Hypothyroidism and sever allergies, both of which she know I have. So my suggestion is lay it out to us girl, we are here ot help!

It is so frustrating! My MIL suggested I just take some vitamins. Yeah, sure, since that would probably make me flare! Here's how I explained it to my own mom, when she didn't "get" why I complained so much. I told her: "whenever you get a uti, bladder infection, etc. you stay home from work and go to the doctor. well, this is like having a uti every day of my life, times 10000!" she did some research (yay for my proactive mom) and is completely sympathetic now. It took my wonderful uro explaining it to my hubby for him to comprehend. I think part of it is because it is not a common disease, it's (usually) not life-threateining, and you can't "see" it. If we had cancer, people wouldn't say "oh why don't you just try chemo and buy a wig". When I broke my leg people didn't say "oh why don't you just drink some more milk". Truthfully, I'd trade the long term neverending bladderpain for a leg injury that will heal any day!

Author Catherine ****** puts it nicely," The irony in IC is that no one can see it"! Maybe if they could see what we go through then they would have a better understanding! I showed my mom my bladder pics compared to a normal bladder and her jaw dropped!!!!! Best Wishes, Teela
