contents: Has anyone tried Pilates? I had to quit Curves a couple of years ago, and have been walking on the treadmill occasionally but usually have a flare if I do it regularly. I'd really like to do some form of exercise. Any info would be appreciated. Thanks- marielle

Originally posted by marielle Has anyone tried Pilates? I had to quit Curves a couple of years ago, and have been walking on the treadmill occasionally but usually have a flare if I do it regularly. I'd really like to do some form of exercise. Any info would be appreciated. Thanks- marielle You need to be assessed for PFD before you do Pilates. I'm one of those weird types that has no high-tone muscle concurrent issues. Pilates is great for me: no bouncing, lots of strengthening--the new studies are showing that strengthening and muscle building do as much as cardio for conditioning. Pilates, however, focuses on the core, and if you have PFD, they will tell you steer clear. Think about yoga, elliptical, and weight training instead.
I LOVE Pilates. It helped me lose weight and tone. It also helps with my Fibro and it makes my brain a bit clearer. I haven't been able to do it in awhile though but I do miss it.

contents: Hi everyone! Just wanted to share my experience. I don't like cardio and most of it involves moving one's legs in a manner that's possibly irritating to the bladder. After almost a year-long break, I went back yesterday for Bikram (hot room) yoga. I sweat like crazy and I think this counts as cardio! My heart rate was up the entire 90 minutes of class. And no bladder irritation today at all. In fact, I feel great. I plan to go back 4 times per week. Please note that I'm not on any meds that have heat warnings.

contents: I have a question in regards to exercising. I have had problems with my bladder since 2003, finally in 2006 I was diagnosed with IC. I am in the military and not many people seem to know what to do with it, so I kind of get bounced around from doctor to doctor. I had a great doctor and then about 8 months ago I was sent to Korea, so I havent really seen anyone here. My biggest problem is frequency at night, I can get up about 7+ times a night. It has been getting more frequent, and I can only assume because I havent really been seeing anyone for treatment. Anyway I went on a 4 mile run today and by the time it finished I felt like I really had to pee. So I ran to the bathroom and hardly anything, but it hurt really bad, kind of like a UTI, I went home and was in the worst pain. I used a heating pad, drank some water with baking soda, and a couple hours later the pain subsided, however there is still some pain when I urinate. Does anyone else feel this way after a exercising? The only thing I can assume is that because I went on a really long run it aggravated things. Any thoughts?

One thing you need to be sure you do is to drink water while you are exercising. I couldn't do the run you did --- couldn't do that even when I was young --- but I do a lot of walking and have to be sure to drink plenty of water during a long walk. Donna

contents: S. Snyder Anything with a jarring motion will aggravate the bladder and pelvic floor and running in particular can set off a flare in a lot of women. You ran a whole 4 miles, apparently without a lot of pain? Do you have frequency during the day and night or just the night? The bladder pressure and feeling like you may have a infection but not showing one are typical IC symptoms. If you can get to a doctor you might ask him to give you a trial of Elmiron and maybe Elavil and see if it helps your symptoms. Being in Korea makes it harder to find someone who understands IC, but if you have a urologist there who will work with you, it may help. Some other things that can set off the pelvic floor muscles and cause bladder pain are working the pedals in a car, sitting for a long time in one position, certain sex positions or even sex at all for some. Pelvic floor muscles can cause shooting pains in the urethra and bladder when they spasm and cramp.
signature: Sammi Meds: Melatonin 3mg @ bedtime if needed. Estrogen 1.5 mg troche and 0.1 mg Estrace cream. Diagnosis: IC, PFD (both in remission)

contents: Snyder You know Snyder, if it were me I would get a urine specimen run just to be sure I didnt have an infection. If it shows no white cells or nitrates, but some red cells, it may be more IC than an infection. For a long time Doc's would think I had an infection because I had symptoms, but only a few red cells so they would treat. But, check it out to be on the safe side. If its an infection it will just get worse.
signature: Sammi Meds: Melatonin 3mg @ bedtime if needed. Estrogen 1.5 mg troche and 0.1 mg Estrace cream. Diagnosis: IC, PFD (both in remission)

contents: Sami I run several times a week, but havent been running a long distance like I did on Friday. There was no pain while running, just afterwards I felt like I had to pee really bad and then the pain came. My main symptom has always been a lot of frequency at night, I think during the day I can kind of ignore it because I am busy. So I rested over the weekend and it went away. Now I'm just trying to calm my bladder down, eat the recommended things, steer clear of the things that I know will aggravate my bladder. It seems to help

Hi all.. I have been a member of the boards for years now, but never ventured over into the exercise area, LOL. i was just curious if any other IC'ers have noticed a significant weight gain over the years of having IC? I have had IC for about 5 years & honestly have gained about 10 lbs per year (yup thats 50 extra pounds!!). I dont know whether to blame it on the Lexapro I take, the bloat from IC or just the comfort eating I do so much. I am thinking it is pretty much all 3?? Has anyone else gained alot of weight over the years?? and 2nd question is how on earth do we excercise?? i joined a gym, Curves, and really enjoyed it, but found that even doing 30 min. of the circuit was rough on my bladder & was gettign too embarrasing to constantly stop & pee. plus i ususally flared pretty badly after working out. i do walk my dog daily, but that is usually just 15 min. tops. i have all these exercise videos but they all involve tons of jumping/etc which doesnt bode well for an IC bladder & fybrom. joints. Any suggestions???

contents: I would say something like rollerblading where you move your legs sideways or a cardio machine that imitates this. Also, step, pilates and hot yoga work for me. I think even going to a sauna would help because it gets the heart rate up and emilinates toxins through the skin instead of the bladder.

contents: Rollerblading Flare... I was on Lexapro before I was diagnosed with Ic and I never gained weight from it. I was diagnosed with IC in February of this year and gained about 8 pounds since. I know it's because I've been eating too much carbs...afraid to eat alot of other foods due to horrible flares. I started doing a lot of stretching and some yoga, but seemed it seemed to keep the pain going. Then I went rollerblading and put me in a horrible flare again. It's so frustrating! I'm not giving up though. I'm going to walk like crazy and maybe join the local gym to at least lift weights. Don't give up! Jaime

hi again everyone. i'm in a flare and wondering what if anything is causing it. i'm wondering why i can lay around all day, feeling like y-u-c-k, drinking water and trying to rest, but when evening comes, i drag myself up, shower, pick out my prettiest dress and drive 40 miles to go(no discomfort cause i'm going....)ISRAELI FOLK DANCING or... CONTRADANCING. i can be sick, suffering, in torturement, but when i go dancing, like i TRANSFORM. i am well! no one can tell i am sick! i can dance for hours. yeah, i have symptoms, like i have to pee the whole time, and i'm constantly drinking water between dances so i dont dehydrate and make my bladder worse. when i go to the toilet, between dances, sometimes i have to pee so bad, and i can't. ow. i sit there, take some deep breaths, and i say to myself, cyd, you have a choice. you can pee now or you can... GO HOME EARLY! and, amazingly, this WORKS. like i actually start to pee... on Command. :o) so i,m asking myself, what is going on here??? am i bringing on flares by dancing too much.? recently, my friends and i brought back some fun old horas, wild and fast, crazy dances, doing 3 or 4 in a row... did this bring on my recent flare??? (i hope not! maybe i just need more sleep. maybe if i sleep more, i can dance more hora. maybe i need to pace myself and not overdo. why is that so hard to do?) yet, flare or no flare, the dancing ALWAYS makes me feel BETTER! it occurred to me, maybe this is endorphins at work. exercise releases endorphins, evens out our seritonins, etc, etc, and those are pain relievers. and folk dancing is more than just exercise: it's beautiful music and poetry in motion; it's friendship, loving community, and fun. when i get home, it takes awhile for me to wind down, and i take my elavil kind of late on those nights, but then i get a good night's sleep. so flare or no flare: i'm a dancing maniac, and i aint stoppin any time soon! ********************* cyd in ocala, fla b. january 18, 1960, chicago, illinois chronic cystitis since 1970 diagnosed interstitial cystitis: oct 2005 mark dersch, MD, edward sabol, MD, ocala greg bailey, MD, gainesville, fla peter sand, MD, evanston, illinois daily meds: elmiron, 400mg elavil, 20mg atarax, 30mg probiotic flora q, 230mg living with chronic illness is walking a tightrope without a net. embrace every day with the skills and attitude of a circus acrobat: courage, power, grace, moxie, agility, creativity, balance, compassion, strength, love, and the invaluable: a sense of humor. be here now, for this too shall pass. peace. Last edited by Guest; 09-23-2008, 02:10 PM. Reason: more thoughts to add
Sometimes just being distracted can help. I think it's great that you are dancing. I know I went square dancing until my husband had two back surgeries and I spent a lot of time in bathrooms! Donna

contents: I don't know for sure: I have taken ballet all my life. I was a professional ballet dancer in a ballet company. I didn't have problems then. Not like I did after I got this thing. Dancing and exercise has always made me feel better, but not when I feel like I have to pee. Or I have a flare.\ I gave up teaching (owning my own school) in 1999. Then I taught privately for a few years, but I noticed that I got flares about one hour after the classes. After a few years of noticing this I gave up teaching completely. It was like tearing my heart out. Depression etc. I have not taken it back up, because I do not want to deal with the flares. I do walk every other day, and it makes me feel ok, and no flares. I don't know what brings on Flares, but my teaching did. But only after I had the AH HA sickness that started this whole cycle of horror. And that was in the '80's. Lived with this since then. I am happy when I am at a 2 or 3, I can tolerate life, but above it, no...........out comes all the meds etc. I can't tell if the hora could cause your flare, but if you are doing a lot of jumping around and stuff, (as in the hora), it could be moving your bladder and rubbing the urethra, so it could possibly be a cause. You would have to follow it carefully, and see if the dancing is causing the flare, or something you might be eating or drinking at the class. Good luck, and I hope you don't have to stop dancing.
signature: Miki Hit and past 60 yrs old. Now 64 Married: with 2 adult kids......33yrs male, 30 female. DX: about 1982: chrontic urethritis, then IC, then Pelvic pain syndrome. (whatever) Meniere's disease, Now 90% loss of balance nerve on left side, inner ear. BPPV, GAD IBS (whole life) Migraines dyspepsia Given up going to Urologist...last one asked me what I wanted him to do for me.

contents: thanks, miki hi, miki! thanks for your quick reply. you know. you have a point. i think moderation is the key, and here's my Q for you: how much sleep is enough for a dancer with I C?? i know deep in my heart i need 10 to 12 hours of sleep daily. hard to get this much. or even to lie down on the bed for awhile. when i'm flaring, i cant relax, you know? so i know i havent been getting as much sleep as i promise myself i will get to counteract the horas. yeah. we shouldnt wear ourselves out so much, but work up to it more gradually, maybe. so maybe, if i sleep more, i can dance more hora. you know, for seven months my period stopped, and my i. c. was noticeably better, now, recently, my period came BAAAAACKKKK! like a horror movie. i think that has jacked up my pain baseline, and that's what i m coping with now. so, we are always in a little adjustment process... rest more, drink more water, take some marshmallow pills (???) whatever. yeah. thanks for being there! peace, joy, and dancing feet, cyd

dancing/sleep Cyd Hi, I don't know what is the correct amount of sleep for any one person. I find that I will stay up late, and get up late, and still feel pretty lousy. When I have my flares, I try to relax with the valium, and vicodin. They tend to help me relax and sleep a little. Of course if I have slept the day away, it is sometimes difficult to sleep at night. My sleeping patterns have always been erratic. My "normal" pattern was.......1am to about 9 or 10 am. now it seems like I need 9-12 hours sleep a day. I go to bed between 1-3 am and am practically never up before noon, or 1pm Many times it will be 2:30 or so. Wintertime sucks since the days are so short. Also, since I am not only dealing with the urethral problem, I have a vestibular problem also: Meniere's. I am dizzy most of the time, but there are times where I can be fairly lucid and see pretty well. Other times.....(like today_) I am brainfogged, dizzy, and so tired. I went to bed at like 10 pm last nite. Got up at 1 am, had a bite to eat, then went back to bed. I woke up at 10 am, but didn't get up. I was too dizzy. (not counting the times I had to rise to go potty at nite) I had an appt. at 3pm, and had to lv. at 2:35pm...........I was really tired. I don't drive much any longer.....my husband drove. When I got home, I did some computer stuff, and then lay down on my bed. I finished one book, and then listened to another audio book. I thought I would fall asleep, since I am so tired, but no, just hung in there. I am up again, and it is 8:30pm. I will watch tv. I did some squats, and plies, since my thighs and legs are so much weaker than they were a few yrs ago. If you flare, I think it is almost impossible to do anything but sit in the bathtub, or go rest in bed. If one can fall asleep it is helpful, just to escape the pain. BTW: your periods will certainly affect your pain level with IC. It is always worse when you have another "downthere" problem. If I get my vaginal herpes, or my hemmoroids are bothering me, I get flares. Plus: stress will do it too. Since I am way past menopause, I don't get periods any longer, but I still will get flares from other things. Hope you can find a doctor, or pain clinic that can help you when you flare like this.

contents: hi miki hi miki, thanks fro your reply about sleep. wow, it sounds like youre coping with some reall challenges. yes, it's not easy. my friend has struggled with the inner ear attacks. she does ginger or ginger tea, i;ll ask her on the weekend when i talk to her, but of course, ginger may be too spicy for your i.c. i know i love ginger but cant tolerate it. well, maybe, ther are some things i'm not sure i may be able to tolerate, but i'm scared to try, and truthfully it's not worth it just for spice or food, however, if the ginger can help your inner ear, you might want to try just a little. i'l;l ask her and let you know , if you like. maybe you already tried it. i will take a nap now, so i can leave for dancing at six! hope your haveing a good day! love, cyd in ocala, it just started storming here. so what else is new? :O)

Hi! Does anyone know of a sports drink out there without citric acid? I have been training to speed walk a half marathon and citric acid causes flares for me. So far I have been doing very well with my ic. However, I still experience urgency, ect... It is more manageable so that I can work out longer!
Hi! I found the following: Unflavored HEED powder Rooibos tea I hope this helps.

contents: hi! re: sports drinks, my uro advised me: NONE! he also says they are not necessary. drink water! lots of water! im not training for a marathon, but! i work for hours as a roofer in the hot florida sun, lots of the guys i work with drink all those pretty colors gatorade stuff. for i c, my doc and i agree, that's POisON!! stay away! i also do : mass quantities of folk dancing, tap dancing and gardening (that last also in hot sun.) i drink 3 quarts of water every day, sometimes more. keep your urine as diluted as possible. i asked my doc about losing trace nutrients from so much sweating he said, nah, not an issue. eat a little snack or a lunch, if you crave something salty, eat a pretzel or something, he advised me when i eat my lunch i'm replacing all i need easily. good luck, and have fun running/walking your race! you GO, Girl!!!
I agree that colorful stuff is probably not the best. There is also a brand of bottled water called Smart Water that has electrolytes.

contents: hello, all. i finally figured out how to start a new thread. woo hoo!! am sitting here sipping my big mug of warm barley water. does anyone besides me flare at certain times of day? 4-6 or so p.m. i can set my watch by it. i hope this is not considered advertising and that i can tell y'all about some lifesaver pants i wear. i mean instead of undies. here in humid hot florida, sweating is a problem, and in spite of my annoying i.c, i am very active. this is what i wear under my soft cotton jersy knit skirts ( when i wear any underwear at all!) none is best! love that fresh breeze! i wear: men's boxer shorts made by patagonia. they are not cheep, $32 per pair. they run small, i am petite and i wear a medium and they barely fit in the waist. but! they are baggy, breezy, long and cute and come in fabulous fun prints and colors. they are 100% recycled polyester, but!!! dont let that stop you; this is polypro, they never get wet from sweat! they stay dry and breezy. theyre so baggy, they dont even touch my vulva or rub against me, and are sooo comfortable. if i wear cotton undies and they get wet and sweaty, i can get a flare!!! no problem with my polypro boxer boys. i highly recommend these. when i'm not flarin and it's not too hot, i will wear a pair of pure cotton bikini undies underneath the boxers after smearing on some a and d ointment. i can dance all night like this with very little to no burning. i searched and shopped and tried and tried many different undies, and when i hit on these i just want to tell everyone how safe and comfortable they feel. hope this little hint can help someone out there feel better. sometimes just a little pointer like this can make a big difference in our happiness and freedom. best wishes to all, cyd in ocala, fla

I weighed myself this morning and I've lost 3 pounds.:woohoo: The only thing I have changed doing is I went from drinking slim fast for breakfast to eating cereal again. The slim fast wasn't agreeing with my stomach and bladder anymore. I guess my body had finally gotten used to the cymbalta being there. Did ask the pharmacist the other day about it. Told him how I had finally stopped gaining weight with it and was wondering if I would be able to lose the pounds or would I be fat forever? He did say yes I would lose it and not to worry about it. He said it in a nice way with a smile so I guess he was right!! :woohoo: Soooooooo, I'm not going to go out and buy new dress up cloths. I did buy some everyday cloths at Family Dollars that were on sale. Found two pair of shorts for $3 each and they have the string so if I am skinnier by next spring I can just tighten that. Oh, and I did find the most compfy pants ever. They had the nurses uniforms there and the pants had rubber. Which is of course what I need. I tried them on that night and talk about wanting to live in them. They were so soft and the rubber didn't bind. They were better then any sweat pants I have worn. Bad part they only had blue in my size. Oh, and I found two nice shirts while at Sam's yesterday. I never bothered to look at the cloths but thought why not. One thing I want to know is what is it now with all the see through fancy shirts??? At least I'm on my way back to where I used to be....maybe I can go a little farther like I had wanted to and be skinnier but I'll settle for back to normal. At least now I can take the cymbalta too and not think or worry.
That's fantastic news, Mary. Yippee!!!!!

contents: Hi there, So my bladder is inflamed, had hydro 1 week ago and it was very red and with lots of pinpoints and wounds. What should I do about exercise? I have been umcomfortable with all types of exercise for weeks. Should I do it anyway without thinking so much? Will it help my simptoms, or it is a bad idea and I should do some only if I am pain free? Thanks April
You should be doing some walking, even if it's five minutes at a time just around the house. Donna

contents: First of all, let me say that I do not have constant pain--just frequency and sometimes urgency, occasional pain and bladder ulcers. I recently started doing step once a week (on Wednesday nights) and noticed that my Thursdays are absolutely symptom-free! My frequency is cut in half or more. Running or ellipticals don't seem to produce the same effect. Does anyone have a similar experience? My husband thinks that cardio helps eliminate toxins through sweating, but I think there's something about muscle movement during step that does this for me... Strange, don't you think?

contents: That's great....I used to do step but hated it,and used to do treadmill and found it boring. I walk now and do handweights...neither of which makes any difference when I am having symptoms.
That is strange! Strange, but wonderful!!!
signature: Kim Diagnosed August 2001 Current IC meds: Elmiron (since 2001), Levaquin (one pill after intercourse to prevent UTIs), Effexor (for depression & anxiety) Past IC meds: Amitriptyline (Elavil), Hydroxyzine (Vistaril), Detrol LA, Lexapro (for depression & anxiety, but also helped my IC) (They all helped, but I was able to discontinue them.) I've been virtually symptom free and able to eat & drink whatever I'd like for about 8 years now. ***************************** “We who lived in concentration camps can remember the men who walked through the huts comforting others, giving away their last piece of bread. They may have been few in number, but they offer sufficient proof that everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of the human freedoms -- to choose one's attitude in any given set of circumstances, to choose one's own way.” ~ Viktor Frankl “You cannot control what happens to you, but you can control your attitude toward what happens to you, and in that, you will be mastering change rather than allowing it to master you.” ~ Brian Tracy

contents: I had the same thing happen with the eliptical trainer. I was in constant pretty severe pain, but found if I could go to the gym early in the morning and drink a lot of water while I worked out, I would be in a lot less pain that day. For me, early mornings are usually a time when I'm not in pain, so this worked out well for me.

I can't tolerate swimming in pools because of the chlorine. The vaseline trick doesn't work for me. I was wondering if anyone has tried swim diapers to keep the water out and avoid irritation. Are there any waterproof swim bottoms on the market? I have searched the net but haven't found any.
You might look at some of the equipment used by divers to see if there's anything there. Donna

contents: Has anyone tried rebounding? I receantly got one and was wondering if any one has had success or trouble with this. It's suppose to be good for depression and to help flush the body.
signature: amanda amanda

contents: Give it a whirl Hi I just bought a rebounder too but afraid to use it incase I get a flair. Going to New York on holidays in a week (Im Irish) so have to be extra cautious in the lead up dont want a painful holiday. But will try when I come back, in addition to doing some walking which I love. But try it out and dont over do it, also drink plenty of water to keep hydrated before and after. Let me know how it goes

Just wondering if there are any real Yoga "no-no's" as some of the positions - even the lotus seem to pull on the lower pelvic area quite a bit. Also, when in the lotus there are several "bend way over forward" (face down) poses - then bend way back that seem a little "iffy" to me in that they tug on that area, too. These positions aren't really painful but I just wonder about them. Thanks.

contents: Yoga does not seem to hurt my IC, but sometimes I get sore from my Fibro. You use a lot of muscles in yoga. The more I do it the less it hurts.

contents: For me, cobra is very uncomfortable. Generally anything that puts pressure on the lower abdomen or pelvic floor is not comfortable. When I started taking yoga classes I got there early so I could ask the instructor to give me an alternate pose for those things that didn't work for me. The instructors were usually very helpful & discreet. Hope you find something that works well for you!

contents: I really don't feel comfortable in pigeon. Now that I have IC, I am thinking about switching to hot (Bikram) yoga--they don't seem to have many positions that affect the pelvic area and you get the added benefit of a cardio workout and toxin elimination through heat. I've done it several times with good results, but it is more expensive in my area than just a health club membership with yoga classes included.
signature: Let's help move IC research along! Do you flare when taking a prescription or non-prescription drug that may be unrelated to IC? Suspect a link between how your IC started and the medications you were taking at the time? The FDA wants to know about this so that they can investigate any possible links and make medications safer. Report IC to the FDA as a possible side effect: https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scrip...tch-online.htm I am on the path toward remission! Had symptoms since 11/2007 Diagnosed with Ulcerative IC on 8/6/2008 (age 25) via an office cytoscopy My symptoms: Urgency, Discomfort, Nocturia, 2 Hunner's ulcers visible without bladder distention, Occasional pinching pain that goes away quickly My flare triggers: Fermented foods and stress IC supplements and lifestyle adjustments: IC diet w/addl restrictions (high protein, low yeast/no fruit, low histamine) Oil pulling with coconut oil Candida cleanse with a Solaray product and diet Cystoprotek Sea buckthorn oil - 1 tbsp daily - this product helps heal wounds faster Magnesium - helps the body produce hyaluronic acid, which is responsible for connective tissue health Flax seed oil Organic Manuka Honey - 2 tsp daily - heals wounds and has anti-bacterial properties Melatonin as a sleep aid for evening flares Stress management & positive affirmations Bladder training Cardio workouts 2-4x weekly Food, exercise, medication and sleep pattern diary to identify flare triggers Other things I tried: Elmiron - took it for a month without side effects but decided to try natural treatments first Elavil - drowsiness & heart palpitations Urine pH testing - mine was in the range of 5.5 to 6.25; did not see any correlation w/flares IgG Allergy testing by Immuno Labs - found food sensitivities On my to-do list: Get tested for vitamin/mineral defficiencies Try passionflower extract for stress management

contents: If you try Bikram, be careful that none of your medicines have warnings about high temperatures. Since I've been on Elavil & Ditropan, I've noticed that hot weather really affects me. So, I have to be extra careful about staying hydrated & seeking out air conditioning... Yoga is a great way to manage stress & get some exercise, which is not easy with IC! It's definitely worth trying.

contents: I finally saw the new Sugarland music video for her new song "All I want to do is ooooo....oooo..oooo." I had a body like hers BEFORE the IC started except I had a little tummy buldge. It makes me sick to think how I let my body go from that to what it is now. A big blob of blubber. SO, my goal is to eat what I should eat. lots of fruit and veggies and no more junk food one and for all! I'm getting that body back if it kills me and then of course if it does kill me I will get that thin sooner or later. Just no one would see it.
Good for you Mary. You have a positive attitude here and I like that. What is sugarlady?? Hey how about you losing the weight and trying Mommy Mia? Great muscial. Vicky
I love that Sugarland song. It's on my Ipod. Good for you Mary. Me too, all my little size clothes are calling my name.

Lol LOVE your humour..lol!!I also LOVE the Sugarland song and I too am bound and determined to get my body back to normal by exercising again and starting to eat right again...I went from having everything low fat, diet etc etc to everything fattening and I think I have had enough!!Am going back to my aerobics just going to start out slower!!
I want to hear this sugarland song. Is that the title sugarland? Who is the artist? I am going to check it out. Vicky

The artist "group" is Sugarland. It's country music. The song is "All I want to do" and it's their last album titled Love on the inside. I am listening to the cd right now in my computer. Here's the lyrics. I don't want to get up baby, let's turn off the phone I don't want to go to work today or even put my makeup on I've got better things to do than my todo list anyway Hide under the covers and waste away the day Let's just lay here and be lazy, baby drive me crazy All I want to do wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wooo All I want to do wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wooo is love you I got my whole life to change the world and climb the ladders Looking at you looking at me is the only thing that matters Come a little closer baby, we can talk without the words Hang a sign on the door, please do not disturb Let's just lay here and be lazy, baby drive me crazy All I want to do wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wooo All I want to do wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wooo is love you Give me a kiss, from that Elvis lip, You don't want to miss this, All I want to do wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wooo All I want to do wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wo wooo is love you wo woo All I really want to do, All I really want to do, All I really want to do is love you, love you, love you Come a little closer baby, we can talk without the words Hang a sign on the door, please do not, please do not, please do not, please do not disturb When I lay down in the evening all I really want to do, When I wake up, when I wake up in the morning baby, all I really want to do... But you can to to their website and here a clip. Sugarland is opening for Carrie Underwood in October and I already have dibs on tickets. You know you are getting old when you kids can "hook" you up with concerts! LOL! Miss Vicky, put on your cowboy hat, it's pretty twangy country music! LOL!

Any of you try to sing the "oooo.oooo....oooo " part with one breath like she does? I've tried over and over and can't do it. When I'm driving and the song comes on I crank up the old radio and I start singing along while bobbing my head. Then I try that part of the song and I'm gasping for air. What wonder the other drivers think of me?? I do know I didn't like the song at first but it grew on me. I've tried using all kinds of soaps to get it off but it's there to stay I'm afraid. Wish I could do areobics again but I've tried a couple times and each time had bleeding from the bladder whichI can't figure out soooooooo it's back to lifting weights. Will use house work as my areobics and picking up after the 6 little monsters is a daily routine and even with that it looks like Romper Room ALL over the house.

OK - so for the most part I am feeling better these days (aside from flares) - but day to day for the most part I have gone from "please rip my bladder out" to "ok I can deal with this". Needless to say after several years of this - I am a bit out of shape. (Not overweight by any means - but flabby) - My Uro and PT both said that walking would be fine - but they did not recommend any other form of exercise at this point. The problem I am running into is - after less than 1 mile - my pelvic area immediately starts cramping (and continues to hurt into the rest of the day)- yet I never get to the point where I have any soreness in my legs etc. and can't seem to get past that point. It's quite frustrating. I'm wearing good shoes, walking on a high school track, I don't know what else I can do to improve this. Any one have any suggestions? (or have the same thing happen - so at least I know I'm not alone).
signature: DX IC Aug 2007 -DX PFD July 2007 Severe Allergies since 18 months old Meds:Aterax 25 mg @ night (only during allergy season - now) Valium 2.5 to 5 mg 2x per day for PFD Elavil 50 mg @ night Bladder Instillations (at home) 3x per week - My recipe is: Lidocaine, Heparin and Sodium Bicarb _____________________________________________ Meds I could not tolerate: Elmiron and Lyrica

contents: I really got into walking last year. I took it very slowly at first. I just walked for about 15 minutes the first week, then gradually built it up. I got to where my friend and I were walking at a pretty quick pace for 2 hours. I did deal with some pelvic pain sometimes after I got home. I would normally take a valium when I first got home or even before I got in the car to drive home. Just about a 2 minute drive. I should have walked to the track actually! LOL! Anyway, walking ended up being a really good deal. I never have had issues with walking or sitting, but I still had to slowly work up my pace. I wish I was walking like that again this year. We called them prayer walks because both of us were going through so much last year. Not like we aren't this year too, but we are both so busy. She only has time when she gets off work at 2:30 and I don't get home until after 6:00 most days.

Walking is great. Go slowly, no power walking or with weights or anything. Just "admire the roses" so to speak. Don't push yourself at all. I used to get pelvic cramps when I tried to run when I had a flare - I was doing too much. To me that's what that symptom would represent while exercising. Just nice, mild walking shouldn't bring them on - are you trying to go fast? The nice thing about walking, as opposed to running, is you get to actually see things, people, nature, etc. I found walkers are friendlier to each other while runners just go sailing by - not noticing anything - just wanting to get it over with!

contents: walking Oh yes walking can really make me feel like I've pulled a groin muscle --maybe it's a pelvic floor issue. It seems to go best if I am on the treadmill with zero incline I can even jog a little I try to do this and Curves a few times a week but I skip any machines that even look like they could cause inner thigh or lower back pain. You get to know what works for you and it really keeps me sane

Sometimes it just takes awhile to get used to it. I do swimteam year-round and even taking a week off can make a huge difference. It takes awhile to get in shape, it doesn't happen overnight. I can tolerate pain and usually choose to, but it's also to where it won't worsen things. That's the kind of pain you want to avoid (there is certainly a difference between good pain and bad pain). My cramping (if any, which isn't often) goes awhile after a short bit and I just ignore it if it seems o.k. (it generally is, but everyone is different). Glad you are trying something. Hope you find enjoyment out of the walking. Having good company or scenary can truly make a difference. I love the beach, as well as having my old landlord's puppy with me (when I had lived there anyhow). Good friends are nice too. Praying for ya.

Thanks everyone - I'm glad I'm not the only one - I'm not walking too fast - but very well may have started out to much at first - I ended up in a flare all day yesterday from a walk the previous night - so I think I'm going to back down on how long I'm walking for a little while and gradually increase. Thanks again.

contents: I am starting to think that exercise, at least fairly strenuous exercise, makes me flare. I have been journaling since my diagnosis a couple of months ago and it seems to be a pattern at least most of the time. I used to be insanely active and I have had to cut back so much over the last couple of years, that I am getting soft and I am gaining weight quite steadily. This morning I was feeling fairly well, so I went for a bike ride with my husband and BAM, I am in a flare. Ouch. Next Thursday I am going to see an IC specialist that I moved heaven and earth to get to see, and I am hoping he will get me started on some treatments. But I am terrified to start Elavil as it tends to pack on the weight. Does anyone find that exercise is a trigger for flares? If so, how do we stay in shape? IC might not be terminal, but if I lay on my couch for the rest of my life under my heating pad, my bones will turn to powder and my heart will clog up CindyLu
signature: CindyLu Current medications: Elmiron 100mg 3x day Vesicare 10mg 1x day Elavil 10mg 1x day Estring

contents: i know exactly how you feel. i used to run with my boyfriend everyday, and i loved being in great shape. now sometimes even simple tasks (like vacuuming or sweeping) can send me into a flare. i suggest that you try different types of exercise. i started working on a stationary bike - the kind where you lean back and your feet are kind of out in front of you (all gyms that i've been to have them). for me, this takes the pressure off my bladder and back area, which are what hurt me the most during a flare, and what seem to be irritated during high impact activities like running. i can still get my heart going and not regret it the next day! it's definitely not as good as running, but it's something. also, i tolerate certain low weights well. hope i helped a little
signature: "Those who dream by day are cognizant of many things that escape those who only dream at night" 21 year old Boston College student Communications major Women's Studies minor Diagnosed IC and PFD December 2007 Also have: Lyme Disease, Hypoglycemia, recurring Ovarian Cysts, and high cholesterol.

contents: I'm currently in a flare from vacuuming last week. I feel your pain. I used to do kickboxing, step aerobics, weight lifting, toning DVDs . . I was an exercise junkie. Now I can't do anything jarring. The only thing I can do is walk on a level surface and if I do too much of that I'll flare too. I've gotten a lot of relief from Elmiron as far as being able to eat more foods but exercise still flares me.
signature: The combo of things that brought me out of remission were artificial sweetners, liquid vitamins with citric acid, tight pants, lower body workouts and stopping all my allergy meds cold turkey during height of allergy season. contents: the whole exercise thing really, really does suck. i had been involved in martial arts for over ten years, and i even got my black belt in kempo. i had to stop it for a while because of work and school, but recently i tried to join a club at my college and it was MISERABLE. i used to love love love it - it's a great stress reliever and honestly really cool for a girl to know (i've used it more than once on over-friendly guys haha). sooo yay for us ICers!! sorry, just thought i'd rant a bit
signature: "Those who dream by day are cognizant of many things that escape those who only dream at night" 21 year old Boston College student Communications major Women's Studies minor Diagnosed IC and PFD December 2007 Also have: Lyme Disease, Hypoglycemia, recurring Ovarian Cysts, and high cholesterol.

contents: Thanks ladies for all your input. I guess I will just have to get used to sending my husband off alone on bike rides and big hikes. I have 3 really lovely bikes hanging in my garage, and I figure I might as well sell the darn things for all the use they are getting. I will try to stay in shape with the lower impact alternatives for now, and ones that don't involve squishing my crotch....sorry. And it is always great to hear that Elmiron and Elavil helped someone without side effects. Very encouraging. As for ranting Samantha...GO FOR IT! I am all for it. Cindy

contents: Biker alert: Get the Spongy Wonder seat! I'm not allowed to post it, but as a long-time biker who hated regular bike seats even before IC (and wonder whether I caused nerve damage that way), I will tell you it may very well allow you to get out there and move again. It's not jarring to any parts.
Thanks Orlando. I will look into that.

contents: I find the bike or treadmill to be murderous to IC.... the treadmill just puts so much pressure on the back bladder etc... have you tried yoga.. omg.. that is a complete wonder to IC.. lots and lots of stretching.. ... try doing light weights.. no more than 5 pounds to start.. especially if you dont typically lift weights.. maybe even 2 pounds just enough to tone you.. try lifting veggie cans.. just do more reps.. and do it sitting down till your body gets used to it and you can stand up..
signature: Healing thoughts Bea

contents: I do lift weights but, much lighter than what I used to do. I also do yoga and I find it does not cause a flare, but it totally can't replace what I used to do. I just don't feel like I am getting a good workout, and neither does my body because I am looking pudgy and soft now. I guess it is an issue of acceptance for me, trying to move on and trying not to compare what I do now and what I could do then. I feel quite angry still, even 3 months after my "unofficial" diagnosis. I just hope that once I have seen my new urologist who knows about IC (this Thursday) that I can get some treatments and maybe some relief. And I just hope I can tolerate the meds. I tend to be kind of drug sensitive so I am fearful of getting my hopes up too high.
signature: CindyLu Current medications: Elmiron 100mg 3x day Vesicare 10mg 1x day Elavil 10mg 1x day Estring

contents: CindyLu I completely feel your frustration. After 18 months I am still not willing to accept that I can never exercise again like I used to. I am hopeful there's a medication out there that will help relieve the pain and swelling I get from exercise. We are all so different so you will just have to experiment with what works for you. I can't do yoga or even lift weights too much (even in a sitting position) and riding the bike is a no-no as well but I can walk on a treadmill as long as it's not on an incline.

contents: Isn't this IC "thing" bizarre. The fact that we are all so different makes it so difficult to get a handle on it. I still kind of don't know my food triggers. I am always pretty much flaring, so it is hard to tell what to eat and what not to. It is a matter of degrees I guess...or what makes it hurt more. I find it weird that my IC doesn't flare up "while" exercising, but afterwards I notice I get worse. I also seem to get worse later in the day. Could be all the days food in my gut. I see by your information on your post that you have done IC while pregnant. I can only imagine what that is like. Congratulations.
signature: CindyLu Current medications: Elmiron 100mg 3x day Vesicare 10mg 1x day Elavil 10mg 1x day Estring

contents: Yeah my flares generally hit me about 24 hours AFTER exercising. Sunday morning I ran the vacuum all over the house. Monday morning I woke up feeling nauseated which progressed into pain later in the day. By Tuesday I was urinating several times an hour and had pain all up in my back as well. It's like a delayed inflammation/reaction . . . very strange. And yes I'm currently pregnant and anticipating the delivery and how it will affect my IC . .just praying a lot right now and hoping for the best.

contents: I totally relate to this. I went for a walk on a beach that was rocky...so my footing was very unsteady...bouncy...etc...I could feel my bladder bouncing around....I knew I would be in a bit of a flare ....and I was later that day. I blame the rough terrain and walk. I was a runner...and now have resorted to walking. I also was someone who did an ABDOMINAL workout every two days. I can not do this now...my bladder becomes sore....so I have been doing another ab workout that does not put so much contraction and pressure on my bladder. Kind regards, Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:39 AM.

I don't think I get flares from exercising and I do a few hours a day most days. Maybe I do, but I'm so used to it that I just oversee it sometimes. I love what I do too much to give it up and it's not hurting my health to keep going. My pain is 24/7 anyway and I don't know patterns of flares, but it bugs me all the time anyhow and I just have that mindset. Would something you always eat or drink before or after exercise cause anything? Best ot luck!
What types of exercise is safe for IC patients? I have been going to the bym lately and I don't know what exactly I should and should not do. Help?? Thanks.
signature: Life is like a box of chocolates, you never know what your gonna get!! Kirstie

contents: Safe exercises. WEll I am no expert at IC. Only being recently diagnosed a month ago. I have done so much reading and researching so I feel quite confident in my personal approach to fitness. Now that being said, I do see that everyone is differen there, and everyone does not react to the same foods as others, and I am going to assume this is the same for exercises. For me personally. I use to teach fitness classes. I will not teach fitness classes come September (I am off for the summer)....with the bouncing and moving, it would not be healthy for my bladder. I was a runner, and will not run again...again same reasons. I teach yoga, and will continue with this (I HOPE....)....still trying to figure it all out as I do have frequency and hope that the alternative medicine that I am on will have benifited me by September. I only just started...and it takes at least a month..... I want to add that I do yoga daily about 2 hours....and it helps my IC tremendously.....when I talk about teaching in September, I am talking about classes that I teach to people who register for my class, and with me peeing so much, right now, I can not see this happening...I HOPE this changes...I have faith that it will.....my alternative approach to dealing with IC just needs time to kick in.... ANy form of exercise that is putting pressure on the bladder (like sit ups) I found irritated my bladder. It is suggested (as I read this on a website for IC) that you should not lift anything heavier then a milk carton when in a flare of IC. SO i assume when you are not flaring.....I certainly would not lift heavy...just to be safe. I swim, I HAVE BEEN SWIMMING three times a day (we have a salt water pool) and this helps me tremendously!! it is the only thing my bladder feels releif from, no pressure, your body is light and there is no pressure placed on any inner organs. It is very relaxing and cooling/refreshing and I think this helps when you feel inflamed and pressure down below. I am a firm believer of walking at a nice pace on flat ground....you can easily add great arm movements and get yoru heart rate up. I do stairclimb....this does not bother my bladder. I did read that this is a form of cardio that should be avoided....I find no reason too. IT helps me. Hope this helps.... kind regards, Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:38 AM. Reason: added info

I recommend swimming too. The chlorine doesn't bother me, and the water supports your body and lets you stretch and use every muscle. Plus my bladder seems to like the cool water. It feels like the coolness reduces my inflammation a bit. Just listen to your body and don't overdo it. Hope this helps.

At the gym, I use the elliptical trainer and the rowing machine. Just keep the resistance low til you know what you can do without provoking more IC symptoms. I also do some free weights (bicep curls, tricep kickbacks, military presses) & the low row & chest press machines. Again, remember to try things one at a time & start out really easy in the beginning. Don't try all these the same day thinking everyone is the same. I'm ok with some things, but not all the same as other people. We're all individual in what works/doesn't. I also can do some yoga (avoid postures that stress the abdomen or pelvic floor), walking, hiking. I have the ICA yoga tape, it's ok, good for beginners or when flaring because it's not much of a workout, but it is good for soothing or maintaining flexibility. I have the walking dvd for sale in the icn shop linked at the top of the page, it's also excellent for me. Hope you find some things that work for you.
signature: Kadi ------------------------------------------------------------- I am not a medical authority nor do I offer medical advice. In all cases, I strongly encourage you to discuss your medical treatment with your personal medical care provider. Only they can, and should, give medical recommendations to you. ------------------------------------------------------ New favorite quote: "God gives us only what we can handle. Apparently God thinks I'm a bad-ass" ~Author Unknown Source - Pinterest " Current treatments: -IC diet -Elavil 50mg at night -Continuous use birth control pills (4-5 periods/year) -Heparin/Marcaine/Sodium Bicarb home instills at night 3-4x per week, more often if needed -Pyridium if needed, -Pain medicine at bedtime daily, as needed during the day several times per week -Antibiotic when doing an instillation to prevent UTI -Colace & SmartFiber to treat chronic constipation from meds, Fleet enema as needed -Dye Free Benadryl 50 mg at bedtime -"Your Pace Yoga: Relieving Pelvic Pain" dvd, walking, treadmill at gym -Managing stress= VERY important! -Fur therapy: Hugging the cat!

contents: I use hand weights every day...upper body 3 days and lower body 3 days. Nothing heavier then 10 pounds. I used to walk 4 miles a day with DH but since this big flare only walk 2 miles. I get uncomfortable and have to pee...when I'm just sitting around I can go much longer between bathroom visits. I quit doing sit ups 3 years ago as I pulled the oblique muscle on my right side and wound up doing physical therapy for it.

You can ask your doctor, but I don't see what exercises make a difference really. I just do things I love to do, such as swimming and biking. Doesn't affect the pain. I have to go to the bathroom a lot, but that's nothing new and would be, regardless of what I'm doing. Swim coaches and teammates know I go often and some know why. It's cool. Hope you find something that works well for you, as well as enjoyable at the same time.

Does anyone know where you can get a very low beginners yoga tape. I need to get some exercise back into my life as I can not walk for long distances. I have looked on line but I am not sure what to get. It would be great to have three different tapes that you could work your way inito doing. I have one that shows three different ways to do the same pose but it is confusing to say the least. Thanks

I moved your post and started a new Thread with it. I hope you get some recommendations. Have you looked in the ICN Shop? There is one available. Here is the link to it: Interstitial Cystitis Network Page Not Found http://www.icnsales.com/Exercise-and-Fitness-p-1-c-18.html The Interstitial Cystitis Network Page Not Found

contents: yoga dvd If you can find a nice KRIPALU HATHA style of yoga you will be all set. It is slow moving, holding postures, and focusing on breath in connection with body movement and mind presence. It is very healing. A wonderful compliment for someone who is strugging with the frustrations of IC. I am a KRIPALU HATHA yoga teacher and I recommend this video to all my beginner students, it is slow moving and very healing. I think you would enjoy it. I know yoga heals me daily, it gives me the mindset to relax feel free and open and look past the IC. It gives me strength and patience daily to cope with my every day hectic lifestyle. It allows me to live life through the energy of my three children. It truly is transformational. (if you have any questions do not hesitate to email me) Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:39 AM. Reason: Removed advertising (not allowed on the IC Network Forums) and website address that linked to online sales
I do the Rodney Yee A.M. Yoga tape I got at Whole Foods. Its very easy and only 20 minutes - a great way to start your day.
signature: Dx'd with PBS, PFD and uro genital atrophy in 4/07, Self dx - General Anxiety Disorder (all my life) Ulcerative colitis (one and only attack but a terrible one -almost lost my colon) 1999. ..................................................... Meds, etc. Asacol - 10 per day Estrace 1 gram, 3 x per week (for atrophic urogenital area.) I'm post-menopausal. Had 6 weeks of PFD therapy summer of 2007. Alternative medicine I practice: Traditional Chinese Medicine herbs (daily) Acupuncture/Traditional Chinese Medicine (MD) for above conditions one time per mo. Yoga (daily) Exercise - daily - used to run but now I walk a few miles or row for 1/2 hr. Meditation and prayer. Self hypnosis for stress and anxiety. Tai Chi - also, not as often as I should - few times a week. Homeopathy (cantharsis 30x for burning.)

contents: Yep, I did it. The bladder aspect of things was my biggest concern, but I got through it. The race was in a lake and consisted of 5 continuous 5K loops. I didn't urinate till the 4th lap (I am good at holding it because I usually have to or have trouble going properly, though I have to go often), but once that first time kicked in, I just kept wanting to go and did more often. I had a hard time going and swimming at the same time, but would quickly stop and tread. I couldn't empty it completely, but did my best and just kept going. Had my paddler go ahead of me when doing it cuz it was so intimidating. Had to go often afterwards as usual, but didn't have any unusual problems since then. Grateful for that, as well as finishing it. And of course, I have to go at this moment and am sure I will soon again. The joys of IC. That and the fibromyalgia aren't going to stop me from what I want to do, yet I also have to be careful. I've had to cut down my swimming this week due to chest pain from the race and am hoping that will heal soon.
What a great accomplishment! Thanks for sharing! Hugs, Tracey
OMG! Are you Nuts??? Ok, sorry. Coffee is starting to kick in a little. Congratulations!!! Michelle.
I'm glad you made it! Congratulations! Donna

signature: Kim Diagnosed August 2001 Current IC meds: Elmiron (since 2001), Levaquin (one pill after intercourse to prevent UTIs), Effexor (for depression & anxiety) Past IC meds: Amitriptyline (Elavil), Hydroxyzine (Vistaril), Detrol LA, Lexapro (for depression & anxiety, but also helped my IC) (They all helped, but I was able to discontinue them.) I've been virtually symptom free and able to eat & drink whatever I'd like for about 8 years now. ***************************** “We who lived in concentration camps can remember the men who walked through the huts comforting others, giving away their last piece of bread. They may have been few in number, but they offer sufficient proof that everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of the human freedoms -- to choose one's attitude in any given set of circumstances, to choose one's own way.” ~ Viktor Frankl “You cannot control what happens to you, but you can control your attitude toward what happens to you, and in that, you will be mastering change rather than allowing it to master you.” ~ Brian Tracy

contents: Thanks guys! The IC creates challenges for so many things in life, but I just make the best of it. I was diagnosed with it a month ago with the cystoscopy with hydrodistention, though I had problems for a whole lot longer. Had a softball game today and of course I had to play the whole time. I held it though, but sometimes it seems like I always have to go anyway and the pain is always going to be there too, so that kind of helps me to get through it too. Probably not the healthiest way to go, but I don't always have a choice either.

Congrats!! on such an amazing accomplishment! A very inspirational story. I am so glad you did it, you belived and you did it! Way to go! I also am just a month into being diagnosed...and I sometimes wonder if I can do things I did in the past, so thankyou for sharing such a positive story! Kind regards, Last edited by bluetou; 11-13-2008, 07:40 AM.

The is soooo great!! I love hearing stories of beating the IC & Fibro!! :woohoo: I would love to do a swim like that!! Even though you were were a little sore, I bet it felt great mentally!!:woohoo: You Go!!!
signature: Meds on now: dextro 10mg 5000mg Vitamin D- when tested, I was sooo low. Compound Hormones- Had hysterectomy when 24, ovaries out 37 At night: 4mg Zanaflex- Fibro & PFD plendil- Raynalds I have Fibro, IC, IBS, PFD, Raynauds. Tried: Past Meds Lexpro- changed to Cymbalta for better pain relief –off both Ultram – three times a day. Helped , I was tired of always being so tired. Elmiron-took for a year- Topamax-Did not need anymore since on dextro. Atarax- did not need anymore Pictures: Hubby & Me, Ozzy, My three Sons!! What a proud Mother I am!! .

contents: Wow - congratulations. I am humbled by that kind of story as I am sitting at my desk at the office - way after hours - feeling sorry for myself. I sometimes wonder what's wrong with me when someone like you is able to look past your condition and your symptoms and just deal with it and I end up acting like an invalid because I'm uncomfortable with the symptoms. Anyways - good for setting an example for people with not only IC but other chronic conditions and letting them know that there can be a life beyond what might seem like obstacles and limitations!
signature: Symptoms: Sensory Urgency - having to urinate when I have lower volume in my bladder. Sometimes bladder discomfort. Sometimes Testicle aching. Heavy Anxiety! Diagnosis: Pelvic Floor Dysfunction. Dr's don't think I have IC but the more I read the more I think this is all related. I also have a severe hormone imbalance - extremely low Testosterone. Medications: Zoloft 100 mg, Valium 5mg (use sparingly) What might help me: Still trying to figure this out.

contents: Thanks for the positive comments and motivation guys. Does help through the tough times. I hope it won't let you stop from doing things if possible. I know I would've done better if my body is normal, but we all have things to work with and our strengths/weaknesses in whatever we do. I have done swimteam since I was 9 (am 29 now), so that has helped. I can't do as much or as well as I used to though. My body is having a hard time now, but am doing my best to help it and rest for awhile before picking up the swimming at more yardage again (though I won't be doing any 25K's for awhile, just some races that are 1-2 miles). It's so hard though wanting to do things and knowing that I'll either have to go often, have trouble going, or taking forever to go. It's so frustrating and embarassing, but I have to learn to get through it. Some people know and it helps and sometimes I have to crack a joke or two in there to ease the awkwardness.

I'm on the weight loss bandwagon now, after realizing I am very overweight and it is putting a strain on my back. So I'm trying to start doing more exercise and attempting to eat right (Carbs are my one weakness however >.<). So, I've been doing Yoga (very basic stuff, working on the 'sun salutation' right now, just to get my muscles to lean up), and trying to walk when I can, but I want some more variety. I really want to ride my bike again, but I've heard that can do nasty things. I'd like to know what some of you other folks out there do to help skinny up!
signature: Clark College Junior, wanting to go to UW for Fine Art Degree Major: Fine Art Taking a course in medical transcription, must work from home IC diagnosed 07/07 PFD developed sometime during late 07 Uterine Cyst removed 12/09 Chronic Pelvic pain developed in 09 Chronic Back Pain developed in 09 Possible vulvodynia Medications: Elmiron, Detrol LA, BC, Prosed DS, Celexa for anxiety, depression, and pain, No longer taking: Atarax (No help),Amytriptalene (no effect, became suicidal), Ditropan (no effect), Probiotics (sick to stomach), Bactrim (hopefully cleared up my UTI!), avoiding Pyridium unless absolutely needed (sick to stomach), Prozac (helped anxiety, no pain relief), Pamelor (gained nearly 20 lbs in a short time, no anxiety relief), Valium, Vicodin, percocet (none of these meds even touch my pain >_<) Current Therapies: acupuncture, heat, Vicodin if needed, lots of laying down, gaming as a distraction, Pain Management classes started 9/22, Wanting to try TENS therapy

contents: I have IC/PFD and Vestibulitus. I was told by my Physical Therapist NO BIKE RIDING - bad for pelvic muscles (which sucks because we did this a lot as a family in the nice weather. I have started walking. I go to the track at our highschool, with my IPOD and think I am really cool! I have just added 1 lb. hand weights to make it a little more of a workout. I like being outside when I can and I can watch some of the kids practice sports which is a nice diversion while walking. I have also started back on push-ups and have a total gym. I was also told my my PT to only work my upper body and absolutely so situps (bad for the bladder). I was an extremely physically fit person and extremely healthy eater until a december when my IC was diagnosed. I hate that I don't feel like I am eating as healthy or excercising as much, but I am slowly doing what I can. good luck to you!

I like water aerobics but I know that others have problems because of the chorine. I find it is OK If I shower immediately afterwards and get out of the wet suit. I know that the Bike at the gym with give me PFD so I only do that once in awhile. I stick with more leg and thigh exercise equipment and arm exercies eguipment. Good luck on loosing wet I know it isn't easy. I have been working on it myself.

contents: The only thing I can tolerate is the gentle Yoga program I found in an inexspensive book created just for IC patients. Swimming helps, but the chemicals bother me "down there", and I have difficulty walking for very long now without my cane (which BTW my 52 year old Mother gave me, LOL!!) Hope you find something that works without making you feel worse.
signature: Rachel DX's: IC; PFD; possible Fibromyalgia; IBS; Symptoms: Frequency, Urgency, pelvic and bladder pain, bladder and pelvic spasms, difficulty starting urination, weak stream, incontinence, vaginal and urethral pain I've Tried: three Hydrodistentions, various Rescue Installments; 6 DSMO treatments; Pyridium Plus; Proced; Detrol (patch); Elavil; Uricet K; Elmiron; Nortiptyline; Ultram; Allegra; Ditropan; Ditropan ER; Vesicare; regular Lidocain; Neurontin; Lyrica; and few more I can't remember! Currently using: Valium; Vicadin; Proced DS; Claritin; Buffered Lidocain; Cymbalta; Baclofen; Prazosin @ night (also on Prednisone and Adderall for my joint pain and severe fatigue) Heating pads; frozen water bottles; A&D ointment; Poise pads; IC dieter since 8/06; yoga; imagery & relaxation CD, Mindfullness, self healing CD's; hot baths; seat cushion; prayer I am 28 yrs old, dx'ed in '06, still trying to get my health undercontrol!!

contents: hi all, i'm having a bad flare and thought walking would maybe make me feel better, but after walking half a mile i noticed my symptoms got a lot worse. as a rule of thumb, is it better to wait till flares are resolved before getting into a lot of activity? thanks...

Chloe, I walk my dog 3 miles every morning. As a rule, the walking does make my bladder hurt a bit, even on a good day. So when I'm flaring, we skip the walks and play fetch in the yard. Try your exercise again when your bladder has calmed down. Hey, it's a good excuse not to vacuum or anything else right now, right?

contents: Chloe I live on the coast and am often in the water...I have both IC and VV and saltwater doesn't cause me problems with either. The beaches where I live luckily are clean and the water clearer and cleaner than most in the US. I can't tolerate pools with chlorine.

I normally go to a beach every summer and spend lots of time laying in my lounge chair in the water. The water doesn't bother me either, but I have read where some people use vaseline to coat the opening and that seems to help. I have several friends with salt water pools, but of course the water is not nearly as salty as the ocean, but it so great to swim in. It changes to chlorine somehow, but it's not strong enough to bother me. T83

I just can't seem to get back on the low fat diet or any diet. I feel better if I don't eat, if I don't eat junk food and when I was thinner so why can't I get off the food wagon???????? Last night I was thinking about starting to smoke! Really......I'm miserable being fat again so why can't I stick to it? Only time I don't snack is when I'm crocheting but can't crochet while washing dishes?

I've had the same problem for years! I'll do just great and then something tosses me off the wagon. There's an old expression: "It's not what you're eating - it's whats eating you". I can get really engrossed in an activity (lately it's been making jewelry) and forget about food, but when I surface I'm starving and grab anything that's available. So I know I eat for comfort, for something to do, when I'm angry, when I'm frustrated, when I'm bored. Does any of this sound familiar? I've seen suggestions about keeping a food diary and writing down how you were feeling and why you were eating. I've never done it, but it might help. Maybe I should take my own advice!!

You sound just like me. I used to keep track of what I ate and counting the calories but I've done it so many times for sooooooooo long I"m sick of it. Do think I will go back to not eating breakfast. Never was one for that and I think that throws my system into wanting food. Plus what calories I save from not having breakfast I have later on in the afternoon when I get hungry. I know they say eating breakfast means losing weight but not for me.

I think that makes us all munchie eaters once I start I can't stop!!!!! With me I think I'm always thinking of food because of this difficult IC diet we try to stick to so I just seem to think food, food, food I also have my days were I'm so full up & bloated & it just makes me feel lousey! I just try my hardest to stear clear from the kitchen unless preparing meals. Don't think breakfast shoud be missed but that's my personal opinion. Have you tried filling yourself up with oatmeal/porridge (not sure what you all call it)? Keeps you full for a longer period without making you feel sluggish? Big hugs xx

What's saving me is eating scheduled, planned, snacks. I just finished my midmorning snack, which I followed by drinking some flavored water. I'm not hungry now so I won't be eating anything until lunch time. Donna

contents: The only thing that saves me from eating the 'junk' is to not even bring that stuff into the house! You can't eat or dink it if it's not there! This way I only have to exert willpower for that 45 minutes I'm at the grocery store. I end up having nothing but low-fat, healthy, foods to snack on for the week! Vicki Last edited by VickiB; 06-22-2008, 05:37 AM. Reason: correction
signature: "The happiest people don't necessarily have the best of everything; they just make the best of everything they have."

contents: Originally posted by ICNDonna What's saving me is eating scheduled, planned, snacks. I just finished my midmorning snack, which I followed by drinking some flavored water. I'm not hungry now so I won't be eating anything until lunch time. Donna Hi Donna, what flavoured waters do you have? Do you flavour them yourself or buy them? I live in England so if they're brought I might struggle???? xx

I like Aquafina Flavor Splash best, but if you can get the flavored syrups made for coffee, they are also good in cold water. We get sugar free DaVinci syrups, which come in all kinds of flavors. Donna
