contents: Thanks Donna, I shall have a look into those see if I can find anything xx

I have rediscovered roasted pumpkin seeds and pretzel sticks. I am also a believer in eating something for breakfast especially since that is when I take most of my supplements and I have to eat food with some of them or I will be nauseated all day. I normally eat a Special K bar (blueberry) or a banana and drink a bottle of water on my way to work. The meal my hubby and I do skip most nights is supper. We eat a low calorie snack or yogurt (it doesn't bother my IC) and I am just fine. They have just raised all the prices on our snack machines at work so we have started a "good for you" snack drawer in our area since so many of us are on the "train" too! We have things like the oreo crisps, Special K bars, pretzels, licorice and we keep popsicles in the freezer. We all just put money in a fund and we take turns "supplying" it. They are healthy as long as you just eat the one package, which I do! I remember when I lost alot of weight a few years ago, I ate sunflower seeds since that is what my kids ate during the sports things. The work it took to open the seed in my mouth and get that tiny sunflower seed kept my mouth busy instead of eating things that I shouldn't. I lost alot of weight back then. I also don't buy sweets for the house that I like. I just buy things I know my husband likes. When my kids come in town for a couple of days, I stock up on their favs, and when they leave, I send the leftovers with them in a bag so I won't be tempted. When I realized how anemic I was several years ago, I was looking for iron-rich foods and found that pumpkin seeds which I have loved since I was a kid are a great source of iron. The bag even says great source of iron. I am sitting here this afternoon with some pumpkin seeds and I haven't once even wanted anything out of our healthy snack drawer. Definitely don't start smoking. You know how bad that is for our bladders! Just hang in there and if you have to munch, find something that doesn't have many calories.

I started drinking bottled flavored water with no calories. It's amazing how I can drink that without any problems from the bladder. I can drink a whole bottle at once . Well, within 10 minutes and I don't ahve to pee afterwards. Not sure where all the water is going but it's somewhere in my body. When I stuck with the low fat diet I did start losing weight. Not very fast but it was coming off. I guess I just need to stay busy and try not to let the twinkies take over. I must have more will power then a twinkie I would think.

Mary - can you eat yogurt at all? If so - try that when you want something sweet. I LIVE and eat YoPlait LowFat Light yogurts all the time and I LOVE LOVE LOVE the Lemon Merangue and Key Lime ones because they taste just like the pies! That way..when I'm craving pie - I eat those especially. Its just like eating the pie filling, except they are only 100 calories and no fat, and the little sugar you get is a natural type of sugar! They have TONS of different flavors too - peach, berries and cream, boston cream pie, white chocolate and berries, blueberry, strawberry, vanilla - oh the list goes on and on! *now you're making me crave a key lime one!* Plus you're getting the added benefit of live cultures, and calcium - they also have the Light Thick and Creamy (also 100 calories and no fat) and they fill me up - its like a custard - so thick the spoon just stands up in them! The regular Thick and Creamy is like 200 calories with like 6 grams of fat (OUCH!) so I dont get those, but if you can tolerate yogurts, give them a try - you wont be disappointed I promise you. My fridge has at least 26 different yogurts at all times in it - and the entire family loves them. You can also take one out of the container and FREEZE it if you want a frozen treat (put it in another container and label it) for a frozen yogurt dessert vs. icecream! YUMMY!!! They even make chocolate and vanilla flavored too! Mix them up and see what you get - its no different that TCBY Frozen Yogurt to me... Try thinking of your "diet" as a change of lifestyle - and not a diet at all. If you deprive yourself of things or you THINK about what you're not having, you'll fail at it for sure. You just have to find recipes and mix them up - like try using oatflour (just grinding oatmeal up really fine - I do it all the time) vs. using white regular flour in a recipe - or mix it half and half (I do this alot), so if a recipe calls for 1 cup of regular flour, I use 1/2 a cup of reg and 1/2 a cup of oat flour, that way I get only half the "bad stuff". I make cookies and cakes this way all the time as well as muffins and my kids dont even know it! Take out the fats and oils (butter, oil, etc) and replace it with applesauce or non-fat plain yogurt - it adds the same moisture content, just not the calories or fats - and you cant taste it in recipes either. Salads are great - if you're usually a sandwhich person, eat everything you would on a sandwhich in salad, just omit the bread. Subway Restaurants make subs into salads now - and they are HUGE - anything you want on your sub, you can get in your salad! I love them! Popcorn is a great snacker -but in moderation only because it IS a high sugar food believe it or not (all corn is, so is carrots, peas, watermelon, and most fruits, even though they are natural sugars, so moderation is the key) but natural fruits are better for you than candy. Try popping some grapes into the freezer and let them freeze - then pull them out and eat them - they taste just like popsicles, I promise! Cherries are HUGE fillers - ever eat a handful of cherries? I guarentee you'll feel full - I cant eat more than 12 at best before I'm full. Plums, bananas, apples...make a fruit salad and snack on it - mix in some peach yogurt and make a parfait - with a small salad on the side and you've got lunch! Oh the possibilities are endless. You should eat small meals every 3-4 hours in order to keep your blood sugar up, and at every meal you should always have a protein and a carb - never have a carb without a protein, as a protein helps the carb digest and not transfer into a sugar as fast. You CAN do it Mary...I know you can. Of course - all of the above are just suggestions and just as we all are different, it all depends on what you can and cannot eat. I can eat alot of those things.....and you should always discuss any diet changes with your doctor. Be sensible - and please, whatever you do ..DO NOT START SMOKING! Hugs, Brittany

Brittany, I ddin't know those custard yoguart ones and pie flavored ones came in no fat!! I thought they were all loaded with fat so I was trying to stick to the no fat ones that I found but were just blueberry, strawberry (which I cannot stand!) or vanilla. I tried so hard to stick with those but I just coudln't get them down. NOW I wish I had read your post this before going grocery shopping this morning!! I love yoguart and it doesn't bother the bladder. I sometimes even mix it with cookied oatmeal instead of using milk. THANK YOU! THANK Y OU! THANK YOU!! for letting me know about this. 26 in the fridge? It reminds me of that commercial where the wife is talking on the phone how she had lime pie and all this yummy dessert and the husband is searching in the fridge looking for it or that one where the woman takes her cloths to be taken in and tells the lady how she hd been eating pie too. NOW I'm hungry. ha You are right to. Strange how last night I was thinking I shouldn't think of it as a diet just like eating normally because I do think about fooooooooood all the time! Took a long time to get peanut butter out of my head but I just buy smooth instead of chunky for others since I can't stand smooth. I have tried using wheat flour for baking but the muffins turned into rubber tires and I swear they could kill someone if they got hit in the head with one. I do like the idea of freezing the yoguart. OH, how I wish I had some of that yoguart and I would go to the store to get it but I'm out in the boonies and just don't like the idea of making another trip BUT Tomorrow maybe I can pop over to the store and get some on my way home from Doc's. yeah, I know smoking would be teh bad way of going but most all the movie stars do it and they are sooooooooooo thin. I did read the "movie" magazine today and it had how some of the famouse actors have fat wives. They like them fat. Thanks again for the yoguart info! Yum..yum...me want yoguart...me want yoguart.........

LOL Mary - I love those commercials Okay here's a trick for the muffins and whatnot. I did the same thing when I first tried switching flours and I could've bounced them as well..*ICK* so..I mixed 1/2 and 1/2 when I needed to (like for cakes) to make them very light...and it works great - but..to make them even lighter - first..sift the flour and add baking powder and soda - it makes it even LIGHTER! You can also add Sprite instead of water if you're making just cake out of oatmeal or muffins (I use Diet, no calories) along with the levening agents. For pancakes this ROCKS and makes them very very light, fluffy and sweet! makes awesome crepes too - add some fruit to them..oh MY LORD are they amazingly sweet and gooey! (okay..now I'm drooling!) Warm up some yogurt and fruit and smear it around on a crepe...oh oh...and add some Fat Free Cool Whip..you've got yourself a gourmet desert for sure! So - try playing around a little bit with the recipes....you can do it! Its how I got all of mine, just by playing with recipes I loved. Some failed (miserably I might add) and some didnt. I have brownies I make for the kids that I make with the half and half recipe and they dont know it (or I make them with full oatmeal) and they dont know they're eating healthy brownies - and sugar free ones at that with hardly any fat at all! I dont mind if they eat a few of them because to get the fat amount and sugar in just one regular one, they'd have to eat like 5! Hugs, Brittany

Oh..you got me thinking of my ALL TIME favorite snack.... Mary since you mentioned you can eat peanut butter - I LOVE Reeses Peanut butter cups (I adore them..but they are so high in fat... oh my lord are they a killer!) here is a great treat for you that is SOOOOOOOOOOO nice on your hips! 1 No Sugar Added Fudsicle (I think they're like 20 Calories if I remember right) 1/2 tsp peanut butter (any kind you want) In a very small bowl - CAREFULLY melt the fudsicle until just slightly mushy (not melted completly) and remove from stick Mix in peanut butter (room temperature) Place back into freezer for about 30 - 45 mins until frozen to your liking! Or you can do what I do...since the Fudsicle isnt all the way melted and moreso like a Slurpee texture when I mix in the peanut butter... I just eat it like that! It is SOOOOOOOOO sweet and tastes just like a Reeses - I can never eat the entire thing because its too much chocolate/peanut butter for me. Again - this is only for those than can tolerate these things - and you shouldnt have more than 1 a day either. This also counts as one of those "afternoon snacks" and as a protien because you're getting 1/2 a tsp of peanut butter - I think a serving is 1 Tablespoon or something like that. Hugs Brittany

Went on a Safari what a time I had finding the no fat yogurt you told me about> All I could find was blueberry and strawberry. I swear I had to throw on my cap, hat and smoking pipe and turn into Shirlock Holmes to find the good stuff. THEY HAD CHOCOLATE!!! I did buy two of apple custard and thought probably won't taste good and it disn't. Blaaaaaah. Mixed it with the oatmeal and then it was semi-blaaaaaaaaah but I'll stick with the good stuff. Chocolate, lemon merguie pie ect. Not sure how many I bought but was many. Hey, I buy those fudgsicles too! They have ones now with white chocolate. have you tried them? Not as good as real white chocolate but not bad. That mixture of peanut butter sounds yummy but I'm not supposed to eat peanut butter. One of the big no nos because of the fat. I wish someone would invent no fat peanut butter. Did pretty good yesterday. Only had 3 cookies at night. Remembered how I got on this diet last time. I ate a LOT of fluff. No fat and figured better to eat that then cookies. Thanks again for the great tips!

Mary, Nope, didnt know they made white chocolate Fudgsicles...wow - I'll have to try those Do you have a SuperWalmart, Sams Club, CostCo, BJ's Wholesale Club, Giant Foods, FoodLion or anything like that by you? We have them here in VA, so I can find the Yoplait Light & Fit Yogurts at any one of those stores. I may not be able to find ALL the flavors at all the stores, but I usually find the ones I love at 2 different ones (not too far apart). Sam's Club and Super Walmart usually carry all of them though. BTW - you should really learn to read labels - and compare, especially if you have high cholesterol and high blood pressure. We need fat in our diets, not to exceed a certain amount (usually no more than 30 mg a day, but thats between you and your doctor to decide). If you go all "no fat" then you're trading the fat for high sodium - which is even worse for your body because it drives up your blood pressure, so please be careful there and learn to read labels. If you dont know how to do that, you can search on the internet and learn how It's not very hard and soon you'll learn whats good for you and what isnt good for you. The first 5 ingredients are the most important - if the first ones are partially hydrodginanted ANYTHING or high fructose corn syrup - put them back - they're horrible for you! Cookies huh? Arent they delicious? Oreos are my downfall...ugh! They call my name! LOL. I had to switch though...when I crave cookies..I eat one Snackwell cookie, they are soooooooooo sweet (and yes, they arent good for me either, contain all the "hydrodginates" - ugh...see...I dont follow to a "T" either), but... Fig Newtons are even better for you - or how about a granola bar, or an oatmeal ceral bar, like NuTra Grain? They're just the right sweetness. Another thing I found to stop the sugar cravings is BlueBerry Frosted Mini-wheats - OMG are they SWEET and gooooood! I eat them dry and they have the added benefit of fiber, and fill me up! Lots of tricks there...sneaking in healthy things with the "not so healthy" things too. Dry cereal is a great "snacking" thing to have - especially if you can find a pretty good healthy one, like Kix - I dont like really really sweet things, more semi-sweet, so I try to go for cereals to eat dry for my snacks. When I want candy - I opt for sugar free LifeSavers (butter rum is my all time favorite) or Sugar Free Jolly Ranchers (oh lord..green apple..YUMMY!) A good place to learn about them is by searching again, online . Its very hard not to find anything with partially hydrodginated anything in it now a days, but.. if you can cut back a few things, then thats better for you. Natural sugars are better than high fructose corn syrup and butter is better for you than maragarine (but olive oil sprays mixed with butter are best - IN MODERATION) because butter is all natural *cows make it, right? * and margarine is man-made. Oh I could go on and on, but I wont..I'll spare you LOL! Its just very interesting how we will take care of our cars and what we feed our pets...but we really dont know what we really put into our bodies. Amazing... Hugs, Brittany Last edited by BrittanysDance; 06-26-2008, 02:40 AM.

Hey waterflow, I just wanted to share my experience. For years, I tried and tried to shed the classic 20 I gained at a desk job. Went to the gym 3x a week and worked out like a maniac. Tried a few diets, thought I was cutting back. It wasn't until I stopped buying anything with fake sugars, junk carbs, crackers, and such that I lost it and kept it off. Last night, I wanted to light into a bowl of ice cream. No dice. I've got carrots, almond butter, and popcorn (had to pop it from scratch). Just sharing that this was the real breakthrough for me. After losing the weight, I'll never go back to having stuff in my house that basically isn't working towards nutrition. Believe me, I still eat sweets, but I really have to think about it when I'm out and about.

I have been on fosamax for 2 years due to osteopenia. I have osteoarthritis in my neck and am limited with what physical exercise I can do. Running was always my sport before IC and neck surgery came along (8 yrs ago). I have since tried Yoga, w/ a private instructor. That put me back at the pain center. Swimming also hurt me. I do walk, but not power walking, which would count as a weight bearing exercise. So now, I have been doing Qigong, at home w/ CD's. At first I thought it was boring, but am starting to get the hang of it and feel the benefits of relaxing. Do any of you do Qigong or Tai Chi? Do you consider this to be a relatively good form of weight bearing exercise? I know its better than doing nothing. Lyn

Tomorrow I am going to try and stick with the low fat diet again. Have been trying adn trying but I just keep falling off the wagon and have so many black and blues. Fell into a cactus the one time. Couldn't keep water in me much after that for a few days. Too many leaks. There is soooooooo much junk food in this house. Not using it as an excuse but the hardest part for me are the first 3 days. If I can get through those days without junk food them I'm set as long as I don't cave in and eat one little sweet thing becaue if I do BAME! I'm right back to the start. I need to do this for one: I have hardly any cloths to wear! All my dressy summer cloths for last year don't fit and those were my fat cloths. I have one fat dress left that fits and I want to so badly fit into my nice skinny cloths again and start dressing up when I go to doc's and groceries. Secondly: I need to get my cholesterol down. You would think the thought of having almost 300 cholesterol would scare me into eating right which it did in the beginning but then it didnt' but most of my junk food eating is due to nerves, thinking and being board. ME borad with 6 cats??? It's just a matter of getting my mind set to it and keeping it there. There are no "tricks' that I can follow to help me so I just have to do it. Maybe I can concentrate on getting the house cleaned and if it ever stops raining taking the kids for a "ride" again. I'm crossing my eyes, fingers, toes, legs and arms I can pull this off. Of course if I keep my legs crossed I won't be able to get up to eat and if I keep my arms crossed I can't feed myself anything. Hmmmm, wonder if I could live like that?? Driving would be a little on the hard side but then anything to be skinny again.

Mary...You are so funny. I hope you are able to stick with it this time. I am going to have to join you in your endeavor. I have not been such a good girl over the last couple of weeks and my scale is punishing me.
signature: Sharon Shopping??? Did someone mention shopping? I'll get my hat... ;-) Where I can be found most days. contents: You know how I said I get bored? Last night the cats were doing their before bedtime play like normal. The one kitten was chasing its toy around in the kitchen and instead of going around their big bowl of dry food she decided to smash through it. Dry food ALL over the place. My legs have been so sore from walking and everything else I have times when all I can do is walk which was last night. Now I had to get down on the floor and pick all the food up. Didn’t want to use the broom and get dust in with the food. So there I sat picking up piece by piece with the kittens sitting along with me watching. As if it was the best thing to do since chasing ants. At times they act like little angels and they will roll around as if saying “Hey grandma, I’m the cutest cat ever. I can’t do anything wrong”. Then the next day I have 5 Tasmanian Devils running around in the house with me after them. Like the one on Bugs Bunny. He ate everything in his path. The one kitten I had to get after because she was chewing on the door frame. I told her this isn’t Hansel and Gretel with a house made out of gingerbread. I think they plot during the night on what they will do to me the next day. Ha I wonder if they will ever learn not to destroy the house??? I might have to get some furniture insurance the rate things are going. Ha I almost started crying this morning trying to find something to wear. I am so miserable being fat again but then it's my fault. If I just get off the junk food the weight will start to come off. Mind wise I want to start exercising like I used to. Lift weights, arobeics all of it but my body just can't do it. I am so sore all over and in pain just from walking the children and cleaning I don't think I could walk at all if I add anymore to it. I gain it so fast but takes forever to get it off.

Was laying in bed last night and thought I have to get back to doing my walking but still have time for the cats. I had stopped walking because they could see me from the windows and would cry wanting to be out with me and I felt cruel. When I walk them they get exercise because they are jumping and running around trying to catch bugs while I just mainly stand there. Well, I bought a pet carrier that you wear on your back like a back pack. I thought I will strap them on (one at a time) and take them for a walk with me. They get to do something new and go farther around here and I get some happy company along with me. Plus I get a good work out so I should start losing weight and that should also help the cholesterol. Going back on that diet today but not so many calories. Of course I haven't tried this all yet. Will be in about 30 minutes so lets see if I live to tell the follow up story. Then I thought during the winter after it starts snowing I will take them outside the same way. Need to buy another coat for them. They out grew the one from last winter. Could put their heating pad in it along with a blanket and they should stay nice and warm. Now if I could only figure out a way to take them shopping with me.....
Good for you! Exercise is very important. Hugs, Donna

contents: That is a good idea Mary, I have also seen pet strollers. You know like a baby stroller but made for pets with a net cover own it! They have so many neat things for a furry little friends now.. My sister has life jackets for her dogs.. They love to go swimming..lol Who would of ever thought..lol
signature: Hugs Ronda ONE Second, ONE Bite, ONE Breath, ONE Pill, ONE Minute, ONE Teardrop, ONE Hour, ONE Sip.. ONE DAY! I will Prevail from this disease! IC Hoping for a Cure! Link to Patient Handbook: http://www.ic-network.com/handbook/ Diet Reference Sheet: http://www.ic-network.com/diet/icndi...tsheet0909.pdf Meds For IC: Lyrica-25mg Glucosamine-500 MSM-500mg, Prosed Ds -When Flaring Other Meds: Levlite- Continious Birtcontrol, Micardis-40mg for High Blood Pressure Meds I have Tried: Topamax,Tofranil, Elmiron, Atarax, Cymbalta, Elavil, Enablex, Detral La, Prydium. Lexapro< Bad reaction to this med! Intstills, could not continue them due to some kind of reaction after 3rd instill. Tasted the lidocaine in my mouth, tongue and lips went numb then went into what seemed like a panic attack. Shaking, racing heart, tingling face/head, blood pressure shot up.. Dx With IC in Nov 2006 with Hydro/Cysto Hydro/Cysto Caused Bladder to Rupture. Other Dxs-Vulvodynia,Fibro, Endo, IBS, HPV, Migraines, Spastic Colon, Mild Dysplasia. contents: It was sooooooooo much fun! I survived!! Ha It was so much fun. Nothing like “oh, I have to do my exercising” you know. The hardest part was getting the thing on. The one kitten out grew her mother and she is very very heavy. I call her my “two armer” cat. I went to stand up with her strapped on my back and I couldn’t do it. Ha It took a few tries and I had to pull myself up with the hand rail. The first kitten was happy. Purring away all the way around. (Of course they aren’t kittens anymore but I still think of htem that way) The other 3 weren’t so happy but I have sheltered them too much and they need to learn to accept different things. The one made her normal cat call thing. The little kitten (she stayed small and I couldn’t even tell I had a load with her on) sounded like a baby crying. It was so funny. I hear them from behind me. The last one she sounded like a wolf howling. I put each one in their outside play cage when the walk was over and the last kitten acted like she did all the walking. Ha She laid down and was panting. I told her I was the one who should be laid out on the ground. They will get used to it but it was a 1 hour walk. The bladder held out too. I was thinking on my last time around how the bladder wasn’t saying anything. My LEGS were. Ha Imagine the bladder was thinking if it said anything or complained it would have to work out more or because I was having so much fun my mid wasn’t on it BUT it started complaining after I was done. SO, that will be my morning exercise from now on. Get up early so they can eat and go potty before I start to take them for a ride. Then after I get so I can take each around twice with no problems I will take them 3 times around. Need to get a battery for my watch so I can time it too. Think it will work out good during colder temp too. I DID IT!!!! Yeah!!! Then before I bring them back in from their cage I will take them for “their” walks. That way they can chase the bugs. I don’t want them to miss out on getting steaks. Ha Gross, gross, gross…. I've seen those strollers too. Would make it so much easier but to bumpy and hilly where I am. would be a very rough ride on them. ha We sure do pamper our little ones don't we? Last edited by waterflow; 06-13-2008, 01:48 AM. Reason: added
Just be careful to not do too much to begin with. Sometimes it's best to start out slow and gradually add time until you reach your goal. I'm glad it was fun! Donna

contents: Does anyone know about any weight lose supplement that we may take? I was reading about Orove Detox and I felt a twinge in my bladder ( ha ha ) when I considered buying it. I tried a supplement for over all better health called Reliv and felt so much better physically and metally and had the worst and I mean the worst flare of my entire life. I wasn't doing very good about drinking lots of water and should of started out more slowly on the supplement instead of taking the recommend complete dose and throwing my bladder in shock. It also helped with cravings and thats my down fall. I guess my question is also if I start out slower taking these supplements will my bladder get used to it and slowly increase dosage? Do alot of IC patients suffer with weight gain from meds and inablitiy to execise do to back pain and such? Any ideas or opinions are welcome, thanks. Cinder

The very best way to lose weight is to put yourself on a healthy, sensible, balanced diet. After I was diagnosed with diabetes and took a diabetes education class, I started losing weight. I find that if I have scheduled meals, with planned snacks midmorning and midafternoon, I do very well. I have lost over thirty pounds since my diagnosis eight months ago and am still losing slowly. Donna

contents: I am right there with you sister! I have never been heavier in my life. I know I am not overweight and other people tell me I look good, but I dont FEEL good! I feel so out of shape and unconfident. I too have had other people tell me to put down the food. I know the uncontrolled eating thing all too well. The worst part is that we know what we need to do to lose the weight! I think the first thing you should do, and I will do it too, is to write down every single thing you eat. Even if it is 1 m&m, write it down. I bought a little food scale and have been weighing my portions. That helped a lot. Now i know what a 1/2 cup of rice is as opposed to 2 cups! We could post our progress and set backs on here to keep each other motivated! I laughed when I read this thread b/c just this morning I swore to myself i was going to do something about this weight/exercise thing. I know I need to exercise more, bottom line. not for looks or anything but for my health. I am in my mid twenties and it is time to start getting serious. We can do it though! Sarah

contents: First of all, 7 pounds in a month is a lot; if you do manage to do it, the weight may not stay off. When I was diagnosed with diabetes last September and I went to a diabetes education class, it gave me the answer to weight loss. I learned that if I avoid being hungry, I actually lose the weight. I eat a sensible breakfast, then have a PLANNED snack midmorning --- I usually have a sandwich for lunch, then another PLANNED snack in the middle of the afternoon. I could have a bedtime snack two hours after dinner, but I'm not usually hungry so I don't do it. I found the 100 calorie snack packs and lots of times that's my snack. And the candy companies have come out with small bars so that helps too. I keep low carb (Healthy Choice) frozen bars in my freezer. I've lost 32 pounds since September without being hungry. Donna

contents: High Protein low carbs Hi everyone, I am sooo with you regarding the weight loss. Since i've been taking Amitriptillyn i am having those cravings. Have you ever had it that while eating a healthy dinner you already think about what you gonna have for dessert? Well have you ever tried protein shakes? If they dont bother you or dont give you a flare you should try them. I am taking a high protein and low carb shake as a snack before lunch and it takes those cravings away. So it is really helping me to cut down on carbs. Because that is the biggest problem. We eat too many carbs i would imagine. For those who do a bit of exercise. Have a half shake before training and a half after. The protein helps you to burn the fat even quicker. They are Protein Shakes as a meal replacement as well but you gotta make sure that you buy one with nutritiens in it. And it does keep you full ...they are around 120 to 200 Calories per shake. So much less than you would normally eat on a lunch or dinner. But make sure that you have a good breakfast, lunch or dinner with veggies and only replace one meal with the shake. If you do that it is a good way to loose some pounds. Hope i was able to help you. I only started this a few days ago but i will keep you updated if i actually loose weight which i should. ANJA

signature: Healing thoughts Bea

contents: Well that's the thing: A lot of the meds that we get put on, and may really need to take, tend to lead to weight gain and make weight loss that much harder. The tricyclic antidepressants like elavil tend to be bad, and neurontin was a horror story for me, but there are others. And, obviously, it's a whole lot harder to exercise when you're in pain. So, absolutely, yes, keep at it! You will be able to do it. But if it's a little harder or it's taking longer than you'd like, don't beat yourself up about it. You can't control everything. Your best is all you can do. Your weight is not a representation of your worth as a human being!

contents: I was in the process of trying to loose the last 10-15 pounds of baby weight. I always gain a ton when I am pregnant. I was trying to eat five small meals a day. I recently found out that I have IC and I am going to have to cut out most of the foods I eat. I try to eat as healthy as I can which can sometimes be hard because their aren't a lot of things I can eat. I usually eat oatmeal with honey and cinnamon for breakfast. I cook chicken and veggies in olive oil and a little bit of salt. For dinner I eat chicken, shrimp and dishes with pesto. Olive oil and nuts are good fats to have in your diet. I know with IC you can't eat many nuts but I try to get some of the IC friendly nuts in my diet. For snacks I eat blueberries, pears, pear-blueberry bars. At night I have a small scoop of ice cream. I try to walk regularly. I know that sometimes irritates my IC and I will have to lie down later on. I am a yoga teacher. There are some days that I feel really bad and don't want to teach. However, I always do feel better after class. One of best thing you can do if you are feeling depressed is exercise. Exercise releases natural endorphins . I know sometimes its hard to get motivated when your feeling down. But I promise you that you will feel better. There are different types of yoga. I enjoy power yoga and it can burn about 400 calories an hour. Good luck!

contents: Well it certainly was not easy! I felt really good when I was walking but only if I did pilates first. So I've been doing Pilates and walking but the past month or so I haven't been exercising daily like I was because I've been fighting with strep throat. I ended up with two rounds of 10-day antibiotics and it's finally just now starting to go away. So I haven't been feeling too well! But I think Pilates was good for toning and the walking was good for the cardio. My blood pressure is perfect now (it was high when I was at my heaviest) and I just feel a lot better. I carry my weight differently though, not too many people thought I was that big of a size before! Where was I going with this? Ohhh...yes, pilates and walking! And I watch my calorie intake as well.

contents: Me too....I gained a TON of weight with this pregnancy. I lost 17 pounds in the beginning but then gained 64 pounds, lol. I've since lost 42. So I still have a ways to go...and it's not like I was totally skinny before getting pregnant.... So...yeah. I got a treadmill last week off Craigslist and have done it for 45-60 minutes each day. And I started SparkPeople.com which is an awesome site that I started losing weight on last year but abandoned it after I moved to Pittsburgh and started working 50-60 hour weeks. I've been on maternity leave since having my baby but I start back on Wednesday of next week. Really dreading it. Anyway, sparkpeople is an awesome website that is totally FREE and lets you track fitness goals, number of minutes doing cardio, strength training...etc, and lets you keep track of nutrients such as fat, cals, protein, even specific stuff like vitamins. My favorite part is that it allows you to view reports---which are these cool charts that show your weight loss over time on a chart, or your cal intake over time, or your fitness minutes at a time. They even let you set up a profile page similar to myspace. If you are interested definitely check it out! I still can't believe this site is free!!! Anyway, is anyone else on sparkpeople.com???

contents: I accidentally deleted you this morning. I have this problem where I don't like emails piling up and I went over board. I can't remember who you are either. I was doing so well with the diet until last night. Still have lost another pound by this morning. :woohoo: but last night I was so upset and mad (I live with two cavemen....need I say more? They think all women are S****) I ate a candy bar and cookie. Not good at all for my cholesterol but I always grab for junk food when I get upset. Only 3 candy bars left so the rest of the junk food I don't like but how will I calm myself down? Seems junk food calms the savage beast.

I find I do best if I plan my food for the day, including snacks between meals and at bed time. Don't try to deprive yourself of foods you love. I am finding that the 100 calorie snacks are a good alternative for me. I'm diabetic so I have to limit carbs. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: The problem I have is when I get upset I want to eat and eat and eat all junk food. With the little snack packs instead of eating just one little pack I would want the whole box. Plus I looked at the fat amount and they aren't on my diet. So far this morning I have eaten no sugar jello right up to my eyeballs. Some sugar free life savers (they are not being my life save at the moment) but I really want some cookies or even a giant spoon of peanut butter. I do plan my meals ahead of time too since I can only have so much of each group but I was wondering other then taping my mouth shut how to get through thesse times of "stress eating".

It really is hard not to try to comfort yourself with food when you are upset. My suggestion is to do like people who are trying to stop smoking. When the cravings hit, find something else to do. Go outside, work in the garden, play with the kitties, go for a nice walk, sweep the sidewalk, do some deep breathing to get past the "I gotta feed my face" feelings, write in a journal, flip through a magazine, get engrossed in a mystery novel, knit, work on a crossword puzzle or Sudoku Puzzle, etc. Find things that will keep your mind occupied and your hands busy. If you can't keep yourself away from high fat, sweet things, cut them into bite size pieces and freeze them. Take one piece out of the freezer, let it thaw, and satisfy your craving with just one bite. Eat it slowly savoring the taste as long as possible before having to swallow, then find something else to do. Another really good tip...Brush your teeth. The cleaner your mouth, the less cravings you will have. Brush your teeth every time a craving hits. Then enjoy the feeling that comes from a just cleaned mouth. It can really make you feel better. Really!!!!!

contents: find a sure cure for stopping snacking I was called FAAAAAAAAAT once again tonight. Minding my own business. had some what of a good day. I was called FAAAAAAAAAT because I wouldn't do what they wanted and because I eat JELLO! They eat bowls of ice-cream, peanut butter, cookies, candy and I have one little container of JELLO and I'm eating everything in the house SOOOOOOOOOOO....no more snacking and only eating what I have to so I can survive. They will not call me fat anymore! I did like your idea Sharon of freezing things. I had thrown some mini marshmallows in the freezer but won't be touching those now.

I was wondering other then taping my mouth shut how to get through these times of "stress eating". lol Mary, thats what I keep telling my self. I know how hard it is to loose weight. I would like at them and smile and say thank you, and say t your self you worthless b*****d or what ever you choose to call them!! its hard I know but it will be you own personal inside joke.

I have been thinking about the brushing my teeth way of doing it. Not sure I can afford all the expense that would go with it. After time I would have to buy everyone in the city a pair of sleeping masks because when I opened my mouth the gleam from the moon shooting off my teeth would wake everyone up. Then after a while they would send a mob after me and tie me up to the nearest light house to be the beacon. Of course that part wouldn't be so bad. I can crochet sitting in a rocker while smiling every 30 seconds. Bad part about that is after about a year I would need face cosmetic surgery due to the fact I would have a mouth that looked like the Jokers on Batman. Taping my mouth shut might be the easier and cheapest way to go.

Waterflow, I agree with Sharon about distracting yourself. I find that when I am craving a snack, grazing style, I want to INDULGE myself. I will not be satisfied with doing a chore or even playing with my dogs. Instead, I look for something luxurious to do. For me this can mean having a cup of herbal tea while taking a bubble bath and listening to my favorite music, or (yikes) shopping, or putting on a negligee and enjoying a wine glass of watered down blueberry juice in front of the fire, or pulling out my vintage jewelry collection and re-sorting it, or smelling my aromatherapy bottles by candlelight, or sitting on a faux-fur throw while slathering on my most decadent body lotion.... The trick is to distract myself with an activity that is out-of-the-ordinary enough to take my mind off of my craving while at the same time making me feel indulged and pampered. Sometimes I let it involve my sense of taste, but usually I try to work in activities that have nothing to do with eating or drinking and instead focus on other senses. Also, like you, generally if I eat anything when I'm feeling like that, there is never a time when it feels like enough because it is the activity of eating that is comforting, not the food itself. I love the teeth brushing idea! I am sad to hear that anyone is giving you a hard time about your weight; that's such a personal thing between you and your body. I can't understand why anyone else would think it should involve them except maybe in a sensitive, concerned way. This is a great place to look for answers; I hope you find one that will work for you. barb

Ok, been on the low fat diet again for a few days. Weighed myself this morning on my new digital scale so I know this is true. No more guessing with the arrow and zero but I have lost 1 pound!! :woohoo: I even had a bagel for mid afternoon snack. I was hungry so thought well, diet says I can have it. Just need to buy more no sugar jam/jelly. I can't believe it! Still have 60 pounds at least to go but hey 1 pound is a start! :woohoo: I will say I feel better and not out of breath anymore too. can roll over in bed at night and not feel like I can't breath. Sharon, I wonder if my love handle pound left yesterday from all the ironing? It just couldn't stand it.
A pound is a start. Hang in there. Hugs, Donna

contents: I swim a mile every MWF, sometimes more sometimes less. It depends when I get there or if the traffic is bad. My recommendations are making sure you use the bathroom before you get in the pool, immediately taking off your bathing suit when done, and to take a shower at the pool facility. If you can use one of the handicap stalls they have a shower head that you can detach from the wall to spray all over. If you have vulvodynia and chlorine bothers you, you can try vasoline over the area before you get in the pool and it will keep the chlorine off of you. Have fun swimming!

I swim at least 3 x a week. Sometimes I do water aerobics and sometimes I just do walking and stretching. I use the handicap stall so I can use the detachable shower head and rinse off. I didn't know about the vaseline I'll have to give it a try.

contents: That vaseline tip sounds awesome. Yes I do get irritated. Now my question. How do you get the vaseline off afterwards?

Hi everyone, I just read some other threads on running and it seems most people say stay away from it. My main problem was urgency and frequency. My symptoms have gotten so much better since I have started PT and am now taking Atarax and Amitryptaline. I used to have to go to the bathroom every 2 hours for sure and sometimes even sooner. Now I can go 3-5 hours during the day and have been sleeping through the night. I have been pretty strict with the diet, but am finding out that I might not be that food sensative. I think the coffee, caffeine, and stress was my biggest threat. With that said, I really have this desire to run. It's the one exercise that always used to make me feel good. I am really scared to start it though after reading everyones responses. For those of you who started running and ended up quitting because it bothered you, did it send you into a flare again? That is the last thing I want. Is there anyone out there that can run without having problems? What about bike riding? Erika

contents: Hi Erika! I run, about 3 days a week. Running makes me feel better and is my stress reliever. I didn't used to be able to run when I was first dxd. I'm not diet sensitive either, and I've gotten back to my normal lifestyle thanks for my dr and meds. When I have my bad days I wont run, but I ususally run around town for a few miles. I started doing sit ups too after running. (trying to get my stomach away) Looking good too! haha I'm recently single so I gotta whip myself back into shape. If your going to try running, start off slow or fast walking. See how your bladder takes it... If I do too much I pay for it! Major flare!!!! It's all about knowing what you can and cant handle. Its just trial and error. Hope it works out for you!!! Rach
signature: ***Rachel*** Dance like no one is watching Love like you've never been hurt Live today like it's your last Dxd with IC in June '06

contents: I used to run, every day. My IC symptoms actually started when I was running (training for a half-marathon). I gave it up over a year ago. That was so painful (emotionally) to give up running. I was starting to feel better after my first hydrodistention and I thought since I could do the ellipse machine for 30-60 minutes (depending on the day), I could try running again. When I run, I run slowly, but I would do intervals. I tried to do a slow steady jog and 1 mile into the run, I was in agony. I walked a bit, but I was still having trouble. That attempt set me up for weeks of misery. I cannot even walk for a while without trouble. Swimming is doable as long as I try not to go to fast and kick too hard. Backstroke is best for me, but I really like freestyle... but I tend to go to fast doing that. So... yeah... I was doing well and ended up in a flare due to the running. I honestly can't see myself running again. I will have faith that one day, maybe even years down the road, that I will be able to run again. I hope I can get the runner's high from swimming or other things that doesn't hurt my bladder too much.
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: I run and love it. It doesn't bother me at all. As a matter of fact, it helps. I don't run if I am flaring, though, because that will aggravate the flare. I also bike. I found a seat(I think it from a post here) called the spongy wonder. It's basically two circles with no middle to aggravate our tender areas. So now I feel so much better, because I am active again. As a matter of fact, I ran Chicago Marathon last Oct. and plan to do again this year. Good Luck. Nothing sucks like not being able to run on a beautiful day!

Running Hi there, I run 4 times a week and have no problem. As long as I am on the meds and things feel good I can run. If I was in a flare or off my meds I don't think I could run. The meds enable me to have a somewhat normal life and eating pattern too!!:woohoo:

Hi there! I discovered I had IC preparing for a half marathon as well. I thought it was just recurrent urine infections. I ran a half marathon in January and did okay. My half in February almost killed me before before we began because I couldn't find a toilet. After peeing, I felt better (thinking it was only a urine infection) and finished. In March, a had a cysto w/hydro distention to find out I really had IC and have felt depressed because I thought I couldn't do what I just discovered I liked! My urologist told me I could still run, but not during a flare. I found out he was right, but unfortunately, I'm having too many of them as I try to figure out what foods are triggering these episodes. I'm signed up for a sprint triathlon in July & August and have been slowly training at the gym. Thanks MARYMOVE for the spongy wonder seat idea! I did find a burning sensation lately and think that will help. Don't give up ladies! Like a well played game of chess, somehow, we can continue with what we like to do, even if for a shorter time!
signature: Symptoms: Come on faithfully after my period: urgent need to "push" and more frequent urination in the late evenings and once at night. Occasional pain after intercouse (if having a flare). Stress during a flare makes it worse. First IC Symptoms: Thought I had recurrent urine infections. ER doctor (12/07) knew something was wrong because I had a swollen bladder, BAD pain and no bacteria in the urine. I was having same symptoms as a urine infection, except that as the months have passed I don't have the burning sensation as I urinate. It's been replaced with the urge to push. Treatments: Jan.-Feb. 2008: - Antibiotics - Prescription strength Pyridium March 2008: - Cysto w/hydrodistension. Confirmed IC. Bladder (fully extended) could barely hold 750ml. - Elmiron, 100MG, 3x a day - 1/2 Darvocet, for pain as needed, although urologist hesitant to re-prescribe because he feels it won't help. - Iron Supplements About Me: - Teacher - Lost 100 lbs. right before being diagnosed - Run 2 Half Marathons (Jan & Feb. 2008)

contents: I don't run never was good at that. I do the gym with bike, free weights, and when I can water aerobic. I have gone when in a flare and felt better afterwards. I think I find it as a stress reliever.

contents: So, it seems like a lot of people can do the ellipse machine. I thought I'd try it and after a few minutes the left side started to hurt (where I feel my bladder pain). I went on for 15 minutes but had to stop. So I switched to the recumant bike and while my butt went numb and my left side pain stopped, my knees couldn't take it. So, I went to the stair climber. Ironically, running is the only thing (besides swimming) that doesn't hurt my knees. I couldn't do much cardio tonight. Just too much pain :'( I stopped before I had major repercussions. I have pain on my side and I will take a hot shower and go lie down with an ice pack until bed. So far, swimming is the only thing I can tolerate. I love to swim, but the pool at my gym was yet again packed and there were no lanes to swim in. I think I'll try going in the morning (but that is always when my bladder bothers me the most...). So yeah... ellipse machine = ouch recumbant bike = ouch (for my knees) stair master was ok for my bladder, but I could only do it for 2 minutes until my asthma and knees kicked me off the machine. I can't go down stairs with my bladder, but going up doesn't do as much damage. Sigh... I used to be so active and healthy. I mourn tonight for a life I once loved...

contents: With the elliptical machine, I have to: keep the resistance very low & not use a machine that moves my arms. Otherwise, I get a flare from it. I also had to start with just 5 minutes & work my way up... I wish I could swim, but the chlorine does such bad things to my skin & eyes, forget letting it anywhere near my bladder... Walking anyone?

contents: I hope your flare calms down! If anything, you can just float and relax in the pool... I am having more and more pain since I got home Oh man this sucks! I have been flaring since last week when I had to drive 4 hours to a city, drove all around that city for a day (with a fair amount of walking... apartment hunting), then drove back for 4 hours. I couldn't believe how bad my bladder was from that trip!!! I shouldn't have tried to exercise tonight considering I was coming off of a flare. I really am fighting tears now. This royally sucks! Pain isn't a big issue most of the time when I flare (urgency/frequency is more of a problem), but the pain tonight is just getting worse! The pain from that road trip was horrendous. I was driving back and the pain from my pelvis was radiating down to my groin and the top of my left leg. I don't think I'll push it and try to swim tomorrow morning, but maybe if I rest tomorrow I could try Saturday morning... I really am mourning for a life I once loved. I am having a really rough time since last weekend. I am really kicking myself for trying to exercise tonight.
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: Kadi... walking sends me into a flare too I was telling Ronda about a road trip that I took and with driving too much and walking too much... I was having horrible pain. I should have given my bladder more time from that flare before trying to exercise. The pain I have tonight is radiating down the top of my leg and I walk around limping. It sucks! I used to run 10 miles and now I can't even do 15 minutes on the ellipse machine! When I did the ellipse machine I had it on level 3 and resistance 3 (they both go up to 15). I didn't feel like I was overdoing it. My knees don't do well on the ellipse machine but they were not bothering me until I got on the bike. So... I thought without knee trouble on the ellipse machine I was doing alright intensity wise. I guess I can't do those machines right now...

contents: I just found this web site that has a ton of no fat recipes. I never knew there was such a thing. All I ever heard of was jello or pudding. Have printed up 4 recipes so far. Going to make this tomorrow morning and can have it as a mid-snack. Could top it with fat free cool whip too. Banana Mousse Ingredients: 2 tablespoons non-fat milk 4 teaspoons sugar 1 teaspoon vanilla One medium banana, cut in quarters 1 cup plain non-fat yogurt Eight, 1/4-inch banana slices Directions: Place milk, sugar, vanilla, and banana in blender. Process 15 seconds at high speed until smooth. Pour mixture into a small bowl; fold in yogurt. Chill. Spoon into four dessert dishes; garnish each with two banana slices just before serving. Recipe makes four 1/2-cup servings. Nutritional information per serving: Calories: 94 Total fat: 0g Cholesterol: <1mg Sodium: 47mg Last edited by SharonA; 05-02-2008, 01:24 AM. Reason: deleted website address that linked to sales sites
Sounds yummy!

Oh Mary...I understand. Ice Cream screams my name. I scream You scream We all scream Even Ice scream...

contents: One thing you might do is to take a very tiny piece, then follow it with a full glass of water. Then brush your teeth! When I was first diagnosed with diabetes I tried skipping all desserts. Now I have learned that I can have very small ones and it's improved my outlook a lot. And I'm still losing weight! Huuugs, Donna

contents: Hey Mary, I have the same problem with frozen pizzas. If there's one in the freezer it's always pounding on the door trying to get out...the whole thing, you know? and no one to share it with so I can and will eat it all! I have found some plain cheese ones that I can tolerate and I add my own fresh veggies on to them to make them really tasty, and if I cook the entire pizza, I will eat the entire pizza. So I have learned to cut the frozen pizza in half before cooking and put the other half back in the freezer. Kinda similar to what Donna said about not denying yourself everything...sometimes just a bite will do to satisfy the craving...of course half a mid-size frozen pizza is not just a bite , but I try to make it my "big" meal of the day and eat with a salad...you probably wouldn't want to eat half the cake but like Donna said, a bite or two, slowly enjoying every yummy taste of a piece of that cake, and then brushing your teeth or chewing a piece of peppermint gum will often satisfy the craving and also leave you feeling energized and refreshed. Best wishes, Bri

Oooh Donna - GREAT IDEA! I had read about the "brushing your teeth" trick somewhere in a health magazine as a trick to "trick" your brain to stave off hunger pangs when you arent able to eat right away or to get rid of a food craving. Thanks for reminding us about that! They also make those little finger toothbrush things that you can carry in your purse for just such occasions (also reminds me..I need to get those LOL) and they keep your teeth nice and clean and breath fresh! You come up with the best ideas ! Mary you are so funny! See - there is that wit and humor we all love! I so know what you're talking about there - my downfall is CANDY! In particular - Chewy Sweet tarts. I could sit there and eat all 5 at once that comes in the package and then I feel miserable because they are soooo sweet. And brownies....and ice cream...and Hostess Lemon Fruit Pies! *okay..now I'm drooling...* I told you earlier in another post that I never deprive myself of anything though, because then whatever I'm trying to do I will sabotage and never stick to. I do exactly what Donna said to do - have a VERY SMALL SLIVER (if you cant cut one small enough with a knife - use dental floss! ) about the width of a pencil *yes..that small*. Its about 100 calories at that width even with the icing on it. You can handle that I'm certain - and you're not depriving yourself of anything either. If its chocolate you crave, or even ice cream - I learned a very long time ago to buy the Dark Chocolate Dove individualy wrapped pieces (THIS IS OF COURSE, IF YOU CAN EVEN TOLERATE CHOCOLATE). They are verrryyyy rich and once piece satisfies me (and is better for me than milk chocolate) - and a serving is like 3 or 4 pieces, so I dont feel guilty having just one. Ice Cream your downfall? Welllll.... Edie's makes something called DIBBS or Dove makes individually wrapped little nuggets that come in all 3 flavors (choc, van, & strawberry) that each piece is 60 calories each - again covered in that delectable dark Dove chocolate! YUM! I get to satisfy both my ice cream and chocolate fix in one little 60 calorie bite and I dont feel guilty about it one bit! So - for those of us that absolutely cannot go without - here are some solutions. Of course, that doesnt mean the boxes of chocolate and ice cream wont be screaming and knocking on the freezer door (That was funny Briza LOL) but - at least you wont have so much guilt over it if you can control yourself. ALSO - there is another ice cream out there called Skinny Cow that is pretty good. I dont know the ingredients in it however so I dont know if it will flare anyone or not. FUDGSICLES with NO SUGAR ADDED are only 40 calories each and are WONDERFUL for satisfying a sweet tooth as is Sugar Free Jello with fat free whipped cream! (again - only if you can tolerate either of these). Hugs, Brittany

I tried that brushing your teeth trick and it didn't work with me. I can't have any real yummies now either. Have to try and get my coloesteral down fast. Did go shopping and found a new frozen treat. Went to Sam's club and they had new frozen jello smoothie things. Not jello pudding but jello jello. The wiggly stuff. It looked like it had vanilla ice-cream mixed in them. Wasn't to sure about buying them since there were like 30 in the box and most all of those kind of pops I had tried were awful tasting but they are yummy! Plus they are fat free, sugar free and only 14 calories so I can eat the whole box at once if I want to. :woohoo: Anything fat,sugar free and under 25 calories I can eat until I'm blue in the face. If I run out before I get back Sam's I can always naw on the box. has pictures of the pops. Better then nothing. Found a new ice-cream too. Lemon Merquian Pie. I hate that kind of pie but in vanilla ice-cream it is so yummy but of course I can't have anymore of that either. The only fat free ice-cream there is is plain vanilla, chocolate (not crazy about) and cappacino but that was awful tasting stuff. I'm not really a sweet eater but when nerves are going I'm a hog about them.
I have been thinking of trying a yoga class. Have no idea what I am getting my self into. Does anyone do yoga and is a IC friendly ?

contents: Originally posted by bigred I have been thinking of trying a yoga class. Have no idea what I am getting my self into. Does anyone do yoga and is a IC friendly ? Hi, Generally speaking, I found most forms of yoga to be IC friendly. However, i think there is a lot of bad yoga being taught out there, and my yoga teacher told me there is a new specialty to help people who get injured in yoga! It's popular, so teachers get whizzed through programs--just be careful not to allow a teacher to make you overdo it. I work with a yoga therapist. His job is to design a program for me that involves meditation and asanas. I only see him 1 or 2x a month, which is way cheaper than paying for classes. He also knows a ton about anatomy, so when I did things like strained my hamstring, he knew exactly what i should and shouldn't be doing. Google yoga therapy if you are interested. It was recommended by an IC doctor at one point.

Yoga I've got to tell ya, that even when I am in a flare & have pain & also the IC belly, I sill do my yoga program. It's not always easy to get through...it does pull on the stomach, but if you watch your breathing & do the poses gently, it is relaxing. I don't even take any pain meds, I just do yoga. I do the PM yoga. I also have the AM program, but I don't have a lot of time in the mornings with getting my daughter off to school. And that program is a little faster...they move different than in the PM program. Good luck to you & I hope you find something that works!! Alisha xoxo

contents: When I took yoga classes, I quietly talked to the instructor before class started & just explained briefly about IC & told her that if something bothered me, I'd just sit quietly. It worked out fine. The only poses that really bothered me were cobra or upward-facing-dog, so I'd sit those out. I now do yoga tapes at home & enjoy it very much, helps with stress & pelvic floor dysfunction.

contents: One thing I'm learning about IC is that everyone's body is different and everyone's case is different. However, one thing everyone seems to agree upon is that running irritates IC. I just started PT two weeks ago and I was heartbroken when my physical therapist told me I had to stop running. I love running, and it has actually been one thing that has brought me relief! After sitting in the office all day running is great relief, even when I'm having a flare. I could be feeling SO sick with my bladder but then I start running and it all goes away. Am I nuts? Is there anyone else out there that has experienced this? I'm thinking it might just be the whole distraction aspect of it, but I'm always amazed when I start out and the discomfort goes away. The fact that it brings me relief made my therapist second guess herself a little bit and now she has changed it to giving up running while I'm in therapy over the next 5 weeks. She basically said the muscles in my legs were too toned/tight and that we need to give them a chance to loosen up while I'm in PT. I think the bigger culprit was all the strength training I was doing for my legs. I immediately stopped doing the inductor/adductor machines since those seem to be a big no-no according to this site. I've stopped doing all strength training for my legs until therapy is over. I've also learned that ab exercises are terrible for my IC. I was taking a difficult mat-based Pilates class that now I realize was lined up perfectly with the start of some of my worst flares. I stopped that but continued with ab exercises and I'm finding that they really aggravate my symptoms. I suppose I'll have to find some sort of workaround or way to safely tone without making myself sick. Finally, I just want to add that hot yoga is amazing! I am so happy that's one thing I haven't had to give up. I love it and it's great because I get my mandatory stretching in for PT. The heat in the room also feels great on my muscles.

i think u should listen to ur physician in letting running for a while, until treatment is over, but i think u should try again afterwards, i think u can fight better when u feel better, i believe it is a marvellous thing to like sports, hope u feel better soon

No I can't run!!! (sobbing over here). I was training for my first half-marathon when my IC symptoms started (pain and horrendous urgency). Running is thus far the worst thing I can do to my bladder and I doubt I will ever try again (if I get better, why rock the boat right?). Since starting Lyrica (I didn't tolerate neurontin), I am doing better and lifting weights doesn't bother me (even some leg exercises and mild ab workout). I cannot do some movements, so I am a little restricted, but I have lifted weights the last 2 days and am doing just fine bladder wise! I am not normal, but to be active again is a miracle to me (it's been over year... I even had a handicap parking pass because walking would send me into a flare). Walking too much (say 20 minutes or something) will bother my bladder, but it doesn't send me into a 2 day flare anymore. It will irritate things for a few hours after and so I just drink my water and rest after walking too much. (I did about 4 hours of shopping the other day and wasn't in terrible shape... like I was when I tried Christmas shopping)! So... things do get better. We want an immediate improvement, but have patience and try meds until you find what works for you. The bladder combo I am on right now involves singulair, lyrica, and sanctura. It seems to be working alright for me! I hope the lyrica continues to calm down my bladder! I just upped the dose to 150 2x/day.

contents: Thanks to everyone for your helpful replies. @ omarito: Yes- it's been hard for my to listen to my physical therapist and stop all together but I really want to give PT a fair chance so I'm committing to everything she's told me to do. She did say that I can reintroduce it slowly toward the end of my treatment so I'm very optimistic about it. @Alyssa: I'm sorry to hear you've had to give up running too. I definitely feel like I'm in the minority since it doesn't bother me, but apparently it is affecting my pelvic floor muscles, which is why my therapist had me stop. Other things like certain foods and not drinking enough water are my major triggers. (Oh and abdominal excercises as I mentioned.) I'm really trying to take a holisitic approach and I'm not taking any medicines. My case isn't as severe as others (unless I'm having a flare and then I have bad pain/discomfort)- it's more of a nuisance so I'm trying these other lifestyle changes first. @ Donna: Thanks for your input as well. This certainly takes some getting used to, but it's great to have a resource like this forum to help educate me and learn from others' experiences.

I love to run. It's a great way to release natural pain relieving endorphins. Anybody that runs and reaches the 'runner's high' know how great that feeling is. I don't see how being tone in your legs makes a difference in PT. I can see 'what' you do to get tone can strain the bladder and it's surroundings. I wonder how long the effects of PT last, if they work, after treatment? You know the old saying 'exercise never killed anybody'. Best Amy

I try running a little, but it jars my bladder too much. I do walk for 40 minutes when I'm having a good day, and pray I don't have to go, but I'm usually getting pressure half way through so it's hard to find joy in it. I've been thinking of aquiring an eliptical (sp?) as it is low impact, and works the muscles, but I would hate to invest in something that I don't know would help or hurt so I think going to a gym and using one there a few times first would be the smart way to go. I think a treadmill would be easier too as I can always get off it and go potty when I need to because when your outside, and that gotta go comes on... My fitness level has gone down terribly since this disease really kicked off in January of 2006, and I have been trying to get the get up and go to start a regime, but it's not easy, but I know it would be beneficial. I suffer mostly from frequency, pressure/pain, urgency sometimes, and hesitancy. Good luck!!

I had to give up running since my IC diagnosis a year ago. My doctor told me to stop for a while to give my body a chance to heal. I knew running bothered my bladder but I felt it helped me in so many other ways I wasn't willing to stop. I miss it a lot and still have hope I'll be back running again in the future. For now I walk on my treadmill as often as I can. I do yoga too.
signature: 3/06 IC symptoms began immediately after endometrial ablation procedure called NovaSure 5/07 IC diagnosed by cystoscopy with hydrodistention finally after 14 months of doctor's appointments and pain Medications: Elmiron since 8/07, Atarax, Synthroid & Cytomel for low thyroid, Lisinopril for high blood pressure, Xanax for anxiety as needed, Vitamin D 50,000 units once a week Supplements: MSM/glucosamine, Quercetin, Desert Harvest Aloe Vera, Flora Biotic 16, Magnesium, Citracal, B-complex What's worked: IC diet, physical therapy for pelvic floor dysfunction, TENS, rescue instillations and acupuncture

contents: I can't run...it aggravates my IC. Any physical exercise that involves bouncing around fast, I cannot do. Also, I have asthma and the big boobies gene (OUCH). hehe
signature: Jen

contents: I run for 30 minutes every night. It hasnt seemed to bother me any

Running I also can run any distance without any bladder pain. Treadmills don't bother me either. The only time I cannot run is when I am in a flare, but then again I can't really walk then either but I guess I am lucky that on a day to day basis I am okay. This maybe due to the meds I take everyday that is making running doable (okay, is that a word??)..
I don't run, but I love to hike. Unfortunately I think my current flare was caused by my hiking. I won't stop hiking, but I will take things a bit slower.
signature: Cindy Myspace: http://www.myspace.com/lovmaui History: 2006 - IC symptoms within weeks after Hysterectomy 2007 - Informally diagnosed with IC 2007 - Remission for 8 months 2008 - Flaring, Mapping my triggers & Seeking a successful treatment 2008 - Since August - REMISSION, SWEET REMISSION!! 2010 - Still in REMISSION!! Evian is my best friend Tried: Proced - Quit after 2 days due to bad headaches AZO - Temporary releif of pressure & burning Marshmellow Root tea - Helps sooth burning..I couldn't survive a flare without it! Pumpkin Seed Oil - It helps sooth the bladder, but causes breast pain Aloe Juice - No improvement Flexeril - Only when my lower ab muscles & nerves get seriously bad ( It knocks me out. IC Diet - No solid results PROBIOTICS - No solid results EVIAN WATER - AWESOME!! A bottle a day keeps the IC away!!!

contents: i am an aerobic teacher and i have really been battling with this question. i used to run alot. and since i seem to have come out of remission, i am noticed that running ( thats my me time) caused sooo much pelvic pressure and it took 2 days, a hot bath and some seriouse palpatations to get it to release. i lost 40 pounds when i was in remission and now i deal with the fear that those pounds will come back on without running. i have stopped for now but i do elliptical and cycle ( both at the gym) and havent had that major flare again. it was such a bad flare that i'm scared to even attempt it. Has anyone ever heard of Chi Running? someone at the gym was telling me about it. i looked it up online but was right expensive.

Yeah, running is horrible on my bladder (probably one of the worst experiences my bladder can go through!). I don't think I will ever be able to run again. Since my IC diagnosis, I have gained 100 pounds. That was just over a year ago. Sickening, I know. Exercise is CRUCIAL for me and losing weight. Also, the IC diet royally sucks and that contributed as well. I can't even do ellipse machine or bike... but I am not in "remission", so hopefully one day, I can do cardio sufficiently. I was able to swim a bit though! Chi running... never heard of it!

contents: I LOVE to run and it actually seems to help my IC. I ran the Chicago Marathon in Oct. with no bad side effects,at least bladder wise. I will say one thing-I don't run when I am flaring. I just walk.Ever since going on Elavil, running is absolutely no problem!!
How about long walks semi fast paced walks instead? is that possible? (considering having vulvodyinia as well) anyone?

contents: I have been noticing after I eat a cookie, candy bar or any real junk food (including potato chips) I get very tired, sluggish and feel blah. Sometimes I also feel sick. Anyone else? Have had all blood work done so nothing there. The no sugar hard candy and normal suckers seem to work ok. Actually if I stick to the low fat/no fat diet I seem to feel better. I don't even get a sudden burst of energy from the junk food which you would think I would.

Hey, go with what works! If you feel better on the healthier diet - by all means stick with it. Who the heck wants IC AND heart disease or stroke or diabetes type 2? I figure one chronic illness is plenty for one lifetime, why add another one of my own making?

contents: I do have to agree, except I have a real bad sweet tooth.
Sweets make me feel very sluggish and tired, and I always feel better when I stay away from them, but I also have a really bad sweet tooth!

I have had to substantially decrease my intake of any kind of sugar since I was diagnosed with diabetes type 2 last fall. It's been a real learning process for me, but I have found that what I need to do is to be sure I don't try to eat a sugar snack when I am hungry. If I eat a snack of a healthy food, I actually have more energy. My body has to work too hard with sugary things. I can have an occasional cookie or piece of candy if it's with a healthy meal and doesn't increase my sugars too much. Donna

contents: I too have a sweet tooth, but my husband was dx with diabetes type 2 a few years ago so he had to change his diet quite a bit, so to make it easier on him, the whole family did too, (I felt that since they changed it for me when was dx with IC many years ago, I felt that we should do it for him as well. Never in my life time would I have thought I see him drink anything diet!)

Why do I have so much trouble with walking??? If I walk too far from my car, my bladder is angry the rest of the day. It is so frustrating! I see so many of you do walking for exercise. Is there anything else that people can do that doesn't aggrivate the bladder? I wish I could walk, but even 10-15 minutes of light walking is enough to set off my bladder. As a result I am so sedentary it is sickening. I miss exercise! I miss running so much! I long for it and fear that I will never be able to run again
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: me too! Yes, walking definitely irritates my bladder and IC. Just today I tried to walk for about ten minutes and right away started having pelvic pain. I have to admit that I wasn't a good exerciser pre-IC; but once you CAN'T do something, it really makes you realize how nice it was to have the choice. I haven't found any exercise or activity that is non-irritating, which is a real bummer. My husband now does most of his activities solo or else we just watch movies or TV. If I have to walk a lot for work, which as a salesperson is often, I wear a Thermacare heat pack in preparation for the pain (and yes Thermacare brand is better than any others IMO). Good luck to you and let me know if you find anything that works for you.

contents: I am hoping that doing weight lifting will be tolerated, but I KNOW that certain moves hurt or up the frequency/urgency dramatically. For example, using a cable pulley to work my triceps REALLY hurts. I was surprised. I mean, squats, lunges and other leg/pelvic type moves I'd expect to bother me... but tricep pull down? I had surgery 3 weeks ago for something else, so I am hoping that I will be able to get clearance to try weight lifting. I love weight lifting (not as much as running though ) and I will give that a shot first. I am just so shocked (and frustrated and depressed) at how little I can actually do without aggrivating my bladder. I mean, sitting around all day is the best thing for my bladder, but man, it's driving me nuts! I used to be so active and unstoppable. Now... I look forward to my couch and TV. This is so sad! contents: Walking has been one thing that I didn't have to give up. Now I have been uncomfortable and had to take pain meds, but when the weather cooperates I walk with my friend on the track around our recreation complex almost daily. Last summer we got up to 2 hours at a pretty fast rate. It was a great way for me to lose alot of my "winter" weight. We are just about to start it back, and I will probably just walk probably one time around and see how I am doing. But even back in my really bad days prior to my implant, walking was how I would deal with my flares, because when I was still the pain was on my mind more. As far as my bladder does though, it doesn't hurt when I walk, its more of an inner thigh thing for me. My doctor did give me cd that had yoga for IC patients. I have to dig around to see if I can find it, but prior to my implant I used that quite a bit too. The strange thing is, I can walk as exercise, but when I go shopping with my friend who is a "lets look at everything " type shopper, my bladder does get irritated, but that may be that I when I shop, I have what I want in mind and go get it. So that is probably a stress induced trigger for me. I will check to see what Dr. Ragi gave to me and let you know. It was more of something to use for relaxation, but it does help tone your muscles too. Hugs, Tracey

I think it depends on how long since you've been diagnosed and also how intense the exercise is. I remember in the beginning, any exercise would flare me. Now it is pretty much VERY intense exercise. But not always. I find using an elliptical machine at the gym is pretty good other than having the stop a time or two to go pee, but it is still some exercise. Also, the rowing ergometer hasn't bothered my bladder. Sometimes if I exercise too hard (doesn't matter what kind of exercise), I'll be in pain for many hours or days. If I am already flaring, even slightly, I skip the exercise and do it when I feel well. I am currently taking spinning classes. I wear these Pearl Izumi super thick padded shorts to deal with the vulvodynia pain and urethral pressure. I've had no problems with spinning. I also just got a mountain bike and that didn't cause me any issues either. I use the elliptical. I weighlift, and I take aerobics classes. I will say that an hour long step aerobics class is probably not something you want to do right now. The class I take only has 20 mins of that. The rest is abs, weight exercises, and stretching. good luck. I know how frustrated you are to be limited.

contents: I walked for the first time around my church recreation complex yesterday. We took it easy at first, then I thought I was doing okay, so we started power walking the 2nd time around. Each time is about 3/4 of a mile. By the time I was done, I could barely breathe. I completely forgot about my infected ears and sore throat since I was busy talking to my friend and trying to keep up with my dog. My body handled it pretty well though, which I was surprised. I won't walk again until I have my next dr appt with my ENT on Thursday. My throat has been so tight since I went walking. Before walking I was able to do low impact bench aerobics pretty well, but my group sort of got busy then I got very out of shape for even doing that. When I would try at home my bladder would get very angry. I am trying to find my yoga cd that my previous IC doctor gave me,but it's probably a lost cause. I am also so sick of gaining this weight and I really am not eating too much, I just am not getting the exercise. It's true what they say when you reach your 40s! If anyone knows of a yoga tape to try, please let me know. At least that may help with some stretching anyway. Hugs to everyone! Tracey
