contents: OK- I am like fighting this notion of my pelvic floor dysfunction and trying to find the "right" exercises (I have 22 non refundable training sessions.....that I haven't been able to use for over a year). Anyway....as a rule, what can people with pretty bad IC do- can you run, walk, swim, yoga, lift weights etc? My hubbby is getting impatient and I just want to get on with it. Right now I am doing myofascial work and dry needing with is expensive and damm painful and just wonder if my poor thighs will ever just shrink and be toned again...... Oh, and anyone who has done the IC yoga tape, does it have a lot of bending at the waist (I can't do that due to an old back surgery). Many thanks, Melanie
signature: I am blessed to have an Indiana Pouch now IC since childhood, finally diagnosed at 31 yo PFD/Pelvic Pain,SI joint and LBP Low Back Pain s/p spinal fusion at 19 with more nastiness in back Hydrodistension 2001, 2005, 2011 w capsacian, 2011 with botox/ super pubic tube Interstim trial and removel 2002,C-Section 2007 and 2010, Post Partum Depression, post partum hypertension, Idopathic hypothalmic amenorrhea Radical Cystectomy with Indiana Pouch 2/3/12--->multiple complications and revision of ureters in pouch due to a giant ball of scar tissue in 8/12, occasional pylo, L kidney small and scarred, low flow Hysterectomy 2/3/12 w one ovary removed Dry Eyes L radical nephrectomy 1//3/14 after a long "what if " the conversation, kidney working 4 %, path said full of absesses Still have issues, still take meds. I have worked some, been doing pilates, just got a post masters in nursing, learning to pace myself. Learning things I can't do, learning things that I need to take a LONG time to master and regain myself, but mostly just so happy to be bladder free.:

contents: Hi Melanie, When my IC was bad, I mostly walked for exercise on flat land or I'd try to find a route that only went uphill & the downhill wasn't steep. (Going down a steep slope seemed to bounce my bladder & triggered spasms). I also went to "gentle" yoga & meditation classes. Now that my IC has improved, I can walk, use the elliptical trainer & rowing machines at the gym, do some small handweights exercises. I can also do a few exercise videos - I like the walking one Jill sells in the ICN shop or a 10-minute yoga video (but it does mainly focus on Sun Salutations, which have a lot of bending.). Hang in there, you're right to search out some form of exercise. I let myself go & am having to work back up to fitness. BTW: We're neighbors! I live in the east bay

Apparently God thinks I'm a bad-ass" ~Author Unknown Source - Pinterest " Current treatments: -IC diet -Elavil 50mg at night -Continuous use birth control pills (4-5 periods/year) -Heparin/Marcaine/Sodium Bicarb home instills at night 3-4x per week, more often if needed -Pyridium if needed, -Pain medicine at bedtime daily, as needed during the day several times per week -Antibiotic when doing an instillation to prevent UTI -Colace & SmartFiber to treat chronic constipation from meds, Fleet enema as needed -Dye Free Benadryl 50 mg at bedtime -"Your Pace Yoga: Relieving Pelvic Pain" dvd, walking, treadmill at gym -Managing stress= VERY important! -Fur therapy: Hugging the cat!

contents: I basically avoid all exercise that involves my pelvic area . My stomach has no tone and I cannot even remember what it is like to suck my stomach in . I lift weights when my bladder is not flaring super bad . Any type of Phy. therapy involving the pelvic floor worsened my symptoms , but everyone is different . I also have hired trainers too. Leg presses , inner &outer thigh are ok with lighter weight and only on ok days.
Can anyone swim? I've started going once a week and so far have not noticed any flare ups related to it x
signature: Love Lauren xx Diagnosed with IC in January 2008. Taking no meds (unbelievably) - apart from the occasional water with baking soda. Controlling flares with the IC diet and so far doing very well

contents: I tried to swim but for me, I need to learn not to overdo it. I think my bladder will tolerate swimming as long as I can go at a slow pace. I tend to do sprints, until I am out of breath, then do backstroke at a slow pace. I think doing swimming slowly will be tolerated by my bladder. Some have issues with the chlorine, so swimmers beware!
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: I started swimming about 7 months ago. I also have fibro and it helped so much. It was the exercise I could without hurting. I use to be a runner, but the IC and past surgies stop that. Exercise does really help the body. I started off slow with the swiming, now I started a Zumba dance class twice a week that I love. I don't think I would have been able to the Zumba without building my endurance with swimming. I still swim at least once a week, and sometimes more. Now that I have added activity to my life, both my fibro and IC have been better. Aslo my head is better for it. The exercise has made the depression of having the fibro and IC so much better. It was a slow process, but so worth it. I never thought I would ever get my body strong again, because of the pain, but I proved myself wrong and I am trilled about it. Make sure you listen to your body no matter what you do. It takes so much longer than people without IC or Fibro, but it is not out of reach. My Zumba teacher website is www.zumbachick.com . She has Fibro and IC. She has given me such hope.. It wonderful to see peolpe beat thier Fibro and IC.:woohoo:

contents: has anyone tried the pilates dvd in the ICN shop? my stomach is HORRIFIC and I need to work on it but I'm nervous.

Sorry, I haven't tried it...hopefully others who have will chime in? The only exercises that I have tried that don't trigger my symptoms or make them worse are swimming, boogie boarding, and the recumbant cycle. The recumbant cycle doesn't do anything specifically for my tummy but it does give a good aerobic workout. Swimming and boogie boarding ( or kickboardng) do seem to give me a good aerobic workout in addition to toning my abs without causing any additional pain. Best wishes
I bought a yoga dvd to try -but that was a month ago and I still havent tried it-oops

I bought the one from the IC website, sorry to say, I would return it if I could. It was done many years ago and is so outdated. She shows you how to do an exercise and then you must pause the DVD and repeat the exercise, then you start it again for the next exercise and then pause the DVD again, etc. I found it to be very distracting to be pausing it all the time. I wouldn't buy it again. I would just go to Walmart or somewhere and buy a beginner pilates and use it. Sorry for the negative review, but that is what I think of it. Healing prayers, Robin

contents: Careful of Pilates if you have PFD. My PT told me to lay off. If you are up for it, The Classic Pilates Technique: The Complete Mat Workout Series is far and away the best CD I've used. You can do beginning, intermediate, or advanced, and the beginning is challenging. It's the real, original stuff. I have experimented with many CDs, yoga and otherwise, and find this one to be the very best. Now that my PT has told me to chill, I do yoga. My tummy is definitely flabbier, unfortunately!

I flare up when i do any kind of sit up or ab work out... I just stick to jogging and walking fast around town. (hills) I run 3 days a week and i'm starting to see a difference. YAY! good luck withthe ab workout.

contents: Most days I cannot stand or walk because of the pain but I have good days once in a while. My ? is does anyone on long term pain meds work out and do cardio? Is it bad to get the heart rate up while taking narcotic pain meds? I would imagine so because the cardio increases heart rate and narcotics slows you down. Right? I do not want to ask my Dr especially PM Dr because she might not think my IC is serious enough asking questions about working out. I have gained 50 lbs in 2 1/2 years from lack of movement because of IC pain and I want to lose the weight!!! I eat okay so I also think there is an undiagnosed thyroid issue but that is another whole story. I just want to feel good again. It is one thing to be in constant pain but to be heavy also just adds to the misery. Does anyone have info about cardio with pain meds? Thanks so much!
Ring your prescribing pharmacist---s/he will know better than any Dr what one should/shouldn't do while taking narcotic pain relievers. Wishing you well... Diana

contents: Ok, it was only 2 pounds of fat but 2 pounds is 2 pounds. :woohoo: Weighed myself this morning. Will have to see if I have been on the cymalta for 4 weeks now or not. Of course it has been on and off of it but maybe now my body is going back to normal? Haven't been dieting just moving more. Oh, that just made my day and I will try my hardest not to get back on the scales for at least 1 week. Will be hard since before the IC I would weigh myself everytime after peeing. Strange huh? I've been thinking the cymbalta is the "life saving" pill for me. Wish I had tried it long time ago. Proves I can't go by what it says for side effects or what people write (not here) on web sites saying what awful things happened to them. I'm just so happy today. Tired from all the stuff I did yesterday but happy at least. Need to get Easter cookies made today to take to Uro's tomorrow. Plus need some for here. I won't be munching on any but others will. Yipppeeee.....2 tiny pounds........:woohoo:

Good for you and you are right, 2 pounds IS 2 pounds. I keep saying I am going to try to move more, but it's very hard to do when you are tired all the time. Hopefully when I see the Dr. on the 27th he will be able to help me. Congrats again on your accomplishment. Tiny steps add up!!!

They do, they do! Mazel tov on your two pounds! Maybe soon you'll drop another two... that's the way it happens, right? My PCP says she wants to see slow, steady weight loss, not stunning changes. (I'm making up for lack of steadiness by being very, very slow, LOL, but it is coming off!)

contents: So this previous Friday, I started exercising again and without the exercise, my diet has plateaued and therefore I am not losing anymore weight. So, Friday was difficult, but I made it through 45min on the Elliptical (Precor) trainer and was sore Saturday. Then, Saturday, my husband and I went back to the gym on base and I did the elliptical for 30min, but after that added in some weights for my chest and laterals. Then, I made a horrible mistake. I did 50 abdominal crunches. Later yesterday evening, I began to FLARE horribly. It has taken two instillations yesterday and one today plus large amounts of pain medication and muscle relaxants to make this flare calm down. Also, I tested my urine and AGAIN, I have a UTI. Does anyone know what abdominal exercises are safe for ICer's? I really want to flatten my belly. It is the most unattractive part of being overweight on me...because my weight tends to congregate on my abs and not my legs and booty. Thank goodness I have sixteen days worth of 500mg Levaquin and after the first couple doses, I already feel better...along with the heparin and lidocaine instills. I just put myself on it and after 7 days I will re-dip my urine and make sure it's gone. When we go to the Cleveland Clinic on the 22nd, I am planning to ask about Macrobid as an everyday thing to suppress bladder infections since they make IC so much worse. Does anyone take Macrobid or Macrodantin everyday as a preventative measure? Blessings and thanks to everyone on this board! This is the best support an IC patient can get outside their husbands if they understand. I am lucky in that respect because Aaron really gets it and understands. He made a point to read about it and understand...and even learned to cath me so when it hurts beyond reason, he does it. Blessings Everyone! April
signature: Diagnosed in 2007, but had IC symptoms since 2002. My current regimen includes: home instillations of Marcaine, Heparin and Solu-Medrol plus vaginal valium suppositories nightly. I also use Tramadol for pain as needed.

contents: You might try the Hatha Yoga for IC Patients DVD, available in the ICN Shop. It has some core work, very very gentle, and I've done it a couple of times now without flaring. I too flare terribly once I start doing crunches or anything like that. Good luck, Honey. You're due for some good news now after all you've been going through!

contents: I walk. That's it! Sometimes, I might take a short bike ride around the block, can't do that very long though. I lost 14 pounds just from walking. Low impact, easy, and makes me feel better when I'm done. When I have really painful days, I make myself walk around my house until I feel better. The endorphins really make a huge difference in my mental and physical health. My physical therapist also recommended swimming and yoga. Don't have a pool handy and I've done yoga in the past. I should start doing it again, it helped a lot too.

signature: The combo of things that brought me out of remission were artificial sweetners, liquid vitamins with citric acid, tight pants, lower body workouts and stopping all my allergy meds cold turkey during height of allergy season. I wish I knew back then what I know now about IC First IC symptoms Spring of 2000; In-office cystoscopy 2002 -negative; Remission until 2007 (yeah!!); Negative PST spring of 2007; IC diagnosed via Cystoscopy and Hydrodistention 7/24/07; Another In-office cystoscopy 4/9/10 with biopsy showed inflammation Treatments for my IC/PFD: Elmiron 400 mg per day; walking, Zyrtec, Claritin, Singulair, Neurontin 100 mg per day; Ice packs and laying down; About to try Cytotec Tried and Failed: Heat (always makes me worse!), Heparin instills -too painful for my urethra; Atarax (interfered with my quality of sleep first time, second time dried my bladder out; Elavil makes me sick, lidocaine patches (just plain don't work), Cystoprotek (only tried a couple months but didn't seem to do much), Aloe Vera caps, pelvic floor therapy, trigger point work flared me worse, Lyrica (bad stomach pain) Other Diagnoses: PFD 2009; Fibromyalgia 2006, Osteopenia 2012, GERD 2006, Gastroparesis (delayed gastric emptying) Anxiety: Other medications: Ambien, Dexilant, Ibuprofren, Necon 1/35, Voltaren gel for tailbone BABY GIRL 10/28/08 *** BABY BOY 7/8/11

contents: Boxing with a heavy bag I have stressss lots of stressss. I work about 60 hours a week (or longer since I work at home and never seem to unplug). For Christmas I got what I asked for -- a heavy bag and boxing gloves. I put on my ipod headphones and beat that bag. I LOVE it. I shuffle with a tiny bounce and it doesn't bother my bladder (and EVERYTHING bothers MY bladder). I consider it the second aerobic type exercise I have found since elliptical that doesn't bother me. Granted it doesn't work the legs a lot but it totally works the back, the arms, and I am bobbing, weaving...and working out a LOT of stress! Grin. Here's a tip for the crunches, which I think would work: don't try to 'do' it all the first day. For instance, with my elliptical, when I am getting back into it I'll only do 3 minutes for several days until I don't feel strain (strain = upcoming flare). If I build up slowly over 30 days, I don't have a flare. I would bet that there is a possibility that if you did just a few crunches a day that you might be able to slowly build. That might sound slow but slow is better than none at all, right? Granted, I won't be trying it any time soon but the day when I am ready, that will be my approach. Lame as it sounds, I will do 1 crunch the first day and see what happens. Still grinning. Oh, what we have to put up with! I was watching Made on MTV and this high school girl was trying to lose weight to do ballet. The personal trainer said, "She's never learned to differentiate between good pain and bad pain." She was talking about in doing exercise. I thought about that -- how that trainer would react to one of us with our 'limitations' that sound so unbelievable. "What do you mean, everything bothers your bladder? Walking on the treadmill, riding a bike, and rollerblading -- those are not high impact....?" Oh well. It's probably why I don't go to a gym. Anyway, one other fun thing to do is to rent canoes and kayaks. You use a lot more muscles than you would expect, but I've never had a flare. In fact, I went on a night kayak tour one time to a bioluminescent lagoon. I was in so much pain driving out there to the nature preserve, going down a bumpy dirt road...I was so scared I was going to be in a kayak desparate for a bathroom. For once in my life, the pain melted away when I got out there. I think it was the motion of the water, the distractions (no, I'm not easily distracted from pain), maybe even the activity, but the urgency and pain were gone (thank goodness).

contents: Disbelief Charisse, It makes me crazy, just crazy (how people can give you that 'Oh sure' look). Perhaps my favorite is that I started with this crazy disease as a normal-sized person and the stress, pain, etc., led to major weight gain (before I figured out what I had and how to get a handle on it). Once I got fat, doctors just loved to say that perhaps if I lost weight my symptoms would go away. I would try to tell them that I got fat as a result of not being able to move w/out pain, eating from stress/pain....I could see the skeptism in their eyes and it irked me beyond belief!! Now, I actually have a GP whose wife has IC. He sure has changed his tune since she was diagnosed! But, back to my heavy bag -- I work out a lot of that stress and emotion on that thing! Grin. I hope you enjoy it as much as I do when you get one / get access to one!

contents: Hi to the other April Ann. That's cool. My mom thought it sounded well together and that's how my name came to be. I am so sorry that no exercise works well for you right now. I do encourage you not to give up though because I thought nothing would work for me and found that lower impact classes and such work for me. However, my workouts have come to a screeching HALT this week because I just realized that the last batch of Levaquin I had to take for a UTI caused a yeast infection which I am having symptoms of now and on top of that I think I have another UTI (at least from the at home test I did). We had to cath tonight and do a heparin and lidocaine instillation because I took my maximum dosage of Darvocet and Valium with no relief. Anyway, the positive side of this is I see my primary doctor tomorrow on base at the internal medicine clinic and hope and pray that he'll address BOTH of those issues. However, it's confusing because if I DO indeed have a bladder infection and need MORE antibiotics, then won't that just make the yeast infection worse. I guess my question is: Do I need to get the antibiotics if needed and TWO or THREE diflucan pills? One to take perhaps tomorrow and one to take once the antibiotics are over. Does anyone know? I have never had a yeast infection after Levaquin, but I figure it could be because I have been exercising and sometimes maybe staying in the sweaty clothes too long. Blessings to all, April
signature: Diagnosed in 2007, but had IC symptoms since 2002. My current regimen includes: home instillations of Marcaine, Heparin and Solu-Medrol plus vaginal valium suppositories nightly. I also use Tramadol for pain as needed.

contents: I don't think it's *what* you are doing that is a problem, but rather how *much* you are doing. You really need to start sllllooooow and move up in increments! Starting out full tilt is a sure way to sabotage your efforts. When you are feeling a bit better, start again, more slowly, meanwhile, try some stretching exercises. You might want to try some exercises with a stability ball, they help with that core stability...... Now...if only *I* can get started! It's so easy to *know* what to do, another to put it into practice. I personally, am afraid...because last year we got a treadmill and I was feeling great and pushed myself too hard and ended up with a terrible RA flare that lasted a long time. So-don't overdo it, sweetie!
signature: My dx: RA, Fibro, Interstitial Cystitis, Bipolar Type 2, Pelvic Floor Dysfunction, IBS My meds: MTX, Leukovorin, Humira, Prednisone for RA flares, Celebrex, Orphenedrine, Tramadol as needed, Wellbutrin, Lamictal, Lyrica, Trazodone, Nexium, Singulair, Atarax, Elmiron, Provigil, and Macrobid/Trimethoprim (rotate monthly). I also take a multivitamin, Magnesium, Calcium supplements, fiber supplements, probiotics, and vitamin D. Current treatments: bladder instillations Come play with me on Facebook!

contents: More on exercising thoughts Guys, This came to me by meeting someone at the gym where I was doing my own exercises, but my husband agreed to pay for me to meet with a personal trainer. I found one here who trains people at Wright-Patt and she knew what IC was and understood it. She was able to design an exercise program designed specifically for weight loss, toning while NOT putting extra stress on the abdominal/pelvic muscles. In the next few days if anyone is interested, I will post what she had to me to do on Saturday. Tomorrow morning at 5am is going to be the first time I do it on my own. So, we'll see how it goes. I know it works though because it made me sore...but sore in a GREAT way! Afterward, I felt so good because I had all those endorphins that were released. Let me know if anyone is interested in knowing what her program is for me. I know it would be different for everyone, but maybe it would help someone else also. Take Care, April

contents: Can running cause a flare? I run several times a week and had no adverse effects before, but last week I ran faster than usual on the treadmill and I have had a horrible flare since then. I never had a flare that lasted this long and I'm finding little relief,it's pretty much constant. I've been lying in bed with an ice pack since then and none of my tricks seem to work. I am not exercising to lose weight or to shape a part of my body- just to stay healthy (as healthy as I can be with IC) and I love walking, but it's not enough for me, so I was wondering if I should switch to something else like swimming. Does anyone know if swimming is OK?
April, I would love to know what your trainer says to do. I have been trying to work out, but I have to be very careful to not over-do it because it can make me flare. Thanks, Janice

I do alot of walking. As soon as it warms up I am going to be right out there walking. Walking has never been an issue with me. I can also do low impact aerobics. I love the bench kind. And like Jan said, just don't push it to hard. Work up to it. At first I would walk for about 15 minutes, but I worked up to about 2 hours. I have the best walking partner. We gab and pray during our walking so the time just flies by.
April, I'm interested, too. Can you please post your regimen? Thanks!
Walking is my only exercise.

Exercise Regimen Since so many have asked about this plan, I am attempting to post it. If you have any questions about what these exercises are, please message me and I will give you privately my phone number and we can talk about them. The MOST important thing is that you work at your own level. This may mean for some people that the five minutes of cardio is just walking at a fast pace or cycling. The important point to remember is not to overdo it and to not stop a lot between sets because this is how you keep your heart rate up. Here goes: Circuit Training Times per week=3 to 4 (allow at least one day of rest between workouts) Reps: 10-15 per set (weight chosen should fatigue muscles by the end of the set) Warm up and stretch before you begin, cool down and stretch again when you are finished with the entire workout. Weights used will vary according to exercise performed. **1 set of each exercise** 1. Lunges *to fatigue; other options: squats, side squats, step ups on aerobic step. 2. Two arm bent over dumbbell row= 10 lb weights, 15 times for a set (make sure back is straight, watch alignment in mirror). 3. Pushups to **fatigue; Other options: Full, on knees, or against wall (make sure your abs are held in and you are pushing your hips forward as to allow this to Work your entire body). 4. On mat: Dumbbell chest flies, followed by dumbbell chest press: 5-8 lbs. 5. On mat: two arm lying down triceps extension; other options kickbacks while standing or pushups with fingers touching (use 5 lb weights for the weight options). 6. Dumbbell Shoulder Press (standing); other options front and side raises or combination raises (weight=5lbs) 7. Dumbbell Bicep Curls; other option Hammer Curls (weights=8-10lbs) *After going through weight circuit above, do 5 minutes of cardio (can be walking at a fast pace around track, jogging, mixed with jumping jacks). Work extra hard here so your heart rate remains up for this portion of the workout. *After cardio, repeat above exercises: Weeks 1 & 2: only 2 “circuits” 15 repetitions per set (except where noted *to fatigue) Weeks 3 & 4: only 3 “circuits” with 12 repetitions (*except fatigue exercises) Weeks 5 & 6: only 4-5 “circuits” with ten repetitions (*except fatigue exercises) NOTES: Build up to a maximum of five circuits per workout, total!!! *Work abdominal and back muscles separately: Side bends, holding weight in right then left arm (10-12lbs): 10-15 repetitions, 1 set only Single leg squeeze or bicycle crunches: 5-10 repetitions per leg. For back stabilization which helps hold your abs in: (2 options) Option 1: Lay flat on back, with knees bent into chest as far as you can. Put fingertips under curve in low back and press down on fingertips= 5-10 times (DO NOT HOLD THIS IN PLACE) Option 2: Lay face down with arms beside body and elevate torso without hands upward for 10-12 repetitions.

contents: I find even walking starts a flare within an hour. I though I had found a great toning workout. It was ballet moves(I used to dance) that included wide squats and plie squats and various other non jarring stretches and moves. Within an hour -good old flare! Do squats cause problems too?? Do you think if I walk 5 minutes for a week, then 10 then 15 ect. it would help. I notice walking around work or back and forth across a store or parking lot does not bother me! I need to move! I am also limited due to chronic back pain in the shoulders and arms if I lift or use them too much. No weights for me or punching bags! Ouch!

Originally posted by loraleena I find even walking starts a flare within an hour. I though I had found a great toning workout. It was ballet moves(I used to dance) that included wide squats and plie squats and various other non jarring stretches and moves. Within an hour -good old flare! Do squats cause problems too?? I am also limited due to chronic back pain in the shoulders and arms if I lift or use them too much. No weights for me or punching bags! Ouch! Don't feel bad about that at all. At my last post on this thread, I was able to DO that plan I posted. But now, since starting the Cytotec...there's more pain, but apparently that is helping me (so everyone says give it a little more time). Anyway, my work out has regressed...and I am only dieting and keeping my weight steady.

contents: I don't need to lose weight, I just want to move! At 38 everything is sagging big time with gravity! I also feel better about myself when I can at least walk. Does anyone have suggestions?? What about using a recumbant bike or the Giselle?

Originally posted by loraleena I don't need to lose weight, I just want to move! At 38 everything is sagging big time with gravity! I also feel better about myself when I can at least walk. Does anyone have suggestions?? What about using a recumbant bike or the Giselle? I am a total yogahead. I really splurged and hired a yoga trainer to create a program that addresses my health issues, including my crappy knees from 10 years of dancing. Guess what? You don't need to sweat bullets 3x a week in the gym, doing yoga for 20 - 40 minutes (with meditation) every day keeps my weight down and better tone on me than I've ever been in my life. I used to do the typical NY gym thing and go to classes that made it impossible to move for two days afterwards. I never lost the extra 10. Now, with walking to and from the train about every other day, and yoga fairly consistently, I am to the weight I want, I east really good foods, and I've never looked better. If I could just feel better.... Truth is, my body is 100x better able to handle this than 10 years ago when I was a chocolate pounding schlub, so it has made a difference. I also believe yoga stimulates the metabolic and lymphatic system, so it's not just about burn burn burn.

I want to do yoga, but the last time I tried some simple modified for my back shoulder and arm issues, and was in pain for a week. I have a fibromyalgia diagnosis. Whenever I put a lot of weight on my arms, or hold my arms out in poses or lift or pull to much I really suffer. My legs are ok, but leg stuff triggers the bladder!!!! AaAAAH! I can feel great (my back) and then fold a lot of laundry and hurt badly! Quite a dilemma. I am sure it is a viscious cycle of deconditioning going on here as well.

I want to do yoga, but the last time I tried some simple modified for my back shoulder and arm issues, I was in pain for a week. I have a fibromyalgia diagnosis. Whenever I put a lot of weight on my arms, or hold my arms out in poses or lift or pull to much I really suffer. My legs are ok, but leg stuff triggers the bladder!!!! AaAAAH! I can feel great (my back) and then fold a lot of laundry and hurt badly! Quite a dilemma. I am sure it is a viscious cycle of deconditioning going on here as well.
Might be time to get a professional trainer. It's worth its weight in gold. Even serious breathing work is extremely healing (although I've been bad about keeping to my regimen this week!!)
What about the elliptical? Does that bother anyone? I have one, and would love to get some more use out of it but am not going to if it's going to cause me to flare.

contents: Sounds great, but not an option for a single mom barely making it as it is! I spend all my money on organic foods for me and my child and supplements I need already. Maybe someone in my family could give me that as a gift for holiday or birthday!
Does anyone know if recumbant bikes cause flares? These are great for my back issues, but don't know if I would flare.

Originally posted by loraleena Sounds great, but not an option for a single mom barely making it as it is! I spend all my money on organic foods for me and my child and supplements I need already. Maybe someone in my family could give me that as a gift for holiday or birthday! I hear you. I make very little freelancing, and have quite a bit on CCs from my illness. In my situation, the supplements were mostly an expensive waste, except for the magnesium, but everyone is different. As for the organics, I don't bother when they are imported from Mexico. Reading about it from Michael Pollan's perspective really changed my attitude about local/organic/etc. But I digress. A birthday present is a great idea. And you don't need to go hog wild. Hiring someone at $90/hour for three sessions with the idea that is all you got--believe me, no one is going to turn that away.

Just personally, I have never had any luck with Diflucan. I now always use the 3 day cream from the drug store. 1 day is not enough and burns anyway. The joy of yeast infections is that they can happen anytime, anywhere, usually at the worst possible time! I would go to your dr. to get officially diagnosed with the UTI, get antibiotics specific for it and buy 2 of the 3-day yeast products just in case. It's always good to have one on hand! Good luck! BTW you and your husband are a cute couple!

My doc says swimming would be the best form of exercise for me. Problem is, every time I try in just about any pool, the chlorine is too much for my bladder. He suggested coating the area with Vaseline and then trying to swim. Has anyone tried this? I absolutely love swimming and I want to find a way to do it more comfortably. I know voiding immediately after is important and to take off my suit right away, but even these things don't seem to help. Thanks in advance for the advice!

contents: The public pools normally have higher than normal chlorine because you know.....and I don't swim in them. We even joined our local community rec center and I don't swim in the pool even though I know it's clean and I can look at the chemical levels since they keep them posted , mainly because it's indoors and I never enjoyed swimming in an indoor pool. Guess too many years of it when I was a Navy brat taking lessons. The smell in the indoor pools just brings back memories. I didn't enjoy swimming lessons. Back then, I loved swimming in the local lakes. Those were the days! I have several friends with pools and most of them now either have the salt water filtration system or the system like I do which is Nature II which doesn't require alot of chlorine to keep the water clear. I have never had issues with chlorine in pools. I do stay away from hot tubs though. I also rinse off and "out" after I swim since it's better for my skin and hair. That may be why I never have issues with chlorine, except that before we started using our Nature II system, my IC was moderate. I hope you can find a way to enjoy swimming! I am so looking forward to it this year since I have my hair back and I don't have to worry about either keeping a hat on or just not getting my hair wet so that my bald spots wouldn't show. T83

When I'm in a flare up, it even hurts to walk the dog. So all I want to do is come home from work, take meds and go to sleep. I'm gaining weight and feel like I have really let myself go. Plz, tell me how you guys get enough strength to exercise during a flare-up? What do you do? Sorry guys but I'm venting. It's been a few months and I'm tired of being this sicky person. I want the old me back.

contents: If I'm flaring about all I can manage is minimal household chores and walking to our mailbox twice daily. It's about two city blocks long so it's a little walk. Donna

contents: Hi I'm new to this site and I'm pretty new to all this IC stuff. I wanted to see if there were others like me that have managed to keep up a pretty substantial fitness routine. I had to give up running for now (cause bad flares) so I'm really trying to focus on bodybuilding to keep my body hard and lean. I've been having really good results so I thought I'd share this since It doesn't seem to be too popular. I find that I don't flare from lifting, if anything the endorphin rush numbs the burn I feel. I don't really feel pain, just an annoying burn above the urethra. I do several sets of 8 to 15 reps at fairly heavy weights Example: Bicep curls: 10 reps at 17.5lbs Leg Press 10 reps at 180lbs Hamstring curl: 10 reps at 90lbs Tricep extension with both arms: 8 reps at 32.8 lbs Shoulder Press: 10 reps at 40lbs Bench Press on smith machine: 10-12 reps at 70 lbs Lat pulldown: 8 reps at 95 lbs I'm not big by any means. I'm 5' 3.75" and about 120 lbs. My dresses and pants are a 4 to 6 and my shirts are small to medium. Its impossible for women to get big like men due to hormonal differences. I also do low impact, but not low intensity cardio such as step (keep jumping to minimum), salsa, the elliptical, and walking at a high incline (no hands on bars). I also do stretchy/toning workouts like Yoga, pilates and Dancers Workout which is inspired by ballet.
signature: Dx with IC on 2/6/08. Symptoms really started in with a UTI 5/07 following an infection but then went away in July. I've had recurrent UTI's for the last 11 years, my first one within days of taking my first and last Depo-Provera shot. Symptoms returned in November '07 but were treated with antibiotics. Returned again in December, and in January after my GP noticed that some of my "UTI's" did not produce positive bacteria cultures, was referred to a urogyn. I have been dealing ever since occasionally having symptom-free weeks. Symptoms are an urgency that I can can differentiate from real urgency, and a burning sensation above and sometimes in the urethra. Tends to get worse right before and during my period and then subside for the week after. Running makes it worse. Sex doesn't make all that worse, but when I have the burn I don't want to. Meds: Elmiron to 100mg 2x a day, Vistaril 25g at night, Hyoscamine as needed for both bladder pain and IBS, Uristat as needed, Ovcon (Balziva) 35, Microbid after sex and as needed. Supplements: Glucosamine/MSM 1000mg 3x a day, Quercetin 500mg 2x a day, about 1 cup of Aloe Vera Gel or Juice a day, Prelief as needed, Calcium Citrate, Fish oil, Saw Palmetto (I notice more pain when I don't take it), Iron 3 times a week, Enzymatic Therapy Thyroid support formula, 7Keto, D-Mannose, L-Carnitine, Fiber tabs, a probiotic Sometimes: Cornsilk and Marshmallow Other treatments: Ice packs when needed, Heat pack nightly, periwashes with baking soda added 2x a day, a paraben-free vaginal lubricant when I'm feeling dry Other medical issues: Allergies, IBS, Very irregular menstrual cycle

contents: I haven't been to the gym in a couple weeks due to injuring my shoulder/rotator cuff and that nasty flu everyones been struck with...but I miss it. I have a trainer to kick my butt 2-3x week and I do cardio on my own. I love doing chest and back, legs are my least favorite (lunges... blech!) Altho I've been doing a lot of them lately to compensate for my shoulder being toast. I have to agree with you...My IC behaves really well when I'm working out, its like all those muscles in my pelvic area get warmed up and aren't so stiff. I have to work out...if I didn't I'd really be huge. The battle against the weight gain (from Elavil) is brutal.

contents: Well, I used to be really into this, but alas, all the pain it causes me tends to make me despise it now. Weightlifting does seem to be better than cardio exercise for me though flare wise. Intense cardio really upsets my bladder, even if it is no impact. I think because exercise stresses the body to some degree....I just don't know why. I do it anyway, unless I'm already in a flare, and then sit on ice packs when I get home.
signature: Sonja Dx: Idiopathic Dilated Cardiomyopathy/Congestive Heart Failure-1997, Vulvar Vestibulitis-2006, IC-Feb 2007, Atrophic Vaginitis 08, Heavy Long and Painful Periods leading to Partial Hysterectomy-2007, Rectal Fissure-2007, Pelvic Floor Dysfuntion-2008, Post-Menopausal-2010 Meds: Coreg, Elmiron, Elestrin, Est/Test Topical Gel, Valium Suppositories, Lyrica, Dyrenium to counteract edema from Lyrica, Pain Meds. IC Diet: Very diet sensitive, esp. to spices.

contents: Thanks for the replies! Are there any lifts you notice that do cause some problems? I've heard its best not to do the adductor/abductor thigh master machine, but I find those to be silly anyway. Lunges are much more effective for targeting that area, especially walking lunges. I also noticed a little discomfort from deep (past parallel) squats, so I cut those out. I keep hearing that ab work causes flares, but I'm not really noticing a difference from that in the way of pain/discomfort. Sometimes cardio kind of makes the burn worse, and I've heard its a reaction to the lactic acid buildup. If I feel that but I lower the resistance or the intensity down a notch or two.

contents: Ok, I have fallen back into my snacking binge AGAIN. Bought bottled flavored water. Seems to be the only way the bladder will accept water without a fight. The only thing is I noticed it has 100 calories per bottle. Now, I was thinking would the 100 calories in the water be better calories then 100 in cookies or candy? I could snack on the water instead of sweets or does it all work out the same in the end? The water would flush my system out. Was drinking snapple but that made me pee too much and made the pain worse. I'm starting to get fatter again. Can tell in my cloths.
Actually 100 calories is 100 calories, regardless of where you get them. You might try plain bottled spring water. Donna

signature: Sharon Shopping??? Did someone mention shopping? I'll get my hat... ;-) Where I can be found most days. contents: What you really need to look for is nutrition per calorie. Neither flavored water nor sweets will give you the nutrition that you need, they're really just wasted calories and they definitely won't fill you up. It's much better to snack on something that's sweet and that will provide you with some nutrition. Are you able to eat any fruits? If I'm feeling like snacking and I can resist the sweet stuff long enough to cut up an apple or peel a banana, I can overcome the urge for sweets and put some good nutrition into my body, which I really believe helps us feel better, both right away and in the long run. Raisins area also great, if you can tolerate them, really they taste like candy. I also find that the mini carrots are great, even though they're not very sweet, they take the edge off my cravings. Good luck! My clothes are also fitting too tightly, I need to take my own advice and avoid the sweets!

waterflow - try drinking watered down gatorade if your bladder can handle it, or better yet - there are a ton of "powders" you can add (again if you can handle it) to your water, that are less than 5 calories. Propel makes a fitness water that is 25 calories for the entire bottle as well. So if you absolutely have to have "flavored" water and it doesnt irriate your bladder - give it a try. Good luck

I did it again and went on a junk food all day eating spree. I am in sooooooooo much pain! Stupid, stupid me! Things pain wise were going much better. It was staying from 5-8 and now it's way above 10. I will have to remember that saying "A twinkie on the lips for 5 minutes is a life time on the hips" or something like that. Not really sure how it goes but when I crave another twinkie or rind ding (Oh, how I miss the old ring dings) I will have to think "pain pain pain for many many hours afterwards". Wonder what it is about sweets that kills the bladder and not too many fruits? Maybe the stuff to preserve it? Plus I've gained back 4 pounds. Boo---hiss,,,,,

Ouch! Ouch! Ouch! I went skiing for Girly Weekend last Friday...what a BLAST! I haven't skied in 18 years and did great! But from carrying my skis back to the rental counter I must have torn up my shoulder...(and I got blisters around my shins from boots--weird.) So per my PCP, off to physical therapy I'll have to go....and I got a script for Naproxen, do we like this drug?....anybody have some good/bad experiences with this?....will it help or possibly aggravate the whole bladder situation? Thanks! y.

contents: Hi Kadi, I have an elliptical trainer in my home gym. It is a SportsArt E82. SportsArt recently changed their model numbers around, but my unit is equivalent to their current E830 product. Here is a link to their product line: http://www.sportsartamerica.com/saf/residential/residential_ellipticals.asp I also have extensive experience with the SportsArt 803P, which is equivalent to the E83 product in their current model numbering scheme. This unit is less expensive and would be a good model. I purchased my E82 from a company in TX and had it shipped directly to my home here in Pleasanton. I saved about $1,000 doing this. Typically these units are not stocked by the fitness companies that sell them and they are just shipped directly from SportsArt in WA. It usually takes about a week to 10 days to receive the unit after you place the order. The local SportsArt dealer here in the San Francisco Bay Area is All American Fitness. They have provided a number of items in my gym, including my True Treadmill. They have an OK service department if something happens to your unit. Their service manager, Jose, can often resolve a problem over the phone. Here is a link to their store, it is a good place to go and look at equipment. All American Fitness http://www.allamericanfitness.com/ Bay Area gym design & install: hotels, schools, offices. We deliver, setup & service all equipment. Buy, sell, rent: SF to San Jose. Visit our showroom! One of the really great features on SportsArt units is that they have an adjustable stride length. This means that you can adjust the stride length from 17” to 29”. This is a really great way to take the stress out of using an elliptical. Essentially, you can use a shorter, easier stride when you are starting out that put less stress on the pelvic area. Also, it allows you to adjust the unit to your natural stride/height. This is especially important if you are shorter because you won’t get stuck with longer stride than you want. As you move up through the model range, you get extra features. For example, the E83 has a manually adjusted stride length, while the E830 has electronic adjustable stride length that can be changed at any time during your workout. Other enhancements include heart rate monitor, heart rate control of workout, upgraded console and number of pre-programmed workouts. It has been my experience that I don’t adjust stride length frequently. This means that the manually adjustable stride length on the lower priced models in the range should be just fine. I also don’t use any of the cardio controlled workout features on my E82 …although I do monitor my heart rate when working out on the console. But, an entry model and a heart rate monitor watch, like a Polar, would work fine also. What I am trying to say is that you will still get the same high quality workout on the models with fewer features. It has been my experience that the E83 has a super smooth stride and you are not really giving up anything going with one of the more basic SportsArts like the current E83. The most important thing to have is a TV (hopefully with DVR or DVD player) in front of the elliptical. This is much more important than a sophisticated console on the elliptical. Just start up your favorite programs and do your workout. One feature on the new SportsArts that isn’t on my unit is the flex feature in the foot rests. I am very interested in this feature because my ankles are the weakest point when working out on an elliptical. I hope to upgrade my E82 with this feature. Elliptical workouts are very easy and one danger is to rely on the elliptical to heavily. It is better to have a diversified workout strategy. The elliptical can be the core of a program, but give yourself a rest from elliptical workouts by switching to walking, weights, ABS, or other activities. This will help prevent any elliptical over use injuries. In terms of IC, I do not find that the elliptical causes a flare. My recommendation is to warm up with a pelvic stretching floor routine before you start your elliptical workout. One advantage of having an elliptical in your home is that you can take as many bathroom breaks as you want. Sometimes this will be none … other times it may be more. One other concern that I have when talking about flares and exercise is the complication of lactic acid. When starting a new exercise routine it is possible for your muscles to produce high levels of lactic acid which is removed from your system in your urine. When this occurs it can feel like burning in your bladder. It can feel like a flare, but is not. This is easy to spot because it will happen after your workout and continue for hours. As your conditioning improves, this will disappear. Lower your workout intensity to reduce this effect as you are improving your fitness. Finally, when you look at ellipticals you will notice that they can get expensive. I have not had a lot of experience with the less expensive units (Costco, Sears, etc.). Sometimes you can find almost new equipment for sale on craigslist.com, eBay or in the paper. When evaluating ellipticals check the nuances of the stride. For example, before selecting SportsArt I also spent a lot of time looking at Precor, Life Fitness, and others. Small details like how the Precor switches direction in the stride are worth thinking about carefully. I’ve been giving myself a break from reading about IC and it has been a while since I have been here at ICN. I hope everything is going well for you this school year. Best wishes, Bill.

contents: we just bought our elliptical 3 weeks ago,and i LOVE it!!! i've always wanted one,when i went to the gym i stayed on the elliptical the longest.we bought it at walmart,its a ***** *** one.it was pretty cheap and its a lot harder to use than the more expensive ones that we've been on.i used to do a good half hour at the gym,on mine,i'm lucky if i can go a full 5 minutes,lol!!!lets just say i'm working my way up.we are moving into our new home in march and we decided we will sell this one and get a better one,we thought it didn't matter which one you buy,but it really does,i still love doing it though.its in my room and i do it everyday during days of our lives(or atleast the first 5 minutes of days,lol).so far it has not bothered my ic at all.jamie

LOVE My elliptical machine This is the only real aerobic exercise I can take! The IMPORTANT thing is in building up. When I have not been doing it for a while I will only go 3 minutes, otherwise my bladder flares. If I do this fo a few days and stop being shaky, I can then do 4 and 5 and within 30 days, I am easily doing 30-40 minutes without a flare! I LOVE IT! I went to Sports Authority originally to see if I could handle it. They had a Horizon Fitness CSE 3.5 which I liked. So, I went on Ebay and Horizon has a refurb store! I got it for $399 with free shipping. Now, they don't have that model any more as it's been a few years, but I just checked and there are ellipticals from Horizon for $399-899 with free shipping. Just do a search for Horizon Elliptical. I think it's a great brand because I have worked mine hard in the garage in the last 2 years and it is still pristine!

I joined a gym a month ago and have gone some but not as much as I thnik I should. My IC flarred up really bad. I had to have a hydrodistinson on Fril I don't know how long I should wait before I go back to the gym since my favorite spot there is the pool. I also am waiting on the results of an Echocardiogram. My pulse rate is over 100 most of the time. Hope to be back at least by the first part of January. Anyone got advice on how to loose weight with the IC diet and exercise without pain. HELP !!!!!! Depression is trying to get the best of me.

contents: Dropping the sugar, adding more vegetables, less carbs and adding a soluble (not insoluble) fiber to my diet did the trick for me! I tend to yoyo but this has been the winning combo! I first experienced a reasonable weight loss and now have maintained a consistent weight for the past year. I also do as much cardio as I can tolerate twice a week and walk almost daily. Keep trying, you'll find what works for you!
signature: Karen

contents: You could try just plain walking every day, even if it's just for fifteen minutes. In my diabetes education class they suggested I wear a step counter so I can be sure I get enough activity every day. Donna

contents: The Elliptical trainers are really easy on your joints and don't seem to set off flares with me. I have done this twice now after starting back to working out since I haven't in over a year. The one I used on base at Wright-Patt is a Precor. I set the settings on Level 1, because elevation seems to hurt my pelvic area. Stay away from abdominal crunches. I did 50 yesterday and that was BAD. We have been through four instillations, pain medication, muscle relaxers and almost considered the ER since the pain was SO bad last night. The UTI I have is contributing also, but I only found out about it last night when I dipped my urine. Anyway, luckily I have 16 500mg Levaquin tablets so I didn't have to go to the ER to get antibiotics. Thank Goodness. Also, I am adding in light weights...no heavier than 5 lbs. for chest, arms and legs. No more ab work though until I determine if that is best for me or not. Good Luck with finding the right Elliptical. When I taught aerobics before my onset of these medical problems, I learned that the Elliptical is like a "natural runner" and therefore does not cause so much weight (bad weight) on the joints. Blessings!
signature: Diagnosed in 2007, but had IC symptoms since 2002. My current regimen includes: home instillations of Marcaine, Heparin and Solu-Medrol plus vaginal valium suppositories nightly. I also use Tramadol for pain as needed.

contents: Don't feel depressed, Big Red. How about yoga. I bought a beginner yoga tape and try to do a little more each day. I used to swim, but the chlorine aggravated my IC big time. Light weights are excellent, as muscle burns more calories!!! Good luck, and just keep chugging along!
Big Red, you might be interested in my thread in this forum, on the ergometer.

contents: Good luck! I hope all turns out well for you. I have a lot of heart disease in my family and I know what you're going through. Please take care of yourself and don't let depression bring you down. I walk every day and try to eat a heart healthy diet. (lots of blueberries and strawberries) It it's red or purple, it's good for your heart! (well, healthy red and purple foods, too bad candy doesn't count!) Watch out for veges like potatoes, corn, and carrots. They are high in sugars which turn into fat. They also aggrivate my IC. Take care! Good luck!

contents: Walking was the best trick for me, just getting my metabolism up since I am over 40 has made a huge difference. I can swim too during the summer. We put a very low chlorine system in our pool so it doesn't bother me at all. We are converting to a salt water system not this summer but next. Last summer I would just get out there with a noodle and do exercises in the water too, plus it relaxed my muscles. Sounds like you are doing great with your weight already though! Good job! T83
Im kind of confused. Do you all just workout even if you feel your bladders and have to hold pee in the whole time?

I, too, flare from exercise, but I have found ice packs to be quite soothing. I sit on one and put one on the front of my bladder when I get home from the gym. However, your medical history concerns me regarding your CHF. I also suffer from this due to dilated cardiomyopathy, and I got to tell you, sister, they will want you to exercise, but you must know your limits. If you start feeling like you are drowning, you must stop. Not permanently, but stop right away and sit down. I know from experience, that the best treatment for me for CHF has been reducing sodium intake. I never add salt to food, and I also do not eat processed food. This is the sodium killer. My doctor recommended that I never intake more than 1500 mg of sodium per day. Well, usually, I don't consume more than 1000 mg per day, and I've still had some days when I'm just so full of water. Has your doctor limited your fluid intake?

contents: I love horseback riding but in the past 2 years i have had issue's doing it.I normally ride saddlebred or walking horses.I ride english style if that makes a differnce.It seems like everytime I ride I flare horribly.Does that mean I want be able to ride ever again? I love to ride I could spend weeks riding and be happy.Is there anything i can do to help me after i ride.

Maybe if you try a muscle relaxer afterwards or before? and use a cushion (one of those which are sold here in teh icn) If you have a sensitive urethra, keep in mind you are putting pressure on it by riding and woulnd't recomed it until things calm down. I used to ride horses when I was a child and I have nice memories from my grandfather's ranch. I don't know about the stiles you mentioned, is it seating on the side? that's probbaly better that the ussual riding. Dont' think you'll never be able to ride again. You don't know that. Keep the faith you may be able to ride again.

contents: Thanks for the help.I am thinking I wont be able to ride or as much anymore.I just love it the best stress relief ever.Here is a website on english riding. http://horses.about.com/od/horsesportsexplained/p/engwestdiff.htm

I'm into kundalini Yoga now, and probbably I would have never known about it since before I was more into going to the Gyn, aerobics, lifting small weights, etc. So I'm grateful I found it. I go to class when I can or do the DVDs at home. Yoga can still be a bit challenging for us but you can control what excersices to do and when to stop. It helps with depression and to find inner balance. The mind and body connection, which is so important. Breathing excercises and relaxation. All that helps the body heal. Its is an option. I recomend you a book: The 8 Human Talents. It's been a great help for me and I hope it can help someone else. Good luck.

contents: I love to ride, but it does bother me at times. I take a zanax after riding, it stops the spasms. its from sitting so straight on my bladder I think. I didn't ride much this summer only twice. But I probably will always keep my horse, just having her gives me comfort and the smell of her when I am brushing her is a natural relaxant to me. For those of you who don't love horses you probably will think I'm crazy. I have had times over the past 10 years where riding doesn't bother me at all. So you also might have those times. I hope so! Kelly

I also have a walking horse. I know exactly how you feel. I love to go trail riding but always get nervous that I will have a flare and I normally cant ride for over an hour. I have also rode english but I have found that a western saddle seems to cause less discomfort and I also found gel coushions that are shaped to the seat of the western saddle. They are fairly cheap and are usually around $25. Dont give up I know how you feel. There is just a feeling you get when you ride a horse. I also came up with a system that seemed to help me be able to ride a little longer. I would saddle him up and before I get on I would go pee. Some days I would only get to ride for 15 to 20 minutes but other days I could ride for over an hour. If you start to hurt or feel like you have to pee stop. Even if you ride only for a short period of time its definatly still worth it. You will have good and bad days but dont let IC take away something you love. If you ever need anything,have any questions or just want to talk about riding feel free to pm me. Also if you decide you want to try a gel cushion let me know and I will message you the website where I got mine.

contents: Well, blew the low fat diet big time today BUT tomorrow I restart it. Plus I do feel better without the sweets bladder wise and sluggish wise. I've been off the celexa for 1 week and my nerves just can't take my family anymore. My mind has turned into blubber brains. I had stopped it because it makes the bladder so much worse but I can't live with blubber brains. Will need to keep the vicodin too because the extra bladder pain from the celexa. You know there is just no winning for me ever! I eat when I don't know what else to do and I'm nervous. Plus went for groceries today and just about crawled out of my skin. Think I get panic attacks.

I'm just wondering if anyone goes bike riding. My brother has gotten into it big time and he's trying to get the whole family together to ride next spring for a diabetes benefit. I've been hesitant because I'm not sure how that would go for my bladder. He's even offering to buy me an upgraded seat. Just wondering if anyone else rides and if there is anything you do to help make it easier on your bladder.

contents: What type of upgraded seat did your brother mention? I think there are seats that are 2-sided, with a separate padded area for each butt cheek, so to speak. I think they're recommended for men because the traditional seat causes pressure in the wrong place and can lead to problems. If there is a seat that's comfortable and has an empty "space" in the middle, all of your weight would be on your butt and not on your urethra, etc.

Originally posted by pingpong What type of upgraded seat did your brother mention? I think there are seats that are 2-sided, with a separate padded area for each butt cheek, so to speak. I think they're recommended for men because the traditional seat causes pressure in the wrong place and can lead to problems. If there is a seat that's comfortable and has an empty "space" in the middle, all of your weight would be on your butt and not on your urethra, etc. I bought the spongy wonder, changed my life. I wrote about it here before. I have been an avid biker--up to 20 miles a day for transportation at times, and lots of charity rides. I was fairly miserable, even before IC, until spongy wonder. The guy built it for people with vulvodynia and the like. Best money I ever spent on anything for my problem!

They make bike seats specifically for women. They have the space in between like the men's seats but it is smaller and the seat is wider for our butts because we sit differently then men. I also have this great gel seat cover that my mom bought me from Marshall's. (of all places) You can get them at any bike/sporting goods store. Excellent! I use it on top of my ladies seat and it works pretty well. I can't ride my bike too long though because it makes me want to pee! Working on it! I love mt. biking and kayaking so I'm trying to get back into them. Good luck! Oh, they also make great padded pants with gel bottoms for men and women. Very great for long trips on bumpy roads and off-road. They don't make your butt look big either! Go to a specialty bike store, mainly one for cross-country and street biking, they usually have that stuff.

contents: Thanks for all your input. My brother sent me some information as well. The ride he'd like us all to do is in June. I'm not sure if I'll be up for it by then. One lap around the track is 2.5 miles, which sounds like quite a bit considering I haven't ridden my bike in about seven years!
signature: ~ Stacey

contents: A new vocabulary word for me and a new exercise: My husband bought me an ergometer, which turns out to be a stationary cycle that you pedal with your arms. So, now I know this new term, and now I have less excuse than ever to lie around and be a bum. Everything I tried that involved my lower body in any way ended up giving me either pelvic or back pain, or both. I had been dieting pretty aggressively since July and not lost an ounce. In early December we made a couple of medication changes, and now I've added the bike, and have lost over ten pounds since the beginning of December. Still have a long way to go, but I'm encouraged. My husband says the particular one we got is the "magnetic resistance" type, and it has a really smooth feel to it, and a very wide range of resistance levels. It's a table top model and cost around $90.00, just in case anyone else is interested.

contents: I started the no fat/low fat diet on Halloween and I have lost 9 pounds so far!!:woohoo: :woohoo: Finally! Started ready fat gram amounts of food and I never realized how much fat there was in them. Just one small peanut M&M's bag has 4 or 5 grams and that is a whole days worth. I do think this diet does help some with the bladder too. Don't know how to explain how it does but it does. Another plus. Hope when I get weighed next month at the doc's his scale will say I've lost too. One bad thing I did today was I ate 2 cookies I made for taking to doc's next week. Just kept calling me so I fianlly gave in and I can say my body doesn't want sweets anymore. Not feeling very good and don't want another one. Yippee!! I made it through the sweets craving. No more of those cravings at night either. I'm hoping by spring to fit back into some of my dresses. I can fit into 2 of them but the weather has been so cold to wear them. This is just really great for me.
Congratulations on your loss. 9 pounds is a nice start. I hope you continue lssing weight in the safest and most healthy way possible. ~ Heather

contents: :woohoo:

update Thanks everyone! I've lost 12 pounds now. Slowly but surely I'm getting it off. Bad part is all my sweat pants are baggy on me and I have to keep pulling them up. I only wear them around the house never to town which is good. Wouldn't want to do any "mooning episodes" in town. The next smaller size is to tight on the stomach. Haven't done any real exercising either. BBB--I left mine behind in the store too but they put it in lost and found section. So far no takers.

My wife is passionate horseback rider for many years, and recently an IC patient. Is it OK that she continue with her hobby, or should she stop because riding can cause even more problems. Who can give me correct answer that I can relly on?

I would think a doctor would be the only answer you could definately rely on. We don't know your wife, her medical history, etc. Only a doctor that really knows her could answer that definitavely. That being said, I think it's great that you came on this site to find answers for you wife. I wish a lot more guys would do what you just did! I'm sure she would appreciate it! Sandy

contents: I don't ride anymore because I was out of riding for a long time before my IC came out. But I think if someone had already been riding they would be in much better shape and it may actually be good for them to get the exercise. I know once you stop exercising it is so hard to start again, it's best to leave well enough alone if possible. Also, definitely talking to a doctor is good.
signature: http://www.TheCraftyEwe.etsy.com

contents: Hi and welcome, My parents own a horse farm and I have actually talked to my doc about riding a horse & riding a bike, since I love both activities. She said to try slowly and see how things go. I ride English and take it very easy cantering. Some days my bladder is achy and some days it's not. I agree that your wife should talke to her doc. My doctor feels that quality of life is part of the equation, and always tells me to start things SLOWLY and see how I feel. Good Luck!

Originally posted by worried husband My wife is passionate horseback rider for many years, and recently an IC patient. Is it OK that she continue with her hobby, or should she stop because riding can cause even more problems. Who can give me correct answer that I can relly on? I have ridden daily for thirty-two years. I have had IC for the last 9 or so years. There are some days when I cannot because my IC is really bad. For the most part, it is the only thing I can do to forget the pain. Keep supporting her because it will help her to not be depressed from dealing with this disease! It was great when my horse was in training so I only had to go when I felt good. Otherwise, I try to turn my horse out if I don't feel good. I asked two of my urologists if they thought I should ride. Boy of them said 'yes" if that is what helps me with my well-being. There have been times over the last ten years where I did not ride for three to six months because of several surgeries I had. However, that did not make any different in how my IC felt. I did not improve or get worse -Vicki

contents: I was diagnosed as having IC with a PST test however a hydro with distention showed nothing. However I do have some bladder pain issues but it has not bothered me to continue riding. There was only once in the past six months that it was too painful to ride. Also my horse (which is for sale) is a real project and not easy to ride so I'm sure she will be able to continue riding in the future.
is your wife able to ride?
I want to do Pilates, but a bit scared it might cause me to flare up. has anyone tried Pilates?
signature: Formerly lorenab

contents: I haven't tried pilates, but I have tried yoga and currently take a spin class. Exercise seems to make me feel better not worse.

i did Pilates for a year before I had IC symptoms. It is the best overall workout and tones EVERY muscle in your body. I felt so strong and fit. I want to start up again and told my URO that and he cautioned me saying that Pilates will tighten things up down there too much. He advised Pelvic Floor Therapy first and once I get my muscles relaxed, maybe then i can venture back to my awesome Pilates. It is a hard workout and should be done with a trainer who is certified. It is very easy to do the poses and exercises wrong and you need someone who knows how to correct you so you do it right. GREAT exercise and this is coming from someone who HATES physical exertion of any kind, Yoga would probably be a better fit. Again, I really recommend taking a class so you don't hurt yourself. The DVD's are great once you've worked with a teacher.

contents: hi mimimama, just wanted to say that your son looks so cute.... i used to do pilates and i havent done it for a while because every time now i do it my muscles down there do tighten up and i get vulvodynia pain!!! i will try yoga instead. maybe for someone who hasnt done pilates should start with yoga. And yes i totally agree with taking a class before trying at home. I LOVE PILATES and hope that one day i can do it again. All the best.. Hi from the Kangaroo Land....

contents: Good post. I stopped in at the health club one night because I wanted to start pilates again, but wasn't sure if it was a good choice for IC. I think I'll try the walking on treadmill, and yoga first, and move up to pilates after.

I LOVED pilates, did it for almost a year at home before my diagnosis. In my case I can't handle Pilates anymore. Too much lying on my back and tightening pelvic muscles. This may not be an issue for you if you have IC alone, in my case, it made my PFD much much worse. I do Kripuli Yoga (except for two back poses) Very simple, very invigorating (feels like waking up from a good night's sleep, what ever that is, LOL!) and very easy on my sore body! I can't afford clases anymore, I did take two intro classes and found great info in a magazine article and online. You do need to be careful not to do the poses wrong b/c you could hurt yourself, I agree that classes may be a good idea, or a good video along with exercising LOTS of caution. Kripuli is considered one fo the simplest and most gentle forms of Yoga, at least to my somewhat limited knowledge. I found I did need a mat for Pilates and Yoga so I would not slip and to have proper back support. I bought my mat at Walmart for $14 and do yoga barefoot b/c socks still slip. The mat is slightly sticky and bare feet work well on it. My balance is off due to lots of meds and I have to be careful not to fall. Even when I hurt too much to walk beyond the front door (walking and biking as exercise is OUT for me right now, OUCH!), I can still do my simple standing & side lying poses, and often use my bladder support cushion to do the sitting poses. Good luck to everyone and hope you find something that works for you lorenab. Last edited by RAS6; 12-16-2007, 03:30 AM. Reason: spelling :)

contents: There is no way I would have been able to do Pilates when I first got dx'd. But now I do them every morning for about 20-40 minutes...depends on how bad off my Fibro is. I feel amazing after wards...very energized and the stretching actually makes me feel better. I don't do rigorous Pilates and you can feel the stretch in all of your muscles.
signature: Mommy to 2 crazy, wonderful kids and wife to the most amazing man in the world!

contents: And I was a little disappointed. The trainer showed us around, and I didn't bother to tell her I used to have a contract with them years ago because I didn't want the sales pitch. She showed us the machines, the pool, sauna, etc, THEN we got the sales pitch anyway. We have pretty much decided to join the YMCA but we're going to use our 2 month free passes to the gym first AND clean out the spare room where we have our workout machine. Tonight Tom and I went shopping for food and it's EXPENSIVE to eat healthy! Do you know that?!?! We spent close to 200.00 and that was just for US....we didn't even buy food for the kids since they'll be at their dad's for a few days. We were so good though....counting carbs, calories, fat grams....it took forever, but we came home with good, solid food that I can eat for my bladder and hopefully lose some weight. If I could lose 20 lbs I'd be thrilled....the prednisone is responsible for most of the weight, but some of it is from inactivity as well. So tomorrow I'm going to the gym when Tom gets home, and I might putter around cleaning up that room so we can use the gym upstairs. The Y is GREAT because it has pool classes for fibro and other painful illnesses. I am really excited about that because you don't have to look like Paris Hilton to work out there either! When I work out I SWEAT and do not look like some svelt redhead....I'm a nasty, grooling mess when I work out and really don't like working out with women who look like they weigh 100 lbs soaking wet! Ya know what I mean? LOL So, wish us luck. We are NOT dieting, we are eating healthy and we allowed ourselves treats in moderation. Nobody ever survived a diet that left out all the things you love to eat. When I had my last baby I weighed 202 lbs and lost over 75 lbs on my own, with no gym, just by cooking right, eating right and exercising at home....so I know we can do this. I wish some of y'all would join in with me, it would be fun to compare notes and root each other on. Think about it! Hugs, Sandy

contents: Good luck to you, Sandy. Losing weight is a lifestyle change that takes commitment. If you're really wanting to do it, you can. We have several Y's in our area, however we're too cheap to join. However we are members of the local recreation centers where we exercise several times per week. Not that we've ever been ones to eat too terribly, but we did find a few things we cut out of our diets. Having said that, we do not and will not ever deprive ourselves of a craving. Like you said, thats the perfect set up for failure, no doubt. One thing I've found that makes me get off my butt everytime is watching Dr. Mehmet Oz; we also follow alot of his "rules" for becoming/staying healthy..including 'enjoying' his green cocktail.haha..if you havent seen or heard of it, google it. Or better yet, msg me and I'll send you a pic (we make them regularly). Good luck to you! D

contents: Hi Sandy! Good luck with - I am also trying to lose weight at the moment- have done the same thing and got some healthier food to snack on as that is my weakest point and started going to the gym more regularly! I joined the gym October and went everyday for a month and cut out foods but only a 2-3 lb loss and then have been flaring on and off so not been that much just weekly or so.. I am bad as when flaring, I tend to turn to comfort foods which is a bad habit to get into but all losing weight ideas go out of my head and think, well am flaring anyway so why not eat something as well... lol, this comfort food is only recent so got to nip it in the bud - for over a year I have lived on mostly staples of veg, wheat, fishes etc and healthier food and not much snacks as never got hungry but must be the meds and thyroid as my snacks are the bane of my life! So bought carrots to make into carrot sticks and dip into humous and put in one of those salad boxes with space for dips to eat.. Also bought crackerbread as this is only 15 calories and plain but kinda nice for a salty snack...or you can add a topping of cheese/ham or something you can tolerate. Also got oat cakes- can't tolerate ryvita... and bananas which I do fine with.. I found sweet stuff wise, is a little more tricky for ICers and to be low in fat - a tiny bit of sweet yoghurt, ot a candy is better for my sweet tooth as then not tempted to have a larger bar of fattening cereal bars etc... Also have about 16lbs to lose- put on about 10lbs with the thyroid problems and about 5lbs with the prednisolone and with diet of about 1000calories have been keeping it stable at 60kg but need to get down to my normal of 53kg at least (was that weight July) Yep, I also allow myself stuff I like but in moderation and count the calories and calories burned through exercise Trying to do 40min workouts instead of my normal 20mins but is harder...esp when flaring all the time!! (but my IC has been bad for 1 1/2 yrs so can't wait for it to get better!)

contents: Even 150 is a bit high. You should ask your doctor for a safe range for you, and you should track it after you finish working out - it should come down pretty quickly. contents: I am so tired of not being able to do any lower body or ab work. Anyone else have the same problem? I got brave and decided to try a pilates/yoga video last night. I should know better but I keep thinking "maybe this time will be different". It was only a 15 min video and I modified everything. It was a few lunges, a few squats and a section where you lift your leg in front of you and twirl it in circles. I felt like my entire bladder area was swollen when I was done. Is this pelvic floor dysfunction (muscular) or IC? I just don't get it. I feel the pain in my bladder and urethra but it's the action of flexing and tightening my muscles that causes the pain. I have no pain below my urethra and no vaginal problems -it's all north. What do you guys think? I guess I need to go back to my walking and upper body work and that's it!
