contents: Originally posted by Charisse I am so tired of not being able to do any lower body or ab work. Anyone else have the same problem? I got brave and decided to try a pilates/yoga video last night. I should know better but I keep thinking "maybe this time will be different". It was only a 15 min video and I modified everything. It was a few lunges, a few squats and a section where you lift your leg in front of you and twirl it in circles. I felt like my entire bladder area was swollen when I was done. Is this pelvic floor dysfunction (muscular) or IC? I just don't get it. I feel the pain in my bladder and urethra but it's the action of flexing and tightening my muscles that causes the pain. I have no pain below my urethra and no vaginal problems -it's all north. What do you guys think? I guess I need to go back to my walking and upper body work and that's it! Charisse, Even though it is/was ridiculously expensive (oh, how many trips to Europe I've spent on IC), I found that working with a professional with experience in autoimmune disorders and special needs was the way to go. I used to work out with yoga tapes and pilates. It was through working with a yoga therapist that I trained myself to use breath properly. Sounds easy, but it isn't. We are so used in this country to "hard core" workouts, and yoga is no different. I'd done a million classes at gyms and studios, and they just don't teach it properly. My guy costs 90$ a session. I only do it once a month, and he has developed a program for me with focus on sun salutes, stretching, deep relaxation, breathing, and spiritual readings. He truly has made me enjoy my exercise! Now I can't go more than a day without feeling icky and wanting to get back on track. Pilates has been said by some to exacerbate problems in the pelvis. Makes sense, because it's core ab work after all, and that is often where the pain begins/radiates.

I can do bench aerobics (I don't do any jumping) and lots of walking (at least an hour a day 5 days a week) and that doesn't bother me. My prior doctor had a yoga CD that was good for IC patients, but I never used it. I will see if I still have it and give you the name of it. Tracey

Exericse flares me, too. I've found that cardiovascular exercise flares me..recently, in the past couple of months it's gotten to the point that I have to stop and use the bathroom halfway through my cardio. I've been in gyms for 20 years and never had this problem. Lifting weights, abs seem fine, but maybe it's because I've been doing these for so long. My lower back has been bad for 15 months, though. At first I thought it was weightlifting, because when I stopped for a couple of months after surgery it seemed a bit better. Not I'm more convinced that it's related to my IC - I've never had back problems before my IC started to flare up real bad the past couple of years.

contents: Hi. I exercise regularly but can only do aerobics that are low impact (no bouncing) and often have to stop half way through to use the restroom. When I try to run, I get sharp pains in my pelvis/bladder when my foot touches down and it keeps getting worse. For now I walk but am hoping after more time on Elmiron I'll be able to run again. I ordered the yoga CD from the IC Shop and like it. I have a Bowflex and do some weightlifting but not nearly to the point that I used to. So sad!

contents: In the beginning, I could not exercise. Then I could do gentle yoga, then walking. Now that I've found treatments that work & have had pelvic floor physical therapy, I can work out at the gym again. I use the elliptical trainer (30-35 min), rowing machine (5-10min), several weight machines (low row & chest press), free weights for biceps, triceps, military presses on a balance ball, also floor exercises & stretches. I also hike, do yoga. I think it's best to start out slowly & try things. Everyone is a little different. I wish I'd not let myself be sedentary for 3 years with this, I now have a lot of work to do (and about 20 more pounds to lose), than if I'd just done what I could, instead of getting mad & frustrated at my limits & done nothing. Good luck! Let us know what works for you!

Originally posted by molly648 How do you deal with the disappointment? I'm gaining weight from the inactivity..... I don't deal with it. I am fighting depression over the inactivity. I was always active my whole life, but was always obese. Well the year before I got bladder trouble, I lost 145 pounds and was an exercise junkie! LOL! I was training for my first half-marathon when my bladder trouble started. I felt better after I had my hydrodistention in August (enough that I could exercise 3 days/week doing weights and ellipse machine). I got cocky and thought I could run again... NOPE! I have been miserible ever since! Running seems to aggrivate my bladder and once that happens... I can't seem to recoup from it. I'm miserible! I know your pain and frustration!

contents: What works for me I've been down this road and while I am frustrated about what I can't do -- there are things I can do. I can't do anything jarring or muscle isolating in the lower body. However, I LOVE using an elliptical machine! I had to build up -- when the leg muscles were not strong I would get bladder pain if I overdid it. I literally did one minute a day for a few days, then 3 for a few days, and within a month I had built up to 30 minutes a day with no problem/reaction. I just can't push to where the thigh muscles fatigue (or whatever it is that makes you shaky). I think it is so wonderful -- it is everything I miss about running on the treadmill and much about doing dance aerobics -- works the arms, legs, and totally cardio. I actually went to sports stores and tried them out for three minutes to see what would happen and then I found refurbished ones from the manufacurer on ebay. I saw someone mention the arm treadmill. Those things are great! I don't know that I would buy one especially since the elliptical has arms but when I went through physical therapy on my arm that was compacted from sleeping on it (I had weird night pain for the first 2 years IC came out of remission and I had to sleep on my side but it messed up my arm). In fact, it played a key role in my success at going kayaking -- which is another thing I'd recommend -- you only have to have strong arms to do this and then only if there is a current! I went night kayaking after a month of the arm treadmill and it was so much fun. I'd love to buy a kayak or at least make time to rent one again! Of course, there are totally low-impact things like walking and water aerobics. I'm sorry to say that I find walking boring usually and way to hot/humid most of the year here in FL! I do try to trick myself with a zoo membership where I'll be 'walking' without realizing it and treks around the mall... For Christmas I have asked my husband to get me a heavy bag and gloves (boxing ??) so I can beat out my frustrations (I work about 60 hours a week and am always stressed). I found at the Walmart website that they have a pink set that donates 5% to breast cancer awareness foundation and look forward to working out frustrations and working my arms at the same time! Granted, I won't be bouncing around but I figure it's good for upper body! Good luck!

contents: My main symptom is pain. I rarely had problems with frequency and urgency. Yoga has been the best thing for me. I work out at a community center with mostly senior citizens so we don't do anything radical. Listen to your body. If it doesn't feel good, don't do it. I've wondered if sweating makes us worse since drinking water makes us feel better. I try to compensate with drinking more water if I do something more aerobic. Dehydration for me was a cause of my problem although mainly from drinking sodas instead of water. Reading about case historys I also wonder if certain positions affect the way blood flows to our bladder. I first noticed problems wearing tight jeans and riding in the car. I could actually feel tingling in my bladder as if it was going to sleep like when you sit on your feet. Maybe the lower body exercises are compressing your bladder and cutting off blood flow. I recently read about 3 patients who had surgery and their bladders went necrotic (I think this is the term). There was one suggestion that it might be from cutting off the blood flow. I don't think this is the whole answer but it could explain the onset for people who have surgery in that area. I think my problems came from a series of lifestyle choices that were not healthful to my bladder and caused its protective layer to deteriorate. I feel like we are trying to rehabilitate our bladder but don't always know what works. If it hurts, stop and try something else. Just keep trying! Lili

I haven't done Yoga in a while. Dr. Ragi Doggweiler had given me a good Yoga CD for patients with IC and it definitely helped especially with relaxing. My main exercise now is walking and I don't have any problems with that. I have tried bench aerobics (low impact) and enjoy that as well, but our church hasn't found a new instructor, so once that gets started back up, I will start doing that from time to time. I don't have problems with my bladder while riding in a car anymore and I don't have the freq/urg. I just have issues with my legs being still for so long, but that has been an issue with me for years. I mainly keep my feet on the dashboard or sitting indian style or mainly on one hip and I seem to do okay. Tracey

Charisse ... a well rounded exercise routine has three main components: strength training, aerobics, and flexibility training. All work together to get to peak fitness. After thinking about your comments, a possible approach might be for you to begin working your problem areas (painful) with a flexibility program. You could use your health insurance to go to a series of sessions with a physical therapist (hopefully one with experience with pelvic pain). They can train you to do a stretching/flexibility program for legs, pelvic area, abs, and back. The flip side of this program would be minor strength training options in these areas. This will allow you to relax and stretch your muscles. This should be a big help in reducing your IC pain and muscle tension in these area. If you stick with the flexibility program for a few months, I feel that you should be able to start to introduce the aerobic and strength training elements gradually back into your fitness program. Good luck.

contents: I've been going to Curves since 2001. I love it, and I don't think it affects my IC adversely.

Hi guys, My ortho says I have to get in the pool and start swimming, to alleviate the severe arthritis pain in my knee. I think I have read here that the chlorine from the pool is bad for IC. I do not have any urethral issues, or any other external problems. I just have bladder pain. The ortho said that the bladder is tightly protected from outside sources and the water would be no problem. Since he doesn't have IC and I do, I am throwing this out to you guys and hoping for some guidance! Thanks, mb
My suggestion would be to give it a try. Some ICers can swim with no problem. I do suggest that after you swim you immediately take a shower and don't sit around in a wet swim suit. Donna

contents: Thanks, Donna. I went to search for the old swimming posts(which I should have done first) and got a lot of good suggestions,as always, here on the ICN.

Turnaround, I started swimming a month ago, and it doesn't bother my IC at all. In fact, it's nice to be able to get a workout without any body parts hurting afterwards! I have bad knees, and a sore back, but the swimming doesn't aggravate them either. Good luck!

I have a swimming pool and we had the normal chlorine for a while, but it didn't bother me, but my husband changed it to Nature II cartridge which is still chlorine, but alot lower, mainly because all the kids would have red eyes after they swam. I too enjoyed that exercise. I am afraid to use our community center pool that is indoors since just the smell of the chlorine is strong to me, so I have been kind of afraid to give it a chance. Probably wouldn't do anything. I may try it someday.

Well I thought I'd update my IC sisters (and brothers) that I have lost 26 pounds now........thank goodness the only exercise I've had to do is walking up and down six flights of stairs a few times a week. And I've been able to keep the weight steady. This is the first time I've ever had good luck with weight loss. (I thought for sure my body would be doomed forever by having a child).......... I hope everyone else is doing well on their programs. Anyone out there want to share their good/bad days??

contents: I've never been a small person, but over the past year when my "IC" (undiagnosed until next week) has gotten worse, I've gained close to 100 lbs. My doctor has been giving me hell for it and I kept saying that it's hard to exercise when you have to worry about leaking or having to urinate constantly. At 5'9", I now weigh just over 300 lbs. No matter how well I eat and how much water I drink, I can't seem to take it off. I know exercise is essential, but what do you do when you don't have the room at home and are afraid to go outside?
signature: Cian and Jul <3

contents: I have these indoor walking tapes by Leslie Sansone, and you don't need a lot of room to use them. There isnt any drastic movements nothing jarring.

Perhaps you could start off with small walks. Or if you have access to a recreation center a lot of those now have recumbant bikes that are quite easy on the body and very effective for weight loss. Another idea would be to ask your treating physician if s/he would be kind enough to design a weight loss program specific for you. Wishing you well..... Diana
signature: (\__/) (o.O ) (> < ) This is Bunny. He's on his way to world domination.

contents: You may need the help of a program like Weight Watchers to help get the weight off. And even if you just walk around in your home for 15 or 20 minutes a day, it will help. And by all means, start a food/drink diary and write down everything that goes into your mouth. When I was trying to lose weight I did that and I was totally amazed at how much I was eating in a day. You'll want to gradually reduce your salt in foods --- if you do it all at once, food just tastes gross, but done gradually you don't even notice. I'm finding that salt levels I thoroughly enjoyed two years ago are totally gross to me now. Hang in there. You can lose that extra weight and you are certain to feel better. Warm encouraging hugs, Donna

contents: Originally posted by windwalker I have these indoor walking tapes by Leslie Sansone, and you don't need a lot of room to use them. There isnt any drastic movements nothing jarring. Hi, I'm new to the IC world (still awaiting diagnosis), and also need to lose some weight. I also have the Leslie Sansone dvd's too and find them a pretty good workout. They're called Walk Away the Pounds - I bet you could find them ebay for real cheap. Good luck! Jamie

Newbie so please bear with me and all my questions. I haven't exercised in nearly 2 weeks due to this bad flare up I'm having (the first one, I have not yet been officially diagnosed but the uro says I have all the classic symptoms - cysto in 3 weeks). Usual routine: free weights (8 lb. at most), 100 crunches on a pilates ball; pully work for bad posture and aches from sitting at the computer all day -nothing too heavy (10-15 lb. weights no more), and then 30minutes on the eliptical trainer (the stand-up one) for cardio and fat burning. I was doing this 3-4 times a week. I have been afraid to go the gym because I'm flaring, but boy do I miss it and my body is rapidly turning into jello. I guess sit-ups are out of the question with this burning bladder from Hades, but what about the rest of my usual routine? Are any of these things likely to irritiate my bladder? Is it even advisable to exercise at all when flaring? What about tylenol before you exercise if you're flaring? Is it possible to make your bladder condition worse if you're too active? Can working out CAUSE a flare-up? Are there exercises or gym machines that might HELP a flare-up? Also, what about the sauna and steam room? I guess the chlorine filled whirlpool would be a no-no. I know people will think I'm crazy for even thinking about exercise while flaring, but somehow I feel a workout would bring some normalcy back to my life. I feel lost and worried right now.

Exercise is essential for me... For me, if the flare is mild, exercise can relieve the discomfort. If the flare is severe, I can't even think about going to the gym, but I will do some mild yoga & stretching; it does help a bit, if nothing else emotionally. The elliptical trainer is one of the more IC-friendly exercise machines because it keeps the hips level. I find the older models (without arm levers) to be more comfortable. But, my mom who also has IC uses the elliptical with arm movement just fine. I also use the rowing machine for cardio. I can't tolerate the treadmill, but mom can with no problems. I use 8lb free weights for biceps curls, tricep kickbacks, & military presses, while sitting on a balance ball. By stabilizing this way, I get a little abdominal workout without the flare I get from crunches. Many patients find that crunches worsen their symptoms. I also use the low row pull weight & the chest press machines without problems, as long as I "listen" to my body & if I start to get burning in the pelvic or lower abdominal muscles, then I back off the weight or the number of reps. The literature & other patients tend to be very negative about exercise & IC. I think this is a mistake, myself. I think maintaining some level of fitness is essential to weight management, emotional health, & overall strength & resilience of the body. It may take some trial & error to see what works for you. You could start with walking. When in a moderate flare, I'll walk gentle laps around the apartment complex, so I'm never too far from my bathroom & don't have to walk back from somewhere if I feel worse. I have a couple of yoga tapes to do at home if not up to the gym. The gym? You might start out with just the elliptical to see if that's okay. Then if that's okay, next time add in one of the weight machines. The next time, another. Then free weights. And wait after adding each thing til the next day to see if you flare the next day. My urologist & physical therapist recommended to avoid weights & exercises that directly focus on the abdominal or pelvic muscles. (crunches, abductor/adductor machines are a big no-no for me & my mom). So for ab work, we do some gentle exercises given to us by physical therapists who have special training in working with IC/pelvic pain patients. About the sauna, steam room. Check your medication labels to see if they have warnings about sensitivity to heat . Two of my medicines (Ditropan & Elavil) have that side effect & I get very dizzy & nauseated if I'm in a high temperature environment. Take it slow & gentle. Don't compare what you can do now with what you did before. For me, maintaining a lower level of fitness is better than doing nothing waiting for a miracle cure, & then having to start again later with an overweight, unfit body.

contents: I totally agree with Kadi - exercise is essential for me. Even if I am flaring I still exercise - I just don't push myself as hard. For me exercising relieves stress and as we all know flaring it is very stressful. For me I would rather go to the gym than lay on the couch and feel bad for myself.

That would be me I worked out Monday (and treadmill on Tues.) and I am sooooooo ridiculously sore. Anyone have any other ideas besides a hot shower that might help with this...I wonder if I took Advil/Motrin if that would help....you think this is considered "minor aches and pains" I look so silly at work, limping around....and my bladder & belly and IBS is not happy with me I need help, please

contents: I take Aleeve. I have just started back to walking myself. It seemed harder than when I started up the first time! It does make me feel better after the first few days, it's just getting started that's the pain! Tracey
If you can, relax with your heat pad. I can't take any of the ibuprofen type drugs. You might try some tylenol. Warm hugs, Donna

contents: I can take ibuprofen but I know that a lot of people have problems with it. If you have prelief you could take the ibuprofen with three prelief and that might counter act the acidic effect of it. I hope you feel better soon. At least had the engery to exercise, I barely seem to have the engery to make it through the day, lol.
signature: Christine I have been diagnoised for 6 1/2 years now. I have taken a long break from the ICN but really miss helping out my fellow IC patients and want to get back into posting. 1st hydro 4/07 showed no visible signs of IC but tons of mast cells in all my biopsy samples which did prove IC. 2nd hydro 4/13/09 showed dark purple glomerulations and I had a capacity of 450 cc's. This hydro proved that my IC had progressed. I have tried every oral medication as well as rescue instills and DMSO. I have been lucky enough to see Dr Hanno, the top IC specialist in PA who has told me due to the fact that I have not responded to any "standard" treatments that I have a severe, end stage case of IC with a horrible quality of life (didn't have to tell me that last part!) Proud wifey of Shane, mommy to Griffin, and step-mom to Logan and Gage Also proud mom to the best Bullmastiff on earth, Claus

contents: I hope you feel better, I don't know what to do with pain, if it gets extremly bad, I take paracentamol 1000mg and rest. Is there anything available there, that you could apply externally, perhaps an ointment. We have alots for product to control muscle spasms, they have helped me at my worse, the ones with menthol inside. But, please know that, I actually really had an infection but it was somewhat pain control, while I stayed in bed for a week straight unable to move or sleep, and finally went to the hospital A&E, in the middle of the night. Vampireness

Thanks so much everybody! Yes I am feeling so much better...my legs are still sore (just a little) and I'm trying to keep up with the whole gym...I prepaid the trainer so i won't quit...I've got a friend meeting me at the gym at work on my days off from the trainer. I'm trying to keep up with the whole weight watchers thing (man! i'm hungry) And I have yet to keep within my points for 2 weeks now (I can't do the Core program b/c of my IBS-d) I think i get the whole idea now...you eat less and excercise more and thus lose weight...duh...basic laws of physics.... ugh! I miss brownies and cake. OK so I bought some flexall stuff, I went to bed smelling like a big Ol' tic-tac and it didn't really help much....time seemed to be the only thing to help. Thanks again for all your great recc's. I really appreciate it

contents: Good to hear you're doing alot better. Also, weight is difficult, I imagine, I'm underweight but at one point, I was gaining some weight and boy did my appetite grow and I was finding it difficult to loose weight and my mom has diabetes and she has struggled with weight issues for years. But, ya know something, when you eat less and excercise more, you'll end up starving and possibly fainting since you've used up all your energy. Another, way to do it is to increase your exercise session, without disturbing your food intake. Like, if you ran 1km to increase that to 2km etc, otherwise the body starts getting used to it and you burn less. And, boy do I know what you mean by craving for cookies and stuff. I feel like I'm on the verge of stealing candy from a kid or robbing a candy store. No thanks to yeast at times and other times, I JUST WANT CANDY!!! Vampireness

I seem to remember posts that discuss problems chlorine and IC. My primary problem is pain. Every once in a while I'll have frequency problems and if I don't drink enough water my urine will get very acidic and burn. My question is: how does chlorine impact your IC? Is it a chemical reaction with your outter skin or does the chlorine actually increase bladder pain? I want to start swimming but wanted to look into potential problems before I begin. I always get such great info from this forum. Thanks for your inputs! Jan S.
signature: Jan

contents: I swam all summer and I was fine. I think the only time you'd have a problem is if the chemicals were wrong. I probably wouldn't trust a highly chlorinated indoor pool. I think I'd want proof of balanced chemicals....
I do not do well with it, but thaats just me, I guess its a eat and see type of thing.
signature: 'The will of God will never take you where the Grace of God will not protect you.'

contents: Tigger_gal: when you say that you didn't do well with swimming, what kind of symptoms did you experience? Is is more skin irritation than direct impact to bladder pain? I'm thinking I might try the elliptical instead of swimming. I would have to use a military facility pool and they use lots of chlorine. Not sure if that is good or bad. Thanks for sharing. JanS.
signature: Jan

contents: I live on a thoroughbred breeding farm. I rode show hunters as my hobby until IC hit. Has anyone tried riding? Wondering if there is a prayer or if I should sell my horses - which may kill me. One was on rest from an injury for six months and now he is ready to go and I have out of control IC. My son is getting marreid 8/11 out of state. Trying to be very conservative until after then. Also used to go to the gym. Now lifting anything heavy can give me a flare. I am losing weight because I'm so afraid to eat. Have to try on my dress for the wedding again to be sure I don't need to have it taken in. When this hit I felt like my horse and I were in the best shape of our lives. Now the muscles are gone and saggy skin has arrived.

Yes, I wouldn't take a chance before the wedding! I didn't ride horses for awhile (several years and it killed me) and just started back last year. Just trail riding since I have the implant, my doctor said no, but she knows I love it (I don't have any of my own), but I do take it very easy. I wanted to just take off in a gallop so bad! After I rode the first time (a couple of hours) I was fine. Funny that I have a harder time riding in a small car than riding a horse. I guess that is one of those things that is mind over matter with me! I made sure that my stirups were high enough, etc. Like I said I was careful.
Thanks Tracey. I'm new at this. I don't know what the implant is.

Sorry...Sacral Nerve Implant (Interstim Implant). Their is a forum about it for more detailed info. I have had it since 2002 and it has helped with my freq/urg sensations for the most part, then in turn has helped me with my pain. I used to be in a flare more than I wasn't, so my doctor suggested that I think about the implant. I went through all kinds of testing (almost a years worth). The implant is about the size of a makeup compact and is placed near the top of my left butt cheek. It has leads to my sacral nerve which I control with a hand held device. That is the main reason that my doctor didn't want me to ride horses is because of the implant. T83 Last edited by Claredale; 07-30-2007, 07:26 AM.

horses Hi, I also have horses. I ride occasionally, and also my horses are gaited horses, so not much bouncing, I take it very slow and am careful. Sometimes it doesn't bother me at all, sometimes it does. But we are thinking about selling our horses or at least getting down to just a couple. But there like part of the family. I ride with a pad on my saddle and that helps. Also, we only trail ride and for short times. HOPE your able to do it alittle if you love it that much. My IC has been bad for about a year, I have had it forever but was diagnosed about 10 years ago. My Uro has horses, but I never ask him if I should ride, he tells me to do what I feel I can, but he thinks the busier I am the better my IC is. Brooding doesn't help me, it just makes my IC worse. Well, sorry to go on and on. Good Luck! Kelly

I LOVE horses. Had a morgan and arab years ago. would love to get another one, IC or no IC I won't give up my passion. What kind of gaited horses do you own? I am looking into a paso fino or Tenn Walker.
signature: Mimi~ Currently battling: Migraines IC Panic attacks/Anxiety Disorder Current Treatments: Pain Meds and Anxiety Meds Gentle Physical Therapy Yoga Heating Pad~my best friend Lots of hot baths w/ Epsom Salts & Baking Soda Tens Unit Prayer

contents: I have ridden almost daily for the ten years I have had IC. There have been a couple of times I had to take a break for shoulder surgery and a hysterectomy. Honestly, my IC was not affected at all by stopping. I say you should ride. If you are like me and it is your passion then it is one of the things that helps you not focus on the pain for an hour a day. Winston Churchill said"the outside of a horse is good for the inside of a man." That is a very wise statement. I know just petting my horses makes me happier and more fulfilled. Just find one to ride that is easy to get along with so you don't become frustrated with a problem horse which defeats the purpose of riding in the first place. Of course, you may enjoy the challenge of training a difficult one. I, for one, don't. I like to ride a willing horse with a lot of try but I have no patience myself Anyhow, happy riding. I sold my good horse about six months ago and now I am really regretting it! At the time, I was having a hard time getting my hormones right and I was insanely tired, moody, etc. I found out I have a B-12 deficiency and got that under control. My energy is back and I think I have my hormones finally adjusted. Unfortunately, I only have two impossible young fillies to ride. So here I sit with nothing to do! I could just kick myself for selling my trusty old gelding! I am happy he got a great home but now that I feel better I miss him terribly!
signature: Blessings and Hope Current Meds. OFF MY MEDS Vivelle Dot: .10 changed every 72 hours Compoounded Progesterone 25mg/night Current Supplements Hydroeye: 2 pills AM Diagnosed 1995: Endometriosis 1998: Interstitial Cystitis 2006: Bladder Endometriosis, Total Hysterectomy and Bowel Re-Section 2008: Removal of Ovarian Remnant and Endometrial Cyst That was Obstructing Ureter 2012: Laparoscopic Removal of Adhesions and Staples

contents: Hi, I have two Kentucky Mountain Horses and one Tennessee Walker. They are all smooth riding and very easy going even my mare. I have a kidney issue also, I had narrowing of the arteries to my right kidney and had to have it ballooned, by nephrologist isn't crazy about my riding. My uro tells me to take it real easy. I do hurt at times after riding, thats what I hate, I never know if it is going to hurt or not. This disease sucks. Kelly

Don't sell 'em Hey, I have horses too!! I grew up with 'em and now have way to many... they really start adding up! But anyways, we have friesians (yep it really is like a fairlytale), and a peruvian. I don't ride like I used to, but I would NEVER consider selling due to illness. It would have to kill me first. So don't make any rask decisions! Anke

I ride I love it. It is a stress relief for me if i have had a hard week in the rescue. To just go and ride for hours I love it. I do have to be more careful but can still ride fine. I have been wanting to try riding a gaited horse to see if it makes a differnce but the stable doesnt have any. That arent people's horses.

I bought a new horse just after being diagnosed with IC. Riding didn't seem to bother me except one day. I had to sell him though as he was just not working out for me. I am looking at a new one this weekend and hope it goes okay, have been in some kind of flare after 2 months being symptom free. Teresa

Riding I used to able to ride but after being catherized a few times I have a major urethra problem and riding does seem to aggravate. I'm so glad you can ride. Gives me hope. Sometimes though it's too much bounce for my bladder. I am going to the doctor Monday. I have some info on how to help the urethra issue and I'm going to ask for the Cystistat installs to build a temporary lining until hopefully Elmiron will do enough on my real lining. Don't know if you have ever used Legend on your horse. It is the same chemical. My bladder gets sore if I ride in the car too long so think I have a way to go. I hired someone to ride my horses to keep them in shape. It made me sad to have to do it. When you get your new horse, please let me know if it goes well.

Pain with riding I am sorry to hear of your pain in riding. I don't ride as much as I used to and I usually ride one of the 'regular' non-gaited - actually they are warmblood style... if my bladder is acting up worse, we do have a Peruvian Paso, and they are super smooth - you may want to look into getting one or maybe trying one out. They don't have any gait like a trot, and you don't get bounced all over the place! anke

Other horses I've been riding thoroughbred horses and show them in jumping classes. Never thought of the paso idea or maybe a Tennessee walker. I love the horse I have so much though. Hard to think of giving her up. Maybe if I can get my urethra better and think up some way of better padding myself I can just trail ride.

If you can't ride... what about carriage driving? I don't know if any of you all thought about this...but there is always the alternative of carriage driving. We do this with our horses, we are in a local club and drive all over (on roads and it is smooth). Just thought I would offer an alternative. Anke

anke- That is a good point! I used to do pleasure driving in horse shows where my horse pulled a two-wheel cart. I have one horse that can do it now but my mother-in-law is using it. She qualified for the world championships with it! I have not done anything with my horses in almost a year. I can feel the fever coming on again! For thirty-five years, I have had horses and what makes me think I don't need them anymore is beyond me. They are just part of my being and what makes me tick. I will say I caught up on a lot of stuff I was neglecting to get done. However, it is time to get back in the barn. I have a cute two-year old that I just sent off to the colt-breaker. I hope to have her back in a month as he put in some time with her this spring. By the way, isn't Anke the name of a very famous grand prix dressage rider? Then again maybe it's Anky? I dunno, just sounds familiar. -Vicki

contents: I wish I HAD a horse! I love to ride and try to whenever I can....usually on vacation. I can't ride a bike for the life of me...........but the horses seem to be just fine.

contents: I have been able to use the ellipse machine 4 times per week for a few weeks and I was feeling pretty alright (not normal, but ok enough to exercise). I tried to run a few days ago... and my bladder is really ****** off at me (for a lack of better words). This makes me so sad! I was training for a half-marathon when my IC symptoms started (out of the blue). Will I ever be able to run again...?
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: I don't have an answer for you Alyssa. I know that any kind of jarring like that drives my bladder nuts, heck when I'm flaring walking hurts it. Hopefully you'll find the right treatment for you and go into remission or get them well enough under control that you'll be able to run again.

contents: Hi Alyssa, It's hard to know, hopefully when your IC is better managed or in remission, you will be able to run again. I know that for me, as time has gone by & I've found more treatments that work, I'm able to do lots of things I couldn't do when I was at my sickest. I generally try not to look at any of this (diet, medicines, symptoms) as "forever", but just "for now" & try to make the best of what I can do each day. I'm not perfect at this, I have my bad days like anyone, but it is a philosophy that helps me when I can do it... It's great that you are able to use the elliptical trainer, that will help you maintain fitness, so that when the treatments work, you'll be able to really see what you can do & not have to start from scratch with a weak & sedentary body. Hang in there, it gets better...

Apparently God thinks I'm a bad-ass" ~Author Unknown Source - Pinterest " Current treatments: -IC diet -Elavil 50mg at night -Continuous use birth control pills (4-5 periods/year) -Heparin/Marcaine/Sodium Bicarb home instills at night 3-4x per week, more often if needed -Pyridium if needed, -Pain medicine at bedtime daily, as needed during the day several times per week -Antibiotic when doing an instillation to prevent UTI -Colace & SmartFiber to treat chronic constipation from meds, Fleet enema as needed -Dye Free Benadryl 50 mg at bedtime -"Your Pace Yoga: Relieving Pelvic Pain" dvd, walking, treadmill at gym -Managing stress= VERY important! -Fur therapy: Hugging the cat!

contents: Kadi and Christine! Thank you so much for your support (now and for the past several months!). I think about running every day and since my last attempt, I have been miserible. I sure hope this flare is temporary. I won't use the ellipse machine with a grudge anymore (after feeling what running does... and why my IC symptoms started while I was at the gym running). I see two new urogynos tomorrow (just moved to a new city) and hopefully one of them has an idea of how to better help me!
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: Alyssa, Don't know what to tell you - I would love to be able to use the elleptical!! (old knee injury rules that out). Treadmills are out for me also. Perhaps you will get better; I hope you will get a wonderful remission and be able to run all you wish. If not, have you considered swimming? or blading (fairly similar to running; not as much impact) I wish you the best of luck, and that you are running again very soon! girl

Hey girl! I really wish I could go swimming, but unfortunately there is no pool very close to me (unless they are outside and I burn too easily to make it a regular routine). So, I have settled for the ellipse machine even if it makes my knees ache. Blading... boy, you would laugh if you saw me attempt to stand up on blades of any kinds. I cannot skate to save my life! Thank you for the good luck wishes! I sure hope I can go running again soon!
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: Hi Alyssa, I am a runner and am currently training for the Chicago Marathon. As long as I am not in a flare, my uro told me to go ahead and run. I have found that it really helps me clear my head. It doesn't cause me to flare, but if I am in a flare, I go back to just walking or eliptical. Being a runner, I can understand how miserable you feel when you aren't out their with the other runners. Good luck, and I hope you are back in your running shoes soon!

It's amazing how wonderful running makes me feel. It is how I relax. Meditation, yoga, pilates, etc are so slow and they make me frustrated. Running lets me clear my head, allow me to work out my stress, etc. Unfortunately the ellipse machine is uncomfortable on my knees, so I don't get into that groove (where I block out everything else). Good luck on the Chicago Marathon! I had a teacher in high school that ran that marathon and collapsed. She died 5 days later in the hospital due to heart failure. Please make sure you are in the best shape and when running that event, if something seems off, stop. I wish she did. She was an athlete, soccer coach, etc. You would think she was in the best shape for the marathon. Not to scare you! I just wanted to caution you to make sure you listen to your body! Good luck! I can't wait until I can run my first half-marathon. Every winter, Walt Disney World hosts a marathon and half-marathon. I was training for that when my IC symptoms started. I don't think the next one is possible (I am incredibly out of shape). Maybe the year after! I just can't wait to get out there running again!
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: Threadmill, running, walking long distances, etc Can you do either orthe above if you have Vulvodynia + IC? Forgot to add, eliptical machine? My current excersice is Kundalini Yoga (mind, body and spirit type of relaxation and repetitions) that helped me a lot. (Can't do it when I'm flaring though) Hoever I feel to I need to add some cardio. I feell I need to do something to sweat. There's a saying that goes: "If you sweat and laugh everyday, that's what makes a person healthy, happy and whole". I used to use the eliptical several times and week as well as goint o the park for long 1 hr or more walks or used the threadmill. I loved to hike in my trips to Northern California. I was in shape! I haven't done any of that after I was dx with IC. My excersice is walking my dog aroung the appartment complex for 6 minutes 4 times a day, using the stair to the second floor at home and work + my yoga once or twice a weeek.

contents: You know, I miss running so much! I would break a great sweat, feel my heart going, my controlled breathing, and my legs felt like they had so much strength and freedom. I miss it so much! I don't know much about Vulvodynia, but I hear cardio can be a challenge with that. I hear swimming is even worse (due to the chlorine). I am not much of a yoga fan. It is too slow for me. Running was actually calming and meditative for me. Ellipse machine hurts my knees a bit, but I can do it. I try not to go too high on the resistance. Weight lifting was doable, but I got thsi weird "twitch" in my pelvis when doing this one exercise standing up using a cable pully (doing my triceps). That was uncomfortable. They ruled out PFD (3 doctors checked me manually and found no sign of PFD). So... whatever that twitch was, I won't do it again!

contents: Born2swim I truly understand, I am not a runner by any means, but I use to ride motorcyles all the time, my husband had just bought me a brand new one right before I was Dx with IC..It was a passion of mine like you with running.. It is so sad how IC can just take it all away.. We are in the process of selling it and it breaks my heart! But if I can't ride it breaks it even more looking at it just sitting there.. I really hate this disease...
signature: Hugs Ronda ONE Second, ONE Bite, ONE Breath, ONE Pill, ONE Minute, ONE Teardrop, ONE Hour, ONE Sip.. ONE DAY! I will Prevail from this disease! IC Hoping for a Cure! Link to Patient Handbook: http://www.ic-network.com/handbook/ Diet Reference Sheet: http://www.ic-network.com/diet/icndi...tsheet0909.pdf Meds For IC: Lyrica-25mg Glucosamine-500 MSM-500mg, Prosed Ds -When Flaring Other Meds: Levlite- Continious Birtcontrol, Micardis-40mg for High Blood Pressure Meds I have Tried: Topamax,Tofranil, Elmiron, Atarax, Cymbalta, Elavil, Enablex, Detral La, Prydium. Lexapro< Bad reaction to this med! Intstills, could not continue them due to some kind of reaction after 3rd instill. Tasted the lidocaine in my mouth, tongue and lips went numb then went into what seemed like a panic attack. Shaking, racing heart, tingling face/head, blood pressure shot up.. contents: Oh no!!! I'm so sorry to hear that you are selling your bike! I just wish you would get better! I know someone with IC and she would drive her Porsche all the time. It became too painful and had to stop. It broke her heart, but she kept it and finally she got well enough that she can drive it, and drive it all over the place. Just have faith that you will be back on the roads again!

contents: I used to be an avid runner. I'd take my dalmation and we'd run about a mile and a half, maybe 2 miles every single day. It was great, and we both felt great. Now he hurts from arthritis and I hurt too. I miss running so much. Heck, I miss WALKING! My daughter is walking right now, training for her high school swim team and she asked me to go with her and I had to say no because I am just hurting too much to go. I can barely get through the grocery store using the buggy like a walker! I'm hoping that one day I'll be able to run again. Not sure if my dalmation will ever run like we used to but maybe I can get him out walking again when the weather cools off. We're both so pitiful sometimes...I feel like we relate to each other...he knows when I hurt and I know when he hurts.
signature: *IC-- Summer 2004; PFD--October 2005 *Fibro--Fall 2000; CFS-- Fall 2000 *MPS--Fall 2000; Crohn's disease-- 1997*IBS,GERD, *Migraines, hypothyroidism, GYN problems *Degenerative Disc Disease/scoliosis Total Abdominal Hysterectomy--adenomyosis--9\08 04/17/09 Crohn's disease almost killed me with a combo of extreme constipation from pain medications. My bowel ruptured, I almost died from peritonitis and spent several days in the ICU then more in a private room on the floor. If you have any questions about severe constipation from pain meds please don't hesitate to send me a message.

contents: Sandy, At least you have company in your misery! I am sorry to hear you hurt too much to even walk. I can't walk either. It isn't from pain, it is due to urgency/frequency that I can't do it. I am serously considering Interstim now. I want to run again. I want to sleep normally again. I want to ride roller coasters, go swimming, watch a movie in the theater without disruption, etc. Once I can run, I know that I am feeling well. That is the worst thing for my bladder and one of my favorite things to do! It's so sad. I miss it like I lost my best friend!

I know it has to be something low impact, but I am just not used to doing anything low impact with exercise and sports. Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated! ~Samantha

I have gotten to where I can walk for almost 2 hours, so I am planning on joining our church groups bench aerobics again. I can always do the low impact when they are doing alot of hopping and stuff.

I had really good luck with an arthritis/fibromyalgia water aerobics class. They were really sensitive to the low impact needs of those dxs and it turned out to be great for my IC. I would have stayed but couldn't afford the health center membership any longer. Hugs, Barb

contents: elliptical and uphill walking. It can get your heart rate up. I used to run, and it has been a difficult process in letting go of that. It is the only excersise i enjoy. I dont enjoy elliptical, its not the same high, but maybe it will work for you. Running increased my frequency and urgency, and caused an intense throbbing all day. My bladder was not happy. i fear gaining weight, so yoga and water aerobics never turned me on, but it could be helpfull for you. Im planning to give it a try. good luck

I use the elliptical trainer and do the weight circuit, though I don't do any of the circuit machines if I feel they're putting pressure on my bladder (some of the leg and ab machines). Sometimes it's not a problem, but if I feel any discomfort on the weight machines I stop and try again next time. Before I started taking Elavil I found that early morning exercise was an important part of my pain management. If I worked out early in the morning I generally felt better that day -- I think the mix of the exercise and the water I was drinking really helped. With Elavil I feel much better, but can't get up early to exercise! I still work out though, just can't do it early.
I am able to use an elliptical machine, I have indoor walking tapes which work out nicely and use light weights, resistance bands too.
I have read that we shouldn't swim because of the chlorine yet it seemed as though it was thought to be okay to swim in the ocean. Is the true? Wouldn't the salt irritate as much?

Hi Swimming really is an individual thing, some can and some can't not go swimming. I do believe this is something you will have to see by trial and error to see if you are able. I hope you are able to go swimming and enjoy it.
I can't swim in a pool with clorine, but the ocean is fine. We have a pool and use Baquacil instead of clorine and that works okay for me too. Donna

contents: Ocean swimming is great! I get AMAZING relief from my burning pain every time I get in the ocean. Something about the cool water just takes the pain away. If you have an ocean nearby, definitely try it out.

OK, I keep posting this every time the subject comes up, but this is my experience: For me, and for some people who are sensitive to chlorine, it is really helpful to (1) Smear some vaseline all over "down there" to help make a barrier to keep the water away from your skin and various openings, and (2) Don't stay in your wet swimsuit. The easiest thing is to wear a tankini, so you can just take off the bottom and put on a pair of shorts whenever you get out of the water, then change back into the bottom when you want to go back in. You really have to experiment and see.
signature: Je vous souhaite de la joie, de la bonne santée, et tout ce qu'il y a de bon dans la vie. Wishing you happiness and good health, and all the best out of life. Peace, Carolyn ___________________________________________________ Laura (11), Susannah (12 1/2) and Maman (that's me!), North Wildwood NJ, September 2007 On the Beach with IC

contents: I have a pool and sometimes it really bothers me and other times I don't have a single issue. When I look back at when it bothers me is when we have friends over, so I am in and out and don't clean myself off right away. Most of the time (like today), I swam for a little while, then when I was done, I took a bath and cleaned myself off well. We also use the Nature II cartridge which doesn't require as much chlorine in the pool. Whenever my hubby first shocks it, I definitely don't get in the pool, but after a few hours, the chlorine level isn't real high, so it's safe as long as I rinse myself off well. We are changing to the salt water pump next year, but it still changes the salt water to chlorine somehow, but it's just not supposed to require so much maintenance. I have swam in our friends that has the salt water and it really didn't seem to bother me. But when I swam in the ocean earlier this summer, I did go into a flare, but it could have been a result of a number of things, so I can't really say what caused my flare. Tracey

Okay everyone, I have to fess up here. I am a total food addict and my weight just keeps climbing!! I have always loved food but tended to like healthy foods more than junk, so I have never been heavy in my life. But it seems like ever since i have had IC and been on all these meds, I crave BAD food all the time! I have become a sugar addict and a snacker. I have gained 20 pounds since I got married, actually make that 25, and I am starting to realize that I have to make some big changes or the weight will just continue to creep on, slowly. I am not overweight by medical standards, 138 pounds at 5'5 1/2 but the weight bothers me so much. I have started to just be so self concious and living in Hawaii, the weight cannot be hidden anymore. I need to get serious about getting in shape and losing this weight but I dont know how! I know that sounds stupid but I have never had to lose weight before and I hate exercise. Plus these meds slow down my metabolism and make me feel lazy. Add to this the fact that lots of days I just dont feel good and i am feeling overwhelmed. Anyone here have any good tips or starting points? I need to just get going and then hopefully things will seem easier. Thanks for your help! Sarah
signature: Current meds; , effexor 37.5 mg 2 times a day, and lyrica 100 mg 3 times a day, lots of reading and snuggling with the pets!

contents: Sarah, I can sympathize with you. It is never easy to lose weight and taking meds makes it doubly so. I have gotten on line with Sparkle. They do bombard you with lots of email - or maybe I just put my name on the wrong lines. Anyway, I like some of the things that are on that site. One of the things is a Journal. It gives questions to think about not just with regard to weight loss, but our state of mind in general. I think that the mental part of weight loss is just as important as the diet, exercise and plain hard work. Realizing that the meds do certain things to the body is one big step. The next part has to do with changing the thinking patterns. I am certainly no one to give advice here, because I'm working like crazy on this and it is an uphill battle. I tell myself, no snacking, then I say - well one won't hurt. Then I say no candy - well, this is just a little bit. No pretzels - well there's no fat in them.... The list goes on. Weight watchers helped me to realize just how much damage certain foods do with regard to calroric intake. The simple fact - more calories more weight. Fewer calories, more exercise - less weight. But my mind has to agree with it. That's why I like the journaling exercise. It reprograms my brain so that I'm not fighting with myself when the time comes to make decisions. I'm actually drinking more water and fewer root beers since doing the journaling. Take it easy on yourself, too. You are dealing with pain and the illness itself. You do need to give yourself some flex room. Be of good heart. Maggie

Maggie I had to laugh at the "fewer root beers" comment. I am struggling with eating now too. It seems like so many of the tangy low-cal foods and sauces are now banned so I'm making up for it with root beer, ice cream, butter etc.
signature: The combo of things that brought me out of remission were artificial sweetners, liquid vitamins with citric acid, tight pants, lower body workouts and stopping all my allergy meds cold turkey during height of allergy season. I wish I knew back then what I know now about IC First IC symptoms Spring of 2000; In-office cystoscopy 2002 -negative; Remission until 2007 (yeah!!); Negative PST spring of 2007; IC diagnosed via Cystoscopy and Hydrodistention 7/24/07; Another In-office cystoscopy 4/9/10 with biopsy showed inflammation Treatments for my IC/PFD: Elmiron 400 mg per day; walking, Zyrtec, Claritin, Singulair, Neurontin 100 mg per day; Ice packs and laying down; About to try Cytotec Tried and Failed: Heat (always makes me worse!), Heparin instills -too painful for my urethra; Atarax (interfered with my quality of sleep first time, second time dried my bladder out; Elavil makes me sick, lidocaine patches (just plain don't work), Cystoprotek (only tried a couple months but didn't seem to do much), Aloe Vera caps, pelvic floor therapy, trigger point work flared me worse, Lyrica (bad stomach pain) Other Diagnoses: PFD 2009; Fibromyalgia 2006, Osteopenia 2012, GERD 2006, Gastroparesis (delayed gastric emptying) Anxiety: Other medications: Ambien, Dexilant, Ibuprofren, Necon 1/35, Voltaren gel for tailbone BABY GIRL 10/28/08 *** BABY BOY 7/8/11

contents: This is what I'm doing about the weight gain... Hi Sarah, I've also struggled with my weight since getting IC. About 3 weeks ago, I went back on an excellent weight loss program at www.sparkpeople.com So far, I've lost 4 pounds in 3 weeks. I log my food & exercise every day. I log my weight once a week & my measurements once a month. I set up my goal weight & target date through the program (go from 164 to 134 by next March). The program is 100%free, has message boards like here, tons of informational & motivational articles. I also made a decision that I would not restrict myself from any more foods, the IC diet is hard enough (and I follow that very strictly), but I would keep within the calorie requirements (1200-1550) and would make choices that would allow for that. For example, last week I had a beautiful 4 serving blueberry pie at home. I made sure I had calories left at the end of the day for that dessert. This meant no hashbrowns for brunch that day, but some day when there is not a pie at home, I'll be able to have them then. Does that make sense? I can have anything my IC allows, but maybe not all the same day. And if I mess up & eat too much in one day, I just recommit & try again the next day. I also got a referral from my urologist for pelvic floor physical therapy, with 2 goals. One, to help with the pelvic pain from constricted muscles around the bladder. Two, to create an exercise program that my IC would allow. The PT has helped in both goals. I can now do yoga, walk, go to the gym, do light weight training, and hike. Anyhow, you are not alone, but wanted to give you some ideas of positive steps you could take. Two years ago, I did this program & lost 12 pounds (I'd gotten up to 175) & have not regained the weight. I got lazy & didn't want to do the work of recording food & exercise, but at this point, I'm now willing to do those things if it means I get back to a healthy energetic weight. Good luck!

contents: Since we aready have to limit or eliminate foods due to IC, it's like adding insult to injury to then monitor food intake to lose weight. Since I've already taken so many foods out of my diet, why can't I eat as much as I want of what's left ! I've always hated to exercise. But I've found having a buddy helps. I have a friend at work that I walk with. We try to do a half-hour before work and another half-hour at lunch. Fortunately, my IC symptoms usually allow me to walk this far without any problems or flares. The other thing I used to do was set up an exercise machine in front of a TV. It's much easier for me to actually get on the thing if I have something to occupy my mind and help pass the time. I was using a Health Rider machine, which was pretty low impact and didn't bother my bladder, but it now bothers my back from a different medical issue.

I felt like Shell for the first few years with IC. For me, that attitude led to overweight. It is very easy with IC to become inactive, very easy to cook with more fattening ingredients trying to get some flavor into the food, Elavil can increase the appetite & cause carbohydrate cravings. Within a year, I gained 40 pounds & they are now very hard to lose. I don't want to develop more health problems than I already have & the excess weight over time will do that. I was shocked when the research came out about women who have waists larger than 35" being at increased risk of heart attack & I measured mine & it was 40"! I'm now tired when I go up stairs and am wearing sizes 16-18. Monitoring food intake is as far as I'm concerned now, just part of trying to be as healthy as I can & trying to do what I can do about my own health. I tried to just "eat when hungry, stop when satisfied", maintained my weight, but couldn't lose any more. I didn't ask for IC, couldn't stop it, but hopefully I can prevent my dad's history from becoming mine. (He's 67, has had two seven bypass operations, one angioplasty, 2 heart attacks, 2 strokes). My grandmother also began having heart attacks in her 40s. I wish that I'd been more careful about my intake & monitoring my weight. If I'd never gained so much to begin with, I wouldn't have to struggle with a full blown diet now. I think Sarah is being wise to try to catch the problem now, before it becomes a real hassle.

contents: I'm also doing Weight Watchers because it allows me to eat whatever I want (including IC safe foods) as long as I count them. I had gotten down to my goal 3 weeks ago and now I'm gaining again. I had my meeting last night and am once again motivated to get back on track. Initially I was having a VERY hard time with it though. Like you said, it was like adding insult to injury. Okay you can't eat all these diet products that help curb your cravings (even seemingly harmless things like vinegar which is in all the low-cal salad dressings) and then you have to try and lose weight. I admit I sit in my meetings and look around at all the women who can drink a diet soda if they want to or have a cup of coffee and I think oh I miss those days! But it's gotten easier. Time really does heal. I made a joke that I broke up with my boyfriend Mr. Coffee and the pain is finally starting to relent a little.
signature: The combo of things that brought me out of remission were artificial sweetners, liquid vitamins with citric acid, tight pants, lower body workouts and stopping all my allergy meds cold turkey during height of allergy season. I wish I knew back then what I know now about IC First IC symptoms Spring of 2000; In-office cystoscopy 2002 -negative; Remission until 2007 (yeah!!); Negative PST spring of 2007; IC diagnosed via Cystoscopy and Hydrodistention 7/24/07; Another In-office cystoscopy 4/9/10 with biopsy showed inflammation Treatments for my IC/PFD: Elmiron 400 mg per day; walking, Zyrtec, Claritin, Singulair, Neurontin 100 mg per day; Ice packs and laying down; About to try Cytotec Tried and Failed: Heat (always makes me worse!), Heparin instills -too painful for my urethra; Atarax (interfered with my quality of sleep first time, second time dried my bladder out; Elavil makes me sick, lidocaine patches (just plain don't work), Cystoprotek (only tried a couple months but didn't seem to do much), Aloe Vera caps, pelvic floor therapy, trigger point work flared me worse, Lyrica (bad stomach pain) Other Diagnoses: PFD 2009; Fibromyalgia 2006, Osteopenia 2012, GERD 2006, Gastroparesis (delayed gastric emptying) Anxiety: Other medications: Ambien, Dexilant, Ibuprofren, Necon 1/35, Voltaren gel for tailbone BABY GIRL 10/28/08 *** BABY BOY 7/8/11

contents: Thank you for the suggestions ladies. I am going to check out that free online program, maybe that will help me to have it all organized for me. I have lost 3 pounds by not eating junk after I eat dinner. IE, no ice cream. I had to end my love affair with ice cream. Said a sad good bye and cursed him out too! LOl. I have a serious ice cream addiction. I have also decided to stop eating any processed foods and to make myself eat breakfast in the morning. I have a really hard time with breakfast. I am just not hungry in the mornings. But I can make myself eat if I have too. The exercise thing is harder for me. I have always been very athletic but hated just exercise, if that makes sense? I have to be doing something, playing a sport to be exercising or I get really bored. So my hubby and I have started surfing, which is really hard work, and I am biking and going for walks. Just starting really slow so I dont scare myself off! I will keep everyone posted on my progress, if you care. Maybe we can all encourage each other. Sarah
signature: Current meds; , effexor 37.5 mg 2 times a day, and lyrica 100 mg 3 times a day, lots of reading and snuggling with the pets!

contents: I started walking around March. I started out walking about 5 times a week for about an hour to hour and a half. I have lost about 25 pounds. Since it has been so hot where I live, we have definitely decreased our walking, but we are starting back up this week. I can tell that I am gaining a little back. I also stopped taking Lyrica which was a huge cause of weight gain for me mainly since it slowed down my metabolism so much. I have also found that walking has helped with my energy level! Like most of the others, I eat only when I am hungry and stop when I feel the slightest bit full. I also drink alot of water! Good luck!

I had the weight gain from neurontin and inactivity also. It's a nightmare. It just crept up on me while I was basically bedridden. I joke around that I went to bed for six months and woke up fat... but it's not funny. Since we decreased my neurontin dosage and added wellbutrin, the cravings are much reduced. The thing is, I LOVE exercise but everything gives me pain. Although I'm getting better with walking. I may join the old ladies at the Y for water exercise, I think that's the only class I might be able to keep up with! School starts for the kids on September 10, and then I have no more excuses. Wish me luck! As I wish good outcomes for all of you too.
signature: Je vous souhaite de la joie, de la bonne santée, et tout ce qu'il y a de bon dans la vie. Wishing you happiness and good health, and all the best out of life. Peace, Carolyn ___________________________________________________ Laura (11), Susannah (12 1/2) and Maman (that's me!), North Wildwood NJ, September 2007 On the Beach with IC

contents: Walking does take a while for my body to build back up to. Like I said we started back on Thursday. We walked for an hour, not too fast though. My friend and I mainly use it for talking, catching up and venting, so it's good for us in many ways! Last night, I was just too sore, but I will try again tonight. I think you will enjoy the pool exercise. I swim from time to time and it takes alot of energy! Mostly I just sit in my float and relax! It is funny that you say that you went to bed 6 months ago and woke up fat. That is exactly how I felt. It was like all of the sudden, I had just a couple of outfits that fit me and I was laying on the bed to put on the one pair of jeans that fit. That was my breaking point especially since the Neurontin and Lyrica really weren't working! I still have about 10 pounds to go, but when my bladder is flaring even a little, I still have that "IC" belly!

Rootbeer Have you guys tried Diet Rootbeer, it has ZERO calories and tastes exactly like regular Rootbeer when really cold. Now having said that, as long as you have no problem with Aspertaime as I think that is what is in Diet Rootbeer, but I switched from Regular Classic Coke to Diet Rootbeer and it is awesome. Diet 7-Up also tastes like regular 7-up.....the Diet Colas are gross!!

Just a heads up to those not familiar with the IC diet... Many, if not most, IC patients have trouble with sodas due to carbonation, acidic ingredients, preservatives & artificial sweeteners. If you are flaring & have been drinking sodas, eliminate them for several weeks & see how you are doing then. Even one of the beverages mentioned in the last post is likely to put me in a long-lasting sleep-depriving & uncomfortable flare... and as nice as they were to drink pre-IC, they are just so not worth it to me now...

contents: Sorry, I was assuming that the person writing about the Rootbeer was able to drink it since she said she was cutting down. Of course anyone should know that they should try the elimination diet before they drink lots of sodas. Sorry, should have clarified more, I wasn't meaning that ANYONE could drink Diet Rootbeer. I am a fortunate person and can drink all the Diet Rootbeer I want but other sodas might bother me. The earlier post person was worried about the calories in rootbeer so I was suggesting to 'her' to try Diet as it tastes just the same as regular with no calories. Sorry folks.

Hello ladies, Well I wanted to post a little message on my progress because I said that I would. I have lost 6 pounds so far! I lost most of that by cutting out all ice cream and cookies.It really wasnt that hard, as long as I dont have any at all. I also have been walking everyday. Somedays I dont feel good enough to go very far but I still go for a little while. I know I still have quite a bit too go, I want to lose 20 pounds in total but at least I have gotten started! I hate darn lyrica and effexor! Sarah

contents: I have a mild case of IC that is usually controlled fairly well by diet with periodic flares. I started riding my bike after a long break and couldn't figure out why I had such bad flares! I really don't want to give it up and am hoping to modify the bike to make it better. Does anyone have any experience with this? I was thinking of 1) getting a stem for the bike seat that was spring loaded to ease the jostling 2) getting a modified seat that wouldn't put so much wieght right over the bladder. I found this web site that has links at the bottom to all sorts of alternative bike seats- two cushions for each buttock, a curved moon shaped thing for the rearend, a lawn chair type arrangement, etc. Bicycle Seats Bike Saddles Explained by Jim Langley http://www.jimlangley.net/crank/bicycleseats.html bike, bicycle, cycle, bicycling, seats, saddles Has anyone tried these types of alternatives? Does anyone have any success with anything? Please advise! abigail Last edited by azugger; 08-15-2007, 05:21 PM. Reason: left out web address
If I ride on a smooth road, I do pretty well. I haven't ridden in a while, but am thinking of getting back to it. Donna

contents: Try the Spongy Wonder. It is a wonder! For years, I was in horrible pain trying to bike, and even got a recumbent before the seat alternatives were any good. Went on a nice long ride last weekend, and report that this really is a miracle seat. Take it from someone serious about biking!

contents: Many of the products I use have vitiman C and other acids. Do you think that affects IC. If so any ideas on okay products? I have dry skin.

Wow ,I never thought about that! I honestly don't know if lotions with acid etc. could affect IC. I do know that it is very common for IC patients to have dry/sensitive skin. I do, that's for sure! I also have sensitive skin "down there" and have to be careful with underwear, sanitary/bladder control pads, toliet paper, and so on. I use cocoa butter for dry skin and Dove moisturizer with sunscreen on my face. Because of all my medications I have to be very careful about being out in the sun, so sunscreen is a must for me! Hopefully someone a little more knowledgable will chime in! Sorry I wasn't more helpful....

contents: Hi, the only thing that I found that really helps my body is Lubriderm skin nourishing moisturizing Lotion with Shea and Cocoa butters in it. I have very sensitive skin and with alot of the other lotions, I will start itching and have to take it off.
I am super allergic to chocolate. Wonder if cocoa butter would be okay for me. Forgot about Lubriderm. Maybe some form would work. Thanks
I don't think so. It wouldn't penatrate the skin. Right? It's just used on the surface of your body and that's so far off from your bladder.
The Aveeno products are good. I use the lotion for dry skin in the winter and it helps a lot.
Ive been using H20 products for years. Ive been very happy. If you would like their web address, PM me. Good luck!

contents: I lost about twenty pounds last summer when I was at my worst and on the Elavil trial (obviously on placebo). I couldn't eat much and was taking Pyridium often, which makes me very nauseous. I had also found that exercise helped my pain, so I was very faithful in my gym attendance. Then, I went on Elavil, started being able to eat more, was too sleepy at first to get up to go to the gym at 5:00 like I had been doing and well, the 20 pounds are back. But, I feel 100% better, so I'm focusing on the positive and trying to get my diet and exercise back to where it should be!

When IC struck and I began the diet seriously, I began to lose weight. Before IC, I had gained a tremendous amount of weight because of poor eating habits I developed. With following the IC diet, I was eating very healthy foods and consciously choosing a very balanced diet. In the first 6-9 months, I lost between 50 and 60 pounds and went down many sizes. I maintained my new weight for almost a year, then added Elavil to my meds. BOOM!!! I went up almost 25 lbs in the first year of taking Elavil. I have leveled off, but am miserable with this extra weight. I desperately want to lose that 25 lbs, but am hitting a brick wall. I then developed ulcerative colitis which often makes people lose weight. Not me....the Elavil kept that from happening. Now my diet is heavy in carbs (my UC can't tolerate any dairy products, high fiber foods, salads, raw or just lightly steamed vegetables which were the basis of the healthy IC diet that helped me to lose before). Though my IC is under good control, my UC is a big challenge and I'm really struggling to get it under control. I just can't get the exercise or maintain the activity levels I need to lose again. I tried lowering my Elavil dosage, but that didn't work for me. It helps with both my IC and UC. So I seem, for now anyway, to be stuck between a very hard place and Elavil.

Work is highly overrated!!! ---My dear hubby ________________________________________________________ Never go to a doctor whose office plants are dead. ---Unknown

contents: Hmmmm, seems like we can't win either way. I had kept the weight off the first I think 5 years of the IC and then it crept up but my biggest problem is eating when I don't want to. Nerves and out of habit now. Annie2, was there a certain type of IC diet? I've seen many but most have a list of nothing to eat other then pears and blueberries. I just need a kick start at the eating right thing but you know I htink the mind goes along way with everything wrong with out bodies. Not right in the mind then you can't really work on anything that needs to be done. I know for me I'm constanly think all the time about being fat, in pain and how I feel like my life is nothing. No good thoughts ever really. Last edited by waterflow; 07-31-2007, 02:17 PM. Reason: typo again

I'm doing the plan on www.sparkpeople.com It's free, very flexible for us with our diet restrictions, there are messageboards like here, and tons of articles & motivational things to keep me positive. I had gained 35 pounds due to IC inactivity, Elavil & emotional overeating (nothing like IC to make a person unhappy & want to eat over it). In 6 months, I went from 140 to 175 pounds. I felt really awful. But on sparkpeople, I lost 12 pounds in 2 months. This was 2 years ago. I stopped doing it because I got tired of logging my food everyday & decided to do an "eat when hungry/stop when satisfied/use prayer & a bible study program that supports that method" approach. It worked ok to maintain my weightloss from sparkpeople, but I've not lost another pound since then. I'm not sure my body signals are reliable enough with the Elavil to really use that method. And after 2 years, I'm still right where I was--163. So, I've decided that logging my food isn't such a bad thing if it helps me, and I'm so unhappy about the weight gain that I'm willing to try it again. The sparkpeople program calculated how many calories per day (1250-1550) & how much exercise (can be just walking) I need per week (1/2 hour 4 times a week) to lose 28 pounds by my birthday next March. I'll be 40 & don't really want to go into that decade overweight. So, I'm going to do this for 3 months. I figure in 90 days I'll know if it's worked or not & whether to continue. So, 6 days down, 84 to go Check out the site, it's pretty amazing!

contents: I have gained 20 lbs. in the last year and a half .The amitriptilyne is abig reason and the other million things I'm on. Next question is anyone else EXTREMELY CONSTIPATED? Candy

Mary, The IC diet I followed is the same one found in the Patient Handbook here. I started by eating only items in the Usually Ok Column and later, as my bladder calmed down, began trying foods in the May Be Ok Column and learned my own diet triggers. Before IC, I ate a lot of high calorie snacks, prepared foods and drank 1 soda almost every day. I am short and eating that way packed the pounds on me. The IC diet is really a very healthy diet. Yes, the fruits are limited, but you can have lots of veggies and salads, meats and fish and poultry as well as balanced portions of carbs (rice, pasta with cream sauces instead of tomato, potatoes, cereals and bread) and milk & eggs as well as some soft cheeses. The key for me was planning......I had to THINK about what I would eat every day and plan each meal instead of eating lots of quick and easy (and not very healthy) prepared foods. Now, because of colitis, I can no longer tolerate all the veggies and those I eat have to be cooked til they are practically mush. High fiber foods are a killer when it comes to colitis as are dairy products. I haven't had a salad in well over a year (almost 2 yrs)........oh how I miss fresh salads and veggies! I am pretty much left with white bread, rice, potatoes, eggs, bland meats, fish and poultry and a few vegetables that I have to cook until they are very soft.....oh, and sugar cookies. Lots of sugar cookies. When I started the IC diet I absolutely HATED IT!!! I felt so restricted and missed my tea and soda and aged cheeses and wine, tomato and chocolate, pizza etc. Now what I miss most are all the healthy veggies and salads with olive oil dressing, cottage, cream and mozzarella cheese and milk and yummy vanilla ice cream with caramel sauce. And did I say veggies? My goal now is to get my colitis under good enough control that I can go back to eating all those healthy veggies again!

contents: Hi there! I've gained a ton of weight in 5 years (about 100 pounds) - and it's depressing. I had started gaining weight before I was ever on the Elavil. I was resistant to taking it, but my urologist thought it would be best. Despite my hesitation, I took it anyways - and it really helped. About a year and a half ago, I was doing so well, we decided to see if I could try not taking it as it might help with the weight issue. Unfortunately, I became extremely miserable and learned I couldn't go without. I'm back on it and despite any dieting efforts I just can't lose weight. It's a very hard thing for me. I'm hoping to be able to get this weight off sometime, I'm just not sure how. Best of luck to everyone, Jennifer

contents: Weight gain for me too Hey there, have also gained heaps since being on Tofranil (Imipramine). Am very unhappy with it - about 10kg in 3 yrs. Had 2 babies in 12 months but only started gaining recently. Was quite slim after number 3 but the pounds just keep on creeping on. Am not holding baby weight but am just plain overweight. I hate it and my GP says, excercise!!! Does she not understand that I live in the moment and find moving really difficult - never mind excercising! Have heard other antidpresseants can make you drop weight but none are used for the treatment of IC!!

I gained initially, but can proudly say I have now lost 55 lbs and kept it off. Only 15 more to go and I will be down to what I weighed at my wedding(his loss)...I haven't really been able to exercise yet, most of the weight loss has been cutting the junk out of my diet. Once the bladder is better, I think I can tone everything up. Hugs, Barb
