contents: has anyone had any luck working their abs? I DESPERATELY need it!!!!! I saw a video called hip hop abs but I am scared to try it.

I can't do even the tiniest bit of ab work. I can't even work my lower body (legs) without going into a flare. However I seem to be more sensitive to activity then a lot of people because I hear about people running (ouch!) and doing pilates which is all core work so I don't see how they can do it. I think it depends also on how crampy and inflammed you feel generally in your pelvic area and the only way to know is to try. Try doing a few crunches a couple nights a week and if you do okay then you'd probably be okay with the video.
signature: The combo of things that brought me out of remission were artificial sweetners, liquid vitamins with citric acid, tight pants, lower body workouts and stopping all my allergy meds cold turkey during height of allergy season. I wish I knew back then what I know now about IC First IC symptoms Spring of 2000; In-office cystoscopy 2002 -negative; Remission until 2007 (yeah!!); Negative PST spring of 2007; IC diagnosed via Cystoscopy and Hydrodistention 7/24/07; Another In-office cystoscopy 4/9/10 with biopsy showed inflammation Treatments for my IC/PFD: Elmiron 400 mg per day; walking, Zyrtec, Claritin, Singulair, Neurontin 100 mg per day; Ice packs and laying down; About to try Cytotec Tried and Failed: Heat (always makes me worse!), Heparin instills -too painful for my urethra; Atarax (interfered with my quality of sleep first time, second time dried my bladder out; Elavil makes me sick, lidocaine patches (just plain don't work), Cystoprotek (only tried a couple months but didn't seem to do much), Aloe Vera caps, pelvic floor therapy, trigger point work flared me worse, Lyrica (bad stomach pain) Other Diagnoses: PFD 2009; Fibromyalgia 2006, Osteopenia 2012, GERD 2006, Gastroparesis (delayed gastric emptying) Anxiety: Other medications: Ambien, Dexilant, Ibuprofren, Necon 1/35, Voltaren gel for tailbone BABY GIRL 10/28/08 *** BABY BOY 7/8/11

contents: I saw that infomercial and wanted to buy it but it was too expensive for me. I do cardio work. I run around by my house 3 times a week if it's not raining or i feel up to it. Did you buy hip hop abs? It looks like it works and it's fun. Let me know how it goes. Charisse is right, you never know if your going to hurt yourself by working out. Everyone's body is different and you gotta try for yourself. I hope everything works out for ya.
signature: ***Rachel*** Dance like no one is watching Love like you've never been hurt Live today like it's your last Dxd with IC in June '06

contents: I love to rollerblade and this company offers rollerblading tours, meals, hotels included, in different beautiful parts of the US and Europe that are conducive to this sport. This is the one exercise besides swimming that doesn't cause me (too) much additional pain. I've always wanted to do this...I live in the flatlands of south Texas where the only shade is cactus, palm trees, and the occasional mesquite tree. Would love to see more of the US this way,especially a place with some small hills, curves and shade trees. Anyone ever taken one of these tours? TIA will probably go alone, if I go, but want to start saving up for next summer.

I weighed myself tonight and I weigh 180! I have been eating just about non-stop and stopped exercising sooooo before I weigh 200 I am putting my foot down tomorrow and start the diet once again. Gee, I only have to lose 60 pounds now!

UUUUGGGGHHHHH welcome to the club, I was doing really well until my birthday last month and every since I have been eating like crap. I dont even want to get on the scale I know I will just cry. I am just going to get back on the wagon, and weigh myself at a later date. Maybe when I have working out and eating well for about 1 month. Good luck, I know I have had problems with the weight my WHOLE LIFE. It is so awful. Oh well, good luck with your adventure. You can do it
Weight loss articles by Dr. Mehmet Oz, doctor resource on Oprah show. Check them out! http://www2.oprah.com/tows/pastshows...20061102.jhtml

contents: Not sure how well this will work but it says you eat 3 servings of fruit every two hours and then you have supper. I'll have to re-read it because I don't remember it saying anything about eating breakfast or lunch. You are supposed to lose 1 pound a day. Think I will try it starting tomorrow. MIght have to re-do it a little but will see how it goes. ANyone else ever read about this diet and done it?
Becareful, to many of the wrong fruits could cause your bladder to flare and then you might not feel so well.
If I were to eat that much fruit, my problem would be diarrhea --- I'd have to end up eating in the bathroom. Donna

contents: I don't think I'd ever want to see a pear again after a few days on that diet!* For me, my only IC-safe fruits are pears, dates, raisins, coconut. Dates, raisins & coconut are not low calorie foods, so I think this diet would be a disaster for me. Not sure I'd want to spike my blood sugar like eating only fruit all day would do either. (*Of course, if I ever go into remission from IC, I won't eat another pear as long as I live.) Given our limited diets, limiting it further just sounds depressing. What I've been doing lately that's working is that I'm eating normally, but cutting my portions in half & increasing my vegetables. And I'm taking 2 walks a day instead of one. No dramatic fast weight loss, but about 1/2 pound a week.

contents: Even though I'm not extemely diet sensitive, I know what my triggers are. As for fruit, as long as I don't even them as a snack or on an empty stomach, I am usually ok... if I eat them with boiled eggs or another proten, or a carb.WHen I eat some fruits alone or on an empty stomcah, I will for sure have pain afterward. But I feel fruits as an intergral part of my diet, so will do whatever it takes to keep the pain away.

I don't think I could do that diet because eating too much fruit bothers my bladder too. Having one a day seems to be ok.....as long as it's not strawberries, oranges, bananas etc (all the good ones!)
signature: Double Spinal Cord Stimulator surgery 8/09 Unsuccessful MiniArc sling surgery 12/07 Dx'd Hypothyroid Dx'd Chronic Axonal Neuropathy & Myopathy June 2007 Dx'd IC May 2006 (after suffering for 25+ yrs!) contents: Hi Guys! I've seen a lot of you mention Spark People before, so I finally signed up. I haven't made any buddies on there yet (it's only been a week or so) but I was thinking maybe we could make this a sticky for people's Spark pages? That way we can all be on eachother's friends list for support and whatnot. I've found that I'm really satisfied with the site. It's AMAZING how many carbs I was consuming. Sheesh! But I love it because I can pick and choose what I'm eating - it's not a strict diet. They do have meal plans on there, but I think it would be too difficult for many of us. Anyway, here's my link: http://sparkpeople.com/mypage.asp?id=RivetKitty There's not much up there, well, there's really nothing up there but I'll change it soon. I'm really hoping that this works for once. I'm world's worst dieter, and IC has made me erm, kinda more than chubby. So we'll see. Anyway, good luck everyone and come say hullo!!
signature: Kitty! Just diagnosed w/PFD - Oh Boy! What I have tried: Elmiron, Lyrica, ,Methadone, Vicodin, Norco, Cystoprotek, imipramine, xanax, hydroxizine, trazadone, a bunch of anti-spasmotics and more I'm sure I can't remember. What I'm presently on: Noritriptyline 25 mg OxyContin 20 mg AM - 10 mg afternoon - 20 mg PM Zanaflex 2 mg at bedtime Percocet PRN for breakthrough Zyrtec Nasonex (allergies) Jolessa (b.c.) Physical Therapy 2x weekly Be my buddy? http://www.facebook.com/home.php?#/p...88&ref=profile

contents: I am going back to the 1,200 calories a day diet......again ......have used this diet since the 80's. It works but I am really tired of having to calculate calories again but need to. haven't lost one pound since Wednesday. Think I should have lost as least ONE!

Hey! Thought you weren't going to weigh yourself for a while!!!! I still haven't for fear that I'll get frustrated. I probably eat about 3000 calories a day, which I know is way to much! I tried sticking to 1600 and was able to do it for a couple of weeks, but then I just couldn't do it anymore. I'm trying now to really focus when I eat. Take really small bites and chew 20 times and all that. Worked last night. I ate really slowly and was full with just a half a sub sandwich, I didn't even want dessert later-really odd for me! I'm trying to savor my food more cause usually I'm in a mad rush just to get it into my mouth!

Yeah, I know I wasn't going to weigh myself but it was just too tempting. Probably should take the scale out of my bedroom. From the book I read you multiply the weight you want to be by 12 and that gives you the total amount of calories you are allowed to weight that much. 1,200 is way below the amount I am allowed but if I can stick to it I will lose 10 pounds the first week. The hard part is STICKING TO IT! One good calorie lunch is a dill pickle sandwich. You can eat the whole jar of dill pickles and still come ahead of the game. Wouldn't actually suggest eating a whole jar of pickles. You might turn green. You're doing the best part with exercisine. I am going to start big time with that again tomorow. I used to walk at least 3 hours a day and I'm going to start shooting for that. Never and I mean never did I sit as much as I have been doing that last few years. Actually, with the computer came the sitting. NEver had a computer and didn't know how to run one before the IC. Are you still walking 1/2 hour on your machine?

Hi, What kind of food intake are you using for 1200 calories a day???? I am stuck at a certain weight, but I know it is because of the carbs I eat. Using the IC diet is a pain. Do you have any ideas????
signature: Endometriosis,Fibromyalgia, Severe Adhesions- 3 c-sections, Particial Hysterecotmy,Laporoscopy for cysts, Appendectomy, R-ovary removed, IBS, Acid Reflux, Asthma, and the worst of all IC Patty

contents: Mary, It isn't wise to try to lose ten pounds in any week. It's unhealthy and even if you can do it, it's usually short lived and only water weight. It comes back on fast with a bunch of friends also. Excercise, lots of water, and eating whole grains and fruits and vegetables, along with legumes, and getting most of your sleep before midnight is the answer.
signature: http://www.TheCraftyEwe.etsy.com

contents: I've read that if you restrict your calories that much all you will lose is water and much needed muscle. You just need to change your lifestyle, eat healthier and start walking. I've been strength training for the last ten years and its a Godsend in more ways than one.

I have read that to determine how many calories you should eat, you should multiply your ideal weight times 12 to 15, depending on how active you are. And if you are trying to lose weight, you shouldn't reduce that number by more than 10% to 15%, again depending on how active you are. Donna

contents: Trishann, I'm doing the slim fast shakes for breakfast and lunch. Think it makes it easier getting in the 1,200 calories. Then I have a snack (anything really) in the afternoon and then supper. I've done this diet lots of times and it helps to kick start it for me. You do multiply what you want to weigh by 12 and that gives you your total number of calories. Got out the water cup measure too today and going to try and get in my 8 glasses of water.

my appetite has dropped off considerably since I started taking topamax. The last few weeks have been extremely stressful on top of it, so I have no idea how many calories I've consumed. I've never been a calorie counter. I've tried to eat vegetables and fruit, but since IC it's hard to find enough of either of them that I like and can eat on the diet. Then the water thing is out of hte question. I can just about live with 3 glasses of water - meaning the trips to the bathroom. If I did more than that I might as well set up my office in the restroom and work from there. I'd never get out. I truly think that is part of the weight gain problem too.

Hey Ladies The safe calorie intake for a women is no less than 2000 a day. anything less than this should only be for a short amount of time so if you are having only 1200 a day (very low!!) you will be missing out on a lot of nutritian and will probably lose a lot of water weight which unfortunately goes quickly back on! I would NOT recommned eating this many calories as its just not good! Just ensure you get lots of fruit, veg, water and do a little exercise (if you can manage it with you IC)! Good luck
signature: Started with symptoms 2004 after a severe infection. Was diagnosed with IC Feb 2006 after cystoscopy. Diagnosed with vulvodyina and PN in july 2006. Dignosed with ME and IBS Oct 2006. Currently taking Amytriptaline 50mg, regular pain killers and birth control! Also doing IC diet and regular use of heat packs. Still struggling with IC and controling my symptoms so looking for some help also recently had abnormal pap, CIN III and just had LEEP done 2007

contents: For me to weigh 120 I can have 1,500 calories but it is only if I am active. The more I sit the less calories I need. 2,000 would be way too many for me. The shorter the person the less calories too. I only put the weight back on after this diet due to the pills I was taking for the IC and now with being older it seems to be harder to get off. The other times I put the weight back on was due to stress and over eating. Other wise this diet plan worked. SrMaggie, I could only get 6 cups of water in. Seems all the bladder can handle. I heard that Topomax can be used as a diet pill. Are you having any side effects from it?

I really don't count calories anymore, I know I do watch what I eat. (sometimes though there are days that I eat more than I should. But no matter what I eat, I can't gain weight. Part of it is probably the Topamax that I'm on - think its keeping me at my current weight, but would like to gain some back that I lost last year. What I weigh now, I haven't weighed since my firstborn was a baby and that was 23 years ago. (last year I lost 35lbs, due to illness and really haven't been able to gain any of it back.)
I eat anywhere from 800 to 1,000 calories per day. I can't eat much because everything I eat tends to nauseate me.
Since my IC appeared I am eating about 1000 calories a day. Sometimes a bit more if my tummy can handle it.

You might look at www.sparkpeople.com It's a free weight loss website, very positive. Has meal plans or you can just record your own food & it gives you the calorie count & nutrition analysis of whatever you set it up for. Like I figure we IC patients are likely to be short on vit. C, so I set it up to track that. And also calcium since osteoporosis runs in the family. What I really like is it has a healthy living checklist you can do every day & it tells you how healthy your choices were that day (diet, exercise, sleep, water intake). It also has message boards, including one on chronic illness & there have been several IC patients posting there. Good luck! (I just cleaned out the pantry of almost all junk foods. I kept the 100 cal packs of pretzels & the small packs of Inka chips (roasted plantain chips-yummy!) - I figured with the heat wave having a few small servings of salty snacks around is not a bad idea).

contents: sparkpeople Kadi---I just went to that website. I had never heard of it before! Wow ! ! ! It is soooo cool! Thanks so much for telling us about it. It's going to be my new addiction. I can't wait to spend more time on it and play around with it.
signature: Lee Ann Current Rx Meds: Atarax, Ditropan, Elmiron Prior to pregnancy: The above 3 meds PLUS Neurontin, Topamax, Loratadine, continuous OrthoCyclen, Lidocaine Patches PRN, Temazepam PRN, & Vicodin PRN Hooray for babies! Misc. lifesavers: Hot baths, ThermaCare Heat Wraps, Ice Packs. The IC Diet has changed my life. Didn't work for me: Detrol LA, Amitryptiline, Morphine, Percocet, TENS unit, Interferential Pain Stimulator Unit, Hypogastric Plexus Nerve Block IC (Mod-Severe) since 1996 UPDATE: 5/21/08 Pregnancy and breastfeeding afterwards have alleviated my IC symptoms more than anything, EVER. Most days are 100% pain free & I now have normal frequency (as long as I take these 3 meds). My little sweetie! Jack weighed 9 lbs 12 oz and was born via c-section on Feb. 28, 2008...

contents: help I am not sure how to put a new thread on here so I thought I would reply to this one since it is a bit similar. I have been trying to watch my weight lately too. I was just diagnosed with IC. My doctor has not been helpful at all but the pamphlet he gave me was. I am now taking elmiron, and have been gaining weight. i also have found that I am having a hard time changing my diet. I have always considered myslef an ok healthy person when it came to eating with a few indiscretions here and there. But after reading aobut what not to eat i have found that my fruits and veggies have been hurting me. So now I don't know what to eat and have been stealing kids cookies becasue i am always hungry. I thought I would start to keep a food journal but i cheat and even started walking on MOnday, it when no further, On tuesday I was in severe pain I guess i cannot due sit ups and a 20 minute walk. I am so frustrated with the weight gain and the pain. Any and all advice will help. I just feel like there is n ot hing I can do. Another issue going on is becasue of the sever pain I have been in my husband and I have not been able to be intimate in 6months. ANd yes I have tried lubricants and they have not helped. I am beyond frustrated!!

Elmiron will do that and so will IC. I had got up to 215 from 135 until just recently starting Topamax and within 2 months I lost 60 pounds! We figure it was all water weight though because my blood pressure is down and everything. I feel great and can exercise and do so much more now!
signature: http://www.TheCraftyEwe.etsy.com

contents: I try to eat 1200 cals per day, too. Any more than that and I gain weight, thanks to Elavil giving me the metabolism of a slug!
signature: I have: Interstitial Cystitis,Eustacian Tube Dysfunction, IBS, Sciatica. I take: Elavil 50 mgs, Balziva birth control, and Elmiron instillations. I am: Not my disease. My Myspace Page: http://profile.myspace.com/index.cfm...iendid=4249067

contents: Moonheart, have you had any problems with the topomax? I've been reading how a lot of people loose so much weight with it and then there is a web site where people post their experiences and some end up like zombies for life or had really bad side effects. I have an awful time with side effects from pills so kind of scared to try it but would be so nice not to be fat anymore. I'm tired of crying over it and thinking about calories all the time. Do you have problems taking pills too? That is sooooo great you dropping all those pounds! :woohoo: Congrats! :woohoo:

I tried the sparkpeople web site too. Everyting was going good until someone complained about the post I had made. It didn't fit their group they thought and I didn't think it was right to attack me about it so I haven't been back. Some people can be very pushy.

Sarajo, You may want to check out to see if there are other problems like pelvic floor dysfunction and vulvadynia. I went to a new doctor that ran some other tests and got me into PT. The physical therapist told me that the muscles around my pelvic area (stomach, back, and inner thighs) so so tight it was like I had a body suit on 2-3 sizes too small. Anyway, my point is that I thought it was ok to do pilates and ok to do crunchs and it was like one of the worst things for me. Also I had the same problem with you and the fruits and vegs because oh no we can't eat hardly and fruits and vegs. . I gained weight the 4 years I have had IC because of all the carbs I eat. I have started eating one pear and 1 cup of blueberries a day and try to eat some veggies like carrots and other things that are on the list. I hope this helps. waterflow, I don't know how you drink those slimfast shakes with all the preservs and crap that they put in them. That would cause me the most painful flair. Krisi

weight Thanks everyone for your advice. I am wondering if you all see a urologist or a gynocologist. My urologist is not helpful at all and I am wondering if I need to see a new gynocologist so maybe I can get so me answers as to why I am in pain when I execrcise?

Hello everyone, I wanted to write about my experiences so far after joining a gym. My brother died in September and I gained a whopping 40 pounds from depression--I sat around at ate for a few months straight. I rarely left my house and when I did I had no energy to exercise. So now, almost a year later, I'm 5'10" and weigh 190 pounds. I used to have a cute body and around 150 was the perfect weight for me because I'm tall. I can't stand all the weight I've gained and now I'm ready to take it off. I've noticed my belly is the biggest problem area but I also ended up with stretch marks all over my thighs and hips, and a few on my stomach. So I joined a great gym last week and have gone twice. I'm finding I do really well with the elliptical, stairmaster, and weights. I also like to use the track for powerwalking. I have a few questions though: 1) I tried out the pool and it was fantastic. Low impact on my body, felt great, and I love to swim. Only problem is, my bladder has been suffering for 4 days now, due to the chlorine I imagine. I have found a few pools that do not seem to irritate my bladder, but many do. I want to continue the swimming but since it's hurting afterward, should I skip this one all together? Or is there anything else I can do? 2) The treadmill, as I have read in some of your posts, seems to be a problem. Same with bikes. Anyone have suggestions on what machines work best to avoid pelvic/bladder pain? The ones I've listed have been good to me so far. What works for you all? Thanks so much everyone! It's going to be tough, but I'm finally motivated and I hope I can get back to my normal weight...I'd say...by the end of the year? :woohoo:

Comes into us at midnight very clean. It's perfect when it arrives and it puts itself in our hands. It hopes we've learned something from yesterday." John Wayne

contents: Any kind of exercise done on a regular basis is good. It does sound like you're on the right track. I'm very sorry about your brother; I lost my brother ten years ago and I miss him so very much. Sending gentle hugs, Donna

contents: Hi Allison, I'm sorry about your brother, it is so hard to lose a loved one. I've also found the gym helpful, physically & emotionally. I can use the elliptical machine (the one that's legs only/ no arms) & the rowing machine. Some weight machines are ok. And my physical therapist created a mat workout for me with exercises for the pelvic floor & back. I don't swim, hate chlorine, suspect my bladder wouldn't like it, but-- some IC patients who do swim try to minimize the effects of the chlorine by not sitting around in their swimsuit afterwards & voiding right afterwards to try to get the chlorine out of their bladder ASAP. Some IC patients are ok with this, some still flare, so it's one of those individual things... Let us know how things go, ok?

contents: I've found that riding a recumbent bike is easier on the vulvodynia than an upright bike. The treadmill doesn't bother me, except that I find it a crashing bore. I don't swim much, but my gym has a warm-water pool for physical therapy that can be used by any member, not just therapy patients. The temperature is about 93 degrees, so it's warmer than the lap pool but not nearly as hot as a hot tub. I can spend a half-hour in the warm water pool without a problem, and since it's warmer than the lap pool, I wouldn't be surprised if they have to use more chemicals than in the lap pool, since organisms love the warm water. Someone mentioned applying vaseline to create a water barrier, and I've also read that some women use diaper cream (Desitin, I believe) which also is a great barrier.

Thanks to everyone for the responses. Briza, you are so lucky to live by the ocean. I miss living near it and I hope to live by a beach when I graduate college. I live in Colorado and I hate it it here. I need some saltwater! I've found that it doesn't hurt my bladder at all, and I'm sure it could even be good for me. But as for the pool, some bother me and some don't. Most do but the one at the gym really kills me! Even if I take my suit off right away and pee and drink a lot of water, I can feel it for days afterward. But the vaseline is an excellent idea! Has anyone tried this and had any luck with it? Thanks again.

contents: I have been doing lap swim, and each time afterward, I would start hurting at my lower right abdomial where the leg does meet. Do any one have this problem? I was thinking maybe it is just a pull muscle but then I wonder if I am pulling the muscle to my bladder to much. Surely this can't be because of the hip can it? I don't have problems with the chlorine just the soreness of the abdomial part. For some strange reason I am doing better in the colder water swimming then I am in the warmer water. Now that puzzles me? Can you believe it, it helps my whole body aches except for the one area. Take care, Trishann
Maybe slow done how much you are doing and then work your way back up. I do water aerobics and enjoy it.
signature: Interstitial Cystitis Stomach problems diastolic dysfunction vascular insufficency of left leg pelvid floor disorder hypothroidisim asthma bread cancer - 5yrsl. remission swollowing difficulty fatigue lots of doctors lots of med. Keep your eyes on the Lord and he will direct your paths.

contents: Thank you, maybe that is what it is, doing to much at one time. My daughter just taught me how to lap swim so I could be over doing it without realizing it. It is just I am enjoying it so much, and I am taking advantage of it because I don't have good days all the time. It just feels good to be able to do sometimes. I really didn't think I could do this but I am doing it, even though it is not a everyday thing. Some days it is just to much pressure on the bladder and then some days it is not bad. Do you have those days to in the water where it is to much pressure on the bladder? I hoping this is building the muscles around the bladder and it will get better in time. Sorry about all the question, I just wonder if you have that pressure in the bladder at times when you are in the water but does it get better. I am just happy I can do it when I can and hoping this pain isn't going to be a major problem and in time it will get better. Thank you so much for responding. I am happy that you are enjoying your water exercise too. Take care, Trishann

Trishann I wixh I could help, but I have no idea.. Maybe you are working to hard in the water.. I know I seem to over do it in the pool because everything seems light while I am in there.. I love to get in our pool. I have no pain while I am in it and no pressure..Good luck..((((((((hugs)))))))) Ronda Last edited by leelee88; 06-20-2007, 02:00 AM.

contents: Ronda thank you for letting me know. I will just continue to do it unless it gets to unbearable. Maybe it is something that will work itself out. I will give it some time and try going slower with it. I am surprised that alot of IC'ers don't have problems with the water pushing against the lower abdominal causing pressure on the bladder. Maybe it is just because of the condition of my bladder. But I really thought all IC'er have this problems, was I wrong. Thanks Ronda for letting me know. We really do learn from each other. Take care, Trishann
I swim alot, and the reason I don't have pain there, is because I hate to say this, but i pee in the pool. Sorry

Akrose, I definitely understand why you do. Getting in and out of the pool so many times get so embarrassing that I give up for that day. But I keep going because I do have better days that aren't so bad. I can't just keep quiting everytime something is embarrassing and hard on me, if I do I would be doing nothing. I am glad you are enjoying the pool too. It is good to hear others keeps pressing in to and not giving up. Thank for letting me know. I hope you continue to enjoy yourself. Take care, Trishann

I look forward to the pool and getting sun at least 3xwk, especially after a work day. the cool water feels really good. i backstroke about 22 lengths and fell real calm after that. the hardest thing for me is sleeping, i use an ice pack to try to reduce the urgency to urinate, then i count the days till august 17, 2007, when the feeling of urgency will be gone. Eventually after some meds I fall asleep. Have to sleep on a couch, can't lay flat on a bed. thanks trishann MSM gel did not relieve urethral burning for me (from Jacob's lab) aileen

I love swimming and am eagerly waiting for our pool to be warm enough. Hopefully very soon....... We used to have a sign by our pool that said, "There is no P in our OOL --- please keep it that way." When we got our new one, I couldn't find another sign. Donna

contents: Aileen I hope the cystectomy and the urethrectomy surgery will bring ALL that you hope for, especially from all the pain and suffering you have been through. Of what I have been hearing after the healing process you will be able to still swim, now that is great you will still be able to still swim, and hopefully even be able to enjoy it more. Not getting enough sleep I do understand, pain pills somewhat helps alittle with it. It is so hard when you don't get enough sleep, your body just can't function normally. Hope you find something that can help you sleep, at least to the day of your surgery, so you can get some comfort. Take care, Trishann

Donna hope your pool will warm up fast so you can enjoy it. I remember as a little girl we had a pool. My brother was a fireman so he used the fire hose to fill it up. My family and friends would go in the pool and swim and I did try. But the water was so bone cold, it hurt my body, so I waited for weeks until it warm up. That water was freezing cold. ENJOY YOUR POOL AND HAVE A NICE SUMMER, Take care, Trishann
You know Donna, if you encouraged people to pee in the pool it would warm up much faster. I'm sorry, I couldn't help it. No offense to Aileen.
signature: I was born with one ureta not attached to it's kidney, I've been poked and prodded for so long. I never had "private" parts. contents: Do people here exercise everyday or just every other day?
Our pool is in a plastic greenhouse type dome building, with just enough room at one end to walk around a little --- and it's nice and warm now! Donna

contents: I love you Donna! I go to water aerobics 5 days a week, sometimes in the morning, sometimes at night. I don't know if it is doing anything yet or not, I just started 2 months ago. Sometimes I feel like it is just a stress reliever and that is fine with me. I take enough pills. Last week we had a different instructor and I actually had pain in my thighs for the first time since my trial week. For some reason I can make the whole hour without having to pee, I even get down time in the hot tub after. At home it seems like I can never go a whole hour without peeing unless I am intentionally holding an instillation. Weird.

contents: I couldn't see a forum specifically for this, and I know there was a new post recently. I've been trying to reach United Airlines to have them let me board early and to be "sensitive" to my condition (i.e., allowing me to use the restroom if the plane is delayed, etc.). I have a doctor's note about the IC and the need to use the restroom. I spoke to them (and got nowhere). The woman said to take my note the day of the flight (already stressful, when I'll be searching for restrooms) and MAYBE they'd consider it. Then I e-mailed them, and haven't received a reply in a week. I really hate to drive to the airport ahead of time, but I'm wondering if I should after the July 4th holiday. Have people had any success with this, any avenues I should follow or suggestions? Thanks, Waves
signature: Waves Diagnosed 6/4/07, also IBS, migraines, allergies, hysterectomy, previous fibroids, cysts. Help measures in process -- hope to start PT, etc. Main pain meds: HEAT, Tylenol, Alleve, Working on diet! Cold infusion of Marshmallow Root tea 2x day, Citrus free Quercetin 2x day I am not my illness -- it's just a facet, I aim to enjoy life and pursue my dreams! I hope it makes me a more compassionate human being.

contents: Several times I have not been very successful with airlines until I have stated that my condition is considered a disability & I have documentation of that. After that, the representatives have become much more pleasant & helpful. When you call the airlines, ask specifically to speak with someone in charge of working with disabled travellers. That might help.

contents: I fly pretty frequently and I've really never run into a problem with having to use the restroom. I just get up whenever I need to and the flight attendents have never said anything to me and I have never even mentioned my disability. I usually like to board last, that way I'm not sitting on the plane so long and I run to the restroom one more time while everyone else is boarding. If your worried about using the restroom on the plane if it's delayed or anything, do you have the restroom access card? I would just show the flight attendent that if she gave you any problems. Try not to worry to much about it. Kari
signature: Kari I'm 47 years old, married 27 years. I have two wonderful boys and two wonderful grandchildren. I was diagnosed in 1994. Life has certainly thrown me many many surprises, all of which I'm trying to stay positive and hopeful, and I try to think about my blessings not my misfortunes, when possible. Stay Strong!

contents: Waves, I think it is great you are planning ahead. Make sure you get an aisle seat and don't let anyone push you out of it because you are a woman. It makes it easier to get to the bathroom as needed or get up and walk around. Below is information about United's Customer Relations Dept. A note from the doc even if it is just on a prescription pad is helpful, but if not take some medical records. Also, as suggested in a previous post using the word disability helps because there is the Americans with Disability Act (ADA), which gives people certain rights. The airlines don't want to get sued, so you will see customer service change for the better when mentioning "disability" or "ADA". http://faq.ua2go.com/display/4/kb/browse/cat.asp?tab=browse&kb=1&c=12&cpc=WnaqDcP5KrMu623S1OA64DwwrR4UyhK6fv2ylkXXS&cid=1&r=0.3142969 Contact United Customer Relations with: Praise or compliments Comments or feedback Complaints Questions regarding past date travel Phone 1-877-228-1327 Available 8 a.m.-7 p.m. CST Mon-Fri If something goes wrong, let us know (You could call this number if the one above doesn't do you any good. Southwest has always been great with me, but some of the regular airlines can't seem to understand a disability that doesn't include a wheelchair or cane...) If one of our airport Complaint Resolution Officials (CRO) cannot assist you, please contact us via e-mail by clicking on the "Contact United" link at united.com, or by calling toll free 877-228-1327. I wish you the best and hope this information gets to you before your flight. They are first number is only open during the week. Safe travels, ads The ICN also has business size cards which explain IC and request you be put to the front of the line. I have multiple ones when traveling, so they are easy to reach. Last edited by ads; 07-01-2007, 06:42 AM. Reason: added P.S. info

Thanks to everyone for their help Thanks to everyone for your suggestions. I did get aisle seats way back before I was diagnosed (because of course I always had to go . . .). But I will try dealing with them re the disability route. Thank you for all of the numbers and encouragement.

contents: I have flown alot. Just before I do fly I have my Drs. note. I get there 2 hours before Boarding. Go to sky Cap and show them the Note. They get me a wheel chair and take me to security and the Chair also has to go through Security. Then I get back in the Cahir. When itis time to Pre-Board they come and Get me First. They also say if you need to Pre-Board Please come when they call . I have a little suit case with my ostomy stuff extra Pantys and Meds. Also when the Plane Lands that all Regular Passangers Get off First that way the Pre Board People can get off Real slow. Then I usually have someone with a wheelChair waiting for me to take me to my Next stop. Than do the same thing with Pre Boarding again. I use to have to Fly from Michigan to Duke Hospital in Durham NC. The Hotel would have a Taxie waiting for me When I landed at the airport. Just when you Get to the Airport just go to skycap and tell them what you neeed and they will help you. The People that do the Wheel chirs Even to me to Mac donalds or What ever I needed just Let them know. I hope that this works. DebbieD

Thanks to all but still having hassles with airlines Your ideas sound good, but I worry a bit about the wheelchair, how would I pee when I need to? I'm an ace at getting to the restroom, have had to be. I have to say the airlines, and I'm not sure if it's OK to mention them by name or not, but United, has been awful. I've spent so much time on this (full days) and they are indifferent that it is an ADA recognized disability or that I have a doctor's note. One woman even asked me if I was planning on cancelling my reservation, which made me REALLY nervous, as this is a trip I've planned for more than a year. After many phone calls, e-mails, with customer service people. They tell me I need to drive to the airport to talk with them there. That means a total drive time round trip of 4 hours, plus paying for parking, which is very expensive. They assure me that someone will be there, but they won't give me a name, number, nothing. I'm really trying to be both assertive, but keep my stress/anger/adrenaline level down. This is a flight into Canada, so that day I already have to catch a shuttle at 4 AM. I sort of feel like giving up and hoping for the best, but on the other hand, if I don't take a stand with this, it will be that much harder for the next IC person. Thoughts?

contents: What a load of *##*! If you were well enough to drive four hours to just meet with them, you wouldn't be needing any assistance now, would you? So, what you need is an aisle seat near a restroom, right? They can't provide you with a seat assignment in advance? As far as restroom access during the flight or if stuck on tarmac, your doctor's note should smooth the way with the flight attendants. Unless there is turbulance or it's liftoff/landing time, I've not had trouble with that. I did have one flight attendant express concern that one of his colleagues during turbulance was bounced pretty hard to the cabin ceiling & cracked some vertebrae in his neck. So, during turbulance I don't think you'd want to be up anyhow. Hang in there anyhow, be very specific in your requests. People have no idea what we are dealing with, so if we don't have specific requests & ways to facilitate their helping us, they don't know what to do & shut down. Good luck! Let us know how things turn out.

contents: Time to call congress for help Waves, What department are you speaking with at the airline? You need to speak with Customer Relations instead of Customer Service because their is a huge difference. (i.e. Years ago, I called the Customer Service department of a major airline and asked them if they had medical tickets and the answer was no, the I called Customer Relations and they said yes and we will book it for you.) What do you want them to do? Maybe we could all come with some ideas. I have done three way calling with my friends before and helped them with issues. You have an aisle seat, which is good, but what are the other accomodations you need? If you post a detailed list maybe we could help you think of a way to approach it. Yes, the airlines aren't friendly to anyone, except for my fav. Southwest -- I am a little partial as they were so kind to me while at my worst health point. It needs to be a list of things they do, not something abstract like being accomodating to your disability. Another idea is to call one of your Federal Congressmen or women. It is silly they are making you drive to the airport. They must not ignore the ADA standards. A congressional office can assist you in contact the correct gov't. agency or maybe help you call the airline. Boy, corporations don't like getting calls from a senator. Figure out the most powerful congressmen in you area and call them. They have staff members assigned to assist with matters like these. Find you senator link: U.S. Senate: Contacting U.S. Senators http://www.senate.gov/general/contact_information/senators_cfm.cfm how to For you representative: Page Not Found http://www.house.gov/house/MemberWWW_by_State.shtml Personally, I have gotten up and gone to the bathroom when people were supposed to be buckled and they said something to me and I just told them I have IC a disaling health conditon and kept moving towards the restroom. I went to the restroom and returned to my seat quickly. It may be a different issue if the plane is on the runway. I know some people with IC will wear a diaper or pad when they travel, so if you need to go just use it. If it bothers you after using it, go change it once you can access the bathroom. Stick a spare one in your purse. There is a whole market for acitve adult diapers in this country and many of them just look like underwear. No one needs to know you are using them. When you get off the plane you could go change into regular pants in airport bathroom before meeting your party. It sounds like you just need to get thru the flight there and back. I would hate to see you trip ruined with stress. I know you made a comment about wanting to make it easier for the next IC person, but you sound a little stressed about this situation so don't try and change the system just make it work for you. If you want to change the system maybe you could do it at time when you are not so stressed. Like when you get back home write a letter to the CEO of the airline and send it registered mail. We are all here to support you, please keep us updated and let us know if you get stuck solving the problem. ads Last edited by ads; 07-07-2007, 07:07 AM. Reason: added information

Thanks, yes, stressed, trying to take suggestions Hi, Yes, I'm angry and stressed about this. They have been rude, dismissive, left me on hold, not responded, implied that I don't want my reservation, and this latest business of suggesting I drive to the airport, pay for parking, to meet with someone (though no name, no contact information, no phone number, no best time to go). I did contact one of our State Representatives (suggested by one person here) via e-mail today, and we'll see. I've been a political activist on ending the war, so I can't contact a couple of others . . . never dreaming I might have to ask a favor , though these two men would not have likely taken it seriously anyway, and the one I did just contact is a fabulous woman, though not in my district. I just want to board with other people needing assistance. So I can park my stuff, hit the bathroom before take off. And if we're stuck, I just want to be able to leave my seat. There have been some many stories about people being denied access to move around if there's a delay, I'm worried. Maybe it's a bit claustrophobic/paranoid, but United is really bad. I've spoken with every department there, customer service, customer relations, reservations, anyone I could possibly get to via phone (which is challenging). Even though I've very active physically and that's what this trip is for, and might be able to deal with car trips, the plane is harder for me . . . with IC, especially as I know I have to stay hydrated to feel OK from the plane ride itself. Maybe because SO much on a plane is out of one's control . . . and I'm trying to take back my life and do what is healing. Not sure if that makes sense. So that's where I am with it now. Waiting to hear from the Rep, and trying to decide whether to drive to the airport next week to maybe find someone to talk with. AAAAGH

contents: I apologize Waves, I am sorry if my post sounded harsh. My writing skills leave something to be desired... I am extremely sorry. I was trying to help and didn't do to good of a job. You have every right to be stressed! As far a the political route, I would go with the Federal Congress because they regulate over things like the ADA and FAA. Perhaps you did contact on of them. It sounds like you have political contact though, which will be helpful. I wish you nothing but the best and again, apologize for putting my foot in my mouth. I just hate to see people have to go thru this sort of thing! With IC it is hard enough just getting packed -- at least for me. I hope your trip goes well despite this the preparation not being fun. ads
No go for the Congressional Rep Just got an automatic e-mail that her office doesn't respond to out-of-district requests, probably because she's our best.

waves Sorry you are still having A hard time. My thoughts would just go on to the Airport with the Dr. note and When you are going to get your Tix ask for a asle seat colse to the rest room. Then they will ask if anyone Needs to Pre Board Please come forward. Then I would go forward.They aslways call for PreBoarding First. "I think it goes with we are going to pre Board at this time any one needing asstiance or With small chilren Please Board at this time." I hope that iy goes okay for you. I will keep you in my Prayers. Hugs DebbieD

You can get an aisle seat reserved ahead of time so it's easier to get to a bathroom. What I do is to wait until most people have boarded. Even if they've already called your row number, you can still board. Then I go to the bathroom one last time immediately before boarding. I've never had a problem with bathroom access on a plane and have been on some really long flights. My last stop before boarding is a restroom and the first one on landing is a restroom! The only times I have seen restroom access restricted is during take-off and during landing, which is for safety reasons. I hope you have a wonderful trip! Donna

contents: Don't forget to take your ICN card and if the line to the bathroom is long pull it out. I would not be embarrassed about it at all. Please let us know how you are doing. Most people don't like traveling with IC -- yes we like to go places, but not deal with the frequency/urgency/pain of IC. I am always glad upon arrival so recovery can begin. I wish you a smooth trip and hope you don't need to recover from it! ads

I have taken to wearing those giant gauzy skirts whenever I fly, just for comforts sake. I usually wear an emergency pad but I could probably wear an entire diaper unnoticed if I wanted to. My frequency always increases due to psychosomatic stress whenever I think I may not be able to make it to the restroom. The diaper idea, although I would probably be too embarrassed to make use of it, may be able to alleviate that aspect. Even if you did have an accident, no one would be the wiser. Can you get some valium before you fly, stress contributes to frequency, so some calming stuff could help. I hope everything works out OK and you have a wonderful trip. You deserve it.
signature: I was born with one ureta not attached to it's kidney, I've been poked and prodded for so long. I never had "private" parts. I had constant infections and took low dose preventive antibiotics into my teens. DX with IC July 1993 after seeing 3 different Uro's and finally switching Primary care doctors and affiliated hospitals. I have 2 beautiful daughters, the oldest has been in heaven since she was 14 when she was hit by a car The youngest is 20 now. She has had one UTI. She went into complete panic that she would end up like me. Now she has some idea of what the pain is like. Poor kid has been forced to know where every bathroom in Disney World is.

contents: ads, you said it really well! I wish Scotty could just "beam" me where I want to go....

contents: I can't do anything jarring (such as the treadmill, ellipse machine, even the recumbant bike). I can't swim either. I can lift weights, which is better than nothing, and it doesn't make me worse. What is also nice is that between reps or machines, I can use the bathroom instead of getting off a cardio machine every 10 minutes (and it being obvious to everyone in my gym). Give it a shot if you can. (ask your doctor first because weight lifting can be hard... I'd even suggest getting a personal trainer and have them give you a routine or two that takes work off your pelvis or lower back and shows you how to do the appropriate moves). I really enjoy weight lifting!
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: That is a good suggestion. A couple of years ago, when my IC was at its worst, I did some copywriting work for a personal training who just opened a gym. I was new in my work, too. He couldn't avoid to pay me, so we exchanged services. He trained my hubby and I a couple of times a week. I thought lifting weights would bother me. I certainly have times when carrying a gallon of milk to the checkout line would make my bladder scream, but the weight lifting we did on the machines and even with some free weights were different. I think because I was positioned correctly so it wasn't straining my bladder at all. I just got an exercise ball to do some exercises for my back. I'm super out of shape right now. Any suggestions on exercises for the exercise ball? I clipped some out of my local newspaper a couple of weeks ago but haven't tried them, yet.
signature: ~ Stacey

contents: I haven't tried the exercise ball other than ab work. I just got a video set from Target (3 DVD's) called body target balance ball. Also, my friend is a pilates instuctor but she works with pelvic disease patients or chronic pain patients. She said it has helped them tremendously. I assume getting an instructor showing you how to do the moves is the best way to protect the bladder. She is going to give me lessons (for free, yay!). I will still lift weights, but this last week and a half have been terrible and I can barely get through class let alone exercise. Cleaning is a major task right now too.
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: I may check out a video. I went to physical therapy a year or two ago for an issue I had with my back. The therapist also taught Pilates, so a lot of what she showed me to do was Pilates related. I still do those stretches a few times a week for my back. She showed me a few on the exercise ball. I might check out some vidoes and stuff to see what I can find. Hope you get to feeling better. My IC is OK right now, but I have some sort of virus or something I"ve been battling since Sunday. I'm heading to the doctor later today to see what's going on. Bluh.
signature: ~ Stacey

contents: I am an exercize - phobic person. I despise most forms of exercise and have only consistently stuck with using my treadmill. After a couple of years of severe illness, (not only IC), I am ready to try to get started again; however, the treadmill is painful, feels as if I am jogging and bruising my bladder. My perpetual never ending flare is worsening from it; anyone have any suggestions?

I can't use a treadmill, does the same thing to me. I use an elliptical trainer without problems or a rowing machine. Simple as this is, make sure your shoes have plenty of support & padding. I found that putting some insoles in my sneakers for extra padding does help with walking downhill (also jarring to my bladder). Maybe also try out different settings (speed, incline) to see if there's one that is more comfortable for you.

contents: Hi Kadi and G, I use my treadmill on 0% grade and a speed of 2.6 when I am flaring - so there's less jostling, less time spent "feeling" my bladder.....swimming and yoga are great to try, too. Strength training with small dumbbells is another great way to stay active by increasing the heart rate and building muscle! Enjoy the long weekend! Beth
signature: IC diagnosis 1999 Meds: Elmiron hydroxyzine hcl DMSO as needed valium as needed librax 3x day TENS therapy Thanks to all on the boards - YOU have helped more than I can say! A person's true wealth is the good he or she does in the world.

contents: Thanks to both of you! At least I know I'm not the only one that has this problem. I have tried the lower settings, about 2.5 is all I can do without worsening. I just felt like that wasn't enough. Maybe I'll look into the yoga idea.......(is there a gagging icon?? I really, really hate other forms of exercize, lol). I will also try the padding, thanks so much!

Girl, I am proud of you because you are trying. I know we have our limitations and sometimes we have to find out exactly how far to go, but doing what we can do is what is important. Sometimes I can do the treadmill, swimming, walking but there are days I can't. I definitely appreciate the times I am able to do it. Take care, Trishann

Absolutely!! Whenever we feel good (or at least a little better...) we should try doing fun things that we can't do when we are flaring.....I LOVE exercising when it is feasible.....besides exercising, keeping a good mind set is imperitive to help us when we are flaring..... Does internet shopping count as exercise?? Hugs to all, Beth

contents: exercise My brother just gave me a Air Dyne exercise bike. I'm hoping this won't bother my bladder.
signature: Pam

contents: Hi Sun, I have found that biking irritates my bladder and increases symptoms. See how it works for you - be aware of the pressure that bike seats may place on your pelvic area. Good luck! Regards, Beth

contents: Beth, I vote that shopping counts!! Hee, Hee, I have a VERY toned index finger! Sunshine, the biking was horrible for me; it puts a lot of pressure in very bad places. Maybe it will work for you - keeping my fingers crossed!

exercise The bike will probably bother me. I know the treadmill did. The bike needs a part so I don't know if I will fix it or not. I do walk during my breaks at work so I get about 2 miles in but I need to do more or eat a lot less. I would love to lose about 30 pounds.!
signature: Pam

contents: Wouldn't we all??? I'd settle for 10 - but 30 would be AWESOME!!!!
signature: IC diagnosis 1999 Meds: Elmiron hydroxyzine hcl DMSO as needed valium as needed librax 3x day TENS therapy Thanks to all on the boards - YOU have helped more than I can say! A person's true wealth is the good he or she does in the world.

contents: girl... my problem started on the treadmill actually. I was running and felt this pain on my left side (in front, not my hip). I stopped running and it went away. I tried to rest for 2 weeks, but running again made it worse. I gave it a few more days and when the pain came back, I said "screw it" and I ran through the pain. After a few days of that I started to have urgency feelings. I stopped to get off the treadmill and those feelings stopped. I started running again and the feeling came back. After a month of just walking on the treadmill, the urgency feeling came with just walking. Now, it is 24/7, but it all started with the treadmill. That impact it has on the body is hard to deal with I guess, but yes, the treadmill, sadly, makes me feel worse. Walking around the grocery store makes me feel worse
signature: [SIZE="1"][B]Be well, Alyssa :hi:[/B] [

contents: I can do a treadmill if I take it easy and work up to increasing my time walking. I don't even try the higher speeds. Donna

contents: The elliptical seems to be much easier on my bladder also. Kari
signature: Kari I'm 47 years old, married 27 years. I have two wonderful boys and two wonderful grandchildren. I was diagnosed in 1994. Life has certainly thrown me many many surprises, all of which I'm trying to stay positive and hopeful, and I try to think about my blessings not my misfortunes, when possible. Stay Strong!

contents: I've been listening to the book "Younger Next Year for Women" and it explains that a slower pace (like 2.5 mph) is excellent for a longer time, like 30-40 minutes. I don't think you have to push the speed at all and you might have to build up to that length of time. I did. Hang in there. According to this book, it's "exercise or rot!" ....and I believe it (since I can feel the "rot", i.e, pain, in my hips for not exercising for 6 months). Now I'm back at it 6 days a week. Best wishes to all, Darlene

Thanks all for all of your replies! You have given me the courage to get exercizing again, and the guts to tell myself I don't HAVE to work out like I used to. I have been staying at the lower speeds and just working up, as several of you have said. Hopefully I will be up to 40 minutes soon. Right now I can do about 25 to 30 most days. Alyssa, I agree, walking around the grocery store is pretty bad. Looking at all of the wonderful food you used to be able to eat........ Thanks again, all of you -it is so great to have a community like this to come to with these questions. You all rock!!

Need some encouragement myself.... Gosh, I need some encouragement today myself. I must have worked out too hard on Thursday because on Friday I could barely function. Left work early, went home and slept for THREE hours. The only reason I can imagine is that I worked this 60 year old body too hard the day before. But what's really getting to me is that my hips are still hurting. Lack of exercise caused it, but every time I get up from sitting, I hurt. When I sleep at night, I can't even lie directly on my side 'cause my hips hurt. Must have an old person disease -- like bursitis. Thanks for listening to my complaining. I just need to let off a little steam...and give myself a good cry. My 84-yr old mother who lives with me fell yesterday while we were shopping, broke open her head (not serious), and broke a bone in her hand. Guess I'm letting off a little steam from feeling guilty about that too. Warmly, Darlene

Darlene, I'm so sorry to hear about your mother. I hope she will be okay? I know what you mean about the hips. I am only 40, but I have osteoporosis from steroid use, and my left hip hurts all the time!! Maybe you should ease up a bit on the workouts until it feels a bit better? I think you are to be commended for working out at all; it is so difficult when you are older, even without IC. Add in this disease and, well, I think you are doing terrific just to be out there trying. (((hugs))) girl

(And of course, I've been reading The China Study). Funny how we just don't want to see the signs of how our foods impact our bodies. Well, not funny, but human, I guess. Hope you continue to do well with your exercise. I wasn't able to do my weight training yesterday, but I'm going today...but will be a little easier, do more reps with lower weights. Have a wonderfuly day. BTW -- Mom's doing fine. Seeing a hand specialist today, getting a cast. She's such a trooper....she made cherry jam the day after her fall, with her arm in a sling. Only problem she had was getting the pits out of the cherries with one hand! LOL. Darlene

Hi Girl ... I have a treadmill, elliptical and bike. One thing that could be a big help on the treadmill is a good stretching program before you try to use it each time. I'm not talking about a few minutes, but something that you can do while watching TV for a while before you start your workout. IC can tense up muscles in your pelvic area and they may not be ready to exercise. This is a good thing to do even if you are not exercising. Also, walking should be all you need to do to get in good shape. 2.6 mph is a start. One thing to look out for is the lactic acid your body produces when you exercise. This is excreted through your bladder and will burn and feel like a flare-up ... but, it is not. It is pretty easy to spot because it will show up right after your workout right on schedule. Drinking water before and after your workout may also help. As your conditioning improves this problem will disappear. Ellipticals are nice and they are my favorite form of exercise. I have SportsArt ellipticals in my home gym and they are a superb product. Good luck. Last edited by UCSB; 06-15-2007, 07:48 PM.

contents: Hi Trishann … The burning will go away and disappear as your conditioning improves. We are all different, but I would say it should be completely gone in less than two months (maybe sooner). Also, you may recover faster from its effects as your conditioning improves.
signature: Bill

contents: Hi Girl … I have been thinking about your problem on the treadmill. Can you walk on the street and around town without getting the bruised feeling? If so, then I would rethink the basics of the treadmill. In my opinion one of the biggest factors on the treadmill are your shoes. A little background … I have a True 540Z HRCO treadmill. It is the Lexus of treadmills ($4,000). It has an orthopedic belt … this is just a way of saying that it has a grippy, cushioned belt. It also has an adjustment for bounce or reflex in the base platform. This is another cushioning or impact absorbing feature. I use Nike shox walking shoes (about $120) on it. These are nicely cushioned. I also have some nice AirMax running shoes that are cushioned and work well. Anyway, I have tried many pairs of shoes on my equipment and the right pair of shoes can really make a big (unbelievable) difference in terms of comfort and perceived easiness/effort. What I am trying to say, is look first at your shoes and then next at your treadmill. Check for features on the treadmill for controlling impact. Another area to possibly avoid is sometimes people make up for slower speed by a higher incline on the ramp. I wouldn’t start with this. Some machines can also go to a negative incline … essentially slight downhill. I would also avoid this because of possibility of impact. Finally, you should look at your posture. Are you walking freely on the machine or are you holding on to handles. If you are holding on to handles this will cause torque in your core and may be setting you up for the problem. I recommend not holding on to anything and using your arms normally. If you are going slower than you want on the machine and want to get a much larger aerobic impact from your workout, consider light handweights (little dumbbells). Maybe start with two pound models. This will really kickup your heart rate. Start slow and condition a little at a time, you don’t need any more lactic acid from your shoulders or arms. Good luck.

contents: Hi GoldSeals … If you like to bike, you might want to consider something like the Kurt Kinetic Road Machine. I have my mountain bike in one in my gym. They are between $250 and $300 and really, really nice. Here is a link: http://www.kurtkinetic.com/ Get a nice low pressure seat. Put a Spinervals spinning DVD on your TV (one comes with the Road Machine) and you are setup for a nice workout. My mountain bike (Specialized) has a full suspension so it adds extra adjustable cushioning as you are moving through your workout.

contents: Hi Girl ... I was going to add another idea to my last post, but the system would not let me ... so here it is. If you suspect impact is part of the problem, another strategy is the base under your machine. I have a .75" three layer, impact absorbing mat under my machine. Slightly expensive at $200. But, throughout the rest of my gym I have a cushioned rubber floor. I picked it up pretty cheap at Costco. Just 2'x2' rubber/foam mats that you can interconnect to build an entire floor. In your case, maybe a couple layers (about $20) under your machine might help if you feel the overall setup is just a little to hard.

contents: Hi Girl ... 10 year old Nikes are to old. You can get probably 80% to 90% of the cushioning in a expensive treadmill on your machine by carefully selecting your shoes. Look for both heal and forefront cushioning (air system) in your new Nikes. Sometimes it is hard to find the really nice shoes at your local store. I usually order either directly from Nike (they have an online store) or from www.eastbay.com . Eastbay sometimes has some great closeouts deals.

contents: Hi Alyssa, I see that you are newly diagnosed. This disease can really destroy your system and ability to do your prior activities when it initially hits you. With time people develop more sophisticated coping and management approaches. It is a balancing act, but usually people can start to rebuild areas of their life with time. In this early IC mode, exercise needs to be put into your overall set of priorities and may be to risky if it endangers other aspects of your life (like your career, for example). Your exercise problem is a tough one. If urgency/frequency increase with exercise, that is going to stop you. I don't know what the answer is ... but, I do have a few observations based on my own experiences when I returned to exercising. When I first returned to aerobic exercising, I would work out on my equipment and make frequent trips to the bathroom. I can remember going to the bathroom, getting on the machine, exercising for a minute and returning to the bathroom. But, with time my body and mind adjusted. Now I don't even think about it. Some days I can do a full segment of my workout without a break (maybe 40 minutes) ... other days I will need to take a break(s). The one thing that I know will not work is trying to keep working out when you need to go the bathroom. It is a tough call ... at that point in my life, I guess I just decided if this disease was going to take me down, then I was going to go down fighting it. I had already given up golf, tennis, skiing, boating, mountain biking and about another 100 interests. A good first step might be to talk to your doctor about getting some physical therapy. A physical therapist can teach you a pelvic oriented stretching program that you will be able to do. This will help with your fitness level and give you the best tool for reversing the muscle pain and tension around your bladder. A good fitness program really has three balanced components: flexibility, strength, and aerobic training. Starting back in from the flexibility angle will benefit your IC symptoms and give you a meaningful path back to fitness. Perhaps after making sound progress in the flexibility area you will be in better shape to fold in some strength or aerobic conditioning next. Anyway, that is the approach that I took. I found a physical therapist that specialized in pelvic pain. Spent six months or more tuning up my flexibility and core strength and then begin to layer in other activities. I started with the treadmill and quick weights program. Later adding an elliptical and bike. It is a journey that can take some time. Since your logon is born2swim, I will assume you are a swimmer. Swimming would be a great aerobic exercise to start with when you try to fold in aerobic exercises again. I have used swimming on and off since my IC started and have always found it to be one of the nicest activities. Good luck. PS -- in your signature, it says pain with running. Running is probably a really fast way to aggravate your IC symptoms. Last edited by UCSB; 06-18-2007, 08:29 AM.

contents: My experience... Hi Ladies! It would probably just depend on you (yes, that seem's to be the theme with IC and PFD!) I used to do Pilates and had great results. This was before my IC and PFD diagnoses. The Gaiam series were what I liked best, short (20 minute segments), up beat, and started from beginner levels up to advanced. Most required only a mat or towel, unless specified (in the ball series obviously you need a ball!) Plus I like Ana Caban, who does most of their Pilates videos. She is fit, but short and normal looking, not a super model or "fake". Plus she is pleasant and encouraging (Pilates can be very hard at first) That being said, in my case, Pilates is no longer an optionfor me right now. I loved it and my tummy was much tighter. But the pain I already have is too much, and yes Pilates made it worse for me b/c you tighten your core muscles (IE: butt, tummy, Pelvic muscles, vaginal muscles) In fact my IC symptoms all peaked when I was at my healthiest- had just graduated from college, was doing Pilates 5 days a week, was eating healthy foods that were IC triggers, (all kinds of fruits, yogurt, tomatos, green tea, etc) and taking a weight loss supplement that was loaded with caffeine) I have tried Pilates since and unfortunately I flare. But remember this is just my case. The thing to do would be to get a beginner DVD (you can find them cheap online, or often at the library) and try it. I wouldn't spend alot of money if I were you or take classes you have to pay in advance for until you know for sure how you will react. In my case, just walking also gives me intense back and pelvic pain (which means more meds, yuck!) I am able to do Yoga. I don't take classes or even have a video, I just found an article on a type of very easy Yoga called Kripalu yoga in a health magazine (May 2006 Natural Health). It focuses on being calm and relaxed (something I desperately need ight now!) gentle stretching and better balance plus most of the poses are standing or on your side. I can't lay on my back for too long, so that was a plus. I can't say I've lost weight or flattened my abs, but it does help give me energy and helps when I have pain. Plus the focus on breathing and relaxation has helped when i have terrible pain days. I am much better nowat visualization, relaxing and deep breathing when the pain is too much to handle! Hope this helped!

contents: I have Plantar Faciitis in both feet. They are better than they have been. About 4 years ago, I went through PT for one foot and it did help. And then last November I had started going to PT for the other foot, but I had to quit going because of babysitter issues. Yes, tennis shoes do get old and tiring. What you can do, is check for shoes stores that are "speciality" stores. For example there are stores here in PA. called Shoe Fly and they fitted me with teh proper tennis shoes and also special inserts for my type of foot problems. I'm sure there is probably some type of shoe store where you live that you could check into. I not only have the Plantar Faciitis, but I also have long skinny feet (9 1/2 AA) so I usually have to spend alot on shoes, casual or dress shoes in order to get them to fit. The exercises for Plantar Faciitis are not hard to do. You can do them at home. Look Plantar Faciitis up on the Internet, and you will more than likely find a site with some exercises that may help you. Kim

Hey kuntrygurl, thanks for replying to my post about my mysterious foot pain on the other thread. Kinda weird that we both just went to the doctor about this at around the the same time! So, kuntrygirl and chainsaw, I was just wondering... could you both describe your symptoms for me? Cuz I was surfing around the net reading about the condition, and I don't seem to fit the profile or have the typical symptoms. Then again, I don't fit the profile or have the typical symptoms for IC, either, but a cysto+hydro showed I had it, so you can never know. But, I don't have more severe pain with my first few steps in the morning like it says you're supposed to. If anything, the pain builds up the more I walk. Also, the pain isn't really in my heel, but on the bottom part of my foot. Like, for instance, on this chart from Wikipedia: (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Image:PF-PainAreas.jpg) If you look on the foot to the far right, I don't have any pain in the area that 52% reported as their most common area of pain. My pain is in the 14% and 6% areas. Also, it's actually been doing better today, after I spent all of yesterday resting, and the pain has gone down to a dull nagging. Right now, I'm walking around my house barefoot and not feeling any desire to limp. So, I'm wondering if it isn't just something else entirely. But, I'm seeing the foot specialist as soon as I can, and, if it is plantar faciitis, I won't be too bummed. Having IC has taught me so much about dealing with chronic pain that I feel ready to take on just about anything at this point. Anyway, where do you guys have your pain? Do your first few steps hurt a whole lot? Does the pain build up the more you walk around? Have you found that high heels actually help the pain, and flats make it worse? I really do have some weird symptoms here
signature: Chronic conditions: IC, bipolar disorder, Lown-Ganong-Levine syndrome, Raynaud's disease, bile reflux, scoliosis Current IC treatments: menstrual suppression Daily treatments for other conditions: Neurontin, Zyprexa, Cymbalta, Modafinil As-needed treatments for other conditions: Klonopin, Ambien

contents: My pain actually started a year or two ago. I started noticing it when my fiance and I would spend an entire day running errands. By the end of the day I was in extreme pain. The pain was the entire bottom of both feet. Then a few months ago, it started in my left heel. The more Im on my feet the more it hurts. I spent the day yesterday running errands. By the end of the day I could barely walk. I iced my feet in the car on the way to town (40 minute drive), wore my good shoes and doctor made arch supports, and it didnt do any good. I even wore my foot splint (boot) that my doc gave me to bed last night. This morning when I first woke up, walking was very painful. Especially in my heel. Im fine now. Ive been outside a bit today doing some gardening, and Im only mildly sore. I start my PT on Tuesday, and I go back to the doc right after my PT apt. Im hoping that she will be able to help me. Right now, nothing seems to be doing the trick. Right now, Im having more trouble with this than my IC.

contents: foot pain I had horrible foot pain a couple of months ago , I thought it was elmiron but it wasn't. I went to the podiatrist and he gave me inserts he said my arch was sagging ( I am sure there is a medical term for this). I have a high arch and when he built up the arch on the inserts I could feel instant relief. With in 2 weeks my foot pain was almost gone. My big toe was also throbbing and I thought I had gout but it was something called sigmoiditis or something like that. Any way I know what you mean I didn't want to get out of bed in the morning or stand on my feet as a result I got fatter. Now I'm starting to get back into shape. My feet feel normal again and I don't have to wear the inserts all the time. Sandy
signature: past meds: elmiron 100mg 3 x a day cystoprotek 6 a day elavil 10 mg Femcon fe non-shellfish vegetarian glucosamine HCL 750mg once a day

contents: I have it in my left foot, bottom of foot/top; suppose to wear tennis shoes as well. I have had this for about 7 years; but only bothers me now and then ( when I wear sandles/dress shoes all the time -but then I'll wear my heel pad in my dressy shoes and that helps.
If you are looking for good, quality sandals you might check the "Teva" brand. They are expensive, but not as expensive as Birkenstocks.

contents: I loved PT! The lady had me put my feet in a hot tub, then she massaged my foot, did some other things, then taped my foot. This lady is an angel! Thank goodness that my insurance covers 100% of PT. I could go every day. :woohoo: The massage felt the best. Im going to be going two days a week for the next few weeks. She wants me to tape my foot every day. She showed me how to do it so that the tape acts as support for the part of my foot that hurts. It helps quite a bit. My doc also wants me to wear my boot 2 hours during the day on top of wearing it to bed. When I wear the boot I cant walk around. So I guess, I have an excuse to be lazy a few hours everyday. Plus, I really need to get better about doing my stretches. She said the more I do them, the faster it will heal. Wish me luck!

contents: I work in a shoe store- have been for the past 8 1/2 years, so I have dealt with lots of people wiht this problem. The best thing is twear shoes that give good FIRM support- shoes, like Birkenstiock, Haflinger clogs, Dansko clogs, Wolky shoes and sandals- all thses brands are good. So is Earth- but make sure it is the Earth shoe with the negative heel- so that your heel is lower than the front part of your foot. Walmart seels Earth shoes, BUT these not the ones that have the negative heel. Also do not go barefoot and before ggetting out of bed in the morning stretch your foot. There are plenty of inserts out there that can be helpful- again make siure they are firm, I know you think you need something soft underfoot, but even though initially it feels good it will not help over time. SuperFeet make good insoles as do Pedag, and some of the Spenco inserts are good too.

......and I am a complete wimp and out of shape. I have been so sick. I hate to be a "usta person" (makes me think of wrinkly old men leaning on canes talking about chicks they usta pick up because of their awesome pecs... ) but I usta bench press 110 pounds, leg press 400, and row 60....as well as do a hell of an aerobic exercise. My husband would ***** and say women should be soft, not hard. (so he has his wish and now we never has sex) So in PT the other day, my PT tells me it is time to start working on my arms. I went straight to the machines, set the seat exactly perfect and had my posture and breathing perfect on the exercise. My PT said "You've done this before...like a few times?" I just laughed and said yeah...I wasn't rowing 5 pounds and getting exhausted though.....AAAARGHHHH. Oh, you guys, I am so disgusted with myself. I am so weak, and so easily tired...Do you work out when you are tired? How did you all start? I know the recommendation is 5 minutes to start with in short burst through out the day. I have a community center by my house I am joining and going to start working out again. I am so lo I am afraid of falling. If I have a bad bladder flare, or this other issue I have going of right now I fear any progress will get lost... talk to you later

contents: Hopefully, just doing a little every day will help you feel better & better. It really helps me, I find. Mostly I walk, but I also do a little yoga & sometimes light gym workouts.

contents: I know...the endorphins kick in and all but I am sooo UGH! and damn, the snickerdoodles my daugher made today are good (Highly annoying, dammit) I roll on my Thera ball during family time in the front room but that creates a "mom can I play on it ?" situation. (Mom's not playing...yeah...it looks like I'm hippity hoppitying and watching TV upside down...what's not fun about THAT?) Man...I really thought stuff has just gotten heavier and to realize how weak I truly am. I can't blame it all on my medical situation, I've just gotten depressed and trying to break out it is tough. But, I am tomorrow...wimpy weights, wimpy walk, a couple times....gotta go
