signature: Christine I have been diagnoised for 6 1/2 years now. I have taken a long break from the ICN but really miss helping out my fellow IC patients and want to get back into posting. 1st hydro 4/07 showed no visible signs of IC but tons of mast cells in all my biopsy samples which did prove IC. 2nd hydro 4/13/09 showed dark purple glomerulations and I had a capacity of 450 cc's. This hydro proved that my IC had progressed. I have tried every oral medication as well as rescue instills and DMSO. I have been lucky enough to see Dr Hanno, the top IC specialist in PA who has told me due to the fact that I have not responded to any "standard" treatments that I have a severe, end stage case of IC with a horrible quality of life (didn't have to tell me that last part!) Proud wifey of Shane, mommy to Griffin, and step-mom to Logan and Gage Also proud mom to the best Bullmastiff on earth, Claus

contents: I LOVE my resistance bands!!! I have the kind that are attached to handles and the kind that look like HUGE rubber bands, I like those ones the most! I use them mainly for my outer thighs. I sit on the edge of the couch and put the band around my legs just above my knees and move my legs open n closed (using control or ya just look like your flingin the band lol) and it helps. I do also put one end of it under my foot and grab the other w/my hand and do curls for my upper arms. You can put it in both hands over your head and stretch it, to work more of your arms. I also have the kind that are a big strip of rubber, its fine too, but ya gotta tie it w/a half bow, if you knot it you wont get it undone lol. I just like the big rubber band kind most because you dont have to do anything w/it like tie it or hold handles. Party cake Ice Cream good Lord that sounds good!!! We have walgreens that sells one called birthday cake in the real small boxes. I am always tempted to have one of those!!!

Chrisitne, do they give out free samples?? Think I would want a job at that place. They do make good ice cream but that kind is to die for. Windwalker, I am going to have to check into getting one of those bands. It sounds simple and bladder friendly. Are they expensive? That party cake ice cream comes in a half gallon. Good thing I can't carry the computer around or I would be typing this while driving to the store for the party cake.

Nah, the bands arent expensive, try WalMart, Kmart, Target etc. before you try anywhere else. I got lucky and got a pack of three different ones for 10 bucks. May not be able to find that price now, like I said, I got lucky. I even got (during the summer) one of the kinds you have to tie for a buck at Target. They have this section where everythings a dollar and I found that and one pound hand weights for a buck each. (they are D shaped so you hold the straight end and let em swing while walking)

I was wondering if every day I cleaned at least one room (maybe 2) from top to bottom would that be enough exercise for a day? Do you have to get in 10,000 steps? Was just thinking if I could use cleaning as aroebics that would be a little bit easier on the bladder and me.

If cleaning could be considered enough excersize, then everyone with a clean house would be thin. My house is clean, but I assure you, I am nowhere near thin! LOL! Also, I can tell you that I know plenty of thin people with filthy houses and plenty of fat people with spotless ones, so I would think it must not be enough. Sorry!

If doing actual aerobic excercise is too much for your bladder, and cleaning doesn't cause a flare up..... then I say go for it. It's better to do something to at least be up and moving everyday, instead of doing nothing just because you can't do "real" excercise... Use it or lose it... Just my two cents

signature: Blessings, Ruth

contents: Originally posted by curlycue ihurttoo I have a question for you............................. How did you find out you had Lupus! My Dr. tested my anti nuclear antibodies and they were positive. That is indicitive an autoimmune disease, but is not specific just to Lupus. I had several of the other signs and symptoms of it for a while, but when he never d/xed me for sure back then b/c I didnt have all of them and when he did the LE Prep test (a test for Lupus), it never showed up as Lupus. However, I was never flaring or symptomatic the days they tested me. But, I had no idea it made any difference. (He didnt either.) He referred me to a local Rhematologist who repeated the tests with the same results. The new Dr. said I definately had an autoimmue disease, but was unable to determine which one. I didnt like him and ultimately found a Dr. I like at Vanderbuilt. She told me that unless I was flaring when they tested me, that it would not have shown up on that test, (unless I had a very bad case.) So, she told me to call her the next time I was having symptoms and she would test me again then. So, that's what we did, and sure enough, it was positive! Many are d/xed just based on the +ANA and symptoms, but at that time, my signs/symptoms were borderline. So, I definately think it helped to be tested while I was actively symptomatic.

Hey, it's working! I lost another pound. :woohoo: :woohoo: Watched what I really ate and did some cleaning and that was all. Didn't have to kill my bladder and body and do exercising on top of that. Unless the effexor is helping out some otherwise, that is the only difference I have done. Hey, I'll have a clean house too.

Hey Waterflow- I agree with Kadi, I put a little rock and roll on very loud, and before I know it the house is clean and I've worked up a sweat. And like you said the bonus is a clean house!! Congrat's on the pound, that's awesome!

I think for myself lately any exercise is better than none. If you get bored I have the need to clean another house mine can use it. (just KIdding). It is my exerices especially in the flare I have been in for weeks. Wish this little guy would clean toilets.

contents: I think cleaning can be an excercise too. All the bending, twisting, scrubbing, and etc. That is a workout in it self. Waterflow you can do it, just doing it alittle bit at a time. Cutting back alittle bit at a time. I think what really gets us in trouble is when we try to do the strict diet. The diet you give up everything, that seems to work only for a short time. I think you can do it. Sending hugs, Trishann

I just checked on SParkpeople and under cardio workout they have heavy cleaning. :woohoo: That will be a whole lot easier to handle. You don't burn many calories. For 20 minutes of cleaning it's only I think 120 calories burned but still. 120 is better then nothing.

Just this past saturday I was lifting weights on a incline bench press, something fairly heavy and I noticed that my scrotum area started feeling really heavy and real uncomfortable afterwards. Its a nasty feeling I am still feeling inside my testicle region/anal. It also radiates to my abdomen making me feel nauseated. I really cant pin point the area though where my discomfort is coming from. I was gonna go see my doctor, but I had this problem in the past when I was working out at the gym and it lasted for 3 weeks. That time I went to see the doctor and they said it was nothing and just prescibed me some pain medz. With my discomfort, I checked everywhere in that area such as my groin, balls, anal area and theres nothing unusual such as any bulges, lumps, or anything protruding out of the ordinary. I even had my girlfriend do a search for me as well for anything unusual. Nothing, so I believe its in the inside some where in that area. Its very frustrating because I feel like its having a effect when I try to urinate.
If you're not better by tomorrow, I think it would be a good idea to get in touch with your doctor --- it's best to play it safe. Donna

contents: I agree with Donna that you should continue to see a doctor if this doesn't go away or continues to happen. Although it was a different area of the body, I was having pain when I exercised or exerted myself. It took several visits to the doctor to find the cause. My initial visits to the doctor where similar to yours - just given meds and told to take it easy. At subsequent visits, after the meds didn't work and the pain got worse, the doctor started investigating further. Every doctor is different, but I sometimes think they're hesitant to order expensive tests if simple treatments work first.

Im Going 2day. Thanks, Im Going To See My Uro Today At 2pm. My Body Still Doesnt Feel Right. Hopefully That Will Go Well And My Uro Finds Out What Is The Cause Of This Annoying Undiscribable Discomfort.
When you go to the dr. make sure they check you for kidney stones, because that is a possiblity with the symtoms you are having.

contents: My Third Cystoscopy Coming Up. RITE ON ABOUT THAT, BUT I NEVER GOT THE CHANCE TO BRING THAT UP AND I NOW JUST CHECKED THIS PAGE OUT. I HAVE ANOTHER CYSTOSCOPY COMING UP ON FEB 2ND. THIS IS GOING TO BE MY THIRD ONE, BUT THANK GOD I WILL BE PUT TO SLEEP ON THIS ONE.(NOT LIKE THE CYSTOSCOPY/URODYNAMICS TEST I HAD THIS PAST AUG 21,2006 WHERE I WAS WIDE AWAKE) IM STILL PRETTY NERVOUS THOUGH, I JUST HATE IT AFTERWARDS WHEN I URINATE, I HAVE THAT AWFUL BURNING FEELING IN MY URETHRA FOR SEVERAL LONG HOURS IS WHAT IT SEEMS TO BE LIKE. YOU FEEL ME??? I GOT TO DO IT THOUGH. ON MY FOLLOW UP APPT ON FEB 9, I WILL MAKE SURE I WILL ASK ABOUT THESE K-STONES 2. I HOPE ALL GOES WELL WITH MY PROCEDURE.

2 things I asked for when I had my cysto under anest. is 1) the smallest scope possible so that there would be less pain after and 2) some pyridium to help w/the burn also. Good luck on your procedure.
... you should have your doctor check for a hernia. A hernia is a tear or weakening in the muscular wall surrounding the tissue. It is can be repaired.
signature: Bill

contents: I wonder if it may be a muscular thing too. With our muscles either very weak or constantly spasming, doing exercise that may utilise these muscles could make it quite possible that you have strained or even torn one/some. I always test my self this way (though it isnt foolproof, it can give some indication) does the pain increase on deep inhalation? if so, it is more likely to be muscular than if the pain is constant. How did the appointment with your uro go...did he have any ideas about the pain? lisa

Nasty Feeling WUTTTTUUUUP. FOR THE URO PART ON FRIDAY, ALL HE DID WAS CHECK MY RIGHT TESTICLE AND KIND OF SQUEEZED IT ASKING ME IF IT HURT? WHICH IT DIDNT. I ASKED HIM ABOUT A HERNIA WHILE MY PANTZ WERE HALF WAY DOWN WHILE I LIFTED UP MY SHIRT TO SHOW MY ABDOMEN AND FROM HIS STARE HE SAID I DONT HAVE A HERNIA IS WHAT HE MENTIONED. ALSO WHEN I INHALE DEEP THERE IS NO PAIN. JUST THIS PAST SAT I WENT TO THE DOCTORS BECAUSE I WAS STILL HAVING DISCOMFORT IN MY TESTICLES DOWN TO MYANAL AREA. FEELS AWKWARD WHEN SITTING UP RIGHT. ALSO, HAVING ALOT OF DISCOMFORT IN MY ABDOMEN WITH CONSTANT BELCHING. THE LADY DID A CHECK WITH HER HANDS ON MY ABDOMEN/TESTICLE AREA TO SEARCH FOR ANY HERNIAS. SHE EVEN GRABBED ONE OF THE OTHER LADY DOCTOR TO DO A CHECK FOR ANOTHER OPINION FOR HERSELF 2 BE SURE. NO BULGES. SHE GAVE ME A PRESCRIPTION FOR PRILOSEC AND FOR ME TO GET A TESTICLE ULTRASOUND. WITH THE ULTRASOUND PART, I DIDNT DO IT YET. NOW AS SUNDAY COMES BY, MY BODY IS FEELING REAL WEIRD LIKE URINE RETENTION WEIRD. SIMILAR FEELING. I NOTICE IM HAVING A VERY DIFFICULT TIME PASSING BOWEL MOVEMENTS, AND WHEN I DID IT WOULD BE VERY VERY TINY AND SKINNY. I WAS EXTREMELY FEELING NASTY NO MATTER HOW PATIENT I WAS ON THE TOILET. EVEN IF I STRAINED ALITTLE, MY POO STILL DIDNT WANNA COME OUT. BY THE EVENING THINGS STARTED GETTING WORSE. I WAS HAVING GAS PROBLEMS WHERE I COULDNT PASS GAS EITHER. FEELING LIKE THERES A TOTAL BLOCK SOMEWHERE. LEAVING MY ABDOMEN SUPER DISTENDED LIKE I WAS 3 TO 4 MONTHS PREGNANT TO BE EXACT. I MADE ANOTHER APPT. THE NEXT DAY WITH THE SAME DOC, LETTING HER KNOW MY SYMPTOMS. SHE CHECKS MY PROSTATE AND SENDS ME TO THE LAB FOR AN ABDOMEN XRAY. my whole abdomen was fully flooded with feces. I GOT TO SEE IT 2, IT LOOKED NASTY. IN MY 25 YEARS OF LIVING, I NEVER EXPERIENCED SOMETHING LIKE THIS. I was given some laxative and some pills to relieve my constipation. Im still feeling pretty bloated. Its feel like i got 50% of my bowel movement out though. Hopefully 2morrow I will feel more better. OTHERWISE IM GOING BACK TO THE DOCTOR OR MAYBE THE E-R. 4GET WAITING...

Just being constipated can cause all sorts of strange pressure and odd feelings in the pelvic and abdominal areas. It sounds like you probably have some reflux as well and the prilosec sounds like a good idea. My guess would be that most of your problems is extreme constipation and a few days on that laxative might do wonders for you. Hope you feel better soon. Sandy

I had to re-do it so I could go along with the tape and had to leave out all the real bouncing around but I still got a good work out. Can feel it all over my body. I was thinking about watching dancing with teh stars and how I wished I could do that. I was dancing, not a nearly as good as them, but I was dancing! :woohoo: :woohoo: I'm hping to tone up the old bod now with exercising and dieting. This is really a big step for me. (one bad part, I had to do the first 10 minutes with the hick-ups. I seem to get them because of the vicodin. ) ha

I have just felt too crummy lately to lift weights. I have walked the dogs a few times through the neighborhood, and that in itself is a major workout! Now that the weather is actually winter-like I guess I'll have to go use that machine sooner or later. Be proud of yourself, you did a great thing!

He did this to me twice. At least the second time was during winter and I skidde din the snow head first of course. He only stopped becuase he couldn't drag me. When you feel good enough to exercise I'm sure you will get in that big gym and start. How about I make a meal and bring it over to you so I can work out in there?? Hi Does anyone do indoor rock climbing and notice any negative effects from it on their IC or PFD? Thanks!

Hi! You know, I'm no expert, but to be honest, I think you are best off seeing how you feel & trying it if you are already at a fitness level for it. Maybe do an easy climb on a Friday night or Saturday. That gives you the rest of the weekend to recover if your IC/PFD flares. I think your own body is the best judge of these things. The main thing, I would guess, is if you are at a fitness level that will let you move as you need to without overstrain. If you try it, let us know how it works out for you!

contents: climb on! Hey - I've been a rockclimber for years and I have never experienced an increase in symptoms when rockclimbing, if anything it lessens frequency urgency and pain, b/c you are totally focused on something else besides your bladder - The harness that you wear wraps around your thighs so it doesn't actually abrade the crotch area. it is a great sport to try out. good luck!
Thanks so much for your replies! I'm so glad that there's something else out there other than gentle exercise that I can do to stay fit.

Well, I've been going to the gym for a year now. I usually made it once a week, although there were a few times where I was busy or had other plans, so I would probably average out my time there to three times a month. Doesn't sound like a lot, but it has definetely made a difference. Yesterday, I ran my 15 minutes on the treadmill, then did my regular workout routine, and then went back to the treadmill. I realized that I wasn't tired at all, so I decided to see if I could run 25 minutes on the treadmill. And, to my surprise, I did it! See, about a year ago, when my mom and I started going to the gym, we got tired really easily. I remember our first time, we did 10 minutes of brisk walking, a 30 hour workout, and then 10 more minutes of brisk walking. Let me tell you, we were exhausted! My bladder hurt, but I knew that I had to work through it if I wanted to get into better shape. So, very gradually, things started to get easier. I moved my time up to 15 minutes, and started increasing my speed. It left me pretty tired, but I wanted to get better. Me and my mom always went together. The gym we go to has a daycare, and my six year old sister loves it there, so it was convinient for us. I guess I didn't really notice we were making progress until yesterday, though. If you would have told me two years ago that I would have been able to run 25 minutes straight, I would have thought you were on crack. I was in so much pain then that even going to school daily was a struggle. My boyfriend (at the time) and I went to Homecoming in a limo with our friends that year, all ready to have a good time. But halfway through the dance, I curled up by the wall and cried. Dancing hurt way too much. Gym class was the hardest part of my day then. I remember that one time I was trying to run a lap when my bladder started to hurt a lot. I clenched it with my hand and practically limped over the gym teacher, asking to please be allowed to sit out. I was given a break that day, but I was already so embarrased that I cried. My uro could have written a note explaining that my interstitial cystitis makes it hard to do the things we were expected to do, but he wouldn't even tell me I had IC back then, much less put it on paper for all to witness. I remember there was a girl in my class who had a broken arm. When she couldn't do something that we were doing in class, everyone was super nice about it. When I couldn't do something, it was because I was lazy. When it was really obvious that I was in pain, I would be allowed to sit out. Generally, though, my gym teacher hadn't heard of IC, and didn't really see what my bladder would have to do with me not being able to run or do active sports. Of course, there was no need to try to help me or make me feel more included, because I had a broken bladder and not a broken arm. I guess people assumed I was some little princess who thought she was too good to run around and get sweaty with the rest of them. I did have some friends in there, but many people just didn't seem to believe that I had a disability. They couldn't see it, so it was probably an excuse. When I didn't get picked for a team, and the teacher ended up assigning me to one, girls would cross their arms and sigh. No one would have done this if I had leg braces, but I didn't, so it was acceptable. After a while, I just stopped trying. I did the bare minimum so I could get one year's credit. Trying hard wasn't worth it. There were times when I couldn't even manage that, though. The first few days I was on Elavil, I could barely drag my feet from class to class. I guess I looked as bad as I felt, because the teacher gave me a break and let me sit on the bleachers.The rest of the time, I just did what I had to do. So, you can see how it was back then. I was in constant, severe pain, and physical activity made it even worse. I didn't think it would ever get better, but very slowly, over a span of many months, it did. I was still scared to work out, though. The pain was just starting to decrease, and I sure didn't want it to come back. It wasn't until a full year later that I agreed to start going to gym with my mom... and only because she insisted. I was afraid, but I think another part of me wanted to prove everyone wrong. At this point, my symptoms were usually low-level, so I didn't mind the temporary increase in pain that came with exertion. I kept at it, not expecting much. And then, yesterday... I did it! I ran for 25 minutes straight! It was an emotional moment for me. There you have it; I'm not lazy. And, you know what? I overcame a lot to get to where I am. I know other people can still run faster and jump higher, but I had to fight to get to where I am, so I'm proud of myself.

contents: This is a very good question and I would also like feedback on ideas. Walking is very hard for me as it starts up severe spasms I guess that is what they are - feels like a charliehorse sort of in my abdomen. I use an exercise ball sometimes (actually that is also really good for pain control because I can hug it while stradling it and that helps sometimes or I can sit there on the floor hugging and rocking which helps. So - I also find that pilates some of the floor exercises work great. I am a farmer so I do a lot of moving in different positions and that is better for my pain than anything that is repetitious - I can't sit fo rlong, stand for long or any one thing for long but gardening has a lot of different possitions to be in which is great for me - my advise would be to get outside and do something like that which can also be relaxing. I would love any other ideas though - my issue is not weight loss, but more of muscle loss and just I am loosing strength at a very fast pace which is scarry. I need to get stronger!
signature: " If I really cared as I thought I did, about the sorrows of the world, I should not have been so overwhelmed when my own sorrow came." ~ CS Lewis

contents: elliptical, some light weight training. I can do very light step aerobics, a little dancing around the living room, I use these indoor walking tapes (I know your body's used to the walking but maybe a different way to do it?) that are real nice. If I am having a better day, I move more and if its a not so good day, I just do smaller movements.

I'm feeling frustrated. I was feeling good enough to not be thinking about my bladder all the time. Night before last I only got up 2x to the bathroom. I was feeling like my bladder capacity was improving too. I've been on meds for only 3 weeks (Elmiron and Elavil) so thought the Elavil must be helping. I was feeling good enough to try to get back to my routine at the gym. Up to now I've been walking without a problem as long as I avoid a lot of hills. I used the recumbent bike for a short while and the treadmill a short while. Then I tried to do a few of the things with weights like I used to do. I noticed a change by the afternoon. I was going to the bathroom more frequently, having a harder time starting to go, and not feeling like I emptied. I was up every two hours last night. I don't usually have pain in my bladder but I had discomfort. I'd feel full but not go a lot. I'm even thinking of doing a home UTI test even though I'm on a once daily dose of Macrodantin. One thing I did was the leg press. I'm wondering if that was too hard on the pelvic muscles. The only other thing I did yesterday was try a new preservative free bread and cashew butter from TJ's. At my last appointment when I told my doctor I suspected that exercise was a trigger he acted like that wasn't typical but I've seen it listed as a trigger before. I've also read that exercise helps. I may have done too much. Maybe weights aren't a good idea at least yet ?? Any thoughts? Thanks.

Sounds like you might have gone to hard and fast. I know I do water exercise with about 6 other ladies at a school pool. Right now the pool is closed because the heater isn't working and the pool is outdoors. I have missed this exercise and know that when we hopefully can go back in Jan. that I will have to pace myself. My doctor says that excercise can bring on pain. I have learned this first hand. I have pain just getting the groceries bought and home. As far as weight I know I can't lift what I used to without pain. As it is a walk from my garage to house I have asked for a garden wagon for Christmas, will see if that comes and if so if that hleps. Do exercise slow and in moderation and you will be OK. Have a Happy Holiday.

contents: Exercise can trigger mast cell degranulation and histamine release into the bladder, which causes inflammation - which in turn causes your symptoms. Many urologists prescribe an antihistamine such as vistaril or atarax, half an hour to one hour prior to exercising. Your symptoms sound exactly like what I used to experience, until I started taking the Vistaril before I hit the gym. Now things are back to normal and I can exercise again without a problem. I suggest that you consult with your healthcare provider if you believe that the information above may be helpful; your healthcare provider will determine the appropriate treatment regimen for you. The contents in this post are provided for informational purposes only. The contents are not intended in any way to be a substitute for professional medical advice. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding your medical condition. Best wishes for a happy and healthy holiday and new year, ICB Last edited by ICB; 07-06-2006, 02:22 PM.

I have the same problem. Exercise is my biggest trigger. Especially any vigorous type of exercise. I had to give up on basketball, because I was in too much pain for a week or more after playing. I've been having some better luck when using antihistamines such as allegra before exercising (as mentioned by ICB). I still have to be careful with anything too vigorous though, because I still have some pelvic pain. I feel great during exercise, but I usually have some pelvic pain for a few days after. Good Luck
I exercise daily however I'm going the all natural supplemental route too. Jen Last edited by ICNDonna; 12-21-2005, 02:32 AM.

contents: I have problems doing certain things like running or high impact exercise. However, I do walk and I went to a store that specialises in running/walking so they measured my feet, etc and I was able to pick shoes which are the best for me and the way i walk (how you place your feet as you're walking). If I can I try and walk on the dirt rather than pavement and this seems to help too. Also, I just started doing Hatha Yoga a little while ago and I haven't had any related flare-ups. If anything I feel more relaxed after my class. Also, my instructor is great and she really promotes that we NOT do anything that may make our bodies uncomfortable (I'm not the only one in the class who has just lay back and practised deep breathing during one of the moves).

contents: Thanks for the responses. I would think the antihistamine would make you drowsy. Is it a small enough does that you get used to it? My doctor didn't offer that to me.
The literature says regular exercise is supposed to help.

It would be interesting to know what exercise they studied and whether it was something like walking or stretching or more intensive exercise including using weights. When I looked on Pub Med there was an abstract from Uro Nurs but I couldn't look at anything but that. All it said in the abstract was that exercising 3 days a week for 6 months benefited 2 people. Anyone know where else to look or if any experts here have discussed exercise?

I am glad to see I am not alone on this. I have gained weight because sometimes even walking briskly causes me extreme urethral pain and burning /lower back/thigh weakness. Forget about running or using an exercise bike or even an elliptical machine. I am only 29 but my doctor ordered a stress test last year for chest pain which they later determined to be due to my worsening asthma. I had to stop the test due to extreme pelvic pain, similar to menstrual cramps and thigh weakness but with the feeling that I was going to lose control of my urine. I have been diagnosed with IC but could this be related to something neurological?

Katie and others starting to exercise again ... one thing to keep in mind. If you have not been exercising and start a program, your muscles are not conditioned and will produce lactic acid. If you were sore the next day, that may be a clue. Your body excretes this excess lactic acid in your urine and for an IC patient this can burn. It will feel like you are flaring or losing ground. You are not ... it may just be the lactic acid going through your bladder. Once you are aware of this reaction you can easily recognize it and not overreact. Ease back on your workouts, drink enough water, put up with a little burning and eventually the symptoms will disappear as your conditioning improves. Also, I think that a good cool down routine can reduce lactic acid problems (but, it has been awhile since I researched this point). One approach that I used was to drink water during and after my workouts. Another approach is to build very slowly from extremely easy workouts ... don't worry over a few weeks/months you will make good progress. When you get back in better shape, keep up with your conditioning and you won't have the problems again. Last edited by UCSB; 12-30-2006, 09:26 PM.

contents: Exercise helps me ... especially stretching … which can relax the muscles in the pelvic area. If you have not been working out, your muscles might produce lactic acid and this will be excreted in your urine. It may burn your bladder and feel like a flare … it is not. As your conditioning improves, the lactic acid is not a problem and the effect disappears. Remember to drink enough water. Things that I find IC friendly ... stretching (a physical therapist can help you develop a program), walking on a treadmill (this can really pay off), elliptical training, and weights. Easy floor exercises to strength the ABS can also be helpful, but not all IC patients can do these types or workouts. All of these things are easy home workouts. Last edited by UCSB; 12-27-2006, 03:00 PM.

contents: In general, exercise helps me. The exercise I find most IC-friendly: 1. Stretching (I also worked with a physical therapist knowledgeable about IC on this) 2. Yoga 3. Walking 4. Elliptical machine 5. Rowing machine 6. Light weight training Exercise that has flared my IC: 1. Running 2. Treadmill (though I see that it's ok for Bill, and my mom also has IC & she has no problem with the treadmill) 3. Biking Try things out! Start off slow & build up

contents: Most of the time I have found walking helps. Unless I am having a really bad IC day then if I do it I end up feeling worse. I don't seem so tired after taking the walk too. Think if I sit for a long time it makes everything worse too.

I still love to ride my bike Excercise saves me everytime. I really go for the yoga cuz it makes me feel happy as well as makes me feel strong. I have a great women's bicycle seat with a hole cut out for the whole pedundal area to sit in: and with that I can still ride my bike - expecially on the road where it is nice and smooth and I can just tool along or push hard or sail down the hills with the wind in my face. I have to be very mindful of my body all the time, though. I never have the pain the day of the exercie. If I am going to be bad it will be the next day or two days later. So I make sure that I am having fun and that there is no tightness or difficulty and I make sure I stretch and COOL DOWN at the end of the ride. JUST STOPPING often can cause ouch ouch ouch. THe toughest part is DOING IT. GETTING UP. Especially when I am in a flare I just sometimes cannot force myself to get moving. Eventhough I know I will feel better. MY poor spouse has had to find so many new and different ways to say "hon. you're a *****. better go for a ride". Have a good day and I hope you are feeling good!
i like yoga and palaties ,it's a relaxing thing.

I didn't do much exercise for a long time. I used to love aerobics classes, but no more. Running to the bathroom two or three times in an hour was embarrassing no matter how I explained it to people. I still can't ride my bike no matter what seat we use. THAT is a heartbreaker for me. I just had to exchange those activities with new ones. I do walk and wear a pedometer. I finally just bit the bullet and bought a really good treadmill. I use it all the time now. I can even run for a bit on it without any problems. I also like the recumbant bikes at the gyms (and my physical therapist's office). Resistance exercises with bands and weights are also good for me. I do stomach crunches without any problem now, but I couldn't when I was first diagnosed. So............for those that can't do much now there is still hope that you can do more later!

contents: recumbent bicycles Just a quick bicycle note: me being a crazy bike person and bike mechanic etc. DId you ever consider a recumbent bike? They are not so expensive now ... check out Maxarya in canada and EZ Rider in the states. We rent them at our shop. You might be able to try one out at a local shop. Not to be pushy, but just an idea to get you out on a bike again. Take care, hope you're having a good day. kt

Hi kt, I use and love the stationary, recumbant bikes they often have in the little fitness rooms of hotels, etc. (I travel a lot for business.) I am not sure I would be a fan of sitting so close to the ground outdoors though. It is always a consideration though. I was able to ride my bike for about a year...two years ago. I was feeling really really good back then, then did some stupid thing like ate grapefruit all winter long. I really was fine with it, until one day BOOM, the IC hit back with a vengence...........it took me ages to get back into the swing of things. Hard lesson to learn. Maybe next summer. :-)

contents: Originally posted by curlycue Hi Kadi, I made a appointment today and they will see me on Friday. I know its sounds like I am over reacting but, things have been going so well for me in the last year and now this neck and shoulder thing is really bothering me.I now it might sound minor and like I am winning but I am really scared. Please keep me in your prayers. Thank You Ruth ... your doctor will be able to tell you what is wrong. But, I would not worry until Friday. When my IC symptoms initially appeared I developed a very tense, knotted area in my back. But, eventually used trigger point therapy (just pressure to relax the muscle) to solve the problem and it has not returned. Later physical therapy and an exercise program allowed me to greatly reduce the pain, soreness, and tension in my pelvic muscles. I don't know if you are a person that talks on the phone without holding it in your hand ... just supporting the phone with your shoulder and neck, but if you are, I would stop that right now. I talk on the phone often and am very careful not to ever support it with just my shoulder and neck. Good luck with solving this problem.

contents: I did my first strength exercising including sit-ups for the first time since taking the vicodin. Have been on it now for a few weeks and I did not have extra pain with the bladder from it. Not even from doing the sit-ups. Have the normal muscle pains that go along with first exercising but I’m not in so much bladder/stomach pain from it all. Wow, haven’t had that happen in I guess 9 years. Maybe now I can get rid of my big stomach? Only problem was I kept getting dizzy and I know that is from the vicodin. Any ideas on how to lesson that from happening? Wait until I tell my Uro next week. He’s on vacation this week. :woohoo: I also get dizzy from the vicodin. I don't know if anything will help to stop the dizziness. Glad the vicodin is working for you.

contents: I bought this new horse before I was diagnosed with IC even though I knew something was wrong. So far I don't have any trouble with riding, even after a tough dressage lesson. Sometimes my bladder feels tender afterwards, but it's worth it cause it provides some relief from thinking about it allllll the time! I plan on competing him in the spring if everything stays okay!

I think if I could still ride, I could get through anything. I think I posted here awhile back how I took a job at a dressage barn last year? The horses were massive warmbloods, and I'm only 5'4" on a good day. I spent the first day of work with my arms up all the time putting on bridles, saddles, being yanked around by giant three year olds in the round pen. By the end of the day I was in excruciating pain. I cried all the way home. Maybe if I had my own horse and wasn't trying to keep up with a trainer prepping 15 horses in a day, I could handle it, but *shiver*, that was just too much for me. Even though it was my dream job.

I work at a trucking company as a clerk/office float. I just took this position right after Thanksgiving. I work 5pm-1am Sun-Thurs. Before that, I had worked as a dispatcher working rotating 12 hour night shifts. My job sorta went from easy to boring...but I like it. I like that I get to work different shifts sometimes. Ive worked there since we opened about a year and half ago, and I know how to do every job in the office. Anyway, that means that my schedule can be sorta flexible. Well, Im getting married in September, and Ive got a few pounds to shed. (like 50) I get bored at the gym. I love to line dance so I was looking for some sort of dance exercise class. I was online and found a local Jazzercise class. The classes tho are from 530-630 on the other side of town. I talked to my boss and he said that I could come in late and work late on the nights that I have the class. How awesome is that?? I will start the class Jan 2. Wish me luck!

contents: Wow that's great that he is flexible from the get go.

That's great that you have a good boss who's easy going. I love doing all the dance aerobic stuff too. I've tried so many of them its hard to find new ones. Been doing Strip-aerobics lately. Love them. It's really fun and slowed paced. Hope all goes well!

After being a bum for about 2 years batteling IC I decided to try running yesterday and then do some sit ups to try and get rid of my stomach. After running I noticed this acidy taste, I had just taken my Elmiron pill about a half hour before. That was gross and felt light headed. Maybe I did to much?? I ran down huge hill and then all the way back up. (very far and hard) I felt like I was going to die! haha after all my exersizing my bladder felt like someone was standing on it. Alot of pressure on it. I tried to wait it out to see if it went away before I poped a codine. It did!!! Today I feel great! I hope I can keep exercising. I noticed a huge difference this morning. Usally I wake up in pain and have to take a pain med. Not today! Today is a good day!!!! :woohoo: Hope all is well with you. <3 Rachel

contents: I've heard that runners have that endorphin rush that certainly can make you feel better, even if you had to struggle, you finished a goal that you set for yourself and that in itself can be totally uplifting. I have this great home gym upstairs in our spare room, and I hear it call to me every single day. Reading your post has got me almost, notice I said almost, motivated to do some easy to moderate exercises on it. Just do not overdo it...better to start real slow and easy and work yourself up, otherwise you'll find yourself sore and unable to continue for a few days. Can ya tell I've done this before? I used to run, loved it...ran every single evening. Ha. Good luck!!!!

contents: Last Saturday, I had an awful bladder day, spent many hours in bed in pain. I forced myself to go through with our plans for the evening, went out to dinner with hubby, then out with a bunch of friends to go dancing to our favorite band. I basically made myself get out on that dance floor, even though I was hurting, and moved and shook my booty for a good three hours. Woke up Sunday morning PAIN FREE for the first time in a month!! I could not believe it!
That's great! Donna

contents: Hahaha, yep, we're going again on Saturday!! I've been feeling weird pains down in my lower hip joints, just below my abdomen. I'm hoping that moving/dancing will help loosen that up again. I'm going to force myself to get out there and boogie again. I've decided that I can sit around and focus on my pain/uncomfortableness, or I can get up, get moving, excersize and take my mind off it just a little bit, at least temporarily. My pain is not bad to begin with though, just constant pressure/frequency that I would rate maybe a 2 or 3.

Exercise is part of my pain management strategy I have found that exercise really helps me from a pain management perspective, particulary if I do it in the morning, when I tend to be in the least amount of pain. A medical student explained that exercise very often helps organ pain due to the chemicals being released by the brain during exercise. All the water that I drink during exercise also helps, as it keeps the urine diluted.

I def. agree it helps! I was in semi remission for several years and I got a horrible yeast infection and have had some bad symptoms ...so I started working out even more and I have been doing so much better since I have added more workout time.

It seemed that movement/dancing helped me so much that I just signed up to take a belly dancing class! It works your core muscles, abs and pelvic floor muscles. I'm feeling better this week, but even if I'm uncomfortable from my IC, I'm making myself get out there and get moving. :woohoo:

???!!! Endorphins are a group of ten neurotransmitters that activate opiate receptors. Endorphins are composed of chains of amino acids between five and several dozen members long (Hollt, 1983). Since the discovery of the endorphins in 1975, scientists have hypothesized that these neurotransmitters are released into synapses when the body encounters stress. After a physical injury, endorphins activate opiate receptors and produce an analgesic effect, alleviating severe pain. During times of emotional stress, endorphins are released in the limbic system of the brain and produce a euphoria that lessens anxiety and melancholy (Rang et al., 1995). Recently, scienctists have hypothesized that the release of endorphins is the neurochemical cause for the feeling of pleasure. For example, a marathon runner's "high," which has been compared to the "rush" following opioid use, is the product of endorphin release (Hawkes, 1992). Unlike opioids, the body's endorphins are not addicting. When endorphins are not activating receptors, no withdrawal symptoms are felt. Enzymes break down endorphins as soon as they act at receptors, so they are never in contact with receptors long enough to form tolerance or dependency. Researchers have been unable to produce drugs that are rapidly broken down by the body's enzymes, but still produce strong analgesia (Snyder, 1996).

contents: That's wonderful that you've found something to help! I notice a big difference after exercise as well.

I was thinking about trying out our local "Curves"and was wondering if anyone out there has tried it or is doing it now. I have a hard time exercising, but am in need of something that won't kill my bladder. Any suggestions?? Christi

I belonged to Curves a few years ago and my experience was not good, so I ended up joining a gym connected to a local hospital that has degreed fitness professionals and a long list of amenities (like a warm-water pool - my favorite place!) The problem with Curves, as I found out later, is that there isn't a great deal of consistency from one location to another. They're franchises, and some owners are more diligent about who they hire, etc. When I went to my local Curves, the women who worked there weren't very helpful - they would be standing around discussing their kids or their vacation plans rather than help the women who were using the equipment incorrectly! If you have any physical problems such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, etc, Curves may aggravate the problem, and you'd be better off going to a gym staffed by people with formal knowledge about anatomy, etc.

I used to manage a Curves. Each Curves is very different. The thing to not, is you do not have to be a fitness instructor to work their. This i always had a hard time with. Basicly the ladies that they hire, watch a video, and they are then qualified to be a fitness tech. I would say if you have a lot of pain, do not join a Curves. I'm a pilates instructor, and i do love doing pilates, another avanue is Yoga. Just some ideas. Carrie Good luck to you

I wouldn't go to a Curves myself. Too many exercises involving bouncing, too quick moving from one machine to another, too many people yakking. I love my 24 Hour Fitness. The staff is friendly, machines are usually available (I use the elliptical & rowing machines & a couple of the weight machines), it's right on the way home from work, and it's $36 a month unlimited visits (a pretty standard rate for the SF Bay Area). I also like that it's right next to a Safeway, so I can pick up stuff for dinner afterwards. Every club is different though. I changed from another 24 Hour Fitness after a trainer there was really rude to me, and I was constantly frustrated by the wait for machines and the snobby people. So, do check out the club personally & make sure you like it.

When my IC was more symptomatic, I took gentle yoga classes at a yoga center nearby. It was a bit expensive, but really helped with the pain & stress! There is a yoga video for IC, available in the ICN store. It's pretty easy, good way to get back into exercise, then move on to a more challenging yoga tape after that...

contents: Hey there readingmom! I go to Curves and I have to say I love it. (As you can see though, everyone's experience etc is different, lol) I have never had a problem with it, as I found for me, it's easy for me to set my own pace. On the "recovery stations" I walk in place gently if things are a wee bit touchy, as long as I keep moving. No jogging in place for me, lol. And I have no problem on the machines or the rotation (but usually when I go there's just myself and a couple other women. I just modify as I need, and the owner (and staff) is very helpful to show me alternatives - plus I can hop out of the circuit any time nature calls, lol. Our club has um, a lot of older women, so there are a few who can't do some of the machines, but they will do stretches of sorts in place of that machine, etc. When I had a sprained ankle she also showed me alternatives to keep my workout routine, but be easy on the body. I have had such a great experience with mine. I had to miss about a month or so, due to some physical therapy I needed to do, and my owner never fails to call and check up on me if she hasn't seen me in a while, it's so nice that she cares enough to do that - I also love I have built a personal relationship with her and the staff - I even discovered another ICer there, she happens to be part of the staff! (In my rural area, I was shocked and "happy" to find another ICer, lol) And everyone at mine is so darn supportive! I have lost 6 pounds since the last time I was weighed in (it had been awhile as I was off there for that PT), and you'd have thought I'd found the cure for cancer, lmao! It was nice to have some cheerleaders!! I do know you can take a tour, and maybe get to "try out" the equipment etc, I would talk to the owner if that person is very involved in running it like mine is, or whoever has the "power" and let that person know your situation and see what you can do, as far as a test-drive, lol. I hope you find whatever happens to be right for you, then the challenge becomes just getting yourself there, lol!! I go right after work, otherwise I'd get home and somehow talk myself out of going... ugh. Hugs!

contents: Thank you for the imput!! I just saw this morning on t.v. that they are having a special where you can try it for one week for free!! Yeah! I love free!! I think I'm going to try it and see what happens. It really sounds like it depends on the people working there if you have a good experience or not. I had my husband go and buy one of those Gazelle machines yesterday because it's supposed to be low impact. I tried it this morning and didn't have any trouble(except for being out of shape lol.) I would love to be able to have somewhere to go for support, though.

What a great time to check it out then!! I too have to say FREE is great!! That's good stuff, lol!!! I really like it because I can set my own pace. Like today, in the middle of PMS, and just lacking energy so I just slowed my pace down and took it easy. Do keep us posted on what you find out and if you like it or not! I have to say I have made some new friends, it's neat. Even my husband noticed how um, much more "crabby" I am went I wasn't going thanks to having to go to physical therapy for my back, lol. I never realized I'd come home and talk about what I learned from one of the ladies! (most of them are older and wiser!! ) Hugs!

I have tried yoga before, and I really did not like it at all. I know that it benefits a lot of people, but it realy isn't for me. Thank you for the suggestion, though, I really appreciate it. I am doing the Gazelle machine at home now, and I like it. It is very low impact and it is not hurting me any. Thank you again!!

I alternate between 30 minutes of cardio or cardio with weights on average 5 days a week. The other days I have yoga and soccer. I haven't always been this active. Wondering if it has been contraindicated by physicians or self help books to work out at this level?
I would think as long as it's not affecting your symptoms, etc it's fine for you. Obviously, if it was making you feel horrible, I would say probably might want to scale it back, lmao!!!

signature: IC for 10 years Comorbid vulvulitis, depression, Happily married Lucky to have this forum I feel your pain (in more ways than one) What I miss most: fruit and eating normally

contents: I did the free week and ended up not joining. I went to New York Sports instead. I know that exercise helps my pain. When I did the free week I wanted to lose some weight for my daughter's wedding. I found out from Curves that the one in my area would only let you go 3 times a week and you could go around their circle of exercise equipment only twice each time. I could not see how I would be able to lose any weight that way. It was not enough exercise. Ginny
is yoga okay to do? i need to do something. i am already a big lady...and getting biggere everyday.

My doctor suggests yoga especially for relaxation. So far all I have have done is look at the DVDs in the store. I have bought some relaxation cds to listen to when I am trying to go to sleep or taking a hot (get rid of this pain) bath. I have noticed a difference.

yoga THere is a yoga video in the ICN shop that is specifically for IC. I ordered it and Part I is pretty basic. I also have pelvic pain and stretching and yoga help me. You just need to be careful not to do any "Power" yoga that may be too strenous. I also went to a physical therapist for a year. Good luck!
www.ic-network./com/chat
signature: One Day At A Time Kathi

contents: Does anyone know any core exercises that won't throw me into a flare? I'd like to strengthen my core muscles but (obviously) can't do crunches or sit ups, they throw me into an awful flare. I do do yoga and know that certain movements do strengthen my core muscles, but would like to know if there are other things icers do to strengthen those muscles. Thanks.
http://www.squeezeoc.com/squeezeoc/livingitup/wellbeing/article_1170672.php I think this may help you....

Core exercises I get great results from a dvd I use called yogalates -- it is a combination of gentle yoga and pilates moves. I am not able to do much in yoga but I can do this dvd without any problems (other than the motivation to do it daily) however, her focus in at least the first section is on core muscles. I will try to recall a few of the things I do on it.. the deep breathing and the method of breathing is the main principle that I think is responsible for the core strengthening. Lie on your back arms at your sides, palms up and if you can, lay on a mat or carpeted area. If you need a small pillow under your head, place it there. place one hand on your abdomen and begin to inhale slowly, but deeply through your nose -- as you inhale, fill your lungs, and suck your abs in toward your back -- use your hand to be sure you are pulling in the lower abs... Leave your belly pulled in and exhale slowly --- then begin to take another deep breath and pull your belly in even further... continue to do this and you will feel your abs working as you try to keep your abs firm while doing the inhale and exhale several times over. Using this same breathing techniqe, take a band -- and put it around the ball of one foot, then as you raise the leg into the air pull down on the bands and inhale (remembering to pull those abs in) I cannot give you the exact moves here, because you would need to see it done, but you can raise and lower your leg in time with this inhale, exhale and abs in method. Then you can also let your leg fall outward putting the band in the hand of the leg that is being let fall outward --- the other hand goes on your abdomen (opening your hand wide as you do this will help you to focus on getting your upper and lower abs pulled to your back bone. DO NOT ARCH YOUR BACK while doing any of this. I don't really think I can go a lot further -- but if you are interested in the DVD I can get the exact name and the lady who leads it -- it is wonderful! Oh, be sure to do the last thing on both legs... I find that if I am not feeling like doing the whole thing (lazy??? tired????) If I just lie on the floor and do the relaxed breathing as well as roll my head from side to side with the breathing, I also get very relaxed. You can do this in bed when you wake up!!! Gets the blood flowing! Best to you. Mary

Does yoga really help? +other questions i am newly diagnosed and looking for alternative exercise options. i don't want to go to a yoga class 'cause i can't find anybody that will do it w/ me. you mentioned a dvd (was it yogaletes?) and i wondered if it was an easy level deal (flexibility is definitely NOT a strength of mine). if it is, i would really appreciate finding out how to get it. i also had some other ?s i was hoping somebody might be able to help me out with: 1. i read somewhere in here that falling and injuring yourself could be a cause of ic. -when i was 3, wearing only a diaper and riding on my tricylcle, i fell and injured myself. my parents came and checked on me, but i apparently acted okay. then, i did the exact same thing again and cried like no other. each time i've gone through growing spurts in my life, i have had terrible pain there because, as my body grew, so did the scar tissue down there. a pediatrician gave me cream to use every day, but i didn't use it 'cause i was too embarrassed. (it was dumb, i know) does anybody know if this could be a factor in my ic (or in my IBS or endometriosis)? 2. IS ANYBODY ELSE SICK OF HEARING THE PHRASE, "AREN'T YOU TOO YOUNG FOR THIS?"- 'CAUSE I AM! -is there anybody else out there who can relate to this feeling? nobody in my high school has these problems (that i know of) besides my 15-year old sister. i would like to know that i am not the only one who feels this way. thanks to any and all for reading at least part of this HUGE entry. -trinklebell Last edited by trinklebell; 10-12-2006, 10:09 AM.

ab lounge here's something that's a bit expensive but may help with ab exercises. my fiance got an ab lounge. it basically looks like reclining patio furniture. you sit in it and lean back as far as you can go, then let your abs pull you back to your starting position. instead of doing crunches, you're doing the opposite motion. i lost 10 pounds using it and have nicely defined, strong abs again. i had to stop running because of ic pain the ab lounge, or ab lounge ultra is between $100-$150 i think. it really works though and doesn't cause pain because you control the range of motion. oh, and for tinkerbell, my ic started when i was 18, at the beginning of college. now i'm 22 and everyone still tells me i'm too young to have this.

reply to ab lounge... whoa, i thought i edited that entry a long time ago (like 5 minutes after i first posted it). i must have not saved it right......i don't know. anyway, my aunt has an ab lounge; she said i can use it anytime i want, i just haven't. now that i know it's easier on your muscles, i should give it a try. i thought i took that part about being too young out, but since you've (squirrelmonkey2) already replied to it, it's nice to know i'm not the only one. i was diagnosed w/ IBS when i was 12. I was diagnosed with endometriosis when i was 15. doctors suspected i had it when i was 13-14 and treated me with birth control pills, but i wasn't i didn't have a laparoscopy to officially diagnose it til i was 15. just last month, i was diagnosed w/ ic. (i'm 17 now.) so, i've definitely heard the phrase "you're too young for this," countless times. anyway, thanks for the tip about the ab lounge. -trinklebell

are ic patients allowed to swim? does it depend on the person? i love to swim, but i always experience burning and pain in my bladder afterwards, whether i swim in the ocean, a lake, or a pool. before, i ignored the pain. but now that i have officially been diagnosed and have received info about it, i know i have to change my lifestyle. if elmiron or other treatments help me, will i be allowed to swim in the future? i can't imagine never being allowed to swim again. anyway, if anyone knows or has had experiences such as this, i would appreciate the info. -trinklebell

Swimming...rather "being in the pool or ocean" has never bothered me. I do know it is difficult for some ICers. It is such a great form of exercises, I am sorry it's difficult for you. Do you feel it's the muscles used or the water itself that bothers you? I do know many here say they must immediately shower and change out of their suits after swimming, etc. I hope you can find a solution, I think any exercise we can find is typically helpful in the long run. Good luck, hopefully someone will come along with a much better suggestion or understanding about this.
signature: Hugs, Betsie ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ "No one can make you feel inferior without your consent." Eleanor Roosevelt, This Is My Story, 1937

contents: I both swim and sit in the hot tub. I simply make sure not to sit around too long in a wet bathing suit afterwards, because I am prone to yeast infections, and this can help them breed up.
signature: **** Jen *Diagnosed with severe IC in 2004 *Also diagnosed with PFD, fibromyalgia, chronic myofascial pain, IBS, migraines, allergies/asthma, dermatographism *Kept trying a million different treatments for all these things until I found what works, and I am doing okay these days with the help of a cocktail of medications and the InterStim, which was first placed in 2007. [I have had 2 revisions - one in 2010 when my battery died and had to be replaced, and one complete replacement (lead and generator) in 2012 after a fall on my stairs caused my lead to move.] *Current meds include Atarax (50mg at night), Lyrica (150mg twice a day), Xanax (0.5mg at night and as needed), Zanaflex (4mg at night), hydrocodone (10/325, every 6 hours as needed), Advair, Nasonex, Singulair (10mg at night), oral contraceptives, home instills containing Elmiron and Marcaine (as often as I need to do them). **I am not a medical authority nor do I offer definitive medical advice. I strongly encourage you to discuss your medical treatment with your personal medical care provider. Only they can, and should, give medical recommendations to you.

contents: I don't do well in a pool with clorine, but in summer I swim every day. We use Baquacil in our pool. Like others, when I get out of the pool, I can't sit around in a wet swim suit. The ocean is too cold on our Oregon coast, but I did swim in the ocean in Hawaii and did very well as long as I showered and put on dry pants afterwards. Donna

contents: I did lots of swimming this summer. I to can not sit around in wet cloths or suits I dry off shower and change works for me. Kelly

contents: Swimming the reasons i love swimming are: 1. i get to be in the water! 2. it's easy on my muscles (i had physical therapy for a shoulder injury and i found that pool therapy was the easiest on my muscles) so, to answer your (beachbaby's) question: no, it's not the muscles i use in swimming that irritate me; i find swimming relaxing on my muscles (much better than biking, that's for sure! ouch!). however, after about five minutes immersed in any water, i always feel irritation. previously, i ignored it and tried to distract myself by talking w/ my friends or to just keep moving around. i fgured it was just 'cause of my bladder infections that never went away, and i didn't want to stop swimming. however, sometimes it can take me up to twenty minutes to an hour before i get to a shower. ( i like to sun dry before i get in my car so i don't have to get everything wet. plus, i don't like having to pull off a sticky wet swimsuit; i'd rather it be dry.) i'll talk to my doctor and see what she says. -trinklebell

It was only today that my little boy asked me why I don't come to the beach and swim with him. Its a little difficult to explain it. I am just plain scared to go into the water, too scared for what pain it might cause me later on.

nss- if you're too worried about getting all the way in the water, maybe you could just go like up to your knees or something. i have six younger siblings, and the littler ones don't mind if i am all the way in--they just want me to at least get wet somewhere- like putting my feet in and walking around. also, i miss alot of their sports games b/c of my endo and ic. although they don't completely understand what that means, i know it's easier for them if i at least try to explain that my bladder and stomach hurts; that way, they know that i don't come to some of their games not because i don't want to, it's just that i can't stand up or sit on bleachers for a long amount of time. best wishes, trinklebell

I was just reading about your water problems. Ugh, I am sorry to hear it does cause you discomfort. I hope you can find a solution. I was interested in your shoulder/therapy in the water. I just had a follow up to a shoulder problem yesterday and am headed to PT in a few weeks. No tear, thankfully but some narrowing where it counts and a pretty good tendinitis, that causes it to slip out of the socket. I am likely headed for back surgery after seeing a few surgeons, but in the meantime, I am off to warm temps and can swim (I think: shoulder is so painful). I am trying to get back to being in decent condition, especially if we decide on surgery. I should pursue water therapy, my PT's tried for this over the summer, but I couldn't get into the colder Atlantic long enough for it to matter. Good luck, it sounds like swimming is very important to you, and I do get it about shedding a wet suit...ick!
signature: Hugs, Betsie ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ "No one can make you feel inferior without your consent." Eleanor Roosevelt, This Is My Story, 1937

contents: Water Therapy Beachbaby- my physical therapy place had a heated pool in it, though it was tiny. (well, one of them did. i went to a different place when i broke my knee cap 'cause my old therapist that i really liked had moved away.) also, i live in missouri, so if i was gonna go the natural-water way, i'd be stuck in a freezing lake. (when i made reference to the ocean earlier, it was 'cause i've taken vacations down in florida before, including last summer; my extended family lives there.) anyway, my shoulder's messed up 'cause my bones aren't completely fused together in there. when it gets pushed on or under stress, one of the bones likes to poke into a little bag of fluid (called the bursa) and get it enflamed and make me have bursitis. so, every time i carried my backpack, lifted heavy things (like my little brothers), or raised my hand above my head, it would hurt. the regular PT was too harsh on it, and i felt that doing the band exercises they gave me just made it worse. so, they thought pool therapy would be easier on it, and it was. it helped to have time to warm up (just moving my hands in the water) so my muscles would relax a little first. also, in most of the exercises in the pool, i didn't have to raise my hand above my head. the therapy didn't take all my pain away (my docs said that it wouldn't completely go away until my bones fused together, and, since i've started growing later rather than sooner, it could be until i'm in my 20's before they do.) but, i do feel that pool therapy helped a lot more than regular therapy. swimming is a big thing for me. my 15-year-old sister and i live w/ my mom, but i have 4 brothers and 1 sister that live w/ my dad and stepmom. (they're not full-blooded siblings like my 15-year-old sister is, but that has never mattered to me. i love them just the same.) i don't get to see them very much, especially in the school year, but in the summer i get to see them more often when we swim together or when i watch their baseball games. my littlest sister (she's 9. by the way-i'm 17) and i like to go to the public pool, but the rest of the family likes to go on my dad's boat or to my aunt's house, where she has a heated outdoor pool. also, when i was little and my parents first split up, some of my best memories w/ my dad were when he took me, my sister, and sometimes my friends out on a boat he shared w/ his best friend. so, ya, swimming has always been associated w/ good things for me. anyway, i hope you can find relief from your shoulder pain. one thing i did was to switch to using a rolly backpack (much to the annoyance of almost every kid in school, and i am so not kidding. now, i've seen more kids switch, and i see that it really is annoying to have to walk behind it! lol) also, i stood on a stool when i put dishes away so i wouldn't have to reach over my head. i eventually had to learn not to pick up heavy things, and it helped when i told my friends about my shoulder thing. they stopped me or took my things whenever they saw me trying to do more than i should. some teachers and kids called me lazy, but when they found out why i was doing it, they felt really bad. thanks for pushing through this terribley long post, and i hope this helps. -trinklebell

I finally stopped snacking and haven't had any sweets and have lost 7 pounds. Gave up on the counting calories and slim fast. I eat just 3 meals a day and eat what I want (except sweets) but I only eat so much. Must say my belly feels somewhat better. Am doing this the slow way and no crash dieting so will take me a while to get down to 115 pounds but I think I will be able to make it that far now.
Sounds Good I wish I had your willpower. I'd also like to get down to 115lbs. I'm sure I'd do well if I knocked off the sweets too. I'll start Monday.

The first 3 days were the hardest. I still will get the craving for cookies, cake or something but I think about the many cloths I have and I want to be thin again. Plus losing those few pounds helps too. Took so long to get them off and would be so easy to put them back on. Good luck to you on Monday.

Well today I went to the gym. I've been reading all the different threads on here and in books and decided I'd try a recumbent bike. So far I am happy to say that after 13 minutes :woohoo: (I stopped because I started feeling a few twinges in my abdomen) that other than my legs being tired and my back aching a bit I feel good! I will have to see what tomorrow brings because I've heard that sometimes flares start the next day. So keep your fingers crossed! I've got to start exercising and get my weight down. I've had a lot of stress in my life over the past 3 years with my mother in law living with me then my son and his girlfriend moving in because they both lost their jobs. 3 years later the kids are still with me. I love them dearly but my husband and I need our house back. My weight keeps creeping up and with all the activity in the house I haven't been exercising. I so want this to work! I want to feel good again! Anyway, I'm excited to have gone. My husband says if I can tolerate the recumbent bike he'll buy us one. I have a cardio glide (not sure if anyone remembers them) and can use that a little, but tire too easily. Want to try yoga but one thing at a time!
signature: Positive thinking and trusting that everything happens for a reason and in time even the bad times will bring sunshine and happiness into your life! I've had IC for 5 or more years, but never knew it. All that time I thought it was my back. Fortunately I was lucky and found a doctor that took me seriously and sent me immediately to a Urologist that knew right off that I had IC and tested me for it.:woohoo: Then I found this site and have and all it's wonderful people and found hope. I have: Interstitial Cystitis: Dx Aug 9,2006 Rx: Elmiron, Advil and Darvocet Diabetic: Dx 1997 Rx: Glipizide Meneieres Disease: Dx 1997 Rx: Valium Anxiety, Depression, Obsessive/Compulsive: Dx: 1995 Rx: Effexor I have 3 wonderful sons, :woohoo: And an amazing husband Life is good and everything in time! Sassy

contents: Hi Sassy, I find that the elliptical trainer at the gym works well if I keep the resistance low & the rowing machine is good for me too. Maybe that will give you a few options. And you are smart to try one machine at a time, so that if you do flare, you'll know the "culprit"! Exercise helps my IC and my mood. Hope it works out well for you too!
signature: Kadi ------------------------------------------------------------- I am not a medical authority nor do I offer medical advice. In all cases, I strongly encourage you to discuss your medical treatment with your personal medical care provider. Only they can, and should, give medical recommendations to you. ------------------------------------------------------ New favorite quote: "God gives us only what we can handle. Apparently God thinks I'm a bad-ass" ~Author Unknown Source - Pinterest " Current treatments: -IC diet -Elavil 50mg at night -Continuous use birth control pills (4-5 periods/year) -Heparin/Marcaine/Sodium Bicarb home instills at night 3-4x per week, more often if needed -Pyridium if needed, -Pain medicine at bedtime daily, as needed during the day several times per week -Antibiotic when doing an instillation to prevent UTI -Colace & SmartFiber to treat chronic constipation from meds, Fleet enema as needed -Dye Free Benadryl 50 mg at bedtime -"Your Pace Yoga: Relieving Pelvic Pain" dvd, walking, treadmill at gym -Managing stress= VERY important! -Fur therapy: Hugging the cat!

contents: Hoping you are still feeling alright after the workout. I also use the elliptical machine, but can also use the stair climber for 10 min. at a time.

Hi! My husband and I purchased a spa and it is ready to go. I just don't know what chemicals to use safely. I was given a start up kit of " Baqua Spa" from the company after telling them of my "chemical sensitivities" (I thought that would be the most discrete way of putting it). My sister uses Nature2 cartridges with "Natural mineral purifiers" and loves it. My question is, what chemicals and minerals, if any, do I need to stay away from? I want to start out right and not have to drain my pool and start over. Please let me know if anyone has any information on this. Thanks a bunch! KK
signature: Hopekeepers Support Group Leader Diagnosed IC 1/07/04 Fibromyalgia, IBS, PFD, Urethral Instability, GERD, Chronic Unexplained Pelvic Pain for most of my life Remember, all is not lost as long as we keep our sense of humor!

contents: The most common irritant is clorine. We use Baquacil for our swimming pool and it's great --- absolutely no odor, crystal clear, and not irritating. The Baqua Spa is made by the same company. We need to shop for a new spa; unless we can get ours repaired --- and I will plan to use Baqua Spa. Donna

contents: Donna, Thanks for getting back to me so quickly. I have confidently used the BaquaSpa start up chemicals to begin enjoying my spa. Do I need to wait any length of time above the usual after shocking the spa? -I'm only familiar with shock from the days where you shocked a pool and couldn't swim for almost 2 days afterwards. ( it has been a while...lol) I was really worried about the whole "chemical" thing. I purchased the spa for my PFD and possible Fibro (I go to Dr. tomorrow for that). I didn't want to heal one thing and anger another... Again, thank you for your input. Have a happy flare free day! KK

contents: I don't know about spa shock, but you can swim just a few hours after shocking the pool. They told me only an hour, but we waited about six hours just to be certain. Donna

contents: Is anyone interested in doing some exercise type activities (I hate to say work out!) and have an accountability partner? It doesn't have to be the same person each day, but we could use the forum to check in with each other and PM something like "Going to ride the bike for 1/2 today from 3-3:30 wanna join me?" and then together you know you are riding with someone else -- and when you check back on the board afterward, it will feel great to know that someone else did it with you -- just a thought! The other may be something I have found works really well -- you get exercise since any housecleaning is generally pretty active -- and you get some room or area of your house cleaned up. For example PM something like: "Heading to do a load of laundry and will dust the living room while it is washing... then while it is in the dryer, I am going to vacuum that same room and put old magazines in a bag to take to the recycle center on Saturday.. wanna join me?" If we find someone else that is just needing a little 'motivation' or 'push' to get started, it can work well -- you can even have three or four or ten doing this at the same time and everyone wins! If anyone else is interested, I am up for it -- I love knowing that someone is going to check on my progress because it truly does cause me to do a little more than if I don't have company coming or anyone to question what I accomplished that day. Other suggestions? Hugs, Mary heading to unload the dishwasher!

Well, it worked for me! I emptied the dishwasher and said "Nothing is going to stay on the counter -- put everything away!" Got that done, and thought, heck, now that the counter is cleared off, I may as well wash it down good -- did that and felt motivated to do the other counter as well. So, all the dishes are put away, two out of the four counters are washed and look wonderful and there are no dishes in the sink because I reloaded the dishwasher -- All this to return to find that the download for my broken sound has completed (a two hour download for my dial up computer) and I ran the program -- I HAVE SOUND!!! I HAVE SOUND!!! It's been over a month since I have had sound and it is wonderful to be able to hear all the messages that came over my "Callwave" for the past month that I was unable to listen to. Anyhow, I am on a roll! Think I should go get those last two counters and feel even better about the way it looks in here!

Wow, this is spooky. I was thinking about this yesterday. How I wished I had someone to do exercising with and such. I'll join if it is ok with you. Tomorrow morning I will get the dishes in the dishwasher (a big big task) and get a load of laundry going. Then walk on my treadmill for at least 10 minutes. That is a good start for the morning I think. What do you have planned?

Plan for Thursday Okay Waterflow (sorry, had the wrong name earlier) -- wlecome aboard! I will probably get on line before I start anything else -- and will see if you have been in to respond -- I am heading off to bed and if the headcold doesn't let up, I may not be up as early as usual -- started out thinking it was just allergies because two nights ago and then again last night, I woke up with horrible sore throat -- now tonight, my head is all plugged up and my ears are stuffy and I am blowing like a horn! Anyhow, that doesn't have anything to do with this.. so, I will be doing my bike -- although, I like to do at least a 1/2 hour and sometimes 1 hour -- I do it while watching television so time flies and I don't think about how long I am riding. I will also plan to do the other two counters tomorrow along with sweeping and then mopping the kitchen floor -- it is nasty and I have wanted to get it done for a few days -- so that is my short term goal --- I guess I will say I need to do that by noon! Let me know what time is good for you to get back on and I will check in with you on your treadmill and dishwashing goals! Last edited by ShePurzz; 08-31-2006, 02:20 AM.

Count me in!!! Hi, Mary. I will join you. I know I hate the word " Work Out " either. so do you count house cleaning as an exercise as well???? I think anything you use your body is an exercise. Cleaning the house, gardening, taking out trash and so on. The important thing is to be active, right??? Not sitting on the couch and watching TV and munching a load of junk food, right??? We as human being eat, and sleep without conscious decision. However, exercise is the one we always tend to neglect in our daily lives. This is a great idea!!! We support one another. My PT said that I need to do 30mins of daily activity at least. It seems pretty easy to do, but you know, when you have IC, it's totally different story......I usually play tennis with my friends when I get a chance. and trying to do 2-3 cardio/week. so what's the plan???
