contents: Sorry to hear about the cold but at least it isn't when the heat is 100 degrees. HOpe you get to feeling better. I did two loads of laundry this morning so far, dishes in the dishwasher and walked my 10 minutes. Tonight I want to get ALL dishes cleaned up and put away, counter cleaned off and take my 1/2 of walking. Of course I go to have the heparin done this afternoon so the walking might not happen. Depends on how bad the pain is but those are my plans. How did you do so far today? Sunflower-you can play tennic with your IC? That is really great. Wish I could but that back and forth running would more then likely kill it.

a plan? Originally posted by Sunflower2 ... so do you count house cleaning as an exercise as well???? ...so what's the plan??? Housecleaning IS exercise if you ask me -- an exercise in self discipline! And as far as a plan??? Hmmmm, no plan here... just start moving! I woke up two days ago with a very sore throat, took something and felt better, yesterday, same thing, took something, laid back down and felt a LOT better when I woke up. Today, I have the 'creepin crud' and it has crept right into my lungs and head and throat and I feel like a giant yuck bug!!! Crud! My ears and my head and my sinuses are stuffed and hurting and I am coughing my lungs out and I hate it because I get pneumonia so darned easily... CRUD! I cancelled a doctor appointment this week because I didn't have the money to go, and now I am going to have to figure a way to go -- I normally end up on antibiotics because of the tendency toward pneumonia, but I have been trying to get through without taking meds the last couple of times this snuck up on me -- LITERALLY -- I wake up and it is there -- now what do you suppose happens??? Some little rotten pneumonia infected BUG crawls up into my bed, finds ME, never my husband... and then gets in my nose while I am sleeping, works his way down into my lungs and builds his home???? For heaven sake -- how does one get this sick overnight! At least this time I had a two day warning, but really thought it was allergies -- this hurts to breathe and I am coughing like a smoker's cough and ugh... I hate getting sick! Okay, exercise... Well, I didn't ride the bike yet, and didn't do the kitchen floor --- have a feeling those will be later today IF today -- I don't want to do the floor cause I don't want to smell the cleaner and add to this grief! Water, I can not believe how much you did already this morning -- :woohoo: You keep going -- I will catch up with you if/when the head clears a bit and I can breath again. I also started my period () so riding the bike should be fun!!! But, I will at least do a walk outside because it is sunny and that should feel good on my chest. I am also going to do my usual protocol when I get hit with this CRUD -- I get a small towel wet with very hot water... load up my chest with Vicks VapoRub and get a heating pad. Put the wet towel on my chest and then a dry towel, then the heating pad... watch a good movie or two and keep turning the hot towel over -- it soaks down into your chest (well, not really, but it feels good anyhow) and hopefully loosens anything before it has a chance to invite more friends in for a two week visit! Water -- you go girl... I will check in later if/when I feel up to it. In the meantime, I would appreciate a few prayers sent this way to help knock this right out of me before it turns into anything! Thanks, Mary

Hi, Mary. glad to hear you feel better today. I too have allergy, but I try not to take meds as Minimize as possible. I don't like to depend on meds so much. I try to take meds as needed. It's been nasty weather over here due to Earnest coming up soon. I think we had 4-8inches of rain since yesterday. We have a flood warning because of it. So no good for outdoor exercise today. Maybe try some tredmill today Water- I have been playing tennis ever since I was in high school. That was wayyyyyyyyyyyy before I was dx with my IC. I do get some irration on my lower abs when I play tennis. However, I hate to give in my passion because of IC. IC will not take over my life. I refuse to give in.

contents: Better????? Originally posted by Sunflower2 Hi, Mary. glad to hear you feel better today. Aya, Who said better? If I get any worse, you guys will need to send flowers!!! ACCCKKKKKKKKK!!! I am dyin here..... send prayers and good health wishes! hittin the sack with a hot drink. Last edited by ShePurzz; 08-31-2006, 04:00 AM.

aid back down and felt a LOT better when I woke up. Today, I have the 'creepin crud' and it has crept right into my lungs and head and throat and I feel like a giant yuck bug!!! Crud! woops, I missed the period before Today... my bad. Sorry that you feel worse. Just take a nice rest and hopefully feel better tomorrow.
signature: Aya IC Diagnosed in September, 2005 My conditions are : IC, PFD, Allergies, and Constipation "It's not easy being grateful all the time. But it's when you feel least thankful that you are most in need of what gratitude can give you." — Oprah Winfrey

contents: Well, so far, my productivity consists of cleaning out the humidifier and getting all the lyme out so I could refill it with filtered water and get it running -- so that is done. I also made a pot of coffee -- yum -- but can't taste or smell it! I also let the dog out and in and out and in a few times, got the mail out of the box, and tried to run a virus checker on my computer -- hmmm... busy day huh? I did sit out in the sun to let my chest absorb the warmth -- oh, I also got up and took some allergy medicine. I haven't showered or done my hair -- but I did have ex-mother-in-law over for a visit -- other than that -- I guess my goals for today are a bit out of my reach -- I will hopefully wake up completely healed of this creeping crud and get the floor done tomorrow -- OH, I did a load of laundry -- they are in the dryer -- so I did accomplish SOMETHING! Probably call in a dinner order though -- don't think anyone wants to eat anything I have sneezed all over... haaaaa chooooo! Okay, don't let me stop this organized activity/exercise program. What's on the list for tonight or tomorrow? Anyone for a nice long walk this evening -- I may be able to handle that! Dog would sure love it! He is always happy to go rouse up the neighborhood kitties! What about tomorrow? Any takers for cleaning the carpets -- I am going to have to buck up and do it! I need to shampoo them while I can still keep the windows and doors open to dry them out good... maybe I will try to just get today's stuff done and let the carpets go until after Labor Day weekend.... Water, where are you??? Are you still cleaning and doing dishes? How long has it been since you did the dishes...? I used to leave them grow on the counter -- don't know where I learned that habit, but for the last 8 years I don't do it anymore -- probably because I have a dishwasher now, so I rinse them and put them in right away -- then actually run the washer every 2 or 3 days when it fills up. Okay, time to get the stuff out to color my hair -- daughter is due home in a few minutes... Ta Ta For Now! Mary

Mary you should just take it easy for the next couple of days until those uninvited guest decide to leave. Hey, how come they decided to join you and not your husband?? You must be an extra nice hostess. Tell you what. This is a sure cold/germ/virus cure. Works all the time. Tell the wonderful germ if he and his buddies do not leave your bod within the next 12 hours then I--waterflow---will come and do the cooking. My cooking is registered as a leathal weapon and has been known to keep the most unwanted guest away. Really, I have had people never come back again after eating one of my wonderful home cooked meals and even if you sneezed and coughed all over the meal you made they would still choose yours to eat over my not contaminated with germs meal. Sunflower, I wish I could still play tennis. I know what you mean about not wanting to give in to the IC. If you can still play it that's great. :woohoo: Well lets see. I did get my 10,000 steps in today. I'm going to shoot more for the steps thing instead of how many minutes on the treadmill. did get the dishes washed but not put away but they are ALL washed. Oh, I've gotten lazy with keeping the dishes all washed too. I have a dishwasher but don't always feel like emptying and refilling. I did buy the powerball dishwasher cleaner stuff. Bought the liquid kind but that left half of the dirt on the dishes. Lets see what to do tomorrow. I want to make bread in the morning. Get 10,000 steps in again. Oh, I'll clean the wood flooring downstairs. I don't have carpeting. Clean the stove which is a job. Have to take it all apart. It's a big job. Can take up to 1 hour. I know...my big project for tomorrow along with the rest will be to make the kitchen/dining room spotless. Other then that, that's all I can think of. I really am going to tackle the kitchen. The rest of the house will look like a disaster but hey...dust bunnies are my pets. Don't have to feed them much and when they do eat they are not picky at all.

I was thinking. (Which can be a dangerous thing.) What happens if we can't get here everyday? I've been having a hard time getting on the internet and sometimes on the weekend I won't be able to make it. Don't want anyone to think I forgot about them.

crawled up to the keyboard..... Water, If I can crawl to the keyboard to check on your messages, you can surely get there in your healthy body... I am now sore from the middle of my chest up to the top of my head -- I cough incessently and I really sound a lot like a ship in the night -- HOOOOOOOOOOONKK!! My dog doesn't even want to sit next to me... Anyhow, I will check in again tomorrow but with the holiday, don't know if we will take a nice drive or do some yardwork and get ready for big mr. snow..... So, have a great holiday weekend and try to get out and enjoy the beautiful weekend -- the sun shined all day today and there was a really nice breeze... however, some of us were too stinkin' grouchy to really enjoy it. Night... going to get my kleenex and head to bed... haaaaaaaa choooooo!

I have done my cardio for 40 mins yesterday:woohoo: Today, I will try tredmill for 40 mins because the weather is not that great again!!!!I hate this cloudy/rainy weather. It is very depressing. Mary, how are you feeling today??? Any better??? Hope that you get bettter for holiday weekend. It's not fun being sick on holiday. Water, do you have a bread maker to make bread??? I too make my own bread. It is ALL DAY work to make bread. I hope that I can get a bread maker for Christmas present or something. 10,000 step is a good work. I used to walk 15,000-20,000 for a day to be active when I was younger. I probably wouldn't walk a half of that much since my job is desk work and don't need to move around that much. I try to be more active around the house at least. Hope that you have a good weekend
signature: Aya IC Diagnosed in September, 2005 My conditions are : IC, PFD, Allergies, and Constipation "It's not easy being grateful all the time. But it's when you feel least thankful that you are most in need of what gratitude can give you." — Oprah Winfrey

contents: Mary, how awful to cough so much your chest hurts. Been there and done that. Imagine it is doing a number on your bladder too. I know it did mine when I coughed non-stop. First the chest was sore and then the bladder. Sunflower-Wow, 40 minutes of cardio. :woohoo: Waaaaaaay to go! :woohoo: When you do 40 minutes on the treadmill is it all at once? I can only do 15 at the most. Hey, you guys can't leave just because of the weekend. Come on...you can't leave me here all by myself talking to no one. So far this morning--stove is cleaned, bread is baking (I made it by hand, I do have a bread maker but I like making it myself), load of laundry in the washer, did 10 minutes on the treadmill and have so far 3,100 steps. Oh, also made stew and that is cooking in the crock pot. made it all by hand. Cut up all the veggies this time instead of bought cut up veggies. Guess I will be posting to myself over the weekend. Have fun. Don't feel guilty about leaving me here all by myself. Nope, don't even give it another thought.

Are you kidding? Good Lord, are you serious -- you did all that this morning all ready? Where are you -- the sun just came up here -- okay, so it was four hours ago, but it seems like it was just a few minutes ago. I just got on to say I will be gone for the weekend, didn't know it but I said more about it in another post -- going fishing -- okay, to a fishing camp -- going to sit by the lake and read and watch the sun come up and go down and rest there rather than here -- it will be wonderful -- My brother is a fabulous campfire cook - he will make fish soup to fish fillets all weekend long -- I will eat without thinking and without cooking! I will not get to taste anything because I have no taste or smell right now -- BUT -- I will KNOW that it was good because it is always good when he cooks...and if by some small miracle I get a break from this cold, I will surely enjoy whatever he is making --- I always do! You keep going on this cleaning frenzy and I am going to buy you a ticket to Michigan and you can come up and do my place too! Consider it a vacation that comes with chores... I fly you here, you clean.. who could want for anything more Okay, so I don't think that will fly, but when I get home, I expect I will be feeling better and then I will get started also. I have a lot of cleaning to do and I don't like to wait until the cold comes -- I like to do it while we can open the windows and let the fresh air inside... don't know where you are that it is rainy and cloudy, but it is gorgeous here in Northern Michigan and we are heading to the UP about 6 tonight, so I am guessing it will be the same up there.. Keep up the great working out -- all those steps -- dumb question for you... how do you know how many steps you are taking -- do you wear a step counter or something? How does that translate into miles or time? How long do you walk to get that many steps in... Well, I don't watch how far I walk, but I try to do about one hour when I ride my reclining bike -- it equals about 14 miles because that is about the speed I ride at. I like to do it when one of my favorite shows is on tv and the time flies.. I get a HUGE glass of water that I drink while riding and generally do have to stop for a quick pee, but sometimes I can get through without... Well, just rambling so I will get off of the computer and go get some of the things to help fight off this cough and cold -- yes, the chest aches from coughing -- my bladder is holding just fine! I do notice when I cough really hard that I sometimes have a leaking problem, but don't think that is too terribly unusual at 49 and after having IC for 16+ years... My entire family of women -- from my mom now down through my daughters.... we all will pee our pants if we laugh too hard -- and I don't mean LEAK, I mean PEE --- can't stop laughing and we pee our pants.. now let me tell you ... if ONE of us does it while any of the others are around -- we will ALL start laughing and sure enough someone else will end up doing it too -- you have never seen grown women laugh so hard as we do sometimes and then we are like 5 whales trying to get through the same bathroom door fighting to be the first to the pot... undoubtedly -- ONE OF US will not make it or hold it and pee right there in our pants... generally that is enough to set someone else over the top... This isn't IC, I am the only one with IC, but it is a very bad case of laughing too hard and not being able to hold it. I suppose others here have similar funny stories... perhaps this would be a funny link to start --- peeing pants stories... then again, maybe you all think that is the stupidest thing you have ever heard of and don't care to have read that much... ooops.. sorry Anyhow, I will chat with you ladies later today if I get packed, or next week if I don't -- I don't have internet up there -- I wonder if anyone in the UP of Michigan gets internet -- Hee Hee -- we live in the boonies and I am still on dial up! one day we will get into the up and coming 'age of electronics' along with the rest of you! Have a great weekend everyone and enjoy the weather while it is still nice out! Hugs, Mary bye!

Labor day weekend water- you are such a superwoman!!! You've already done all those cooking and cleaning in such a short time??I heard this from someone before, if you are stressed or angry, make a bread!!!Why???You get to punch the dough as hard as possible. Yeah, sort of stress relief per se Mary, have fun with your family It's always good to have someone who is a good cook in the family. My husband's family is a great cook, my FIL, who used to be a professional chef, my MIL, who used to be a baker. Guess who's next?? My hubby.. he likes to cook too, and he's a good cook as well. I guess my hubby got his passion for cooking from his parents. I like to cook, but I don't like cooking 2-3hour of cooking. I like speedy cooking. anything has to cook less than hour or less. Ok, it's time for me to go now. Have a safe trip and good holiday
signature: Aya IC Diagnosed in September, 2005 My conditions are : IC, PFD, Allergies, and Constipation "It's not easy being grateful all the time. But it's when you feel least thankful that you are most in need of what gratitude can give you." — Oprah Winfrey

contents: I did 11,131 steps today. I have a person follow me all day long and keep count how many steps I take. Have a step counter. Waaaaaaaaaaay back before the IC happened I would walk one mile in half hour. Would walk for 1 1/2 hour once or twice a day. Depending on the weather and how much other work I had to do. Dishes are all washed, put away, counter cleaned off and dirty dishes in dishwasher. Yupe, did all that this morning. Of course I got up 1 hour earlier but I try and do as much as I can in the morning and then konk out during the afternoon and start up again in the evening. Then when I finally konk out again I'm done until the next days. Sunflower-I used to make bread to take my aggravations out on too. found out years later that you are not really supposed to do that. It takes all the rising air bubbles out. No wonder I always ended up with pancake bread. Guess I will be the only one working this weekend. Have fun on your trips. Hey, I can let the dishes go for 3 days! :woohoo:

back in the saddle! I may not have had the best weekend, but whoever suggested that I try using the Mucinex is a genious! It worked great and didn't flare my bladder. I had a HORRID headache almost every day, but don't know if that was the sinus congestion or if the Mucinex gave it to me---either way, I kicked it with my motrin 800 which in turned kicked me in the gut... ugh. However, the chest congestion is now pretty much gone -- and NO PNEUMONIA!!! that is a HUGE thing for me -- I get it so easily and NEVER get over a congestion thing this fast -- it had to be the Mucinex -- so THANK YOU to whomever told me to try it! I also did the shower soothers every day and I also got the baby vicks with lavender --- very nice and I am ready for anything that tries to come back!

Ok, where did my post from this afternoon go? I did get two windows cleaned but the kitchen counter is not cleaned up and I only did 7,090 steps. Just not feeling well. Over did things yesterday. I was going to push it with the steps but if I do I know I will be sitting for 3 days. I just feel really tired and kind of achy. When I start to ache all over inside I know I've done to much. Tomorrow--1 window, clean kitchen, do laundry (not sure how many loads) Glad you are feeling better. Where is everyone?

I am back. How's everybody doing Hope everyone had a good weekend. I am feeling a lot better today after horrible flare/TOM combo for a couple of weeks. I couldn't go to my therapy past couple of times because I was in a huge pain.However, I managed to go to my PFD therapy this morning. My PT told me that I have made quite improvement ever since I started my therapy. That's really good news:woohoo: I did play tennis with my friends on Sunday. It was really fun I took yesterday off and just being lazy. Today, I did 3 loads of landry and cleaning the kitchen and did some stretching in the afternoon. Tomorrow, I will do my cardio for 40-50mins.
signature: Aya IC Diagnosed in September, 2005 My conditions are : IC, PFD, Allergies, and Constipation "It's not easy being grateful all the time. But it's when you feel least thankful that you are most in need of what gratitude can give you." — Oprah Winfrey

contents: Sunflower glad you are feeling better. Some days you just need to take off. Sure wish I could do 40 minutes of cardio. Decided to skip the window. Will wash all three tomorrow. Made spaghetti sauce and cookies this morning. Figured should do that before cleaning the kitchen. Also did 2 loads of laundry. Rest of the day will be to clean the kitchen and get my steps in.

back in the saddle again! Okay Waterflow, Sunflower and anyone else who is lurking and thinking of joining in on all this exercise: I am just finishing up with a long morning of helping my 19 year old deal with her bills and some other stuff... she needs a mentor so badly -- ADD, but more than that, just learning to DO what she needs to do WHEN she needs to do it and not allowing 'stuff' to get in the way -- so emotionally, I feel like I just put in about 3-4 hours of energy. However, it is going on 1pm and I haven't really done anything for ME or the house... so, here's my goal for the next hour or two and I will repost and set another for the latter part of the day. I am going to go take a shower, take the dog for at least a mile walk, put a load of clothing into the washer and will get it out after I return from the walk. Then, I am going to move some furniture out of the way and continue with the carpet cleaning I started on Monday night... I will plan to complete the dining/computer area today and hopefully by nightfall, the living room as well -- moving the big furniture is the only thing in the way of doing that -- and, I will also remember to get something out of the freezer for dinner... any suggestions anyone? WHAT'S for dinner tonight??? Oh, that is on a different thread... but I need a suggestion - yesterday, DH had fixed meat loaf because I had to take my parents to their doctor appointments and didn't get back until after 6:30 pm, so he was home, had made dinner and was getting a spare car running so I can put for sale signs on it and get it sold... OH, that too, I will make for sale signs and put the car out by the road! So, off to shower, walk, make signs, put laundry in, move some furniture and start carpet cleaning --- and get dinner started somewhere in there as well. You girls are go getters! I would LOVE to ride my bike today, but a little flare on my vulva from the shingles is started and I don't think that would be a good activity until it clears up... anyone else get those sores after your cycle? I use zovirax, and it works well, but they hurt awful until they go away... Off to get stuff done -- check in with ya later! ShePurzz (Shep)

Wow ShePurzz, just reading that makes me tired. Have fun moving the furniture. I think that is the worse job of cleaning there is. Just came in from doing some mowing. With all the rain it looks like a jungle out there. Will be getting started on making supper. Oh, I'm making meatloaf tonight. No one ever likes it. They always throw ketchup on it. Even I do. After that then I will start to tackle the kitchen. Almost have my 10,000 steps in for today.

meatloaf trick Originally posted by waterflow Wow ShePurzz, just reading that makes me tired. Have fun moving the furniture. I think that is the worse job of cleaning there is. Just came in from doing some mowing. With all the rain it looks like a jungle out there. Will be getting started on making supper. Oh, I'm making meatloaf tonight. No one ever likes it. They always throw ketchup on it. Even I do. After that then I will start to tackle the kitchen. Almost have my 10,000 steps in for today. If you have any saugsage (ground) add a little to your meatloaf to give it a little extra flavor -- I bet noone kills it with catsup! Enjoy! I also posted on the meatloaf link with some other ingredients I have used and enjoyed! Didn't get any carpet cleaning done -- however, I did the rest.. I am off to unload the dishwasher and move the car that I am putting up for sale out to the front yard so people will see it and HOPEFULLY someone will buy it and take it out of my yard! Hugs, Mary (Shep)

I tried the sausage thing. No one liked it. Then we are not sausage eaters. Got it all done today. For a second there didn't think I was going to make it. I'm tired, feel lousy but got it all done. Still need to "tidy" myself up yet. After that I'm calling it a day and turning into a bump on a log in front of the tv. Tomorrow--wash 3 windows, wash bedding to one bed, 10,000 steps and keep the kitchen counter cleaned and dishes put away.

I am done my cardio for 40 mins today I am proud of myself doing my exercise, but I do regret eating sundwich that gave me a little flareHopefully, it won't get worse tomorrow. I am going to Chicago for my work tomorrow, so I won't be able to post until Saturday when I come back. This is my first time I get to see real Chicago:woohoo: It's my work thing, but my boss offered me to bring my hubby with me. So at least that's fun I am little nervous about flying tomorrow since the new regulation started with British terroist plot My goal for this weekend is lots of WALKING!!!! I am sure that I will meet my goal since my hubby and I will be walking around downtown Chicago. Hope that nothing goes wrong with this trip Water, Mary, how are you two doing????? Busy with house cleaning?????
signature: Aya IC Diagnosed in September, 2005 My conditions are : IC, PFD, Allergies, and Constipation "It's not easy being grateful all the time. But it's when you feel least thankful that you are most in need of what gratitude can give you." — Oprah Winfrey

contents: sweaty betty I am not kidding you -- I have been cleaning carpets on and off all day -- it is a huge project because there is so much 'junk' around -- stuff I don't want to get rid of, but it doesn't really have a place of its own -- so tomorrow, I am going to set stuff out by the road with a 'free' sign on it and hopefully get rid of some of it. I run a free recycling site where people post stuff they no longer want and request things they do want -- it works out great and keeps good stuff out of the landfills. Cleaning -- okay, got the entire living room done today/tonight short of one small section that needs to be done tomorrow -- DH helped me move the big furniture tonight after dinner so that was great -- but I am not kidding when I say that I was sweating so bad that it was running down my back, under my skirt and down between the cheeks where the sun doesn't shine! I was cracking up laughing that I was sweating so badly, but hoped that I was working off a little fat cell or two! I kept saying "ooohh! ahhh!" when it would trickle down and 'tickle' my backside -- DH just kept cracking up and looking at me like perhaps it was time to find something a little more exciting to do with my time! Regardless, my living room floor carpeting looks a lot better, but bottom line is this: It is never going to look good again -- it is too old, too dated, and I hate the thought of having to carpet the house in order to sell it in a couple of years... ugh... but, I don't see how anyone would walk into a house that is completely carpetted in ONE COLOR throughout the entire dining/living/bedrooms and master bathroom areas. The only reason the spare bathroom isn't the same color is that last winter I worked on remodelling it with things I got from the recyling site -- and it looks so awesome! It was a ton of work because the walls are all that vinyl type stuff - and YEP, it is the same throughout the entire house. I finally got brave and just tried painting it -- it turned out so incredibly beautiful that I can't believe in 8 years of living here I have never tried it before... so now, the plan is to start on our master bath, then bedroom, paint through out the living room and dining area and hopefully make the place come alive a bit... I just don't have the eye for it -- fortunately, two daughter are extremely artistic and creative -- they have some of the rooms all laid out on paper with what colors I should use, etc... awesome gift from God they are!!! Anyhow, I got the floors vacummed, the carpet shampooed in a very big part of the house, and helped daughter do her bills again today. I don't think I got a lot more done -- but tomorrow... hmmm.. what is the plan??? Oh, tomorrow I have to take my car in for new tires and belts -- all free!!! :woohoo: because it is under warranty so they are going to replace them!!! In the morning though, I am going to have to get something done --- I guess it will be to put the living room and dining room furniture back and to get some stuff out front for give-a-ways, then off to Traverse City for my car appointment -- I am not going to plan to do anything after that because trips to TC never end as soon as I expect and I am guessing it will be pretty late when I get back. It's great to see that you two are also getting so much done -- I really want to get into a routine asap -- however, I know right now that I have two trips to TC next week for doctor appointments and that is going to put a kink in getting something done daily. I do have a mini trampoulene that I got because my doctor said it was a good way to help clean out the toxins in lymph glands... That's something I could do without taking up too much time tomorrow -- so that's my plan -- get up, jump and then get cleaned up and ready to head to TC... Oh, and get the furniture put back into its proper place too. Have a great day tomorrow and I will check in then! Shep

Things are looking GOOD!!! I am starting my week very hopeful!!! I had a pretty good weekend My hubby and I enjoyed the city of Chicago. We walked a LOT!! The nice thing about Chicago is very pedestrian-friendly environment, whereas my city is totally the opposite. We can walk everywhere without being hit by a car. I walked until my feet felt numb. It was very fun The highlight of this trip was Chicago deep pizza. It was unbelievably huge!!! Pizza crust was 3 inch thick, and I only ate 1 1/2 piece. It was YUMMMMMMMMMMMMM. The most amazing part was that I didn't get any flare from eating tomato sauce to dark chocolate.:woohoo: :woohoo: :woohoo: I was so scared of my consequence, but it didn't happend!!!! This morning, I went to my therapy for my PFD, and told my PT about no flare whatsoever. She told me that I have been doing so well, if things go well continuously, then I just need 2 more sessions to graduate my therapy I am very hopeful for that. Even after finishing my session with my PT, I still need to do all exercises that I learned from my PT at home. My IC/PFD is far from over yet, but thins are looking a lot better than few month ago. I am starting my tennis league this week, so I am so excited about that. I hope playing tennis won't slow this progress down hopefully. I just cannot wait to play tennis with other people. I will do tredmill for 30-40 mins today. How's everybody doing???? Where are you ?????

contents: Coffee on the Carpet, anyone??? Okay, so I spent all of last week working on moving furniture, carpet cleaning and just plain trying to get the junk outta the house and cleared away so I have less "stuff" around -- was going pretty good. Then, Saturday, our grandson had a football game and our son, not the parent of the football player, but another of our kids, came over after the game with our 3-year old granddaughter -- what a cutie! Right away she wanted to see our 'horsie' and go riding -- he is a cute little wooden rocker so she was having a great time. We ate dinner standing in the kitchen because we didn't want to get any food on the clean carpets... not a big deal, but made me realize TODAY I MUST clean off the kitchen table -- we generally eat in the living room -- that is going to have to change. Well, after eating, we returned to talk in the living room and the three of us had coffee while the granddaughter played and enjoyed the horsie. At one time, she walked by grandpa and was swinging her arms and singing and her hand came down and landed right inside of Grandpa's coffee cup, which flew, when she pulled her hand out of it -- landing on not only the freshly cleaned carpet, but also on the decorative floor rug with the long tassle type thingies all around the outside edge of the rug. UGH... I just said -- don't worry about it I will get it later -- and went about our visit (I didn't realize at that time that it had gotten on the decorative rug and the tassles) --- so an hour or so later, when they left, I went to get the carpet cleaner and figured I would just clean it up and no big deal --- WRONG --- the darn coffee stained the cream tassles and I wasn't able to get it out --- Okay, I can get mad, or I can get advise --- any advise on how to get it out of the tassles -- I tried the hand attachment and got them very wet and sucked it up several times, but still stained.. bummer... I guess I can turn the rug around so that the stained tassles are by the wall, and not so noticeable -- if anyone has other suggestions I would appreciate it --- I can't take it to the dry cleaners -- I don't have the money for that -- the only other thing I think is to hang that corner of the rug in a pan of water and let it soak over night??? Oh well, the granddaughter is happy and the coffee wasn't hot enough to burn her - - so we have much to be thankful for -- and we HAVE carpet -- some people live in cardboard boxes on the streets... everything in proper perspective... it's just a rug.

Try using a combination of salt and lemon juice; this makes a very mild bleach that we use to get stains out of the sails for out boat. It works best if you can expose it to sun to set off the cleaning process... Good luck- Lynne
Thanks! I'll give it a try Mary

I did my exercise yesterday-playing tennis for about 2 hours or so:woohoo: Today's plan is AGAIN-playing tennis for 2 hours. I hope that I can win the match I am having a mild pain today I know that I should have gone easy to myself, but I didn't last night. However, I did enjoy myself and did my exercise, so I am proud of myself. :woohoo: :woohoo: :woohoo: I think if you really enjoy doing something, you can easily keep doing it. To me, it's my tennis. My hubby knows that, and he has his thing too. Even though I really don't understand his passion Mary, How's your carpet cleaning??? Did it get cleaned somehow I hope you are keeping yourself busy with your housework Have a good day!

contents: Great job Sunflower -- good luck on the game tonight too. My carpet is a lot better, but still a little stain where the coffee landed -- I will continue to work on it and soon I will just redo the whole room again. I am frustrated today, because I really did work hard this week on diet and today I weigh the same as I did last week. I am struggling with a couple of things -- one, I am bloated, not in a flare though, but tomorrow is my sister's wedding and I am grumbling because when I am getting dressed up and going out, the tummy really bothers me a lot -- I feel like I look pregnant! Anyhow, today I have to finish the pillows I made for her gift, started them last night and will get them done today, and I am going to manicure and pedicure so that should be nice. I would like to continue getting the 'clutter out' and so today I will post more stuff on my 'recycling site' where people post things they no longer want/need and others post things they do need -- keeping good stuff out of landfills! Everything is free and everyone gets what they want! I LOVE IT! Have a great day and don't over do it -- but I do hope you win your game! Mary

Mary, good to hear that your carpet is lot better. I know it's frustrating that the number on the scale doesn't want to move at all. How's your clothes fitting you??? Sometimes, you need to shift your focus rather than the number on the scale. I try to measure my size once a month or so. Did you try that???? Do you think you are going to have your TOM pretty soon??? Maybe that's the reason that you have this bloating problem. I have the same problem beofe my TOM. I hope you enjoy your sister's wedding tomorrow. Getting your nails done is always nice I have a bad habit of bitting my nails all the time. Now I am trying really hard not to bit my nails, so I can grow my real nails. It's very irritating to have my nails grow. I don't like the damage done to my nails after putting fake nails, but they look really nice though. I will have a lazy day tomorrowOH, thanks for the advice. I will try my best not to overdo it.But I must win my game:woohoo: Last edited by Sunflower2; 09-15-2006, 04:22 AM.

contents: Hey Sunflower 2 Way to goooooo on winning the tennis game. :woohoo: :woohoo: You sure do have lots planned for this week. Here's hoping all of us keep up with things. So far no sweets today. Hardest time is evening. Will keep to crafting and maybe that way I won't snack.

Unbelievable headache and muscle spasms here.... My goal for the week: Mon - Friday -- do my yogalates/ stretching video and take a nice long walk every day... If I can do that hopefully this neck will loosen up for a while and give me a break. The drug withdrawal is getting old in a hurry - the shakes and the headache stink -- but this too shall pass. Will check in this week and hopefully one or both of you will NAG ME about doing my stretching -- that is what I really need the most, but what I FEEL LIKE doing the least. Thanks Mary

You don't have to push too hard to do exercise. I myself had a little spasm yesterday, but I did my exercise anyway. Hope that you are feeling better today. Mary-how are you doing??? contents: thanks for asking -- Thanks for asking Sunflower -- I have a plan -- here is what is on it right now... unfortunately, I didn't have my yogalates in the plan, but since you asked, I am going to try to put it in there someplace. I own/operate several recycling groups, and yesterday I cleaned out a ton of junk from my cupboards that I 'think I am going to use sometime' but never do -- a young man whose roommate left and stole most of his stuff needed kitchen stuff, so I boxed it all up for him -- my husband looked into the plastics cupboard yesterday and said "Wow! That looks great -- was thinking of asking you to clean it out soon... SO GLAD I did it before he asked!" Today it is my closet and my dresser(s). Anything that doesn't fit goes. I posted late last night/early this morning about doing this -- it is being done for a variety of reasons, but mostly to remove the excess stress from my life. I never have extra money (we are lucky to get our bills paid and often run behind) so buying clothing isn't something I can afford to do, however, I have held onto clothing for so long because I don't want to sell things and even when you do, you get so little for it you end up wishing you had just given it away and saved yourself the yard sale time. So, rather than thinking how I can save some money and get back into these clothes (adds so much stress doesn't it??? Anyone else know what I mean by that???) I am going to get them out of my closet, out of my sight and off of my mind ... I plan to get a few items that I CAN wear and I want my closet to contain ONLY things that fit me... NO MORE WAITING for the ten-twenty pound loss first... IF/WHEN I lose the weight, I will trust the Lord to bring along something that works -- maybe a great hand me down gift from someone else like I will be doing today. I have a lady coming to take EVERYTHING on Thursday or Friday -- Can't wait to have it done -- now, just urge me not to get 'clingy' when I see something I really like and wish fit me --- seeing it there NOT FITTING makes me feel lousy -- NOT SEEING IT there will inevitably reduce some more of the stress in my life. Anyone else feel like this? Mary

On the way to a less-stressful closet! I just did my closet -- most of it anyhow -- still have some shoes and other oddities that I want to get to. I have a large pile of dresses and four piles of pants and blouses and tops. It feels so good to take that HUGE stack of hangers out and then organize what is left. Coats, Dresses, Skirts, Slacks, Blouses, Tops with all the sweaters and jeans are on a top shelf. It looks CLEAN :woohoo: and it looks like I can open the door and actually find what I need. I decided to do a load of whites to get them pearly clean again, so that will be nice. I also pulled out some things that need a little mending and will get that done before they return to the closet. I did really well 'letting go' of most of it, however, I did find a dress or two that I know are not going out of style and I just love -- so I did allow myself to keep those two dresses and just tucked them in the back near my wedding dress. I still have two dressers to go through -- and when that is done, I am going to start on another closet that has some winter heavy sweater stuff -- but I think that is already sorted -- I think I did it before I put them away last spring -- so hopefully I am nearly completed. It is great to know that someone who really needs some nice clothing that can't afford to buy it will benefit and I am blessed because now I don't open it and WANT TO WEAR something that doesn't fit -- it is refreshing to actually see that things can hang loosely in the closet too! I am even giving away the hangers so I don't get any ideas!!! I would love to challenge someone else to do a closet today! FEELS GREAT! JUST DO IT! :woohoo:

Mary, good job finishing your closet. I am just so tired of cleaning all the time. My hubby doesn't clean at all. He throws his socks everywhere living room to computer room.... so I pick them up all the time I clean up 99.9999999 % of the time around the house. I keep telling him when he cooks, cook and clean at the same time. However, he always leaves the huge mess in the kitchen. I know he's into a huge cooking, and always ended up exsausting himself from cooking, and never cleans up the mess He calls me a clean freak!!!!!! In fact, I am NOT!!!
signature: Aya IC Diagnosed in September, 2005 My conditions are : IC, PFD, Allergies, and Constipation "It's not easy being grateful all the time. But it's when you feel least thankful that you are most in need of what gratitude can give you." — Oprah Winfrey

contents: Sunflower --- thanks for the pat on the back!!! I feel wonderful this morning --- the STUFF IF GONE!!! A lady just came and got all of the clothing and another came and got all of the kitchen stuff for her son yesterday!! :woohoo: Clean closet and HUBBY noticed quickly -- in fact, he joked "Do I get half now?" I just smiled and said -- only 'till I refill it with new stuff... NOT!!! Sunflower -- regarding the socks on the floor -- I have to refer to the guy who I think tells it like it is "DR PHIL" my hero! He says that we teach people how to treat us -- if you are picking up his socks (and he admittedly considers you a clean freak) then you are basically teaching him that it is okay to drop them where he takes them off --- the change will come when YOU change how you respond to his actions. Here's my suggestion --- hope you can try it and follow through.. that is the tough part because in this case it is going to bug YOU a lot more than it bugs HIM -- AT FIRST!!! Make a statement of fact when the socks are NOT laying on the ground. State that from here on out, you are only washing the clothes that are in the hamper(s) and I suggest that if you don't have a hamper you take this opportunity to buy one or two and make it very clear that this is a NEW RULE YOU intend to follow. I put a hamper in everyone's room, as well as another in the laundry room for towels and extras that don't really go in someones personal hamper. Let the WHOLE FAMILY KNOW that you bought or put out NEW HAMPERS for each person and that starting TODAY, you will not be picking up any clothing that is laid on the floor -- (will get into what to do with those items in a moment) -- and only laundry in the hamper(s) will be washed on wash day -- they may NOT wash on other days unless there is a full load of clothing... Then, DO NOT PICK UP THE CLOTHES ON THE FLOOR!!! Leave them set until you have done wash and let them set to rot a few times.... If your husband goes to get a pair of socks from the drawer and doens't find them, just remind him KINDLY that you very clearly stated on "such and such a day" that you were no longer going to be a cleaning crew, however, the things he DID put in the hamper are all cleaned and returned to the drawer -- then add, I will be doing another load of clothes on "such and such a day" if you want to throw them into your hamper I will get them then... (IF HE WANTS TO WASH HIS OWN SOCKS EARLIER, let him, but do not offer to do them or to change your wash day.) I guarantee that it won't be long before he gets the message that socks on the floor remain on the floor (turn your eyes away from them if company comes -- let your husband feel awkward and go pick them up) and socks in the laundry get cleaned and returned to the dresser drawer -- simple as pie and any two year old can and will do this if taught to do it. Same with the kids -- however -- when it comes to the kids, I would INSIST that before they go to bed, their clothing or items in the general living area are taken back to their own rooms -- if they don't put them in the hamper, don't pick them up off their bedroom/bathroom floor -- TEACHING people how to do this for themselves is a GIFT to THEM and you are teaching them how you will be treated -- not like a slave, or like a paid maid service, but like a person who isn't there for their personal comfort -- but like a person who loves to serve her family in a respectful manner. Regarding the kitchen, I feel differently -- My husband loves to cook -- I primarily make burnt offerings -- (one pan is currently out in the yard on a table as proof that I cannot make sausage and take a shower at the same time! Believe it or not, we ate at McDonalds that night... ) If my husband is willing to cook a meal, I do my best not to expect him to clean as he goes (which is the way I do it) but rather, I am just grateful that he is taking the time to help in the kitchen. I clean up and don't say a word -- I consider that MY PART of doing the dinner. If he were to leave his dishes in the dining area, they would sit there until hell froze over -- however, on occasion if I get up first, I take his plate to the kitchen and rinse it off and put it in the dishwasher -- and LO AND BEHOLD -- he often does that for me as well -- but he NEVER leaves a dish in the dining area that was HIS dinner plate -- I would walk away from it and leave it and within a short period of time, he would go put it away --- he knows it will rot there. This isn't stubborn stuff -- I am not trying to find ways to 'teach him' -- I make every effort to do something FOR him often - take his dishes, offer to bring him coffee, etc... because I love him and that is how I show my love to him -- he LOVES gifts of service... I also love gifts of service -- and he will very often do things for me -- it is a relationship, not a mother/son one, but a husband/wife one and that means mutual responsibilities and respect for the boundaries. I hope you will consider reading "self matters" by the wonderful mr. Phil McGraw -- he is truly wonderful at saying it like it is, but he isn't haughty -- he is firm and commands respect. Just keep in mind what I said earlier -- YOU are going to have a harder time with this than they are because YOU don't want a mess -- so, be firm, expect it to hurt a while and expect it to take a while -- but on the other side of this issue, you will have commanded respect and gotten it. Now, for those clothes that get left laying around in areas you really want cleaned up --- (again, I stress that you NOT do this in the beginning! and NEVER do this shortly after something was laid down... example. I am often tired when I sit down on the couch at night. I HATE bras... so when I come in from the outside world, the first thing to go is my shoes, followed closely by my bra -- pulling it out of my shirt sleeve or whatever -- I just want it off... sometimes it lands on my computer, sometimes it lands on the kitchen table, and sometimes on the couch -- but not very often do I walk into the bedroom and put it away where it belongs... at least not right away... Allow your family the freedom to "LIVE in the house" not to feel like they are company there.) But, after the family has realized that you mean business about picking up their own things, and perhaps with a warning that you would like all clothing articles removed from the livingroom floor before dinner -- well, just before dinner is served -- walk through the living room and pick up any articles of clothing that are lying around -- have a box that you keep in a place that isn't convenient to get to -- the bottom of the basement steps, in the garage, on an enclosed porch, etc... someplace where you don't feel that company will see... And when you 'clean sweep' the open places in your house, grab these articles, (REFRAIN from turning them right side out and folding them) and drop them into the box. When Johnny asks 'where are my boxers? I need them for a game tongight?' Ask him..."Did you put them in your hamper?" "Johnny is going to say "I don't know where I put them... they are just missing!" To which you reply, "Well, if they aren't in your hamper or your drawer, you may want to check the missing clothing box and direct him to the area where the box is... (REFRAIN FROM SMILING FROM EAR TO EAR OR FROM LAUGHING LOUDLY OUTLOUD --- not because it isn't slightly hysterically funny -- it is --- ) but remember, you are teaching them how to treat you -- and you want to save that big laugh for when they have left the house --- share that with us too --- Hope this is helpful and that you will 'pick your battles wisely' since in reality, you don't want to teach them to be unkind -- you are trying to teach them responsibility -- so remember to do this with the right attitude in mind and your presentation will reflect that caring!!! Hugs, Mary

My standard VS My hubby's standard of cleaniness Hey,Mary.Thanks for the tips. I know I have to be tough to my hubby. I just cannot help cleaning up the mess. You are right about teaching him being resonsible. I try not to pick his socks on the floor all the time. It is really annoying me if I don't do something about it. I don't know if you ever seen a show called " How clean is your house?" I think it was on lifetime channel if I remeber correctly. It comes on Saturdauy night at 10 or so.I saw that show a couple of times in the past. It was so shocking to see how people are living a dirty place!!! I thought my friend was the worst person to live with when we were roommates. Oh, boy... I was wrong!!!!! In this show, two British ladies visit someone's home, and inspect how dirty the house is. And after the inspection, those two ladies bring the cleaning crew to the home to clean the house. I cannot honestly believe how in the world people are living in such a dirty place. I told my hubby about this show, and we are NOT going to live like those people.

contents: In my business, I get a lot of email from medical professionals. Today I received one on sleep that I thought I would post -- It is quite different from the Sleep Hygiene post I put up a few weeks back, but it may be better suited for those who don't think they can follow the other recommendations. I hope this helps SOMEONE get more zzzzzzzzzzzzz's tonight! Sleep Disorders Having trouble getting to or staying asleep? Difficulty sleeping is becoming more common and interferes with quality of life in many ways. Lack of adequate sleep can cause weight gain, affect mood and bring on symptoms of depression, reduce concentration and make work more difficult, and result in health issues ranging from glucose intolerance to compromised immunity. There are several contributors to an inability to fall asleep or stay asleep. Physicians tend to prescribe drugs for these conditions, many of them are habit forming, and none of them solve the underlying issues causing the problem, making medication a poor choice for most people. So, if you're having trouble sleeping, here are some things to consider. Exercise promotes sound sleep. People often don't like to hear this, but engaging in adequate exercise (45-60 minutes in your target heart zone 5-6 days per week) will help you to sleep more soundly. Being "active" is not the equivalent. I talk to people daily who try to convince me that cleaning house, weeding the garden and walking the dog are all that is required in the area of exercise, but this is not true. Activities such as running, cycling, fitness boxing, and strength training, practiced on a regular basis, are required. Additionally, yoga can be particularly helpful since regular practice results in deeper breathing, which calms the body and mind, and results in better sleep. Many of the sleep deprived people I talk to sleep like babies within days of starting and appropriate exercise program. Proper diet promotes sound sleep. The Wellness Forum's diet is based on the consumption of complex carbohydrates, which are precursors to serotonin. Serotonin is one of the precursors to melatonin, which helps to regulate your sleep cycle. The consumption of too much protein and fat, which reduces carbohydrate consumption, can contribute to sleep disorders. Of course, the consumption of alcohol and caffeine can interfere with sleep as well. A bedtime ritual promotes better sleep. (good place to mention the sleep hygiene suggestions) Many people run around at full speed, fall into bed and expect to drop off and rest immediately. Calm yourself before going to bed - read or engage in quiet activity and then go to bed. Putting your mind at rest will help to put your body at rest. Stress and your response to stress can interfere with sleep. People often have trouble sleeping during stressful times. Stress is often a sign that things need to change, and if therapy is needed in order to make changes, seek it. Cognitive therapy is particularly effective, as it requires fewer sessions in order to be effective. If you cannot change the things causing your stress, you can change your response to them. Again, therapy can be helpful. Exercise can mitigate your response to stress, and I'll mention yoga(:woohoo: -- great plugs for yoga fans!) again as a valuable tool. I can speak from experience here - my breathing patterns and my ability to calm myself in adverse circumstances have changed dramatically as a result of practicing yoga for the last several years, and if I do not take classes for a few days, I notice a difference. Many people have reported the same results to me over the years. What to do if these things don't work? Sometimes people form the habit of waking up throughout the night, tossing and turning, watching TV, etc., falling back to sleep at 4:00 or 5:00 and then getting up, exhausted at 6:30AM. If this pattern is repeated enough, it becomes a habit. (May I add -- going to the potty???) The best way to break the pattern is to form a new one. The next time you wake up at 3:00AM, get up and start your day. Stay awake and functioning, no matter how difficult it is until your regular bedtime. Napping will interfere with re-training your body. Continue to do this until you induce exhaustion sufficient to allow you to sleep through the night. I will acknowledge that this is not fun, but it will work if you discipline yourself to do it. Of course, medication may be a necessary option for a small percentage of the population for whom there are medical reasons accounting for lack of sleep, and those people should seek medical attention. But most people will find relief for their sleep issues by following the protocols offered here. ENJOY the suggestions and I sure hope that someone will be sleeping better soon because of it!!! Mary

I'm not looking for exercises to do FOR pelvic floor dysfun. Those of you suffering from PFD, what kinds of exercises can you do that won't worsen your symptoms? I need to get my body moving, but found most things exacerbate the IC and PFD. Thanks for the input. Stephanie *

I do not have PFD, but about the only exercises I can tolerate are walking and swimming. I'm really sorry to see fall is here. I will be looking for somewhere I can swim through the winter, but it's not as fun as being able to just go out in our yard to the pool. Donna

contents: I'm not pregnant but a friend who know I have IC suggested to try Yoga for pregnant women. This mayu not be hard on the pelvic area I suppose. I haven't excercise in almost a year since I got this IC problem. I got a book, Stretch for IC patients but I can't excercise at home. I feel that I have to go somewhen, even if it is at the gym in my complex, but I'm still afraid to even try the tredmill. I want to give Yoga a try because I know it'll also help me mentaly, not only fisically. Anyone trying this kind of Yoga? or any other Yoga that is safe for us?

contents: We just moved to FL and bought a house with a pool so I could do water aerobics. I've only lately been able to use the pool much, but since a recent flare I've been fighting the last couple of months, I'm afraid to use the pool in case the chemicals would cause a problem. We have a service take care of the pool - I know zero about pools. My urogyne says he's never heard of this being a problem but I'm still skeptical, especially since most of my discomfort is urethral. Any thoughts or experience with this?
I don't do well with clorine, but we have a pool and use Baquacil --- I don't have any problem at all with that. Donna

contents: While I personally don't have any problems with chlorine pools (assuming the chemistry is in good standing), we have our pool and spa now maintained by a salt water generator. So, no chemicals and it was a fairly easy switch over. It is very kind to the skin, the kids love it on the eyes and there is just never any chance of it getting out of whack and becoming too harsh. Love it. Pools are usually fine, but make sure the pool company maintains good chemistry with not only the chlorine, but the Ph levels. Very simple to learn to check yourself and corrections are quite simple too. Usually a good batch of baking soda will fix a Ph problem, and chlorine is easily remedied, especially if it is done after swimming hours. Enjoy!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

contents: Your symptoms sound a lot like mine. I can't work out either. Did you have IC before you got pregnant? I'm 26 and newly married. My husband and I really want to have kids, but I don't know if I can go through pregnancy with the IC. I'd like to know about your experience with it. Good luck on finding a work out that works! I had an elmiron insillation yesterday and it seemed to help the pain a bit. I'm going to have treatments twice a week. Have you tried that?

I was at work the other night. One of the girls from the other part of the building was in to bring one of the guys lunch. She said she had just got done with softball practice. I said that sounded like fun. She said she was on a league with a bunch of girls our other building and a few from our building and that they were looking for more people to join the team. I told her I would be interested. So, Tuesday, I got an email that there was practice last night. I had to take a break from work on Tuesday night to go buy a glove. I havent played softball in 15 years. Practice was last night, and it was so much fun! I suprised myself by hitting the ball, and getting a good grounder and throwing it to first base. Im not sure what position I will play, but I dont care. As long as Im out there doing something. After practice, I met up with my best friends hubby (hes my best friend too and she had to work). We went to a country bar. We had a blast. Years ago, there was a group of us that used to hang out at a bar called Bradleys. It has since closed, and the gang split up. Now it seems, as if the gang is slowly finding this new bar, and its so fun! I cant wait till I can go again to see who else shows up. One of the guys that I ran into last night taught me how to swing dance and cha cha years ago, and last night we did a cha cha together. FUN! Last night was a great night. I think my exercise boredom prayers have been answered. Softball games are Thursdays and practice is on Tuesdays. Then there's always the batting cages that we can go to for more practice. Then there's the old gang hanging out again, so I have more of an excuse to go dancing. Im very excited! Yall have a good day!

contents: I've been diagnosed with IC for about 9 months now and the better I feel, the more weight that comes on. I now weight 10lbs. over what I did when I first got diagnosed! I think this is because I don't do Tae- Bo anymore becasue I discovered that it gave me IC flares. So do you have any sugestions on how I can lose these extra pounds?

weight, wait, weighed, way So sorry to hear that you were diagnosed with IC, but you have found a great place to come to in a short time! That is great -- you will find lots of help and helpful ideas here. There may even be old links on losing weight that you can find by clicking on the SEARCH above the posts and put in "weight". For me, I got Dr. Phil's weight loss book and it was good because it isn't a 'diet' it is a lifestyle. I have never believed in DIETING because they never work long term -- however -- changing the way you eat or exercise is a lifetime change and will give lifelong change. If you can't do TaeBo any longer, consider some other things you enjoy and see if you can turn them into your exercise -- the best exercise is "the one you will do" -- so, do you swim, ride a bike (ouch!), dance, walk, jump on the trampolene, etc.. and make it a little more of a workout -- walk faster and longer, swim laps rather than just swimming slow and playing, etc... turn something you already enjoy into the exercise by increasing the intensity and buring more calories. DIET -- ugh -- like I said I hate the word -- besides, look at the first three letters -- D I E --- that should tell us something! When you diet, your body believes you are starving it and the body will begin to store the glucose as FAT and it will hold onto the fat for 'a rainy day'. If you eat smaller portions, control sugar and other junk foods, your body will still get the necessary calories and a well balanced diet, and will soon recognize that it isn't being starved... that is when it will begin to let go of stored fat and you will not lose MUSCLE or LEAN MASS, or ORGAN tissue that you need -- all part of starvation. However, a diet that is high in fruits and vegetables and whole grains will fill you up, keep you from feeling hungry and will tell your body -- "Hey, plenty of food here, no need to hold tight to all those fat pockets.. time to let some go..." and trust me -- you WILL lose the weight... But, if you are losing more than ONE OR TWO POUNDS A WEEK -- it is NOT FAT -- it is either water, muscle or organs.... you cannot lose more than ONE - TWO pounds of FAT in a week -- I guess in some athletes (talking professional athletes like runners and bikers) it is possible for them to lose up to three pounds of fat in a week) so keep in mind that slow and steady is the WAY to lose WEIGHT. Be sure you are getting at least 10-12 LARGE glasses of good water daily -- Be sure that a huge portion of your vegetables and fruits are FRESH and RAW, and hopefully organic! Be sure you cut out junk like sugar, white flour and white rice -- "the whiter the bread, the sooner yer dead!" DIET FOODS -- like sodas, or other things that say DIET on them will do you more harm than good -- you don't need aspartame, or msg, or splenda -- you should avoid pop completely -- and if you check, you will see there are sites that talk about DIET POP --- if you STOP drinking DIET POP you would lose weight in one year if you changed NOTHING ELSE in your routine. Anyhow, avid Dr. Phil fan, and also get a lot of information from my Juice Plus cd's including the one that talks about weight loss (it is by a professional trainer who teaches about weight loss myths) -- so check out Phil's book -- it has a great deal of information that is well worth the $7 you pay for the book!!! Best to you and don't give up! It is a slow process, but fast is NOT better in the case of losing weight!

Yupe, you are right. The more you starve yourself the more the body thinks it needs to store for later on. Also, the more starvation diets you do the harder it is to loose the weight. I've done these many times and am having the hardest time getting the fat off. Have you tried just walking? I bought a treadmill (small one) and am trying to do at least 10,000 steps a day. Also, added doing small weight lifting. You just have to experiment on different types of exercising. It's different for everyone. I know some do yoga. I do just a few yoga moves and that all. Bladder can not seem to handle that. Hope you find something that will help.

I lost 10 lbs this summer by reducing the amount of meat (chicken, beef, whatever) I ate and keeping hard-boiled eggs by the dozen in the fridge. I usually eat only the white, because I don't like the yolk and it's loaded with fat and cholesterol, but a couple times a week I eat the yolk because it IS packed with nutrients. Whenever I feel like I need a snack, I just peel one of those eggs, eat the white, and I'm satisfied. Anyway, I believe that getting enough low fat protein in your diet (ie,hard-boiled eggwhites, fish) is very important, especially when dieting, because we often resort to trying to restrain ourselves to just salad, fruit, good carbs...but proteins, like egg, nuts fish, are important, too, and help you feel fuller longer and are considered "brain" foods (based on what I learned in a nutrition course in college). Best wishes and good luck!

For me, it's about being active! I workout 3-4 times a week... usually on the elliptical, rower or reclining bike. 30 minutes of aerobics and then some weights.. but not alot. Also, I agree about not drinking your calories and, more importantly, just reducing the sweets in your diet. The more sweets you eat, the more you crave them. My snack each day is a Balance Bar. I love making stir fried veggies!!! My dessert is a 150 calorie Soy Ice Cream Sandwich! Jill

contents: I saw on an Info commercial yesterday (yes, I do watch those at times. Plus not much else on during the weekends). She showed 2 moves to get those wonder stomach look. You're standing through the whole thing. You pull your leg up and your arms down at the same time. Better to not try and pull both your legs up at the same time. You don't have to touch your knee and some how this works one set of stomach muscles. The other move is you touch knee to elbow sideways. That works the side stomach muscles. I am going to start giving this a try. Don't see how it could do it but the woman modeling the moves had a stomach to die for.
I tried these today and they do work the abs. Couldn't do too many but hopefully I will be able to work my way up. They make it look so easy. I feel like I am talking to myself about this.

LOL, nope not talking to yourself about it!! LMAO!!!! After my stint here lately at PT for my lower back, I have learned exercises I never knew would work my abs, lol!! Now, if I only do them as I am supposed to (2x's a day), I will have a back that feels better and some serious abs! Thanks for posting a couple moves that allow me to stay OFF the floor, lol!!!!!

contents: Tracey, thank you for answering my post. Thank you Thank you Thank you lol Not fun having my post all to myself. What moves are you doing? I have been lifting weights long enough I can tell in little ways. In the beginning I couldn't lift any amount of weight up behind my head. Now I can. it isn't much but a start. Plus I can do squats with weights and I don't haev so many times with almost going wee wee. Bladder seems to be getting more use to it. How doing squats can affect the bladder I will never figure out. I'm hoping within a month this will all start showing on my old bod.

I do Jazzercise but lately I've been having trouble. I did have a hyst in FEb so I think I'm still healing from that. Is it a bad exerecise for ICers? I really like it so I'd hate to quit but bouncing around really bothers me some days. Tracy

Tracey I used to do Jazzercise but quit because I got board, and I had hip and knee trouble, but I would love to go back, but the IC flares make it difficult. A suggestion would be not to do the jumping. Keep it very low impact. Do all the movements but slow and fluid instead of jumpy. That's what I did to baby my hip and knee. We can't all jump up and down. So make it at your own pace and keep your feet on the ground. Nobody is going to care. The positive thing is that you still are getting exercise. Kudos to you. Good luck, and send a little prayer my way so I can get active again!!! I could use all the help I can get!!

I had to give it up too because of my knees. When I was doing this instead of jumping up and down I would do like a small jog. That way my bladder didn't feel like it was being bounced all around. If it hadn't been for my stupid knees I would still be at it.
Does anyone swim? I have been thinking about trying it, but I am interested in if it helps or hurts others.

Apparently God thinks I'm a bad-ass" ~Author Unknown Source - Pinterest " Current treatments: -IC diet -Elavil 50mg at night -Continuous use birth control pills (4-5 periods/year) -Heparin/Marcaine/Sodium Bicarb home instills at night 3-4x per week, more often if needed -Pyridium if needed, -Pain medicine at bedtime daily, as needed during the day several times per week -Antibiotic when doing an instillation to prevent UTI -Colace & SmartFiber to treat chronic constipation from meds, Fleet enema as needed -Dye Free Benadryl 50 mg at bedtime -"Your Pace Yoga: Relieving Pelvic Pain" dvd, walking, treadmill at gym -Managing stress= VERY important! -Fur therapy: Hugging the cat!

contents: I haven't seen the DVD, but there are many gentle yoga poses that are relaxing and beneficial to our bodies. Yoga teaches us to respect what our body is telling us and to work with it, not against it, for wellness and healing. I find it difficult to do yoga DVDs at home (lack of space and a dog who is always trying to be underfoot) but regularly attend classes at a local studio. It has done so much good for me, mentally, physically and spiritually. I had not gone in the past few weeks because of schedule conflicts and a bad cold/sinus infection and really missed it. I finally went yesterday and it was so gratifying! I hope you like the video -- don't push yourself if you find any of the poses too difficult to do completely, especially stretches. Just focus on breathing deeply and being present to what your body is experiencing. (One thing I realized very quickly yesterday was how tight my hamstrings were...oh boy!)

contents: Don't get discouraged if you can only do 10-15 minute segments in the beginning. That's perfectly fine and many yoga DVDs have segments of varying lengths so you can do as much or as little as you need. You're trying something new and you may need to take a week or so to work your way up to 30 minutes of yoga. Just to learn the poses as best as you can and remember to breathe! Also, be careful about overstretching, which is a big temptation since the instructors make even touching their toes look easy. But no one expects you to get the pose perfect and most of us who have been doing yoga for a while still can't do the "full expression." It's OK. Yoga is not what we do, but who we are! I'm sure you'll do great! Let us know how you do...and feel free to PM me if you have any questions.

contents: I enjoy the yoga zone DVDs and we have Yoga Zone on Cable TV here. I think the DVD I have is called Conditioning and Stress Release. They show modifications for poses and it was a good place for me to start with yoga. I go to classes now. Yoga can be about as strenuous as you desire it to be depending on what type of class you go to. I found some, but not all, of the Vinyasa classes to be a bit too fast paced for me and defeated some of my purpose for yoga, stress release. I was trying too hard to keep up with the class that was more advanced than I. I'd be interested to know which poses the PT recommended to help stretch the pelvic floor muscles. Do you know the names of them?

I would be interested in knowing that also! I have to say that I have been faithfully doing my yoga once and sometimes twice per day. Yesterday I came home from work in extreme pain. After 45 minutes of yoga and relaxation poses, I felt wonderful! Last night when I began to feel tense again, I did several relaxing poses and I just cannot believe how beneficial it is for me! I hope others would be willing to try this as I cannot believe the difference in my pain level after a gentle workout.

contents: Yogalates I have a DVD called Yogalates and as the name suggests, it is a combination of yoga and pilates. The woman who made this DVD said it was a result of having tried yoga and getting hurt because it was too much for her and she didn't have proper instruction where she was attending.. The result is that she is very gentle and gives you levels of involvement -- you decide what you can do -- I noted that someone earlier mentioned this as well. Commonly, early yoga DVDs that I have gotten were so far beyond my stretch ability that I started seeking stretching or gentle stretching alternatives. It is great to have stretches from your PT, but when you get home, first it is hard to remember them all and second it is a bit boring to work out alone.. so for me, this DVD has been a great and calming way to bring about stress reduction as well as the gentle stretches that help me to relax in all areas of my body. With fibromyalgia being my worst problem currently, it even helps with that. Done before bedtime, it doesn't rev you up, so I am thinking it may also be good for anyone who doesn't rest well at night -- it may help you relax and sleep better. PM me for more info. Mary

Names of the CD/VHS that I referred to I found the CD and the VHS that I use and thought I would post the titles and the women who lead them.. the first is Karen Voight -- Pure and Gentle Stretch: This is a 1/2 hour video of gentle stretching and slower moves with longer holds. Not aerobic, so for anyone with difficulty doing harder videos, this would be wonderful. I love it because with fibromyalgia, I find a lot of videos and cds difficult to keep up with or I am too sore and feel like I am not getting anything out of it. With this one, I actually get a little sweat going, but not because it is fast, it is just a good gentle but active stretch video. I love it! I found a copy on eBay for very inexpensive -- I don't know if it is available new anymore, but if it is, I am guessing it would now be on CD as well... This is older -- her clothing tells me she made this in the late 70's early 80's. The second is called" Yoga and Pilates with Louise Solomon. She is gentle and soft spoken and this CD has several differnt levels on it. The first is the only one I have used and I use this again for the fibromyalgia -- it is very good to limber up and there are NO POSES that are difficult -- in fact -- the first set is all done either sitting down or lying on your mat. No special equipment needed other than a stretch band and if you have a hard floor, you would want a mat or a thick towel. This one is only 26 minutes {the first set} and the cover says -- lose weight and look great in 28 days! I believe from doing this CD, it is because she focuses a lot on your core muscle group -- so you will notice a flatter stomach if you do it regularly! The second workout is 30 minutes then there are other segments to teach you the breathing technique as well as an interview with Louise Solomon. As I said, I have never done workout two so I don't know how involved it gets, but in the beginning, Louise tells how she was injured doing yoga -- and that because she wasn't shown the proper moves and wasn't worked within her own limits. She incorporated yoga and pilates to bring together two great ways of strengthening core muscles and teaches very well. I LOVE this one and think you would too if you have fibromyalgia and can't do a lot of aerobic training. If you have sore muscles and do this regularly, you will notice a difference! Hope this helps someone to find something of interest... as well as a great stress reliever in these exercise videos! Blessings, Mary

Don't know if this fits under exercise and fitness or if there should be a I've lost alot of weight and think I'm super woman and then pay for it. I've lost almost 50 lbs in FL and dropped 4 dress sizes by exercising and cutting out junk. The problem came to night when I thougt I was a macho woman and lifted a gas dryer--the thing hasn't worked since I moved it so I THOUGHt I would check one more time before I had some one for dinner. Let's just say lifting a gas dryer alone is not smart. Not to self, bladder ****** off. How stupid could I be? Hugs, Barb
