contents: Well, the old bladder is ok with doing this. I just do my own little jogging type instead of jumping up/down and all around. I lost 5 pounds so far. :woohoo: Bad part is my knees are killing me.

I am so thrilled. I have got to tell someone. I have finally fit into my size 12s again, and some of my size 10s. (I know most of you would be writing in the depression board if you were a 10 or 12, but I have been a 16 and some 18s. So, for me, this is awesome!! I have lost 17lbs in the last 2 weeks alone. (That part probobly isnt good, since I havent been trying. I have been pretty sick, and had no apetite, and everything that I DO eat comes right out) I know I need to go to the Dr to get checked out, but to be honest, I want to wait until I lose just a tad more, LOL! I figure it is probobly the Lupus, which will mean back to the Prednisone, and of course that means, back on with the weight! But, I just want to enjoy this size for a day or two. I used to be very small, before I got Lupus and before the Prednisone, and also, I gained 72 lbs when I was pregnant. (Yes, that was 72!!!) So, I am thrilled to be a semi-normal size at the moment, even though it isnt skinny like most of you here. But, I am tall, (5ft 9), so I finally look decent. I just hope nothing serious is going on.

Hi Amy! I've recently lost alot of weight too..... Doesn't it feel so good? I shopped for a pair of shorts yesterday, and had to go back to the rack for a smaller size :woohoo: !! For me, I actually FEEL better the less I eat!! It seems that whenever I vary my diet to include something new, or when I even eat a large meal, that it increases my pain and symptoms! Hence, the weight loss! I am trying to get the needed vitamins in me by drinking herbal teas, which has also helped alot! Congrat's to you, girl!! Just make sure to keep taking care of yourself!

contents: GRRRR! I've been working out at the gym for an hour every day and eating 1200-1400 cals a day for over a month now and I STILL haven't lost a single pound-- or inches! Every week when I weigh myself I try to myself that next week I'll start to see results...but it's just not happening. I'm going crazy! I really think it might be the Elavil....I have never had trouble losing weight like this. Maybe I should get my thyroid checked again. On the plus side, my resting heart rate is lower (went from 85 to 65!), I have muscles, my back pain is much better, and I have more endurance. Which is awesome. But...I want to lose weight darn-it! Every time I weigh myself, I feel totally crushed. Ah well. I emailed my boyfriend this morning complaining about it and he responded with: "But honey it doesn’t matter, at this weight you’re just as beautiful and sexy as any other." I love that man. Anybody else not seeing results and wanna join my pity party?

contents: Hey Megan - do you find that exercise makes your IC symptoms worse? Maybe certain kinds of exercise? I've been in a four month flare (along with plantar fasciatis in my heel) and I NEED to get moving, but I'm afraid things are going to ramp up. What's your experience? Personally, the IC Diet is actually helping me lose a little weight. I've had to remove all the junk. Thanks!

Hi Roseitty...I'm actually only able to exercise because Elavil has put me into a semi-remission. I took it easy at first, exercised very slowly and moderately, and then I stepped it up slowly. So far, no problems. If anything, I'm better than I was before I started exercising. My advice would be just to take it slow and listen to your body and probably okay it with your doctor too. But I feel exercising is helping my health a lot.

contents: I've heard that Elavil will decrease metabolism and make you hungrier, thus gaining weight. That's the last thing I need! My consult uro wants me to go on 12.5 mgs. of Elavil, but my treating uro wants to add and remove one thing at a time. (the consult uro doesn't believe in Elmiron - that's the rub between the two). Keep plugging away, you may not be losing any weight, but you're in better shape and healthier! Too bad we can't exercise our bladders!

I understand your grief. I quit smoking, I start exercising more often (yoga twice a week and walking on treadmill for 30 -45 min 3x week) and what happens *poof* I gain 10 pounds. Now, maybe some of it's muscle development but my favourite jeans (yes I had a pair of jeans I could wear) no longer fit. GRR! Melissa
signature: You must be the change you want to see in the world. ~ M.K. Ghandi

contents: Oh! Can I join this vent too??? LOL I am right there with ya! I went shopping for some jeans last week, as the ones I was wearing (my lonely little 2 pair) were starting to feel like leggings...thank god they had stretch in them though...So, decided that this was stupid, go buy some that are at least comfortable and look decent. Where I live, I have 2 choices, WalMart and KMart, lol. And Kmart is closer than WalMart by a half an hour. So, hubby and I went. I tried on a bunch of jeans, all in increasing sizes...and none of them looked right or fit right. Then hubby remembered that on weekends I will sometimes wear his around the house. Yep, you guessed it. He ran to the Men's department, and after trying on at least 10 pairs, we found 2 that looked pretty good. How depressing though. That's how weird my shape has gotten thanks to meds. I have NEVER been able to wear guy's jeans before, it was hard to keep smiling about it. He helped though, we decided once I ditch the weight (I am thinking positively!) he can have them, lmao!! Granted, they will have to be made into shorts, he's close to 6'4". But on a brighter note, this weekend I found a pair of women's jeans that fit great! The girl who does my hair mentioned that she and I were the same build, short legs with weight that tends to go to butt and thighs, and that she has fallen in love with Tommy Hilfiger Boyfriend jeans. So, I went with mom-in-law and first pair I tried on, in my NORMAL size fit fabulously!!! She says that even when she's not a few pounds heavier, she loves wearing them anyway!! I honestly can't remember the last time I have spent 60 bucks on jeans, , but it was worth it to feel a better about myself! What a boost it was! :woohoo: I am not giving up on this weight loss thing, but dang, sure is hard sometimes to just not throw in the towel. Hubby says that if we can get through the IC diet, we can do this, lol. It's hard, cause he and my mom can't afford to be on a diet, so it's a challenge. But, like he said, we did it with the IC diet, I can do it again...I hope...LMAO! hugs!

contents: I think men's jeans fit better because you can get them in the proper width & length. How come women's jeans just come in sizes "10, 12, 14", etc... Just because one woman fits perfectly in a 14 doesn't mean that the next woman will. I find that if a pair of jeans fit around my waist, then they're way to short for my long legs. Anyways, keep up the good work! I (from seeing your picture) don't see that you need to lose much weight, but we all see our bodies differently. I look pretty decent dressed, but take those clothes off & OMG! Turn off the lights! Good luck to you!

contents: Karma you can get womens jeans in lengths- I now because I am a shrimp, just over 5', so I get petite sizes. I also am frustrated about my trying to lose weight. I will do really well for a awhile and then all of a sudden it will be back on me. I do mostly stretching exercises and things like crunches. Am looking forward to the nice weather so I can really get back into hiking and long walks. I live in Vermont and do not ski so winter is pretty much spent indoors unless I am out shoveling snow! Judith

LOL, I know what you mean Karma...I feel good in clothes, but then...at some point they gotta come off..LMAO! Then I am reminded of it all again. UGH! I have always had the trouble of getting them to fit in the waist and the butt/hip/thigh - if they fit in the waist, they won't fit the butt area, so I have to get ones that are huge in the waist to fit the butt, and make sure I have lots of belts, lol. Women's clothing is so frustrating anyway. GRRRR. I also think all try-on rooms should be lit much more flattering and have "non-reality" mirrors, so we think we look great. I have been depressed in a flash after visiting one of these carnival attractions... And let's not even mention shopping for bathing suits, that alone can send me into therapy for years..... I keep telling hubby I want a treadmill, I hate walking outdoors sometimes, in winter cause it's cold, lo l- and in summer cause it's hot and buggy. I hate bugs.

contents: Forget about the weight, how did you find that man? (lol = laughing out loud) Lucky. I too have gained some unwanted weight, but I think it's because my active level is down cause I flare up with exercise. Sigh.

How can yo exerice,the pain is so bad that it hurts to sit. I have to sit on a angle. I lost alot of wieght last year, but since all this started with me I have gain it all back plus some. I am so depressed about it. what is this diet that every one is taking about? I am new to the forum and just learing about ic.

I'm not alone! Thank goodness I am not alone. I have been going to the gym, and watching what I eat for almost three months now, and I haven't lost a single pound!! I even tried diet pills, but to no avail. It is getting soooooo frustrating. I have to be careful what I do though. I can do the treadmill, and the recumbent bike (the one where the seat leans back a bit). I have to stay away from anything that brings my knees up past my bladder (any other bike, the eliptical machine....). I just started lifting again. I'm slowly working my way back into it, and I found an ab workout that I can do. It focuses on twisting moves for the abs instead of crunching. Five "normal" crunches will put me in the hospital, but this workout doesn't cause any flares. It's on page 26 of the May issue of Fitness magazine if anyone is interested. I was just able to walk in my first 5k this past weekend, which was great. Running still sends me into flares, but I am hoping that will change someday. I'm hoping that slowly, I will get there, but in the meantime, I am just focusing on the positive things I see, more energy, and feeling good about myself. Two weeks ago, I decided that I no longer care what the scale says. I feel so good after my workouts, but as soon as I step on the scale, it depresses me, so why ruin a good thing. What I am doing for myself is a good thing, and that is what is important.

My GP and I were talking about weight. I let her know that since my cancer doc. but me on new med. I have gained weight. I am still doing water aerobics 3 x a week. (except this week IC pain is getting the best of me and my son and daughter are coming out Sun. so housework is taking time.) She says to keep up the exercise for your health and quit worrying about the weight loss. I have lost 80 pounds but there is the end . My surgeon wants me to loose about 40 pounds more before he will do breast reconstruction. So to say the lease I am trying to loose weight but my body is not coming along for the ride. I also was told that threre is a period in weight loss were you stop loosing and but on muscle.
signature: Interstitial Cystitis Stomach problems diastolic dysfunction vascular insufficency of left leg pelvid floor disorder hypothroidisim asthma bread cancer - 5yrsl. remission swollowing difficulty fatigue lots of doctors lots of med. Keep your eyes on the Lord and he will direct your paths.

contents: I hear you all! I QUIT taking tricyclics because of the weight gain. I was gaining about a pound every two weeks. That adds up pretty quickly! And I work out five days a week powerwalking. The SSRI's aren't blameless either, especially Paxil. When I went off of it two years ago, I lost 10 pounds without trying. And I wasn't eating any differently. In fact, I went on vacation, and was eating MORE! So I truly think that these drugs can affect your metabolism, not just make you eat more. So don't blame yourselves! Don't get me wrong: These drugs are very helpful (currently I'm having some of the same pudge factor with Lyrica, but it helps my anxiety enough that I'm trying to live with it.) I guess it's just a matter of weighing (unfortunate pun) costs and benefits. I feel your pain! Amy (Mrs. B)

Patients with chronic pain, frequency and burning may be prescribed Topamax®. Topamax® appears to reverse some of the nerve damage that leads to a painful condition called peripheral neuropathy, one of the most common complications of diabetes, in which diabetic people often feel like their feet are being stabbed by needles or that their hands are on fire. In a previous trial, Topamax® appeared to relieve the symptoms of pain that characterize peripheral neuropathy. But what was even more striking is that all the patients in the study unexpectedly lost weight and experienced a drop in cholesterol and diastolic blood pressure levels. Topamax® can be acidic in the higher doses and may exacerbate symptoms in an IC patient. Moreover, it may cause kidney stones to develop. However, the potential advantages of this drug (pain relief, ease/deter prediabetic syndrome, weight reduction, etc.), makes it worth considering as a treatment option for IC patients. If patient experiences burning or kidney stones, Urocit-K® may be added to counterbalance those side-effects. I have been on Topamax® 30 mg. (after gaining 50 pounds on Elavil) and have not had any side-effects. Moreover, I've lost 30 pounds to date without exercising. The lowest dose available for Topamax® is 15mg in a sprinkle tab - I take 2 daily. The contents in this email are provided for informational purposes only. The contents are not intended in any way to be a substitute for professional medical advice. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding your medical condition. If you would like more information on Topamax® to share with your healthcare provider, please send your request for "Topamax® Info" to [email protected]. Best wishes, ICB
ICB, Do you feel dumb on Topamax? One of my doctors said it's often called Dopamax because of how dopey it makes people feel. With my job, I definitely can't be dopey! Amy (Mrs. B)

Amy: I've heard the same things from patients taking a high dose of Topamax. However, I don't find myself any more forgetful, than I was before I started taking the Topamax; but, I'm on a very small dose (30mg). Remember that starting at a low dose is always a safe choice; it may be the only amount you need to take care of your symptoms. If you need a higher dose, starting at a low dose will accustom your body to the medication and may produce less or no side effects. Remember to check with your healthcare provider for his/her recommendations. Best wishes, ICB

Megancolleen, Have you considered that maybe your body is in "starvation" mode because you're not eating enough calories/day? It could be that 1200-1400 isn't enough. I have been using SparkPeople.com (FANTASTIC free weight loss site) which helped me to realize that you have to take in enough food and nutrients to lose weight efficiently.

The great herb You may think this is crazy, but try Hoodia ( I think i'm spelling it right). It is an all natural herb that helps to cure hunger. The ancient African tribes would use this during times of drout and when they went on hunting trips. That is what I have used and it really helped. Give it a try, it won't bother you at all.

Hi everyone, My name is Sam, I'm 19, and I was diagnosed with IC in November. I am very active and have been doing martial arts for over 2 years. The problem is that the martial art that I do is very vigorous. It is called kendo (Japanese fencing) and it involves alot of moving about (and jostling is hard on my bladder). We wear about 20 pounds of armor and practice generally lasts a few hours. It is very demanding and is difficult for healthy people. My instructors know of my condition and are very understanding and let me take breaks when I need to, but I can't seem to anticipate when I have a flare up, sometimes I am fine and others I am doubled over in pain. I think my IC is milder than many people I've read about on this site but the problem is that I never know how I'm going to feel. I know it is a weird hobby but it has become a big part of my life and I love it, so I was wondering if anyone has advice on how to prevent pain caused by vigorous exercise. I'm 19 and a college student and I am not willing to give up this part of my life because of my stupid bladder! Any advice would be very welcome. Sam
signature: daily meds: elmiron, hydroxyzine, ditropan, ambien as needed: prydium, lortabs bladder cocktails: heparin, lidocane, depo-medrol, sodium bicarb... all that jazz

contents: I've been there Hi Sam. I am also 19 years old and had praticed martial arts since I was 8 years old. I had to quit when I was 14 because of my health, but the doctors didn't figure out what was wrong until a year ago. After being diagnosed and knowing what was wrong, I tried ot get back into martial arts, but it still caused me too much pain. I had to give up something I loved and it was hard, but it is apart of this disease. In my experience, the only thing you can do is find a less intense martial arts. I am also a college student and school is very important to me, as was martial arts, but I had to give it up in order to do well in school. I don't know how severe you are, but I have been doing pilates and yoga and enjoy it very much. Alot of the movement and stances remind me of the ones I did in martial arts, but has less of an impact on my bladder. I really hope this helps you and I'm sorry that I couldn't give you better advice. I believe we all have to give up something with IC, but it can sometimes be better. For example, we can't eat chocolate, right, however, there is a substance called carob and it tastes like chocolate and is actually healthy for you. What kind of medications are you taking? If you found the right amount or combination, then maybe your hobby would get easier. I really hope everything works out for you and you find some way to continue your hobby and not have to give it up like I did. Good luck!
I did Aikido for over a year with no problems
signature: After suffering with IC for 40 years, my bladder was removed in Dec 05...YAY!

contents: Hi Laura: I asked my physical therapist before I bought my elliptical, which one she recommended. She said to buy one that did not have arm pulls. She said they tend to make your body go out of alignment, which can cause pain in your hips, back, etc. and may cause IC symptoms to flare. I bought a Precor and have been very happy with it! I'm sure that it would work much better if I used it more often. I hope that it works for you! Best wishes, ICB

Thanks, ICB. I actually went to Dick's Sporting Goods today and tried out two of their Horizon elliptical machines. I spent three minutes on it (my max after no cardio in about two years) and did not have a flare! I figure if I can do two minutes, 3-4 times a day I will be good as a start. Thanks for the tip about the handles. The one I am buying has both so I can 'try' it and avoid it if necessary. I knew I did not want one with only the pulls because your arms get so tired (I had an exercise bike with the moving arms and remember). :-)

(i have to remind myself constantly..lol). Try different exercises... don't give up! You'll find some that work for you too!!!
signature: IC since 1997 I couldn't make it without my husband- He IS the greatest!!! I try to believe that God wouldn't give me anything more than what he knows I can handle

contents: Yes, I am having great difficulty finding an exercise that doesn't increase my IC symptoms: pressure, soreness/pain, burning ... Even if I walk too far, I get increased pain. It is very frustrating and ridiculous--and very difficult to stay fit!

Do you like swimming? When I'm so sore in my bladder that I don't want to walk (forget anything with even more impact, those things went out long ago), I swim laps at the local community pool. In fact, today I did 1 mile, and this was on a bad bladder day. The key is that I am horizontal, there is no impact, and I love the water. The pool has very little chlorine, due to an ultraviolet filter. I did have to get out once to pee. If you don't swim, perhaps a water exercise class would help? There is very little impact involved in those. Good luck.

Walking on flat ground is best (with extra padding in my shoes), gentle yoga classes also are good. At the gym, I can use the rowing machine & the elliptical trainer. (The treadmill gave me a flare right away & the recumbent bike would've been okay except my legs kept bumping my tummy & bugging my bladder - how embarrassing Maybe when I'm skinnier, I'll try that again.).

contents: Crunches or anything else that crunches that area (pilates, the eliptical machine at the gym, and certain types of bikes) are the worst idea in the world for me. What I have found that doesn't cause pain are ab exercises that twist instead of crunch. Try the workout in the May issue of Fitness magazine (page 26). It's the only thing that I have found so far to work my abs and keep myself out of tremendous pain. Hope it helps!
Lower ab workouts -- BAD for me! Amy (Mrs. B)
Just wondering if this happens to any of you, but I tend to retain a boatload of fluid when I try weight lifting or any other serious exerize. I'm thinking yoga might be the thing to try for me..

Thought this info from Dr.Moldwin may help. I am thinking Mr. Moldwin agrees that yoga is the best choice. I suspect the swelling is due in part to the muscles feeling over worked.....It may help to take a bath afterwards.....keep us updated on how things go. Question: Why is there pain the day after intercourse? Dr. Moldwin: That is like getting punched in the eye. You don’t see the swelling the day it happens. When you work out in the gym, you don’t feel the pain until the next day. Pelvic floor dysfunction and vulvodynia go hand in hand. Most practitioners who deal with vulvodynia find that most of their patients have pelvic floor dysfunction. That is treated immediately as well as neuropathic problems. They use creams as well as medications for nerve problems. The patient’s significant other can do myofascial release by putting their finger into the vaginal vault, finding the trigger points, and pushing on them. Question: I have a lot of pain after I exercise. Dr. Moldwin: You have to know your limitations. The body gives you pain for certain reasons. Your body is telling you not to do that. I am a big believer in keeping fit. Yoga is probably the best thing for you. Even people who are doing bench pressing, which requires bearing down, are tightening their pelvic muscles in order to keep from urinating. That is a natural reaction in that set of circumstances. You should not be doing that. You have to notice that while you are exercising. If you are having problems with certain exercises, hold off on them for a while or at least tone down your exercise regimen. Be careful of sitting on a bicycle seat. There are seats with no cushioning between the scrotum or vagina and the anus. When people sit on the gap in the seat, they tighten their muscles, because there is no support there.

contents: I've been doing some yoga stretches again. Nothing major. I'm not twisting myself into a pretzel. I have noticed the cobra postition seems to help a little with the bladder/back pain. Has anyone else noticed this? Seems if I don't do it the bladder/back pain is stronger. Maybe stretching the bladder muscles helps?

Good for you! :woohoo: I'm glad that cobra seems to help with your bladder/back pain. My yoga teachers say that working opposing muscles (in this case, your lower back muscles) can help strengthen those muscles, stretch them out further and relieve discomfort. For example, strengthening the quadriceps actually helps you stretch your hamstrings better. You may want to follow up cobra with the child's pose. Child's pose always helped me when I had lower back pain and abdominal discomfort from my period, and it's a gentle, restorative posture that is often used to counteract spine stretches like cobra. Focus on deep gentle breathing too, which will calm your nervous system down. I've noticed that if my bladder is spasming some before yoga class, it is nice and calm afterward. You might also be interested in the following book: Cystitis: A Time to Heal with Yoga & Accupressure, An Eight Week Exercise Program with Special Information on Interstitial Cystitis & Urethral Syndrome Amazon.com http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/1403388709/102-2626834-7704101?v=glance&n=

contents: Hey Laura, I too go to Curves 3 days a week. I have not had any troubles with my bladder at all. I do really well, I think it's because you can make it as hard or as easy as you like, the pace is up to you and what you are able to do. The recovery stations between machines you can walk in place, jog in place, dance in place etc. I usually do a nice even walking in place, lol. Then I thought I'd get fancy and do a light jog and I believe that's how I sprained my ankle, LOL. Go figure, doesn't hurt the one body part I though it would (bladder) but I hurt my ankle!! LOL Honestly, I love it. I even got my mom-in-law going to one where she lives, and she likes it too! I am finally starting to see improvements in my body and that is just giving me the boost I need to keep at it!! I signed up last Aug, but with on and off health troubles I wasn't able to be "regular", but I started being able to get serious 6 weeks ago, and I have lost 5 lbs and a few inches! Plus, I see more tone in my arms and I believe I am starting to see some ab muscles again!! That's my 2 cents on it, get a guest pass to one and see what it's about, etc. That might help you to see if it's do-able for you!! Hugs!

contents: I belong to a gym next to a curves.... and I chose the gym because I like to do longer periods on the treadmill and elliptical. Plus... one of my favorite exercises is the rower which is totally IC friendly, a huge workout and burns lots of calories!! I usually do 30 to 40 minutes of aerobics... then then, a few times a week, work my way through the weight machines. But I stay away from the weird pelvic machine where you open your legs up and close them! No thank youuuu om that torture device! jill

contents: I go to Curves and I love it!!! I havn't been in a while and just started back this week. I take it slow b/c of my IC and I have been told I have Chronic Fatigue and i also believe I may have fibro (never dx) though. I will say i had to skip the one that you do squats on b/c it would aggravate my IC but the ladies were real nice and told me i could do leg lifts or skip it and do another machine twice. They also understand that if they don't see me in a while it's probabley b/c of my illness. Join, you'll love it, when i went 3x regularly i lost 7 pounds in 2 mths!! Just pace yourself, believe it or not their are a lot of ladies in mine that have health problems so we all pace ourselves at our own pace and we all have gotten results

I agree with danedane! Since spraining my ankle a week and half ago, I have been skipping the squat machine and any that might put too much strain on it, I just a few of the others twice! And when it was really hurting I just stood still on the recovery station, lol. It's just so flexible to meet your needs...And at 30 min a day (or longer if you choose, some of the gals go around 3-4 times! Um, no thanks, LMAO) 3x's a week, I can do. I leave from work, get there and I am home in an hour. I joined to get stronger right after my bout with the colitis, I was a little weakling at 116lbs. I needed to rebuild muscle etc, and if I could do it at that point, anyone can! Yesterday there was a lady in there with me I swear was close to 80!! LOL Heck my mom-in-law is 60 -OH! Please don't tell her I told!! I think you'd like it, I am an avid exercise hater, and I find it do-able, lol. And they do have guest passes and I think you can get like a 1 week trial or something as well.... Do let us know if you try it!!

contents: I guess I am so scared now of this body I live in. I used to do an hour of Richard Simmons, dancing and sweating, each day; and I loved it. I work from home and dread having to go out to Curves, so I decided to try Richard today. I took it soooo easy; no bouncing, knee lifts, etc. I was so afraid that I was close to tears half the time; isn't that pathetic? I spent so much of my early life conquering all my imaginary fears, that I am just having a really hard time coming to terms with real, continuous pain. I am coming out of 'denial' stage and it doesn't feel good at all. I never imagined I would be scared to operate my own body. Anyway, I really enjoyed the 16 minutes of movement (sloooww) that I did but didn't feel that great afterwards. We'll see if it will fade soon.... Thanks everyone, for letting me whine a little.

contents: Hey, no problem!! I know that feeling, that what if my body won't do what I want it to do, or will it make me suffer for days afterwards??? I think you are on the right track, seeing what you can do in the comfort of your own home before venturing out, I call it prudent! No sense in putting yourself in an iffy situation until you know how the old body's going to react. I see it as smart!!! I am one who only signed up to go because a friend signed up with me, we had a deal - we were doing it together. I would have NEVER gone by myself, way to shy for that sort of thing, lol. We started together, but she ended up with some health problems and that left me alone! Yikes!! But, I did it. I kept going even though I was uncomfortable as heck, so out of my comfort zone it wasn't even funny, but I pushed through it, and here I am now, going all the time by myself! My hubby is amazed that I am going alone. So unlike me!!! I still get that "oh my goodness I don't want to do this" anxiety feeling every now and then about going - alone...but once I get there and get moving, it goes away and before I know it I am home, lol. So, the fears thing I hugely understand!

contents: Well, I am getting tired of just walking..........and one day I saw some African dancers on TV and I got kind of excited thinking maybe I could do some of that..........so........I just bought an instructional DVD from Amazon........(it was about half price from them also) I just got it today and I have watched about 30 minutes of it and did a bit of it........you do it at your own pace.........she shows you slower movements and more advanced movements...........it is really fun actually..............I was looking at other instructional-dance-for-exercise-type videos..........I am not going to break the bank........but I may just get a couple more. I don't think it is going to bother me...........on the contrary, the way it is presented..........as an African healing dance is very inspirational......I can't wait to do more.......... Just thought I would share!

contents: Hey. I'm a dancer. Yet because of IC I haven't been going to classes. My symtoms get worse when I'm active. I miss jazz and ballet. I was even trying hip-hop. I was wondering if anybody else danced before IC, stopped because of pain, then got back into it full on? How long did it take? What meds do you take that helped you? Can you be comfortable in dance tights again?

hi dancer hey, i just wrote a long response to you, but this computer kicked me off in the interim, and all was lost i'm gonna try again, briefer this time, and if you further questions, i will respond. i had to give up dancing. the postures that were called for in it were not helping according to my many physical therapist and rehab specialist/physiatrist (specialist in musculoskeletal issues). It was long time hobby, ballet and modern mostly, and it has been about 4 years since last class. however, running to the bathroom in midst of class not great either docs say the pulling in and up (think ballet) not conducive to relieve pain. also a lot of the postures in ballet especially are not appropriate. They actually want me to relax my stomach muscles (but not hunch over), so pulling in=not good. i do elliptical, free weights, core strengthening, and some stuff specific to my phys therapy rehab. meds which have worked--not too many. neurontin and valium. actually i'm noticing that my supplements--b6, calcium citrate, fish oil, psyllium (fiber in pill form for IBS), magnesium, small iron supplements (only slow release and cut in half), seem to work. I also take Ph science "PH control" if i eat something outside the IC diet, which I don't usually do. seems to help. i'm not endorsing them, but i found it more effective than prelief. hang in there, i know it's overwhelming, and i'm still searching too. a lot of the postures i have learned, i have to unlearn. i have PFD too and CPP, so took a long time to i.d. those muscles and retrain myself to know how to relax. the issue for me, is that my "resting rate tonicity" is still high. so, i'm working on that. oh, tights are doable on some days, and actually feel good (well pantyhose are lighter and easier), but some days i can do nothing more than sweats or cotton soft gauchos if it's a work day.

Hello everyone: I've recently decided to get fit so i've been going to the gym about 3 times a week. The only problem is that i've been flaring up lately and I don't know what the cause is. It could mean that my period is coming, which it often does, but i'm concerned about it being related to my exercises. I've been doing crunches to make my abs stronger. Could this be a problem? I just wondered because I know that's around the area where but bladder is but I honestly don't know if there's a link. I may just be flairng, who knows, but if other people have noticed any similar results when they do crunches, I figured it wouldn't hurt to ask. Hope everyone is doing well (as can be!) dramaqueen

Hi dramaqueen, I workout also. I find that aggressive exercise will make me flare, but low impact exercises, such as crunches makes my bladder feel better in the long term. I still get flares, but I don't think they are related to my exercises. When I'm in a flare, I simply don't exercise. I actually try to put the focus of my crunches on my lower abdamen, as it seems to help relieve my bladder discomfort by strengthening the muscles in that area. This is just my experience, though. You could be different. Hope you can figure it out and feel better!

contents: I do hurt more after doing any exercises, like walking or dancing, sadly. The dance moves had crunch like movements and it caused flares. But I still have to get IC meds (after last test) and that should help me so I can be active again. Luck to you.

contents: before IC I would swim all the time. I have not tried and would like to swim again. Has anyone been able to swim without a flair. My step daughter wants me to take my life guard with her. I was a life guard for a love time but it scares me a little. what do you think.

contents: I do fine as long as the pool is not heavily clorinated. We have an above ground pool at home and we use Baquacil to keep it clean and I do fine in it. Donna

contents: I habe a Urostomy and I love to swim. Go for it. I even love the Ocen. Do not worry. Hugs DebbieD

I tried the IC yoga dvd and I have to say I am kinda disapointed. IT was set up very stale not relaxing at all. Normally I do yoga to release tension and I wanted to make sure none of my movements were hurting my bladder. I didn't seem to find it helpfull there so I just wanted to put my 2 cents in about that. I wish I could return it but they do not take DVD's back. I am bumbed and was hoping for some therapudic relaxation.
I'm sad to hear this...I was really wanting to try it, too. I really don't like any of my exercise tapes that I've bought over the years....
signature: Holly 1/19/2006 diagnosed with PBS/possible IC 2/1/2006 Elmiron & Elavil 50mg 3/2006 began PN symptoms 7/18/2006 broth culture sent (found Strep D) stopped Elmiron & Elavil and began long-term amoxicillin - IC symptoms relieved at last! 09/29/2006 PNMLT found pudendal nerve damage on rt side (nerve block to be done Oct 4) 10/20/2006 - lyrica for PN pain (didn't last) 11/20/2006 - cymbalta for PN (didn't last) 12/2006 - stopped all meds and started Dr. Moldwin's therapy of valium and PT for PFD, which is still working for me UPDATE 7/1/2008 - weaning off the valium...taking 2mg nightly about 5 days a week, so doing great. No more pudendal nerve pain, although I'm careful about how I sit at work and try to exercise to stay relaxed to minimize PFD. A powerplate workout machine helps massage those inner PF muscles, and I take MSM and glucosamine (in MonaVie) religiously, which are both natural antiinflammatories. I feel GREAT! UPDATE 2/16/2010 - completely pain free! Looks like the "IC" pain caused my PFD, which pinched and damaged my pudendal nerve, which in turn sends out misguided signals to my PF muscles....battling this pain cycle, but at least I have been pain-free bladder-wise for 2 years now! JMHO - I don't believe that antibiotic therapy should be considered controversial....the theory that "IC" is a disease should be controversial. I believe that we all must find out what causes the damage to our bladder linings, stop that, and then let it heal. In only 9 short months, I added EVERYTHING back to my diet and have been "IC"-free for 2 years and counting.

contents: What kinds of excersizes do you like to do? I like my current yoga dvd I was just looking for something that may be helfull about the bladder. The one yoga dvd I do now is an evening meditation and morning meditation, they are 20 min each and they are great to get me relaxed for the day or after the day. I am just kinda bored with it. I supose I will just look for the same people that host it when i buy my next one:/.

I like pilates/yoga, but don't have the windsor pilates. I have Denise Richard's, which is probably the best one in my collection, as it has both pilates and yoga. The only drawback is all the balancing on your arms things...I have syntendonitis in both wrists and it is impossible to hold up my weight on them. I'm taking MSM which had done wonders for my wrists, but I'm still not able to use them to the extent that I need to do this video. What is the name of that yoga tape you have?

contents: I bought the yoga Zone Evening stress release for beginners. Also I was wondering if you have ever tried Hyaluronic acid for your joint problems? It provides joint lubercation and skin hydration and skin repair. I take the one by Jarrow because it is the purest I could find it has condroitin in it too and along with my fibro it seems to help my bladder and thirst problems. We are allways releasing fluids so it is important to replace them.

I have a yoga zone one that I forgot about. A friend taped it for me. I wonder wher eit is. Thanks for the info on hyaluronic acid...I would love to try anything to help with the thirst, not only my creaky knees.

I did some research and found a product that has both hyaluronic acid and MSM, which I'm already taking. That way, I only have to take one pill for both, and not increase my daily amount of pills. Yay! Not only will that help my joints, but it appears that it's good for the synovial tissues. I also read that you can tell a difference in 2 to 4 months. I'm impressed so far. Thanks again for the info.

contents: Your so welcome Anytime I can help I am happy too

Just wondering how many of you count them? I use to long ago and dreaded going back to it but guess I will have too along with using the slim fast. I took out my old trusty diet book and did some calculating. I'm 5' 4" and should weigh 115. Heaviest would be 125 which is what I use to weigh. I'm allowed 1350 calories a day but going by the 1200. I sure am hungry right now..... but I've had my calorie limit for today. Where is my duct tape????
I used to count calories but haven't done that in a long time. I'm also trying to diet by following weight watchers. I was afraid to try slim fast. Are the ingredients ok in that for your ic?
signature: Pam

contents: The slim fast cappocinio is the only one that bothers the bladder. Otherwise, the rest are ok. I've been counting calories and didn't relize I was waaaay over my limit. Did have one lifesaver last night. I was so hungry but that helped to stop the cravings. The diet I'm following you are suppose to loose 10 pounds fast. Here's hoping.

weight watchers. Hi Pam, I am on WW too. In fact, my instructor is named Pam. I am nursing, so I have more points and the weight loss is slow. But it is steady. my IC is not bad right now, probably due to nursing. Once I stop nursing though, I will heve to watch it. what have you found to be IC friendly and low in points?
If you are trying to count calories, check out Sparkpeople.com www.sparkpeople.com Weight loss program with built in calorie, exercise counters, free! I lost 13 pounds on it

contents: kadi, thanks for the tip. i will try this online program
signature: hugs, Janie

contents: I use something similar -- fitday.com. It really helps me keep track of my eating and exercise patterns. I am counting calories, but I've struggled with what to make my limit. At first I was restricing to 1200 a day but after a couple of weeks I got tired of being hungry all the time. I upped it to around 1400, but now I'm not losing weight, so I think I'm gonna have to go back down to 1200. I am also trying to eat based on the new food pyramid -- veggies, oils, nuts and legumes, whole grains, etc. but it's hard because a lot of the stuff that's healthy for you (like avocados, olive oil, nuts, etc) are high in fat and calories. I don't much like eating only 1200 calories, but Elavil has given me the metabolism of a slug, so I guess I'm going to have to do it in order to lose weight.
signature: I have: Interstitial Cystitis,Eustacian Tube Dysfunction, IBS, Sciatica. I take: Elavil 50 mgs, Balziva birth control, and Elmiron instillations. I am: Not my disease. My Myspace Page: http://profile.myspace.com/index.cfm...iendid=4249067

contents: counting calories WW has a point system based on your weight. It takes a certain amount of calories to help you lose weight. If you eat too few, your body goes into starvation mode and locks down your metabolism. This makes it nearly impossible to lose weight. If you tell me your weight, (you can send me a private message if you want to ) I could give you a better idea about how many calories you need. I have a friend at WW who has to make her self eat all of her points in order to lose weight. ( I don't have this problem) It has been working for me. I have lost ten pounds in the last month(from 167 to 157) and I still have about 20 to go. I have found that it helps to drink a whole lot of water, in any form you can take it. I make my protein shake in a blender with a lot of ice. This way I get a lot of water without even realizing it. It also helps to eat foods high in fiber and protein. Vegetables are very helpful, especially in a soup ( more water) but you should make it yourself so you don't get all of the preservatives you get from a can. (horrible on an IC bladder.) Good Luck!

I'm doing the Sparkpeople thing too, just got serious about it yesterday, lol. (counting the calories that is, lol) Great job Kadi!! I can't wait til I can honestly say I lost something - besides my mind, lol. I about flipped when I saw it in "black and white" just what the heck I was eating, holy cow. I didn't realize that 2 tbsp honey had like 128 calories, and I know I use a lot of honey in my herb teas...yikes! I did discover though, that there's lots of stuff I can cut out and not miss - that are empty calories. Like the handfull of M&M's I sneak at least once a day, either peanut or plain...yikes. I saw the tally's for those two and I think I passed out for a second!! LMAO!! For my goal weight of 120, (at 150 now), I am supposed to be eating 1200-1550 calories a day. So far so good...but only been closely watching since yesterday. I actually even put my cereal (Special K, lol) in a measure cup to see what 1 cup looked like...lol. Nice to see I've got good company to do this with!

contents: I started back on WW too. I haven't been doing so well, though. I haven't lost a pound in 2 weeks! I started exercising again yesterday & today. I hope I can get it back off. I hit lifetime a few months before I found out I had IC (when the horrible pains began) I have since gained back 25lbs. and feel disgusting. I tried going to meetings again, but I can't eat anything they are talking about. It all has tomatoes and fruit, yogurt, soy, artificial sweetners, etc. I eat bread and pasta, that's my downfall. I wish there was a support group for IC/WW wouldn't that be great?? If you have any WW ideas for IC, PLease let me know. You can send me a private message if you like. I need all the help I can get! Thanks, KK
signature: Hopekeepers Support Group Leader Diagnosed IC 1/07/04 Fibromyalgia, IBS, PFD, Urethral Instability, GERD, Chronic Unexplained Pelvic Pain for most of my life Remember, all is not lost as long as we keep our sense of humor!

contents: Sorry Tracey! Sorry Tracey, I didn't see your post until just now! At 150 you should get 22 points a day, with 35 weekly flex points that you can use any time. Now, 150 is actually the cut off point, and once you get below 150, you should go down to 20 points a day. I am not exactly sure how many calories that is, because the point system goes by a combination of calories, fat grams, and grams of fiber. I've actually been surprised at what a good program this is. About what to eat IC wise- I have just now started having a problem with this. I went into remission with my pregnancy, and since I started nursing I have been able to keep my IC under control by drinking a lot of water. Otherwise, I haven't had the IC restrictions on my diet that I had before my pregnancy. Lately though, I have had a lot of IC pain. It is harder to do WW with IC restrictions for sure. I am just starting to do some research for recipes I can safely use. My WW leader keeps a website with recipes you might find useful. It is: members.cox.net/debilost43 Good Luck! P.S- This weekend I got rid of all the maternity clothes in my closet. Yeah! :woohoo: Hopefully the weight just keeps coming off.
Thank you so much Sherry!!! I copied and pasted it so I could save my "numbers". Oh, and congrats on putting away the maternity clothes too!! Yahoo!!!! Hugs!

contents: I am doing the sparkpeople ----it is really great! I was on weightwatchers online for a year a few years back and lost 30 lbs on weight watchers, but I like this program just as much and it is free! I'm starting to exercise again too - just going for 30 - 45 minute walks. It flares me up a little but I'm going to have to do it cause I found out I have high cholesterol and they want to put me on a medicine for that. I am saying "NO WAY" to more meds right now and i'm going to do my best to lose weight and exercise! Good luck to everyone else who is also struggling with getting fit with IC.
signature: hugs, Janie

contents: What is sparkpeople? Never heard of it. Glad you like it. Glad you are doing walks too. Keep up the good work.
pottywoman, Check out: http://www.sparkpeople.com It's a great online tool for counting calories and giving nutrional information. I really love it and it is FREE! :-)
signature: hugs, Janie

contents: Thanks Janie for that great web site! :woohoo: How did you find it? I signed up and planned my meals for tomorrow. I couldn't believe how much I could have to eat from their list. Also did take a while for me to figure out how to add a food item. I mean I like peanut butter but not 3 times a day. Have you added any points? I have 3 for signing in. I will have to forget the cardio workout plan they made. Don't know how to remove it so will have to add walking instead. They want crunches...outcheeeeeeee This will be so easy. Do you know how to print the meal day plan without having to print the whole page with ads? Thanks again for the letting me know about this site.

I actually heard about it from another ICN member. Unfortunately I can't remember who it was right now. Kadi maybe? Anyway, I've been wildly enthusiastic about it too. Especially since I had been on weight watchers online program and spent a lot of money on that (it worked) but this seems to be just as good and free! Glad you like it. We'll be sparkpeople buddies then and try to encourage each other. My favorite thing is the "Favorites" where I am able to have all the foods I eat regularly and can find them easily. sorry i haven't been printing anything so I haven't figured out how to print.
signature: hugs, Janie

contents: Instead of printing them out I wrote it down on paper. Just had my cereal with milk and talk about being full. Plus I bought that new vanilla weight watchers puff wheat kind. It's nothing to write home about. Didn't notice the "favorite" part on that web site so figured out how to do that also. This makes dieting a whole lot easier. No figuring out calories, portion ect. They even had Lean Cuisine listed and slim fast. Sure, I will be your sparkpeople buddy. What kind of exercising are you doing? I kind of have to do what I can on different days. That part I couldn't really fill out right. Have to go by what I feel like doing but most days I am going to at least walk. Need to do the muscle building exercises too but can't as often. Good luck with this Janie.

Thank you pottywoman! :-) Good luck to you too. Dieting is hard but I know we can do it with a little help from our friends! My exercise is walking. I am up to over a mile on the treadmill. I have it set on a slight incline so it is a harder work out. Before I got sick I used to walk 3-5 miles a day. Back in my younger days I used to run - even competed in a half marathon once. Now, I have gained so much weight I feel like I am lucky to "waddle" a mile! I was recently diagnosed with high cholesterol (271) and so I am determined to lose weight again but since I have had IC i have been really laying around and overeating on high carb foods. I can't believe how fast a person can pack on the pounds doing that but I am living proof that high carbs and no exercise is not the way to live!
signature: hugs, Janie

contents: Hi Everyone, I have had IC since about August and was diagnosed about a month or so ago officially with a cysto/hydro. I am starting to feel better and resuming some of my normal activties. (YAAAAY!) I used to be a big time athelete and worked out a lot (Soccer, Tennis, Running, Weight Lifting, Bikram Yoga, etc.). I haven't exercised in quite awhile from the IC pain but I was thinking about slowly getting back in there. I signed up for a pilates class (10 sessions over 10 weeks). Do you think this will make IC symptoms worse? Anyone have experience with pilates and IC? Id rather be playing soccer but right now that doesn't seem like an option. Pilates felt safer. What do people think? Thanks--KJ

I took up Bikram Yoga after being diagnosed and recently stopped for 2 months. What a mistake. I I just started up again and already feel better. I just modify my poses if I need to. Did you have trouble with it ? Paula

Bikram Yoga, Pilates, and Sex I haven't done it in awhile. I loved it. I love the idea that you do what you can do and that its not about getting the poses perfect but more with the process. Some days your on some days your not but either way you are helping your bodya. I moved last year and its been harder to find a place I like out here. But when I used to do it with my roomate we loved it. We became addicted. We are both really athletic and loved the rush and real work out from yoga. I mean beforehand I did regular yoga and couldnt really get into it because Im more of an active sports person and it was also hard for me to concentrate, relax, and get the rhythm down (all more reasons to do it right). But with Bikram I loved the heat, the way the heart really got working and it made it easier for me to focus, relax, and concentrate. I miss it a lot and thought pilates (since it was close to home) may be something similar that could help. I miss my stress relivers (exercise and sex). and I think it would help my IC a lot to get active again. I want to forge a good relationship with my body since I feel like Im a prisoner to it now. Where do you live? Would you advise me to do bikram again if I could find it? What about the pilates? I figured it was related and I could probably ease into that and get the healing going. I just dont want to push myself too hard I am just getting my symptoms under control. On the other hand Im really craving being active, missing the health benefits and stress relief. What you think? Should I go for it?-KJ P.S. I have mild IC
I was wondering about this, I bought a pilates reformer a few months ago...but haven't had the nerve to try it!
signature: current meds: cytotec 200mcg twice daily toprol allegra ONLY 3 MEDICATIONS!!! YAY! contents: I live in Massachusetts. You may be able to find a class near you.. BYTT Spring 2023 in Thailand https://www.bikramyoga.com 26 Asanas | 90 Minutes Use the class finder option. There are probably many other "Hot Yoga" private studio's near you.This website is for Bikrams franchise. I wish I could advise you either way, however as you know, all of us are different in this horrible disease. It's all up to your body. If you do find a class talk to your teacher before hand so that she/he will understand your situation. It does really make me feel better. I know for me riding a bike is the worst I can do, however some people on this site can and do enjoy that. My goal is to Rollerblade this summer again, that is my favorite. On a good note, I did scuba dive this summer. I passed on maybe two dives due to IC. My podiatrist recommended Pilate's over yoga. (due to all my tight leg muscles )I haven't tried that yet. If you try Yoga let me know how you made out. Paula

I took personal Pilates on the reformer lessons and it was alot of fun. It did not stress my bladder at all. You should give it a try! Also, try Pilates on the mat classes which will work your core area even more so.

Pilates....so far so good Hey guys- Went to my first pilates class today and I think it went really well. There are only four other people in the class which means a lot of individual attention from the instructor. I told her about my IC after the class and she thinks this would be good for me based on what I told her about Pelvic Floor Muscles and stuff. I read some stuff online that says pilates and yoga help to strengthen these muscles and your core. I didnt have too much pain which was great and it was great to be active again. I am definitely going to be sore tomorrow but it feels great to have been active. The instructor was really cool with me just doing what I felt comfortable. I hope this class helps me feel more "normal". It might even help my IC. I am about to get my period any second now so that of course didnt make it easier. With that being said I almost felt no pain while I was doing the exercises. I had to concentrate and that distracted me from my pain. Very cool. I had done some pilates and yoga before and some of the stretching, posture, and poses were similar. It def made me miss "hot yoga" and my old roomie who used to be my partner in crime. We would sweat like crazy and have such a rush leaving the place. Well Im going to run with the pilates and see how the 10 week session goes. My goal is to get through then I might start adding some more exercise including yoga and weight lifting back in. Thanks everyone for the support. If anyone else has success stories or info on pilates (I think I saw someone on another post talking about it) please let me know. I am also thinking about buying a video to do at home once I feel comfortable with it. Has anyone did that? Hope you guys are doing well--KJ P.S does anyone have other sports that were nice to their IC. I know Im not ready yet but I am totally missing soccer and tennis.

KJ, I'm glad Pilates is good for you, I'll be keeping my fingers crossed for you. I'm in Scituate,I'm not sure where Lunenburg is but I know it's not near me.I just got a brochure in the mail yesterday from a Yoga center in the Berkshires, is that near you? It's a retreat type setting, you go overnight, take classes, hike etc.It seems cool, I wish I could go. The hardest part for me right now, is getting to the class.But it does make you feel so good. Take care, Paula

KJ, I have been taking Pillates and Yoga since August and I have found that it has helped me manage my pain more than just using medications. If possible, I recommend finding a class that mixes both Pillates and Yoga or you rlocal library may carry exercise tapes/DVDs of these. Target carries a nice selsction of DVD's for $10 that involve mixing both of these. Pillates has given me more benefits in my IC than yoga but i find much of Yoga helps with stress. Hope you continue to have good results. mick

Pilates I got a DVD from Wal mart a couple months back & did really well until the beginning of December when I started getting that virual infection that went around, I've been back on my Air Walker & husband's recumbent bike 5 min. each morning for 2 weeks now. Now I'm slowly recovering from that infection, man has it ever taken a toll on me. Normally I don't get sick or catch things. I guess around christmas time is when things go around huh?! Everyones cooped up!!! Now my ears are finally opening up & I've got sinus drainage so I know the infection's on it's way out but of course not fast enough for me, LOL!! From what I've heard this infection has taken people up to a month to kick it, I was like NOOOOOOOOOOO!!! I think my other downfall is that I'm in peri menopause & it's taken a toll on my immune system. Boy am I ever taking lots of vitamins & supplements but I'm determined to get better darn't :woohoo: I'm normally a high energy & positive person but when you lose your desire to do anything & don't have much taste it makes things difficult. I'm grateful that the burnt taste that was in my mouth is finally gone though. I've been spraying disinfectant/germicide in each room 3 times a day for the last 2 weeks & cracking the windows just a hair to get some fresh air in here. Anyway back to the Pilates, I did them every other day for like 2 months I really felt a difference!!! When I got sick & couldnt' do them I ACHED for over a week it was awful. Until I know I'm 100% better I'm not gonna start them to be on the safe side.

contents: To Arizonian: Where in Arizona did you take Pilates? I am in the phoenix area. Also to the lady who bought the Reformer. I heard from a chiropractor that the Reformer was the safest for people with lower back problems. Sammie
signature: Sammi Meds: Melatonin 3mg @ bedtime if needed. Estrogen 1.5 mg troche and 0.1 mg Estrace cream. Diagnosis: IC, PFD (both in remission)

contents: Class #2 hey everyone. I went to pilates again today. Boy am I going to be sore. I did pretty well but I am in a class thats a bit above my level. The good thing is the instructor gives lots of individual attention and is aware of my condition. I am not feeling as good today as last week but I was already not feeling so hot before class. I think its blueberries. I might just have to accept that even a few of them are not going to work for me. I actually felt a little better after class. I tried some of the higher level stuff but some of these people are awesome in the class. I really dont care about that. I tend to be really competitive with my other sports but this for me is like yoga. Just do what you can do. A good way to accept yourself and your limitations -including your body's limitations. Ive been hating my body's limitations in more ways than one. On a serious notes does anyone know any research about exercise and IC? Pilates or otherwise? Im just interested. What do other people do for exercise? The class is only 10 weeks and I want to stick with it. I may have to take some of your advice about buying a tape. Next time in Target I'm gonna check it out. I miss playing soccer and running but it feels good to be doing something. Plus I feel like pilates is something I could do long term. I was even thinking about getting my fiance to do it with me. He is trying to eat better and work out- get in better shape for the wedding. It seems that all the weight I lost from IC hes gaining. Its actually been helpful though. This way we are both in a healthy mindset. I thought it would be nice to do (we vowed to learn something new together). He has a bad back and I think this would also help with that. Im not sure though he might think its too girly? Hes into playing basketball and tennis. Ill keep you posted if I get him to go with me. That would be fun just to watch him try. Let me know how you guys are doing with the exercise and IC. Feel good--Kelly

Class #3 Well I went again today. I am flarring a bit but again Im not sure if its the pilates or the cereal I had for breakfast. I think it was for kids and there was some added folic acid in it. While I was doing it my pain wasnt bad but now its acting up a bit. I think I might need to just work through it. It seems that the more regular you exercise the better it gets. Im only doing it once a week? I was thinking of taking private lessons or doing something else like stretches or yoga another day. That way there is not so much time in between. I also read that sometimes people take their atarax before working out? Does anyone take that or use it before exercise? Something to do with mast cells? Thanks--KJ

I got a DVD from walmart cost around $10, it helps abs, butt & thighs although I skip the butt section!! I really like it & it only takes 10 minutes. Once I kick this virual crap I'm gonna start it up again, I had a sinus thing that is now in my ears & breaking up & draining not fun!!! Lots of vitamin C & zinc right now along with saline rinses which are gross but they help clean all the "gook" out!

contents: I think Pilates caused most of my problems. I was in a nice remission and then started doing Pilates. I felt like my saprapubic muscles was strained one day. In Pilates, you contract your pelvis muscles. I now have pevic floor dysfunction, and will have to relax the pelvis floor muscles. I'm totally confused how this can help an IC patient. Your contridicing your body.

I did Pilates (privately) and felt the best ever-I hate to exercise! I ended up leaving due to a personal family situation out of state and the commitment was not only too hard to keep, but very costly...so I haven't returned. Since all of that, I have been dxd with Ulcerative /colitis and like IC, it has truly interfered with my life....BUT!!! I do hope to get back to Pilates, I have not only lost a ton of weight again, but my muscles and strength are really hopeless right now! For that I know Pilates is fabulous. Many of my die hard "athletic/jogger/gym" friends have switched from the more rigorous exercises, to Pilates as it is easier on aging joints, etc...and others Yoga. I hope to get back, will need private to get back in form, but did not have any problems with my IC and on days that were more flare oriented, we just worked different areas to keep pressure, etc away from my pelvis. Good luck!
signature: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~"No one can make you feel inferior without your consent." Eleanor Roosevelt, This Is My Story, 1937

contents: Another thing I truly believe it depends upon the person & the serverity of their IC, I was doing the abs & thighs faithfully for like 3 months last year, then my sinuses started up & I stopped. Then I battled Seasonal Affective Disorder. Thankfully I'm now on the road to recovery after this starting around chrismtas time! However I really benefitted from them, I sure felt it if I didn't, LOL!! However I also believe I have mild IC, I watch my diet, watch what I drink, am consistent with all my supplemens & vitamins and I've been okay. Yea it's a lot of work but i feel so much better because of it & that makes it all worth it to me! It took me from 1997 to last spring to find the right vitamins & supplements for me though...wasted a lot of $$ too but I think we all learn from our mistakes, right?! I use a Pro Form Air Walker & a commercial Recumbent Bike 5 minutes each machine every morning no matter what. When I've been better for a couple of weeks I'll probably start up the Pilates DVD again, heck I've gotten it down to like 10 minutes a day once you get the moves down pat.

contents: Pilates alright so far Thats kind of what I was hoping. Im also a milder IC. I have been on the strict diet and had a hydro about two months ago. Im doing better but of course have not figgered out what vitamins, supplements, and meds are the best cocktail for me. The pilates does seem to flare me a bit but it also seems to help me feel like me again. Release some toxins, sweat a bit, and if done correctly think it is helping my muscles down there. I havent been diagnosed with PFD though. I am also doing kegels to help relax the muscles and take some anxiety away so I can start having sex again. Get some of the hard wired anxiety out of the way. I def think it helps me immune system when I do them but Im trying to be careful and take it slow. I cant go tomorrow so Ill prob go sat instead which is actually an even easier class. They are small and the woman is real hands on me which I like. Im going to double check with my doc when I see him in two weeks about pilates. But so far Im hoping it will be something I do over the long haul to help keep me healthy, balanced, and stronger. Thanks for the motivation Jen and others. Keep it coming. I'll check in with you guys later--Kelly

I also love doing Pilates. When I feel better, I'm going to give it another go, but If I experience what I'm going through now, I'll have to go back to a stationary bike. In March I have appointments with the GYN and Urologist. I think I might also have a rectocele because of a bulging pain in my rectum. Maybe this was truly the cause of my vulvodynia symptoms.

Pilates questions Hi Mick, Do you happen to know the name of the DVD you got from target? Classes are going well but I might want to keep it going after the winter session and I have no money. I like the idea of mixing them both too? Sammie, What exactly is a reformer? Thanks--Kelly

Pilates DVD Kelly, I got mine from Wal Mart but I'm sure you can get it elsewhere, it's called Pick your spot Pilates, The instructor is Ellen Barrett, it's a purple colored case too. Hope this helps!!

It always take s me a while to realize that it is my IC since I really only (now anyways) get a burning/itchiness feeling in lower extremities and usually mistake it for a yeast infection when it's not. Loriebelle, Yes, chocolate is supposed to be one of our top five trigger foods and I don't know what else is in your protein shake but if you want to list all the ingredients we can help you sort it out.

contents: Wow! It always amazes me how many foods can trigger problems. I finally got the list of ingredients (I kept forgetting) They are: Myopro (unique blend of whey protein from specially filtered and non-exchanged whey protein concentrate and isolate, calcium caseinate, milk protein isolate, sodium caseinate, alpha-ketoglutaric acid, egg albumin, taurine and l-glutamine), maltodextrin, cocoa (processed with alkali), witamin and mineral blend (tricalcium phosphate, diptoassium phosphate, potassium chloride, magnesium oxide, choline bitartrate, disodium phosphate, beta carotene, dl-alpha tocopheryl acetate, potassium citrate, ascorbic acid, ferrous fumurate, boron proteinate, manganese gluconate, biotin, niacinamide, zinc oxide, d-calcium pantothenate, vitamin a palmitate, copper sulfate, chromium citrate, cyanocobalamin, selenium amino acid chelate, molybdenum amino acid chelate, folic acid, pyridoxine hydrochloride, riboflavin, thiamine, hydrochloride and potassium iodide, natural and artificial flavors, oat fiber, sunflower oil, carrageenan, salt, xanthan gum, acesulfame potassium, sucralose (splenda), mono and diglycerides, medium chain triglycerides and soy lecithin. Oh my! That's alot of ingredients and I don't even know what half of them are. Since a lot of you have mentioned that vitamin Bs can be a problem too, this contains 50% of daily value based on a 2000 calorie diet of B6 and 90% of B12. Also, I don't take the whole pkg, I split it in three servings and I drink it with water. it's hard tobelieve that there could be enough of anything to bother anyone in one third of a pkg I also take a B Complex Vitamin (redoxon-B) which I do know increases my frequency and urgency but does not cause any pain. However, I feel better otherwise (less stressed). If I were to quit taking the redoxon B, does anybody have any other suggestions to help facilitate destressing oneself? thanks again

just a thought...but if you research amino acids (essentials work with non-essentials) there is one there for stress. I don't know what it is at the top of my head, but I'm sure if you surf the internet you will find it. Exercise is good for destressing, yoga, and a trip to the health food store, I'm sure they could suggest something. Good Luck.

contents: Loriebelle, That is a lot of ingredients - many of which i haven't heard of either. To be honest, when I look at the nutritional information on a package and it has a list of ingredients like this I steer waaaaaaaaaaaay clear of it. There is just so many things that can trigger IC symptoms so I try to eat very simply prepared foods and my IC has improved considerably. Good luck to you - I hope you feel better soon.

contents: Weighed myself again today and I have lost 6 pounds all together now. :woohoo: Was hoping for 10 but I had a couple of days I couldn't exercise. Didn't feel good enough for it. 6 is better then zero. How is everyone else doing with their diets?
:woohoo: :woohoo: congrats.

I buckled down this week and am doing alright, only got to the gym on Monday, but I did some careful step aerobics and some gentle aerobics (like indoor walking I have on a tape) tues thru thurs. Also using little one pound weights during my aerobics to give my workouts a little added kick. Congrats on the 6 pound weight loss! You are doing fabulous!

What are the step aerobic tapes/dvd kind? I didn't know they had step aerobics. That would be something different then always using the treadmill or walking outside. Do you put the weights on your ankles or do you hold them?

The step is something I do on my own, (I took step classes a while ago and modified what I learned so I could do it now) the regular aerobics is from a walking tape from Leslie Sansone called Walk away the pounds. Its mostly marching in place, some low kicks, knees up, and some hand movements to get the heart rate up. I use 1 pound hand weights, they are "D" shaped so they are very comfy in my hands, lol got em for a buck at Target a cpl weeks ago! I do have the ankle weights too, so I do either or both, depending on how I am feeling. Sometimes I use weighted balls that came w/the walking tapes. I think they are 2 1/2 pounds, cant remember at the moment. I also use a website that you can keep track of your exercise, calories, water intake etc. to help me out.

Hi pottywoman, Congratulations on your 6 pound weight loss! That is great! I've had a bad cold/sinus infection so I haven't been exercising but I've been pretty strict on my diet too. I think I've lost 4 or 5 so far. Keep up the great work!

contents: Megancolleen, Are you eating over 800 calories? I have learned that if you eat less than that, your body goes into starvation mode and hold on to the fat. I dont do any real ab work tho, causes the owies lol. I have a tai chi dvd also with David Caradine (spelling?) from the movie Kill Bill, and oh! I bought a belly dancing infused w/jazz cd, I just dont move as much as they do lol!!! Both are fun also. I enjoy changing my routines a lot. The tai chi, oh do I enjoy that! Lets all keep up the good work!!!

Weight loss Great job on the weight loss, YEA!!!! I need to get back to my pilates DVD but am waiting until I kick this virual infection for good. Meanwhile I do my 5 minutes on my Air Walker & 5 minutes on my husband's recumbent bike every morning no matter what...if I don't then I ache all day I've still got 5 more pounds to go to reach what I was at before I started taking anti depressants nearly 3 years ago...basically I'm really getting good at disciplining myself in what I eat & drink...not fun but it sure is worth it, I feel a lot better too. Another thing, I have my/our exercise equipment in the basement, in front of the television that is hooked up to the cable tv, otherwise I doubt I'd last the 10 minutes that I do every morning, LOL!!! I usually watch the news, music videos or weather channel, I'm a weather "nut"!! There are many mornings that I have to tell myself you gotta do this, you'll feel better after you do!!!

contents: Congrats to all. Keep up the good work. Jen, if you don't mind me asking. How much have you lost all together and how long did it take you?

I gained 20 pounds over 2 1/2 years of being on anti depressants. Given I'm not that big of a person, I'm 5'1" now 100 pounds & it wasn't until I was on anti depressants that I even gained & carried more weight ( I'm the odd ball of my family honestly!!! isn't aging just lovely, NOT!!!). I got up to 115 & I don't care who you are when your clothes start not fitting your self esteem drops!!! Anyway I've been working on this weight loss for over a year. I'm normally a very high energy & active person, pretty much always on the "go". The last nearly 3 years my body started changing (peri menopause), my desire to do anything diminished so I've been doing a LOT of sitting & doing nothing. That doesn't "sit well" with me @ ALL!! Once I had the Hydrothermal Ablation done & when my body started going through the menopausal changes I couldn't deal with it anymore I knew I had to do SOMETHING!!! That is when I called the leader of the company I'm with & asked what I should do. Added more supplements & vitamins & what a difference it's made!! My cravings are gone. Before I got this virual infection @ christmas time, I pretty much had my hormones balanced out & wasn't depressed. It's been difficult to take all my supplements & vitamins since I got the infection but I'm getting there slowly. The infection started out as sinuses, then went to my ears. Now my ears are really opening up so within a week or so I hope to be on the full road to recovery!!! Again for me it's a matter of discipline...not easy doing things every day sometimes I have to tell myself Jen you KNOW you need to exercise today, you'll feel like crap if you don't!!!

contents: Jen, sorry to hear about the infection. THat can't be fun. I gained 50 pounds between the birth control and the anti depressants. I think the birth control threw my system off. I had a problem with yo-yo dieting in the past but I had manged to finally keep it off for 3 years and then the bc pills and boom.....I'm 5' 4" and I now weigh 165 lbs. Was past 170 at one time. I want to get down to 115 but I don't know. If I could just stick with the less eating I know I could get rid of it all. You know, I feel better too after I exercise. Hmm, that's interesting. I know if I sit all day I feel worse then if I had at least gone outside (weather permiting) and walked 10 minutes. I use to walk 3 hours at a time before the IC happened and sometimes I think I should still be able to do that. Think I am going to buy the tv dinners for weight loss. Anyone try them? We have to all stick together. Hope you get to feeling better. Oh, what supplyments are you talking about? Vitamins

Gosh sorry to hear about your weight gain, that is not fair!!! i've been trying to motivate myself today to go down stairs & get on the bike again but haven't gotten there yet these clouds are just so depressing, I've got the lights on & candles lit in almost every room!!! Now, I just saw on the weather channel, we're gonna get up to a foot of white crap by noon tomorrow, NOOOOOOOOOOOO!!! Thankfully my husband who is on his way back from Texas has made arrangements with our friend who has a 4x4 pickup to go & get him tomorrow if the roads get too bad, our neighbor's agreed to come over & plow out the driveway...we have a snowblower that my parents gave us but, I gotta get to the shed 1st to get it out!!! If we get a foot I'm screwed!! LOL!! My anxiety has been pretty high today because of the weather, I deal so much better with the heat & humidity just not the tornado watches!!! As for the vitamins & supplements I take you can e-mail me for more information: [email protected], I can say this you can't buy them in any store...I've tried those!!
