contents: Exercise is just agonizing for me. Does anyone have any suggestions? The elliptical machine hurts as bad as treadmill. At one point I was doing the 2.5 miles in 30 min, 5 times/week, but I would just go home and get in the fetal position afterwards due to the pain and fatigue. I am afraid of swimming because of UTI and infection. Yoga doesn’t hurt quite as bad, but it doesn’t provide the cardio. Before the CPP came along I loved to inline skate -- it made me so happy I wish I could find a way to do it on a perfectly smooth surface and not fall. I shouldn't even think about it -- maybe when there is a cure for IC ads

Hi everyone, I have been working out since Nov. Weights five times a week plus 30 mins of cardio at the gym. Then pilates class three times a week. Now Im going to start a running program in the mornings. I still haven't lost any weight....ugh..Im not overweight it's just im four sizes bigger now than I was before my baby two years ago. I lost the first fifteen pounds by the time he was six weeks, those last fifteen pounds have been hanging on now for two years. I stick to the IC diet and really don't eat alot. For example; Today I had two boiled eggs for breakfast - 9am 4 ounces of turkey and 2 ounces of cheese - 11am garden salad without dressing - 1pm 5 crackers with peanut butter - 4pm 6 inch personal veggie pizza with half the tomato sauce and feta cheese - 6pm IS this too much for me? this is a typical day...I have no patence for counting calories. I never drink my calories, just water. You would think that even cutting out juice, fruit, alcohol, pop I would lose some weight. I have tried weight watchers, atkins ect...nothing budges. Before the baby I would litterally eat out everyday and drink up to 10 drinks a weekend at the bar such as white russians ect....in university. That was a ton of calories...I had mcdonalds every day or wendys ect. This is just not fair...lol. Help me. Im doing a food journal starting today and going to a deitition to help with the IC diet. So Im going to ask her for some tips..I know she is going to look at me like im crazy but I really want to lose some weight. I have some great clothes that are still in style that I can only get one leg into....so I really want to try to fit into them. Anyway I hate this struggle. I swear no matter what I do nothing changes. On a positive note the IC diet is working so at least I don't feel sick. Hmm Im on elavil could that be slowing my metabolism? Also I was always on the birth control pill before the baby and Im not on it now. Could it have sped up my metabolism?

Hi Carolyn Sounds like you are doing a good amount of exercising and certainly your meals sound light. I wonder if you are getting enough calories for the amount of exercising you are doing. Sometimes your body goes into "starvation mode" if you are exercising regularly and not eating enough food. Have you lost any inches? or maybe a pant size? Sometimes when you first start out with a new program you gain muscle and lose inches but not weight! It's great that you are controlling your IC with diet and some medications. At least this disease has not stopped you from working out! Another thing I know from the Elavil from when I was on it for migraines was that not matter what I did I could not lose weight. It seemed to slow my metabolism from a rabbit to a snail! I exercised and tried to eat right but nothing helped. I stopped it about three years ago and started Weight Watchers. It helped me go from 160 to 125. Unfortuantely lack of exercise and a bit of depression from this condition has upped my weight about 10 pounds and climbing. One of my reasons for starting this thread. Tell us about your meeting with the dietician, maybe some good ideas for all us. Keep up the good work.

To Jen (aka trytosmile) Hi, noticed that you were interested in walking tapes from Leslie Samone (?) I was in Zellars yesterday and they had a hugh selection of workout tapes to choose from, there were some from that particular person. They were around $10.00. Also don't know if she is (Leslie that is, I notice you are from Sarnia ) American but there is a Canadian website that I use all the time for ordering workout tapes. It is cksales.com and they are great. I just ordered three more tapes to add to my over 100 tape collection. All costs are in Canadian dollars and they are excellent to deal with. Keep up the great job!

Febuary Fitness challange Hi Cybersis I have the TreadMoves sport conditioning tape. I really enjoy this one it is really challenging. I hope to get some more soon. I'm going to check out the website you suggested. Do you ever use music when you work out on the treadmill? Wondering if you make your own discs or tapes. I take my discman to the gym, I can't workout on the treadmill or stairclimber without my music now. I get so bored, I find watching the TV's boring without the sound. I really enjoy weight training, I mainly use free weights, I like the control of this more than machines. Cables are also my preferred choice. I've worked with a trainer in the past and have a number of weight routines that I use. Do you use the swiss ball at all? They are great.

I have a treadmill at home as well as using the one at the gym. I saved for two years to buy a good treadmill and I have it in my basement near my TV, so at home I watch TV and at the gym I use my own mixed tapes that I make. I have a full set of free weights at home as well as barbells, weightballs, bands and exercise ball. I alternate between the gym and home. The gym when I want some company and at home when my day is just to busy. Another day anothe workout!

I usually walk in the office during my lunch time (8 times around the building plus I go up the stairs) which is 1 mile every day, when I first started doing this, I lost about 20 pounds and 2 dress sizes! Plus where I work, I take the long way around to the offices that I need to go, so that is extra. I have a pedometer that tells me how many steps that I take daily.
Anybody out there a member of CURVES? I have been for 2 years and I just love it! Cyndie

I am not a member of curves but have been a lifetime member of Weight Watchers for two years. Over the past six months I have gained back around 10 pounds and hope these challenges will help me get off my butt and back to exercise. I've heard curves is a great place with good people!
I also am a weight watchers lifetime member. Curves works for me since it is only 30 mins a day I can go during my lunch! It works great for the body and the mind!! :woohoo:

Cybersis... thanks for the information. I as at Chapters the other day and saw the Leslie Samone tapes and they where $20 plus. Eck. Will check out Zellers today and the website. Thanks for the information. I went for a 2 hour walk yesterday afternoon it was so nice outside (couple pit stops behind trees). I had the worse day and needed to vent/cry by myself. It worked. I gave "being a lady" a whole definition on that walk. Good thing it was out in the country. I'm sure if somebody saw me they would have instantly admitted me in the physc ward !
signature: Jen ...

contents: Can't use an elliptical? Originally posted by ads Exercise is just agonizing for me. Does anyone have any suggestions? The elliptical machine hurts as bad as treadmill. At one point I was doing the 2.5 miles in 30 min, 5 times/week, but I would just go home and get in the fetal position afterwards due to the pain and fatigue. I am afraid of swimming because of UTI and infection. Yoga doesn’t hurt quite as bad, but it doesn’t provide the cardio. Before the CPP came along I loved to inline skate -- it made me so happy I wish I could find a way to do it on a perfectly smooth surface and not fall. I shouldn't even think about it -- maybe when there is a cure for IC ads Hey ads. I haven't been able to get on an elliptical either. The pool is just one big flare waiting to happen. I know how frustrating it can be! Based on my personal experience, though, I had to start out really slowly, one of the hardest things for me to accept. I started walking at a leisurely pace about 15 minutes a few days a week. Now I can walk about 45 minutes, but speed walking is still a no, no. I also worked up to being able to ride a stationary bike for an hour 3 times a week--funny, though, I can't ride the recumbant ones because that leads to a flare. Wishing you well, Amaris

signature: After suffering with IC for 40 years, my bladder was removed in Dec 05...YAY!

contents: Just completed a 1-1/2 mile walk sponsored by our wellness group for Womens Heart Month. We had a good crowd. I had a give a little talk to the women/and some men because they asked me to since I was the only one they knew who have any kind of heart condition. The weather was pretty nice, a little cool, but by the time you were half way done, you were beginning to get hot.

Any fellow dieters out there? I've got a quick question-- Does anyone flare after a yoga session, pilates session, or a treadmill work out? My husband just bought me a treadmill (early anniversary gift/quit-nagging-me-to-death gift ) and I was wondering for those who do use a treadmill, how do you feel afterwards? I do yoga and pilates, and sometimes I get a nasty flare up...just would like some experience input. Thanks
signature: Mommy to 2 crazy, wonderful kids and wife to the most amazing man in the world!

contents: I actually just started working out myself and I do the treadmill for 30 minutes, usually about 2 miles and then I do either Pilates, or a strength and training class and I also do some equipment. I have my worst flares after the treadmill. I don't know why, maybe it is because of the constant movement of the bladder. I also drink water while I am walking so that doesn't help. It is everything I can do to make it 30 minutes without jumping off to hit the restroom. I thought about trying the Eliptical machine but I think that would create more irritation, I don't know. Very interesting that you brought this up though, I would love to know how everyone deals with it because I do feel good after exercising. Lisa

Thanks for replying, Lisa...I thought maybe I was a loon or something because of how I end up flaring--but I so desperately need to work out, and my fibromyalgia feels much better after some stretching and walking. I drink a lot of blue powerade (I can tolerate it) while I am working out too--and I end up having to leave my workout to go to the bathroom!
signature: Mommy to 2 crazy, wonderful kids and wife to the most amazing man in the world!

contents: That is funny you say that about your Fibromyalgia. I also suffer from that as well and it seems to help me with my Fibro as well. I can feel like crap when I go into the gym and when I leave my muscles feel much better. One thing I can't do is ride the bikes there. I have a bike and in the summer it is like torture for me to take a ride with my family. Thank god for the treadmill!! Lisa
I lowered the incline on my treadmill. I also lowered the speed. It's not as good of a workout, but at least it's something!! Not as intense, but at least I feel I'm working out! liz

Jess, I think recent studies have shown that you don't need to power walk or run in order to achieve a sense of fitness. Try less intense and smaller time increments...just do them maybe 3 times a day. I think less is more sometimes and working yourself into a flair is no good. Twenty minutes on the tread mill spread out a few times during the day is a great fitness opportunity. You might also want to incorporate low weight resistence training...not only will this increase your strength and is a major metabolism booster as well as a proven weight loss regimen...it should be very kind to your bladder...start with easy weights, 5 lbs max and when ready work up to 8lbs. The repetition and regular activity far outweighs trying to get a super workout in all at once. If no weights there are many programs you can do and achieve results, the isometric version of resistance training. I am sure you can google up lots of versions of programs. Good luck, you can do this...and you know how I love Pilates...its perfect. Just remember, go easy and moderation is the key to staying the course. Not to mention being a gentler workout for your fibro.
signature: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~"No one can make you feel inferior without your consent." Eleanor Roosevelt, This Is My Story, 1937

contents: I am not really sure about weights...maybe some really light ones I suppose. The worst part of my fibro is in my shoulders and arms, and my neck. Thanks for the input--I'm kind of hoping that my fibro will lessen with more weight loss.
signature: Mommy to 2 crazy, wonderful kids and wife to the most amazing man in the world!

contents: Don't be too afraid of weights...use very small ones, just for control...you can do this without them as well...but this is the first line of treatment recommended to my husband for fibro, low weight resitance training as well as cardio...you know what works best for you. Just a thought.
signature: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~"No one can make you feel inferior without your consent." Eleanor Roosevelt, This Is My Story, 1937

contents: I just started back at the gym last week after a 3mth absence. I walk on the treadmill usually 30 minutes sometimes more sometimes less depending on how i feel. I am taking Elmiron for about 6 weeks now and I get dizzy so I have to watch my speed. I have long been a step aerobics fan but there is no way I can do that right know. I sometimes break up the treadmill session in two and do my free weights in between. I usually end up hurting by the end of my workout but in the long run I find it is helping me. I use to run alot but that won't be happening until I feel a whole lot better. I guess do a little of something is better than nothing at all.
Lifting weights helps my fibro. I just am careful not to overdue it. It really helps....
signature: ~TRACEY~ Wife and Mother of three beautiful children.

contents: I used to run but that is a no no know. The treadmill, I was getting off to go potty more than I was on it. Yes, did cause some irritation. Yoga, tried, works well for me. I just have to limit my poses that involve alot of twisting in the stomach area. Same with pilates. Lift small weights and no problems. Baseball, ballhockey, golf, kickboxing and spinning classes, that I had to give up
signature: Jen ...

contents: Tried the treadmill and now I am hurting again. Bladder feels inflamed. I used to run A LOT before I got IC. I only did 20 minutes on a very slow speed with high impact shoes. Still irritated my bladder. Poop!

I used to run a lot before I had IC, along with roller skating, biking, walking...I was rarely home, always on the go. Now I'm a couch potato. Ugh...can't wait to get back on track. Thanks for the input...maybe I'll try weights, just so afraid of ending up in a fibro flare.
signature: Mommy to 2 crazy, wonderful kids and wife to the most amazing man in the world!

contents: With a proper workout plan, preferably a mirror for form...use the smallest weights Jess and i think it will be okay. If you were to do isometric arm exercises with no weights, I promise you will feel it anyway until you get those muscles going a bit...I know, I have avoided going back to Pilates for too long. Your arms are much younger, so give it a try....I think it can evenrually help you rather than flare your fibro....uh, could you do a few for me?
signature: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~"No one can make you feel inferior without your consent." Eleanor Roosevelt, This Is My Story, 1937

contents: Try doing the bike instead of the treadmill. You can exercise more effeciently and longer because you aren't putting pressure on your bladder! I discovered this a few weeks ago when my husband convinced me to start exercising to "feel better" and be less stressed. I can't really do weights because many of the exercises can flare ups, but the bike and swimming I can do successfully!

I use my elliptical about 15 minutes every other day. I have found that this light schedule is not very bothersome to my bladder. But I do make sure I take a pain pill about thirty minutes before exercising.
signature: Janelle I am a soul whose intentions are good. Oh Lord please don't let me be misunderstood.

contents: Elliptical is better for me than treadmill. I use it at low resistance & only for 10-15 min, then go do weights. Walking on land is no problem, walking uphill is okay, but walking downhill can trigger a flare. I put some extra Dr. Scholls insoles in my shoes (the thickest ones) and that has helped with walking. Yoga is okay, but I also avoid too much tummy twisting & the poses that require lying flat on stomach (cobra is not good for me at all, but child's pose is okay). What has worked best for me at the gym is the rowing machine. If you're skinny, the exercise bike that leans back (i think it's called recumbent) might be okay. It didn't work for me because I've developed a tummy & my legs were bumping into it, causing flare. (Geez, how embarrassing). Good luck, I hope you find something you like.

contents: Flare after exercise Hi Jess124 I usually flare after exercise. My thing is that I didn,t exercise for almost 3 months from October/04 to December/04. I have been an avid exerciser for years and this was really devasting to me. I had just started running last year with a running club and I had to stop in August because the pain was just to much for me. I was not diagnosed with IC until November. I am trying really hard to get back into fitness but it is tough after a long day at work. Stress as my job really causes me some serious flare problems. I haven't done any fitness classes since October and I hope I can get back at that soon if this dizziness goes away.

I noticed with great interest the 5 pounds before Christmas thread. What about an update on that? How did everyone do, did you diet, did you try and exercise more? How about a new challenge every month where we sign in each week to encourage and congratulate each participant. So starting on February 1st, I will sign in for all of us. I won't tell you my weight but will tell you if I exercised at least 3 times that week and managed to put down the pint of vanilla ice cream! So how about it IC Girls and Guys, anyone feel up to the challenge!
Sounds good - count me in

contents: Count me in, -I should be ready to go again by Feb 1st. I was chopping wood last week when something in my back popped and I barely made it back to the house. From the level of piercing pain with any movement I'd thought I'd really done some serious damage! These past two days I'm getting around pretty good again, but will have to put off my weight training for a while. -Still hurts to lift anything. All I can think of is gentle walking. Swimming would be great but there are no public or gym pools where I live. Anyone else have some exercise suggestions which are easy on the back? My personal goal is to look better in my bathing suit by the middle of April when I'll actually be seen in public wearing the darn thing! I'd like to lose another 13 pounds yet. Vicki
